Absorbing Shield

# Absorbing Shield

*Armor (shield), very rare*

**Attunement:** Requires Attunement

**Description**

A ring of runes runs along this shield's circumference. While holding this shield, you gain a +2 bonus to AC. This shield has 6 charges, and it regains all expended charges daily at dawn. When you take damage that isn't bludgeoning, slashing, or piercing damage, you can use your reaction to expend one charge to produce the effects of a 1st-level Absorb Elements spell, but it can also absorb force, necrotic, poison, or radiant damage. This feature does not function if the triggering damage is paired with bludgeoning, slashing, or piercing damage.

Accretion Beam

# Accretion Beam

*Very Rare, Weapon (Firearm)*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

An unstable device that outputs a staggering amount of energy the longer it remains active.

The weapon has a normal range of 60 feet and a long range of 180 feet, and it has the two-handed property. It deals 1 fire damage on a hit.

**Unstable -** The weapon's damage cannot be modified by your ability modifier, or any other bonus provided by items, spells, or class features. However, for each consecutive hit against the same target, the weapon's damage doubles. This effect resets if an attack from this weapon misses, you attack a different target, or you do not attack the target on your turn.

**Continuous -** No more than two attacks can be made with this weapon per round.

Aci Feather

# Aci Feather

*Wondrous Item, common*

**Description**

A beautiful blue and white feather that when worn ones hair will billow majestically, even if there is no wind present as a bonus action; works with the cloak of billowing.

Alchemist'S Soothing Tea

# Alchemist's Soothing Tea

*Wondrous Item (Consumable), uncommon*

**Description**

When measuring and balancing the herbal specimens \[Devil's Bloodleaf leaves and Phorus Blossom nectar\] needed to make this tea, 2d4 + 2 bags of magical tea can be created with each 'batch'. You can steep a bag of tea for 1 minute in a cup of boiling water to transform the water into a rich, flavorful tea that calms the nerves and revitalizes the spirit. When you drink a cup of the hot tea, you gain 2d6 + 2 temporary hit points. In addition, you may roll with advantage on saving throws caused by any effect causing you to be charmed or frightened for 10 minutes. You can drink the tea using an action, although it's most commonly finished over the course of at least 1 minute. If the tea has been left to cool \[10 minutes+\] before you drink it, you regain only half the amount of temporary hit points, and no other benefits are present.

Alchemy Blade

# Alchemy Blade

[![Alchemy Blade](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/d/d4/Alchemy_Blade.png/revision/latest/scale-to-width-down/180?cb=20241009130243)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/d/d4/Alchemy_Blade.png/revision/latest?cb=20241009130243)

[](/docs/file:alchemy-blade.png)

Alchemy Blade

*Weapon (All Blades), rare*

**Description**

This sword has a glass channel running down its length. In place of a traditional pommel, it instead has a open hole designed to fit atop a typical flask or vial. While holding the sword, you can use an action to attach a flask or vial to the sword and draw its contents into its blade; doing so changes the sword's properties based on the attached flask or vial.

*   **Acid Vial**. The sword deals an extra 1d6 acid damage on a hit. When the target is an object or structure that isn't being worn or carried, the acid damage is maximized.
*   **Alchemist's Fire**. The sword deals an extra 1d6 fire damage on a hit. When you reduce a creature to 0 hit points using the sword, a creature of your choice within 5 feet of it also takes 2d6 fire damage.
*   **Basic Poison**. The sword deals an extra 1d6 poison damage on a hit. The first time that the target hits with an attack that uses Strength before the start of your next turn, the damage is reduced by the poison damage dealt.
*   **Holy Water**. The sword deals an extra 1d6 radiant damage on a hit. The radiant damage is doubled against fiends and undead.

Each attached vial can produce the above effects for 3 attacks before the contents of the vial are exhausted. You can remove the flask or vial using an action, destroying its contents and leaving the container empty.

Amentum

# Amentum

*Ring, uncommon*

**Description**

While wearing the Amentum, you can throw a weapon with the Thrown property a further distance equal to the object's initial minimum distance. Additionally, the Amentum corrects an object's trajectory while in flight. If the player rolls a 1 on a ranged attack roll from a weapon with the thrown property, while wearing the Amentum, they may choose to reroll, but must take the results of the second roll. This feature can be used three times, resetting at dawn.

Amonkhet Naga

# Amonkhet Naga

## Ability Score Increase

Your Constitution score increases by 2, and your Intelligence score increases by 1.

## Age

Like humans, naga reach adulthood in their late teens. They show no signs of aging beyond that point except for growing larger, so in theory, a naga could live well over a century.

## Alignment

Most naga are either neutral or neutral evil in alignment.

## Size

Naga stand about 5 feet tall when upright, but the total length of their bodies, head to tail, ranges from 10 to as much as 20 feet. Your size is Medium.

## Speed

Your base walking speed is 30 feet.

## Speed Burst

Using your tail as a propellant you can give yourself a speed boost. Immediately after you move at least 10 feet in a straight line, you can propel yourself 15 feet in the same direction without provoking opportunity attacks. You can use this a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

## Natural Weapons

Your fanged maw and constricting serpentine body are natural weapons, which you can use to make unarmed strikes. If you hit with your bite, you deal piercing damage equal to 1d4 + your Strength modifier, and your target must make a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution modifier). On a failed save, the target takes 1d4 poison damage.

If you hit with your constrict attack, you deal bludgeoning damage equal to 1d6 + your Strength modifier, and if the target is one size larger or smaller than the naga, the target is grappled (escape DC 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength modifier). Until this grapple ends, the target is restrained, and you can’t constrict another target.

## Poison Immunity

You are immune to poison damage and can’t be poisoned.

## Poison Affinity

You gain proficiency with the poisoner’s kit.

## Languages

You can speak, read, and write Common and Naga.

Arcane Core

# Arcane Core

*Wondrous item, rare*

This wondrous, arcane-fueled stone bearing the power rune, can be fitted to any tiny object, this process takes an hour to complete. Once the item is complete the item becomes a weapon that can only be thrown. The weapon has the following properties:

**Thrown:** (20/40),

**Damage:** 1d6\[magical bludgeoning\]

The item appears normal but can be detected using spells like detect magic. It uses your Dexterity or Strength modifier for attack and damage rolls.

**Invoking the Rune.**  When you make an attack with the item and hit, it deals an additional 2d6 force damage to the target. Any creature or object within 5 feet of the impact must make a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, they take 3d6 force damage and are pushed back 10 feet from the point of impact. On a successful save, they take half damage and are not pushed back.

Once the rune has been invoked, it can't be invoked again until the next dawn.

[![Hexcore](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/d/d3/Hexcore.png/revision/latest/scale-to-width-down/180?cb=20250204144603)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/d/d3/Hexcore.png/revision/latest?cb=20250204144603)

[](/docs/file:hexcore.png)

If the attack misses, it will travel to max thrown range and can be picked up and used.

Arcane Filter

# Arcane Filter

*Wondrous item, uncommon*

**Description**

This quante, sphere shaped metal device is just big enough to be held in one hand when inactivate. When activated, either by a command word or by a button activation on the side of the object, it unfurls and begins to hover soundlessly 5 feet off the ground near where it was activated. It can be pushed or moved out of the way, and will magically slow down to avoid collision if pushed into a wall or person. It has a 12 in AC, and 12 hit points with resistance to all damage types, but immune to psychic damage. While active, it spends 10 minutes turning all available air in it's facility into comfortable, clean and breathable air and will constantly renew this process and keep the area as comfortable as possible until destroyed or deactivated. Once it has been active for 24 hours, it cannot be used again until the next dawn. This item cannot be used in the middle of combat to filter poisonous air, such as by a Green Dragon's Breath weapon, but can clean extra dimensional spaces such as a bag of holding.

Arcane Guardian

# Arcane Guardian

## Description

*A sorcerer of selfless nature and protective pursuit, your magic manifests itself through your desire to support others, bringing you safety and security in the process. Imbuing allies with the same magic that suffuses themselves, these sorcerers augment their strengths, covering their weaknesses, and upon reaching mastery, become catalyst for the inevitable success of everyone under their influence.*

*Often called Arcane Guardians these sorcerers take a backseat to the events of life, letting others shine while they lift them higher, watching and protecting them. Many a hero was made by the hands of an Arcane Guardian.*

Features:

*   [1st Level - Guardian Magic](#1st_Level_-_Guardian_Magic)
*   [1st Level - Loving Protection](#1st_Level_-_Loving_Protection)
*   [6th Level - Gifting Inner Strength](#6th_Level_-_Gifting_Inner_Strength)
*   [14th Level - Altruistic Splinter](#14th_Level_-_Altruistic_Splinter)
*   [18th Level - Arcane Enlightenment](#18th_Level_-_Arcane_Enlightenment)

## 1st Level - Guardian Magic

You learn additional spells when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown on the Guardian table. Each of these spells counts as a sorcerer spell for you, but it doesn't count against the number of sorcerer spells you know.

Whenever you gain a sorcerer level, you can replace one spell you gained from this feature with another spell of the same level. The new spell must be an abjuration or a transmutation spell from the sorcerer, warlock, or wizard spell list.

### Arcane Guardian Spells

*   1st: bless, protection from evil and good
*   3rd: aid, pass without trace
*   5th: aura of vitality, protection from energy
*   7th: aura of life, aura of purity
*   9th: mass cure wounds, skill empowerment

## 1st Level - Loving Protection

Starting at 1st level, your selfless nature begins to show in your spellcasting. Whenever an ally within 30 feet of you fits the casting requirements for a spell with a casting time of 1 Reaction, you can spend an amount of sorcery points equal to the spell level to cast the spell and give the effects to them. You can do this an amount of times equal to your Charisma modifier, resetting when you finish a long rest.

## 6th Level - Gifting Inner Strength

Starting at 6th level, you harness the ability to spread your talents to your allies. Once per turn, as a bonus action, you can strengthen an ally's next spell or attack with a Metamagic option you have access to, spending sorcery points as you would to use the options normally. These options last for a minute. The viable Metamagic options are as listed:

### Quickened Assault

When your target casts a spell that has a casting time of 1 action, they can choose to change the casting time to 1 bonus action for this casting.

Alternatively, they can make one melee or ranged weapon attack as a bonus action.

### Subtle Assault

When the target casts a spell, they can choose to cast it without any somatic or verbal components.

Alternatively, they can give themself advantage on one melee or ranged weapon attack roll.

### Empowered Assault

When the target rolls damage for a spell or weapon attack, they can reroll a number of the damage dice up to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one). They must use the new rolls.

### Heightened Assault

When the target casts a spell that forces a creature to make a saving throw to resist its effects, they can give one target of the spell disadvantage on its first saving throw made against the spell.

Alternatively, if they make a melee or ranged weapon attack and hit, they can add your Charisma modifier to one of the damage rolls (minimum of one).

## 14th Level - Altruistic Splinter

Starting at 14th level, you learn to manipulate the bounds of your sorcery. Whenever you cast a spell that has a range of Self, you can spend an amount of sorcery points equal to twice the spell's level to change the range to Touch as long as the target is a willing creature. The spell level limit of this feature is half your Charisma modifier.  You can do this an amount of times equal to half your proficiency bonus, resetting when you finish a long rest.

## 18th Level - Arcane Enlightenment

Starting at 18th level, you gain the ability to funnel your strength into your allies. As an action on your turn, you can boost your allies' power until the end of your next turn. Every creature of your choice that is currently under the effects of a concentration spell cast by you receives the following additional effects:

*   Their speed is increased by 30 feet.
*   Every melee and ranged weapon attack roll they make is made with advantage. This advantage ignores any disadvantage also being applied to the target.
*   Any attacks the target makes that hit receive a damage bonus equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one).
*   The target gains a bonus to AC equal to half your Charisma modifier (minimum of one).

This feature can be used a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, resetting on a long rest. Simulacrums are unable to use this feature.

Arcane Tent

# Arcane Tent

*Wondrous Item, rare*

**Description**

This wondrous item takes the form of a normal-sized two-person tent. Setting up the tent takes ten minutes of effort. What appears to be an ordinary tent, yurt, or otherwise culturally appropriate transient dwelling from the outside is revealed to be significantly larger on the inside. Upon stepping through the entrance flap the individual finds themselves in a larger than expected space - containing 240 sqft of floor room and a 15ft high ceiling, with a small kitchen, separated outhouse, and a central hearth and sitting area. There are enough beds and accommodations for 4-6 people to sleep comfortably, even if more may fit physically. Rain will not enter through the roof, but the tent is not waterproof and will flood if submerged. Air also enters the tent from outside, leaving those within vulnerable to suffocation or otherwise if something should happen to that air.

If the tent is punctured or damaged to a significant degree (AC 11, HP 15, resistant to all damage except fire and acid), the magic will cease to function until repaired with the mending spell. All creatures and objects inside will be dumped into the space of the two-person tent, possibly breaking it further, and until repaired it will function like the tent it appears to be. Additionally, the tent has a number of furnishings and furniture which are bound to it; these will remain inside at all times, and cannot be removed. If destroyed, the furniture will reappear the next time the tent is set up. Any objects left inside will be dumped out after the tent is dismantled.

Arcanist'S Magnificent Magnifier

# Arcanist's Magnificent Magnifier

[![Arcanist's Magnificent Magnifier](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/6/67/Arcanist%27s_Magnificent_Magnifier.png/revision/latest/scale-to-width-down/180?cb=20241009140316)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/6/67/Arcanist%27s_Magnificent_Magnifier.png/revision/latest?cb=20241009140316)

[](/docs/file:arcanist%27s-magnificent-magnifier.png)

Arcanist's Magnificent Magnifier

*Wondrous Item, rare*

**Description**

This magical magnifying glass helps deduce the arcane nature of items. While looking through the glass at an object within 1 foot of you, your proficiency bonus is doubled for any Intelligence (Arcana) or Intelligence (Investigation) check you make regarding that object’s magical properties if your proficiency bonus is not doubled already. In addition, you can speak the glass’s command word as an action to cast the "identify" spell from it, targeting an object with the spell that you can see through the glass within 1 foot of you. Each time you do, there is a cumulative 5 percent chance that the glass shatters and is destroyed after casting the spell.

Archivist'S Tome

# Archivist's Tome

*Wondrous Item, uncommon*

**Description**

The pages in this tome are crafted so they never run out. When the user fills up the pages within the tome, the book automatically creates new, blank pages. The older pages are automatically erased from the pages and stored, and the contents will reappear on command.

Armament Tattoo

# Armament Tattoo

*Wondrous Item (tattoo), varies*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

Produced by a special needle, this magic tattoo depicts an intricately designed weapon.

**Tattoo Attunement.** To attune to this item, you hold the needle to your skin where you want the tattoo to appear, pressing the needle there throughout the attunement process. When the attunement is complete, the needle turns into the ink that becomes the tattoo, which appears on the skin.

If your attunement to the tattoo ends, the tattoo vanishes, and the needle reappears in your space.

**Proficiency.** While attuned to this tattoo, you have proficiency in the weapon type associated with the tattoo.

**Transformation**. As a Bonus Action, you can transform this tattoo into the depicted weapon or change the weapon back into a tattoo. You must be holding the weapon to transform it back into a tattoo. The weapon form of this tattoo gains a bonus to attack and damage rolls depending on the tattoo's rarity, as shown below.

RARITY |  BONUS

*   Uncommon  | +1
*   Rare      | +2
*   Very Rare | +3

Arrow'S Reach Talisman

# Arrow's Reach Talisman

*Wondrous item, uncommon*

***Attunement***

*Requires Attunement*

**Description**

While attuned to this talisman, your ranged weapon attacks no longer suffer disadvantage at long range. This is not limited to bows, but also affects crossbows, spears, javelins, daggers, etc.

Artificer'S Blueprint

# Artificer's Blueprint

*Wondrous Item, uncommon*

**Description**

This item appears as a rolled-up scroll of blue parchment paper with white gridlines at regular intervals. When unfurled, a creature holding it may use an action and envision a creature, landscape, or anything they can think of the blueprint projects a three-dimensional illusion of the thing envisioned as a rough sketch of lines, tracing the contours and edges of the item. The illusion can be freely tampered with through intuitive hand gestures, such as enlarging or reducing, rotating and turning. Changing the illusion itself requires a bonus action. The illusion lasts for 10 minutes starting from the time it was left inactive or deactivated with a bonus action. A creature has advantage on the check (if needed) when creating or constructing something according to the blueprint (DM's discretion).

The extent of the detail the illusion has is limited, it appears as white lines, and grants the observer no knowledge they otherwise wouldn't have, such as the inner workings of a specific lock they have not disassembled.

Artificer Subclasses

# Artificer Subclasses

*   [Augmenter](/docs/augmenter "augmenter")

*   [Greaser](/docs/greaser "greaser")

*   [Physicist](/docs/physicist "physicist")

*   [Scrapper](/docs/scrapper "scrapper")

Artificer: Artillirist Additions

# Artificer: Artillirist Additions

Optional Additions

*   They are now granted proficiency with firearms if they do not already have it.
*   *Eldritch Cannons*, when tiny, can now be integrated into a firearm. They now move with you if integrated, but otherwise function RAW.
*   The *Arcane Firearm* feature can now be used on firearms as well, instead of just a wand, staff, or rod.

Artificer: Spell Storing Item

# Artificer: Spell-Storing Item

Optional (Replaces Current Spell-Storing Item feature)

At 11th level, you learn how to store a spell in an object. Whenever you finish a long rest, you can touch one simple or martial weapon or one item that you can use as a spellcasting focus, and you store a spell in it, choosing a 1st- or 2nd-level spell from the artificer spell list that requires 1 action to cast (you needn't have it prepared).You may upcast a 1st level spell to use a 2nd level slot, if previous conditions are applicable.

While holding the object, a creature can take an action to produce the spell's effect from it, using your spellcasting ability modifier. If the spell requires concentration, the creature must concentrate. The spell stays in the object until it's been used a number of times equal to twice your Intelligence modifier (minimum of twice) or until you use this feature again to store a spell in an object.

Audioshard

# Audioshard

*Wondrous Common*

**Description**

The Audioshard is the audio version of the Spellshard. As an action, the user can activate the shard which allows it to store sound up to a max of 10 ft away, determined whenever the user activates the shard. The shard can store up to 40 hours of audio.

The user can also take an action to play any recorded audio which can be played at a volume ranging from a whisper to a scream.

\*Artisans Guild invention

Augmenter

# Augmenter

## Description

The Augmenter is a unique blend of artificer and psychic, merging technology with mental prowess. These specialists understand the psyche and wield their own bodies as tools for both combat and manipulation. Whether augmenting their limbs or delving into the minds of others, Augmenters are masters of self-enhancement

**Features:**

*   [3rd Level - Augmenter Spells](#3rd_Level_-_Augmenter_Spells)
*   [3rd Level - Self Augmentation](#3rd_Level_-_Self_Augmentation)
*   [5th Level - Extra Attack](#5th_Level_-_Extra_Attack)
*   [9th Level - Mind Over Matter](#9th_Level_-_Mind_Over_Matter)
*   [15th Level - Master Augmenter](#15th_Level_-_Master_Augmenter)
*   [Augmenter Infusions](#Augmenter_Infusions)

## 3rd Level - Augmenter Spells

You always have certain spells prepared after you reach particular levels in this class, as shown in the Augmenter Spells table. These spells count as artificer spells for you, but they don’t count against the number of artificer spells you prepare.

Augmenter Spells reflect your understanding of the psyche and allows you to influence the mind of others.

**Augmenter Spells**

3rd Level - Command, Dissonant Whispers

5th Level - Tasha’s Mind Whip, Crown of Madness

9th Level - Antagonize, Fast Friends

13th Level - Raulothim’s Psychic Lance, Compulsion

17th Level - Synaptic Static, Modify Memory

## 3rd Level - Self Augmentation

Starting at 3rd level, you master the technique of augmenting your own body. Each of the following parts of your body can be infused using your artificer Infusions; Torso, Head, Legs. You also gain access to Augmenter Infusions, these infusions are known and does not count toward your total of known infusions. For the purposes of normal Infusions, the Torso is seen as Armor, head as a Helmet or any peripheral that is worn on the head, and legs are seen as boots.

You also learn how to augment your arms with special arm weapons. These can also be infused, and you can only have one arm enhancement active at a time. Whenever you finish a long rest you can swap arm enhancements.

### **Arm Enhancements**

These Enhancements count as weapons and infusions can be applied to them. Each enhancement grants the artificer an applicable fighting Style based on the installed enhancement.

#### **Mantis Blade**

You gain retractable blade attachments on either of your arms

**Properties**: Finesse

**Fighting Style:** Dueling

**Damage**: 1d6 piercing

**Special**: The blade can be retracted or unsheathed as a bonus action, leaving the hand free to wield other items. When you score a critical hit with the mantis blade the target needs to make a constitution save against your Spell DC or start to bleed. When bleeding the target loses hp equal to your proficiency bonus at the start of each of its turn. It can make the save again at the end of each turn.

#### **Monowire Attachment**

These fine wires get pulled from your wrists and are wielded like a whip.

**Properties**: Finesse, Reach

**Fighting Style**: Two-Weapon Fighting

**Damage**: 1d4 slashing

**Special**: The wires count as two separate weapons for the purpose of two weapon fighting, but only require a single infusion. The wires use Intelligence for its attack and damage modifiers.

#### **Gorilla Arms**

Your Hands and forearms are augmented with heavier metals and mechanical support.

**Properties**: Heavy

**Fighting Style**: Unarmed Fighting

**Damage**: Unarmed Strike, Bludgeoning

**Special**: You make your attacks with the Gorilla Arms as unarmed strikes. You also have advantage on Strength( Athletics) checks to escape, attempt, maintain grapples.

#### **Arm Launcher**

You can load bolts or sling bullets into your arm and fire them as projectiles.

**Properties**: Ranged (40/80), Reload (6)

**Fighting Style**: Archery

**Damage**: 1d8 piercing (bolts), bludgeoning (sling bullets)

**Special**: The Launcher leaves your hands open while firing. On a hit, the launcher does 1d4 force damage to the target, all creatures within 5ft of the target must make Dexterity save against your spell save DC or take 1d4 force damage.

## 5th Level - Extra Attack

Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

## 9th Level - Mind Over Matter

At 9th level, your deep understanding of the mind allows you to manipulate the physical world with your thoughts. You gain the following abilities:

*   Mental Shield: You gain resistance to psychic damage. Additionally, when you take psychic damage, you can use your reaction to reflect half of that damage back to the attacker.
*   Psychic Insight: You can use your action to read the surface thoughts of a creature you can see within 30 feet. This effect lasts for 1 minute, and the target must make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, you can also implant a simple suggestion (as per the Suggestion spell) into their mind. This ability can be used once per short rest.

## 15th Level - Master Augmenter

At 15th Level your ability to enhance your augmentations has reached unparalleled heights:

*   You can now add 2 infusions to any of your augmentations.
*   You are able to use master properties for your arm augmentations. Each arm augmentation has two possible properties. When you switch out your arm augmentation you can choose which master property to use.
    *   Mantis Blades: Graze, Sap
    *   Monowire: Nick, Slow
    *   Gorilla Arms: Sap, Push
    *   Arm Launcher: Push, Vex

## Augmenter Infusions

### **Subdermal armor - 3rd Level, Torso**

**Prerequisite**: 3rd Level Augmenter, Unarmored

**Item**: Torso Augmentation (requires Attunement)

**Effect**: Your skin is reinforced with a layer of protective material, making you more resilient to physical attacks. Subdermal Armor makes the wearer’s AC equal 10 + Constitution Modifier + Intelligence Modifier

### **Chameleon Skin - 3rd Level, Torso**

**Prerequisite**: 3rd Level Augmenter

**Item**: Torso Augmentation (requires Attunement)

**Effect**: You can use an action to blend into your surroundings, gaining advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks for 1 hour. This ability can be used once per short or long rest.

### **Instant Disguise - 3rd Level, Torso**

**Prerequisite**: 3rd Level Augmenter

**Item**: Torso Augmentation (requires Attunement)

**Effect**: A sophisticated system of micro-projectors and sensors enables you to alter your appearance at will. As a bonus action you can cast the Disguise self spell on yourself, without spending a spell slot. This ability can be used a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once) per long rest.

### **Enhanced Sight - 3rd Level, Head**

**Prerequisite**: 3rd Level Augmenter

**Item**: Head Augmentation

**Effect**: You gain proficiency in the perception skill, and can use Intelligence instead of Wisdom for Passive perception and perception checks

### **Cat Eyes - 3rd Level, Head**

**Prerequisite**: 3rd Level Augmenter

**Item**: Head Augmentation

**Effect**: You gain dark vision out to 60 feet, if you already have dark vision from another source, its range is extended by 60 feet.

### **Alluring Visage - 3rd Level, Head**

**Prerequisite**: 3rd Level Augmenter

**Item**: Head Augmentation

**Effect:** The infusion highlights your positive features and filters your words, making you sound and look more alluring than you are. You can add your Intelligence modifier to any charisma(persuasion) check you make against a single target.

### **Fortified Ankles - 3rd Level, Leg**

**Prerequisite**: 3rd Level Augmenter

**Item**: Leg Augmentation

**Effect**: You gain a +10 feet to your walking speed, and your high and long jump distances are doubled.

### **Lynx Paws - 3rd Level, Leg**

**Prerequisite**: 3rd Level Augmenter

**Item**: Leg Augmentation

**Effect**: Your footfalls are dampened allowing for silent movement. You gain advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks. Damage taken from falling is halved.

### **Swift Step - 3rd Level, Leg**

**Prerequisite**: 3rd Level Augmenter

**Item**: Leg Augmentation.

**Effect**: You can take the dodge action as a bonus action. This ability can be used a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once) per long rest.

### **Blood Pump - 6th Level, Special**

**Prerequisite**: 6th Level Augmenter

**Item**: Special Augmentation (requires attunement)

**Effect**: After finishing a short rest you can load any magical potion into the pump. You can then as a reaction to taking damage, prior to taking damage or making an attack inject the potion to activate its effect. Once activated the potion is consumed.

### **Arcane Shielding - 6th Level, Special**

**Prerequisite**: 6th Level Augmenter

**Item**: Special Augmentation (requires attunement)

**Effect**: When you are hit by a melee attack, you can use your reaction to cast Shield without expending a spell slot. This ability can be used a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once) per long rest.

### **Healing Nanobots - 6th Level, Special**

**Prerequisite**: 6th Level Augmenter

**Item**: Special Augmentation (requires attunement)

**Effect**: As a bonus action, you can release nanobots that heal a creature you touch for 2d8 + your Intelligence modifier hit points. This ability can be used a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once) per long rest.

### **Psychic Blades - 10th Level, Arm**

**Prerequisite**: 10th Level Augmenter

**Item**: Mantis Blade

**Effect**: When you hit a creature with the Mantis Blades, you can choose to deal an additional 2d6 psychic damage. This ability can be used a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once) per long rest.

### **Entangling Wires - 10th Level, Arm**

**Prerequisite**: 10th Level Augmenter

**Item**: Monowire Attachment

**Effect**: When you hit a creature with the Monowire Attachment, you can choose to restrain the target. The target must make a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC or be restrained until the end of your next turn. This ability can be used a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once) per long rest.

### **Impact Fists - 10th Level, Arm**

**Prerequisite**: 10th Level Augmenter

**Item**: Gorilla Arms

**Effect**: When you hit a creature with an unarmed strike, you can choose to knock the target prone. The target must make a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC or be knocked prone. This ability can be used a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once) per long rest.

### **Targeting Reticle - 10th Level, Arm**

**Prerequisite**: 10th Level Augmenter

**Item**: Arm Launcher

**Effect**: You gain a +4 bonus to attack rolls with the Arm Launcher. Additionally, you can use your reaction to impose disadvantage on a ranged attack made against you.

### **Optical Camo - 14th Level, Torso**

**Prerequisite**: 14th Level Augmenter

**Item**: Torso Augmentation (requires attunement)

**Effect**: Advanced optical technology allows you to blend into your surroundings, becoming nearly invisible. As a bonus action you become invisible until the end of your next turn, or until you take damage from any source. This ability can be used a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once) per long rest.

### **Reactive Coil - 14th Level, Head**

**Prerequisite**: 14th Level Augmenter

**Item**: Head Augmentation (requires attunement)

**Effect**: When you are hit by a melee attack, you can use your reaction to deal 1d6 lightning damage to the attacker. This damage increases to 2d6 if the attacker is wearing metal armor.

### **Hydraulic Jumps - 14th Level, Leg**

**Prerequisite**: 14th Level Augmenter

**Item**: Leg Augmentation (requires attunement)

**Effect**: You can use a bonus action to jump up to 30 feet in any direction. If you land within 5 feet of an enemy, you can make a melee attack as part of the same bonus action.

Aura Of Kineses

# Aura of Kineses

*2nd-level abjuration. Artificer, Cleric, Paladin*

**Meta**

**Casting Time:** 1 Bonus Action

**Range:** Self (10ft)

**Components:** V, S, M (a small magnet)

**Duration**: 10 minutes

**Description**:

You grant yourself and allies within range the ability to transfer the kinetic energy of attacks. Until the spell ends you can use an action to gravitate all attacks against allies within range until the end of your next turn. All allies within range can use their reaction to redirect any attack made against them, or an ally within 5ft of them, to you. Melee attacks will still be able to be made regardless of the effective range of the attack. Attacks made at long range because of this effect aren’t at disadvantage.

**At Higher Levels:** When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the range increases by 5ft for every 2 slot levels above 2nd.

Avariel

# Avariel

## Ability Score Increase

Your Dexterity score increases by 2.

Your Wisdom score increases by 1.

## Age

Although elves reach physical maturity at about the same age as humans, the elven understanding of adulthood goes beyond physical growth to encompass worldly experience. An elf typically claims adulthood and an adult name around the age of 100 and can live to be 750 years old.

## Size

Avariel typically range from under four and a half to over five and a half feet tall and have slender builds with hollow bones. Your size is Medium.

## Speed

Your base walking speed is 30 feet.

## Flight

Thanks to your wings, you have a flying speed equal to your walking speed. You can’t use this flying speed if you’re wearing medium or heavy armor.

## Darkvision

Accustomed to twilit mountains and the night sky, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can’t discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

## Keen Senses

You have proficiency in the Perception skill.

## Fey Ancestry

You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed, and magic can’t put you to sleep.

## Trance

Elves don’t need to sleep. Instead, they meditate deeply, remaining semiconscious, for 4 hours a day. (The Common word for such meditation is “trance.”) While meditating, you can dream after a fashion; such dreams are actually mental exercises that have become reflexive through years of practice. After resting in this way, you gain the same benefit that a human does from 8 hours of sleep.

## Languages

You can speak, read, and write Elvish and one other language of your choice, typically Common, also known as Thorasta. Avariel typically speak the Skaevrym variant of Thorasta which is derived from North Eeastern Faerun near Sossel, the region nearest their settlement atop Mount Sundabar. Elvish is fluid, with subtle intonations and intricate grammar, which some Avariel speak with a more relaxed and breathy intonation akin to Auran, and the language is sometimes mixed with their own unique form of sign language based around aerial movement. Involving things such as flaps, stalls, and wind dips. It was a less subtle sign language than those of non-flying races, having been developed primarily for communication during flight.

Avasculate

# Avasculate

*8th-level necromancy. (Wizard, Sorcerer, Warlock)*

**Meta**

**Casting Time**: 1 action

**Range**: 30 ft

**Components**: V, S

**Duration**: Instantaneous

**Description**

You shoot a black ray of entropic energy from your outstretched hand, on a hit, your foe violently expell blood or similar vital fluids from their skin.

Make a ranged spell attack to strike a target within range. On a hit, the target must make a successful Constitution Saving Throw vs your Spell Save DC, or take Necrotic damage equal to half its total hit points (rounded down), and be stunned until the end of its next turn.

Avenger

# Avenger

Avengers seek to right a wrong, stopping at nothing to correct a past sleight. They dole out their revenge with their own hands, using steel just as much as spirit to forward their goals.

## Soul of Vengeance

At 3rd level, you gain proficiency with medium armor, shields, and martial weapons, and you can use weapons as your spiritual focus. When you activate your spirit tether, you gain an amount of temporary hit points equal to 1d6 + half your spiritualist level (rounded down).

## Avenger Arts

Also at 3rd level, you gain access to new spirit arts that reflect your soul’s hunger for vengeance.

## Duel of the Fates

As an action, you can spend 1 spirit point to initiate a spirit duel with your target. The target must make a Charisma saving throw or be blinded. While the target is blinded this way, you are also blinded, except you and your target can still see each other normally. As long as this blindness persists, your weapon attacks deal an additional 1d6 damage.

### Spirit Shield

When you are attacked by your tethered target while you are holding a shield, you can use your reaction to spend 1 spirit point and force the target to make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, the attack misses, and the next attack you make against the tethered target has advantage.

### Phantom Pain

As an action, you can spend 2 spirit points to share your suffering with the tethered target. For the next minute, whenever you take damage, the target takes half as much necrotic damage.

## Extra Attack

Beginning at 6th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn. Moreover, you can use a spirit art that uses an action in place of one of these attacks.

## Avenging Link

At 13th level, your soul lashes out at any creature that would harm you or your allies. Whenever a creature you can see within range of your spirit tether damages you or an ally within 30 feet, you can use your reaction to immediately create a spirit tether. The creature must be one of the targets.

## Master Avenger

At 18th level, you master the art of vengeance. You learn the following spirit art:

### Soul Coating

You can use 5 spirit points as an action to empower your weapon. For the next minute, whenever you hit a tethered target with a weapon attack, the attack deals an additional 3d6 force damage. The first time you hit a tethered target after using this art, it must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, the target is rended by your spirit, and cannot restore hit points or gain temporary hit points as long as your spirit tether lasts.

Awaken Undead

# Awaken Undead

*9th-level necromancy. (Wizard, Sorcerer, Warlock)*

**Meta**

**Casting Time**: 1 action

**Range**: Self (60 ft. sphere)

**Components**: V, S, M ((A ritual knife with an obsidian blade worth at least 25,000 gp infused with the essence of a powerful undead to be quested for and a humanoid brain embedded with onyx worth at least 10,000 gp, which the spell consumes))

**Duration**: Instantaneous

**Description**

You strike the onyx embedded in the blade, sending a wave of intelligence into nearby undead.

Up to 6d6 undead under your control within 60ft of you gain 2d4 intelligence, to a max of 18. Such undead gain the armor and weapon proficiencies of the caster. In addition, they gain proficiency in a single skill, and learn one language, to represent the skills they had in life. This effect last until it is dispelled or you lose control of the undead. You may dispel this effect on a single undead under your control as an action.

Casting this spell sacrifices your own life force, a fragment of mind spreading through the undead. The strain on a construct such as a simulacrum kills it instantly, and the spell fails to take effect. You gain 1d4 levels of exhaustion which must be removed naturally (no magic). If you die from this exhaustion, you lose the ability to cast this spell.

Backbiter

# Backbiter

*2nd-level Necromancy (Wizard, Sorcerer, Warlock)*

**Casting Time:** 1 action

**Range:** 30ft

**Components:** V, S, M (A dagger)

**Duration:** 1 hour

The weapon you indicate during the spell's casting briefly shimmers with a black aura that disappears in an eyeblink.

When you cast this spell, choose one melee weapon within range. The next time a creature uses this weapon to make a melee attack, they have advantage on their attack roll and then the weapon turns on its wielder, provided the spell hasn't ended. The next attack afterward that hits a creature deals damage to the weapon's wielder instead of their target, in addition to the weapon's typical effects. This damage cannot be made non-lethal.

As an action, a creature can attempt an investigation check against your spell save DC when closely analyzing the weapon to find that it was tampered with. As an action, a creature can make an arcana check against your spell save DC to disarm the weapon and end this spell.

Once the weapon attacks its wielder, regardless of whether the attack hits or misses, the spell ends.

**At Higher Levels:**

When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd or 4th level, this spell lasts up to 8 hours. When you use a spell slot of 5th or 6th level, this spell lasts up to 24 hours. When you use a spell slot of 7th level or higher, this spell lasts until dispelled.

Bag Of Colding

# Bag of Colding

*Wondrous Item, uncommon*

**Description**

This item resembles a bag of holding, but is cool to the touch and emits visibly cold air while opened. It can only hold 100 pounds of items, but any perishable items do not degrade while within the bag. It is otherwise identical to a bag of holding.

Balance

# Balance

### Game Statistics and Description

**Balance**

*Weapon (greatsword), legendary*

**Attunement:** Requires Attunement

**Description**

**Magic Weapon.** You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

**Antithesis.** Attacks with this weapon deal extra damage of a predetermined type depending on the user's alignment. If the user is of evil alignment, Balance deals an extra 2d6 radiant damage. If the user is of good alignment, Balance deals an extra 2d6 necrotic damage. If the target is of neither good nor evil alignment, Balance deals an extra 1d6 radiant and 1d6 necrotic damage.

**Unwavering Neutrality.** You may use an action to cast the *Protection from Evil and Good* spell on yourself at will, requiring no material components or concentration.

**Unity.** While this weapon is visible, you have advantage on Charisma (Persuasion) and Charisma (Intimidation) checks made to interact with creatures that can see it.

Bandolier Of Scroll Storing

# Bandolier of Scroll Storing

*Wondrous Item, rare*

**Description**

This bandolier comes equipped with a single scroll case, inside of which up to 100 levels worth of scrolls can be stored. The wearer of this bandolier can produce a scroll from this case as part of the action used to cast the spell.

Bard In A Box

# Bard-in-a-Box

[![Bard-in-a-Box](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/4/47/Barker%27s_Bugle.png/revision/latest/scale-to-width-down/180?cb=20241009135606)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/4/47/Barker%27s_Bugle.png/revision/latest?cb=20241009135606)

[](/docs/file:barker%27s-bugle.png)

Bard-in-a-Box

*Wondrous Item, common*

**Description**

This small music box can play songs at the request of the holder. As a bonus action, you can command the box to play a song that it knows, causing the contraption to emit a slightly tinny rendition of the musical number at a moderate volume. The box can know up to five songs at a time, and can not recreate lyrics or other creature sounds. It has a small knob on the side that adjusts the box's volume, being as quiet as a whisper to as loud as a scream. Sheet music can be fed into the box through a narrow slot along the bottom of the device. Sheet music must be labeled with a song title, page number, and instrument in order to be replayed. Pages failing to meet the requirements causes the box to make a sad, trumpeting noise, and are spat back out. Once a sheet is successfully fed into the box, it is consumed and cannot be retrieved. If the new piece of music will cause the box to forget an older one, it quickly plays several bars of the now-forgotten number before slowing to a stop.

Bard: Counter Charm

# Bard: Counter Charm

Optional Class Feature (Replaces Counter Charm)

At 6th level, you gain the ability to use musical notes or words of power to disrupt mind-influencing effects. As a reaction when you or a creature within 30 feet of you fails a saving throw to prevent or remove the charm or frightened condition, you can have the creature reroll the saving throw with advantage. A creature must be able to hear you to gain this benefit.

Bard: Everlasting Inspiration

# Bard: Everlasting Inspiration

Optional Class Feature (Replaces Superior Inspiration)

At 20th level your inspiration becomes more than a just a short lived burst. When a creature uses your bardic inspiration, the effect is not expended, allowing for the creature to use it multiple times. Your bardic inspiration now only ends once used a number of times equal to your charisma modifier, or once ten minutes pass.

Bard: Magical Secrets

# Bard: Magical Secrets

Optional (Replaces standard Magical Secrets)

By 10th level, you have plundered magical knowledge from a wide spectrum of disciplines. Choose two spells from any classes, including this one. A spell you choose must be of a level you can cast, as shown on the Bard table, or a cantrip.

The chosen spells count as bard spells for you and are not included as part of the Spells Known column of the Bard table.

You learn two additional spells from any classes at 14th level and again at 18th level.

Barkers Bugle

# Barkers Bugle

*Wondrous Item, Uncommon*

*Requires use by a creature with any musical instrument proficiency*

**Description**

This bugle is magical, lined on it's edges with a faint swirling pattern which flares at the horn end. The unique grooves of the instrument allow it to reverberate sound more effectively. Condensing it and emerging as volumes louder than originally played from one end.

This item comes with an inter-changeable mouthpiece, which is much wider and allows speech through the piping.

Sounds made through this instrument are audible out to a distance of 300 feet. As an action once per day, you may choose to blare this instrument. Doing so requires creatures in a 30-foot cone originating from you to make a DC 13 constitution saving throw unless they are covering their ears. On a failure, they take 1d10 thunder damage are deafened for one minute. Objects in this area take 3d10 thunder damage. They may repeat this saving throw again at the end of their turns, ending the effect on a success.

Beacon

# Beacon

*4th-level divination. (Artificer, Bard, Paladin, Ranger, Warlock, Wizard)*

**Meta**

**Casting Time**: 1 action

**Range**: 30 feet

**Components**: V, S, M (a piece of glas)

**Duration**: 24 hours

**Description**

When you cast this spell, target either a creature, object, or location within range. When you do so, choose up to 10 creatures that you are familiar with. If a creature you choose is on the same plane as you, they become aware of the target, and know the most direct path.

While within 1 mile of the target object, the creatures you chose can see a pinpoint of light above the target. This light can be seen through solid objects, except a thin sheet of lead.

When you cast the spell, you may also send a message of 25 words to each of the creatures you choose to be aware of the target. They all receive the same message.

Belt Of Returning

# Belt of Returning

*Wondrous Item, rare*

**Description**

A finely crafted dark leather belt with two loops, each decorated with a shard of sapphire. The magic of the belt leaves an invisible mark on the surface of the weapons stowed in each loop and invisibly inscribes the name of the item on the sapphire shard.

Two one-handed weapons with the thrown property can be stowed in the belt. When you make ranged attack with a weapon marked by the belt, immediately after the attack, hit or miss, the weapon returns to the belt.

Black Cleaver

# Black Cleaver

[![Black Cleaver](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/f/f2/Black_Cleaver.png/revision/latest/scale-to-width-down/180?cb=20241009135229)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/f/f2/Black_Cleaver.png/revision/latest?cb=20241009135229)

[](/docs/file:black-cleaver.png)

Black Cleaver

*Weapon (battleaxe), very rare*

**Attunement:** Requires Attunement

**Description**

You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

After you have made a successful weapon attack with this weapon, you gain an additional 10 feet movement speed which lasts until the end of that turn.

Additionally, you can also lower the defensive prowess of the one you are attacking. The battleaxe has 5 charges, and it regains all expended charges daily at dawn. When you attack with this weapon, you can use your reaction to expend a charge to lower the target's AC by 2, includes against the triggering attack, until the start of its next turn. You can only use feature once per turn.

Bladed Cestus

# Bladed Cestus

*Weapon: Simple, Melee* 1gp, 2 lbs. --- 1d4 slashing - light, two-handed

**Description**

Proficiency with a cestus allows you to add your proficiency bonus to the attack roll for any attack you make with it.

Special: As a bonus action, you can make one attack with this weapon.

Blessing Of Arvandor

# Blessing of Arvandor

Once every 7 days you can summon 1d4 +2 Spectral Warriors to fight by your side for 1 hour.

These warriors use the same statblock as the Bandit captain, but can be flavored as any relevant type of ally.

Blessing Of Health (Rework)

# Blessing of Health (Rework)

Your constitution score increases to 19. If it is already 19 or above, it instead increases the score by 2, to a maximum of 30.

Blessing Of Knowledge

# Blessing of Knowledge

The time it takes you to gain tool and language proficiencies is reduced by 15%.

Blessing Of Moradin

# Blessing of Moradin

Your crafting tools are enchanted by the gods. 15% reduction to your crafting time.

Blessing Of The Masked Lord (Soulbind)

# Blessing of the Masked Lord (Soulbind)

***Prerequisite:** Blessing of the Masked Lord (Soultheft)*

*The practice of Soultheft has become second nature to you, allowing you to better subjugate the souls you capture and extract even greater potential.*

When you use Soultheft, and you can exploit a soul up to 12 times before the soul is released instead of 6. In addition you gain two new ways in which you may exploit souls:

*   Ethereal Stride (2 uses). As a Bonus Action you can teleport to the Ethereal Plane from the Material Plane for up to 1 minute or until you end the effect (no action required). You can expend another use of this exploit to extend the duration by up to 1 minute per use expended. At the end of the duration, you reappear in the same location on the Material Plane, or the nearest unoccupied space if that space is occupied.,

*   Soul Mask (3 uses). You can use an action to call the Soul to you and disguise your appearance. When you do so, your appearance magically transforms into the souls as it was in life, healthy and alive. Another creature can see through this disguise by succeeding on a Wisdom (Insight) check contested by your Charisma (Deception) check. You gain a +5 bonus to your check. This disguise lasts for 1 hour or until you end it as a bonus action. You can expend another use of this exploit to extend the duration by up to 1 hour per use expended.

Blessing Of The Masked Lord (Soultheft)

# Blessing of the Masked Lord (Soultheft)

*Guided by the Masked Lord himself, the caster can snare a departing soul to fuel their spells, a practice called Soultheft.*

*   Once per day as an action, the caster can touch their holy symbol to a creature that’s been dead no longer than 1 minute, and capture their departing soul into the holy symbol, placing them under the effects of the ‘Soul Cage’ spell until the soul is released, or this blessing is used again.
*   In addition to the spell's normal effects, the caster can also dispel the effects to cast a spell they know or have prepared without expending a spell slot, of a level equal to proficiency bonus of the captured creature. When casting a spell in this way, the creatures soul is damaged, and cannot be revived by any means short of a wish or divine intervention.

*Souls obtained via the Soultheft blessing MUST be canon. Hunts, Trials, Delves or Raids do not count, the usage must explicitly be approved by staff*

Blessing Of The Metallic Dragon

# Blessing of the Metallic Dragon

This blessing is bestowed by a powerful metallic dragon upon a creature it recognizes as a dragon friend—one who has proven loyalty, courage, or respect worthy of draconic favor.

**Draconic Recognition**

Other metallic dragons instinctively recognize the bearer of this blessing as friendly, unless the bearer acts with obvious hostility. This recognition does not compel obedience or trust, but it greatly improves initial attitudes.

**Draconic Transformation**

You learn the Draconic Transformation spell. Once per long rest, you can cast a special version of the Draconic Transformation spell without expending a spell slot or material components. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell. While concentrating on the Special Draconic Transformation spell cast in this way, you gain the following additional benefits:

*   **Scaled Majesty**. You gain 60 Temporary Hit Point as scales harden around your skin.
*   **Draconic Potency.** Whenever you roll damage dice of the same type as the dragon that granted this blessing (such as fire for a gold dragon or lightning for a bronze dragon), any roll of 1 or 2 on a damage die is treated as a 3.
*   **Altered Breath**: You can change the damage type of the Draconic Transformation spell's breath to the type associate to the dragon that granted the blessing.

**Draconic Gift**

This blessing can only be granted directly by a dragon. It cannot be replicated, transferred, or bestowed through training, magic items, divine intervention, or the wish spell. OOC clarification, you cannot learn this blessing via wish or the free boon or blessing, it must be quested for.

Blessing Of The Moon Weaver

# Blessing of the Moon Weaver

*The blessings of a deity aligned to the moon protect and empower you. You gain resistance to radiant damage, and are empowered in the following ways at night:*

**Lunar Magic**

Your learn the Moonbeam (DC19) and the Healing Spirit spells, and they are always prepared for you. You can cast each of these once per Short Rest without expending a spell slot, empowering it. While empowered, these spells are centered on and follow you, and their radius is increased to 15ft instead of 5 ft. You are immune to the damage caused by your own Moonbeam cast in this way.

**Moon Fever**

As a reaction immediately after you take damage or fail a saving throw against a creature you can see within 60 feet of yourself, you can force the creature to make a Wisdom saving throw (DC19). On a failed save, the creature feels an intense urge to move towards or away from you (your choice). It must move as close or as far (depending on your choice) as its movement allows on its turn, taking the safest and most direct path available. You can use this feature a number of times equal half to your proficiency bonus. You regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

Blessing Of The Swift Hunter

# Blessing of the Swift Hunter

*The Cat Lord, god of tabaxi, sometimes confers the blessings of his people to those who prove to be great allies of his people.*

*   You are granted Feline Agility.
*   Your reflexes and agility allow you to move with a burst of speed. When you move on your tum in combat, you can double your speed until the end of the turn. Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you move 0 feet on one of your turns.
*   If you are a tabaxi and receive this boon, your walking speed increases by 15ft.

Blessing Of Understanding (Rework)

# Blessing of Understanding (Rework)

Your intelligence or wisdom score increases by 2, to a max of 30.

In addition, skill checks of the chosen ability score are rolled with advantage.

Blessing Of Valkur

# Blessing of Valkur

*Granted by the god of sailors, storms, and naval prowess, Valkur, this blessing imbues the recipient with the courage, resilience, and maritime skill befitting a legendary seafarer.*

*   **Storm's Resilience:** You have resistance to lightning and thunder damage
*   **Navigator’s Instinct**: You always know which direction is north, and you have advantage on Wisdom (Survival) checks made to navigate or predict weather conditions at sea.
*   **Mariner's Migh**t Your swim speed is equal to your walking speed, and you can hold your breath for double beyond your normal capacity.
*   **Blessing of the Sea:** You gain proficiency with navigator's tools and vehicles (water). If you already have these proficiencies, you add double your proficiency bonus to checks you make with them.

Blessing Of Weapon Enhancement

# Blessing of Weapon Enhancement

*   When wielding a ranged or melee weapon, you receive a +1 to attack and damage rolls with the affected weapon.
*   In addition, you can add a +5 bonus to attack rolls affected by the blessing a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, resetting at dawn. You can use this after you see the roll, but before you know the result.

Blightstaff

# Blightstaff

*Staff, rare*

[![Blightstaff](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/6/66/Blightstaff.png/revision/latest/scale-to-width-down/180?cb=20241011070525)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/6/66/Blightstaff.png/revision/latest?cb=20241011070525)

[](/docs/file:blightstaff.png)

Blightstaff

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement by a Druid

**Description**

This magic staff was created from a long, straight branch from a petrified tree. It has a small animal skull on the top with thorns to mimic antlers, and two dark gems in the eye sockets.

While holding this staff, you gain a +2 bonus to the saving throw DCs of your Druid spells.

When you create an area of difficult terrain, any creature moving through it takes 1d4 necrotic damage for every 5 feet it moves in that area.

Blood Hunter (Version X)

# Blood Hunter (Version X)

*This overhaul is mostly a buff to the base class as written by Matt Mercer. There were a number of features that went largely unused, were very situational, or generally underwhelming in practice. The main draw the class presents in this overhaul is that it leans more into the sacrificing of your hit points, using your health more as a typical mana pool for your abilities, and gives you more ways to spend your hit points to do so. Every other class resource the Blood Hunter had previously is gone.*

*This overhaul also features a true distinction between using intelligence and wisdom as your hemocraft modifier. Intelligence leads to a more monstrous take on your blood magic, granting you greater offensive ability as your hemocraft takes shape. Where wisdom takes a more defensive route, and later grants more control over your hemocraft.*

**The Blood Hunter Table**


LevelProficiency BonusClass FeaturesBlood Curses KnownHemocraft Die
1+2Hunter’s Bane, Blood Maledict21d6
2+2Fighting Style, Crimson Rite21d6
3+2Blood Hunter Order21d6
4+2Ability Score Improvement21d6
5+3Extra Attack21d8
6+3Brand of Castigation31d8
7+3Blood Hunter Order Feature31d8
8+3Ability Score Improvement31d8
9+4Grim Psychometry31d8
10+4Dark Augmentation31d8
11+4Blood Hunter Order Feature41d10
12+4Ability Score Improvement41d10
13+5Tethering Brand41d10
14+5Hardened Soul41d10
15+5Blood Hunter Order Feature41d10
16+5Ability Score Improvement51d10
17+6Blood Price51d12
18+6Blood Hunter Order Feature51d12
19+6Ability Score Improvement51d12
20+6Blood Hunter Order Feature51d12


## **Class Features**

As a Blood Hunter you gain the following class features.

**Hit Points**

**Hit Dice:** 1d12 per Blood Hunter Level

**Hit Points at 1st Level:** 12 + your constitution modifier

**Hit Points at Higher Levels:** 1d12 (or 7) + your constitution modifier per Blood Hunter level after 1st

**Proficiencies**

**Weapons:** Simple, Martial

**Armor:** Light, Medium, Shields

**Tools:** Alchemist’s Supplies

**Saving Throws:** Dexterity, Intelligence

**Skills:** Choose three from Acrobatics, Arcana, Athletics, History, Insight, Investigation, Religion, and Survival

### **Hunter’s Bane**

At 1st level, you have survived the Hunter’s Bane - a dangerous, long guarded ritual that alters your life’s blood. You have advantage on Wisdom (Survival) checks to track fey, fiends, and undead, as well as Intelligence checks to recall information about such creatures.

The Hunter’s Bane also empowers your body to control and shape hemocraft magic, using your own blood and life-essence to fuel your abilities. Choose either your intelligence or your wisdom score. Your hemocraft modifier is equal to the modifier of the chosen ability. The ability you choose also determines the effects of features you gain at later levels. When you reach a feature that has **\[Monstrous\]** or **\[Stable\]** options, you gain the **\[Monstrous\]** option if your hemocraft modifier uses your intelligence, or the **\[Stable\]** options if your hemocraft modifier uses your wisdom.

Some of your Blood Hunter features require you to sacrifice hit points in order to activate or sustain. When a feature asks you to sacrifice hit points, your hit points are lowered by that amount. This sacrifice cannot be reduced in any way. If the sacrifice would leave you with 0 hit points, you fall unconscious as normal, and the ability fails.

When any of your Blood Hunter features require a target to make a saving throw, the save DC is calculated with the following:

**Hemocraft save DC =** 8 + your proficiency bonus + your hemocraft modifier

### **Blood Maladict**

Also at 1st level, you gain the ability to sacrifice your vital essence to curse and manipulate creatures through hemocraft magic.

**Blood Curses Known:** You know two Blood Curses of your choice, and learn additional blood curses at later levels as shown by the “Blood Curses Known” column of the Blood Hunter Table.

**Invoking a Blood Curse:** To use a Blood Curse, you must roll your hemocraft die, sacrificing hit points equal to the number rolled.

Amplifying a Blood Curse requires you to roll three hemocraft dice instead of one.

You can Invoke or Amplify your known Blood Curses as many times as you like, so long as you pay the hit point cost each time.

### **Fighting Style**

At 2nd Level, you adopt a particular style of fighting as your specialty. Choose one of the following options.

You can’t take a Fighting Style option more than once, even if you later get to choose again.

**Archery:** You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls you make with ranged weapons.

**Blind Fighting:** You have blindsight with a range of 10 feet. Within that range, you can effectively see anything that isn’t behind total cover, even if you’re blinded or in darkness. Moreover, you can see an invisible creature within that range, unless the creature successfully hides from you.

**Defense:** While you are wearing armor, you gain a +1 bonus to AC.

**Dueling:** When you are wielding a melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons, you gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls with that weapon.

**Great Weapon Fighting:** When you roll a 1 or 2 on a damage die for an attack you make with a melee weapon that you are wielding with two hands, you can reroll the die and must use the new roll. The weapon must have the two-handed or versatile property for you to gain this benefit.

### **Crimson Rite**

Also at 2nd level, you learn to invoke a rite of hemocraft that infuses your weapon strikes with energy. As a bonus action, you can activate any rite you know, enhancing one weapon that you are holding. The rite remains in effect until you finish a short or long rest. When you activate a rite, you sacrifice hit points equal to one roll of your hemocraft die.

While a rite is in effect, attacks made with that weapon are considered magical for the purposes of overcoming resistance and immunity to non-magical damage, and deal extra damage equal to one roll of your hemocraft die of a damage type determined by the chosen rite.

When you attack a creature with a weapon affected by any of your Crimson Rites, and deal damage to them, you can choose to amplify the effect. Doing so causes you to sacrifice hit points equal to one roll of your hemocraft die.

A single weapon can only hold one rite at any given time, and other creatures cannot benefit from your rite.

Choose one of the following rites when you gain this feature. You learn one additional rite at 7th and 14th level:

**Rite of the Flame -** The extra damage dealt by your rite is fire damage.

When amplified, the target is burned, taking fire damage equal to your hemocraft modifier at the start of each of it’s turns. You may stack this effect on a single creature up to a number of times equal to half your proficiency bonus. The burning creature, or a creature within 5 feet of it can use an action to douse the flames.

**Rite of the Frozen -** The extra damage dealt by your rite is cold damage.

When amplified, the target’s move speed is reduced by 5 times your hemocraft modifier, to a minimum of 0 feet.

**Rite of the Storm -** The extra damage dealt by your rite is lightning damage.

When amplified, electricity arcs to other targets within 20 feet of your choice. You can target a number of creatures equal to your hemocraft modifier. Each extra target takes lightning damage equal to one roll of your hemocraft die.

**Rite of the Dead -** The extra damage dealt by your rite is necrotic damage.

When amplified, the target has its strength sapped until the end of its next turn. While sapped, it has disadvantage on Strength and Dexterity checks and saving throws.

**Rite of the Oracle -** The extra damage dealt by your rite is psychic damage.

When amplified, visions of dread flood your target’s mind. The target is frightened of you until the end of their next turn.

**Rite of the Roar -** The extra damage dealt by your rite is thunder damage.

When amplified, a thunderous crash slams into your target. The target is either pushed up to 10 feet away from you or knocked prone (your choice).

**Rite of the Dawn (Order of the Ghostslayer) -** The extra damage dealt by your rite is radiant damage. Additionally, while the rite is active, you gain the following benefits.

*   Your weapon sheds bright light out a range of 20 feet, and dim light out 20 past that.
*   You have resistance to necrotic damage.
*   When you hit an undead creature with a weapon effected by this rite, roll one additional hemocraft die when determining the extra damage.

When amplified, if the target is an undead, they are placed under the effect of turn undead.

**Rite of the Blighted (Order of the Symbiote) -** The extra damage dealt by your rite is poison damage. Additionally, hitting a creature with a weapon affected by this rite that is neither undead or a construct deals extra poison damage equal to your hemocraft modifier.

When amplified, the target suffers the poisoned condition until the start of your next turn, bypassing immunity to the condition.

### **Blood Hunter Order**

At 3rd level, you commit to an order of blood hunters whose philosophy will guide you throughout your life. Your choice grants you features at 3rd level, and again at 7th, 11th, 15th, and 18th level.

### **Ability Score Improvement**

When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can’t increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

Using the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

### **Extra Attack**

Starting at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the attack action on your turn.

### **Brand of Castigation**

At 6th level, when you damage a creature with your crimson rite, you can channel hemocraft magic to sear an arcane brand into that creature (no action required). Doing so requires you to sacrifice hit points equal to two rolls of your hemocraft die. You always know the direction to the branded creature, so long as it is on the same plane as you.

Additionally, each time the branded creature damages you or creatures of your choice within 30 feet of you, it takes psychic damage equal to your hemocraft modifier (minimum of 1).

Your brand lasts until you dismiss it or until you use this feature to brand another creature. Your brand can be removed with a \[Remove Curse\] spell or similar effects.

### **Grim Psychometry**

When you reach 9th level, you gain a supernatural talent for discerning secrets surrounding relics or places touched by evil. You can cast the \[Contact Other Plane\] spell without needing to make an intelligence save. When you do so, you may ask only about your immediate surroundings, but you will only receive clear answers if what you are asking about is linked to evil. Once you cast the spell in this way, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

You also learn the Identify spell, and can cast it as a ritual. The spell fails if the item you attempt to identify is not linked to evil in some way.

### **Dark Augmentation**

When you reach 10th level, the magic of hemocraft suffuses your body. You either control it, or let it control you. You gain a bonus to strength, dexterity, and constitution saving throws equal to your hemocraft modifier.

Additionally, your hemocraft begins to warp your body and mind, the way you channel your blood magic determines how. You gain one of the following benefits:

**Monstrous:** You gain a bonus to weapon damage rolls equal to your hemocraft modifier.

**Stable:** Your walking speed increases by 10 feet.

### **Tethering Brand**

Starting at 13th level, your brand works to bind your foes. The damage dealt by your Brand of Castigation doubles to twice your hemocraft modifier (minimum of 2).

Additionally, creatures branded by it cannot take the dash or disengage actions. If a branded creature attempts to teleport, or leave its current plane by any means, it takes 4d6 psychic damage and must succeed on a wisdom saving throw. On a failure, the teleportation or attempt to leave the plane fails.

### **Hardened Soul**

Your mind hardens to external influence when you reach 14th level. How you channel your blood magic determines its effect. Gain one of the following benefits:

**Monstrous:** You are immune to the frightened condition, and you have advantage on saving throws to end or resist the charmed condition.

**Stable:** You are immune to the charmed condition, and you have advantage on saving throws to end or resist the frightened condition.

You also gain resistance to psychic damage, regardless of how your hemocraft takes shape.

### **Blood Price**

When you reach 17th level, you gain the ability to fuel your magic more efficiently, so long as your wounds remain open. While you have half your maximum hit points or less, you roll one less hemocraft die when determining the hit point cost of your hemocraft abilities (to a minimum of 0).

### **The Cost of Power**

Upon reaching 20th level, your blood magic reaches its height, and either grants you mastery over it, or consumes you. How you channel your blood magic determines the effect. Gain one of the following benefits:

**Monstrous:** You roll one additional hemocraft die when determining the damage dealt by your crimson rites, including their amplified effects.

The cost of amplifying blood curses increases by one roll of your hemocraft die.

**Stable:** When you use a blood curse and roll a hemocraft die, you can choose to reroll the die and use either result. You can only reroll each die once.

The cost of amplifying crimson rites increases by one roll of your hemocraft die.

## Blood Curses

As a blood hunter, you have access to a range of blood curses that can tax the resilience of any foe.

##### Blood Curse of the Anxious

As a bonus action, you harry the body or mind of a creature within 30 feet of you, making them susceptible to forceful influence. Until the end of your next turn, Charisma (Intimidation) checks made against the cursed creature have advantage.

***Amplify.*** The next Wisdom saving throw the cursed creature makes before this curse ends has disadvantage.

##### Blood Curse of Binding

As a bonus action, you attempt to bind a Large or smaller creature you can see within 30 feet of you, which must make a Strength saving throw. On a failure, the cursed creature’s speed is reduced to 0 and it can’t use reactions until the end of your next turn.

***Amplify.*** This curse lasts for 1 minute and can affect any creature regardless of size. The cursed creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the curse on itself on a success.

##### Blood Curse of Bloated Agony

As a bonus action, you curse a creature that you can see within 30 feet of you, causing its body to swell until the end of your next turn. For the duration, the creature has disadvantage on Strength checks and Dexterity checks, and takes 1d8 necrotic damage if it makes more than one attack during its turn.

***Amplify.*** This curse lasts for 1 minute. The cursed creature can make a Constitution saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the curse on itself on a success.

##### Blood Curse of Corrosion

*Prerequisite: 15th level, Order of the Mutant*

As a bonus action, you cause a creature within 30 feet of you to become poisoned. The cursed creature can make a Constitution saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the curse on itself on a success.

***Amplify.*** The cursed creature takes 4d6 necrotic damage when you inflict this curse, and it takes this damage again each time it fails a Constitution saving throw to end the curse.

##### Blood Curse of the Exorcist

*Prerequisite: 15th level, Order of the Ghostslayer*

As a bonus action, you choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of you that is charmed or frightened, or which is under a possession effect. The target creature is no longer charmed, frightened, or possessed.

***Amplify.*** A creature that charmed, frightened, or possessed the target of your curse takes 3d6 psychic damage and must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be stunned until the end of your next turn.

##### Blood Curse of Exposure

When a creature you can see within 30 feet of you takes damage from an attack or spell, you can use your reaction to temporarily weaken its resilience. Until the end of the target’s next turn, it loses resistance to all the damage types dealt by the triggering attack or spell (including for that triggering effect).

***Amplify.*** The target instead loses invulnerability to the damage types of the triggering attack or spell, but has resistance to those damage types until the end of its next turn.

##### Blood Curse of the Eyeless

When a creature you can see within 30 feet of you makes an attack, you can use your reaction to roll one hemocraft die and subtract the number rolled from the creature’s attack roll. You can choose to use this feature after the creature’s roll, but before the DM determines whether the attack hits or misses. The creature is immune to this curse if it is immune to the blinded condition.

***Amplify.*** You apply this curse to all the creature’s attack rolls until the end of the creature’s turn. You roll separately for each affected attack.

##### Blood Curse of the Fallen Puppet

When a creature you can see within 30 feet of you drops to 0 hit points, you can use your reaction to instill that creature with a final act of aggression. The creature immediately makes one weapon attack against a target of your choice within its range.

***Amplify.*** You can first cause the cursed creature to move up to half its speed, and you grant a bonus to its attack roll equal to your Hemocraft modifier (minimum of +1).

##### Blood Curse of the Howl

*Prerequisite: 18th level, Order of the Lycan*

As an action, you unleash a bloodcurdling howl. Each creature within 30 feet of you that can hear you must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or become frightened of you until the end of your next turn. If a creature fails its saving throw by 5 or more, it is stunned while frightened in this way. A creature that succeeds on its saving throw is immune to this blood curse for the next 24 hours.

You can choose any number of creatures you can see to be unaffected by the howl.

***Amplify.*** The range of this curse increases to 60 feet.

##### Blood Curse of the Marked

As a bonus action, you mark a creature that you can see within 30 feet of you. Until the end of your turn, whenever you hit the cursed creature with a weapon for which you have an active crimson rite, you roll an additional hemocraft die when determining the extra damage from the rite.
