***Amplify.*** The next attack roll you make against the target before the end of your turn has advantage.

##### Blood Curse of the Muddled Mind

As a bonus action, you curse a creature that you can see within 30 feet of you that is concentrating on a spell or using a feature that requires concentration. That creature has disadvantage on the next Constitution saving throw it makes to maintain concentration before the end of your next turn.

***Amplify.*** The cursed creature has disadvantage on all Constitution saving throws made to maintain concentration until the end of your next turn.

##### Blood Curse of the Soul Eater

*Prerequisite: 18th level, Order of the Profane Soul*

When a creature that isn’t a construct or undead is reduced to 0 hit points within 30 feet of you, you can use your reaction to offer their life energy to your patron in exchange for power. Until the end of your next turn, you make attacks with advantage and you have resistance to all damage.

***Amplify.*** Additionally, you regain an expended warlock spell slot. Once you’ve amplified this blood curse, you must finish a long rest before you can amplify it again.

Blood Hunter Subclasses

# Blood Hunter Subclasses

## Order of the Symbiote

*Those who enter the order of the symbiote are either a crazed or brave few. Taking in a new being into their own during an induction to hemocraft, they are able to tap into a special power granted by being now two halves of a greater whole. Whether the symbiote be embedded within their flesh, or coiling around their skin, as this new creature's host the Blood Hunters of this order boast an unmatched ability to tear down the biologies of those they may face.*

### Rite of the Blighted

When you join this order at 3rd level, you learn the rite of the blighted as part of your crimson rite features. When you activate the rite of the blighted, the extra damage dealt is poison damage. Additionally, hitting a creature with a weapon infused with this rite that is neither undead or a construct deals extra poison damage equal to your hemocraft modifier.

### Symbiotic Awareness

A limited consciousness within your symbiote grants you an increased perception of your surroundings. You gain proficiency in the perception skill. Additionally, you may add your hemocraft modifier to your initiative rolls.

### Infected Exposure

At 7th level you know the Blood Curse of Exposure, which does not count towards your total number of blood curses known, and its potential rises to great power in combination with your toxins. If you apply your Blood Curse of Exposure to a creature after dealing poison damage from your Rite of the Blighted, the curse will instead last for 1 minute. If you have amplified the curse, you take damage equal to a roll of your hemocraft die at the start of each of your turns in exchange for your symbiote sustaining the infection.

If you already know the Blood Curse of Exposure, you may learn a different blood curse of your choice that you qualify for.

### Brand of Contagion

At 7th level, when you apply your Brand of Castigation to a creature while you have your Rite of the Blighted active, you may choose to inflict the target with a virulent infection. The target must succeed a constitution saving throw or experience one of the following effects, as determined by rolling your hemocraft die.


Contagion Table
Hemocraft Die RollEffect
1The target becomes Poisoned for 1 minute, and can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, expelling the poison on a successful save.
2A wave of weakness falls over the target. All attack rolls have advantage against the target until the start of its next turn.
3Your opponent's blood mixes with your symbiote, allowing a temporary breach in their immune system. The next blood curse you apply to that target within 1 minute that requires a saving throw will take effect regardless of whether or not their saving throw is successful or not.
4A mild paralytic compound infuses itself within the target, reducing all of its speeds by 10 ft for 1 minute. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.
5The toxins of your weapon attack invade the target's mind, causing them to make concentration saves at disadvantage until the start of your next turn.
6Your target has its physical attributes ravaged to some degree. The target suffers a penalty to all Strength, Dexterity, and Constitution Checks and Saving throws equal to your hemocraft die until the end of its next turn.
7Your target's vision fogs temporarily, causing them to become Blinded until the end of its next turn.
8Toxins invade your target's body entirely, causing their muscles to lock. The target is Paralyzed until the end of its next turn.
9An increased spike in potency causes your rite to deliver vastly increased lethality. For one minute, your Rite of the Blighted deals extra damage equal to three rolls of your hemocraft die instead of one.
10Toxins manage to tear into your target regardless of their biologies. The target loses all resistance and immunity to poison damage for 1 minute, gaining vulnerability instead. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a successful save.


Effects listed under a higher number than you can roll with your hemocraft die remain inaccessible until a level in which you can.

### Mutualistic Host

Starting at 11th level, your symbiote is able to grant you a further boon to your health. Whenever an effect would restore your hit points, you regain an additional amount of hit points equal to a roll of your hemocraft die.

Additionally, you learn the druidcraft cantrip. Your hemocraft ability is your spellcasting ability for this spell.

### Walking Dead

At 15th Level, If you are reduced to 0 hit points, your symbiote may assume control over your basic motor functions for a limited time in order to protect you. During this time you experience the following:

*   Your speed is halved
*   You cannot use your extra attack feature
*   You cannot take reactions
*   You Still make Death Saving Throws at the start of each of your turns
*   You suffer the normal effects of taking damage while at 0 hit points

The symbiote can maintain its control for a number of rounds equal to your hemocraft modifier (minimum of one), until you die, or until you regain at least one hit point. Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

### Blood Curse of Lethality

At 18th level, your symbiotic connection grants you the ability to entirely ravage a creature's health. You gain the Blood Curse of Lethality for your Blood Maledict feature. This doesn’t count against your number of blood curses known.

**Blood Curse of Lethality** - When you hit a creature with a weapon infused with your Rite of the Blighted, you may choose to apply this Blood Curse. The target must make a constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes 8d8 poison damage and suffers a random effect of your Brand of Contagion. The target takes half as much damage on a successful save and suffers no other effects.

Amplify - Amplifying this curse will instead reduce the target's hit points to 1 should they fail the saving throw, and take the full 8d8 poison damage on a success.

Blood Hunter: Martial Versatility

# Blood Hunter: Martial Versatility

Optional (Additional Feature, 4th Level)

Whenever you reach a level in this class that grants the Ability Score Improvement feature, you can replace a fighting style you know with another fighting style available to Blood Hunters. This replacement represents a shift of focus in your martial practice.

Blood Knight

# Blood Knight

## Blood Knight

*The blood knight is the title given to an eldritch knight who chooses to delve into the taboo discipline of hemomancy, or the magic of controlling blood and life force. These warriors thrive in the heart of battle where the blood flows thickest, using it to enhance their offensive and defensive capabilities.*

### Combat Hemomancy

Starting at 3rd level when you choose this archetype, you gain access to a series of hemomantic abilities that allow you to use the blood of your enemies to your advantage.

When you damage a creature that has blood within 30 feet of you with a weapon attack, you gain a special die called a blood die, which is a D6.  You can have a number of these dice up to a maximum equal to your proficiency bonus. Creatures that do not have blood do not generate blood dice, and you do not gain blood dice from attacks that are modified by blood dice.

You can expend blood dice to perform a number of hemomantic abilities described at the end of this features description.

Each time a hemomantic ability asks you to expend your blood dice, you can choose how many you want to roll and gain a benefit based on what you rolled.

#### **Hemomantic Abilities**

**Combat Regeneration.** At the start of your turn, you can spend blood dice to gain temporary hit points. Roll the dice you spend, add them together, and gain temporary hit points equal to the total, replacing any temporary hit points you already have.

**Crimson Strike.** You can use the Attack action to make a special melee attack, a crimson strike. If you're able to make multiple attacks with the Attack action, this attack replaces one of them. You brandish the weapon used in the technique and make a melee attack with it against one creature within 15 feet of you. On a hit, the target takes magical piercing, bludgeoning, or slashing damage equal to the total of the blood dice you spent.

**Blood Shell.** When you are hit by an attack, you can use your reaction to spend blood dice. Roll the dice you spend, add them together, and increase your AC by the amount equal to the total rolled against that attack.

**Blood Clotting.** As a bonus action, you can spend blood dice to throw blood onto a surface at a range of 15 feet and have it stick in a 25 ft. square. Any creature that starts its turn in that area or that enters that area during its turn must make a Strength saving throw with a DC of 10 + the amount you roll on your blood dice (A maximum DC of 8 + proficiency bonus + Strength or Dexterity modifier). On a failed save, the creature's speed is reduced to zero until the start of their next turn.

### Sense Life

At 7th level, you gain advantage on wisdom (perception) checks to spot and track enemies that have blood in their veins. In addition, your ability to sense blood can be so precise that you have advantage wisdom (insight) checks to tell if someone is lying to you.

### Blood-Fueled Overdrive

At 10th level, when you use your indomitable feature, you can expend one blood die and add it to the roll.

### Hemomantic Precision

At 15th level, you have a very tight control over blood you spill. You can re-roll one blood die once whenever you roll them for a hemomantic ability.

### Bloody Tyrant

At 18th level, when you hit a creature with an attack and have the maximum number of blood dice, you can expend them all to force the creature to make a Constitution saving throw with a DC of 8 + proficiency bonus + Strength or Dexterity modifier. On a failed save, the creature is paralyzed for 1 minute. At the end of each of its turns, the paralyzed target must make a Constitution saving throw at the end of each of its turns. On a successful save, this paralyzing effect ends.

Blood Magic

# Blood Magic

## Description

*Considered taboo by many societies and magic practitioners, the use of blood magic—also known as hemocraft—is a rare art that harnesses the latent powers of a creature’s vitality to fuel and amplify the caster’s own capabilities, while manipulating and weakening the bodies of enemies from the inside. Some of the more macabre mages seeking to empower their arcane pursuits turn to hemocraft as a means of bolstering their spells, giving their own life’s blood to reach new heights of frightening magical prowess.*

*The secrets of hemocraft and other forms of blood magic were once the sole purview of the mysterious Claret Orders. In recent years, however, these secrets have begun to spread beyond the order’s members and into the hands of less scrupulous mages. Blood magic has a bad reputation in Tal’Dorei, even though its powers can be used for much more than evil.*

**Features:**

*   [2nd Level - Blood Channeling](#2nd_Level_-_Blood_Channeling)
*   [2nd Level - Sanguine Burst](#2nd_Level_-_Sanguine_Burst)
*   [6th Level - Bond of Mutual Suffering](#6th_Level_-_Bond_of_Mutual_Suffering)
*   [10th Level - Glyph of Hemorrhaging](#10th_Level_-_Glyph_of_Hemorrhaging)
*   [14th Level - Thicker than Water](#14th_Level_-_Thicker_than_Water)

## 2nd Level - Blood Channeling

When you choose this arcane tradition at 2nd level, you are able to use your own depleted life essence to channel your magical abilities. Whenever your current hit points are below your hit point maximum, you can use your own body as an arcane focus.

In addition, when casting a wizard spell that requires a costly material component, you can forego the component by taking 1d10 necrotic damage per 50 gp of the cost of the component (minimum 1d10). This damage can’t be reduced in any way. If this damage reduces you to 0 hit points, the spell fails but the spell slot is not expended.

## 2nd Level - Sanguine Burst

Also at 2nd level, you learn how to weave your life force into a spell you cast, boosting its intensity at the cost of your vitality. Whenever you roll damage for a spell you’ve cast of 1st level or higher, you can choose to take necrotic damage equal to the spell’s level to reroll a number of the damage dice up to your Intelligence modifier (minimum one). This damage can’t be reduced in any way, and you must use the new rolls.

## 6th Level - Bond of Mutual Suffering

At 6th level, when a creature you can see hits you with an attack, you can use your reaction to bind your vitality to the attacker and force them to share your pain. The attacker takes damage equal to the damage you took.

This feature cannot be used against constructs or undead. You can use this feature once. You must finish a short or long rest before you can use it again.

At 14th level, you can use this feature twice between rests.

## 10th Level - Glyph of Hemorrhaging

Starting at 10th level, when you damage a creature with a spell, you can choose to curse that creature for 1 minute. While cursed in this way, whenever the creature is hit by an attack, it takes an extra 1d6 necrotic damage. At the end of each of the creature’s turns, it can make a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC, ending the curse on a success.

This feature cannot be used against creatures that are undead or constructs. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

## 14th Level - Thicker than Water

Upon reaching 14th level, the blood that flows through your veins is empowered with arcane vigor that mends wounds and helps preserve your life. Whenever a spell or magical effect causes you to regain hit points, you regain an additional number of hit points equal to your proficiency bonus.

In addition, while you are concentrating on a spell, you have resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks.

Bloodhunters Brand

# Bloodhunters Brand

*Wondrous Item, uncommon(+1), rare(+2), very rare(+3)*

**Attunement:** Requires Attunement by a Blood Hunter

**Description**

This symbol resembles that of the clan, coven, or circle that brought you into the blood hunting tradition to which you belong. It may be placed anywhere on your body. Typically, these are applied via the use of a needle and removed upon losing attunement to this item.

While wearing this brand, your hemomancy DC is improved by an amount corresponding to this item's rarity.

In addition, you may use a bonus action to regain a use of your blood maledict feature. This ability recharges at dawn.

Bloodneedle

# Bloodneedle

*Weapon (dagger), legendary*

**Attunement:** Requires Attunement

[![Bloodneedle](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/4/47/Bloodneedle.png/revision/latest/scale-to-width-down/180?cb=20241009134936)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/4/47/Bloodneedle.png/revision/latest?cb=20241009134936)

[](/docs/file:bloodneedle.png)

Bloodneedle

**Description**

Bloodneedle sits in a sheath covered in glimmering dark rubies that always seem to be crusted in blood. It's blade appears to be hollow glass.

You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. When you hit with an attack, the target takes an extra 1d10 necrotic damage (ignores resistance and immunity).When you throw it and it hits or misses, it teleports instantly to your hand.

Whenever you roll a 1 on an attack roll using this weapon, you takes 3d8 necrotic damage(ignoring resistance and immunity). This damage decreases your maximum hit points until you have rolled a 20 with this weapon(ending attunement with the weapon does not removed the decreased hit points). You can also remove this using a greater restoration spell.

While you are holding Bloodneedle, you can use an action to cast the harm spell (save DC 15) from the weapon, and this action can't be used again until the next dawn.

Body Back Up

# Body Back-up

**Prerequisite**: Skeleton

*   Your wisdom score increases by one.
*   You can designate one inanimate skeleton by touch as an action. After 30 days, an invisible spiritual connection is made between you and the skeleton. If you are destroyed while such a connection is active, you rise again from the skeleton you had designated. You can only have one skeleton designated in this way at a time.

Bombardier

# Bombardier

## Description

*Where the Alchemist brings life, the Bombardier brings ruin. Bombardiers are masters of volatile reagents, crafting incendiary flasks, caustic vials, and toxic brews designed to engulf their foes in lingering devastation. On the battlefield, they are walking arsenals, hurling explosive concoctions that set the world ablaze.*

**Features**:

*   [3rd Level - Tool Proficiency](#3rd_Level_-_Tool_Proficiency)
*   [3rd Level - Bombardier Spells](#3rd_Level_-_Bombardier_Spells)
*   [3rd Level - Experimental Bomb](#3rd_Level_-_Experimental_Bomb)
*   [5th Level - Volatile Savant](#5th_Level_-_Volatile_Savant)
*   [9th Level - Corrosive Concoctions](#9th_Level_-_Corrosive_Concoctions)
*   [9th Level - Rapid Alchemy](#9th_Level_-_Rapid_Alchemy)
*   [15th Level - Master of Ruin](#15th_Level_-_Master_of_Ruin)

## 3rd Level - Tool Proficiency

When you adopt this specialization at 3rd level, you gain proficiency with **alchemist’s supplies**. If you already have this proficiency, you gain proficiency with another type of artisan’s tools of your choice.

## 3rd Level - Bombardier Spells

You always have certain spells prepared after you reach particular levels in this class, as shown in the Bombardier Spells table. These spells count as artificer spells for you and don’t count against the number of artificer spells you prepare.

**Artificer Level | Spells**

*   3rd | grease, inflict wounds
*   5th | web, Melf's acid arrow
*   9th | gaseous form, stinking cloud
*   13th | greater invisibility, vitriolic sphere
*   17th | wall of force, immolation

## 3rd Level - Experimental Bomb

Starting at 3rd level, whenever you finish a long rest, you can magically produce an experimental bomb in an empty flask or vial you touch. Roll on the Experimental Bomb table to determine its effect, which is triggered when you throw it.

As an action, you or another creature can throw the bomb up to **30 feet**, where it shatters and releases its effect. A creature in the bomb’s area must make a saving throw against your artificer spell save DC to avoid or reduce its effects.

You can create additional experimental bombs by expending a spell slot of 1st level or higher for each one. When you do so, you use your action to create the bomb and roll for its effect on the Experimental Bomb table.

Creating a bomb requires alchemist’s supplies on your person, and any bomb you create with this feature lasts until it is thrown or until the end of your next long rest.

### Experimental Bomb Table

1.  **Corrosive Acid.** Deals 2d6 acid damage in a 5-foot radius (Dex save for half). On a failed save, the acid clings to creatures, dealing 1d6 acid damage at the start of each of their turns until a creature uses its action to scrape or wash it off.
2.  **Incendiary Fire.** Deals 2d6 fire damage in a 5-foot radius (Dex save for half). On a failed save, creatures are set ablaze, taking 1d6 fire damage at the start of each of their turns until they or an ally uses an action to douse the flames.
3.  **Toxic Gas.** Creates a 10-foot radius cloud of poisonous gas that lasts for **1 minute**. (Con save or be poisoned until the start of your next turn; repeat the save at the end of each turn). A creature must make the save whenever it enters the gas or starts its turn in the gas.
4.  **Cryo Flask.** Deals 2d6 cold damage in a 5-foot radius (Dex save for half). On a failed save, a creature is hindered by ice formations for 1 minute, or until it or another creature within reach uses an action to break the ice.
5.  **Shrapnel Bomb.** Deals 2d6 piercing damage in a 10-foot radius (Dex save for half). On a failed save, the creature takes 1d6 slashing damage the first time on each turn it moves 1 foot or more without teleporting. An affected creature or ally can use its action to remove the shrapnel.
6.  **Sonic Blast.** Deals 2d8 thunder damage in a 10-foot radius (Str save for half). Creatures that fail the save are pushed 10 feet from the center of the blast and are deafened until the start of your next turn.

**Scaling:** The initial and persistent damage increase at higher levels:

*   **6th level:** +1d6 (or +1d4/+1d8 for relevant bombs)
*   **11th level:** +2d6
*   **16th level:** +3d6

## 5th Level - Volatile Savant

At 5th level, you have mastered the art of combining spellwork and alchemical destruction into seamless attacks.

*   **Whenever you cast an artificer spell or cantrip on your turn**, you can throw one of your **Experimental Bombs** as a **bonus action**.

You can treat any alchemical item (such as *alchemist’s fire, acid vial*, or an experimental bomb) as a **simple ranged weapon** with a range of 30/90 feet, using your Dexterity or Strength modifier for attack rolls. Damage dealt by the throw is 1d4 slashing damage plus your Dexterity or Strength modifier, in addition to the damage or effect normally dealt by the item.

## 9th Level - Corrosive Concoctions

At 9th level, your concoctions weaken enemy defenses and allow you to control the battlefield:

*   Creatures that fail their saving throw against one of your bombs have **disadvantage on saving throws** against the next alchemical effect you deal to them (from your bombs or alchemical items such as *acid vial* or *alchemist’s fire*) before the end of your next turn.

## 9th Level - Rapid Alchemy

Also at 9th level, you are able to craft bombs with remarkable speed:

*   You can use a bonus action instead of an action to craft your Experimental Bombs.

## 15th Level - Master of Ruin

At 15th level, your expertise in destruction allows you to maximize the potency of your creations and have better control over what you use:

*   You gain **resistance to fire, and acid damage**.
*   When a creature takes persistent damage from one of your Experimental Bombs (*acid* or *fire*) or an alchemical item (*alchemist’s fire, acid vial*), you can use your reaction to make the creature take maximum damage instead of rolling. You can use this ability a number of times equal to half your Intelligence modifier (minimum of 1). All expended uses are restored when you finish a long rest.

Boneblade

# Boneblade

*3rd-level necromancy. (Wizard, Sorcerer, Warlock)*

**Meta**

**Casting Time**: 1 action

**Range**: Self

**Components**: V, S

**Duration**: 1 hour

**Description**

You dig into your chest and pull out a bone which turns into a magical bladed weapon for the duration.

You take 2d6 bludgeoning damage when you first cast this spell, and each time you make an attack with this weapon, you take 1d6 necrotic damage. This damage cannot be reduced in any way, and bypasses resistance and immunity. If this damage would reduce you to 0 hit points, it reduces you to 1 hit point instead, and the spell ends.

When you cast the spell, choose a mundane melee weapon that deals slashing damage. The bone becomes the chosen weapon, with which you are considered proficient. You can use your spellcasting modifier for the attack and damage rolls with this weapon. This blade deals an extra 1d6 necrotic damage on a hit and has advantage on attack rolls made against Good-aligned creatures.

This blade lasts for the duration. If you ever let go of the weapon, it disappears, and you can resummon it to your hand as a bonus action.

**At Higher Levels**

When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the additional damage increases by 1d6 for every two slot levels above 3rd.

Book Of Instant Copying

# Book of Instant Copying

[![Book of Instant Copying](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/e/ed/Book_of_Instant_Copying.png/revision/latest/scale-to-width-down/180?cb=20241009134738)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/e/ed/Book_of_Instant_Copying.png/revision/latest?cb=20241009134738)

[](/docs/file:book-of-instant-copying.png)

Book of Instant Copying

*Wondrous Item, common*

**Description**

This book has a glass cover and is typically found with 2d10 + 30 blank pages. As an action, you can place a piece of paper or similar object (such as a leaf or patterned fabric) between the book's glass and the page below it and speak the book's command word. When you do, the book magically inscribes an image of the placed object in black ink as it appears pressed against the glass on the first blank page of the book.

Copied magic scrolls or spellbooks appear as nonsensical glyphs when copied in this way. When the last page of the book has been used, you can rebind it with 50 blank new pages using 50 gp of fine materials and 8 hours of work.

Book Of Misspells

# Book of Misspells

*Wondrous Item, rare*

**Description**

This magical tome appears to contain several spell scrolls. Each scroll can be used once per day, and regenerates daily at dawn. If the scrolls are ever removed from the tome, they disappear. The spells are inscribed wrong, and their effects vary wildly from the intended. The spells are cast as normal, except have the following effects rather than their original effects:

**Message / Massage.** You point your finger toward a creature within range. The target receives a pleasant back rub.

**Mage Armor / Mage Armoire.** A fancy wardrobe is conjured in the nearest empty space to the caster, containing three sets of clothes. Roll a 1d20 three times. On a result of 1, the clothes are common quality. On a result of 2-10, the clothes are traveler's quality. On a result of 11-15, the clothes are costume quality, on a result of 16-20, the clothes are fine quality.

**Hold Person / Fold Person.** Choose a humanoid that you can see within range. The target must succeed a Wisdom saving throw or be bent over backwards. On a failed save, the target takes 4d6 bludgeoning damage and is paralyzed for one round.

**Speak with Dead / Speak with Dad.** For one minute you can communicate telepathically with your biological father, as long as they are living and on the same plane as you.

**Ice Storm / Ick Storm.** Gross sludge rains down for one minute creating difficult terrain in a 20-foot radius centered on a point within range.

**Teleport / Telepoot.** Creates a loud fart at a destination you select. The destination you choose must be known to you, and it must be on the same plane of existence as you.

Boomburst Hammer

# Boomburst Hammer

*Weapon (maul, warhammer, light hammer), very rare*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon. Attacks against constructs and objects are made with advantage. The hammer has 10 charges. When you hit with a melee attack with this weapon, you can expend up to 3 of its charges. For each charge you expend, the target takes an extra 1d6 thunder damage. Alternatively, you can spend 5 of its charges for the target to take 3d6 thunder damage and force them to make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or be stunned for 1 minute, creatures immune to thunder damage are unaffected by the stun and the stun cannot be applied if there is no sound. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of its turn. Recharge 1d6+4 charges at dawn.

Boon Of Accuracy

# Boon of Accuracy

Whenever you have advantage on an attack roll, you can reroll one of the dice once.

Condition: Cannot be taken with Elven Accuracy

Boon Of Attunement

# Boon of Attunement

*   You gain the ability to attune to one additional magical item, through a special attunement slot.
*   Additionally, as an action, you may end your attunement to the item in the slot granted by this boon, unless the item is cursed, to immediately attune to another. Once you do this, you cannot do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

Boon Of Bewitching Appeal

# Boon of Bewitching Appeal

You can bypass the charmed condition immunity of creatures

Boon Of Dimension Travel: Updated

# Boon of Dimension Travel: Updated

Whenever you move, you may instead expend movement and teleport an amount of feet equal to the expended movement to an area you can see. You may do this an amount of times equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining all expended uses on a short or long rest.

Boon Of Elemental Expertise

# Boon of Elemental Expertise

**Prerequisites**: Elemental Adept Feat

*   The chosen element for this boon is the element chosen in the elemental adept feat. If you have taken the feat multiple times, you may choose which of these elements applies to this boon. This element cannot be later changed.
*   You can treat immunity to the given element as resistance. this resistance cannot be overcome by the elemental adept feat.
*   When you cast a spell of 5th level or lower that deals damage of your chosen element, you can choose to treat all damage dice rolled as their maximum amount. once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

Boon Of Fate: Updated

# Boon of Fate: Updated

When another creature that you can see within 60 feet of you makes an ability check, an attack roll, or a saving throw, you can roll a d10 and apply the result as a bonus or penalty to the roll. You may do this an amount of times equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining all expended uses at the next dawn.

Boon Of Firepower

# Boon of Firepower

**Prerequisite**: Crossbow Expert or Gunner

Your mastery over the weights of these weapons has allowed you to use them at higher efficiencies. You can use any two-handed crossbow or firearm with one hand, and you do not require a free hand to load ammunition into one.

Using a two-handed crossbow or firearm with one hand gives a -2 penalty to attack rolls. Additionally, two-weapon fighting now applies to any crossbow or firearm you wield.

Boon Of Fortitude (Update)

# Boon of Fortitude (Update)

Your hit point maximum increases by 40.

If, instead, you have gestalted or ascended, your hit point maximum increases by 80.

Boon Of Ice'S Heart

# Boon of Ice's Heart

You have immunity to cold damage. You can cast Frost Fingers (save DC 15) at will, without expending a spell slot or using components.

Boon Of Ice'S Reign

# Boon of Ice's Reign

As an action, you unleash a freezing aura that radiates from your body in a persistent chill. This aura remains active until you dismiss it as a free action or until you become incapacitated, at which point you must reactivate it to regain its effects. While this boon is active, any creature that touches you or hits you with a melee attack takes 10 (3d6) cold damage. Additionally, any creature or object that is grappling, restraining, carrying, or otherwise holding you takes the same cold damage at the start of each of your turns.

Boon Of Implacable Terror

# Boon of Implacable Terror

You can bypass the frightened condition immunity of creatures

Boon Of Legend Slaying

# Boon of Legend Slaying

Once per turn, if a legendary monster would use a legendary resistance to succeed on a saving throw, you can use your reaction to have them use a second legendary resistance to succeed on that save. If they cannot, they fail the save.

Boon Of Life'S Embrace

# Boon of Life's Embrace

You have a certain level of mastery over positive energies.  You have resistance to necrotic damage, and your maximum hp cannot be reduced.  Additionally, when you cast a spell or use an ability that restores hit points to other creatures, you may apply one of the following additional effects to targets other than yourself:

*   One of the targets gains double the hit points.
*   All of the targets benefit from a Lesser Restoration
*   The targets regain lost maximum hp equal to twice the healing done.

Adding on an effect can be done an amount of times equal to your proficiency bonus, resetting upon finishing a long rest.

Boon Of Limited Magic Immunity

# Boon of Limited Magic Immunity

**Prerequisite**: Boon of Magic Resistance

You may not be affected or detected by spells of 3rd level and lower unless you choose to be. (Disclaimer that upcasting spells past third level bypasses this immunity)

Boon Of Poison Mastery

# Boon of Poison Mastery

**Prerequisites**: Poisoner Feat

You can treat immunity to poison damage as resistance. This resistance cannot be overcome by the poisoner feat.

*   When you cast a spell of 5th level or lower that deals poison damage, you can choose to treat all damage dice rolled as their maximum amount.
*   When you hit with a weapon attack that deals poison damage, you can choose to treat all damage dice rolled as their maximum amount.

Once you use one of these features, you cannot use either again until you finish a long rest.

Boon Of Reactivness

# Boon of Reactivness

You gain one additional reaction per round. This additional reaction is subject to the following restrictions:

**Diminishing Returns:** You cannot use this reaction to take a Reaction Option that you have already taken this round (such as Cast a Spell, Opportunity Attack, or a specific class feature), nor can you take it in response to a trigger you have already reacted to.

**Defense Limit:** You cannot apply a bonus to your Armor Class if you have already done so since the start of your last turn.

Boon Of Resilience (Update)

# Boon of Resilience (Update)

You have resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from weapon attacks.

Boon Of The Cloud Scion

# Boon of the Cloud Scion

**Prerequisite:** Boon of the Storm Born

**Calamity of the Storm:** First there was lightning…then there was thunder! Once per turn, you may add 10 extra lightning damage to an attack . After you've dealt this damage three times you become supercharged allowing the recipient as a bonus action to emit a wave of thunder dealing 2d8 thunder damage in a 15 ft cube originating from the recipient. When you would take lightning damage you may instead add half of that damage to your next thunder wave.

**Cloud Jumper:** The recipient of this boon gains a flight speed equal to half their movement speed

**Where the wind takes you:**  As an action the recipient of this boon to assume the form of a cloud. While in this cloud form, a creature has a flying speed of 150 feet and has resistance to damage from nonmagical weapons. The only actions a creature can take in this form are the Dash action or to revert to its normal form. Reverting back take a full round during which time the recipient is incapacitated as they reconstitute themselves. If hit by lightning while in cloud form the recipient becomes 'Charged' for 1 minute, while Charged they may cast call lightning at will in the area directly below them (dc 15)

Boon Of The Magical Gardener

# Boon of the Magical Gardener

*   You gain a +5 bonus on Arcana, Nature, or Survival checks made to discern plants.
*   In addition, you may cast the spell *Find Familiar* and summon a Chwinga (Missing it's magical gift) using it

Boon Of The Phoenix

# Boon of the Phoenix

**Prerequisite:** Boon of the Fire Soul

Whenever you are subjected to fire damage, you take none of that damage and regain a number of hit points equal to half the fire damage dealt.

***Fire Form.*** The recipient of this boon can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing. Any creature that touches the phoenix or hits it with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it takes 5 (1d10) fire damage. In addition, the recipient of this boon can enter a hostile creature’s space and stop there. The first time it enters a creature’s space on a turn, that creature takes 5 (1d10) fire damage. With a touch, the recipient of this boon can also ignite flammable objects that aren’t worn or carried (no action required).

***Fiery Death and Rebirth.*** When the recipient of this boon dies, it explodes. Each creature within 60-feet of it must make a DC 20 Dexterity saving throw, taking 22 (4d10) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The fire ignites flammable objects in the area that aren’t worn or carried.

The explosion destroys the recipient's body and carried items and leaves behind an egg-shaped cinder that weighs 5 pounds. The cinder is blazing hot, dealing 21 (6d6) fire damage to any creature that touches it, though no more than once per round. The cinder is immune to all damage, and after 1d6 days, it hatches a new Phoenix. Due to the strain put on the Phoenix’s soul, this forces a new prestige, requiring a small tattood Roman numeral on their body to increase by one. The tattoo can be placed wherever this boon recipient wishes. If the recipient of this boon has paragon levels, they are reset to the beginning of a gestalt prestige in their current Paragon.

Boon Of The Shapechanger

# Boon of the Shapechanger

The CR range for wildshape, shapechange, and polymorph is increased by half of your post 20 lvl rounded down.

This does not function for things such as chain polymorphing, shapechanging, etc; For example you can not poly into a CR23 creature, then a CR26, then CR29. You are limited to CR23 at lvl 20 with 6 post 20 lvls. For clarity, avilable CR is 21 at lvl 20p2, 22 at 20p4, and 23 at 20p6.

Boon Of The True Trickster

# Boon of the True Trickster

Your illusions have become so perfectly crafted that even those with true sight have become impaired by their effects. Your illusion spells appear real to any creature.

Truesight no longer automatically succeeds through your illusions and will now provide only advantage on saving throws and checks made to discern your illusions.

Boon Of Trolls Heart

# Boon of Trolls Heart

You regain 5 hitpoints at the start of your turn. If you are damaged by fire, acid, or radiant damage, this trait ceases to function until your next short or long rest.

Boon Of Vampiric Strikes

# Boon of Vampiric Strikes

*   Your melee weapon attacks deal an additional 1d8 necrotic damage.
*   Additionally, you regain hit points equal to half the amount of necrotic damage dealt by this boon, unless the target is an undead or construct.

Bottle Of Storing

# Bottle of Storing

*Wondrous Item, uncommon*

**Description**

This magical ivory bottle can store up to 40 gallons of liquid at once (As much as 1 barrel), after adding any amount of liquid to the bottle, no other liquid can enter the bottle (meaning can't mix liquids, only liquids of the same type, eg Potion of Superior healing to Potion of Superior healing, salt water can't mix with water etc). The liquid can be poured or drank like any other container Time passes for the liquid inside as usual

Bow Of Silence

# Bow of Silence

*Weapon (any bow), rare*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

This magical bow has the power to silence its victims. You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. On a successful hit the victim must make a dc15 charisma saving throw or be mute for 1d4 rounds. If they fail by 5 or more they won't be able to say the name of their assailant for 1d4 days. If the victim passes a save, they are immune to this effect for the next 24 hours.

Breath Of Life

# Breath of Life

*2nd-level necromancy. (Bard, Cleric, Druid, Paladin, Warlock)*

**Meta**

**Casting Time**: 1 action

**Range**: 60 feet

**Components**: V, S, M ((a small whistle made of petrified wood))

**Duration**: Instantaneous

**Description**

You transfer your vital essence upon a spectral wind to a creature within range. Choose another creature within range, then spend and roll one of your unspent Hit Dice. The target gains a number of temporary hit points equal to 5 + the number rolled.

**At Higher Levels**

When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the number of temporary hitpoints increases by 5, and you can roll an additional Hit Dice for every two slot levels above 2nd. At 9th level, the number of temporary hitpoints increases to 25, and you can roll five Hit Dice.

Bulwark

# Bulwark

## The Bulwark Class

Outside the king’s chambers, still as an armor display, the royal guard stands post as they await the coming rebelion force. Ready to meet it head on, no matter the result.

A scrappy militiaman clad in chainmail stands on the battlefield, now alone and bloodied, having outlasted all others through wit and defensive positioning.

Beneath a greathelm, the dwarf’s face pools with sweat as he crosses the great desert, sent on a mission to deliver an important message that cannot afford to fall into the wrong hands

Bulwarks spend large portions of their lives covered in heavy armor to the point that they are seldom ever seen without it. Their mastery over using it fully to their advantage naturally comes as second nature, allowing them to outperform nearly any other. What truly distinguishes a bulwark from other users of heavy armor is their distinct focus on making the absolute most of the defense it offers, knowing how best to move themselves to avoid or block incoming assaults. Bulwarks are often kept as personal guards to high ranking officials, as front-liners in the field of battle, or even as couriers who’s senders need certainty their message will arrive. Adventuring parties who are graced with the inclusion of one of these masters of defense can rest assured that at least one of them will be making it back alive.

### The Best Offense...

A Bulwark will tend to focus fully on a defensive standpoint in battle, as an overtly pressing offense might lead to gaps in your attention. In fact, some Bulwarks even refrain from having an actual offense entirely, relying on those around them to defeat their enemies while they spend their attention and energy on lasting as long as possible.

### Creating a Bulwark

When creating a Bulwark, consider why your character might have needed to train such a rigorous defense. What training have they undergone to truly set themselves ahead of their peers? Perhaps you were a member of a royal guard, someone trained to protect at any cost. Or rather just a soldier who has taken to protecting themselves a bit further than most. A Bulwark could be anyone of the great strength needed to use heavy armors, and utilizes it to the fullest, and you get to decide just how your character does that.

### Multiclassing

When multiclassing with the Bulwark class, use the following:

**Prerequisite:** Strength 13 and Constitution 13

**Proficiencies Gained:** Light armor, Medium armor, Shields, and Heavy armor

## Bulwark Table

|Level || Proficiency Bonus || Features

| 1st  || +2 || Defensive Stance, Weapon Mastery

| 2nd  || +2 || Fortitude

| 3rd  || +2 || Defensive Specialization

| 4th  || +2 || Ability Score Improvement

| 5th  || +3 || Extra Attack, Emissary

| 6th  || +3 || Conditioning

| 7th  || +3 ||Specialization Feature

| 8th  || +3 ||Ability Score Improvement

| 9th  || +4 ||Dueling Stance

| 10th || +4 ||Mental Fortitude

| 11th || +4 ||Shattering Stance

| 12th || +4 ||Ability Score Improvement

| 13th || +5 ||Specialization Feature

| 14th || +5 ||Reinforcing Fortitude

| 15th || +5 ||Steel Endurance

| 16th || +5 ||Ability Score Improvement

| 17th || +6 ||Mountain Stance

| 18th || +6 ||Specialization Feature

| 19th || +6 ||Ability Score Improvement

| 20th || +6 ||Adamantine Endurance

## Class Features

As a Bulwark, you gain the following class features.

### Base Class Features

#### Hit Points

**Hit Dice:** 1d10 per bulwark level

**Hit Points at 1st Level:** 10 + your constitution modifier

**Hit Points at Higher Levels:** 1d10 (or 6) + your constitution modifier per Bulwark level after first.

#### Proficiencies

**Armor**: Light, Medium, and Heavy Armor, Shields

**Weapons**: Simple Weapons, Warhammers, Heavy Crossbows

**Tools**: none

**Saving Throws**: Constitution and Charisma

**Skills**: Choose two from Animal Handling, Athletics, Insight, Intimidation, Medicine, Persuasion, and Religion

#### Equipment

You start with the following equipment, in addition to any equipment granted by your background:

*   (a) a warhammer or (b) a simple weapon of your choice and a shield
*   A suit of chainmail armor
*   (a) an explorer’s pack or (b) a diplomat’s pack
*   (a) a light crossbow and 20 bolts or (b) a shortbow and 20 arrows

### Defensive Stance

While you are wearing heavy armor, you can choose to take up a defensive stance to bolster yourself for incoming force. As an action, you can give yourself a +2 bonus to your armor class and resistance to force damage for 1 minute, or until you are no longer wearing heavy armor.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your constitution modifier before completing a long rest.

### Weapon Mastery

Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of weapons of your choice that you have proficiency with, such as maces and light crossbows.

Whenever you finish a long rest, you can change the kinds of weapons you chose. For example, you could switch to using the mastery properties of warhammers and handaxes.

### Fortitude

At 2nd level, you can lessen the impact of weapons against you. While you are wearing heavy armor, and are hit by a weapon attack, you can use your reaction to reduce the damage by an amount equal to your bulwark level.

### Defensive Specialization

When you reach 3rd level, you choose a defensive specialization that dictates the types of strategies you employ to protect yourself and others.

*   Juggernaut
*   Phalanx
*   Rook
*   Spellbreaker
*   Unfettered

Your choice grants you features at 3rd level and again at 7th, 13th, and 18th level.

### Ability Score Improvement

When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase two ability scores of your choice by one. As normal, you can’t increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

Using the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

### Extra Attack

Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the attack action on your turn.

### Emissary

Additionally at 5th level, your voice carries with you a supernatural sense of purpose and authority. You gain a bonus to all charisma (intimidation and persuasion) checks equal to your strength modifier (minimum bonus of +1).

### Conditioning

At 6th level, you gain an uncanny knack for endurance. You no longer suffer the adverse effects of a forced march, and exhaustion does not affect you until you have at least three levels.

### Dueling Stance

Starting at 9th level, you radiate an aura that compels your foes to battle against you, and you alone. While your defensive stance is active, enemies that start their turn within 10 feet of you must make a charisma saving throw (DC 8 + your strength modifier + your proficiency bonus) or be forced to use their action to attack, or otherwise harm you.

### Mental Bastion

At 10th level, you gain the ability to harden your mind to attacks with a similar effectiveness as your armor can block physical blows. You gain proficiency in intelligence or wisdom saving throws (your choice). You can replace your selection with the other whenever you finish a long rest.

### Shattering Stance

When you reach 11th level, you learn to redirect the energy meant for you back to your attacker. While your defensive stance is active, you deal extra damage equal to your proficiency bonus when you attack with a weapon. You can deal this extra damage only once per turn.

### Reinforcing Fortitude

Starting at 14th level, your ability to shrug off attacks extends to even the mightiest of blows. When you use your Fortitude feature, you gain a number of temporary hit points equal to the amount of damage you prevented. Once you gain these temporary hit points, you cannot do so again until you finish a short rest, or enter a new defensive stance.

### Steel Endurance

At 15th level, when you roll initiative and have no remaining uses of your defensive stance, you regain one use.

### Mountain Stance

When you reach 17th level, your stance becomes as unbreakable as the very earth itself. While your defensive stance is active, you cannot be moved against your will, and you are immune to the frightened condition and critical hits.

### Adamantine Endurance

At 20th level, the duration of your defensive stance increases to 10 minutes, and the bonus it confers to your armor class increases to +4.

Buster Bat

# Buster Bat

*Weapon (greatclub), legendary*

**Attunement**

[![Buster Bat](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/d/d0/Buster_Bat.png/revision/latest/scale-to-width-down/180?cb=20241009134234)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/d/d0/Buster_Bat.png/revision/latest?cb=20241009134234)

[](/docs/file:buster-bat.png)

Buster Bat

Requires Attunement

**Description**

This bat is created from unknown origin and exudes devastating capabilities. It is made from a type of wood called Ironwood, an extremely hard but light wood when compared to oak wood and while remaining natural wood in almost every way. It is as strong, heavy, and resistant to fire as steel. You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, in addition to the following abilities.

*   **Battery**. You are able to deflect anything coming your way. All ranged attacks against you are made with disadvantage and you take no damage from magic missile.
*   **Knock**. The impact caused by this weapon will undeniably cause damage. The weapon ignores resistance to bludgeoning damage. When you hit and roll a 15 or higher on the attack roll with this weapon, the target takes extra 2d12 bludgeoning damage.
*   **Home Run**. At extreme cases, this weapon will perform impossible feats. Once in each combat, the third time you roll a 20 on an attack roll with this weapon, the target by the last roll dies instantly and their soul is destroyed if its a creature. A creature can negate this effect by using one use of their Legendary Resistance if they have the trait. If the target is a nonmagical object or structure, the object is pulverized to dust and a portion of the structure is destroyed making a hole 10 feet in diameter and can go as far as to 20 feet deep into the structure.
*   **Bulk Up**. The longer the battle persists, the more battle-hardened you become. When you are in combat for 5 rounds or longer, you gain a +3 bonus to AC, advantage on saving throws, and you have resistance to all damage except force, radiant, and psychic damage. And when combat reaches 10 rounds or longer, you also gain immunity to being inflicted with conditions.

Cartomancer

# Cartomancer

***Prerequisite***

*4th Level, Spellcasting Feature*

**Description**

You have learned to channel your magic through a deck of cards. You can use a card deck as your spellcasting focus, and you gain the following benefits:

**Card Tricks.** You learn the Prestidigitation cantrip and can use it to create illusions that duplicate the effects of stage magic. When you use Prestidigitation in this way, you can conceal the verbal and somatic components of the spell as ordinary conversation and card handling.

**Hidden Ace.** When you finish a long rest, you can choose one first level spell from your class's spell list and imbue that spell into a card. The chosen spell must have a casting time of 1 action. The card remains imbued with this spell for 8 hours. While the card is imbued with the spell, you can use a bonus action to flourish the card and cast the spell within. The card then immediately loses its magic.

Caustic Spell

# Caustic Spell

**Casting Time:** 1 Action

**Range:** Self; 10 ft cubes

**Components**: S, M (a citrus fruit)

**Duration**: concentration up to 1 minute

A viscous and caustic substance spills out across eight 10 foot cubes, which you can arrange as you wish. Each cube must have at least one face adjacent to the face of another cube.

Each creature in the area when the spill appears must make a dexterity saving throw or take 6d4 acid damage. After which the spill settles, making the spell's area difficult terrain.

For the remainder of the spell's duration, the first time a creature enters the spill on their turn, or starts their turn in the spill, they take 6d4 acid damage. A creature also takes 2d4 acid damage for every 5 feet they move through the spill.

Celestial Legacy

# Celestial Legacy

**Prerequisite**

Aasimar

**Description**

You learn how to better control your celestial power, and are able to draw more energy from it. You gain one of the following benefits, which will replace your Celestial Revelation feature. You may activate your Celestial Legacy as a bonus action or an action (your choice). Your transformation lasts for 1 minute or until you end it as a bonus action. Once you transform using your Celestial Legacy, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

Celestial Reaper.

This feature replaces your Necrotic Shroud. Your eyes briefly become pools of darkness, and ghostly, flightless wings sprout from your back temporarily. Creatures of your choice within 30 feet of you that can see you must succeed on a Charisma saving throw (DC 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier) or become frightened of you until the end of your next turn. Furthermore, while they are frightened in this way, you have advantage on any attack rolls against them. Until the transformation ends, once on each of your turns, you can deal extra necrotic damage to one target when you deal damage to it with an attack or a spell. The extra damage equals your level.

Cestus

# Cestus

*Weapon: Simple, Melee 1gp, 1.5 lbs. --- 1d4 bludgeoning - light, two-handed*

**Description**

Proficiency with a cestus allows you to add your proficiency bonus to the attack roll for any attack you make with it. Special: As a bonus action, you can make one attack with this weapon.

Chakram

# Chakram

*Weapon: ( Martial, Melee)* 30gp, 3lbs. --- d4 slashing - finesse, light, thrown (10/30),

Description

Proficiency with a chakram allows you to add your proficiency bonus to the attack roll for any attack you make with it. On a missed throw, a chakram returns to the thrower's hand.

**Finesse**: When making an attack with a finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

**Light**: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.

**Thrown**: If a weapon has the thrown property, you can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. If the weapon is a melee weapon, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and damage roll that you would use for a melee attack with the weapon. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the finesse property.

Champions Weapon

# Champions Weapon

*Weapon (Any simple or martial weapon), legendary*

*quest-able; uncraftable*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

This weapon carries the last breath and fighting spirit of old champions, buried with them in their tombs as they carry their greatest arms into the afterlife.

You have a +3 to attack and damage rolls made when using this weapon. While wielding this sword, the threshold required for you to score a critical hit with weapon attacks is reduced by 1.

Changeling Metamorphosis

# Changeling Metamorphosis

Prerequisite: Changeling

Your ability to shapechange is more than skin deep, and you are able to assume more forms than the average Changeling. You gain the following benefits:

Increase your Consititution score by 1, to a maximum of 20

When you use your Shapechanging trait, you can choose one of these benefits which lasts until you change your shape again:

\- **Darkvision** You adapt your eyes and gain Darkvision out to 60ft.

\- **Aquatic Adaption** You sprout gills and are able to breath underwater, and you gain a swimming speed equal to your walking speed.

\- **Natural Weapons** You grow claws, fangs, spines, horns or a different natural weapon of your choice. Your unarmed strikes deal 1d6 bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage as appropriate, and you are considered proficient with unarmed strikes.

\- **Wings** You grow wings, which can take on whatever appearance/shape you desire. You gain 30 ft. of flying speed.

\- **Extra Appendage** You grow a prehensile tail, tentacles, crab claws, or another limb of your choice, which can only be used to grapple someone as an action (even if your main hands are currently full), and cannot be used to hold equipment, weapons, or any other objects.

You can use these features an amount of times equal to your proficiency bonus (shared among all), resetting on a Long Rest.

Channel Of Artistry

# Channel of Artistry

[![Artistry Channeler](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/b/bf/Artistry_Channeler.png/revision/latest/scale-to-width-down/180?cb=20241011104723)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/b/bf/Artistry_Channeler.png/revision/latest?cb=20241011104723)

[](/docs/file:artistry-channeler.png)

Artistry Channeler

## Description

*The Channel of Artistry represents channelers who want to master controlling the flow of their arcane energy, manipulating their power with finesse and elegance. They weave through combat like dancers and cast spells with the delicacy of an accomplished maestro.*

**Features**

*   [1st Level - Armaments of Artistry](#1st_Level_-_Armaments_of_Artistry)
*   [1st Level - Elemental Savant](#1st_Level_-_Elemental_Savant)
*   [6th Level - Weaving Strike](#6th_Level_-_Weaving_Strike)
*   [11th Level - Beautiful Virtuosity](#11th_Level_-_Beautiful_Virtuosity)
*   [11th Level - Harmonized Strikes](#11th_Level_-_Harmonized_Strikes)
*   [17th Level - Culmination of Artistry](#17th_Level_-_Culmination_of_Artistry)

## 1st Level - Armaments of Artistry

When you pick this channel at 1st level, you gain proficiency in shortswords, scimitars, and whips. You also gain proficiency in one type of artisan’s tools of your choice.

## 1st Level - Elemental Savant

Your use and control of elemental energy is beautiful to many, and distracting to your opponents. Starting at 1st level, when you hit a creature with an attack, you have resistance to the first attack they make against you until the beginning of your next turn. Additionally, you may imbue two weapons instead of one. The imbued damage types must be the same for both weapons.

## 6th Level - Weaving Strike

At 6th level, you can sculpt your elemental energy with your weapon, enhancing both your own movements and your energy’s potency. When you deal damage with your Elemental Imbuement, the next attack roll you make during this turn has advantage, and your movement doesn’t provoke opportunity attacks until the beginning of your next turn.

## 11th Level - Beautiful Virtuosity

At 11th level, the beauty of your movement and the flow of your energy is entrancing and inspiring to allies. When you deal damage with your Elemental Imbuement, you may choose a creature within 30 feet that can see you. That creature may add your Elemental Imbuement die to the next attack roll, saving throw, or ability check they make until the beginning of your next turn.

## 11th Level - Harmonized Strikes

At 11th level, if you hit with a weapon attack from your attack action and hit with a spell attack roll made with your Overchanneled Imbuement in one turn, you may take a second bonus action that turn.

## 17th Level - Culmination of Artistry

At 17th level, you’ve perfected your casting as a form of art. You may use your Flash Charge feature an unlimited amount of times per day. When you hit with an attack, you may use your Flash Charge feature as a reaction. When you do, the attack deals additional damage equal to two rolls of your new Elemental Imbuement die.

Channel Of Containment

# Channel of Containment

## Description

Features:

*   [1st Level - Armaments of Containment](#1st_Level_-_Armaments_of_Containment)
*   [6th Level - Brawl Binding](#6th_Level_-_Brawl_Binding)
*   [11th Level - Elemental Fortitude](#11th_Level_-_Elemental_Fortitude)
*   [11th Level - Extra Attack](#11th_Level_-_Extra_Attack)
*   [17th Level - Culmination of Containment](#17th_Level_-_Culmination_of_Containment)

## 1st Level - Armaments of Containment

When you pick this channel at 1st level, you gain proficiency in improvised weapons. You also gain proficiency in heavy armor. Elemental Core You learn to confine your arcane energy within your body, empowering your unarmed attacks. You may choose to Imbue your body with elemental energy as opposed to a weapon. While imbued, you gain the following benefits:

*   Your unarmed strikes use your Elemental Imbuement dice for its damage type.
*   When you attack with an unarmed strike, you may use your Intelligence modifier, rather than your strength modifier, for attack and damage rolls.
*   You gain resistance to the damage type you chose for your Imbuement
*   You can use your body as a focus for your Channeler spells

## 6th Level - Brawl Binding

Beginning at 6th level, when you use the attack action to grapple a creature, the target takes damage equal to one roll of your Elemental Imbuement die if the grapple succeeds. Whenever you take the attack action against the creature on subsequent turns, if you are still grappling the creature, you may choose to deal this damage again. Additionally, if you succeed on grappling a creature, you can use your Overchanneled Imbuement as a bonus action. Creatures you grapple have disadvantage on saving throws against spells you cast.

## 11th Level - Elemental Fortitude

At 11th level, your contained energy provides a defensive ward that sits just under your skin, protecting you from harm. When you are subjected to an effect that has you roll a saving throw to take half damage, if the damage type is acid, cold, fire, lightning, or thunder, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw, and only half damage if you fail.

## 11th Level - Extra Attack

Beginning at 11th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the attack action on your turn.

## 17th Level - Culmination of Containment

Beginning at 17th level, your arcane energy binds perfectly to your flesh. You gain the following benefits while Imbued:

*   You gain immunity to the damage type you chose for your Imbuement
*   Your walking speed increases by 20 feet
*   You no longer need to hit with an Imbued weapon attack to cast Channeler spells as a bonus action.
*   When you cast a Channeler spell, add your Intelligence modifier to your unarmed strike damage and any Strength, Dexterity, or Constitution checks you make until the end of your next turn.

Channel Of Devastation

# Channel of Devastation

[![Devastation Channeler](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/b/bf/Devastation_Channeler.png/revision/latest/scale-to-width-down/180?cb=20241011104518)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/b/bf/Devastation_Channeler.png/revision/latest?cb=20241011104518)

[](/docs/file:devastation-channeler.png)

Devastation Channeler

## Description

*The Channel of Devastation represents channelers who prefer focusing their energy in short, powerful bursts, often leading to disastrous results. Those who choose this channel work tirelessly to keep their energy concealed and suppressed, until they can let it all out in a violent burst.*

**Features**

*   [1st Level - Armaments of Devastation](#1st_Level_-_Armaments_of_Devastation)
*   [1st level - Accelerant](#1st_level_-_Accelerant)
*   [6th Level - Devastating Presence](#6th_Level_-_Devastating_Presence)
*   [11th Level - Devastating Strikes](#11th_Level_-_Devastating_Strikes)
*   [11th Level - Extra Attack](#11th_Level_-_Extra_Attack)
*   [17th Level - Culmination of Devastation](#17th_Level_-_Culmination_of_Devastation)

## 1st Level - Armaments of Devastation

When you pick this channel at 1st level, you gain proficiency in glaives, greataxes, greatswords, halberds, and pikes. You also gain proficiency in heavy armor and shields.

## 1st level - Accelerant

At 1st level, you’ve learned how to create an accelerant with your devastating energy, overcharging your attacks. When you hit with a melee weapon attack, you can force the target to roll a strength saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failure, the target is knocked prone and suffers an additional effect depending on your imbuement. You may use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier. When you take a short or long rest, you regain all expended uses.

##### Accelerant Effect

| Damage Type | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| Acid | The target’s AC is reduced by 1 until the end of your next turn. |
| Fire | The target takes additional fire damage equal to one roll of your Elemental Imbuement die. |
| Cold | The target’s speed is reduced by half of their maximum until the end of your next turn. |
| Lightning | You may force one other creature of your choice within 10 feet of the target to take lightning damage equal to two rolls of your Elemental Imbuement die. |
| Thunder | The target is pushed back 15 feet. |

## 6th Level - Devastating Presence

At 6th level, your mere presence is recognized as a threat, inciting opponents to focus their aggression on you. Each creature of your choice that starts its turn within 30 feet of you and can see you must roll a Wisdom saving throw against to your spell save DC. On a failure, the creature has disadvantage on attacks that don’t target you until the end of that creature’s turn. If a creature succeeds on this saving throw, they are immune to this effect for the next 24 hours.

## 11th Level - Devastating Strikes

Starting at 11th level, whenever you deal damage with your Elemental Imbuement or a channeler spell that shares a damage type with your chosen Elemental Imbuement, you ignore resistance to that damage type, and you may treat immunity to that damage type as resistance.

## 11th Level - Extra Attack

Beginning at 11th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the attack action on your turn.

## 17th Level - Culmination of Devastation

At 17th level, you’ve wrought elemental devastation everywhere you’d gone, and have found the perfect way to channel it. When you hit with a melee weapon attack, you can reroll that attack against any number of other targets within 30ft, hitting each target simultaneously. You may use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier. When you finish a long rest, you regain all expended uses.

Channel Of Madness

# Channel of Madness

[![Madness Channeler](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/e/ee/Madness.png/revision/latest/scale-to-width-down/180?cb=20241011104310)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/e/ee/Madness.png/revision/latest?cb=20241011104310)

[](/docs/file:madness.png)

Madness Channeler

## Description

*The channel of Madness represents a rare and often fanatical sect of channelers that believe in submitting control over their mind and actions to the powers within themselves, even to their own detriment*

**Features**

*   [1st Level - Armaments of Madness](#1st_Level_-_Armaments_of_Madness)
*   [1st Level - Medium For Madness](#1st_Level_-_Medium_For_Madness)
*   [6th Level - Braincharge](#6th_Level_-_Braincharge)
*   [11th Level - Mental Overchannel](#11th_Level_-_Mental_Overchannel)
*   [11th Level - Psychic Armaments](#11th_Level_-_Psychic_Armaments)
*   [17th Level - Culmination of Madness](#17th_Level_-_Culmination_of_Madness)

## 1st Level - Armaments of Madness

When you pick this channel at 1st level, you gain proficiency in hand crossbows, heavy crossbows, and longbows.

## 1st Level - Medium For Madness

At 1st level, you may select two 1st level enchantment spells. You learn these spells and may cast each of them once per day without expending a spell slot. At 6th level, you may choose two 2nd level enchantment spells, two 3rd level enchantment spells at 11th level, and two 4th level enchantment spells at 17th level.

Additionally, you may pick psychic damage as the damage type for your Elemental Imbuement.

## 6th Level - Braincharge

At 6th level, you’re able to overload the minds of others, driving them further towards insanity. When a creature fails the first save from an enchantment spell you cast, they take extra psychic damage equal to your Elemental Imbuement die. Then, you may have one creature you can see within 30 feet of you gain advantage on the next Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma saving throw they make until the beginning of your next turn.

## 11th Level - Mental Overchannel

At 11th level, when you deal psychic damage to a creature, you may choose to deal maximum psychic damage rather than rolling. If you do, the target is stunned until the beginning of your next turn. You cannot use this feature again until you finish a long rest.

## 11th Level - Psychic Armaments

At 11th level, when you imbue a weapon with psychic damage, you can change that weapon’s base damage type to psychic for as long as it’s imbued. Add your Intelligence modifier (minimum of +1) to the damage of your weapon attacks imbued with psychic damage.

## 17th Level - Culmination of Madness

At 17th level, it’s hard to tell if you’re still sane or not; your internal energy has corroded your mind past the point of no return. You gain advantage on saving throws against enchantment spells. In addition, you gain immunity to psychic damage and immunity to effects that would charm or frighten you. Once per day, when you cast an enchantment spell, if it would target only one creature and no other creatures, you can instead make it target as many creatures as you can see within 60 feet of you.

Channel Of Volatility

# Channel of Volatility

## Description

The Channel of Volatility is a controversial path to undertake, as though it is the easiest channel to ‘master’, it may prove most perilous to the channeler using it. Those who practice the channel of Volatility must exercise extreme caution, else they risk losing control of their magic to horrific consequence.

**Features**:

*   [1st Level - Armaments of Volatility](#1st_Level_-_Armaments_of_Volatility)
*   [1st Level - Volatile Charge](#1st_Level_-_Volatile_Charge)
*   [6th Level - Volatile Surge](#6th_Level_-_Volatile_Surge)
*   [11th Level - Risk and Reward](#11th_Level_-_Risk_and_Reward)
*   [11th Level - Extra Attack](#11th_Level_-_Extra_Attack)
*   [17th Level - Culmination of Volatility](#17th_Level_-_Culmination_of_Volatility)

## 1st Level - Armaments of Volatility

When you pick this channel at 1st level, you gain proficiency in flails, mauls, and warhammers. You also gain proficiency in heavy armor and shields.

## 1st Level - Volatile Charge

Your internal energy is difficult to predict and control, yet it assists you when you need it most. Starting at 1st level, whenever you hit with a weapon attack using your imbued weapon, note the result of the attack roll and your Imbuement die roll.

*Odd.* If the result of the attack roll is odd, you gain temporary hit points equal to the result of your Imbuement die, which disappears whenever you take a short or long rest.

*Even.* If the result of the attack roll is even, the attack deals extra damage equal to the result of your Imbuement die.

## 6th Level - Volatile Surge

The unpredictable effects of your magic have branched into even more peculiar effects. Starting at 6th level, when you cast a channeler spell, roll your Elemental Imbuement die and note the result. Consult the Volatile Surge table for additional effects.

##### Surge Effect

| Result | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | You take damage equal to one roll of your Elemental Imbuement die. |
| 2 | You add another roll of your Elemental Imbuement die to the spell’s damage. |
| 3 | Your AC increases by 1 until the start of your next turn. |
| 4 | You gain advantage on your next attack roll until the end of your next turn. |
| 5 | You gain additional temporary hit points equal to one roll of your Elemental Imbuement die. |
| 6 | You can make an additional weapon attack as part of the same bonus action. |
| 7 | You gain resistance to the next attack that hits you until the start of your next turn. |
| 8 | Reroll on this table twice, and the effects of both rolls occur. If you roll the same effect twice, the effect only applies once. |

## 11th Level - Risk and Reward

At 11th level; your frequent interactions with fickle energies have allowed you to lean further into their volatility. You learn one additional Ante option, and gain an additional Ante charge.

## 11th Level - Extra Attack

Beginning at 11th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the attack action on your turn.

## 17th Level - Culmination of Volatility

At 17th level, your destructive magic is longing for escape from your mortal shell. Once per day, when you’re reduced to zero hit points, you may choose to release all of your internal energy in one burst. Each creature of your choice within 30 feet of you must roll a dexterity saving throw equal to your spell save DC. On a failed save, a target takes 15d10 damage and is stunned until the end of their next turn. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage and isn’t stunned. The damage type is the same as your elemental Imbuement. In either case, the explosion kills you immediately, disintegrating all non-magical gear and equipment you’re carrying and wearing.

Channeler'S Clay

# Channeler's Clay

*Wondrous Item, uncommon(+1), rare(+2), very rare(+3)*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement by a Channeler

**Description**

This is a glob of discolored clay that you apply to a weapon you're proficient with. While wielding the applied weapon, you gain a bonus to spell attack rolls and to saving throw DCs of your channeler spells. This bonus is determined by the clay's rarity. In addition, you can regain 1 ante as an action while wielding the applied weapon. You can't use this property again until you finish a long rest.

Channeler

# Channeler

## Channeler

*Clad in rusted armor with a fizzling blade in their right hand, a warrior strikes down their goblin foe with the crackling might of a bolt of lightning. Electricity arcs up and down their blade, converging on the tip and electrocuting its unassuming victim. Laughter echoes across the dank walls of a dungeon as an elf sits, cross-legged, amongst the bodies of her victims, eyes clouded by maddening power. Though her victims show little signs of external damage, their eyes roll up into their sockets, leaving behind only a white gleam. Snowflakes whiz across the air in a beautiful miniature blizzard, centered on a Dragonborn with scales as white as the snow surrounding him. Raising his twin daggers, he weaves together a pattern of rime and frost that leaves his enemies disoriented in a dazzling display of elemental prowess.*

*These individuals, regardless of origin, share one point in common; pure energy flows through their veins, seemingly bursting from their bodies with little control or conviction. Whether they’ve received this energy through a higher power’s blessing, an experiment gone terribly wrong, or were born as natural capacitors for magic, each of these individuals must quickly learn to channel their internal energy, else it burns them up from the inside.*

### A Channel for the Arcane Arts

*A channeler is forced to learn to control their energy, as it can be dangerous to both themselves and others to leave it unmanaged and unchecked. How they choose to do so differs from channeler to channeler: some embrace the chaos caused by their energy, some try to hide it and seal it away as much as they can. Some notice the value of the power they hold and try to shape it to their whims, while others still allow their energy to shape themselves and their actions. Regardless of how they decide to use their power, there still comes the issue of channeling it in the first place.Channelers generally train their whole lives to control their energy, from the moment they receive it onwards. Particularly talented channelers might be able to channel their power into objects at will, though regardless of talent, mastery over their power requires hard work. What constitutes ‘mastery’, however, is different from channeler to channeler; some may tell you complete control represents mastery, while another may tell you that you’re not a master lest your mind and body are completely broken under the weight of your own might.*

### Imbued Weaponry

*Most commonly, channelers use weapons to control their abilities. Channelers use the same martial training these swordsmen and soldiers use to visualize and concentrate their own energy. As martial warriors consider their weapons as extensions of their own body, it’s the easiest for channelers to use weapons as a focus. A trained channeler is as deft with a weapon as any other combatant, and can use their energy to amplify their combat abilities to their liking.*

### The Channeler Class Table

| Level | Proficiency Bonus | Feature | Imbuement Dice | Antes Known |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1st | +2 | Arcane Channel, Elemental Imbuement | 1d4 | — |
| 2nd | +2 | Arcane Attraction, Fighting Style | 1d4 | — |
| 3rd | +2 | Ante | 1d4 | 3 |
| 4th | +2 | Ability Score Improvement | 1d4 | 3 |
| 5th | +3 | Overchanneled Imbuement | 1d4 | 3 |
| 6th | +3 | Arcane Channel Feature | 1d4 | 3 |
| 7th | +3 | Elemental Boon | 1d4 | 4 |
| 8th | +3 | Ability Score Improvement | 1d4 | 4 |
| 9th | +4 | Overchanneled Imbuement Improvement | 1d4 | 4 |
| 10th | +4 | Elemental Imbuement Improvement, Raze | 1d6 | 4 |
| 11th | +4 | Arcane Channel Feature | 1d6 | 4 |
| 12th | +4 | Ability Score Improvement | 1d6 | 5 |
| 13th | +5 | Up The Ante | 1d6 | 5 |
| 14th | +5 | Flash Charge | 1d6 | 5 |
| 15th | +5 | Greater Boon | 1d6 | 5 |
