| 16th | +5 | Ability Score Improvement | 1d6 | 5 |
| 17th | +6 | Arcane Channel Feature | 1d8 | 6 |
| 18th | +6 | Elemental Imbuement Improvement, Ruin | 1d8 | 6 |
| 19th | +6 | Ability Score Improvement | 1d8 | 6 |
| 20th | +6 | Pinnacle Channel | 1d8 | 6 |

## Creating a Channeler

As you’re making a channeler, you must take a few things into consideration; where did the source of their internal energy come from? Was it the result of a deal with a higher being? A curse put on them by a practiced mage? An artificer’s experiment on humanoid test subjects? These may have all been reasons for the extensive energy you find yourself containing. In addition, think about the method that you used to channel your energy. How you managed to circumvent your energy destroying you internally is just as important as how you managed to receive that energy in the first place.

## Class Features

As a channeler, you gain the following class features.

### Hit Points

* * *

**Hit Dice:** 1d10 per channeler level

**Hit Points at 1st Level**: 10 + your Constitution Modifier

**Hit Points at Higher Levels**: 1d10 (or 6) + your

Constitution modifier per (your class) level after 1st.

### Proficiencies

* * *

**Armor:** Light armor, medium armor

**Weapons:** Simple weapons, longswords, rapiers

**Tools:** None

**Saving Throws:** Constitution, Intelligence

**Skills:** Choose two from Acrobatics, Arcana, Athletics,

Insight, Intimidation, Investigation, Perception, and

Survival.

#### Equipment

* * *

You start with the following equipment, in addition to the equipment granted by your background:

*   *(a)* chain mail and a shield (if proficient) or *(b)* scale mail or *(c)* leather armor and 2 daggers
*   (a) a longsword or *(b)* a rapier or *(c)* a martial weapon (if proficient)
*   *(a)* a light crossbow and 20 bolts or *(b)* two handaxes
*   *(a)* a scholar’s pack or *(b)* an explorer’s pack

### Spell Casting Ability

Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for your channeler spells. The way you channel your innate abilities relies greatly on your experience. You use your Intelligence whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability. In addition, you use your Intelligence modifier when setting the saving throw DC for a channeler spell you cast and when making an attack roll with one

**Spell save DC** = 8 + your Proficiency Bonus + your Intelligence modifier

**Spell Attack modifier** = your Proficiency Bonus + your Intelligence modifier

### Multiclassing

*Ability Score Minimum: STR 13 or DEX 13, and INT 13*

**Armor:** light armor, medium armor

**Weapons:** simple weapons, longswords, rapiers

### Arcane Channel

Choose one outlet in which to channel your energy. Your choice grants you certain features and proficiencies when you choose it at 1st level. It also grants you additional features at 6th, 11th, and 17th level. The choices are detailed at the bottom of this document.

*   [Channel of Artistry](/docs/channel-of-artistry "channel of artistry")
*   [Channel of Madness](/docs/channel-of-madness "channel of madness")
*   [Channel of Volatility](/docs/channel-of-volatility "channel of volatility")
*   [Channel of Containment](/docs/channel-of-containment "channel of containment")
*   [Channel of Devastation](/docs/channel-of-devastation "channel of devastation")

### Elemental Imbuement

Your arcane energy is beginning to leak uncontrollably, forcing you to find a focus to channel it through. Starting at 1st level, whenever you finish a long rest, you can imbue your energy in one weapon you are proficient with. Choose a damage type from acid, fire, cold, lightning, or thunder. The weapon deals an additional 1d4 damage of the chosen type. This damage increases when you reach certain levels in this class: to 1d6 at 10th level, and to 1d8 at 17th level.

When an effect would deal damage using your Elemental Imbuement die, the damage type is the same as your Elemental Imbuement unless stated otherwise.

### Arcane Attraction

At 2nd level, your internal magical power tends to seek out various sources through which to channel itself to, allowing you to get a sense for what is magical and what’s not. As an action, you can sense the presence of magic in the area. Until the end of your next turn, you know the location of each source of magic within 30 feet of you— whether it’s a magic item, a spell, or a creature created by a spell. You know which kind of magic source it is, but you do not know the details of the source; you can’t tell the type of magic item, the specifics of the spell, or the kind of creature.

Once you use this feature, you cannot use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

### Fighting Style

At 2nd level, you adopt a style of fighting as your specialty. Choose one of the following options. You can’t take a Fighting Style option more than once, even if something in the game lets you choose again.

#### Archery

You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls you make with ranged weapons.

#### Dueling.

When you are wielding a melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons, you gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls with that weapon.

#### Great Weapon Fighting.

When you roll a 1 or 2 on a damage die for an attack you make with a melee weapon that you are wielding with two hands, you can reroll the die and must use the new roll, even if the new roll is a 1 or a 2. The weapon must have the two-handed or versatile property for you to gain this benefit.

#### Two-Weapon Fighting.

When you engage in two-weapon fighting, you can add your ability modifier to the damage of the second attack.

#### Unarmed Fighting

Your unarmed strikes can deal bludgeoning damage equal to 1d6 + your Strength modifier on a hit. If you aren’t wielding any weapons or a shield when you make the attack roll, the d6 becomes a d8.

At the start of each of your turns, you can deal 1d4 bludgeoning damage to one creature grappled by you.

### Ante

At 3rd level, your elemental energy burns and crackles within you, proving to be a fickle source of power. You can use the energy to empower yourself, but you run the risk of your power backfiring on you. You gain three Ante options of your choice, detailed at the bottom of this document. You gain another at 7th level, 12th level, and 17th level.

You have an amount of Ante charges equal to your Intelligence modifier. Each use of an Ante consumes a charge. You may not use more than one Ante at the same time. You replenish Ante charges on a short or long rest.

Additionally, when you gain a level in this class, you may replace one Ante option with another that you meet the prerequisites for. A level prerequisite refers to your level in this class.

### Ability score improvement

When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can’t increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

### Overchanneled Imbuement

Starting at 5th level, you gain more control over the energy channeled through your weapon of choice. Whenever you finish a long rest, choose two cantrips that share a damage type with your chosen Elemental Imbuement and have a casting time of one action or bonus action. Immediately after you hit with an attack from your imbued weapon, you may cast one of the chosen cantrips as a bonus action. If a spell allows you to choose the damage type you deal, the chosen type must match your Elemental Imbuement.

At 9th level, you may choose two 1st level spells that share a damage type with your chosen Elemental Imbuement. You may cast either of those spells rather than a cantrip without expending a spell slot.

### Elemental Boon

Starting at 7th level, your experience with channeling your energy through weapons is beginning to allow you to channel your energy through living beings as well. As a reaction whenever another ally within 30 feet hits with an attack roll, you can channel your energy through their attack. The attack deals bonus damage equal to your Elemental Imbuement die, and shares the damage type you’d chosen for your Elemental Imbuement.

You may use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier, and you regain all expended uses on a short or long rest.

### Raze

Starting at 10th level, your imbued weapons are growing in destructive force, letting you use them to tear objects to shreds. Attacks made using your imbued weapons deal twice as much damage to objects and structures.

### Up The Ante

Starting at 13th level, the power of your Antes grows, but so do their destructive effects. When you use an Ante, you may choose to Up The Ante, making you roll your Elemental Imbuement die an extra time, adding it to the effect. If the Ante cares about whether the total is even or odd, rather than adding the die an extra time, you instead may use either roll.

Once you use this feature, you must finish a short or long rest before you can use it again.

### Flash Charge

At 14th level, you’ve learned to quickly and deftly control the elemental energies inside of you. Once per day, as an action, you may change the damage type of your Elemental Imbuement, as well as the spells you’d chosen for your Overchanneled Imbuement. You may optionally also choose to imbue your energy in a different weapon.

### Greater Boon

At 15th level, the boons you bestow upon others strengthen to their peak. The elemental boon now deals extra damage equal to twice your Elemental Imbuement die, and the attack ignores damage resistances.

### Ruin

At 18th level, your magic energies can bring down the very foundations of structures around you. By spending one minute concentrating as if concentrating on a spell, you can plunge your imbued weapon into the ground and begin breaking the foundations of nearby buildings.

Once one minute has passed, choose a non-magical object or structure within 60 feet. If that structure can fit within a 30 foot cube, it is immediately destroyed, crumbling to dust. If it is any larger, you can instead destroy a 30 foot cube portion of it within range.

### Pinnacle Channel

At 20th level, you’ve reached the height of elemental control, and can display your mastery on a consistent basis. At the end of a long rest, choose two 2nd or 3rd level spells that share a damage type with your Elemental Imbuement; you may cast those spells at will or any time you can cast one of your Overchanneled Imbuements.

If you use your Flash Charge feature, you may also exchange these spells for any others that fit the prerequisites. Additionally, you may upcast your 1st and 2nd level channelers spells to 3rd level.

## Antes

*Antes are a core part of a channeler’s toolkit, and are the representation of their capricious magic. Each ante provides some sort of benefit to you that can help you accomplish certain tasks or utilize certain abilities, but every single one runs the risk of backfiring, leading to inconvencience, injury, or even certain death.*

*A good channeler knows when to use an ante and when not to take needless risks, but each and every channeler would do well to understand that no matter how many steps one might take to mitigate risk, chances of failure are never zero.*

#### Pinpoint Ante

When you make an attack roll, you may add your Elemental Imbuement die to it. If the attack misses, your AC decreases by an amount equal to the Elemental Imbuement die’s result until the end of your next turn.

#### Reflex Ante

When you make a dexterity saving throw, you may add your Elemental Imbuement die to the save. If the save fails, you take damage equal to twice the Elemental Imbuement die’s result.

#### Prodigious Ante

When you make a skill check in a skill you’re not proficient in, you may add your Elemental Imbuement die to the check. If the check fails, you subtract the Elemental Imbuement die’s result from all ability checks for the next hour.

#### Quickstep Ante

When someone makes an opportunity attack against you, you may add your Elemental Imbuement die to your AC. If the attack hits, your speed is reduced by five times the result until the end of your turn.

#### Blasting Ante (Prerequisite: Channel of Devastation)

When you make an attack roll, you may roll your Elemental Imbuement die. If the attack hits, each creature of your choice other than the target within 15 feet takes damage equal to the result. If the attack misses, each creature other than the target within 15 feet of you takes damage equal to the result.

#### Mindmeld Ante (Prerequisite: Channel of Madness)

When a creature makes a saving throw against a channeler spell you cast, you may subtract your Elemental Imbuement die from the save. If the save succeeds, you become the spell’s target, and your saving throw against the spell’s effect is reduced by your Elemental Imbuement die’s result. If the spell would require you to concentrate, it doesn’t require concentration when cast against you.

#### Curtain-raiser Ante (Prerequisite: Channel of Artistry)

When you roll initiative, you may add your Elemental Imbuement die to the result. If you’re in the top half of initiative, you have advantage on attack rolls against each creature below you in the initiative order until the end of your next turn. If you’re in the bottom half of initiative, each creature above you in initiative has advantage on attack rolls against you until the end of your next turn.

#### Unstable Ante (Prerequisite: Channel of Volatility)

Before you make an attack roll, call if the result will be even or odd. If the result matches your guess, add another Elemental Imbuement die to your Volatile Charge feature. If the results don’t match your guess, you take damage equal to one roll of your Elemental Imbuement die.

#### Fray Ante (Prerequisite: Channel of Containment)

If you’re within 5 feet of an enemy, roll your Elemental Imbuement die. If the result is even, you regain hit points equal to three times the roll’s result. If the result is odd, your AC decreases by the roll’s result.

#### Arcane Ante (Prerequisite: 5th Level)

Whenever you cast a channeler spell, you may use this Ante. If the spell hits or if the target fails the save against it, you may add two rolls of your Elemental Imbuement die to that spell’s damage. If the spell misses or the target passes the save, you take damage equal to one roll of your Elemental Imbuement die.

#### Reactive Ante (Prerequisite: 5th Level)

Whenever an attack roll is made against you, you may use this Ante as a reaction. Roll your Elemental Imbuement die; if the result is even, you gain immunity to the triggering attack’s damage until the end of the turn. If the result is odd, you gain vulnerability to the triggering attack’s damage until the end of the turn.

#### Goading Ante (Prerequisite: 7th Level, Channel of Devastation)

When a creature rolls a saving throw against your Devastating Presence feature, you may force them to subtract your Elemental Imbuement die from the result. If they succeed, they add the Elemental Imbuement die’s result to their attack and damage rolls that target you until the end of their turn.

#### Headspin Ante (Prerequisite: 7th Level, Channel of Madness)

If a creature within 30 feet of you rolls an Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma saving throw with advantage, you may add your Elemental Imbuement die to the result. If they fail the save, you subtract the Elemental Imbuement die’s result from your saving throws for the next minute.

#### Blitzkrieg Ante (Prerequisite: 7th Level, Channel of Artistry)

If you make an attack roll against a creature, you may roll your Elemental Imbuement die. If you hit, your speed increases by an amount equal to five times the result until the end of your turn. If you miss, you fall prone and your speed is reduced by an amount equal to five times the result until the end of your turn.

#### Surge Ante (Prerequisite: 7th Level, Channel of Volatility)

After you roll on the Volatile Surge table, you may use this ante. Roll your Elemental Imbuement die and call if the result will be odd or even. If you guess correctly, you may use the effects of both the new and the old roll. If you guess incorrectly, you may use neither effect.

#### Grasp Ante (Prerequisite: 7th Level, Channel of Containment)

When you make a Strength (athletics) check to grapple a creature, you can add your Elemental Imbuement die to the check’s result. If the creature succeeds against the grapple, you are restrained until the start of your next turn.

#### Mental Ante (Prerequisite: 11th Level)

When you make an Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma saving throw, you may reduce the result by your Elemental Imbuement die. If you pass the save, you may add the Elemental Imbuement die’s result to the failed saving throw of a creature of your choice within 60 feet of you.

#### Proactive Ante (Prerequisite: 11th Level)

Whenever an attack roll is made against a creature other than you within 60 feet, you may use this Ante as a reaction. Roll your Elemental Imbuement die; if the result is even, they gain resistance to the triggering attack’s damage until the end of the turn. If the result is odd, you take damage equal to the triggering attack’s damage.

#### Sticky Ante (Prerequisite: 13th Level, Channel of Devastation)

You may use this Ante at the start of a creature’s turn. If the creature ends their turn within 5 feet of you, they take damage equal to three rolls of your Elemental Imbuement die. If the creature ends their turn more than 5 feet away from you, you take force damage equal to three rolls of your Elemental Imbuement die.

#### Hivemind Ante (Prerequisite: 13th Level, Channel of Madness)

When a creature you can see within 60 feet of you makes a saving throw or ability check that they’re not proficient in, if you’re proficient in that saving throw or ability check, you can roll your Elemental Imbuement die and add the result to their roll. If they fail, you take force damage equal to three times the result.

#### Haphephobic Ante (Prerequisite: 13th Level, Channel of Artistry)

When you hit with an attack roll, you may roll your Elemental Imbuement die. If the target damages you before the start of your next turn, you take force damage equal to five times the result. If the target does not damage you before the start of your next turn, they take damage equal to five times the result.

#### Anti-Ante (Prerequisite: 13th Level, Channel of Volatility)

You may use this ante immediately after using another ante. Roll your Elemental Imbuement die; if the result is even, neither this Ante nor that Ante consumes an Ante charge. If the result is odd, you lose one Ante charge.

#### Stance Ante (Prerequisite: 13th Level, Channel of Containment)

When a creature hits you with an attack, roll a concentration check and add your Elemental Imbuement die. If the check succeeds, you gain resistance to the attack’s damage. If the check fails, you gain vulnerability to the attack’s damage.

#### Gambler’s Ante (Prerequisite: 18th Level)

When you use your last Ante charge, you may roll your Elemental Imbuement die; If the result is even, you regain an amount of Ante charges equal to the roll. This amount cannot exceed your maximum. If the result is odd, your hit point total is set to 1. You may only use this Ante once per long rest.

#### God Ante (Prerequisite: 18th Level)

You may use this Ante as an action. Roll your Elemental Imbuement die; if the result is even, you may immediately cast a ninth level spell with a casting time of one action from any spell list without expending a spell slot. If the result is odd, you die and cannot be revived except by the effects of a wish spell. You may only use this Ante once per long rest.

#### Warmind Ante (Prerequisite: 20th Level, Channel of Devastation)

When you make a melee weapon attack, you may roll your Elemental Imbuement die. Up to that many other willing creatures of your choice within 30 feet of you may make one melee weapon attack as a reaction. If any of them miss, each creature takes damage equal to five times the result.

#### InsanAnte (Prerequisite: 20th Level, Channel of Madness)

When you cast an enchantment spell on one or more creatures, you may roll your Elemental Imbuement die. If they fail the save, they take psychic damage equal to four times the result, and if any of them pass the save, you take force damage equal to four times the result.

#### Masterpiece Ante (Prerequisite: 20th Level, Channel of Artistry)

When you use your Flash Charge feature, roll your Elemental Imbuement die. The next time you cast a channeler spell, if it hits or if the spell’s target fails its save against it, you gain hit points and temporary hit points equal to three times the result. If it doesn’t hit or the target’s save is successful, you take damage equal to three times the result.

#### Soulshredder Ante (Prerequisite: 20th Level, Channel of Volatility)

When you use your Culmination of Volatility feature, you may roll your Elemental Imbuement die. If the result is even, rather than dying immediately, you regain half of your maximum hit points and your gear and equipment aren’t destroyed. If the result is odd, your soul is shredded into nonexistence and you cannot be revived by any means, even through divine intervention or a wish spell.

#### Havoc Ante (Prerequisite: 20th Level, Channel of Containment)

When you make an unarmed strike against a creature, they must make a strength saving throw against your spell save DC. Roll your Elemental Imbuement die and subtract it from the save. If they fail, they are knocked back a distance equal to ten times the die’s result. If they succeed, you are knocked back a distance equal to ten times the die’s result. The one knocked back by this effect takes 1d6 bludgeoning damage for every 10 feet they traveled.

Chaos Domain

# Chaos Domain

*In the domain of chaos, where whims rule and order trembles, the chosen clerics of the Chaos Domain walk a path paved with unpredictability and capriciousness. Whether by the whispered machinations of trickster deities or the primal forces of wild gods, these clerics wield the very essence of chaos itself.*

## Chaos Domain Spells

You gain domain spells at the cleric levels listed in the Chaos Domain Spells table. See the Divine Domain class feature for how domain spells work.


Chaos Domain Spells
Cleric LevelSpells
1stChaos Bolt, Dissonant Whispers
3rdMirror Image, Enlarge/Reduce
5thHaste, Slow
7thConfusion, Polymorph
9thDominate Person, Modify Memory


## Chaos Bonus Proficiencies

When you choose this domain at 1st level, you gain proficiency with martial weapons.

## Channel Divinity: Chaos Warp

Starting at 2nd level, you can use your Channel Divinity to warp probability around you, causing unpredictable effects. As an action, you present your holy symbol and a number of creatures equal to your wisdom modifier you can see within 30 feet of you. Those creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your cleric spell save DC or be affected by one of the following effects rolled randomly for each target:


Chaos Table
1d4Effect
1The target takes 2d10 psychic damage.
2The target is blinded until the end of its next turn.
3The target is charmed by you until the end of its next turn.
4The target is frightened until the end of its next turn.


## Twisted Fate

At 6th level, your connection to the chaos that drives your every action allows you to twist the threads of fate in the world around you. As a reaction when a creature within 30 feet of you excluding yourself fails a saving throw, or check or misses an attack roll, the triggering creature must reroll the d20 and use the higher roll.

You can then choose a different creature you can see within 30 feet range. The chosen creature has disadvantage on the next attack roll, ability check, or saving throw it makes within 1 minute.

You can use this reaction a number of times equal to your Wisdom Modifier (minimum of once) per long rest.

## Divine Strike: Chaos Domain

At 8th level, you gain the ability to infuse your weapon strikes with divine energy. Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 psychic damage to the target. When you reach 14th level, the extra damage increases to 2d8.

## Empowered Warp

At 17th level, when you use your Chaos Warp, rather than rolling 1d4, roll 2d4. Each target that fails the save is affected by both effects you rolled.

If both d4s land on the same number, the target makes the saving throw with disadvantage and the effect is enhanced as follows:


Empowered Chaos Table
d4Effect
1The target takes 4d10 psychic damage and can't use reactions until the end of its next turn
2The Target is blinded and subtracts 1d6 from any attack roll or saving throw it makes until the end of its next turn.
3The target is under the effects of the confusion spell until the end of its next turn.
4The target is under the effects of the slow spell until the end of its next turn.

Circle Of Beauty

# Circle of Beauty

## Description

*Druids of the Circle of Beauty vow to protect all that brings beauty in nature. From the patterns of symmetry designed to attract mates, to visual marks designed to confuse camouflage observers, these are all sights that should be admired and emulated. They marvel at how nature seamlessly blends together both form and function in a way man-made objects never could.*

Features:

*   [2nd Level - Circle Spells](#2nd_Level_-_Circle_Spells)
*   [2nd Level - Natural Form](#2nd_Level_-_Natural_Form)
*   [6th Level - Blessing of Symmetry](#6th_Level_-_Blessing_of_Symmetry)
*   [10th Level - Beautiful Plummage](#10th_Level_-_Beautiful_Plummage)
*   [14th Level - Radiant Appearance](#14th_Level_-_Radiant_Appearance)

## 2nd Level - Circle Spells

Your eye for all things visually harmonious grants you access to some spells when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown on the **Circle of Beauty Spells** table.

Once you gain access to one of these spells, you always have it prepared, and it doesn't count against the number of spells you can prepare each day. If you gain access to a spell that doesn't appear on the druid spell list, the spell is nonetheless a druid spell for you.


Druid LevelSpell
2nddisguise self, feather fall
3rdhealing spirit, lesser restoration
5thdaylight, hypnotic pattern
7thcharm monster, compulsion
9thcreation, dawn


## 2nd Level - Natural Form

As a bonus action, you can expend one use of your Wild Shape feature to draw upon nature's splendour, rather than assuming a Beast form.

While in your natural form and wearing no armour, your Armour Class equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Wisdom modifier. You can use a shield and still gain this benefit. The form lasts for 10 minutes. It ends early if you dismiss it (no action required), if you die, or you use this feature again.

Whenever you assume your natural form, choose the manner of natural appearance your body transforms into; your choice gives you certain benefits while in the form.

**Attractive.** When you enter this form, and as a bonus action on your subsequent turns while in this form, you can choose a creature you can see within 30 feet of you. That creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC or be charmed by you until the start of your next turn. The target automatically succeeds on its saving throw if it can't see you. It stops being charmed by you if it is attacked or damaged, or if another creature shakes it awake as a bonus action.

**Camouflage.** You can take the Hide action as a bonus action. You can attempt to hide even when you are only lightly obscured by foliage, heavy rain, falling snow, mist, and other natural phenomena.

**Verdant.** When you enter this form, and as a bonus action on your subsequent turns, you can choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of you (including yourself). That creature gains your Wisdom modifier temporary hit points, which lasts until the start of your next turn.

## 6th Level - Blessing of Symmetry

6th-level Circle of Beauty feature

You strive to emulate the symmetry that occurs in the natural world. When you or a willing creature you can see within 60 feet of you casts a spell with a casting time of 1 action and which deals damage to at least one target, the caster can forgo rolling damage dice and take the average damage. If the spell has an ongoing effect that can deal damage after it is cast, this feature only applies to the initial instance of damage.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

## 10th Level - Beautiful Plummage

The transformation into your natural form is now even more stunning. Your forms gain extra benefits as detailed below:

**Attractive.** When you charm a creature, you can command it as part of the same bonus action to use its reaction to move up to its speed and make one weapon attack against a target you can see. After the creature completes this command, it is no longer charmed by you.

**Camouflage.** When you use the Hide action and are also obscured by natural foliage or phenomena, you become invisible until you move away from the natural foliage, or until you make an attack or cast a spell.

**Verdant.** The you roll is now a when you give temporary hit points. While a creature has these temporary hit points, it has a bonus to AC and advantage on Constitution saving throws.

Moreover, at the start of each of your turns while in your Natural Form, you can change the appearance of your form. If you do, any effects from your previous form immediately end.

## 14th Level - Radiant Appearance

Your body's aesthetically pleasing patterns and colours make others hesitate to harm you. When a creature makes an attack roll against you while in your Natural Form, you can use your reaction and force it to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the creature can't attack you with an attack or harmful spell until the end of its current turn, and must choose a new target or lose the attack or spell (this doesn't protect you from area effects, such as the explosion of15 a *fireball*).

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

Circle Of Bravery

# Circle of Bravery

*5th-level abjuration. (Bard, Druid, Cleric, Paladin)*

**Meta**

**Casting Time**: 1 action

**Range**: Self (30 ft. sphere)

**Components**: V, S

**Duration**: Concentration, up to 10 minutes

**Description**

You conjure a field of magical inspiration that bolsters the resolve of your allies. Any creature you choose while they are in this field will have resistance against psychic damage and gain advantage on saving throws against the frightened and charmed conditions. If their save fails, any additional saves against the same effect succeed automatically.

Circle Of The Sky

# Circle of the Sky

## Circle of the Sky

### Circle Spells

Your connection to the sky allows you to learn certain spells. At 2nd level, you learn the gust cantrip, and it does not count towards the number of druid cantrips you know. At 3rd, 5th, 7th, and 9th level you gain access to the spells listed for that level in the Circle of the Sky Spells table. Once you gain access to one of these spells, you always have it prepared, and it doesn’t count against the number of spells you can prepare each day. If you gain access to a spell that doesn’t appear on the druid spell list, the spell is nonetheless a druid spell for you.


Circle of the Sky Spell
Druid LevelSpells
3rdAir Bubble, Skywrite
5thFly, Lightning Bolt
7thConjure Minor Elementals, Freedom of Movement
9thControl Winds, Steel Wind Strike


### Where the Wind Guides

Your connection to the winds allows you to reliably control vehicles and other structures that rely on them. You gain proficiency in either water or air vehicles (your choice), and while you are aboard such a vehicle, you always have optimal wind for traveling to your intended destination.

### Early Bird

When you use your wildshape feature, the beasts you transform into can have a flying speed.

### Gale Force

At 6th level, you gain the ability to harness windflow to greater effect. Any wind powered structure, such as a wind mill, works at twice it's normal rate while within 5 miles of you. Additionally, water or air vehicles that utilize sails move twice as fast while you are on board.

Additionally, whenever you roll lightning, thunder, or bludgeoning damage from a druid spell you cast, you add your wisdom modifier to the total damage.

### Jetstream

When you reach 10th level, you gain the ability to move with a supernatural flow. Your walking speed increases by 15 feet, as does any flying speed of creatures you transform into with your wildshape feature. Additionally, you are considered to always be under the effects of the Freedom of Movement spell.

### The Sky's the Limit

At 14th level, your connection to the skies has given you a shared connection with all life that calls it home. When you use your wildshape feature, you can transform into any Beast, Dragon, or Monstrosity of Challenge Rating 4 or lower, so long as it has a flying speed.

Circle Of The Verdant Stalker

# Circle of the Verdant Stalker

## Description

Druids of the Circle of the Verdant Stalker embody the lethal stillness and precision of Chult’s jungles. They move like serpents through the undergrowth, striking with poison and vanishing into foliage.

**Features:**

*   [3rd Level - Circle Spells](#3rd_Level_-_Circle_Spells)
*   [3rd Level - Venomous Marksman](#3rd_Level_-_Venomous_Marksman)
*   [6th Level - Aspect of the Striking Viper](#6th_Level_-_Aspect_of_the_Striking_Viper)
*   [10th Level - Primal Toxin Mastery](#10th_Level_-_Primal_Toxin_Mastery)
*   [14th Level - Deathbloom Aspect](#14th_Level_-_Deathbloom_Aspect)

## 3rd Level - Circle Spells

Your eye for all things visually harmonious grants you access to some spells when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown on the **Circle of Beauty Spells** table.

Once you gain access to one of these spells, you always have it prepared, and it doesn't count against the number of spells you can prepare each day. If you gain access to a spell that doesn't appear on the druid spell list, the spell is nonetheless a druid spell for you.


Druid LevelSpell
3rdEnsnaring Strike, Poison Spray
5thPlant Growth, Spike Growth
7thGrasping Vine, Stoneskin
9thTree Stride, Cloudkill (reflavored as a deadly plant toxin cloud)


## 3rd Level - Venomous Marksman

Your training centers on the precision and toxins of Chult’s hidden hunters.

*   **Blowgun Proficiency.** You gain proficiency with the blowgun. You can use your Wisdom modifier instead of Dexterity for blowgun attack and damage rolls.

*   **Toxin-Enhanced Blowgun.** Your blowgun darts deal greater piercing damage as you grow in mastery.


Druid LevelBlowgun Damage Die
3rd1d6
10th1d8
14th1d10


*   **Poisoner’s Tools.** You gain proficiency with the Poisoner’s Kit.
*   **Tough to Toxin.** You have resistance to poison damage and advantage on saving throws against being poisoned.
*   **Jungle Stride.** You ignore nonmagical plant-based difficult terrain.

## 6th Level - Aspect of the Striking Viper

You channel primal ferocity into a deadly, focused trance.

**Activation.** As a bonus action, expend one use of your Wild Shape to enter this Aspect for 1 minute. You can’t activate it again until you finish a short or long rest.

While in this Aspect:

*   Your blowgun attacks deal an extra 1d8 poison damage.
*   When you take the attack action to attack with your blowgun, hit or miss, you can make another attack with the blowgun.
*   If your first blowgun attack each turn hits, the target must make a Constitution saving throw (DC = your spell save DC) or be Poisoned until the end of its next turn.
*   A creature that fails this saving throw also takes an additional 1d8 poison damage.

## 10th Level - Primal Toxin Mastery

Your venoms pierce the resilience of even the hardiest foes.

*   **Toxic Bypass.** Your blowgun attacks and druid spells that deal poison damage treat immunity to poison damage as resistance instead.

*   **Debilitating Barrage.** When you hit a creature that is Poisoned, you can use your reaction to force it to make a Constitution saving throw (DC = your spell save DC). On a failed save, it is Blinded until the end of its next turn. You can use this reaction a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, regaining all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

## 14th Level - Deathbloom Aspect

Your mastery of venom and motion refines your Aspect of the Striking Viper into the Deathbloom Aspect.

When you activate your Aspect of the Striking Viper, it gains the following additional benefits:

*   **Deadly Venoms.** Your extra poison damage increases to 1d10, and creatures make their Constitution saving throws against your poison with disadvantage.
*   **Viper’s Retreat.** Immediately after you hit a creature with a blowgun attack, you can move up to half your speed without provoking opportunity attacks from that creature.
*   **Predator’s Cunning.** While the Aspect is active, you can take the Hide or Disengage action as a bonus action. You can also attempt to Hide even when you are only partially obscured by natural terrain, foliage, or dim light.
*   **Toxic Perfection.** You are immune to poison damage and the poisoned condition.

Circle Of The Warden

# Circle of the Warden

*There are many things that need to be protected, some worth protecting with your life. Druids of this circle protect the natural order of the wilderness with their lives, often picking a place and staking their claim there for a large portion of their lives. Though they protect the land, the spirits of nature see fit to protect them in turn, the mark of a true Warden.*

## Improved Shillelagh

At 2nd level, you unlock the versatile potential within the Shillelagh cantrip. You learn the shillelagh cantrip. If you already know this cantrip, you may learn a different cantrip of your choice from the druid spell list. Whenever you roll initiative and are not surprised, you can choose to immediately cast shillelagh as a reaction.

Additionally, when you cast shillelagh, you can choose to change the damage type of the weapon to bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage for the duration of the spell. The form of the weapon changes to suit the damage type chosen.

## Warden Form

At 2nd level, you can allow the spirits of nature to wreath your form in protective foliage to enhance your endurance and martial capability. As a bonus action, you can expend a use of Wild Shape to take on your warden form. Warden form lasts for 10 minutes. It ends early if you are incapacitated or until you dismiss it (as a free action). While in warden form, you gain the following benefits:

*   When you enter warden form, you gain temporary hit points equal to your wisdom modifier + double your druid level
*   Your AC is equal to 13 + your constitution modifier.
*   You are immune to all plant-based difficult terrain, as well as all difficult terrain created by Druid spells you cast.

## Forestwalker

At 6th level, you become a natural at navigating the rugged and tough terrain of forests. You gain the following benefits while in warden form:

*   You are resistant to damage from Druid spells you cast.
*   You gain a bonus to all strength checks and saves equal to your wisdom modifier while in warden form.

Additionally, you are now unaffected by plant-based difficult terrain when not in warden form.

## Extra Attack

At 6th level, when you take the attack action, you can make an additional weapon attack as part of the same action. You can replace up to one of these attacks with one of your druid cantrips.

## Arboreal Fortitude

At 10th level, the fortitude of your warden form grows to reflect a growth in your resolve. While in warden form, you gain a bonus to any constitution saving throw you make to maintain concentration on a spell. This bonus is equal to your wisdom modifier. If you begin your turn in warden form and do not have any temporary hit points, you gain temporary hit points equal to your wisdom modifier.

Additionally, you have adapted your warden form to be fluid and fierce. You gain the following additional benefits while in warden form:

*   Your walking speed increased by 10 feet.
*   Your melee weapon attacks deal an extra 1d8 force damage on a hit.

## Protector’s Resolve

At 14th level, your will is inseparable from that of the forest, and you learn to command nature as your blade. You always have the wrath of nature spell prepared, and it doesn't count against the total number of druid spells you have prepared. Additionally, as part of the same bonus action that you take to activate your warden form, you may cast this spell. If you cast wrath of nature in this way, it will not require concentration, and the area of effect remains centered on and moves with you throughout the duration of the spell. You may only cast this spell in this way once per long rest.

Claw Caps

# Claw caps

*Weapon: Martial, Melee*

*5gp, 1 lbs. --- 1d4 Slashing - Finesse, Light, Nick*

**Finesse:** When making an attack with a finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

**Light:** A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.

**Special:** Claw Caps can be fitted over a creature's natural weapons as an action changing it's base statistics and properties to match that of the natural weapon it is fitted over, it then gains a new weapon mastery depending on the natural weapon's damage type:

*   Bludgeoning: Topple
*   Piercing: Sap
*   Slashing: Vex

*Natural Weapons with Claw Caps cannot be used to make unarmed strikes.*

Clawshot

# Clawshot

*Wondrous Item, very rare*

**Description**

This magical item is made up of two distinct parts, the first being a claw-like structure, and the second being a handle. The two pieces fit together in such a way that the open claw points away from your hand when held.

While you are holding the handle, and the claw is attached, you can use a bonus action to speak the items command word, causing the claw to be magically propelled up to 30 feet. When you do so, you may target either an object or creature. At the end of the claws journey, the claw embeds itself harmlessly into the target and two pieces are magically pulled back together. If the target object or creature is either your size or larger, you are pulled up to 30 feet towards it. Inversely, if the target object or creature is at least one size smaller than you, it is pulled up to 30 feet towards you.

If the target is a creature, make an attack roll with a +10 bonus against its armor class. If the attack misses, the claw returns to the handle without moving either you or the target. This movement does not provoke opportunity attacks.

The clawshot is a magical device first invented by the artificer Flare. The clawshot is used for quick movement and repositioning, and for getting past otherwise impassable obstacles. It is also quite effective in low gravity environments, like those it's inventor commonly reside.

> *"It's perfectly safe to use, I swear. Just look at me, I use it all the time!" - Flare*

Cleric Subclasses

# Cleric Subclasses

## Chaos Domain

*In the domain of chaos, where whims rule and order trembles, the chosen clerics of the Chaos Domain walk a path paved with unpredictability and capriciousness. Whether by the whispered machinations of trickster deities or the primal forces of wild gods, these clerics wield the very essence of chaos itself.*

### Chaos Domain Spells

You gain domain spells at the cleric levels listed in the Chaos Domain Spells table. See the Divine Domain class feature for how domain spells work.


Chaos Domain Spells
Cleric LevelSpells
1stChaos Bolt, Dissonant Whispers
3rdMirror Image, Enlarge/Reduce
5thHaste, Slow
7thConfusion, Polymorph
9thDominate Person, Modify Memory


### Chaos Bonus Proficiencies

When you choose this domain at 1st level, you gain proficiency with martial weapons.

### Channel Divinity: Chaos Warp

Starting at 2nd level, you can use your Channel Divinity to warp probability around you, causing unpredictable effects. As an action, you present your holy symbol and a number of creatures equal to your wisdom modifier you can see within 30 feet of you. Those creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your cleric spell save DC or be affected by one of the following effects rolled randomly for each target:


Chaos Table
1d4Effect
1The target takes 2d10 psychic damage.
2The target is blinded until the end of its next turn.
3The target is charmed by you until the end of its next turn.
4The target is frightened until the end of its next turn.


### Twisted Fate

At 6th level, your connection to the chaos that drives your every action allows you to twist the threads of fate in the world around you. As a reaction when a creature within 30 feet of you excluding yourself fails a saving throw, or check or misses an attack roll, the triggering creature must reroll the d20 and use the higher roll.

You can then choose a different creature you can see within 30 feet range. The chosen creature has disadvantage on the next attack roll, ability check, or saving throw it makes within 1 minute.

You can use this reaction a number of times equal to your Wisdom Modifier (minimum of once) per long rest.

### Divine Strike: Chaos Domain

At 8th level, you gain the ability to infuse your weapon strikes with divine energy. Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 psychic damage to the target. When you reach 14th level, the extra damage increases to 2d8.

### Empowered Warp

At 17th level, when you use your Chaos Warp, rather than rolling 1d4, roll 2d4. Each target that fails the save is affected by both effects you rolled.

If both d4s land on the same number, the target makes the saving throw with disadvantage and the effect is enhanced as follows:


Empowered Chaos Table
d4Effect
1The target takes 4d10 psychic damage and can't use reactions until the end of its next turn
2The Target is blinded and subtracts 1d6 from any attack roll or saving throw it makes until the end of its next turn.
3The target is under the effects of the confusion spell until the end of its next turn.
4The target is under the effects of the slow spell until the end of its next turn.

Cloak Of Levitation

# Cloak of Levitation

*Wondrous Item, legendary*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement by a Sorcerer, Wizard, or Warlock of a Non-Evil Alignment

**Description**

The Cloak of Levitation is one of many items imbued with power to sustain magics that practitioners of the mystic arts cannot. While you wear this cloak, you have a fly speed equal to your walking speed. If you fall while in the air when wearing this cloak, you descend at a rate of 30 feet per round until you land, taking no fall damage. If you are not wearing armor, your base Armor Class is 13 + your Dexterity modifier while wearing this cloak. When a melee attack made against you would reduce you to 20 points or lower, you can use your reaction to have the cloak fly off of your shoulders and immediately attempt a grapple against the attacker. The cloak counts as a medium sized creature for the purposes of grappling and has a +8 modifier to Strength (Athletics) checks made to grapple. If the the grapple is successful the attacker also gains the Blinded condition for the duration of the grapple. As a bonus action you may call the cloak back to you if it is within 60 feet.

When targeted by an attack or by a feature that forces a Dexterity saving throw, you may use your reaction to have the cloak take the brunt of the attack, transferring half of the damage you take to the cloak. Once the cloak is used this way, it cannot be used to fly until the next dawn. Additionally, this property cannot be used again until the next dawn.

**Sentience**. The Cloak of Levitation is a sentient chaotic good wondrous item, with an Intelligence score of 16, a Wisdom score of 14, and a Charisma score of 17. It has hearing and blindsight out to a range of 120 feet. The cloak can communicate by transmitting basic emotion to its wielder and can communicate basic concepts through gestures. It can read and understand Common. While you are attuned to it, the Cloak of Levitation also understands every language you know.

**Personality**. The Cloak of Levitation has a flamboyant and playful personality, but is also a stout defender of its wearer. The cloak generally dislikes anyone of evil alignment, and tends to display said dislike to its wearer. The cloak's purpose is to protect its wearer, and assist in their pursuits in whatever way it can. The cloak is drawn towards assisting in academia and adventuring, being quick thinking in combat and unafraid to communicate its ideas in the study.

Cloud In A Bottle

# Cloud in a Bottle

*Uncommon, Wondrous*

**Description**

A swirling cloud sealed within a small glass bottle. While a Cloud in a Bottle is on your person, you gain the ability to jump while mid-air. Additionally, you can treat your long and high jumps as if you had moved 10 feet before doing so, even if you didn't. Once you jump in this way, you cannot do so again until you land on the ground.

Cold Cube

# Cold Cube

*Wondrous Item, common*

**Description**

This 3-inch cube is the direct counterpart of the thermal cube, it emanates cold to keep the temperature within 15 ft of it at 41 degrees Fahrenheit (5 degrees Celsius)

Cold Hypercube

# Cold Hypercube

*Wondrous Item, Legendary*

**Description**

This 3-inch cube is a direct counterpart of the Thermal Hypercube. The cube resonates slightly when touched, and emanates cold to keep the temperature within 30 ft of it at -20 degrees Fahrenheit (~-29 degrees Celcius). This area is also subject to extreme cold.

College Of Fables

# College of Fables

## Description

*Any bard can write a story or compose an epic, yet none with as much flair and verbal panache as one who studies under the college of fables. Such bards are masters at crafting narratives, telling tales, and captivating readers far and wide. They tell stories of that which was, that which is, and with enough creative magic, that which will be.*

**Features:**

*   [Level 3 - Natural Writer](#Level_3_-_Natural_Writer)
*   [Level 3 - Wordcraft](#Level_3_-_Wordcraft)
*   [Level 6 - Life Imitates Art](#Level_6_-_Life_Imitates_Art)
*   [Level 14 - Art Imitates Life](#Level_14_-_Art_Imitates_Life)

## Level 3 - Natural Writer

Beginning at 3rd level, you can spend at least one workweek of downtime (max. 5) and 10 gp of materials per week to write a story. When the story is complete, roll a d20 and add your charisma modifier, as well as a number of dice equal to the amount of workweeks you spent on the story to the roll. The value of the dice is the same as your bardic inspiration dice. The quality of the story you create depends on the result of the roll as shown below.

**Story Quality:**

Check Totals > Quality

*   1-10 > Poor
*   11-20 > Good
*   21-30 > Great
*   31-40 > Sensational
*   41+ > Genre-Definining


Depending on the quality of the book, your reputation as a writer may broaden. Your skills might be recognized by nobles and peasants alike, and you may receive invitations to events that are difficult to enter, and might be able to curry favors with your fame.

## Level 3 - Wordcraft

At 3rd level, when you cast a spell with only verbal components and no somatic or material components, you can expend one use of your bardic inspiration die to cast the spell as a story. When you do so, you may add your bardic inspiration die to one of the spell’s attack rolls, or subtract your bardic inspiration die from the saving throw of one creature affected by the spell. Alternatively, you can have a creature within 60 feet of you that can hear you gain temporary hit points equal to one roll of your bardic inspiration die.

## Level 6 - Life Imitates Art

At 6th level, you can spin together tales that can bend reality around them to an extent. As a reaction to when a creature fails an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw within 60 feet of you, you can describe that course of action succeeding. That creature can then reroll the d20 and use the new roll, potentially allowing it to succeed. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your charisma modifier. You regain any expended uses when you finish a long rest.

## Level 14 - Art Imitates Life

At 14th level, some of your tales are powerful enough to gain corporeal form. As an action, you can create a living story, the manifestation of your wordcraft, in an unoccupied space you can see within range. If you already have a living story, it disappears when the new one is created

The creature is an ally to you and your companions. In combat, the creature shares your initiative count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you). If you don’t issue any, it takes the Dodge action and uses its move to avoid danger.

You can only use this feature once per long rest, unless you expend a spell slot of 5th level or higher to use it again.

[![Living Story](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/b/b3/Living_Story.png/revision/latest/scale-to-width-down/339?cb=20240613115241)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/b/b3/Living_Story.png/revision/latest?cb=20240613115241)

College Of Poetry

# College of Poetry

## Description

*The College of Poetry celebrates the beauty of concise expression, weaving magic through the ancient art of word play. Bards who follow this path find inspiration in nature, emotions, and fleeting moments. Their words resonate with power, evoking both sorrow and hope.*

**Features:**

*   [Level 3 - A Poet's tools](#Level_3_-_A_Poet's_tools)
*   [Level 3 - Whispered Verses](#Level_3_-_Whispered_Verses)
*   [Level 6 - Serene Resonance](#Level_6_-_Serene_Resonance)
*   [Level 14 - Tranquil Ode](#Level_14_-_Tranquil_Ode)

## Level 3 - A Poet's tools

At level 3 the stillness required to master poetry has given you an above natural insight into the world and the people around you. You gain proficiency in Calligraphy Supplies and can use it as your spellcasting focus. You also have extraordinary insight which allows you to use your charisma instead of wisdom when making an insight skill check.

## Level 3 - Whispered Verses

At Level 3 you gain the ability to infuse your spells with the essence of poems. Whenever you cast a spell that requires a spell slot and has a verbal component, you can choose to infuse one of the following poems into your spell before casting the spell

*   Poem of Lamentation: When you cast a spell that deals damage you can infuse it with the Poem of Lamentation to deal extra damage of the spells damage type equal to two rolls of your bardic inspiration die.
*   Poem of Dissociation: When you cast a spell that forces a saving throw from a single target you can infuse it with the Poem of Dissociation to Increase the saving throw DC of the Spell by 2
*   Poem of a Soothing Breeze: When you cast a spell that targets a single ally you can Infuse it with the Poem of a Soothing Breeze to grant the ally temporary hit points equal to your Bard Level.

You can use each Haiku once per short rest and can only one Haiku per spell, when you reach certain levels you gain an extra use per Haiku. 2 uses at level 6, 3 uses at level 14.

## Level 6 - Serene Resonance

At Level 6 your poems resonate with the natural world, granting you deeper insight. When you use your Bardic Inspiration die to inspire an ally, they gain the following benefits:

*   Resistance: The inspired creature gains resistance to a damage type of your choice until the start of your next turn.
*   Guidance: The inspired creature has advantage on the next saving throw they make while they are inspired.

## Level 14 - Tranquil Ode

At level 14 Your mastery of poems reaches its zenith. As an action, you can recite a Tranquil Ode—a profound Poem that resonates with the cosmos. Choose one of the following effects:

*   Harmony's Blessing: All allies within 30 feet regain hit points equal to your Bardic Inspiration die roll + your Charisma modifier.
*   Elegy of Stillness: All enemies within 30 feet must make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failure, they are stunned until the end of their next turn.

You can use each Tranquil Ode only once per long rest.

College Of The Moon (Ua)

# College of the Moon (UA)

## Description

*Inspire Allies with Primal Tales*

*The College of the Moon traces its origins to the ancient druidic circles of the Moonshae Isles, who entrusted the first Bards of this tradition with chronicling the stories of the islands and their people. Bards of this college draw from the isles’ Fey magic and the primal power of the moonwells to bolster their allies, protect the natural world, and inspire their bardic works. Such works tend to be based on well-known Moonshae myths such as the whimsy of fairy pranksters, the viciousness of the Beast, and the mysteries of the moonwells.*

**Features:**

*   [Level 3 - Moonshae Folktales](#Level_3_-_Moonshae_Folktales)
*   [Level 3 - Primal Lorist](#Level_3_-_Primal_Lorist)
*   [Level 6 - Blessing of the Moonwells](#Level_6_-_Blessing_of_the_Moonwells)
*   [Level 14 - Bolstered Folktales](#Level_14_-_Bolstered_Folktales)

## Level 3 - Moonshae Folktales

As a Magic action, you can invoke the power of a folktale, imbuing yourself with primal magic until you use this feature again. When you use this feature, choose which of the following folktales you invoke; your choice gives you benefits while this magic is active.

*   Tale of Life. You invoke a tale of vitality and thriving earth. When you restore Hit Points to a creature with a spell, you can expend a Bardic Inspiration die and increase the amount of Hit Points restored by a number equal to a roll of the Bardic Inspiration die. You can do this only once per turn.
*   Tale of Gloam. You invoke a tale of mystery and secrets. When you use a Bonus Action to give a creature a Bardic Inspiration die, you can take the Disengage or Hide action as part of that Bonus Action.
*   Tale of Mirth. You invoke a tale of merriment and fey guile. When an enemy you can see within 60 feet of yourself succeeds on a saving throw, you can take a Reaction to expend a Bardic Inspiration die. The creature then must roll the Bardic Inspiration die and subtract the number rolled from the save’s total, possibly causing the save to fail.

## Level 3 - Primal Lorist

You learn Druidic and one cantrip from the Druid spell list. It counts as a Bard spell for you but doesn’t count against the number of cantrips you know.

Additionally, choose one of the following skills: Animal Handling, Insight, Medicine, Nature, Perception, or Survival. You have proficiency in that skill.

## Level 6 - Blessing of the Moonwells

You always have the Moonbeam spell prepared.

As a Bonus Action, you can cast Moonbeam without expending a spell slot. When you cast it using this feature, you glow faintly while you maintain Concentration on the spell. While glowing, you shed Dim Light out to 5 feet, and whenever a creature fails its saving throw against the effects of this Moonbeam, another creature of your choice that you can see within 60 feet of you regains 2d4 Hit Points.

Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 3+ spell slot (no action required).

## Level 14 - Bolstered Folktales

The power of your Moonshae Folktales improves. When you use your Tale of Life or Tale of Mirth, you can roll 1d6 and use the number rolled in place of expending a Bardic Inspiration die, and when you use your Tale of Gloam, you can also teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see as part of that Bonus Action

Commerce Domain

# Commerce Domain

## Description

*Clerics of the Commerce Domain are devoted to the principles of trade, wealth, and prosperity. They believe that economic strength and fair exchange can bring about harmony and stability in the world. Gods of commerce, such as Abadar or Waukeen, often bless their followers with the ability to navigate markets, negotiate deals, and protect their wealth.*

**Features:**

*   [1st Level - Commerce Domain Spells](#1st_Level_-_Commerce_Domain_Spells)
*   [1st Level - Sales Strategy](#1st_Level_-_Sales_Strategy)
*   [1st Level - Divine Bargain](#1st_Level_-_Divine_Bargain)
*   [2nd Level - Merchant’s Blessing](#2nd_Level_-_Merchant’s_Blessing)
*   [6th Level - Spiritual Investments](#6th_Level_-_Spiritual_Investments)
*   [8th Level - Potent Spellcasting](#8th_Level_-_Potent_Spellcasting)
*   [17th Level - Treasury of the Divine](#17th_Level_-_Treasury_of_the_Divine)

## 1st Level - Commerce Domain Spells

*   1st Cleric Level: Distort Value, Identify
*   3rd Cleric Level: Arcane Lock, Zone of Truth
*   5th Cleric Level: Incite Greed, Tongues
*   7th Cleric Level: Faithful Hound, Secret Chest
*   9th Cleric Level: Animate Objects, Scrying

## 1st Level - Sales Strategy

At 1st level, you gain proficiency with one type of artisan's tools of your choice, as well as proficiency in the Persuasion or Deception skills.

## 1st Level - Divine Bargain

Also at 1st level, your spiritual savvy manifests itself as material wealth. You gain a number of divine coins up to a maximum equal to your wisdom modifier, and you regain any spent coins during a long rest. As a Bonus Action on your turn, you can magically conduct a spiritual trade with one conscious creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself. Choose either Buy or Sell:

**Buy.** Give one of your divine coins to the target. It can spend the divine coin as if it had your Divine Bargain feature. The target takes 1d8 Necrotic damage, and you regain Hit Points equal to the damage taken.

**Sell.** Gain a divine coin. You take 1d8 Necrotic damage, lose maximum hit points equal to the damage taken, and the target regains Hit Points equal to the damage taken. The damage cannot be reduced by any means.

## 2nd Level - Merchant’s Blessing

You can use your Channel Divinity as an action to enhance your dealings. For the next hour:

Whenever you make a Charisma ability check, you may substitute your Wisdom modifier for your Charisma modifier.

If the check uses the skill you gained from Sales Strategy, you may add your proficiency bonus twice instead of once.

## 6th Level - Spiritual Investments

You can spend divine coins to empower your miracles. When you cast a spell or use a feature, you may spend coins for one of the following effects:

**Extend Contract.** As a bonus action when you are currently concentrating on a spell, you can spend 1 coin to extend its duration by a round.

**Sweeten the Deal**. As a reaction when you roll damage or healing for a spell you can spend 1 coin to reroll any number of dice for the spell’s healing or damage, taking the new results.

**Collateral Loan.** As a bonus action, you can spend 1 coin to grant temporary hit points equal to 2d8 + your Wisdom modifier to a creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself.

You may only use one of these benefits per turn.

## 8th Level - Potent Spellcasting

You add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any cleric cantrip.

## 17th Level - Treasury of the Divine

You gain an incorruptible storehouse of divine wealth. At the start of each of your turns, you automatically gain 1 divine coin. In addition, the maximum amount of divine coins you can hold increases up to 2 times your wisdom modifier.

Communication Mouthpiece

# Communication Mouthpiece

*Wondrous Item, uncommon*

**Description**

This mask like accessories produces a voice based on the wearers thoughts, it fits over the mouth and allows someone or something who doesn't have capabilities of speech to speak a language they know, in addition, you are able to speak and understand spoken Common while wearing this mask

Consecrated Scabbard

# Consecrated Scabbard

*Wondrous Item, very rare*

**Description**

This scabbard is decorated with depictions of the deity it was dedicated to, and it automatically adjusts to what kind of melee weapon it will hold, such as changing size to fit any blade or becoming a series of metal rings to holster weapons like axes, hammers, or spears. When you draw a melee weapon that is held by this scabbard, you can bless the weapon. For the next minute, whenever you make an attack roll with this weapon, you can roll a d4 and add the number rolled to the attack roll. Additionally, attacks made with this melee weapon are considered magical and deal an additional 1d4 radiant damage. This effect ends early if you stow the weapon or if a different creature other than you wields it. The effect can be applied three times, after which you regain all expended uses at the next dawn.

If you are under the effects of the bless spell while wielding a melee weapon with the scabbard's effect, the d4s granted by the bless spell and this item turn into d6s.

Conspirator'S Coat

# Conspirator's Coat

*Wondrous Item, rare*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

This magic longcoat is adorned with raven feathers and carved jet buttons. While wearing this coat, you have advantage on Charisma (Deception) checks. Inaddition, the coat has 5 charges and regains 1d4 + 1 expended charges daily at dusk. While wearing the coat, you can use a bonus action to expend 1 of its charges to disappear, releasing up to three ravens from your location under your control that scatters into the air. Each raven has AC 12, 1 hit point, and can fly up to 40 feet as part of this bonus action without provoking opportunity attacks. At the start of your next turn, the ravens vanish, and you reappear in the space of one of the ravens (your choice).

If all the ravens are reduced to 0 hit points, you reappear early in the same space as the last one that died. When you do, any excess damage dealt to the raven carries over to your normal form.

Container Of Heat And Frost

# Container of Heat and Frost

*Wondrous Item, uncommon*

[![Container of Heat and Frost](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/1/1c/Container_of_Heat_and_Frost.png/revision/latest/scale-to-width-down/180?cb=20241009130015)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/1/1c/Container_of_Heat_and_Frost.png/revision/latest?cb=20241009130015)

[](/docs/file:container-of-heat-and-frost.png)

Container of Heat and Frost

**Description**

This dense metal container is adorned with three arcane designs along the edge of its removable lid. When filled with up to one gallon of watery liquid (Liquid with the same viscosity as water), the vessel can either heat, chill, or maintain the liquid's temperature. As an action, you can press one of the arcane symbols to heat the liquid to a boil (212 degrees Fahrenheit or 100 degrees Celsius), chill it (33 degrees Fahrenheit or 1 degree Celsius), or keep it at temperature for up to 24 hours.

It takes one minute for the vessel to heat or cool a liquid down, and, once at temperature, maintains it. Liquids placed inside without activating the arcane designs will maintain its temperature for up to one hour before becoming tepid.

Corrosion Cube

# Corrosion Cube

*Wondrous Item , Uncommon*

**Description**

This 3' cube glows slightly with yellow light while active, which can be identified as a standard "hazard" symbol while in darkness.

The cube has 3 charges. As an action, you can touch the cube to a non-magical metallic object. If the object is smaller than the cube, it is destroyed as the object melts over the course of one minute. Otherwise, the cube melts away a section of the object equal to the cube in volume over the course of one minute. Either result spends one charge of the cube. The cube regains all of it's charges daily at dawn.

The cube makes a fairly loud cracking sound as it melts metal, which can be heard easily from up to 30ft away.

Counterriposte Duelist

# Counterriposte Duelist

**Prerequisite:** Defensive Duelist, 12th level+

You turn a parry into the opening for a deadly counter.

*   **Counterstance**. While you benefit from the AC bonus of your Defensive Duelist Parry, you gain the following special reaction:
    *   **Counterattack**. When a creature misses you with a melee attack, due to the AC Bonus from the Defensive Duelist feat, you can make one melee weapon attack against it. You can use this reaction once on each creature’s turn, except your own. You can only use this counterattack a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus per long rest\*
*   **Ranged Parry**. When you aren’t wielding a shield, you can also use your Defensive Duelist Parry against a ranged weapon attack that targets only you.

Courier'S Cradle

# Courier's Cradle

*Wondrous Item, very rare*

**Description**

*A Couriers Cradle needs two pieces to have any sort of effect. A Sending Stone, and a Couriers Cradle. Without both components it has no function.* A Courier's Cradle consists of three parts. A chassis, typically squared and made of polished stone, granite, or marble for particularly wealthy folk. A basin sat in the cubed bottom, around half a foot wide and typically filled with water. And lastly a bowl mounted over the basin, made of rare material such as gemstone, brass, or pure quartz. While a creature is within 5 feet of a cradle, it may place a sending stone into it's bowl. It then takes a minute to fasten and adjust the cradle for speaking. After this process is complete the cradle is assembled.

Connection A creature may connect to another Courier's Cradle within a distance of up to the plane they exist according to the following difficulties, using an Intelligence (Arcana) check. If another Cradle is connected to this one, they establish a line of communication. This allows each to hear from the other side as if they were in the same room.


Connection
Distance (Miles)DC
510
5015
20020
Planar25


**Disconnection** A Courier's Cradle is automatically disconnected if both sides are not connected to one another or if the cradle is moved from where it was first activated.

Craorag Dromlach

# Craorag Dromlach

Weapon (spear), legendary

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

*The spear once used by the Storm Maiden of Norland, Hergatha, was said to be lost following her ultimate defeat during her efforts to conquer the Sea of Swords. It was written to have been bestowed by Tempus, but rumors whisper that it was actually bestowed to her by Valkur.*

You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. In addition, the weapon ignores resistance to piercing damage.

**Blood Ocean**. When you draw this weapon within one mile of a large body of water such as an ocean or lake, the seas within a five-mile radius become red like blood. When you attack a creature with this weapon while they are within a mile of the blood water, you have advantage on the attack roll. If it hits, the blood waters darken, and you deal an extra 2d6 piercing damage, or 2d8 piercing damage if wielded with two hands. The water returns to its normal color when you sheathe this weapon again.

**Umberlee's Blessing.** While holding this spear, you can breath underwater and have a swimming speed equal to your walking speed. You also gain the following character trait: "I have the compulsion to drown other living creatures".

**Norland's Champion.** Each time you attack with this weapon, creatures of your choice that you can see within 60 feet of you are inspired. Each creature gains temporary hit points equal to your constitution modifier. While a creature has these temporary hit points, they are immune to being frightened.

Crown Of The Four Seasons

# Crown of the Four Seasons

*Wondrous Item, Uncommon*

**Description**

*Control your emotions, sweetheart, or the witches will come and steal your feelings away.*

Originally used as a toy to train young eladrin, this fey-infused flower crown changes appearance based on the wearer's mood. These moods also reflect the four seasons: winter, spring, summer, and autumn.

The flowers will shift between four seasonal types dependent on the wearer's mood and the corresponding season. In addition, a harmless illusory effect will hover over the blossoms to reflect the same thing (Snowflakes fluttering around for winter/sadness, Fiery sparks for summer/passion etc).

When the wearer undergoes a burst of any type of emotion (Flash of anger, sudden surprise etc), the flowers will wilt to a dull gray and the illusory effects will vanish to signify a loss of control. The next morning, the flowers will blossom to their full color after the lesson has been learned

Cryomancy

# Cryomancy

*Specializing on ice magic, wizards that have learned from the Cryomancy arcane tradition manipulate the crystalline structure of ice and bend it to their will. Many foolishly only see this magic as beautiful, and it is, but within the beauty of all that shimmering ice, is a slow and shivering death.*

## Frigid Magic

Your manipulation of ice allow you to acclimate to it and change the properties of spells. When you cast a spell that deals damage, you are able to change the damage type to cold damage unless its bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (a minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

You gain resistance to cold damage. When you reach 13th level on this class, you also gain resistance to fire damage, and at 17th level, you gain immunity to cold damage and the area 5 feet of you has the effects of extreme cold.

## Ice Sculpting

As an action, you can freeze a 5-foot cube of nonmagical water. The cube will remain frozen for one minute or until your concentration ends (as if you were concentrating on a spell). You can manipulate the ice as a bonus action to mold its shape to form objects or structures. The objects or structures you make have hit points equal to your wizard level. At 8th and 15th level, you freeze an area of 15-foot and 25-foot cube respectively. You can also create the ice out of moisture in the air, though the cubic footage you are able to manipulate is halved (i.e. you can produce a 7-foot cube from a 15-foot cube of air).

Additionally, when you reach 15th level you can also freeze magical water, manipulate magical ice, and the cube can now be frozen for up to 1 hour.

## Cryostasis

Your body fills with ice magic and acts at times in preservation from external factors. When you become unconscious or asleep or are rendered unconscious or asleep, you are immediately encased in a layer of dense ice. This ice has hit points equal to twice your wizard level and an AC equal to your spell save DC.

When the ice encases you as a result of being reduced to 0 hit points, the ice will stabilize you and freezes the area around you. All creatures within 10 feet of you must make a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC or be restrained until the end of their next turn. On a successful save, they are not restrained but their speed is halved. When the restrained condition from this feature ends, the creature's speed is also halved. They can make another Constitution saving throw at the end of each of their turns. On a success, their speed returns to normal.

The ice lasts for 1 minute, until it is destroyed, or when you choose to break the ice upon regaining consciousness. Once this feature is used it cannot be used again until a long rest.

## Absolute Zero

Your control over ice have reached to an abnormal extreme, even by magical standards. Choose one wizard spell that deals damage in your spellbook. That spell's damage type permanently becomes cold damage when you cast it without using your Frigid Magic feature or other similar effect. You can change the spell for a different spell by spending 8 hours in study.

The first time you deal damage to a creature that does not have resistance or immunity to cold damage on your turn, they take extra cold damage equal to your wizard level.

## Whiteout

After extensive use of bending ice to your every whim, you understand all of its inner workings. You're able to apply this knowledge by performing grand feats on a monumental scale. As an action, you are able to freeze everything within a 60 foot radius sphere centered around you. This creates sleets of ice and snow on the ground and super chilling the air, rendering the area as difficult terrain. All creatures of your choice takes 3d6 cold damage upon activation and must make a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC or be frozen. While frozen, a creature is considered incapacitated and their speed is reduced to 0 until the end of their next turn.

Furthermore, you create a 30 foot radius sphere of a perpetual blizzard that is centered around and moves with you. When a creature of your choice enters the area for the first time or starts their turn in it, the creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC or take cold damage equal to half your wizard level and have its speed halved until the start of its next turn. The area inside the blizzard acts as a heavily obscured area for all creatures except you.

Both the difficult terrain and blizzard lasts for 1 minute or when you end both effects using an action, afterwards you no longer gain the effects of the Frigid Magic feature until you take a short rest. Once this feature is used it cannot be used again until a long rest.

Daredevil

# Daredevil

## Daredevil

*The Daredevil fighter thrives on style, versatility and unpredictably. Their use of different weapon types makes them dangerous to any foe and they gain more confidence the more stylish their fighting is.*

### Daredevil impulse

Starting at 3rd level, every time you make an attack of a type different from the previous attack in the current combat, you gain one Style point. You have a maximum amount of style points equal to your proficiency bonus. When you roll Initiative your Style points reset to 0.

Attack types are classified as: Melee weapon attack, Ranged weapon attack and thrown attacks.

Style Points can be used to power your Flairs.

#### Flairs:

*   **Wide attack:** If you hit with a melee attack, you may spend 2 Style points to deal melee damage equal to half your Fighter level to all foes within 5 feet of your target.
*   **Laser Focus:** Spend 2 Style Points to gain advantage on your next attack roll.
*   **Range Extender:** You can add 10 feet to any ranged or thrown weapon's normal range for every point spent
*   **Rush:** Gain 10 feet of movement speed for each Style Point spent until the end of your turn.
*   **Burst (4th level and up):** As a bonus action, Spend 3 Style points to make a weapon attack.

### **Hotswap:**

At 3rd level, whenever you make an attack roll with a weapon, you can sheathe it and draw another in the same action. You can only do this once per action.

### Fight or Flight
