From 7th level you have a choice between staying in action and fighting defensively or moving around or to continue as is.  As a bonus action you can choose to either fight or flee from combat.

*   **Fight:** As a Bonus action you can give yourself a Bonus to your AC equal to the amount of Style Points you spend. This bonus lasts until the end of your next turn or until you are incapacitated. While Fight is active you cannot gain more Style points.
*   **Flight:** You can spend 2 Style points to disengage, you can't be targeted by opportunity attacks. You gain an additional 10 feet of movement until the end of your turn for every additional Style Point spent.

### Double Trouble

You can choose an additional Fighting Style.

Additionally, using the Disengage, Dodge or Dash actions also builds your Style chain. For example: If you use a dodge action and make an attack on your next turn or vice versa you also gain a Style Point

### Wild Rush:

Starting at 15th level, when an enemy within 30 feet of you gets incapacitated or restrained you can use 2 Style Points as an reaction to dash straight toward the enemy. You move 10 feet for every point spent (minimum 2 points) and can spend additional Style for added Rush movement. You can  sheath your current weapon and draw another as part of the dash and make a single attack against that target.

### Inferno Trigger:

From 18th level, once per short rest you can expend 6 Style points as an action to trigger your Inferno action.

**Inferno action:** Can attack 6 times, ignore the loading and ammunition properties if the weapon has it. Any Critical hit deals an additional 10 damage of the weapons damage type. These attacks don't provide any Style points

Darkweavers Mantle

# Darkweavers Mantle

*Wondrous Item, very rare*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

While you are in an area of dim light or darkness, you can Hide as a bonus action if you are not being directly observed. The cloak has 3 charges for the following other properties. The cloak regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

**Blacklight**. You can spend 1 charge as an action to cast the Shadow of Moil spell.

**Umbral Augment.** You can spend 1 charge as a reaction when you fail a saving throw or ability check and add half your proficiency bonus, rounded down, to the roll.

Dawnbreak Pearl

# Dawnbreak Pearl

*Wondrous Item, legendary*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

Used to summon daylight, this 6-inch-diameter, gold-white orb is warm to the touch. You can spend 10 minutes to activate it, causing the area within 10 miles of it at the moment of activation to become daylight even if it is nighttime. This daylight lasts for 24 hours, until you cancel it as an action, or until your attunement to the pearl ends. Once used, the pearl can't be used again for 24 hours.

Deadeye

# Deadeye

## The Deadeye

*The embodiment of grit, luck, and a well-placed shot, fighters who choose this martial archetype strive to emulate paradigms of accuracy. Deadeyes generally prefer fighting from comfortable ranges, employing the use of crossbows, firearms, and even throwing knives if a situation calls for them.*

*   [3rd Level - Perfect Sixth](#3rd_Level_-_Perfect_Sixth)
*   [7th Level - Line ‘em Up](#3rd_Level_-_Perfect_Sixth)
*   [10th Level - Gambit](#3rd_Level_-_Perfect_Sixth)
*   [15th Level - Duelist’s Aim](#3rd_Level_-_Perfect_Sixth)
*   [18th Level - High Noon](#3rd_Level_-_Perfect_Sixth)

## 3rd Level - Perfect Sixth

Once per attack action beginning at 3rd level, when you make a ranged weapon attack roll against a creature, you can add a d6 to the result. You can add the d6 before or after seeing the results of the attack roll, but before the effects are applied. If the result of the d6 is a 6, you severely injure your target. Roll on the severe injury table to see the effect of the injury.

##### Severe Injury Table

2d6 | Severe Injury

2 | **Shock**. The target is stunned until the end of its next turn.

3 | **Fracture**. The target can only take either an action, bonus action, or move on its next turn.

4 | **Punctured Lung**. The target gains one level of exhaustion for the next minute.

5 | **Crushed Throat**. The target cannot speak, use verbal components, or use any features that involve its voice until the end of its next turn.

6 | **Crushed Grip**. The target drops anything it is currently holding, including grappled creatures.

7 | **Whiplash**. The attack deals one additional die of weapon damage.

8 | **Broken Leg**. The target’s movement speed is halved until the end of its next turn.

9 | **Embedded Ammunition**. Until the end of its next turn, for every 5 feet the target moves, it takes 1d6 slashing damage.

10| **Tear**. The target has disadvantage on melee attacks until the end of its next turn.

11| **Blind.** The target is blinded until the end of its next turn.

12| **Dead Shot.** The triggering hit becomes a critical hit.

*   Before you make a ranged weapon attack, you can take a -5 penalty to the attack roll. If you hit, you apply a severe injury immediately. You can only apply one severe injury with each attack action.
*   A creature cannot be affected by the same severe injury more than once at a time, and a creature can expend one use of legendary resistance to ignore a severe injury’s effect.

## 7th Level - Line ‘em Up

At 7th level, you become a master at hitting successive targets. When you take the attack action on your turn, each time you target a different creature than any other you’ve targeted this turn, you gain a cumulative +1 bonus to the attack roll.

## 10th Level - Gambit

At 10th level, when you roll initiative, you can gambit a d4, d6, d8, or d10. Roll the chosen die and subtract it from your initiative. Until the end of your next turn, every creature below you in initiative takes extra damage when hit with your ranged attack rolls equal to the chosen die.

## 15th Level - Duelist’s Aim

At 15th level, when you use the attack action to make a ranged weapon attack and you hit at least three consecutive attacks, your third attack inflicts a severe injury.

## 18th Level - High Noon

At 18th level, you are adept at ending multiple targets at once. As an action, choose any number of targets that you can see within a cone the size of your weapon’s short range, up to 60 feet. You may make one ranged weapon attack against each target you chose inflicting the Dead Shot severe injury with each of them. You may use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining all uses on a long rest.

Death Knight

# Death Knight

The archetypal death knight focuses on honing negative energies into their attacks and integrating necromancy into their fighting style. While death knights don’t have to be tragic and dark, the energies used often corrupts the user

## Death Tokens

During combat, when you kill a hostile creature, you gain a number of death tokens equal to half the creature's proficiency bonus (rounded down), which can be used to fuel your death knight abilities. Once a creature grants a death token, it may not do so until you finish a long rest. You may have a maximum number of death tokens equal to 1 + half your fighter level, rounded down, which last until your next long rest.

## Undead Soldier

Starting at 3rd level, you learn to reanimate undead in the midst of combat. As an action, you may expend 2 death tokens to infuse negative energy into a corpse or pile of bones that you can see to turn it into an undead soldier. The corpse becomes a zombie or skeleton which lasts until your next long rest, where it then turns into dust. Undead soldiers share initiative with each other, but roll initiative on their own. As a bonus action, you may give them commands. If no commands are given, they do their best to defend themselves and you. Your undead soldiers additionally count as you for the purpose of gaining death tokens.

You may have a total number of undead soldiers equal to half your proficiency bonus (rounded down).

## Rebuke of the Damned

Starting at 3rd level, you can expend one death token as a reaction when you or an undead soldier within 30ft of you is hit by an attack. Roll a number of d4 dice equal to your proficiency bonus, and reduce the damage taken by the total rolled, dealing the total back to the attacker as necrotic damage.

## Sink or Swim

Starting at 7th level, you learn various uses for your death tokens.

*   As a bonus action, you can use a death token and select an undead soldier within 30 feet of you. Then, spend and roll up to a number of hit dice equal to your proficiency bonus. The undead soldier regains hit points equal to the total of the dice rolled.
*   As an action, you can spend a number of death tokens and target a undead soldier or corpse within 30 feet of you. Every creature within 10 feet of the undead soldier or corpse must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution modifier) or take 1d4 necrotic damage and 1d4 fire damage per death token expended.
*   Once per turn, you may expend a death token slay an undead soldier early, instantly turning it into dust. When doing so, you may make an additional attack before the end of your next turn when you take the attack action.

## Cull the Horde

Starting at 10th level, you can use death tokens to empower you and your undead.

*   Your undead soldiers gain a bonus to attack rolls and saving throws equal to half the death tokens you currently carry, rounded down.
*   Attacks done by your undead soldiers count as magical for the purpose of overcoming defenses.
*   You may spend two death tokens when creating an undead soldier to grant the soldier proficiency in light, medium, and heavy armor, shields, and martial weapons.

## Black Speech

Starting at 15th level, You learn to speak, read, and write Black Speech, which can be used to communicate with undead and can not be learned through traditional means. Most undead lack the intelligence to convey or understand sophisticated concepts, but a non-hostile undead could relay what it has seen or heard in the recent past. This ability doesn't grant you friendship with undead, though you can combine this ability with gifts to curry favor with them as you would with any nonplayer character.

Additionally, having access to this speech grants you resistance to necrotic damage.

## Grave Warden

Starting at 18th levels, when using your undead soldier feature, you can expend 6 death tokens to create a sword wraith warrior instead. In addition, you can use death tokens to do the following:

*   **Armor of Bones:** You may spend a death token when creating an undead to create large spikes of bones which protrude out the created undead. When a creature attacks that undead with a melee attack, they take magical piercing damage equal to your constitution modifier.
*   **Lament of the Lost**: As a bonus action, you can spend a death token to reposition any of your undead soldiers within 30 feet of you. You determine the new arrangement of these soldiers, and may swap your location with one of them.

Designated Mage'S Rifle

# Designated Mage's Rifle

*Weapon (Musket) Rarity Varies (Rare/Very Rare/Legendary)*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement by a spellcaster

**Description**

This magical firearm is designed for use in magical combat. Due to this fact, a spellcaster can use the weapon as their spellcasting focus. You gain a +1/2/3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magical weapons, and the same bonus to spell attack rolls you make while holding this weapon. When you cast a spell using this weapon as your spellcasting focus, your next attack made with the weapon uses your spellcasting ability instead of dexterity. Additionally, if the spell deals damage, the weapon's damage type changes to that of the spell.

Devastation

# Devastation

*6th-level evocation. (Wizard, Sorcerer, Warlock)*

**Meta**

**Casting Time**: 1 action

**Range**: 120ft

**Components**: V, S

**Duration**: Concentration, up to 10 minutes

**Description**

You conjure a ball of concentrated magic energy the size of a soccer ball loudly thrumming with energy, which slowly moves toward a target of your choice, moving through any nonmagical barrier. If it hits a magical barrier made from a spell of 9th level or lower, the orb and the barrier disappear. Otherwise, the orb disappears.

It appears 60ft away from the target at a random location, moving 15ft closer at the beginning of your turn. If the creature touches the orb, they must make a Dexterity Saving Throw or take 20d6+40 force damage on a failure, or take half that much on a successful save. The orb then fades into nothingness.

**At Higher Levels**

When you cast this spell at 7th level or higher, you deal an additional 5d6 force damage for every level above 6th.

Divine Reprisal

# Divine Reprisal

*1st-level evocation. (Cleric, Druid, Paladin)*

**Meta**

**Casting Time:** 1 bonus action

**Range:** Self

**Components:** V, M (a shield worth at least 1 sp)

**Duration:** Concentration, up to 1 minute

**Description**

As a bonus action, you can infuse a shield you're currently wielding with divine energy. If the shield is not magical, your AC increases by 1 until you stop wielding the shield or the spell ends. The first time a creature targets you with a melee attack and misses during this spell's duration, your shield bursts with divine radiance as you deflect the blow. The stored energy within releases and deals 1d6 radiant damage to any hostile creatures within 5 feet. When this blast hits an undead or a fiend, it deals an additional 1d6 radiant damage to it. After the blast, the spell ends.

**At Higher Levels**

When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the radiant damage dealt by the blast increases by 1d6 for each slot level after the 1st.

Double Bladed Scythe

# Double-Bladed Scythe

*Weapon: Martial, Melee*

120gp, 8 lbs. --- 2d4 slashing - Reach, Two-handed, Special

**Description**

Proficiency with a double-bladed scythe allows you to add your proficiency bonus to the attack roll for any attack you make with it.

**Special**. If you attack with a double-bladed scythe as part of the Attack action on your turn, you can use a bonus action immediately after to make a melee attack with it. This attack deals 1d4 slashing damage on a hit.

Draconic Expansion

# Draconic Expansion

Dragonborn and Draconic Bloodline Sorcerers, Drakewarden Rangers and Ascendant Dragon Monks are updated to include the new types of dragons. New racial types follows gem dragonborn guidelines, This updated only changes the given Damage and Resistance types.

\-NEW-

Time Force

Moonstone Radiant

Solar Radiant

Lunar Cold

Deep Psychic

\-Traditional-

Black    Acid

Blue    Lightning

Brass    Fire

Bronze    Lightning

Copper    Acid

Gold    Fire

Green    Poison

Red    Fire

Silver    Cold

White    Cold

\-Gem-

Amethyst    Force

Crystal    Radiant

Emerald    Psychic

Sapphire    Thunder

Topaz    Necrotic

Dragon Fang Gun

# Dragon Fang Gun

*Weapon (any firearm), rare*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

The barrel of this magic firearm is shaped like a dragon's maw, and the weapon is infused with the essence of a chromatic, gem, or metallic dragon's breath. When you hit with an attack roll using this magic firearm, the target takes an extra 1d6 damage of the same type as the breath infused in the firearm--acid, cold, fire, force, lightning, necrotic, poison, psychic, radiant, or thunder.

If you load no ammunition in the weapon, it produces its own, automatically creating one piece of magic ammunition when you pull the trigger. The ammunition created by the firearm vanishes the instant after it hits or misses a target.

Dragonscale Knuckles

# Dragonscale Knuckles

*Wondrous Item, rare*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

While wearing these knuckles you get a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with unarmed strikes. While these knuckles are equipped you can’t use other weapons and the knuckles take an action to equip or unequip. The knuckles deal an additional 1d6 damage of which the type is determined by the dragon scales used to create it.


Scale ColorDamage Type
RedFire
GreenPoison
BlueLightning
BlackAcid
WhiteCold
GoldFire
BrassFire
CopperAcid
SilverCold
BronzeLightning
SapphireThunder
EmeraldPsychic
AmethystForce
CrystalRadiant
TopazNecrotic

Dragonwrought Kobold

# Dragonwrought Kobold

## Description

A rare type of kobold, with a stronger connection to true dragons. Often bearing the scale colors of their related dragons, and even some of their elemental abilities. Dragonwrought kobolds also tend to posses wings, and increased durability compared to the more common kobolds.

## Languages

Your character can speak, read and write common and draconic.

## Creature Type

Your draconic lineage makes you closer to the dragons than other kobolds. Your creature type is Dragon.

## Size

Dragonwrought kobolds stand between 2 and 4 feet tall, and weigh between 30 and 45 pounds. Your size is small or tiny.

## Speed

Your base walking speed in 30 feet.

## Darkvision

You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can’t discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

## Draconic Wings

You either hatched with, or later grew a pair of leathery dragon wings. At 5th level, they become usable, granting you a flying speed of 30 feet.

## Treasure Appraiser

You have a knack for appraising gemstones and other such treasures. By spending 1 minute studying an object, you can determine its approximate value to the nearest 100 gp.

## Dragonwrought Lineage

Your connection to dragons can manifest in a number of ways, choose one of the following traits when you select this race:

*   **Draconic Endurance** - Your hit point maximum increases by 1, and it increases by 1 every time you gain a level.
*   **Unhindered** - Magic cannot cause you to have the paralyzed condition.
*   **Elemental Potency** - Once on each of your turns when you deal damage, you can deal 1d6 extra damage of a type correlating to your draconic lineage

Drifter

# Drifter

## Descriptions

Those that follow this path believe that rather than hard training, true mastery comes with application. Like a branch drifting across a river, such practitioners make do with what’s thrown at them, adapting to the issues they are faced with and prospering in the worst of conditions.

## Thus, the Branch Drifts

At 2nd level, you find it easy to manipulate your techniques to fit their situations. As a bonus action, you can enter the flowing river stance for 1 minute, gaining the following benefits:

*   If you focus a technique, you can choose whether it’s a mental or physical type for you.
*   When you make a weapon attack with a technique, you can use your mental ability for attack and damage rolls rather than Strength or Dexterity.
*   If a creature fails its save against your technique, you can use a reaction to regain mastery points equal to half your maximum (rounded up). After you use this feature, you must take a short or long rest before you can use it again.

You can exit the flowing river stance as a bonus action. It ends early if you are incapacitated or if your turn ends and you haven’t used a technique since your last turn. You can enter the flowing river stance a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining all uses on a long rest.

## The Stone Remains Unmoved

At 6th level, you improve upon your ability to adapt to change. When you enter the flowing river stance, choose a damage type aside from bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage. You become resistant to that damage type until the flowing river stance ends.

## A Fork in the River

At 14th level, you become adept at stringing techniques from different forms together. After spending mastery points to focus a technique while in the flowing river stance, you can use a bonus action to use another technique from a different martial form.

## Laminar Flow

At 18th level, if you use three physical techniques or three mental techniques in a row, you gain a benefit:

**Physical.** The next time a creature hits you with an attack until the end of your next turn, you can use your reaction to make one weapon attack against them.

**Mental.** The next time you use a technique that forces a creature to make a save until the end of your next turn, they have disadvantage on that save.

## Martial Form - Drifting River

### Form 1: Floating Pebble

**Type:**

Varies

**Prerequisites:**

Novice, Drifter

Make a weapon attack against a creature or object. The attack deals an amount of d6s equal to your proficiency bonus in additional psychic damage if this technique is mental or bludgeoning damage if this technique is physical.

**Focused (1 point, or 0 points if in flowing river stance).** If the target is a creature, they must make a Constitution saving throw or have their speed halved until the end of their next turn. Your movement speed increases by the same amount until the end of your turn.

### Form 2: Liquid Rebuttal

**Type:**

Varies

**Prerequisites:**

Intermediate, Drifter, Flowing river stance

If you take damage that matches the damage type you chose for your flowing river stance, you can use your reaction to become immune to that damage instead.

**Focused (1 point).** In the same reaction, you can use another technique that includes a weapon attack.

### Form 3: Hyperfocus

**Type:**

Varies

**Prerequisites:**

Advanced, Drifter

The next technique you use from a different form until the end of your next turn can be focused with 1 less mastery point (min. 1)

**Focused (1 point).** The next technique you focus does not require mastery points, and once performed, you can immediately make a weapon attack against a creature or object.

### Form 4: The River’s Song

**Type:**

Varies

**Prerequisites:**

Master, Drifter, Flowing river stance

**Duration:**

Concentration, 1 min.

If you use a physical or mental technique, you gain a benefit.

**Physical.** If you hit a creature with a weapon attack during this technique, they must succeed on a wisdom saving throw or be forced to use their action to make one melee weapon attack against you on their next turn.

**Mental.** When you hit a creature with a weapon attack during this technique, they must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

**Focused (1 point).**

Whenever you use a physical or mental technique, it counts as if you had used three physical or three mental techniques respectively.

Drow Shadow Blade

# Drow Shadow Blade

*Weapon (dagger), uncommon*

**Description**

You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. You also add 1d4 to all stealth checks made while you are holding this weapon.

This dagger appears to be of Elven make, and has a long, cruel, slender black blade. The dagger always appears wreathed in shadows, making it hard to focus upon clearly, even on a bright summer's day. It also bestows some of this uncanny ability to its wielder.

Proficiency with a Dagger allows you to add your proficiency bonus to the attack roll for any attack you make with it

Druid Subclasses

# Druid Subclasses

## Circle of the Sky

### Circle Spells

Your connection to the sky allows you to learn certain spells. At 2nd level, you learn the gust cantrip, and it does not count towards the number of druid cantrips you know. At 3rd, 5th, 7th, and 9th level you gain access to the spells listed for that level in the Circle of the Sky Spells table. Once you gain access to one of these spells, you always have it prepared, and it doesn’t count against the number of spells you can prepare each day. If you gain access to a spell that doesn’t appear on the druid spell list, the spell is nonetheless a druid spell for you.


Circle of the Sky Spell
Druid LevelSpells
3rdAir Bubble, Skywrite
5thFly, Lightning Bolt
7thConjure Minor Elementals, Freedom of Movement
9thControl Winds, Steel Wind Strike


### Where the Wind Guides

Your connection to the winds allows you to reliably control vehicles and other structures that rely on them. You gain proficiency in either water or air vehicles (your choice), and while you are aboard such a vehicle, you always have optimal wind for traveling to your intended destination.

### Early Bird

When you use your wildshape feature, the beasts you transform into can have a flying speed.

### Gale Force

At 6th level, you gain the ability to harness windflow to greater effect. Any wind powered structure, such as a wind mill, works at twice it's normal rate while within 5 miles of you. Additionally, water or air vehicles that utilize sails move twice as fast while you are on board.

Additionally, whenever you roll lightning, thunder, or bludgeoning damage from a druid spell you cast, you add your wisdom modifier to the total damage.

### Jetstream

When you reach 10th level, you gain the ability to move with a supernatural flow. Your walking speed increases by 15 feet, as does any flying speed of creatures you transform into with your wildshape feature. Additionally, you are considered to always be under the effects of the Freedom of Movement spell.

### The Sky's the Limit

At 14th level, your connection to the skies has given you a shared connection with all life that calls it home. When you use your wildshape feature, you can transform into any Beast, Dragon, or Monstrosity of Challenge Rating 4 or lower, so long as it has a flying speed.

## Circle of the Warden

*There are many things that need to be protected, some worth protecting with your life. Druids of this circle protect the natural order of the wilderness with their lives, often picking a place and staking their claim there for a large portion of their lives. Though they protect the land, the spirits of nature see fit to protect them in turn, the mark of a true Warden.*

### Improved Shillelagh

At 2nd level, you unlock the versatile potential within the Shillelagh cantrip. You learn the shillelagh cantrip. If you already know this cantrip, you may learn a different cantrip of your choice from the druid spell list. Whenever you roll initiative and are not surprised, you can choose to immediately cast shillelagh as a reaction.

Additionally, when you cast shillelagh, you can choose to change the damage type of the weapon to bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage for the duration of the spell. The form of the weapon changes to suit the damage type chosen.

### Warden Form

At 2nd level, you can allow the spirits of nature to wreath your form in protective foliage to enhance your endurance and martial capability. As a bonus action, you can expend a use of Wild Shape to take on your warden form. Warden form lasts for 10 minutes. It ends early if you are incapacitated or until you dismiss it (as a free action). While in warden form, you gain the following benefits:

*   When you enter warden form, you gain temporary hit points equal to your wisdom modifier + double your druid level
*   Your AC is equal to 13 + your constitution modifier.
*   You are immune to all plant-based difficult terrain, as well as all difficult terrain created by Druid spells you cast.

### Forestwalker

At 6th level, you become a natural at navigating the rugged and tough terrain of forests. You gain the following benefits while in warden form:

*   You are resistant to damage from Druid spells you cast.
*   You gain a bonus to all strength checks and saves equal to your wisdom modifier while in warden form.

Additionally, you are now unaffected by plant-based difficult terrain when not in warden form.

### Extra Attack

At 6th level, when you take the attack action, you can make an additional weapon attack as part of the same action. You can replace up to one of these attacks with one of your druid cantrips.

### Arboreal Fortitude

At 10th level, the fortitude of your warden form grows to reflect a growth in your resolve. While in warden form, you gain a bonus to any constitution saving throw you make to maintain concentration on a spell. This bonus is equal to your wisdom modifier. If you begin your turn in warden form and do not have any temporary hit points, you gain temporary hit points equal to your wisdom modifier.

Additionally, you have adapted your warden form to be fluid and fierce. You gain the following additional benefits while in warden form:

*   Your walking speed increased by 10 feet.
*   Your melee weapon attacks deal an extra 1d8 force damage on a hit.

### Protector’s Resolve

At 14th level, your will is inseparable from that of the forest, and you learn to command nature as your blade. You always have the wrath of nature spell prepared, and it doesn't count against the total number of druid spells you have prepared. Additionally, as part of the same bonus action that you take to activate your warden form, you may cast this spell. If you cast wrath of nature in this way, it will not require concentration, and the area of effect remains centered on and moves with you throughout the duration of the spell. You may only cast this spell in this way once per long rest.

Druid: Wildfire: Spirit Adept

# Druid: Wildfire: Spirit Adept

Optional (Additional 2nd Level Feature)

Starting at Second Level, if you have the Elemental Adept (fire) Feature. It also applies to your Wildfire Spirit

Dual Wielding Master

# Dual Wielding Master

**Prerequisite**

Dual Wielder feat

**Description**

Excessive training and mastery of fighting with two weapons has allowed you to make use of your attacking momentum as well as to strike swiftly against those that would grant you an opportunity

*   If you have hit with at least three attacks on your turn, including your two-weapon fighting bonus attack. You can make another attack as part of that bonus action
*   When a creature provokes an opportunity attack from you while you are engaged in two-weapon fighting, you can make one attack with each weapon you are wielding as part of that reaction, instead of a single attack.

Duality Blade

# Duality Blade

*Weapon (double bladed Scimitar), rare*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

One Blade of Light contains the essence of goodness and holiness, the other Blade of Darkness contains the essence of evil and deceit.

When attacking with this weapon you choose with which blade you attack.

**Light Blade**: Deals an additional 1d6 radiant damage, and another 1d4 against undead or fiends

**Dark Blade:** Deals an additional 1d6 necrotic damage, and restores 1d4+4 hp to the wielder if a humanoid or celestial is killed using this blade.

The additional damage applies to the bonus attack damage.

Dugger'S Tome

# Dugger's Tome

*Wondrous Item, rare*

**Description**

A Dugger's Tome is a large leather bound book. Each tome can only be used once by placing it on any book, as soon as it touches the other book, the Tome will become an exact replica of the copied book, and the copied book now has a magical aura of conjuration magic, whatever happens to one book will likewise happen to the other if both are on the same plane (Words written, water spilled, pages torn etc)

It was originally created to sync libraries and records across long distances but was later adapted by spies and criminal for espionage.

Eldritch Knight: Improved War Magic

# Eldritch Knight: Improved War Magic

18th level feature: - Optional (Replaces Standard Improved War Magic)

When you take the Attack action on your turn, you can replace two of the attacks with a casting of one of your Wizard spells of 1st to 4th level that has a casting time of one action.

Elemental Weapon

# Elemental Weapon

*Weapon(any), rare*

**Description**

You have a +1 bonus to attack rolls made with this magic weapon and on a hit, the weapon deals an extra 1d6 damage of a type determined by the runes engraved upon the weapon, which the DM chooses or determines randomly.


D5Extra Damage Type
1Fire
2Cold
3Lightning
4Thunder
5Acid

Endless Flask Of Hot Cocoa

# Endless Flask of Hot Cocoa

*Wondrous Item, uncommon*

**Description**

A simple, platinum-plated square flask carved with simple, almost childish sylvan runes. You cannot fill this flask with anything, as it is perpetually filled with fresh hot cocoa, peppered with little marshmallows, and partially melted chocolate chips.

Sprinkling a pinch of flavorings, such as caramel sugars or peppermint crumbs, will permanently flavor the hot cocoa similarly. This can be reversed by dropping in a pinch of unflavored chocolate chips.

If you pour hot chocolate from the bottle, rather than drinking from it, the liquid vanishes the moment it leaves the bottle.

Enduring Scabbard

# Enduring Scabbard

Wondrous , Common

**Description**

While a sword is within the scabbard, the blade can't be damaged by fire or rusted by water. In addition, the scabbard preserves the current sharpness of the blade ensuring it never dulls while sheathed.

Ethical Planar Binding

# Ethical Planar Binding

*5th-level abjuration. (Bard, Druid, Cleric, Wizard, Arcana Domain)*

**Meta**

**Casting Time**: 1 hour

**Range**: 60 feet

**Components**: V, S, M (a jewel worth at least 1,000 gp, which the spell consumes)

**Duration**: 24 hours

**Description**

With this spell, you attempt to bind a celestial, an elemental, a fey, or a fiend to the Prime Material Plane for the Duration. The creature must be within range for the entire casting of the spell. At the completion of the casting, the target must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is persuaded to stay on this plane for the duration. If the creature was summoned or created by another spell, that spell's duration is extended to match the duration of this spell.

A bound creature is friendly to you and follows your instructions to the best of its ability. You might command the creature to accompany you on an adventure, to guard a location, or to deliver a message. The creature obeys the letter of your instructions. If the creature carries out your instructions completely before the spell ends, it travels to you to report this fact if you are on the same plane of existence. If you are on a different plane of existence, it returns to the place where you bound it and remains there until the spell ends.

**Ethical Caveat**: The binding is a mutual agreement. Should the celestial, elemental. fey, or fiend feel that its binder is acting contrary to its original alignment, or forcing the creature to act in ways that it would not normally wish to do, the summoned creature may make a new CHA save with advantage to immediately teleport back to its own plane of existence. It may do this every round and will not take other actions beyond this save and protecting itself.

**At Higher Levels**

When you cast this spell using a spell slot of a higher level, the duration increases to 10 days with a 6th-level slot, to 30 days with a 7th- level slot, to 180 days with an 8th-level slot, and to a year and a day with a 9th-level spell slot.

Exorcist

# Exorcist

Exorcists use their spiritual abilities to purge the lands of evil spirits and those that would do harm to society. With pure souls and unmatched bravery, they face the most vile of creation head-on.

## Horror Hunter

At 3rd level, you become adept in tracking down and hunting all manners of evil. You gain proficiency in Investigation and Survival, and undead and fiends have disadvantage on saving throws against your exorcist arts.

If the target of your spirit tether is a creature and is reduced to 0 hit points, you can regain a number of spirit points equal to half your spiritualist level (rounded down). If the target was an undead or a fiend, you regain additional spirit points equal to your wisdom modifier. Once you use this feature, you must finish a short or long rest before you can use it again.

## Exorcist Arts

Also at 3rd level, you gain access to new spirit arts that reflect your power over evil forces.

### Spirit Leash

If your tethered target is a creature, you can use your action to spend 1 spirit point to root your target in place. The target must make a strength saving throw or be restrained for one minute and take 2d6 radiant damage at the start of each of its turns. A target can repeat this saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending this effect on a success.

### Radiant Lash

You can use an action and spend 1 spirit point to lash out at your target with your righteous spirit. The target must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, the target takes 4d6 radiant damage, taking half of the damage on a success.

### Spirit Flare

You can use an action and spend 1 spirit point to cover your target in radiant light. The target must make a Charisma saving throw or be covered in an aura of light that emits bright sunlight for 30 feet and dim light for another 30 feet. When the target fails its saving throw against your radiant lash while this sunlight persists, you can blind them until the end of its next turn.

## Exorcize

At 6th level, you can use an action to exorcise creatures of your choice that you can see within 60 feet of yourself. For each creature, you can spend 1 spirit point to end one effect that’s causing the creature to be charmed, frightened, or possessed.

## Cleansing Field

At 13th level, you can cleanse an area and temporarily ward it from evil. As an action, you can spend 3 spirit points to create a field in a 20-foot-radius sphere at a point you can see within 120 feet of yourself. The area becomes pure, and creatures of your choice within the area cannot be frightened, charmed, or possessed, and have advantage on Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws. The field lasts for one minute, whereupon its cleansing abilities fade and its effects end.

## Master Exorcist

At 18th level, you master your abilities to drive evil from this world. You learn the following spirit art:

### True Banishment

You can use 10 minutes of chanting and 20 spirit points to attempt to banish a creature from existence. At the end of the ten minutes, the target must make a Charisma saving throw or be banished. Regardless of the target’s native plane, they are banished to an empty and infinite demiplane that is accessible by nothing less than a god. It is impossible for the creature to leave the demiplane from the inside by any means. While banished, the target does not need to breathe, eat, or sleep, and it doesn’t age. Divination spells can’t locate or perceive the target.

You can spend 10 minutes to bring a banished creature back from its banishment. If you’re within 30 feet of the location the target was originally banished from, you can bring them back to a point you can see within 30 feet of you.

Eye Of The Bookworm

# Eye of the Bookworm

*Wondrous item, uncommon*

**Description**

This small, glass sphere is enchanted with weak divination magics to help you find specific words or phrases in writing. It has a stylized iris etched into the glass.

You can use a bonus action to speak the Eye's command word aloud, followed by the word or phrase you're searching for. The word or phrase magically appears and floats within the sphere. By holding the Eye in a free hand, your vision becomes magically enhanced to subconsciously highlight the word or phrase you searched for in a green light whenever you see it. Once you've used the Eye's ability for the first time, you can change the word or phrase as many times as you like for the next hour, after which the item can not be used again until the following dawn.

Faceless Cloak

# Faceless Cloak

*Wondrous Item, uncommon*

**Description**

There are several known versions of this hooded cloak; some look plain and common, while others appear intricate and expensive. When the hood is raised, a minor illusion appears which hides the wearer's face in total magical darkness. The wearer can see out with no penalties, but only truesight or other magical means of seeing through illusions can penetrate the hood's darkness from outside.

Faerie Elf

# Faerie Elf

***Physical Description***

*Faerie elves are roughly the same height as gnomes but weigh roughly 25 to 60 lbs. Faeries tend to have more vibrant eye colors of shimmering greens, blues, and reds. They have a pair of transparent wings that allow them to fly. Other than that they look like most wood elves.*

***History***

*Although not many people have seen these crossbreeds, the faerie elf is a mixture of a elf and a fey. They tend to keep their societies and culture hidden from public view. Faerie elves are known to have many conflicts within their society, from what little has been observed. They tend to have very different views of other races and cultures.*

***Society***

*Faerie elves tend to keep to themselves and tend to hide from other outside sources, mainly due to the harsh discrimination and prejudice their ancestors have experienced. They are buried deep within forests and caves and have a strong sense of family. They tend to have a hard time developing new relations with others, especially if they're neither fey, elf, or faerie. The ones who do leave the villages tend to be brave adventurers determined to prove the old ways are over.*

Elf Subrace

#### **Ability Score Increase**

Your Charisma score increases by 1.

#### **Size**

Unlike most elves Faerie elves range from 3 to over 4 foot tall but have slender builds and weigh very little. Your size is Small.

#### **Speed**

Your base walking speed is 20 feet and your flying speed is 30 feet.

#### **Fairy Wings**

You have a pair of light, transparent wings that grant you a fly speed of 30-feet, allowing you a certain degree of flight. However, these wings can only fly up to a height of 10 feet, any higher and they fail until you are 10 feet above the ground again. This flight speed cannot negate damage inflicted from falling a height of 20 feet or more, only halve it. You lose your flying speed until you complete a short or long rest if they are directly exposed to water.

#### **Tranquil Magic**

The typical faerie focuses more on their mind rather than their strength. You know one cantrip of your choice from the cleric spell list. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for it.

Failnaught

# Failnaught

*Ammunition (Arrow), Legendary*

**Description**

This arrow has a black lusterous shaft and adorned by an arrowhead made from a crystal like material that shines a gentle blue hue.

In its original form, this magic arrow is ammuntion for bows but can alter its form(no action required) into a bolt that can be loaded to crossbows. The Failnaught never misses on a ranged attack and is an automatic hit, it will curve and move on its own in mid-air until it hits its target. If no open pathway is available, it pierces through any physical obstruction that is less than 1 foot thick or any magical barrier that may block its path (i.e. a metal door or a wall of force spell). The Failnaught uses a damage die of 1d8 instead of the weapon's damage die and ignores any damage resistances and immunities that it deals or inflicts. It retains its magical properties after an attack and can be reused again.

Alternatively, the Failnaught can be used without a bow or crossbow, it can be used as a dart with a damage die of 1d6.

\[When obtained in any way, quest, loot, craft(if CMs make it craftable), etc., you only get 1 arrow and not a bundle.\]

Fallen King'S Halberd

# Fallen King's Halberd

[![Fallen King's Halberd](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/3/34/Image.png/revision/latest/scale-to-width-down/180?cb=20241007205105)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/3/34/Image.png/revision/latest?cb=20241007205105)

[](/docs/file:image.png)

Fallen King's Halberd

*Weapon (halberd), legendary*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

This halberd was owned by a great king whose name is long forgotten who reigned from a kingdom that has long been destroyed. Its old but strangely robust body has scattered dents and damage from all around. In contrast, both the point and blade remain seamless and sharp against all the roughness of use and wear it has experienced.

**Unflinching**. While you wield this weapon, you gain immunity to the charmed and frightened condition.

**Ever Strong.** When you hit with this weapon, you deal an extra 5 weapon damage. If you have less than half your maximum hit points, this damage instead becomes 10.

**Till my Last Breath**. When you drop to 0 hit points, you can use your reaction to make a weapon attack with this weapon on that turn at a target within range with advantage before going down. If you have the Extra Attack feature, you can make those attacks from this reaction as if its an Attack action(No other feature or effect can treat this reaction this way). You regain the ability to use this reaction again at dawn.

Far Touched

# Far Touched

Exposure to distant forces - those that observe from beyond time or space - has left a mark on your soul. You now wield magic that shields and strikes in equal measure.

*   Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma score by 1, to a maximum of 20.,
*   You learn the sanctuary spell and one 1st-level spell of your choice. The 1st-level spell must be from the abjuration or evocation school of magic. You can cast each of these spells without expending a spell slot. Once you cast either of these spells in this way, you can't cast that spell in this way again until you finish a long rest. You can also cast these spells using spell slots you have of the appropriate level. The spells' spellcasting ability is the ability increased by this feat.

Fey Duelist

# Fey Duelist

## Description

The Fey Duelist archetype merges the cunning and agility of a rogue with the enchanting power of the Feywild. These rogues are gifted (or cursed) with the boons of Archfey patrons, weaving magic and mischief into their every movement. They are agile combatants and masters of manipulation, channeling Fey magic to outmaneuver foes, charm allies, and spread chaos on the battlefield.

Whether as emissaries of a Fey court, champions of their Archfey patron, or independent agents who stumbled into the gifts of the Feywild, Fey Duelists tread a fine line between mortal pragmatism and the capricious beauty of the Fey. Their motivations are as varied as the Feywild itself, often driven by a blend of personal ambition, devotion to their patron, or a desire to unweave the chains of mundane reality and reshape the world with Fey-inspired artistry.

#### Playstyle and Motivations

The Fey Duelist excels in roles that combine agility, cunning, and creativity. They bring versatility to combat and social interactions, offering a mix of mobility, control, and spellcasting that allows them to adapt to nearly any situation. Players who enjoy weaving spells and martial combat together will find the Fey Duelist a rewarding choice.

Motivations for a Fey Duelist might include:

*   **Devotion to an Archfey**: Serving a powerful Fey entity as a trusted agent or champion, spreading their influence or carrying out their will.
*   **Personal Freedom**: Using Fey gifts to escape or defy systems of control, embracing the chaotic and whimsical nature of the Feywild.
*   **Vengeance or Redemption**: Seeking to right a past wrong or balance a perceived injustice using the unique powers of the Fey.
*   **Curiosity and Mischief**: Exploring the boundaries of mortal and Fey realms, motivated by wonder and the joy of discovery.

#### Who Would Thrive as a Fey Duelist?

The Fey Duelist appeals to characters who want to:

*   **Command the Social Arena**: Features like *Binding Words* provide robust tools for persuasion, manipulation, and intrigue.
*   **Outmaneuver and Disrupt Enemies**: Abilities like *Elusive Action* and *Distant Dodge* emphasize mobility and tactical positioning.

#### Ideal Players and Characters

*   **The Charismatic Manipulator**: Players who enjoy talking their way out of trouble, influencing NPCs, and using charm to solve problems.
*   **The Tactical Trickster**: Players who like to use cunning, positioning, and the environment to control the flow of combat.
*   **The Fey Enthusiast**: Players who love the whimsical, mysterious, and chaotic themes of the Feywild and want to embody its spirit.

**Features**

*   [3rd Level - Spellcasting](#3rd_Level_-_Spellcasting)
*   [3rd Level - Vexing](#3rd_Level_-_Vexing)
*   [9th Level - Delicate Deception](#9th_Level_-_Delicate_Deception)
*   [13th Level - Binding Words](#13th_Level_-_Binding_Words)
*   [17th Level - Archfeys Guile](#17th_Level_-_Archfeys_Guile)

## **3rd Level - Spellcasting**

When you reach 3rd level, you augment your martial prowess with the ability to cast spells.

**Cantrips**

You learn three cantrips: Vicious Mockery and two other cantrips of your choice from the warlock or bard spell list. You learn another warlock or bard cantrip of your choice at 10th level.

**Spell Slots**

The Fey Duelist Spellcasting table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your warlock or bard spells of 1st level and higher. To cast one of these spells, you must expend a slot of the spell's level or higher. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a long rest.

For example, if you know the 1st-level spell Charm Person and have a 1st-level and a 2nd-level spell slot available, you can cast Charm Person using either slot.

**Spells Known of 1st Level and Higher**

You know three 1st-level warlock or bard spells of your choice, two of which you must choose from the enchantment and illusion spells on the warlock or bard spell list.

The Spells Known column of the Fey Duelist Spellcasting table shows when you learn more warlock or bard spells of 1st level or higher. Each of these spells must be an enchantment or illusion spell of your choice, and must be of a level for which you have spell slots. For instance, when you reach 7th level in this class, you can learn one new spell of 1st or 2nd level.

The spells you learn at 8th, 14th, and 20th level can come from any school of magic.

Whenever you gain a level in this class, you can replace one of the warlock or bard spells you know with another spell of your choice from the warlock or bard spell list. The new spell must be of a level for which you have spell slots, and it must be an enchantment or illusion spell, unless you're replacing the spell you gained at 3rd, 8th, 14th, or 20th level from any school of magic.

**Spellcasting Ability**

Charisma is your spellcasting ability for your warlock or bard spells, since you learn your spells through dedicated study and memorization. You use your Charisma whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability. In addition, you use your Charisma modifier when setting the saving throw DC for a warlock or bard spell you cast and when making an attack roll with one.

Spell save DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier

Spell attack modifier = your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier

## **3rd Level - Vexing**

Start at 3rd level, when you cast Vicious Mockery, you can now cast it as a Bonus Action. Additionally, the first attack roll, ability check or saving throw you make against an affected creature before the end of your next turn is made with advantage.

## **9th Level - Delicate Deception**

Starting at 9th level, you can weave Fey Magic into your movements and attacks, enhancing them. Once per turn, you can modify a rogue feature to add an additional effect, as listed below:

*   **Fey Attack**: Sneak Attack deals an additional 1d6 psychic damage. This additional damage is increased at 13th (2d6) level & 17th (3d6) level.

*   **Elusive Action**: Immediately before or after using Cunning Action, a 5ft cube surrounding you becomes Heavily Obscured to everyone except you until the start of your next turn.

*   **Distant Dodge**: Whenever you use Uncanny Dodge after being hit with an attack, you may teleport up to 10 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.

You can use this ability a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. You can also regain uses by expending a spell slot for each use you want to restore (no action required).

## **13th Level - Binding Words**

At 13th level, you can weave a magical enchantment into your words, undermining and binding those that hear it. As an action you can speak a word infused with fey magic to a creature within 30 feet that can hear you, forcing them to make a Wisdom saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier). On a failed saving throw, fey magic binds it, and the only actions that it can take before the end of its next turn are the Attack (one weapon attack only), Dash, Disengage, or Hide action. If the target succeeds on its saving throw, it has no hint that you tried to bind it.

Any creature under the effect of Vicious Mockery cast via your Vexing feature automatically fails the saving throw for this feature.

Once you use this ability, you cannot use it again until you finish a short or long rest. You can also regain uses by expending a spell slot (3rd level) for each use you want to restore (no action required).

## **17th Level - Archfeys Guile**

Starting at 17th level, as an action you can make a Charisma (Persuasion or Deception) check against a creature that you can see that isn’t incapacitated, contested by a Wisdom (Insight) check. If you succeed, for the next minute, any deception or suggestions you make towards that creature are suffused with fey magic, and that creature remembers the conversation in a way that favors your intent, even creating temporary artificial memories that support your statements. Any contradictory evidence to oppose your deception is disregarded and they will rationalize any memories or statements from friends as illogical or even possible attempts to deceive them. When presented with facts contradicting your deception, the target can repeat the Wisdom (Insight) check, ending the beguiling effects on a success.

This benefit lasts for 10 minutes or until you successfully use this feature against a different creature. The effects of your magic linger on the target for up to 1 hour after you’ve successfully used this feature, or until they have a Dispel Magic or Remove Curse cast upon them. If the target remained beguiled for the full duration, the affected memories and altered perceptions become permanent.

Fighter'S Band

# Fighter's Band

*Wondrous Item, uncommon(+1), rare(+2), very rare(+3)*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement by a fighter

**Description**

This finely detailed band can be a ring, bracelet, necklace or anklet. While wearing it, you gain a bonus to the saving throw DCs of your fighter features. The bonus is determined by the band's rarity.

In addition, when you use your *Second Wind* feature, you can regain additional hit points equal to 1d10 + half your fighter level. You can't use this feature again until the next dawn.

Fighter Subclasses

# Fighter Subclasses

## [Blood Knight](/docs/blood-knight "blood knight")

*The blood knight is the title given to an eldritch knight who chooses to delve into the taboo discipline of hemomancy, or the magic of controlling blood and life force. These warriors thrive in the heart of battle where the blood flows thickest, using it to enhance their offensive and defensive capabilities.*

### Combat Hemomancy

Starting at 3rd level when you choose this archetype, you gain access to a series of hemomantic abilities that allow you to use the blood of your enemies to your advantage.

When you damage a creature that has blood within 30 feet of you with a weapon attack, you gain a special die called a blood die, which is a D6.  You can have a number of these dice up to a maximum equal to your proficiency bonus. Creatures that do not have blood do not generate blood dice, and you do not gain blood dice from attacks that are modified by blood dice.

You can expend blood dice to perform a number of hemomantic abilities described at the end of this features description.

Each time a hemomantic ability asks you to expend your blood dice, you can choose how many you want to roll and gain a benefit based on what you rolled.

#### **Hemomantic Abilities**

**Combat Regeneration.** At the start of your turn, you can spend blood dice to gain temporary hit points. Roll the dice you spend, add them together, and gain temporary hit points equal to the total, replacing any temporary hit points you already have.

**Crimson Strike.** You can use the Attack action to make a special melee attack, a crimson strike. If you're able to make multiple attacks with the Attack action, this attack replaces one of them. You brandish the weapon used in the technique and make a melee attack with it against one creature within 15 feet of you. On a hit, the target takes magical piercing, bludgeoning, or slashing damage equal to the total of the blood dice you spent.

**Blood Shell.** When you are hit by an attack, you can use your reaction to spend blood dice. Roll the dice you spend, add them together, and increase your AC by the amount equal to the total rolled against that attack.

**Blood Clotting.** As a bonus action, you can spend blood dice to throw blood onto a surface at a range of 15 feet and have it stick in a 25 ft. square. Any creature that starts its turn in that area or that enters that area during its turn must make a Strength saving throw with a DC of 10 + the amount you roll on your blood dice (A maximum DC of 8 + proficiency bonus + Strength or Dexterity modifier). On a failed save, the creature's speed is reduced to zero until the start of their next turn.

### Sense Life

At 7th level, you gain advantage on wisdom (perception) checks to spot and track enemies that have blood in their veins. In addition, your ability to sense blood can be so precise that you have advantage wisdom (insight) checks to tell if someone is lying to you.

### Blood-Fueled Overdrive

At 10th level, when you use your indomitable feature, you can expend one blood die and add it to the roll.

### Hemomantic Precision

At 15th level, you have a very tight control over blood you spill. You can re-roll one blood die once whenever you roll them for a hemomantic ability.

### Bloody Tyrant

At 18th level, when you hit a creature with an attack and have the maximum number of blood dice, you can expend them all to force the creature to make a Constitution saving throw with a DC of 8 + proficiency bonus + Strength or Dexterity modifier. On a failed save, the creature is paralyzed for 1 minute. At the end of each of its turns, the paralyzed target must make a Constitution saving throw at the end of each of its turns. On a successful save, this paralyzing effect ends.

## [Daredevil](/docs/daredevil "daredevil")

*The Daredevil fighter thrives on style, versatility and unpredictably. Their use of different weapon types makes them dangerous to any foe and they gain more confidence the more stylish their fighting is.*

### Daredevil impulse

Starting at 3rd level, every time you make an attack of a type different from the previous attack in the current combat, you gain one Style point. You have a maximum amount of style points equal to your proficiency bonus. When you roll Initiative your Style points reset to 0.

Attack types are classified as: Melee weapon attack, Ranged weapon attack and thrown attacks.

Style Points can be used to power your Flairs.

#### Flairs:

*   **Wide attack:** If you hit with a melee attack, you may spend 2 Style points to deal melee damage equal to half your Fighter level to all foes within 5 feet of your target.
*   **Laser Focus:** Spend 2 Style Points to gain advantage on your next attack roll.
*   **Range Extender:** You can add 10 feet to any ranged or thrown weapon's normal range for every point spent
*   **Rush:** Gain 10 feet of movement speed for each Style Point spent until the end of your turn.
*   **Burst (4th level and up):** As a bonus action, Spend 3 Style points to make a weapon attack.

### Hotswap

At 3rd level, whenever you make an attack roll with a weapon, you can sheathe it and draw another in the same action. You can only do this once per action.

### Fight or Flight

From 7th level you have a choice between staying in action and fighting defensively or moving around or to continue as is.  As a bonus action you can choose to either fight or flee from combat.

*   **Fight:** As a Bonus action you can give yourself a Bonus to your AC equal to the amount of Style Points you spend. This bonus lasts until the end of your next turn or until you are incapacitated. While Fight is active you cannot gain more Style points.
*   **Flight:** You can spend 2 Style points to disengage, you can't be targeted by opportunity attacks. You gain an additional 10 feet of movement until the end of your turn for every additional Style Point spent.

### Double Trouble

You can choose an additional Fighting Style.

Additionally, using the Disengage, Dodge or Dash actions also builds your Style chain. For example: If you use a dodge action and make an attack on your next turn or vice versa you also gain a Style Point

### Wild Rush

Starting at 15th level, when an enemy within 30 feet of you gets incapacitated or restrained you can use 2 Style Points as an reaction to dash straight toward the enemy. You move 10 feet for every point spent (minimum 2 points) and can spend additional Style for added Rush movement. You can  sheath your current weapon and draw another as part of the dash and make a single attack against that target.

### Inferno Trigger

From 18th level, once per short rest you can expend 6 Style points as an action to trigger your Inferno action.

**Inferno action:** Can attack 6 times, ignore the loading and ammunition properties if the weapon has it. Any Critical hit deals an additional 10 damage of the weapons damage type. These attacks don't provide any Style points

## [Deadeye](/docs/deadeye "deadeye")

The embodiment of grit, luck, and a well-placed shot, fighters who choose this martial archetype strive to emulate paradigms of accuracy. Deadeyes generally prefer fighting from comfortable ranges, employing the use of crossbows, firearms, and even throwing knives if a situation calls for them.

### Perfect Sixth

Once per attack action beginning at 3rd level, when you make a ranged weapon attack roll against a creature, you can add a d6 to the result. You can add the d6 before or after seeing the results of the attack roll, but before the effects are applied. If the result of the d6 is a 6, you severely injure your target. Roll on the severe injury table to see the effect of the injury.

##### Severe Injury Table

| 2d6 | Severe Injury |
| --- | --- |
| 2 | Shock. The target is stunned until the end of its next turn. |
| 3 | Fracture. The target can only take either an action, bonus action, or move on its next turn. |
| 4 | Punctured Lung. The target gains one level of exhaustion for the next minute. |
| 5 | Crushed Throat. The target cannot speak, use verbal components, or use any features that involve its voice until the end of its next turn. |
| 6 | Crushed Grip. The target drops anything it is currently holding, including grappled creatures. |
| 7 | Whiplash. The attack deals one additional die of weapon damage. |
| 8 | Broken Leg. The target’s movement speed is halved until the end of its next turn. |
| 9 | Embedded Ammunition. Until the end of its next turn, for every 5 feet the target moves, it takes 1d6 slashing damage. |
| 10 | Tear. The target has disadvantage on melee attacks until the end of its next turn. |
| 11 | Blind. The target is blinded until the end of its next turn. |
| 12 | Dead Shot. The triggering hit becomes a critical hit. |

Before you make a ranged weapon attack, you can take a -5 penalty to the attack roll. If you hit, you apply a severe injury immediately. You can only apply one severe injury with each attack action.

A creature cannot be affected by the same severe injury more than once at a time, and a creature can expend one use of legendary resistance to ignore a severe injury’s effect.

### Line ‘em Up

At 7th level, you become a master at hitting successive targets. When you take the attack action on your turn, each time you target a different creature than any other you’ve targeted this turn, you gain a cumulative +1 bonus to the attack roll.

### Gambit

At 10th level, when you roll initiative, you can gambit a d4, d6, d8, or d10. Roll the chosen die and subtract it from your initiative. Until the end of your next turn, every creature below you in initiative takes extra damage when hit with your ranged attack rolls equal to the chosen die.

### Duelist’s Aim

At 15th level, when you use the attack action to make a ranged weapon attack and you hit at least three consecutive attacks, your third attack inflicts a severe injury.

### High Noon

At 18th level, you are adept at ending multiple targets at once. As an action, choose any number of targets that you can see within a cone the size of your weapon’s short range, up to 60 feet. You may make one ranged weapon attack against each target you chose inflicting the Dead Shot severe injury with each of them. You may use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining all uses on a long rest.

## [Raven Knight](/docs/raven-knight "raven knight")

*A Raven Knight is fearsome in battle, fusing martial prowess with strange abilities gifted by the Raven Queen. Many Raven Knights work alone, though some may find a small band of companions to aid them in battle, and some may even command and lead entire armies into battle. Masters of death, Raven Knights seek to protect those who cannot protect themselves from undeath and the afterlife. Powerful Raven Knights may even become champions of the Raven Queen, and thus champions of the battlefield.*

### Knowledge of Death

When you choose this Archetype at 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the Investigation and Medicine skills. Additionally, you can make Intelligence (Investigation) and Wisdom (Medicine) checks with advantage when attempting to determine the cause of death of a creature.

### Soul Severing

At 3rd level, when you reduce a creature to 0 hit points you sever the connection of the creatures soul to its body. That creature cannot be turned into an undead. Additionally, as a reaction, when a creature is reduced to 0 hit points within 15ft of you you can empower your next attack with that creatures soul energy. Your next weapon attack deals additional necrotic damage equal to half your fighter level.

### Feather Step

At 7th level, you move with the grace of the raven. Your walking speed increases by 10ft. Additionally, as a bonus action you gain a flight speed equal to your walking speed until the end of your turn, if you end your turn in midair you drop to the ground.

### Queens Blessing

At 10th level, the Raven Queen grants you a blessing. You gain advantage on death saving throws, immunity to the frightened condition, and resistance to necrotic damage.

### Deaths Embrace

At 15th level, when you or an ally critically hit a creature, you can use your reaction to add 3d10 necrotic damage to the attacks damage roll.

### Duty in Death

At 18th level, when you are reduced to 0 hit points or killed outright you can use your reaction to make a weapon attack against the creature that killed you. On a hit you critically hit that creature and regain hit points equal to the damage dealt. You can do this once per long rest.

## [Revised Arcane Archer](/docs/revised-arcane-archer "revised arcane archer")

### Arcane Archer Lore

At 3rd level, you learn magical theory or some of the secrets of nature -- typical for practitioners of this elven martial tradition. You choose to gain proficiency in either the Arcana or the Nature skill, and you choose to learn either the prestidigitation or the druidcraft cantrip.

### Arcane Shot

At 3rd level, you learn to unleash special magical effects with some of your shots. When you gain this feature, you learn two Arcane Shot options of your choice (see Arcane Shot Options below).

Once per turn when you fire an arrow or bolt from any bow as part of the Attack action, you can apply one of your Arcane Shot options to that piece of ammunition. You decide to use the option when the ammunition hits a creature, unless the option doesn't involve an attack roll. You have a number of uses equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses of it when you finish a short or long rest.

You gain an additional Arcane Shot option of your choice when you reach certain levels in this class: 7th, 10th, 15th, and 18th level. Each option also improves when you become a 15th-level fighter.

#### Arcane Shot Options (Revised)

The Arcane Shot feature lets you choose options for it at certain levels. The options are presented here in alphabetical order. They are all magical effects, and each one is associated with one of the schools of magic.

If an option requires a saving throw, your Arcane Shot save DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Intelligence modifier.

##### Banishing Arrow

You use abjuration magic to try to temporarily banish your target to a harmless location in the Feywild. The creature hit by the arrow must also succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be banished for 1 minute, or until you release the magic as a free action. While banished in this way, its speed is 0, and it is incapacitated. The affected creature can make a Charisma saving throw at the end of their turn, ending the effect on a success. At the end of the duration, the target reappears in the space it vacated or in the nearest unoccupied space if that space is occupied.

After you reach 15th level in this class, a target also takes 4d6 force damage when the arrow hits it.

##### Beguiling Arrow

Your enchantment magic causes this arrow to temporarily beguile its target. The creature hit by the arrow takes an extra 2d6 psychic damage, and choose one of your allies within 30 feet of the target. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw, or it is charmed by the chosen ally for 1 minute or until you release the magic as a free action. The target or any creature that can reach it can use its action to remove the arrow with a successful Strength (Athletics) check against your Arcane Shot save DC, ending the effect on a success. Additionally, this effect ends early if the chosen ally attacks the charmed target, deals damage to it, or forces it to make a saving throw.

The psychic damage increases to 4d6 when you reach 15th level in this class.

##### Bursting Arrow

You imbue your arrow with force energy drawn from the school of evocation. The arrow detonates after your attack. Immediately after the arrow hits the creature, the target and all other creatures within 10 feet of it take 2d6 force damage each and succeed on a Strength saving throw against your Arcane Shot save DC, or be knocked prone.

The force damage increases to 4d6 when you reach 15th level in this class. (edited)

##### Enfeebling Arrow

You weave necromantic magic into your arrow. The creature hit by the arrow takes an extra 2d6 necrotic damage on hit, as well as at the start of each of their turns for 1 minute or until you release the magic as a free action. The target must also succeed on a Constitution saving throw, or the damage dealt by its weapon attacks is halved until the start of your next turn. The target or any creature that can reach it can use its action to remove the arrow with a successful Strength (Athletics) check against your Arcane Shot save DC, ending the effect on a success.

The necrotic damage increases to 4d6 when you reach 15th level in this class.

##### Grasping Arrow

When this arrow strikes its target, conjuration magic creates grasping, poisonous brambles, which wrap around the target. The creature hit by the arrow takes an extra 2d6 poison damage, its speed is reduced by 10 feet, and it takes 2d6 slashing damage the first time on each turn it moves 1 foot or more without teleporting. The target or any creature that can reach it can use its action to remove the brambles with a successful Strength (Athletics) check against your Arcane Shot save DC. The brambles last for 1 minute, until you use another option, or until you release the magic as a free action.

The poison damage and slashing damage both increase to 4d6 when you reach 15th level in this class.

##### Piercing Arrow

You use transmutation magic to give your arrow an ethereal quality. When you use this option, you don’t make an attack roll for the attack. Instead, the arrow fires forward in a line, which is 5 foot wide and 30 feet long, before disappearing. The arrow passes harmlessly through objects, ignoring cover. Each creature in that line must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes damage as if it were hit by the arrow, plus an extra 2d6 piercing damage. On a successful save, a target takes half as much damage. This option lasts for 1 minute, until you use another option, or until you release the magic as a free action.

The piercing damage increases to 4d6 when you reach 15th level in this class.

##### Seeking Arrow

Using divination magic, you grant your arrow the ability to seek out your target, allowing the arrow to curve and twist its path in search of its prey. When you use this option, you don’t make an attack roll for the attack. Instead, choose one creature you have seen in the past minute. The arrow flies toward that creature, moving around corners if necessary and ignoring three-quarters cover and half cover. If the target is within the weapon’s range and there is a path large enough for the arrow to travel to the target, the target must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, it takes damage as if it were hit by the arrow, plus an extra 2d6 force damage, and you learn the target’s current location and distance for 1 hour or until you release the magic as a free action. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage, and you only learn the targets current location and distance for one round. The target or any creature that can reach it can use its action to remove the arrow with a successful Strength (Athletics) check against your Arcane Shot save DC, ending the effect on a success.

The force damage increases to 4d6 when you reach 15th level in this class. (edited)

##### Shadow Arrow

You weave illusion magic into your arrow, causing it to occlude your foe’s vision with shadows. The creature hit by the arrow takes an extra 2d6 psychic damage, and it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be unable to see anything farther than 5 feet away for 1 minute or until you release the magic as a free action. The target or any creature that can reach it can use its action to remove the arrow with a successful Strength (Athletics) check against your Arcane Shot save DC, ending the effect on a success.

The psychic damage increases to 4d6 when you reach 15th level in this class.

### Magic Ammunition

At 7th level, you gain the ability to infuse ammunition with magic. Whenever you fire a nonmagical arrow from a bow, you can make it magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage. The magic fades from the ammunition immediately after it hits or misses its target.

### Curving Shot

At 7th level, you learn how to direct an errant ammunition toward a new target. When you make an attack roll with a magic ammunition and miss, you can use a bonus action to reroll the attack roll against a different target within 60 feet of the original target.

### Quick-draw Shot

Starting at 15th level, your magical archery makes your movements swift. Whenever you hit a target with an Arcane Shot you can use your bonus action to make another ranged weapon attack against the same target using the same weapon.

### Eldritch Mastery

At 18th level, your mastery of blending magic with archery teaches you to infuse your arrows with even greater magic, as well as combining them for greater effect.

Once per attack action, you can infuse a single arrow with two different arcane shot options, excluding piercing shot, having the target be affected by and taking damage from both shots. However when doing this you expend two uses of your Arcane shot.

You have two uses of this ability, and you regain all expended uses of it when you finish a long rest.

## [Weapon Master](/docs/weapon-master "weapon master")

### Deft Grip

Starting at 3rd level, after you make a weapon attack using the attack action you can use a free action to swap to another weapon.

When you attack with a weapon, you receive a bonus to the attack based on the list below, however you may only benefit from a single bonus once per turn, even if your attack misses; You can however receive an additional bonus if you attack with a different weapon that causes a different type of damage. Ranged weapons do not benefit from Deft Grip, nor do generic “Elemental Weapons” (which collectively count as a single weapon), consumable items that grant additional damage, or spells.

Deft Grip DC: 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Dexterity modifier


Deft Grip List:
Damage TypeEffectBonus Damage
BludgeoningThe heavy blow causes both stone and steel to crumple. Additional 1d6 damage if AC ≥ 151d6 or 2d6 bludgeoning damage
PiercingA swift thrust gives you extra reach. +5ft attack range1d6 piercing damage
SlashingA swift slice carves through lightly armored opponents with ease. Additional 1d6 damage if AC ≤ 151d6 or 2d6 slashing damage
AcidThe acid begins to eat away at flesh and damage armor. Dexterity saving throw. Upon a failure, the creature’s AC is reduced by 1 until the end of your next turn. At level 10 the AC reduction is increased to 2, and at level 20 it is increased to 3.1d6 acid damage
ColdFrost begins to coalesce around the creature’s body, restricting movement. Constitution saving throw. Upon a failure the creature’s speed is reduced by half until the end of your next turn.1d6 cold damage
FireFlames engulf the target. Dexterity saving throw. Upon a failure the creature is set ablaze and takes 1d6 fire damage at the end of their turn. The fame can be put out by a suitable liquid or by using an action to extinguish it.1d6 fire damage
ForceThe weapon impacts with incredible force. Strength saving throw. Upon a failure the creature is knocked prone.1d6 force damage
LightningLightning arcs to the flesh of another creature on impact. A random hostile creature within 10ft is chosen. That creature makes a dexterity saving throw. Upon a failure, they take the full damage of the attack.1d6 lightning damage
NecroticThe necrotic energy tears apart facsimiles of life. Doubled damage against temporary hp and creatures not in their original form (wildshape, poly, true-poly, shapechange, etc). Any damage that goes over the temp HP or the HP of the transformed creature is not doubled when applied to their HP/base form.1d6 necrotic damage
PoisonPoison seeps into the wound. Constitution saving throw. Upon a failure the creature suffers from the poisoned condition. The creature can repeat the save at the end of each of their turns.1d6 poison damage
PsychicPsychic damage racks the mind of the target. The creature has disadvantage on any concentration saving throws for the damage caused by this attack.1d6 psychic damage
