RadiantRadiant light bursts from the weapon. Constitution saving throw. Upon a failure the creature is blinded. The creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns.1d6 radiant damage
ThunderA thundercrack roars forth from the weapon. Constitution saving throw. Upon a failure the creature is deafened. The creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns.1d6 thunder damage


### Flowing Stance

At 3rd level, when you successfully hit another creature with a weapon that benefits from the Deft Grip bonus you receive a +1 to hit and to damage for your next attack if your next attack will also benefit from the Deft Grip bonus. These bonuses stack then reset to zero at the end of your turn.

### Rapid Strike

Starting at 7th level, you learn to trade accuracy for swift strikes. If you take the Attack action on your turn and have advantage on an attack roll against against one of the targets, you can forgo the advantage for that roll to make an additional weapon attack against that target, as part of the same action. You can do so no more than once per turn.

### Shatter the Horde

Starting at 10th level you can expend a use of your Action Surge to cause your melee attacks to strike any number of creatures within 5 feet of you, with a separate attack roll for each target. This benefit lasts for a single turn. This does not prevent a second Action Surge from being used on the same turn.

### Focused Strike

Starting at level 15 you can expend a use of your Action Surge to focus all of your ability into a singular strike. Instead of making multiple attacks, you make a single attack against a target, and if it hits that attack is essentially multiplied by however many attacks you have remaining, including bonuses for each attack from Deft Grip and Flowing Stance; Additionally, after you use this ability, you can make no more attacks on the same turn. This does not prevent a second Action Surge from being used on the same turn. This ability must be declared prior to knowing if the Focused Strike hits.

### Omnipath

At level 18, attunement slots for weapons that require attunement can shared in a single slot however, you can not dual wield weapons that require attunement without using a second slot, you receive no benefit from the weapon when it is not in use (such as auras or other passive buffs), and you can use one of the weapons at a time.

## [Revised Purple Dragon Knight](/docs/revised-purple-dragon-knight "revised purple dragon knight")

### Rallying Cry

When you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you learn how to inspire your allies to fight on past their injuries.

When you use your Second Wind feature, each creature of your choice within 15 feet of you regains hit points equal to your fighter level, provided that the creature can see or hear you.

### Royal Envoy

A Purple Dragon knight serves as an envoy of the Cormyrean crown. Knights of high standing are expected to conduct themselves with grace.

At 7th level, you gain proficiency in the Persuasion skill. If you are already proficient in it, you gain proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: Animal Handling, Insight, Intimidation, or Performance.

Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses Persuasion. You receive this benefit regardless of the skill proficiency you gain from this feature.

### Inspiring Surge

Starting at 10th level, when you use your Action Surge feature, you can choose an amount of creatures equal to half your fighter level (rounded down) within 15 feet of you. Those creatures can make one melee or ranged weapon attack with their reaction, provided that they can see or hear you.

Starting at 18th level, the range of your inspiration increases to 30 feet.

### Bulwark

Beginning at 15th level, you can extend the benefit of your Indomitable feature to your allies. When you decide to use Indomitable to reroll an Intelligence, a Wisdom, or a Charisma saving throw and you aren't incapacitated, you can choose an amount of creatures equal to half your proficiency bonus (rounded down) within 30 feet of you that also failed its saving throw against the same effect. If those creatures can see or hear you, they can reroll their saving throw and must use the new roll.

### Loyalty to the Crown

Starting at 18th level, when you target an ally with any of your bannerette features, that ally gains advantage on the next attack roll, ability check, or saving throw it makes until the end of its next turn.

## [Jade Lord](/docs/jade-lord "jade lord")

*Throughout the continent of kara-tur, one mineral stands above all else in terms of status. Where in the west you would display decadence with gold, in the east, the truest symbol of power is jade. Legends tell of warriors embellished with regality, often proud in status and seeped in regality. The Jade Lords of Shou Lung were legendary figures that displayed power and wealth in equal measure, fighting with both honor and flair.*

### Regal Countenance

Even at 3rd level, you carry yourself with grace and nobility. You gain proficiency in either Persuasion or Intimidation. If you already have both of those proficiencies, you can choose a different proficiency instead.

### Jade Shards

At 3rd level, the gutteral flow of combat splinters jade around you. When you hit a creature with a weapon attack or a creature hits you with an attack, a jade shard splinters off of you and orbits you for the duration. You can only have a number of jade shards equal to half your fighter level (rounded up) at a time. When initiative ends, any remaining jade shards drop to the floor and disappear.

You can expend any number of these shards to take special actions called jade tricks. If the trick requires the target to make a saving throw, the DC is calculated as follows:

**Jade Trick DC**

\= 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice)

If you’re able to make multiple attacks with the Attack action, a trick replaces one of them. You can only use a trick once per attack action.

#### **Jade Blade.**

Make a melee attack with a melee weapon you are holding against a target within range. On a hit, the target suffers the weapon attack’s normal effects and takes a number of d6s in additional damage equal to the amount of shards spent.

#### **Nephrite Burst.**

Make a ranged or thrown weapon attack (your choice) against a target within 120 feet of you. The attack deals a number of d8s of piercing damage equal to the amount of shards spent.

#### **Jadeite Blast.**

Choose a point you can see within 60 feet of you. Creatures within 10 feet of that point must make a Dexterity saving throw or take a number of d10s in slashing damage equal to the jade shards spent.

### Mystic Stone

At 7th level, the jade you create is fortified. Damage from jade tricks count as magical for the purposes of overcoming resistance and immunity to non-magical damage.

### Jade Armor

At 10th level, you learn to pull in the jade shards orbiting you to use as armor. As a bonus action, you can expend any number of jade shards to create jade armor. While this armor is active, you have half cover, or 3/4ths cover if you spent five or more shards. This armor lasts for a number of rounds equal to the jade shards spent or until the end of initiative.

### Verdant Generosity

At 15th level, you are able to protect allies with your jade armor as well. If any ally is attacked or is forced to roll a saving throw, you can use your reaction to expend any amount of jade shards and encase them in jade armor. This armor confers the same amount of cover as your jade armor feature, but only lasts until the end of that ally’s next turn.

### Jade Emperor

At 18th level, you learn a new jade trick.

#### **Jadestorm.**

By expending any amount of jade shards, you can create a jadestorm around you in a 20 foot radius, designating any creatures you wish to be unaffected by it. The storm travels with you, with its area being lightly obscured. An affected creature that begins its turn within the jadestorm takes bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage equal to the shards expended. Additionally, their AC is reduced by half the expended amount (rounded down) until the start of their next turn or until they leave the jadestorm. The jadestorm lasts for the expended amount of rounds or until the end of initiative.

## [Death Knight](/docs/death-knight "death knight")

*The archetypal death knight focuses on honing negative energies into their attacks and integrating necromancy into their fighting style. While death knights don’t have to be tragic and dark, the energies used often corrupts the user*

### Death Tokens

During combat, when you kill a hostile creature, you gain a number of death tokens equal to half the creature's proficiency bonus (rounded down), which can be used to fuel your death knight abilities. Once a creature grants a death token, it may not do so until you finish a long rest. You may have a maximum number of death tokens equal to 1 + half your fighter level, rounded down, which last until your next long rest.

### Undead Soldier

Starting at 3rd level, you learn to reanimate undead in the midst of combat. As an action, you may expend 2 death tokens to infuse negative energy into a corpse or pile of bones that you can see to turn it into an undead soldier. The corpse becomes a zombie or skeleton which lasts until your next long rest, where it then turns into dust. Undead soldiers share initiative with each other, but roll initiative on their own. As a bonus action, you may give them commands. If no commands are given, they do their best to defend themselves and you. Your undead soldiers additionally count as you for the purpose of gaining death tokens.

You may have a total number of undead soldiers equal to half your proficiency bonus (rounded down).

### Rebuke of the Damned

Starting at 3rd level, you can expend one death token as a reaction when you or an undead soldier within 30ft of you is hit by an attack. Roll a number of d4 dice equal to your proficiency bonus, and reduce the damage taken by the total rolled, dealing the total back to the attacker as necrotic damage.

### Sink or Swim

Starting at 7th level, you learn various uses for your death tokens.

*   As a bonus action, you can use a death token and select an undead soldier within 30 feet of you. Then, spend and roll up to a number of hit dice equal to your proficiency bonus. The undead soldier regains hit points equal to the total of the dice rolled.
*   As an action, you can spend a number of death tokens and target a undead soldier or corpse within 30 feet of you. Every creature within 10 feet of the undead soldier or corpse must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution modifier) or take 1d4 necrotic damage and 1d4 fire damage per death token expended.
*   Once per turn, you may expend a death token slay an undead soldier early, instantly turning it into dust. When doing so, you may make an additional attack before the end of your next turn when you take the attack action.

### Cull the Horde

Starting at 10th level, you can use death tokens to empower you and your undead.

*   Your undead soldiers gain a bonus to attack rolls and saving throws equal to half the death tokens you currently carry, rounded down.
*   Attacks done by your undead soldiers count as magical for the purpose of overcoming defenses.
*   You may spend two death tokens when creating an undead soldier to grant the soldier proficiency in light, medium, and heavy armor, shields, and martial weapons.

### Black Speech

Starting at 15th level, You learn to speak, read, and write Black Speech, which can be used to communicate with undead and can not be learned through traditional means. Most undead lack the intelligence to convey or understand sophisticated concepts, but a non-hostile undead could relay what it has seen or heard in the recent past. This ability doesn't grant you friendship with undead, though you can combine this ability with gifts to curry favor with them as you would with any nonplayer character.

Additionally, having access to this speech grants you resistance to necrotic damage.

### Grave Warden

Starting at 18th levels, when using your undead soldier feature, you can expend 6 death tokens to create a sword wraith warrior instead. In addition, you can use death tokens to do the following:

*   **Armor of Bones:** You may spend a death token when creating an undead to create large spikes of bones which protrude out the created undead. When a creature attacks that undead with a melee attack, they take magical piercing damage equal to your constitution modifier.
*   **Lament of the Lost**: As a bonus action, you can spend a death token to reposition any of your undead soldiers within 30 feet of you. You determine the new arrangement of these soldiers, and may swap your location with one of them.

Fighter: Eldritch Knight: War Magic

# Fighter: Eldritch Knight: War Magic

Optional (Repalces War Magic)

When you take the Attack action on your turn, you can replace one of the attacks with a casting of one of your Wizard cantrips that has a casting time of one action. You can only use this feature once per Attack action.

Final Messenger

# Final Messenger

Wondrous item, uncommon (requires use by a warforged)

A final messenger is a tiny homunculus—a delicate winged creature designed to facilitate communication between warforged soldiers during the Last War.If you are a warforged, you can use an action to hold a final messenger and integrate it into your body. While it is part of your body, the messenger cannot be targeted by any attack. You can only have one messenger attached to your body at a time.

A final messenger holds four pieces of data, accessible only to the warforged it is attached to:

*   A destination for the messenger. This can be very specific (a particular apartment in Sharn) or more general (an open marketplace).
*   A target individual. This could be a specific person or a category (“Any Karrnathi warforged”).
*   A single image recorded through the eyes of a warforged attached to the messenger.
*   A message of up to 25 words.

While the messenger is attached to you, you can use an action to review the data currently stored within the final messenger, and if you choose, to change any or all of these data points. You can release the final messenger as a bonus action, at which point it flies to its destination by the most direct route possible. Once it reaches the destination it seeks the target individual; if no target has been set, it approaches the nearest warforged.

If you are reduced to 0 hit points while a final messenger is attached to you, it immediately detaches and seek out its target. It retains its stored message, but replaces its stored image with the last thing you saw before dropping to zero hit points.

A final messenger is a Tiny construct with an AC of 20, 6 hit points, and a flying speed of 60 feet. It is immune to poison damage, psychic damage, and all conditions other than unconsciousness. If it is forced to make an ability check or a saving throw, treat all its ability scores as 10 (+0). It has darkvision with a range of 60 feet. It has no limbs and the only action it can take on its turn is the Dash action. A messenger can only remain active for 8 hours after it detaches from a warforged body, after which time its energy dissipates and it falls unconscious. An unconscious messenger must complete a short or long rest attached to a warforged, after which it regains its energy, as well as regaining all lost hit points.

Flame Cartridge

# Flame Cartridge

*Ammunition (Firearms), uncommon*

**Description**

A selection of various flammable substances combined with a hint of evocation magic allows this specialty ammunition to turn any firearm into a different style of weapon.

Weapons loaded with a flame cartridge deal fire damage instead of the damage type it would normally deal. Additionally, when you attack with this weapon, instead of making an attack roll, the target must succeed a dexterity saving throw (DC 8 + the weapon's attack bonus) or take the weapon's damage, or half as much on a successful save.

Effects that require you to hit a target instead require them to fail their saving throw.

Flame Stride

# Flame Stride

*5th-level conjuration. (Artificer, Sorcerer, Warlock, Wizard, Circle of Wildfire, Arcane Domain, Forge Domain)*

**Meta**

**Casting Time**: 1 action

**Range**: Self

**Components**: V, S

**Duration**: Concentration, up to 1 minute

**Description**

You gain the ability to enter any source of flame and move from inside it to inside another flame within 500 feet. Both flames must be at least the same size as you. You must use 5 feet of movement to enter the source of fire. You instantly know the location of all other flames within 500 feet and, as part of the move used to enter the flame, can either pass into one of those sources of fire or step out of the fire you're in. You appear in a spot of your choice within 5 feet of the destination fire, using another 5 feet of movement. If you have no movement left, you appear within 5 feet of the flame you entered.

You can use this transportation ability once per round for the duration. You must end each turn outside a flame.

You are temporarily immune to fire damage caused by the flame you're stepping into, but not other sources.

Flameburst Devastator

# Flameburst Devastator

*Weapon (scrapshot), very rare*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

This heavy incendiary weapon functions by superheating the metal scraps typically used as ammunition by a scrapshot, resulting in a spread of molten slag being launched at its targets.

This magical weapon has a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls, and deals fire damage instead of slashing. Targets hit by the weapon are coated in molten metal until they, or a creature within 5 ft of them, use an action to scrape it off of them. A creature or object coated in this molten metal takes 1d10 fire damage at the start of each of its turns. The molten metal effect can stack up to three times.

Additionally, creatures reduced to 0 hitpoints by damage from this weapon are reduced to ash.

Flashfingers

# Flashfingers

*Wondrous Item, varies*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

These magical gloves allow you to throw objects at astounding speeds. When you make a ranged weapon attack using a non-magical thrown weapon, the weapon gains a bonus to attack and damage rolls and is considered magical for the purposes of overcoming resistance and immunity to non-magical damage. The bonus depends on this item’s rarity.

Additionally, if you attack with a thrown weapon with advantage, you deal one additional weapon damage die in addition to the weapon’s normal damage.

+1 : Uncommon

+2 : Rare

+3 : Very Rare

Form Undead

# Form Undead

*9th-level necromancy. (Wizard, Cleric)*

**Meta**

**Casting Time**: 8 hours

**Range**: Touch

**Components**: V, S, M (an intricate black onyx idol inlaid with precious gems worth 10,000gp, which the spell consumes)

**Duration**: Instantaneous

**Description**

This spell must be cast within ten minutes after dusk. You must press the idol used for the material component of this spell to the corpse of a creature with a challenge rating of 10 or lower and hold it there for the duration of the casting of this spell. The corpse must be intact for the duration of this spell’s casting— a destroyed corpse causes this spell to fail.

The corpse reanimates, returning to life and using the statblock of the creature it belonged to, with the following changes:

*   its creature type becomes undead
*   its hit point maximum is halved
*   if it has class levels, it loses all of them
*   it loses any legendary actions and legendary resistances
*   It is unable to cast spells
*   it gains immunity to necrotic damage

As a bonus action on each of your turns, you can mentally command any creature you animated with this spell if the creature is within 120 feet of you (if you control multiple creatures, you can command any or all of them at the same time, issuing the same command to each one). You decide what action the creature will take and where it will move during its next turn, or you can issue a general command, such as to guard a particular chamber or corridor. If you issue no commands, the creature only defends itself against hostile creatures. Once given an order, the creature continues to follow it until its task is complete.

An undead raised by this spell is under your control for 24 hours, after which it makes a charisma saving throw. On a failure, you regain control of it for another 24 hours, whereupon it makes the save again. Each time it fails this saving throw, it gains a cumulative +2 bonus to the Charisma save.

On a success, it is hostile to all creatures (including yourself) and is under no compulsion to follow any orders you give it. It has disadvantage on attack rolls, ability checks, and saving throws it makes against you (except for the saving throws it makes to break free from your control).

Fortune'S Shadow

# Fortune's Shadow

*In the clandestine world of rogues, a select few find themselves touched by the enigmatic forces of fate. These shadowy figures, known as Fortune’s Shadows, navigate the perilous dance of danger with a unique connection to the threads that weave destiny. Embracing Fortune’s Shadow means walking a razor’s edge, where every calculated risk and precise strike is a step further into the weave of destiny. Those who witness a Fortune’s Shadow in action may question whether their abilities are a result of skill, luck, or a mysterious force guiding their every move. One thing is certain – in the world of shadows and secrets, the Fortune’s Shadow is a rogue unlike any other, their fate entwined with the very essence of chance.*

## Fortune's Gambit

At 3rd level, upon dealing sneak attack damage with an even number, you gain a Fate Point.

As a bonus action, you can expend a Fate Point to make a daring gambit. Roll a d6 to determine the effect of the Gambit.

d6 : Gambit : Effect:

1.  **Fleeting Shadows:** *You become invisible until the start of your next turn, you lose invisibility whenever you attack, or force a saving throw.*
2.  **Lucky Strike:** *Your next attack gains advantage.*
3.  **Evasive Maneuver:** *The next attack against you must be made with disadvantage.*
4.  **Weave of Fate:** *You gain temporary hit points equal to your rogue level.*
5.  **Deft Diversion:** *You can move up to half your speed without provoking opportunity attacks.*
6.  **Fortune's Grace:** *Add your proficiency bonus to your AC until the start of your next turn.*

When rolling initiative your Fate Points reset to 0. You can a flip a coin, on a head to you gain a Fate point.

## Fateful Escape

At level 9 when you are reduced to 0 hit points and not killed outright, you can expend a Fate Point to cheat death. Instead of falling unconscious, you remain conscious with 1 hit point, and can immediately use a reaction to move up to your speed, without provoking opportunity attacks. Additionally, you gain advantage on Dexterity ability checks and saving throws for 1 minute, representing the temporary escape from fate’s grasp. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

## Greater Gambit

At 13th level your Gambit's are improved. when you roll for a Gambit, you instead use the Greater gambit table:

d6 : Gambit : Effect:

1.  ***Persistent Shadows*:** *You become invisible until the end of your next turn.*
2.  ***Foretold Strikes*:** *You gain advantage on attacks until the end of your next turn.*
3.  ***Evasive Expert*:** *You impose disadvantage on all attacks against you until the start of your next turn..*
4.  ***Architect of Fate*:** *You gain temporary hit points and regain hit point equal to your rogue level*
5.  ***Perfect Diversion*:** *You can move up to your speed without provoking opportunity attacks.*
6.  ***Fortune's Gift*:** *Add your proficiency bonus to your AC until the start of your next turn. Reduce damage taken from attacks by the same amount.*

When rolling initiative you can now flip 2 coins, gaining a Fate point for each coin that lands on a head.

## Uncanny Fate

At level 17, when you use your uncanny dodge feature to halve damage received, you can spend a Fate Point to roll a d6. On an even result, the attacker takes force damage equal to the damage you received. On an odd result, you don't take any damage from the attack.

Forward Battalion

# Forward Battalion

*Weapon (glaive), legendary*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement by a creature with a Strength score of 15 or higher

**Description**

This magic glaive has tattered remains of a banner from the base of its blade and coiled around its shaft. The color and symbol have long faded and forgotten but whose task still persists. This weapon feels heavier than it looks to the wielder. Some say the banner appears at moments when the glaive is swung, showing the vibrant colors it once had. You have a +2 bonus to attack rolls made with this weapon. You also gain the following abilities.

**Strength in Numbers**. You gain an additional +1 bonus to attack rolls for each friendly creature within 20 feet of you up to a maximum of +3(total bonus of +5). You also give a +1 bonus to attack rolls to friendly creatures within range.

**Assisted Assault.** While you have at least one friendly creature within 30 feet of you, you deal an extra 3d4 of the weapon's damage on hit. Alternatively, you can forgo the extra damage at the start of your turn to give one friendly creature an extra 3d4 weapon damage on one attack they make on their next turn.

Frostflames Of The North

# Frostflames of the North

*Wondrous item (torch), very rare*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement by a creature with resistance or immunity to cold damage

**Description**

The torch, whose wood carry no magical properties, brings an icy blue flame that defied the very essence of warmth but still possess its comfort and reliability. This torch provides bright light in a 20-foot radius and dim light for an additional 20 feet much like normal torches except this is unimpeded by effects caused by cold environments and instead overcomes them (i.e., instead of being subjected to a heavily obscuring blizzard, you and those around you are able to see with ease within this torch's light). Creatures within its light's range are free from the effects of extreme heat. If you make a melee attack with this lit torch and hit, it deals 1 cold damage. If the flames are in contact to a body of water, it will slowly freeze the water within its light's range.


You can freely decrease the size of the flame and the light it makes to an inch in diameter and revert it back, but it cannot be completely snuffed out. You can transfer it to another torch and leave the previous holder as a mundane torch.

If this torch is held by a creature that does not have resistance or immunity to cold damage, they will take 1 cold damage every minute until they drop this torch.

The blue flame has 6 charges. While holding the torch, you can use an action to expend 1 charge to blow on the fires to cast the cone of cold spell (save DC 17; you can choose whether each creature makes a Constitution or Dexterity saving throw) from it. Each expended charge makes the flames smaller and smaller. If you expend the last charge, the flames and light it produces will only be 1 inch in diameter and will stay in that size. The blue flame regains 1 charge daily at dawn, as well as increase in size bit by bit.

Gambler'S Weapon

# Gambler's Weapon

Weapon (any weapon), rare

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

Choose a magical bonus of +1 to +3. This weapon gains that bonus to its attack and damage rolls. For each point of bonus you choose for the weapon, you take a corresponding penalty (−1 to −3) to your death saving throws. You can change this magical bonus each day at dawn.

**Curse**. This weapon is cursed, and becoming attuned to it extends the curse to you. Until the curse is broken with a remove curse spell or similar magic, you are unwilling to part with the weapon.

Gauntlets Of Gunslinging

# Gauntlets Of Gunslinging

*Wondrous Item, uncommon*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

While wearing these gauntlets, you have proficiency with the musket and pistol, and you gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls on ranged attacks made with such weapons.

Gift Of The Upper Planes

# Gift of the Upper Planes

**Prerequisite**

Aasimar

**Description**

You manifested some of the powers from the Upper Planes, granting you the following benefits:

*   Your Healing Hands and transformation traits can now use either an action or bonus action to activate and can be used again after finishing a short or long rest.
*   Each of your transformations also gain additional effects:
    *   **Necrotic Shroud.** The range of the effect increases to 30 feet, and you gain advantage on attack rolls against creatures frightened by this trait.
    *   **Radiant Consumption.** The radius of both bright and dim light increases to 30 feet. Hostile creatures can't benefit from being hidden while within the light. If you are within magical darkness, the light would only remain on your body and won't be shed but you are able to see through that darkness instead. Creatures within or outside the magical darkness would be able to see you.
    *   **Radiant Soul.** You can hover. When the transformation ends while you are not on the ground, your rate of decent slows to 60 feet per round until you land. Upon landing, you take no falling damage and can land on your feet.

Glacial Shard

# Glacial Shard

*1st-level transmutation. (Cryomancy Magic)*

**Meta**

**Casting Time:** 1 action

**Range:** 60 feet

**Components:** V, S, M (a piece of pure ice)

**Duration:** Instantaneous

**Description**

You form a large ice shard from the moisture of your surroundings to launch at a target that you can see within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. If the attack hits, the target takes 2d6 cold damage and the shard shatters into pieces that embed into the target until the start of your next turn. If the target takes an action or bonus action before then, the target takes 1d4 cold damage.

**At Higher Levels**

When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d6 to the target, and the damage the target takes for doing an action or bonus action increase by 1d4 for each slot level above 1st.

Gladiator (Ua)

# Gladiator (UA)

## Description

*Master Brutality and Blood Sport*

*Gladiators are just as much performers as they are warriors. Whether brawling in underground fighting rings or fighting for survival in bloodstained arenas, a Gladiator combines martial skill with theatrics to awe and intimidate their audiences.*

**Features:**

*   [Level 3 - Brutality](#Level_3_-_Brutality)
*   [Level 3 - Combat Theatrics](#Level_3_-_Combat_Theatrics)
*   [Level 7 - Flourish Parry](#Level_7_-_Flourish_Parry)
*   [Level 10 - Bolder Brutalities](#Level_10_-_Bolder_Brutalities)
*   [Level 15 - Brutal Resurgence](#Level_15_-_Brutal_Resurgence)
*   [Level 18 - Mutilate](#Level_18_-_Mutilate)

## Level 3 - Brutality

Shaped by the intensity of the arena, you’ve learned to execute brutal martial moves. Once per turn, when you hit a creature with an attack roll using a Melee weapon, you can add one of the following Brutality effects of your choice. You can do this a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Short or Long Rest.

**Bleed.** You can activate the Sap mastery property in addition to a different mastery property you’re using with that weapon, and the target takes extra damage equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1 damage). The extra damage’s type is the same type as the weapon’s type.

**Bluff.** You can activate the Vex mastery property in addition to a different mastery property you’re using with that weapon, and you have Advantage on the next saving throw you make before the end of your next turn.

**Stumble**. You can activate the Topple mastery property in addition to a different mastery property you’re using with that weapon, and on its next turn, the target can take only an action or a Bonus Action, not both.

## Level 3 - Combat Theatrics

You’ve honed your combat skills to entertain, devastating opponents and dazzling onlookers. You gain the following benefits.

**Athletic Flair.** Whenever you make a Dexterity (Acrobatics) or Strength (Athletics) check, you gain a bonus to the check equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of +1).

**Bonus Proficiency.** You gain proficiency in one of these skills of your choice: Acrobatics, Athletics, Deception, Intimidation, or Performance.

## Level 7 - Flourish Parry

You’ve learned to stylishly counter blows from your opponents. When an enemy hits you with a melee attack roll, you can take a Reaction to add your Charisma modifier (minimum of +1) to your AC against the attack, potentially causing the attack to miss.

**Flourish Counter.** If this Reaction causes the attack to miss, you can retaliate with a powerful counterattack as part of the same Reaction. Make an attack roll with a Melee weapon against the triggering creature. If this attack hits, you can use one of your Brutality effects on the target without expending a use of that feature. Once this counterattack hits, you can’t use this feature to make another counterattack until you finish a Long Rest. You can also restore your use of this counterattack by expending a use of Second Wind (no action required).

## Level 10 - Bolder Brutalities

Your brutal martial prowess has improved. The following effects are added to your Brutality options.

**Rive.** You can activate the Cleave mastery property in addition to a different mastery property you’re using with that weapon, and you can add your ability modifier to the damage of the additional attack made as part of that property’s activation.

**Rush.** You can activate the Push mastery property in addition to a different mastery property you’re using with that weapon, and you can immediately move up to your Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

**Stagger.** You can activate the Slow mastery property in addition to a different mastery property you’re using with that weapon, and the target has Disadvantage on the next saving throw it makes before the end of your next turn.

## Level 15 - Brutal Resurgence

Whenever you use your Second Wind to regain Hit Points, you regain an expended use of Brutality. You also regain an expended use of Brutality whenever you use your Action Surge.

## Level 18 - Mutilate

When you hit a Bloodied creature with an attack roll, you can attempt to critically injure it. The target makes a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 plus your Charisma modifier and Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, the target suffers the following effects:

**Maimed.** If the target takes the Attack action, it can make only one attack.

**Sluggish.** The target’s Speed is halved, and it has a −2 penalty to its Armor Class.

These effects last until the target regains Hit Points. Once a target fails its saving throw against this feature, you can’t use it again on that creature until you finish a Long Rest.

Glider Staff

# Glider Staff

*Weapon (quarterstaff), rare*

**Description**

As a reaction or an object interaction, this quarterstaff can be converted into a glider, or from a glider back into the quarterstaff. It can't be used as a weapon while in glider mode. As a bonus action you can will the glider to activate.

For 1 hour you have a flying speed of 45 feet, but you can't use this speed to gain altitude, and while using this speed you lose 5 feet of altitude plus any unused lateral movement at the end of each turns. (For example if you fly 30 feet on a turn, you will fall 20 feet at the end of the turn. You can land safely this way.)

Glimpse

# Glimpse

*5th-level divination. (rtificer, Bard, Cleric, Warlock, Wizard)*

**Meta**

**Casting Time:** 1 minute

**Range**: Self

**Components:** V, S, M (a focus worth at least 1,000 gp, such as a crystal ball, a silver mirror, or a font filled with holy water)

**Duration**: Concentration, up to 10 minutes

**Description**

You can choose a small or smaller object that is familiar to you that isn’t being worn or carried and is on the same plane of existence as you. The spell creates an invisible sensor within 10 feet of the object’s location for the duration. You mentally receive visual information from the sensor, which has normal vision and darkvision out to 30 feet. The sensor allows you to look in every direction. The sensor moves with the object, remaining within 10 feet of it for the duration. A creature that can see invisible objects sees the sensor as a luminous orb about the size of your fist.

At any time while concentrating on the spell, you can use an action to ping the object’s location. If the object is on the same plane as you, you learn the object’s general direction (but not its distance or any other information).

Gloves Of The Spirit Shroud

# Gloves of the Spirit Shroud

*Wondrous Item, rare*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

These gloves have 3 charges. While wearing them, you can use a bonus action to expend 1 or more of its charges to cast the spell Spirit Shroud from it. For 1 charge, you cast the 3rd-level version of the spell. You can increase the spell slot level by one for each additional charge you expend. The gloves regain all expended charges daily at dawn.

Grave Conclave Ranger

# Grave Conclave Ranger

## Description

Grave Wardens are grim trackers who walk the line between life and death. They hunt those who would unnaturally subvert the mortal cycle—powerful undead, necromancers, and entities that cling to a profane existence. By drawing on the cold, final energy of the grave, they imbue their weapons with grim power and attune their senses to the echoes of mortality.

**Features:**

*   [3rd Level - Grave Warden Magic](#3rd_Level_-_Grave_Warden_Magic)
*   [3rd level - Armament of the Grave](#3rd_level_-_Armament_of_the_Grave)
*   [3rd level - Grim Hunter](#3rd_level_-_Grim_Hunter)
*   [7th Level - Armament's Vigil](#7th_Level_-_Armament's_Vigil)
*   [11th Level - Soul's Vigil](#11th_Level_-_Soul's_Vigil)
*   [15th Level - Veil Walker](#15th_Level_-_Veil_Walker)

## 3rd Level - Grave Warden Magic

You learn an additional spell when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown in the Grave Warden Spells table. The spell counts as a ranger spell for you, but it doesn't count against the number of ranger s1pells you know.

### Grave Warden Spells

Ranger Level | Spell

*   3rd | Inflict Wounds
*   5th | Ray of Enfeeblement
*   9th | Speak with Dead
*   13th | Blight
*   17th | Negative Energy Flood

## 3rd level - Armament of the Grave

You learn a ritual that channels grim power into one weapon. Over 1 hour, which can be done during a short or long rest, you can touch one weapon you are proficient with. This weapon becomes your Bound Armament and gains the following benefits:

*   The weapon counts as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.
*   The weapon can be used as a spellcasting focus for your ranger spells
*   Once on each of your turns, when you hit a creature with an attack using this weapon, the attack deals an extra 1d6 necrotic damage. This extra damage increases to 2d6 at 11th level.

You can perform this ritual again to bond with a different weapon, which ends the bond on the previous one.

## 3rd level - Grim Hunter

Your attunement to the echoes of death sharpens your senses. You gain proficiency in your choice of either the Insight or Investigation skill. Additionally, you have advantage on Wisdom (Survival) checks to track Undead and Fiends, and on Wisdom (Insight) checks to discern the motives of those creature types.

## 7th Level - Armament's Vigil

Your Bound Armament glows faintly, visible only to you, whenever one or more Undead are within 120 feet of you.

Additionally, as a bonus action, or as a reaction when this glow first activates, you can project a 15-foot radius aura of censure. The aura lasts for 1 minute. When an Undead of your choice starts its turn within the aura, it must make a Wisdom saving throw against your ranger spell save DC. On a failed save, it has disadvantage on all attack rolls until the start of its next turn.

You can use this aura a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (a minimum of one). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

## 11th Level - Soul's Vigil

Your constant exposure to the energies of undeath fortifies your mind and body. You gain the following benefits:

*   You have advantage on saving throws against being frightened or charmed.
*   You gain resistance to necrotic damage.
*   When a creature you can see within 30 feet of you drops to 0 hit points, you can use your reaction to gain temporary hit points equal to your Wisdom modifier + your Ranger level.

## 15th Level - Veil Walker

You can step through the veil between life and death. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

*   **Grave Step**. As a bonus action, you can expend one use of this feature to teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. When you arrive, each creature within 5 feet of you (other than yourself) must make a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC. A creature takes \*3d10\* necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.
*   **Ethereal Reprisal**. As a reaction when you are hit by an attack, you can expend one use of this feature to teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. If this movement takes you out of the attack's range, the attack misses. The creature that attacked you must then make a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC, taking \*3d10\* necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

Greaser

# Greaser

## Description:

*Since the dawn of humanity, there has always been a frontier to conquer. Between the wastes of the east, the fires of the hells, and the vastness of the astral plane, there has always been those that dared to tread the road less traveled. Traversing each regional obstacle brings with it a unique solution— the hellfire engines of Avernus, the spelljammers of the astral sea, and the machinations inspired by the great inventor Kwalish, all formed to blaze a trail through every path they come across.*

*Through the use of arcane influence and artificing prowess, a new school of thought has arisen among artificers; why spend your days walking through the most hazardous conditions when you can drive, sail, or fly through them instead? Thus, under the influence of vehicles from all different planes, the most ingenious of traveling artificers got to work.*

**Features:**

*   [3rd Level - Bonus Proficiency](#3rd_Level_-_Bonus_Proficiency)
*   [3rd Level - Greaser Spells](#3rd_Level_-_Greaser_Spells)
*   [3rd Level - Arcane Vehicle](#3rd_Level_-_Arcane_Vehicle)
*   [5th Level - Extra Attack](#5th_Level_-_Extra_Attack)
*   [9th Level - Enhanced Modifications](#9th_Level_-_Enhanced_Modifications)
*   [15th Level - Overdrive](#15th_Level_-_Overdrive)

## 3rd Level - Bonus Proficiency

When you adopt this specialization at 3rd level, you gain proficiency with smith’s tools. If you already have this proficiency, you gain proficiency with one other type of artisan’s tools of your choice. In addition, you gain proficiency in land and water vehicles

## 3rd Level - Greaser Spells

Starting at 3rd level, you always have certain spells prepared after you reach particular levels in this class, as shown in the Greaser Spells table. These spells count as artificer spells for you, but they don’t count against the number of artificer spells you prepare.

Artificer Level Spells

3rd Level - Burning Hands, Fog Cloud

5th Level - Locate Object, Dust Devil

9th Level - Slow, Stinking Cloud

13th Level - Hallucinatory Terrain, Sickening Radiance

17th Level - Passwall, Rary's Telepathic Bond

## 3rd Level - Arcane Vehicle

By 3rd level, you’ve successfully managed to create your first vehicle for traversal. At the end of a long rest, you may create a vehicle by choosing one of each of the following vehicle mod options below. The vehicle is a large magical object that can be mounted as if it were a creature, after which it acts as a controlled mount. It shares its turn with you, and can only take the dash and disengage actions. It is immune to psychic damage and uses your saving throw bonuses when applicable.

If the mending spell is cast on it, it regains 2d6 hit points. If it has been destroyed within the last hour, you can use your smith’s tools as an action to repair it, provided you are within 5 feet of it and you expend a spell slot of 1st level or higher. The vehicle returns to working order after 1 minute with half of its hit points restored.

At the end of a long rest, you can create a new arcane vehicle if you have your smith’s tools with you. If you already have an arcane vehicle from this feature, the first one is immediately destroyed. The vehicle is also destroyed if you die.

### **Vehicle Mods**

Each vehicle can only have one vehicle mod of each option listed below active at one time. At the end of a short rest, you can use your smith’s tools to replace one vehicle mod of your choosing with another eligible of the same category. If a vehicle mod has prerequisites, you must meet them to learn it. A level prerequisite refers to your level in this class.

#### Vehicle Frame

Frame Type | Level | Speed | Hit Points | AC

*   Trailblazer| N/A | 60ft.| 5 x artificer level | 13 + PB
*   Seafarer | 5th level | 10ft., swim 50ft. | 4 x artificer level"\\t"| 12 + PB
*   Cloudstrider | 9th level | 0ft., fly 40ft. | 3 x artificer level | 11 + PB

#### Vehicle Archetype

Archetype | Prerequisite | Details

*   Assault | N/A | The vehicle’s maximum hit points increase by 2 x artificer level.
*   Transport | N/A | The vehicle can hold up to 500lbs of items in a 5ft. cube of space.
*   Transit | 5th level | The vehicle’s speed increases by 10ft.

#### Mounted Weapon

Weapon | Prerequisite | Details

*   Skewer | N/A | **Melee**. The skewer deals 1d6 piercing damage for every 10ft. of movement used immediately before the attack, up to 3d6 piercing damage.
*   Glob Launcher | N/A | **Ranged (60/120)**. The glob launcher deals 1d10 bludgeoning damage. A creature hit by this weapon has its movement speed reduced by 10ft. until the end of its turn.
*   Gas Sprayer | 5th level |**15ft. cone**. Creatures in the area must make a Constitution save against your spell save DC or take 2d8 poison damage and be poisoned until the end of their next turn.
*   Bombard | 9th level | **Ranged (30/60)**. The bombard deals 2d6 fire damage. When you hit with an attack, all creatures within 5ft. of the target must succeed on a Dexterity save or take 2d6 fire damage.
*   Sol Spear | 15th level | **Ranged (40/80.)** The sol spear does 1d8 radiant damage. The first creature hit by the spear each turn must succeed on a Constitution save or be blinded until the end of its next turn.

Weapons mounted on your vehicle are simple weapons you are proficient in that can only be used by yourself while you are mounted. If the weapon requires an attack roll, you can add your Intelligence modifier, instead of your Strength or Dexterity, to the attack and damage rolls. Use your spell save DC for any saving throw related to your weapon. These weapons can be infused.

#### Specialized Modification

Mod | Prerequisite | Details

*   **Bladed Edge** |N/A| Creatures you move within 5ft of take 5 slashing damage. A creature can only take this damage once per turn.
*   **Seatbelts** | N/A |You automatically succeed on any checks you make to remain mounted.
*   **Improved Treading** | 5th level | Your vehicle’s movement ignores difficult terrain.
*   **Sealed Cockpit** | 5th level | You ignore the effects of extreme heat and extreme cold while mounted on your vehicle, and have access to a constant supply of fresh air.
*   **Overtuned Engine** | 9th level | The vehicle’s speed increases by 20ft.
*   **Grappling Hook** | 9th level | You can use an action to launch a grappling hook attached to a chain up to 50ft. away.
*   **Heavy Shielding** | 9th level | You have half cover while riding your vehicle.
*   **Self-Repair Module** | 15th level | If your vehicle has lower than half of its maximum hit points, it regains 5 hit points at the start of each of your turns. |Drop Compartments|15th level|Adds six small compartments that can each release their contents (such as caltrops/ball bearings) on the ground within 5ft. of the vehicle as a bonus action.

## 5th Level - Extra Attack

Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn. Additionally, attacks from your mounted weapons count as magical for the purposes of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.

## 9th Level - Enhanced Modifications

At 9th level, you’re able to fit more modifications on your vehicle. You may now have two specialized modifications and two mounted weapons active at once.

## 15th Level - Overdrive

At 15th level, you learn to overclock your vehicle to force it to perform past its peak temporarily. As a bonus action, you can send your vehicle into overdrive for one minute. Doing so doubles its movement speed, makes it immune to all damage, and allows you to make up to three attacks with your mounted weapon each turn. At the end of the minute, your vehicle is immediately destroyed. You must finish a long rest to use this feature again, unless you spend a spell slot of 5th level or higher to use it again.

Greater Healing Kit

# Greater Healing Kit

*Wondrous Item (consumable), rare*

3 lbs.

**Description**

This kit is made from a combination of rare herbs and magical compounds, providing potent healing properties.

Special bandages infused with healing magic, designed to accelerate the natural healing process.

**Healing Salve**: This kit contains five salve charges and as an action, one charge of the salve can be applied to the wounds of a creature, The creature restores 2d6 + 4 hit points.

**Healing Bandages:** This kit contains 3 bandage charges and as an action, one charge of the bandages can be applied to the wounds of a creature, The creature restores 1d8 + 2 hit points and stabilizes them.

Greater Reincarnate

# Greater Reincarnate

*7th-level transmutation. (Druid)*

**Meta**

**Casting Time**: 1 hour

**Range**: Touch

**Components**: V, S, M (rare oils and unguents worth at least 5,000 gp, which the spell consumes)

**Duration**: Instantaneous

**Description**

You touch a willing living humanoid. The spell transforms their body into an adult form of another race. If the target isn’t willing, the spell fails.. The GM rolls a d100 and consults the reincarnate table to determine what form the creature takes when the spell is complete or the GM chooses.

The reincarnated creature recalls its former life and experiences. It retains the capabilities it had in its original form, except it exchanges its original race for the new one and changes its racial traits accordingly.

Greater Ritual Casting

# Greater Ritual Casting

**Prerequisite:** *Ritual Caster feat (2014 or 2024); Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma score of 18 or higher*

Your mastery of ritual magic reaches new heights. You gain the following benefits:

    •    **Ability Score Increase.** Increase the same ability score you chose for the Ritual Caster feat (Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma) by 1, to a maximum of 20.

    •    **Quick Ritual**. Once more per long rest, you can cast a ritual spell at its normal casting time instead of adding 10 minutes.

    •    **Expanded Ritual Repertoire**. You can learn one additional ritual spell of each level equal to or lower than your spellcasting ability modifier(maximum 5).

    •    **Growth of Knowledge**. For each level you gain, you may replace one ritual spell of that level with another once on a level up, or when you PERMANENTLY increase your casting modifier.

Guardian Angel

# Guardian Angel

*Armor (shield), very rare*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

This shield is adorned with angelic wings and a crystal at the center which glows with a calming blue hue. While holding this shield, you have a +1 bonus to AC and resistance to necrotic damage.

When you die as a result of failing all your death saving throws or when you are subjected to an effect which causes you to instantly die, you instead fall into a stasis until the start of your next turn. During this stasis, you are incapacitated while remain conscious of your surroundings, you are immune to all damage and cannot be affected by any other effect or condition. If you are under other effects or conditions when you fall into stasis, all of these instantly end. At the start of your next turn, your current hit point total resets to 20 hit points and you gain advantage on the next attack roll, ability check, or saving throw you make. Once you have used this feature, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

Guild Palace Lance

# Guild Palace Lance

[![GPL](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/e/e9/GPL.png/revision/latest?cb=20241008092018)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/e/e9/GPL.png/revision/latest?cb=20241008092018)

*Weapon (lance), legendary*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. Attacking a target within 5 feet of you with this weapon doesn't impose disadvantage on the attack roll.

If you are attuned to Guild Palace Shield, you gain access to the following additional properties:

*   The bonus to attack and damage rolls instead becomes +3.
*   You can wield this weapon with one hand, instead of two, while also holding the shield.
*   When you attack with this weapon, you deal an additional 1d12 force damage. If you roll a 20 on the attack roll, it rolls damage dice three times, instead of twice.

You can cast the Raulothim's Psychic Lance spell (save DC 17). Once you cast the spell this way, you regain the ability to use it again at dawn.

Guild Palace Shield

# Guild Palace Shield

[![GPS](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/7/72/GPS.png/revision/latest/scale-to-width-down/180?cb=20241008092244)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/7/72/GPS.png/revision/latest?cb=20241008092244)

[](/docs/file:gps.png)

*Armor (shield), legendary*

**Attunement** Requires Attunement

**Description**

While holding this shield, you have a +2 bonus to AC. This bonus is in addition to the shield's normal bonus to AC. If you are attuned to Guild Palace Lance, you gain access to the following additional properties:

*   Your attack rolls using the Guild Palace Lance have advantage against targets that is 10 feet away from you.
*   The bonus to AC instead becomes +3.
*   The first time an attacker misses you with a melee attack in a turn, they take 1d8 force damage.
*   You can cast the Globe of Invulnerability spell. Once you cast the spell this way, you regain the ability to use it again at dawn.

Half Gnoll

# Half Gnoll

*Be it from the merging of peaceful clans, or a more unwelcome coupling of savage gnolls and their victims, some cases of Half-Gnolls can appear.*

*Half-Gnoll's heritages are human and gnoll, as different as night and day. The result of this often portrays itself as instinctual and common sense confusion: with two separate parts fighting on what the proper choice is. The only thing all can agree on is the strong sense of being part of a group, be it a village or a hunting pack. Whether they attempt to do so with humans or gnolls, they often face an uphill battle.*

*Half-Gnolls still have a craving for chaos and killing that their Gnoll blood possesses, but it is mostly easier to control, and can often be quelled with hunting and the occasional consuming of any blood and guts. If they end up killing or consuming an intelligent creature, however, there is still a high risk of them going on similar rampages.*

## Ability Score Increase

When determining your character’s ability scores, increase one score by 2 and increase a different score by 1, or increase three different scores by 1. Follow this rule regardless of the method you use to determine the scores, such as rolling or point buy. The “Quick Build” section for your character’s class offers suggestions on which scores to increase. You can follow those suggestions or ignore them, but you can’t raise any of your scores above 20.

## Age

Half-Gnolls usually mature by the age of ten, and they often die of various reasons by the age of 35, but there are cases where they live up to the age of 75 without a real sign of old age, before seeming to lose all their strength immediately and falling into an eternal slumber.

## Size

Half Gnolls are lean, lanky and tower over humans, often found in the range between 6 and 7 feet tall. Your size is Medium.

## Speed

Your base walking speed is 35 feet

## Darkvision

Thanks to your gnoll blood,  you can see in 60ft of darkness as if in bright light, and the rest in dim light. You can't discern colour in the dark, only black and white.

## Septic Bite

Your jaws are a natural weapon, and your mouth is a breeding ground for disease. If you hit with this bite attack, you deal 1d4 + STR piercing damage plus an additional 1d4 poison damage.

## Keen Smell

You gain advantage on any Wisdom (Perception) or Intelligence (Investigation) checks that involve smell.

## Rampage

When you reduce a creature to 0 hit points with a melee attack on your turn, you may use a bonus action to move up to half your speed and make a bite attack

## Languages

You can speak, read and write Common, Gnoll.

Gnoll is an abrasive language, communicating in growls, barks and broken Abyssal. Beings that can speak Gnoll can understand general concepts and few keywords in Abyssal.

It has no script of its own, often using Infernal script.

Hammer Mastery

# Hammer Mastery

**Prerequisite**: Proficiency with at least one type of hammer

You have become adept at using hammers and can make use of their crushing weight against your opponents.

When you wield a hammer and attack a creature with it, the target's AC is reduced by 2 for that attack, and you ignore the detrimental effects of the heavy property of hammers.

Additionally, once per turn, when you hit a creature with a hammer, you can immediately take the shove action targeting the same creature.

Hammers: Light Hammer, Warhammer, Lucerne Hammer, Maul

Hammer Of The Ram

# Hammer of the Ram

[![Hammer of the Ram](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/a/a1/Hammer_of_the_Ram.png/revision/latest/scale-to-width-down/180?cb=20241011085755)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/a/a1/Hammer_of_the_Ram.png/revision/latest?cb=20241011085755)

[](/docs/file:hammer-of-the-ram.png)

Hammer of the Ram

*Weapon (Maul), Rare*

**Attunement**

Requires attunement.

**Description**

This all-silver hammer carries a mighty force behind it with every swing.

You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

If the hammer hits an object that isn’t being worn or carried, the hit is a critical hit.

The hammer has 3 charges and regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn. When you hit a target with the hammer, you can expend 1 to 3 of its charges to force the target to make a DC 15 Strength saving throw. For each charge expended, the target takes an extra 1d10 force damage and is pushed 5 feet away from you on a failed save. On a successful save, the target takes half as much force damage and isn’t pushed.

𝙍𝙞𝙣𝙜 𝙤𝙛 𝙩𝙝𝙚 𝙍𝙖𝙢. If you’re also attuned to the “ring of the ram,” your bonus to attack and damage rolls with the hammer increases to +2, and the hammer’s every swing creates a projection of the spectral ram from the ring.

The ram lurches forward from the head of the hammer, effectively increasing your reach with the weapon by an additional 5 feet. In addition, the hammer and ring share a pool of charges: any charge you have with the ring can be used for the hammer’s properties, and vice versa.

Harbinger

# Harbinger

Harbingers are foretellers of death and destruction, serving as a warning for disaster and ruin. Oftentimes, they take it upon themselves to cause the catastrophes themselves.

## For Whom the Bell Tolls

At 3rd level, you learn the toll the dead and sacred flame cantrips, which counts as a spiritualist cantrip for you. When you cast a spiritualist cantrip, the DC is equal to your Art save DC.

Whenever you activate your spirit tether, the target has disadvantage on the saving throw against spiritualist cantrips you cast until the end of your turn.

## Harbinger Arts

Also at 3rd level, you gain access to new spirit arts that reflect your penchant for bringing about tragedy.

### Mark of Suffering

As an action, you can spend 1 spirit point to attempt to imbue your target with a Suffering mark. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw or be marked and take 2d6 necrotic damage.

Whenever you cast a spiritualist cantrip on the marked target, you may trigger this spirit art’s effect again without spending spirit points.

### Mark of Ruination

As an action, you can spend 1 spirit point to attempt to imbue your target with a Ruination mark. The target must make an Intelligence saving throw or be marked and subtract 1d6 from each saving throw it makes against your harbinger arts or spiritualist cantrips until the end of its next turn.

Whenever you cast a spiritualist cantrip on the marked target, you may trigger this spirit art’s effect again without spending spirit points.

### Mark of Tragedy

As an action, you can spend 1 spirit point to attempt to imbue your target with a Tragedy mark. The target must make a Charisma saving throw or take 1d6 psychic damage each time it attacks or casts a spell until the end of its next turn.

Whenever you cast a spiritualist cantrip on the marked target, you may trigger this spirit art’s effect again without spending spirit points.

## Enervating Marks

At 6th level, whenever the target of your spiritualist cantrips has a Suffering, Ruination, and Tragedy mark, you can add an additional 2 damage dice to the cantrip’s damage roll.

## The Ninth Bell Tolls True

At 13th level, you find new ways of inflicting woe upon foes. The damage from your Suffering and Tragedy marks increase by 2d6.

In addition, when a target fails its saving throw against one of your marks, you can consume the mark. The mark is removed from the target, and you regain 2d6 hit points.

## Master Harbinger

At 18th level, you master your prowess at bestowing tribulation. You learn the following spirit art:

### Mark of Calamity

As an action, you can spend 5 spirit points to imbue your target with a Calamity mark. The target must make a Constitution saving throw or be marked, taking 4d6 force damage and suffering one level of exhaustion.

Whenever you cast a spiritualist cantrip on the marked target, you may trigger this spirit art’s effect again without spending spirit points. This mark can count as a Suffering, Ruination, or Tragedy mark for the purposes of satisfying the requirements of your enervating marks feature, but only one of the three at a time.

Healing Bow

# Healing bow

*Weapon (Longbow or Shortbow), rare*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

This bow has 6 charges and regains 1d4+2 charges at dawn. When you use your action to fire this bow, you can expend a number of charges to shoot one of the arrows at an ally within normal range. This arrow deals no damage, but restores 1d6 HP to the target for each charge expended. Alternatively, once per turn, you may instead use one of your attacks to fire a single arrow at an ally within the normal range of the bow. When you do so, you Cast a Spell you have prepared that restores HP to a single target other than the caster, does not deal damage, and has a casting time of 1 action. The arrow deals no damage, but the effects of the spell are applied to the target. Casting a spell in this way does not require a free hand. If the spell requires a spell slot, the slot is expended as usual.

Heartseeker Bow

# Heartseeker Bow

*Weapon (longbow), legendary*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement by a Non-Evil Alignment

**Description**

A Heartseeker bow is a beautifully crafted elven longbow +3 made of polished wood. The elven craftsmen made the bow so strong even stone cannot scratch the marvelous wood, and the deep finish of its polish can be returned by a single brush of the hand. You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, in addition to the following abilities.

**Lightning Arrow**. A Heartseeker Imbues the arrows it shoots with lightning. Add an additional 1d8 of lighting damage

**Heartseeking**. A Heartseeker aids the archer in finding the most vulnerable parts of their targets. A critical hit is achieved on a roll of 19 or 20.

**Elven Craftsmanship**. Due to the strength of the fine wood used in the crafting of a Heartseeker, it can be used as a quarterstaff dealing 1d6 damage.

Holy Arrow

# Holy Arrow

*Evocation Cantrip (Cleric, Celestial Warlock)*

**Casting Time**: 1 Action

**Range:** 90 feet

**Components:** V, S

**Duration:** Instantaneous

You conjure and fire a bolt of radiant energy at a creature or object within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes 1d8 radiant damage. If the target is an undead or fiend, it takes 1d10 radiant damage instead.

This spell's damage increases by one die when you reach 5th level (2d8 or 2d10), 11th level (3d8 or 3d10), and 17th level (4d8 or 4d10).

Homebrew System & Index

# Homebrew System & Index

## How to Homebrew

In The Wandering Realms, we allow homebrew. Homebrew get approved in the server on a dual voting system. Any Player can submit homebrew in the #homebrew-ua-submissions Channel. Each homebrew submission follows the same method:

1.  A Player Creates a Post in the #homebrew-ua-submissions channel. They Ping @homebrewer in the post. And react on the post with a 1, 2 and 3.
2.  Players then vote on the submission by reacting.
    1.  Yes
    2.  No
    3.  Abstain
3.  Once the Submission reaches 10 Yes votes, the submission is moved to homebrew-ua-approver-votes where a team of staff members will either approve or deny the homebrew

Some Rules:

1.  A Player is allowed one submission per week. Unless their submission get's approved by staff, in which case they can submit another.
2.  #homebrew-ua-submissions is reserved for submissions only. Players can discuss or query active homebrew in #homebrew-ua-discussion.

**In-Character Homebrew Creations:**

When submitting Homebrew, a player can declare an item to be invented, or otherwise created by one or multiple characters. The creation or access to that Homebrew is then restricted to those with the same in-character access to it. In order to maintain this restriction, the actual creation must be roleplayed within a reasonable timeframe of the item's passing (no people rushing to craft an item the minute it passes before a person can rp)

ie: a wizard sharing a spell they created with another; an inventor selling items or sharing designs

**Discord Lists:**

Items:

*   **Armors:**
    *   /wiki title:Homebrew Item List: Armors section:# Mundane
    *   /wiki title:Homebrew Item List: Armors section:# Rare
    *   /wiki title:Homebrew Item List: Armors section:# Very Rare
    *   /wiki title:Homebrew Item List: Armors section:# Legendary

*   **Weapons:**
    *   /wiki title:Homebrew Item List: Weapons section:# Mundane
    *   /wiki title:Homebrew Item List: Weapons section:# Common
    *   /wiki title:Homebrew Item List: Weapons section:# Uncommon
    *   /wiki title:Homebrew Item List: Weapons section:# Rare
    *   /wiki title:Homebrew Item List: Weapons section:# Very Rare
    *   /wiki title:Homebrew Item List: Weapons section:# Legendary

*   **Wondrous:**
    *   /wiki title:Homebrew Item List: Wondrous section:# Common
    *   /wiki title:Homebrew Item List: Wondrous section:# Uncommon
    *   /wiki title:Homebrew Item List: Wondrous section:# Rare
    *   /wiki title:Homebrew Item List: Wondrous section:# Very Rare
    *   /wiki title:Homebrew Item List: Wondrous section:# Legendary
    *   /wiki title:Homebrew Item List: Wondrous section:# Various

## Homebrew Index

*   [Blessings](/docs/category:homebrew-blessings "category:homebrew blessings")
*   [Boons](/docs/category:homebrew-boons "category:homebrew boons")
*   [Classes](/docs/category:homebrew-classes "category:homebrew classes")

Hot Shot

# Hot Shot

*Evocation cantrip. (Artificer, Ranger, Sorcerer, Warlock)*

**Meta**

**Casting Time:** 1 action

**Range:** 20 feet

**Components:** S, M (a ranged weapon worth at least 1 sp)

**Duration:** Instantaneous

**Description**

You brandish the weapon used in the spell’s casting and make a ranged attack with it against one creature within 20 feet of you. On a hit, your walking speed increases by 5 ft until the end of your turn.

This spell’s damage and speed bonus increases when you reach certain levels. At 5th level, the ranged attack deals an extra 1d6 fire damage and your speed increases by 10 feet instead of 5 feet. The damage roll and speed bonus increases by 1d6 and 5 feet at 11th level (2d6 and 15 feet) and 17th level (3d6 and 20 feet).

House Of Light

# House of Light

*9th-level Conjuration (Cleric, Celestial Patron, Druid, Sage, Sorcerer)*

**Casting Time**: 1 action

**Range**: Sight

**Components**: V, S, M (a diamond worth 5,000gp)

**Duration**: Concentration, 1 minute

A pillar of radiance rises from a point you can see, enclosing an area of up to a 360ft radius, and 1 mile high in bright light. When you cast this spell, creatures of your choice must make a Constitution saving throw or be blinded for 1 minute. They may repeat the saving throw at the end of their turn, ending the blindness on a success.

Each round that you maintain concentration produces an additional effect, in addition to the last:

*   Healing Waves (Round 2): Creatures and objects within the area regain hit points equal to your spellcasting modifier. Creatures of an alignment you choose receive no healing and instead take the same amount as radiant damage so long as they have no fewer than half their hit points total.
*   Community (Round 3): Up to 5 willing creatures within the area can each sacrifice a number of hit points up to half your level. These hit points are represented as a pool of temporary hit points that can be divided as you choose among any number of creatures that you can see within range. Creatures can only sacrifice hit points once over the duration of the spell.
*   Vitality (Round 4): Creatures and objects within the area gain your choice of either bludgeoning, piercing or slashing damage resistance until the start of your next turn. Creatures of an alignment you choose receive no resistances and instead lose resistances, if any, of the chosen damage types until the start of your next turn.
*   Guiding Light (Rounds 5): Creatures within the area gain a 1d4 bonus to ability checks and saving throws until the start of your next turn. Creatures of an alignment you choose instead receive a 1d4 penalty to ability checks and saving throws until the start of your next turn.

Hurler

# Hurler

**Description**

You are a master hurler of thrown weapons and can make shots that others find impossible, impaling your foes with the greatest of ease. You gain the following benefits:

*   Attacking at long range doesn't impose disadvantage on your thrown weapon attack rolls.
*   **Pinning Impalement:** When you hit a creature with a thrown weapon attack from a spear, javelin or trident, you may attempt to impale them onto an adjacent surface. The creature must make a DC 8+PB+STR strength saving throw. On a failed save, a creature is pinned to the surface for 1 minute, or until it or another creature within reach of it uses an action to remove the weapon. A creature impaled by the weapon has its speed reduced to 0. If a flying creature with no adjacent surface is impaled this way, instead reduce their movement speed by 10ft until the weapon is removed as an action. Pinning Impalement can be used a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus and you regain all uses on a long rest.

Iaijutsu

# Iaijutsu

**Prerequisite**

Proficiency in a Martial weapon

**Description**

Iaijutsu is the martial art of drawing a weapon and attacking with it in the same fluid motion. It is the foundation of some forms of dueling in Kara-Tur, and its masters are feared and respected throughout Rokugan and other lands where iaijutsu is practiced. When you draw a weapon in combat, you have advantage on the first attack you make with that weapon until the end of the turn. If that attack hits, you may reroll the weapon's damage dice, taking the higher rolls.

Imp

# Imp

Cowardly tricksters which are often used as battle fodder, and yet even so they are deft thieves and shapeshifters.

**Ability Score Improvements:**

When determining your character’s ability scores, increase one score by 2 and increase a different score by 1, or increase three different scores by 1. You can't raise any of your scores above 20.

**Age:**

Imps reach maturity at around 5 and live until they are killed.

**Creature Type:**

Imps are considered fiends.

**Size:**

Your size is tiny.

**Speed:**

Your walking speed is 20ft.

**Languages:**

You speak common and infernal

**Darkvision:**

Thanks to your infernal heritage, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions.

You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can’t discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

**Hellish Resistance:**

You gain resistance to fire damage.

**Fiendish Trickery:**

You gain proficiency in Stealth or Deception checks, and starting at 5th level you can cast Invisibility once per long rest.

**Flight:**

You have a flying speed of 40ft while not wearing medium or heavy armor.

**Barbed Stinger:**

You have a have stinger as a natural weapon attack which deals 1d4+your dexterity or strength modifier piercing damage and any target hit by this attack must make a dc 11 constitution saving throw or take 2d4 poison damage.

Impact Cannon

# Impact Cannon

*Weapon (Blunderbuss), rare*

**Description**

This modified blunderbuss instead condenses all of its ammunition into a single clump, which detonates on impact.

This weapon deals bludgeoning damage instead of piercing damage. When you hit a target with this weapon, it and every other creature within 5 ft of the target must succeed a DC15 dexterity saving throw, taking 2d6 slashing damage on a failed save.

Inner Elemental Sorcery (2024 Sorcerer)

# Inner Elemental Sorcery (2024 Sorcerer)

## Description

Your soul is bound to the primal weave of the four great elements—Fire, Earth, Water, and Air. Each spell you cast leaves a lingering **Strain**, a trace of elemental magic you can mold into disruptive bursts or overwhelming force.

## Inner Elemental Spell List

When you gain a level in this class, you can choose from this list in addition to the sorcerer list. These spells count as sorcerer spells for you and don’t count against the number of sorcerer spells you know.

Sorcerer Level | Spells

*   3rd | Chromatic Orb, Earth Tremor, Thunderwave, Melf’s Acid Arrow
*   5th | Fireball, Lightning Bolt
*   7th | Ice Storm, Stone Shape
*   9th | Cone of Cold, Wall of Stone

## Level 3 — Elemental Strains

You gain access to **Strains**, elemental charges tied to the four primal elements.

**Elements & Damage Types:**

*   **Fire** — Fire damage
*   **Earth** — Bludgeoning or Force damage
*   **Water** — Cold or Acid damage
*   **Air** — Thunder or Lightning damage

### Gaining Strains

*   Whenever you deal damage with a spell and spend a sorcery as part of the spell, you gain 1 Strain of the matching element.
*   You can only gain **1 Strain per spell**, even if it affects multiple creatures.
*   Strains reset to 0 on a short or long rest or if you don’t use any Strains for 1 minute.

**Maximum Strains per Type:**

*   3rd Level: 1
*   6th Level: 2
*   14th Level: 3
*   18th Level: 4

### Strain Surge

You may only expend Strains when you also spend at least 1 Sorcery Point on the spell, you use a bonus action to surge a spell. The Strain adds an extra effect to the spell:

*   **Fire:** Target ignites, taking 1d6 fire damage at the start of its next turn.
*   **Earth:** Target’s speed is reduced by 10 ft. until your next turn.
*   **Water:** Target has disadvantage on the next Strength or Dexterity saving throw it makes before your next turn.
*   **Air:** Target cannot take reactions until the start of your next turn.

You can apply only one Strain Surge per spell at this level.

You do not gain Strain when you Surge

## Level 6 — Chromatic Layering

You can now expend up to **2 Strains** on a single spell, applying both single-element Surge effects to the same target.

If you choose **two different elements**, the combined magic creates an additional combo effect from the table below. This effect applies **in addition** to the normal Surge effects of both elements.

**Two-Element Combo Table**


Elements (2 Strains)Combo Effect
Fire + EarthA violent explosion scatters debris; creatures within 5 ft. of the target (other than the target) take 1d6 bludgeoning damage and must succeed on a Strength save or be knocked prone.
Fire + WaterSteam burst blinds the target; it must succeed on a Constitution save or be blinded until the start of your next turn.
Fire + AirSuperheated wind scorches and shoves; target takes an extra 1d8 fire damage and is pushed 10 ft. away from you.
Earth + WaterSlick mud grips the target’s feet; target’s speed is halved and it must succeed on a Dexterity save or fall prone.
Earth + AirShrapnel-laden gust; target takes an extra 1d8 piercing damage and must make a Constitution saving throw, on a fail loses concentration if concentrating.
Water + AirIcy wind chills the target’s bones; target takes an extra 1d8 cold damage and has disadvantage on attack rolls until the start of your next turn.


**Save DC:** All saves use your Sorcerer Spell Save DC.

**Extra Damage Scaling:** Extra damage dice from combo effects increase to 2d8 at 14th level.

## Level 14 — Elemental Mastery

You can now expend up to **3 Strains** on a single spell to induce a Major Strain effect, when you do so replace its normal Surge with a Major effect, these effects can only target a single creature

**Major Strain Effects:**

*   **Fire:** Dexterity save or the Target is engulfed in flames, taking 3d6 fire damage at the start of its turns for 1 minute, the target can make a dexterity saving throw at the end of it's turn to stop the burning.
