*   **Earth:** Strength save or Target is restrained as stone or force binds them, the target can make a strength saving throw at the end of it's turn to free itself from its binding.
*   **Water:** Wisdom save or a single target is slowed (as the *Slow* spell) for 1 minute, the target can make a wisdom saving throw at the end of it's turn to end the effect.
*   **Air:** Constitution saving throw or the target is pushed 20 ft. and stunned for 1 minute the target can make a wisdom saving throw at the end of it's turn to end the effect.

If the initial saving throw is successful the target still suffers the effect from single surge table

**Strain Conversion:** As a bonus action, when you have at least 1 Strain of three different elements, you can expend all three to regain 1 Sorcery Point.

## Level 18 — Chromatic Ascendance

When you have **4 Strains** stored, you can absorb them as an action, entering an **Ascendant Form** for 1 minute. You automatically enter your **Innate Sorcery** state without spending Sorcery Points. While in an Ascendant Form, you gain resistance to the relevant damage types and special powers as listed below.

### Single-Element Forms *(4 Strains of one element)*

#### Inferno Sovereign *(4 Fire Strains)*

*   Fire damage you deal ignores resistance.
*   **Flame Wreath:** A creature that touches you or hits you with a melee attack while within 5 feet of you takes 1d10 fire damage.
*   **Blazing Ingress:** You can enter a hostile creature’s space and stop there. The first time you enter a creature’s space on a turn, that creature takes 1d10 fire damage.

#### Earth Titan *(4 Earth Strains)*

*   You gain temporary hit points equal to twice your sorcerer level.
*   You cannot be pushed, knocked prone, or restrained unless you wish it.
*   **Seismic Crash (1/turn):** When you deal damage, you can cause the ground to quake in a 10-ft radius around the target. Each creature of your choice in the area must make a Strength save or be knocked prone and take bludgeoning damage equal to your Charisma modifier + your proficiency bonus.

#### Tide Monarch *(4 Water Strains)*

*   You gain a swim speed equal to twice your walking speed and can breathe underwater.
*   *Water Form.* You can enter an enemy's space and stop there. You can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch without expending extra movement to do so.
*   **Crushing Pressure (1/turn):** As an action you can attempt to crush a creature in your space, it must make a Strength saving throw (DC = your spell save DC). On a failed save, the creature takes 4d8 + your Charisma modifier bludgeoning damage and, if it is Large or smaller, it becomes **grappled** (escape DC = your spell save DC). While grappled in this way, the creature is **restrained**, begins **suffocating** unless it can breathe water. A creature within 5 feet of you can use its action to pull a grappled creature free, succeeding on a Strength (Athletics) check against your spell save DC. You can only grapple one creature in this way

#### Tempest Emperor *(4 Air Strains)*

*   You gain a flying speed equal to your walking speed.
*   When you deal lightning or thunder damage, you deal an extra 1d8 of that damage type, you can roll an extra d8 of damage if you roll an 8. This damage can do a max of 5d8 damage.
*   **Storm Burst (1/turn):** When you deal damage, you can unleash a 10-ft radius shockwave centered on the target. Creatures of your choice in the area take lightning damage equal to your Charisma modifier + your proficiency bonus and can’t take reactions until the start of your next turn.

### Prismatic Overlord *(1 of each Strain)*

Choose 4 damage types from the following list:

fire, bludgeoning, cold, acid, lightning, and thunder damage

*   You gain resistance to the chosen types
*   Your spells ignore resistance to any of these damage types.
*   When you deal damage of one of the chosen damage types, you deal an extra 1d6 for each of the other damages (your choice each time).
*   **Prismatic Pulse (1/turn):** The first time you damage a single creature each turn, you unleash a burst in a 15-ft radius around it. Each creature of your choice in the area, excluding the target,  takes damage equal to your Charisma modifiers of **each** of the four elemental types

Iron Heart

# Iron Heart

*Wondrous Item, Very Rare*

**Description**

Made as a replacement for a faulty heart, this metallic organ was created to help terminally ill patients become well again. To use this item, you need to undergo an hour-long ritual in which the heart merges into your chest, permanently replacing your original heart. As well as being able to function as a normal heart, this magic item grants you advantage on any saving throws or checks made to resist or end blood-borne diseases and injury poisons.

Iterative Perfection

# Iterative Perfection

**Prerequisite**: Vedalken

“Perfection is a myth. Progress is measurable.”

Your relentless pursuit of improvement turns every flaw into a chance for refinement. You gain the following benefits:

*   +1 ASI to any ability score up to a maximum of 20
*   Analytical Adjustment. When you miss a melee/ranged/spell attack, should the next attack be made with the same weapon, or the same spell, against the same creature/object, that attack can be made with advantage and adding half of your ability modifier to that attack roll.

Jade Lord

# Jade Lord

Throughout the continent of kara-tur, one mineral stands above all else in terms of status. Where in the west you would display decadence with gold, in the east, the truest symbol of power is *jade*. Legends tell of warriors embellished with regality, often proud in status and seeped in regality. The Jade Lords of Shou Lung were legendary figures that displayed power and wealth in equal measure, fighting with both honor and flair.

## Regal Countenance

Even at 3rd level, you carry yourself with grace and nobility. You gain proficiency in either Persuasion or Intimidation. If you already have both of those proficiencies, you can choose a different proficiency instead.

## Jade Shards

At 3rd level, the gutteral flow of combat splinters jade around you. When you hit a creature with a weapon attack or a creature hits you with an attack, a jade shard splinters off of you and orbits you for the duration. You can only have a number of jade shards equal to half your fighter level (rounded up) at a time. When initiative ends, any remaining jade shards drop to the floor and disappear.

You can expend any number of these shards to take special actions called jade tricks. If the trick requires the target to make a saving throw, the DC is calculated as follows:

**Jade Trick DC**

\= 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice)

If you’re able to make multiple attacks with the Attack action, a trick replaces one of them. You can only use a trick once per attack action.

### **Jade Blade.**

Make a melee attack with a melee weapon you are holding against a target within range. On a hit, the target suffers the weapon attack’s normal effects and takes a number of d6s in additional damage equal to the amount of shards spent.

### **Nephrite Burst.**

Make a ranged or thrown weapon attack (your choice) against a target within 120 feet of you. The attack deals a number of d8s of piercing damage equal to the amount of shards spent.

### **Jadeite Blast.**

Choose a point you can see within 60 feet of you. Creatures within 10 feet of that point must make a Dexterity saving throw or take a number of d10s in slashing damage equal to the jade shards spent.

## Mystic Stone

At 7th level, the jade you create is fortified. Damage from jade tricks count as magical for the purposes of overcoming resistance and immunity to non-magical damage.

## Jade Armor

At 10th level, you learn to pull in the jade shards orbiting you to use as armor. As a bonus action, you can expend any number of jade shards to create jade armor. While this armor is active, you have half cover, or 3/4ths cover if you spent five or more shards. This armor lasts for a number of rounds equal to the jade shards spent or until the end of initiative.

## Verdant Generosity

At 15th level, you are able to protect allies with your jade armor as well. If any ally is attacked or is forced to roll a saving throw, you can use your reaction to expend any amount of jade shards and encase them in jade armor. This armor confers the same amount of cover as your jade armor feature, but only lasts until the end of that ally’s next turn.

## Jade Emperor

At 18th level, you learn a new jade trick.

### **Jadestorm.**

By expending any amount of jade shards, you can create a jadestorm around you in a 20 foot radius, designating any creatures you wish to be unaffected by it. The storm travels with you, with its area being lightly obscured. An affected creature that begins its turn within the jadestorm takes bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage equal to the shards expended. Additionally, their AC is reduced by half the expended amount (rounded down) until the start of their next turn or until they leave the jadestorm. The jadestorm lasts for the expended amount of rounds or until the end of initiative.

Juggernaut

# Juggernaut

## Description

*While in many cases using heavier two-handed weapons would put the user at a disadvantage defensively, Juggernauts utilize this augment to their offensive capabilities to compliment their defenses with little tradeoff.*

**Features:**

*   [3rd Level - Martial Training](#3rd_Level_-_Martial_Training)
*   [3rd Level - Defensive Battering](#3rd_Level_-_Defensive_Battering)
*   [5th Level - Crushing Force](#5th_Level_-_Crushing_Force)
*   [13th Level - Demolishing Stance](#13th_Level_-_Demolishing_Stance)
*   [18th Level - Raw Terror](#18th_Level_-_Raw_Terror)

## 3rd Level - Martial Training

When you select this specialization at 3rd level, you gain proficiency in martial weapons.

## 3rd Level - Defensive Battering

Additionally at 3rd level, you have the ability to use larger weapons to block and deflect incoming attacks. While you are wielding a weapon with two hands, you gain a +1 bonus to your armor class.

## 7th Level - Crushing Force

Starting at 7th level, the sheer force you deliver with your attacks is enough to shatter your foe’s defenses. When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can reduce the effectiveness of their armor, if they are wearing any, reducing their armor class by 3. Shields, and armor created by spells or other magic are unaffected, and you cannot reduce a creature’s armor class below what it would be without armor. This reduction lasts until the end of the creature’s next turn.

## 13th Level - Demolishing Stance

When you reach 13th level, your martial capability reaches new heights in conjunction with your defenses. While your defensive Stance is active, you gain a bonus to damage rolls with weapons equal to the armor class bonus given to you by your stance.

## 18th Level - Raw Terror

At 18th level, your very presence strikes terror into your foes. While your defensive stance is active, you can use a bonus action to force each creature of your choice within 30 feet of you to make a wisdom saving throw (DC 8 + your strength modifier + your proficiency bonus) or be frightened of you for one minute. An affected creature can repeat this save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a successful save. Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until you take up a new defensive stance.

Kitsune

# Kitsune

## Ability Score Increase.

Your Dexterity score increases by 2. You may put +1 to any ability that does not already benefit from a racial ASI.

## Age.

A typical kitsune lives to 600 to 1000. It gains an additional century for every tail it grows, to a maximum of 9 tails.

## Alignment.

As kitsune are natural tricksters, they tend towards gentler forms of chaos.

## Size.

A kitsune is usually between 4'5inch and 5'10inch. Your size is Medium.

## Speed.

Your base walking speed is 35 feet.

## Darkvision.

You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

## Keen Senses.

Much like the foxes they resemble, kitsunes have heightened senses. You are proficient in the Perception skill.

## Fey Ancestry.

Your fey lineage gives you advantage on saving throws against being charmed. Magic cannot put you to sleep.

## Human form.

You can appear as a human indefinitely using a very simple illusion magic. However, on a (DC 10 + proficiency bonus + Charisma Modifier) Investigation check, a person looking directly at you can tell that you are not what you appear. You always appear to be the same human when you cast this spell. While in human form you have -1 to all dice rolls.

## Fox Tails.

You have one tail when you are born and every 3 levels you gain another tail; lvl3 = 2, lvl6 = 3, etc. A kitsune's tails are a symbol of growth and realization for themselves and reflects how powerful they are. To your DM's discretion, A kitsune can also grow tail's for pivotal moments in its life or personal growth.

## Fox Fire.

So long as your tails are not severed, you can cast certain spells. You know the dancing lights cantrip. At 3rd level you gain the ability to cast faerie fire once per long rest. Charisma is your casting ability for these spells.

## Languages.

You can read and write both Common and Sylvan.

Kuo Toa

# Kuo-Toa

## Description

Once mad servants of ancient gods, kuo-toa are strange, amphibious humanoids capable of surviving both land and sea.

## Creature Type

Humanoid

## Ability Score Increase

+2 Wisdom, +1 Constitution

## Age

Mature by 10, live up to 70 years.

## Size

Medium

## Speed

Walk 30 ft., Swim 30 ft.

## Amphibious

You can breathe air and water.

## Slippery

Advantage on ability checks and saving throws made to escape a grapple or restraint.

## Sunlight Sensitivity

Disadvantage on attack rolls and Perception checks relying on sight in sunlight.

## Languages

Undercommon, Aquan

Kusarigama

# Kusarigama

*Weapon (martial), 1d6 slashing or 1d4 bludgeoning,* 50gp, 4 lbs. Finesse, Reach, Two-Handed, Special

**Description**

**Special**: You can choose to attack with either the kama (sickle) or the weighted chain on your turn. You may then make an attack with the other weapon as a bonus action.

In addition, this weapon may be a monk weapon despite its properties.

*   **Kama (Sickle):** The sickle deals 1d6 slashing damage. The range of this is 5ft.
*   **Weighted Chain (Kusari)**: The chain deals 1d4 bludgeoning damage. The range of the chain is 10ft.

Last Stand Handgun

# Last Stand Handgun

*Weapon (Pistol), Very Rare*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

This pistol feeds off of its users vitality, but can provide great assistance in their hour of need.


You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls with this magic weapon. Every time you attack with this weapon, you take 2 necrotic damage that cannot be reduced in any way. Additionally while you are below 25 hit points, all targets are vulnerable to the piercing damage dealt by this weapon.


If you are reduced to 0 hit points or die from causes other than this weapon's self damage, you are revived with 1 hit point at the start of your next turn. During this turn, the weapon does not require your hit points to attack, but you die at the end of your turn. Once the weapon has revived you, it cannot do so again until the next dawn.

Leonin Pride

# Leonin Pride

**Prerequisite**

Leonin

**Description**

Your ancestry manifests in your fierceness; the pride of Oreskos echoes through you. You gain the following benefits:

*   Increase your Strength or Constitution score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
*   You have advantage on saving throws to avoid or end the frightened condition on yourself.
*   Your natural weapons count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.
*   The range of your Daunting Roar trait increases to 30 feet and you can add your Charisma modifier to its Save DC. Additionally, you regain a use of this trait when you bring a Huge or larger creature to 0 hit points.

Lesser Sun Blade

# Lesser Sun Blade

*Weapon (longsword), uncommon*

**Description**

This item appears to be a longsword hilt. While grasping the hilt, you can use a bonus action to cause a blade of pure radiance to spring into existence, or make the blade disappear. While the blade exists, this magic longsword has the finesse property. If you are proficient with short-swords or longswords, you are proficient with the lesser sun blade.

You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon, which deals radiant damage instead of slashing damage. When you hit an undead with it, that target takes an extra 1d4 radiant damage.

The sword's luminous blade emits bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet. The light is sunlight. While the blade persists, you can use an action to expand or reduce its radius of bright and dim light by 5 feet each, to a maximum of 30 feet each or a minimum of 5 feet each.

Lesser Thought Gem

# Lesser Thought Gem

*Wondrous Item, Uncommon*

**Description**

While holding the lesser thought gem, you can use an action to store a single thought strand created by the encode thoughts cantrip. The thought strand remains present for seven days before it decays, vanishing from the gem. You can use an action to touch the gem's surface and retrieve a thought strand within it, though it maintains its original duration as well as the decay time from being stored. Only one thought strand can be stored in a lesser thought gem at a time.

Leviathan'S Vessel Sorcerous Origin

# Leviathan's Vessel Sorcerous Origin

## Description

*Water is the element of change. The creatures of the deep survive because they can adapt to anything — shifting, flowing, enduring. They are never truly alone; the ocean itself is community, a vast and endless embrace where life persists against crushing darkness.*

*The Leviathan is this truth given form: the abyssal power of the ocean, both merciless and sheltering, both devourer and protector. Those who carry its mark become vessels of unfathomable power, embodying the adaptability, love, and crushing might of the deep. A Leviathan Vessel suffocates breath, bends water into chains, and calls forth the jaws of the abyss to devour all who defy them.*

**Features:**

*   [1st Level — Body of Water](#1st_Level_—_Body_of_Water)
*   [Leviathan Vessel Expanded Spells](#Leviathan_Vessel_Expanded_Spells)
*   [6th Level — Crushing Currents](#6th_Level_—_Crushing_Currents)
*   [14th Level — Abyssal Chains](#14th_Level_—_Abyssal_Chains)
*   [18th Level — Leviathan Ascendant](#18th_Level_—_Leviathan_Ascendant)



## Leviathan Vessel Expanded Spells

The Leviathan Vessel lets you choose from an expanded list of spells whenever you learn a sorcerer spell. The following spells are added to the sorcerer spell list for you.

Sorcerer Level    Spells

1st    create or destroy water, fog cloud

2nd    blur, misty step

3rd    tidal wave, wall of water

4th    watery sphere, greater invisibility

5th    maelstrom, cone of cold

7th\*    control water, dimension door

9th\*    tsunami, gate

\*Spells above 5th level count as Signature Spells. DMs may gate access to these as high-tier content

## 1st Level — Body of Water

You are bound to the ocean’s depths.

*   You gain a swim speed equal to your walking speed and can breathe underwater.
*   Once per long rest, or by spending 2 sorcery points, you can slip your form into liquid for an instant. As a bonus action, you may move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing.

## 6th Level — Crushing Currents

You can choke the life from others with conjured water.

As an action, choose one creature within 30 feet that needs to breathe. Spend 2 sorcery points and force the target to make a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC.

*   On a failed save, water floods their throat, making them gag. They cannot speak or cast spells with verbal components until the start of your next turn. If the creature is already underwater, it instead takes 2d6 cold damage as the abyss crushes its lungs.
*   On a success, the creature resists the effect.
*   Creatures that do not need to breathe are immune.

## 14th Level — Abyssal Chains

The abyss answers your will with shackles of water.

*   As an action, choose up to 3 creatures within 30 feet. Each must succeed on a Strength saving throw or become restrained by spectral chains of water until the start of your next turn.
*   While restrained in this way, a creature is treated as if caught within crushing currents.

Ocean’s Reach. You can spend 1 sorcery point as a bonus action to conjure and shape water manifestations. You may:

*   Create a platform of hardened water (Large, lasts 1 minute, supports creatures). The platform can hover and move up to 20 feet per round in any direction while you remain within 60 feet of it. You may mentally command its movement as a bonus action.
*   Form a wall of water (10 ft high, 10 ft wide, 1 ft thick, lasts 1 minute).
*   Summon a tendril of water to push or pull one creature within 20 feet up to 10 feet (Strength save negates).

## 18th Level — Leviathan Ascendant

You call forth the jaws of the deep.


Maw of the Abyss. As an action, you may spend 3 sorcery points to manifest the Leviathan’s spectral jaws. Choose a 30-foot line, 20 feet wide, within 60 feet.

    •    Each enemy in the line must make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC.

    •    On a failed save, the creature takes 2d10 force + 1d10 cold damage and is restrained inside the jaws until the start of your next turn.

    •    On a success, the creature takes half damage and isn’t restrained.

    •    Lingering Rift. After the jaws vanish, the ground (or space) in the area becomes a zone of churning abyssal water until the start of your next turn. Enemies treat this area as difficult terrain.

Light Blade Mastery

# Light-Blade Mastery

**Prerequisite**:

Proficiency with at least one type of light-blade

**Description**

You have become adept at using light-blades, allowing you to quickly put out attacks with them.

*   When you hit with a light-blade, the target and up to one other creature within 5 feet of you takes slashing damage equal to half your dexterity modifier as you cut across them.
*   Additionally, when you score a critical hit with a light-blade, the weapon's damage die becomes 1d12 instead of it's usual amount.

Light-Blades: Dagger, Sickle

Light Trap

# Light Trap

*3rd-level abjuration. (Bard, Wizard, Sorcerer, Warlock)*

**Meta**

**Casting Time**: 1 action

**Range**: 60ft/30ft sphere

**Components**: V, S, M (a bit of fleece)

**Duration**: 1 minute

**Description**

This spell creates a mental phantasm of lights and sounds that dull the senses. Choose a point within range. Each creature you choose in a 30-foot-radius sphere centered on that point must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, their vision and hearing are restricted to 30ft for the duration. An effected creature can make a Wisdom saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

**At Higher Levels**

When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the effected targets lose an additional 5ft of vision and hearing for each slot level above 3rd.

Light Weaver

# Light Weaver

## Description

*Your powers are drawn from the spark of light kindled within you. In places of great darkness, there are those born with an affinity for light, and every shadow has a source. The magic within you represents cosmic balance, flickering from radiance to darkness moment to moment.*

Features:

[1st Level - Trick of the Light](#1st_Level_-_Trick_of_the_Light)

[1st Level - Flickering Aura](#1st_Level_-_Flickering_Aura)

[6th Level - Refraction Shield](#6th_Level_-_Refraction_Shield)

[14th Level - Aura Magnification](#14th_Level_-_Aura_Magnification)

[18th Level - Light Weaver](#18th_Level_-_Light_Weaver)

## 1st Level - Trick of the Light

Starting at 1st level, you have darkvision out to a range of 60 feet. If you already have darkvision, it increases its range by 60 feet.

You learn the Light and Sacred Flame cantrips. At 3rd-level you learn the Darkness and  Faerie Fire spells, which are sorcerer spells and do not count against your spells known. They can cast normally, or by expending 2 sorcery points.

## 1st Level - Flickering Aura

Starting at 1st level, you can magnify the properties of light around you. At the end of a  short or long rest, you can choose whether your magic is light-shifted or dark-shifted.

While light-shifted you can use an action to emit an aura in a 15-foot radius around you.  This area is bright light. While the aura persist, you can use a bonus action to expand or reduce its radius by 5 feet, to a maximum of 30 feet each or a minimum of 10 feet. While dark-shifted you gain the same ability, but you project an aura of nonmagical darkness. This effect lasts for 1 minute, or until you are incapacitated.

Once used, you cannot use this ability again until you finish a short or long rest

## 6th Level - Refraction Shield

At 6th level, you can manipulate the light around you for protection. As a bonus action, you can spend 2 sorcery points to magically create a shield of light or dark. The shield lasts for 1 minute, or until you are incapacitated.

While this shield is in place, whenever a creature within 10 feet of you hits you with an attack, this shield flashes with searing energy, and the attacker takes 2d6 radiant damage if you are light-shifted or 2d6 necrotic damage if you are dark-shifted.

## 14th Level - Aura Magnification

While your Flickering Aura is active, you can now expand it up to 10 feet as a bonus action, up to a maximum of 60 feet. While within the aura, a number of creatures equal to your Charisma modifier also gain the benefit of your Refraction Shield, but no more than once per turn.

Additionally, you and those creatures have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) skill checks made while light-shifted, or Dexterity (Stealth) checks made while dark-shifted. You can add your proficiency bonus when making these skill checks by expending a sorcery point.

## 18th Level - Light Weaver

Embodying both aspects of Light and Dark, you gain resistance to both radiant and necrotic damage. Additionally, as part of the action to activate your Flickering Aura, you can transform yourself and gain the following benefits for up to 1 minute:

Upon activation or as part of the bonus action to expand or reduce it's radius, you can switch to either light-shifted or dark-shifted. Each creature of your choice within your aura must make a Constitution saving throw, taking 2d8 radiant and 2d8 necrotic damage and becoming blinded till the end of their next turn on a failure, or half as much damage and not being blinded in a success.

While your Radiant Shield is active, the radius of its effect increases to 30 feet, and you deal both the radiant and the necrotic damage when hit with an attack.

Once you use this action, you can’t use it again until you finish a long rest, unless you spend 5 sorcery points to use it again.

Long Bo Staff

# Long Bo Staff

*Weapon: Martial, Melee*

*10gp, 4 lbs. --- 1d8 Bludgeoning -  Reach, Two-handed, Special*

**Description**

Proficiency with a Long Bo-Staff allows you to add your proficiency bonus to the attack roll for any attack you make with it.

**Special**:

Counts as a Monk weapon (For martial arts), as well as monks would be proficiency

This weapon has the following weapon mastery:

**Push:**

If you hit a creature with this weapon, you can push the creature up to 10 feet straight away from yourself if it is Large or Smaller

Love Domain

# Love Domain

## Description

Clerics of the Love Domain serve gods of love, passion, and beauty, such as Sune, drawing on the purifying and enchanting power of the sacred waters, the Evergold. They are focused on protective healing, social grace, and captivating presence.

**Features:**

*   [Love Domain Spells](#Love_Domain_Spells)
*   [Level 1 - Bonus Proficiencies & Evergold Touch](#Level_1_-_Bonus_Proficiencies_&_Evergold_Touch)
*   [Level 2 - Channel Divinity: Purifying Cascade](#Level_2_-_Channel_Divinity:_Purifying_Cascade)
*   [Level 6 - Flow of Beauty](#Level_6_-_Flow_of_Beauty)
*   [Level 8 - Potent Spellcasting](#Level_8_-_Potent_Spellcasting)
*   [Level 17 - Aura of Ideal Radiance](#Level_17_-_Aura_of_Ideal_Radiance)

## Love Domain Spells

Cleric Level | Spells

1st | Charm Person, Ceremony

3rd | Aid, Calm Emotions

5th | Beacon of Hope, Hypnotic Pattern

7th | Compulsion, Aura of Life

9th | Commune, Mass Cure Wounds

## Level 1 - Bonus Proficiencies & Evergold Touch

When you choose this domain at 1st level, you gain proficiency in the Persuasion skill and the Performance skill.

### Evergold Touch

Starting at 1st level, whenever you use a spell of 1st level or higher to restore hit points to a creature, that creature gains \*\*temporary hit points equal to your Cleric level + your Charisma modifier\*\* (minimum of 1). These temporary hit points last for 1 hour.

## Level 2 - Channel Divinity: Purifying Cascade

You can use your Channel Divinity to instantly cleanse and refresh those around you.

As an action, you present your holy symbol and evoke a wave of purifying golden energy. Choose any number of creatures within 30 feet of you. Any target you choose that is \*\*Charmed, Frightened, or Poisoned\*\* immediately ends that condition. Additionally, any creature whose condition is removed \*\*regains a number of hit points equal to 1d6 + your Wisdom modifier.\*\*

## Level 6 - Flow of Beauty

Starting at 6th level, you are perpetually surrounded by a faint glamour. You are under the constant effect of the \*Disguise Self\* spell, but you can only choose to appear as yourself at your most ideal and radiant. This effect cannot be dispelled by normal means.

Moreover, when a creature you can see within 30 feet of you succeeds on a saving throw against the \*\*Charmed\*\* or \*\*Frightened\*\* condition, you can use your reaction to grant that creature \*\*advantage\*\* on its next attack roll made before the start of your next turn.

## Level 8 - Potent Spellcasting

Starting at 8th level, you add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any cleric cantrip.

## Level 17 - Aura of Ideal Radiance

At 17th level, the Evergold flows through you in a constant, palpable wave of grace, affecting all who draw near. You gain the following benefits:

**Sanctuary of Beauty.**

When a creature within 30 feet of you makes an attack roll or targets you with a harmful spell, it must first succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your Cleric Spell Save DC. On a failed save, the creature must choose a new target or the attack or spell is wasted. On a successful save, the creature is immune to this effect until the start of its next turn.This effect functions only if you haven't made an attack roll or cast a spell that affects an enemy creature since the end of your last turn. If you break this condition, the effect automatically resumes at the start of your next turn.

**Protective Aegis.**

When a friendly creature within 30 feet of you takes damage, you can use your reaction to reduce the damage taken by an amount equal to your Cleric level + your Wisdom modifier. You can use this reaction a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus. You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

Lucerne Hammer

# Lucerne Hammer

*Martial Melee Weapon - 20 gp, 6 lbs - 1d10 bludgeoning - Two-handed, Heavy, Reach, \*\*Topple\*\**

A pole hammer designed for armored combat, or cracking the thick hides of monsters. Counts as both a hammer and a polearm.

Luminite Armor

# Luminite Armor

*Armor (Heavy), Non-Lootable*

**Description**

Armor infused with the essence of stars. A suit of luminite armor holds with it the power to boost it's wearers abilities across a spectrum of focuses. When luminite armor is made, it can come in one of the following forms, which determines it's properties:

*   Solar - While wearing this magical armor, you have a +3 bonus to your armor class, and resistance to fire damage. Your melee weapon attacks deal an additional 1d8 fire damage, and have a +1 bonus to hit. The armor has three charges, and regains all charges daily at dawn. When a creature hits you with a melee attack, you can expend one charge as a reaction to halve the damage, and cause the attacking creature to take fire damage equal to the reduction.
*   Vortex - While wearing this magical armor, you have a +2 bonus to your armor class, and resistance to lightning damage. Your ranged weapon attacks deal an additional 1d6 lightning damage, and have a +2 bonus to hit. The armor has three charges, and regains all charges daily at dawn. As a bonus action, you can expend one charge to become invisible until you move. Attacking does not break your invisibility.
*   Nebula - While wearing this magical armor, you are considered to be proficienct with it even if you lack proficiency with heavy armor, and you are resistant to psychic damage. Your spell attacks have a +3 bonus to hit. The armor has three charges, and regains all charges daily at dawn. As an action, you can expend one charge to gain one of the following effects for one minute:
    *   Regeneration - you regain 5 hit points at the start of each of your turns.
    *   Power - each of your spells gains a 2d8 bonus to one damage roll.
    *   Mana - When you cast a spell of 3rd level or lower, roll a d6. If the result is greater than the level of the spell, you do not expend a spell slot.
*   Stardust - While wearing this magical armor, you are considered to be proficient with it even if you lack proficiency with heavy armor, and you are resistant to cold damage. Creatures you summon through class features or spells gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls. The armor has three charges, and regains all charges daily at dawn. When you summon a creature throigh a class feature or spell, you can expend one charge to summon one additional creature. This creature lasts only half as long, or for one hour in the case of indefinite durations.

Mace Mastery

# Mace Mastery

**Prerequisite**:

Proficiency with at least one type of mace

**Description**

You have become adept at using maces, and can lever their use more effectively.

*   When you attack with a mace, you deal bludgeoning damage equal to your strength modifier if you miss on the attack roll.
*   Additionally, when you hit a creature with a mace, you can reduce that targets AC by 1 for one minute so long as they are wearing armor. You can do so no more than once per turn, and you cannot reduce a targets AC to a score lower than they would be without armor.

Maces: Flail, Mace, Morningstar

Magebane

# Magebane

**Prerequisite**:

Mage Slayer

**Description**

*   **Guarded Mind:** If you fail an Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma saving throw, you can cause yourself to succeed instead. Once you use this ability, you cannot use it again until you complete a short or long rest, but you can only regain uses of this ability up to twice per day through short rests.
*   **Spellbreaker**: When you hit a creature with an attack as part of your reaction granted by the \*Mage Slayer\* feat, and that creature is casting a spell, you can force it to make a Constitution saving throw as if the creature were concentrating on a spell. On a failed save, the spell is interrupted, and the spell slot and action used to cast it are wasted. If the target is concentrating on a spell, they make their concentration save immediately after their Constitution save for this ability. You may use this a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. You regain all uses upon completing a short or long rest.

Magic Toggle

# Magic Toggle

**Casting Time:** 1 minute (ritual)

**Range:** 30 feet

**Components**: V, S, M (a pinch of iron and cobalt powder worth at least 10 gp, which the spell consumes)

**Duration**: Until Dispelled

You implant a small repeatable magical mechanical force within a tiny object within range. When you do so, choose two rotational states relative to it's current rotation for the item to switch between. When the spell causes the item to switch between the two rotational states, it does so with the least required movement and can be designated to take anywhere from 1 second to 10 minutes. Finally, determine the circumstance that will trigger the spell to rotate the object.

When the circumstance occurs, the object rotates to the next or previous state (the second state when triggered for the first time). If an outside force or object would prevent the object from rotating, it stops harmlessly and that particular trigger is ignored. When you cast this spell, you can have the spell end after it returns to its original position, or it can remain and repeat its rotation whenever the trigger occurs.

The triggering circumstance can be as general or as detailed as you like, though it must be based on visual or audible conditions that occur within 30 feet of the object. For example, you could instruct a lock to automatically reset its rotation when the door is closed.

Magicked Bolt

# Magicked Bolt

*4th-level evocation. (Bard, Druid, Sorcerer, Warlock, Wizard, Battle Smith)*

**Meta**

**Casting Time:** 1 action

**Range**: Self

**Components**: S

**Duration**: Concentration, up to 1 minute

**Description**

You charge a free hand with crackling magical energy. When you cast this spell, and as a bonus action on subsequent turns, you can point at a creature you can see within 60 feet of you and release a bolt, provided the spell hasn't ended. The target must make a constitution saving throw or be incapacitated until the end of its next turn.

When you successfully incapacitate a target with this spell, you can instantly teleport to an empty space of your choice within 5 feet of it, and your melee weapon attacks against the incapacitated creature deal an additional 1d6 lightning damage.

The spell ends early if a target succeeds on its saving throw, or if you use your bonus action to do anything else.

**At Higher Levels**

When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the additional lightning damage increases by 1d6 for every two slot levels above 4th.

Marshwarden Bow

# Marshwarden Bow

[![Marshwarden Bow](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/e/ed/Marshwarden_Bow.png/revision/latest/scale-to-width-down/180?cb=20241011085700)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/e/ed/Marshwarden_Bow.png/revision/latest?cb=20241011085700)

[](/docs/file:marshwarden-bow.png)

Marshwarden Bow

*Weapon (any bow), Rare*

**Attunement**

Requires attunement.

**Description**

This bow is made of twisted cypress roots and covered in long, stringy moss. The bowstring is always slick with water.

You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon and nonmagical arrows fired from this bow deal an extra 1d6 acid or poison damage on a hit (your choice each time).

When you’re in a forest or swamp and are hidden from a creature, missing with an attack against it doesn’t reveal your position.

Wretched Burst. When you hit a target with an arrow fired from this bow, you can cause it to erupt in a putrid cloud centered on the target, duplicating the effect of the “Stinking Cloud” spell (save DC 15).

This version of the spell doesn’t require concentration. Once this property of the bow has been used, it can’t be used again until the next dawn or until it’s spent at least 1 hour submerged in naturally occurring swamp water.

Marvelous Magic Marker

# Marvelous Magic Marker

*Wondrous item, uncommon*

**Description**

This quill-like tool has a narrow brush that extends from its end, which produces its own ink. You can use this marker in place of ink and a quill as calligrapher's supplies. While holding it, you can use a bonus action to mentally change the color of the ink that flows from the brush. Decorating the marker's metal casing is a dazzling spectrum of colors, which move and shimmer while the marker is being used. The brush can be retracted into the metal casing to keep it from leaving errant marks by clicking a button at the top of the marker.

While holding the marker, you can use a bonus action to triple-click its button. For the next minute, the marker magically erases any of its own-generated ink it's brushed across without a trace, instead of leaving ink behind. This effect ends early if you use a bonus action to triple-click the button again. For the duration, you can erase up to 1 square foot of the marker's ink, containing no more than 150 characters or 25 words. Once this property of the pen has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

Mask Of Kings

# Mask of Kings

*Legendary, Wondrous*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

These masks are made of dark tempered steel usually found within the infernal courts, used to mark those who bear them with a respect and leadership, and the capabilities for subterfuge that would befit a king.

While attuned to this mask, you gain the following benefits:

Your Charisma Score becomes 20, unless it is already 20 or higher.

When you see a creature, and analyze them for 1 minute or more, as an action, you may turn yourself into a perfect illusionary representation of it, mimicking both its appearance and its voice for as long as you maintain concentration. (For clarification, this illusion can be dispelled by dispel magic, amf, and can be seen through with truesight)

You have advantage on charisma checks

Mask Of The Wild Beasts

# Mask of the Wild Beasts

*Wondrous Item, varies*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

These dark ceramic and gold masks are fashioned after animals that are sacred in certain cultures. Each mask's appearance and properties are based on the sacred beast it represents. While attuned to a mask, you can end your attunement to it and attune to a different one over the course of 1 minute, instead of over the course of one or more short rests.

**Crocodile (Common).**

While wearing a crocodile mask, you gain a swimming speed of 30 feet and can hold your breath for up to 10 minutes at a time.

**Lion (Uncommon).** While wearing a lion mask, you have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on smell. In addition, your nails extend into claws, turning your unarmed strikes with your hands into magic weapons that deal slashing damage with a damage die of 1d6.

**Hawk (Rare).** While wearing a hawk mask, you have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight, and attacking at long range doesn't impose disadvantage on your ranged attack rolls. In addition, you can cast the feather fall spell at will, targeting only yourself.

**Mule (Very Rare).** While wearing a mule mask, your Strength score increases by 2, to a maximum of 20, you count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift, and you have advantage on Strength and Dexterity ability checks and saving throws against being knocked prone. In addition, when you hit a Large or smaller creature with a melee weapon attack, you can use a bonus action to force that creature to make a DC 16 Strength or Dexterity saving throw (its choice). On a failed save, the creature is knocked prone.

**Jackal (Legendary).** While wearing a jackal mask, you have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks and immunity to necrotic damage.

In addition, you can use an action to fire a beam of deathly green magic from your forehead at a creature you can see within 60 feet of you. The creature must make a DC 17 Constitution saving throw, taking 7d8 + 30 necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Once this property of the mask has been used, it can't be used again until the next dusk.

A humanoid killed by this necrotic damage rises at the start of your next turn as a mummy under your control. The mummy acts on its own initiative count. On your turn, you can mentally command the mummy if it is within 120 feet of you and you aren't incapacitated (no action required). You decide what action the mummy takes and where it moves during its next turn, or you can issue it a general command, such as to attack your enemies or guard a location.

The mummy remains until you dismiss it as a bonus action, until it's reduced to 0 hit points, or until you are no longer attuned to any mask of the sacred beast, causing it to turn to dust. You can only create and control one mummy at a time in this way.

Masquerade Mask

# Masquerade Mask

*Wondrous Item, Common*

A blank, porcelain masquerade mask, it covers the full face and sports a pair of holes for eyes and a slit for the mouth. While it is being worn on the face, the wearer may choose to have the appearance of the mask change (no action required). It can change to any design, but must be of a style typical for a masquerade.

Maul Of Unbridled Rage

# Maul of Unbridled Rage

Weapon (maul), legendary

**Attunement**

requires attunement by a Barbarian

**Description**

You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

Seething with unbridled rage and untamable anger this weapon was made to force enemies to their breaking point. This magic weapon reacts to the rage pouring out of barbarians and uses their anger to their advantage. While raging this weapon deals an additional 3d6 force damage when you hit an enemy.

**Bloodlust**: Once per attack action, if you score a  critical hit, you may make one additional attack as part of that same action.

Medic

# Medic

**Description**

You master the physician's arts, gaining the following benefits:

*   Increase your Wisdom score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
*   You gain proficiency in the Medicine skill. If you are already proficient in the skill, you add double your proficiency bonus to checks you make with it.
*   During a short rest, you can clean and bind the wounds of up to six willing beasts, fey, and humanoids. Make a DC 15 Wisdom (Medicine) check for each creature. On a success, if a creature spends a Hit Die during this rest, that creature can forgo the roll and instead regain the maximum number of hit points the die can restore. A creature can do so on a number of Hit Dice equal to half your Wisdom modifier, regardless of how many Hit Dice it spends.

Medium

# Medium

Mediums are the bridge between life and death, often treading over the gap between the two. They call forth spirits of the past, using them for power and knowledge.

## Seance

At 3rd level, you are able to tap into knowledge from spirits that you know of. You learn speak with dead and can cast it only as if it were a ritual spell. Rather than targeting a corpse, you can speak the name of a deceased individual who died within 1 mile. If their soul is able, they can choose to manifest in an empty space within 5 feet of you and answer your questions.

Once a soul is manifested in this way, you can’t manifest the same soul again for 24 hours.

## Medium Arts

Also at 3rd level, you gain access to new spirit arts that reflect your capacity to channel souls.

### Channel Warrior

As an action, you can spend 1 spirit point to channel the spirit of a warrior. The spirit manifests as a spectral fighter that is always 5 feet away from your target. Whenever you activate your spirit tether, you can have the warrior strike at the target in the same action. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take 2d6 force damage. If you use this spirit art again on the same target, you can channel another warrior, up to a maximum of 3 total. Each new warrior increases the force damage dealt to the target by an extra 2d6.

### Channel Trickster

As an action, you can spend 1 spirit point to channel the spirit of a trickster. The spirit manifests in the form of an imp, fairy, or any other tricky creature that is always 5 feet away from your target. Whenever you activate your spirit tether, you can have the trickster distract at the target in the same action. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have disadvantage on attack rolls and Perception checks until the end of its next turn.

### Channel Guardian

As an action, you can spend 1 spirit point to channel the spirit of a guardian. The spirit manifests as an ancestor, patron, or angel that is always within 5 feet of you. Whenever you activate your spirit tether, the guardian bestows 1d6 temporary hit points to you. If you use this spirit art again, you can channel another guardian, up to a maximum of 3 total. Each new guardian increases the temporary hit points gained by an extra 1d6.

## Eternal Spirit

At 6th level, your frequent interactions with other spirits has strengthened your own considerably. The range of your spirit tether increases to 300 feet. In addition, if

your tether breaks while you have at least one active spirit art, you regain one spirit point.

## Claircognizance

At 13th level, by drawing upon the experiences of the dead, you become temporarily able to utilize their skills. If you make a skill check in any skill you’re not proficient in, you can spend a spirit point to add your proficiency bonus to the roll. You can use this feature after the roll is made, but only before you know the results.

## Master Medium

At 18th level, you master your influence over the souls of others. You learn the following spirit art:

### Possession

If your target is a creature, you can use an action and spend 5 spirit points to attempt to possess them temporarily. The target must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be possessed by you for a minute. During this time, your soul is transferred into the target’s form, and your original body is left paralyzed. You now control the body but you don’t deprive the target of awareness. You use the possessed target’s statistics, but you retain your alignment, Wisdom, Intelligence, and Charisma. You don’t gain access to the target’s knowledge, class features, or proficiencies.

You may end the possession at any time, upon which you return to your body without harm. At the end of each of their turns, the target can repeat the Charisma save, ending the possession on a success. If the target dies while you possess it, your soul is returned to your body without harm. However, if your body dies while possessing the target, the possession ends and you die.

Megaton Hammer

# Megaton Hammer

[![Megaton Hammer](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/0/0e/Megaton_Hammer.png/revision/latest/scale-to-width-down/180?cb=20241011085851)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/0/0e/Megaton_Hammer.png/revision/latest?cb=20241011085851)

[](/docs/file:megaton-hammer.png)

Megaton Hammer

*Weapon (Maul), legendary*

**Attunement**

requires attunement by a creature with Strength 17 or higher

**Description**

The Megaton Hammer is a maul of extraordinary size, shape and weight. This maul is so heavy, in fact, few can lift it off the ground and fewer still can wield it in battle. The haft of this weapon is made of a sturdy wood, reinforced by adamantium, and attached to the end of this haft is a hammer head of unusual build. One side of the head is a spiked head whilst the other side is a one-foot diameter cylinder of pure metal with rivets and inscribed runes circling the side of the head. Between these two heads lies a single, fat, rounded spike that caps the end of the haft.

The Megaton Hammer has a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls and knocks back Large or smaller creatures 1d4 spaces (5-20 feet) on hit. The wielder can call upon the power held within the Megaton Hammer and strike the ground in front of them causing a fiery tremor within a 40-ft cone in front of them. Creatures caught in this cone must make a DC 16 Dexterity saving throw to take half of the 3d6 bludgeoning damage and 3d6 fire damage, and to avoid being knocked prone if they are a Large or smaller creature. This ability, rightfully called Earth-Shattering Strike, can not be used again until after you finish a long rest.

Memory Thief Sorcery (2024 Sorcerer)

# Memory Thief Sorcery (2024 Sorcerer)

## Description

The Memory Thief is a sorcerer touched by the silent weight of unspoken thoughts, lost truths, and stolen moments. Whether chosen by a god of shadows and secrets or born with the uncanny ability to pierce the veil of another’s mind, these sorcerers draw power not from raw elements—but from **memory, emotion, and identity** itself.

Every spell is an intrusion, every glance a chance to unravel another’s story. They are masters of **mental trespass and psychic influence**, collecting knowledge the way others collect coins. Some act as spies for hidden powers like *Vhaeraun*, gods of forbidden knowledge, or remnants of psionic empires. Others awaken to their abilities after surviving moments of profound trauma or divine contact—left able to **steal what others cannot even remember**.

*"Thoughts and Emotions are the truest form of currency. I never pay full price." - Memory Thief*

### *Playstyle and Motivations*

*The Memory Thief excels at **subtle control**, **emotional manipulation**, and **deep utility** in both narrative and combat play. With features that allow you to read and manipulate the thoughts and motivations of others, this subclass rewards **strategic roleplay**, **creative problem-solving**, and players who love to know more than they should.*

*   ***Reclaim the Past**: Seeking to recover a stolen or fragmented identity by stitching together memories from others.*
*   ***Weaponize Secrets**: Collecting powerful memories from kings, archmages, or ancient evils to manipulate the tides of history or blackmail enemies into silence.*
*   ***Protect the Mindscape**: Standing as a psychic guardian or counter-spy, exposing hidden corruption or breaking mind control through counter-manipulation.*

* * *

### *Who Would Thrive as a Memory Thief?*

*The Memory Thief is perfect for players who want to:*

*   ***Influence Story Through Subtlety**: Gain leverage by collecting key memories, discovering hidden motives, or creating complex emotional bonds with NPCs.*
*   ***Play with Identity and Perception**: Use your magic to assume false roles, mimic memories, or veil your soul against detection.*
*   ***Master Battlefield Presence Without Direct Violence**: Weaken enemies emotionally, disrupt their thoughts, and track them across time and space.*

* * *

***Ideal Players and Characters***

*🧠 **The Intrigue Manipulator***

*Players who thrive in espionage-heavy or social-intrigue campaigns, especially those who enjoy steering conversations with secrets and subtle leverage.*

*🕯 **The Shadowed Seer***

*Players who love mysteries and clues, piecing together narrative threads from the minds of others, treating every memory like a breadcrumb on a hidden trail.*

*🎭 **The Identity Shifter***

*Players who want to play with masks, personas, and the line between truth and falsehood—taking on aspects of others through their memories, voices, and fears.*

**Features:**

*   [3rd Level - Umbral Spike](#3rd_Level_-_Umbral_Spike)
*   [6th Level - Thought Scribe](#6th_Level_-_Thought_Scribe)
*   [14th Level - Hollow Mask](#14th_Level_-_Hollow_Mask)
*   [18th Level - Dark One's Trespass](#18th_Level_-_Dark_One's_Trespass)

## 3rd Level - Umbral Spike

Once per turn when damaging a creature with a spell or attack, you can deal an additional 2d6 psychic damage and mark them. While the creature is marked, you know their direction and distance while they are within 90 ft of you, lasting up to 1 hour. You can spend sorcery points to extend the duration by an additional hour per sorcery point spent.

The extra damage increases at 6th (3d6) level, 14th (4d6) level, and 18th (5d6) level.

You can use this a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier, regaining all uses on a Long Rest, or by expending a spell slot for each use restored (no action required).

## 6th Level - Thought Scribe

When you cast a spell of 1st level or higher that forces a creature to make a saving throw, you can extract a thought strand (as with the Encode Thoughts cantrip) on a failure. Once you use this feature on a creature, you cannot use this feature on that target again until you complete a short or long rest.

The thought strand contains one memory of a significant event within the past 24 hours. Significant events typically involve powerful emotions, such as battles and betrayals, marriages and murders, births and funerals, or meetings with important friends or leaders. However, they might also include more mundane events that are nevertheless important to that creature.

In addition, while holding the thought strand, you can communicate telepathically with the target while they are on the same plane of existence as you. You can choose if they are able to respond or not.

## 14th Level - Hollow Mask

As a bonus action, you can spend 1 or more sorcery points to magically transform your essence for 10 minutes. For each sorcery point you spend, you can gain one of the following benefits of your choice, the effects of which last until the transformation ends:

*   You can see through solid objects to a range of 30 feet, and can see any invisible creatures to a range of 60 feet. Within that range you have advantage on checks made to find hidden creatures, traps, or secret doors for the duration of the transformation.
*   You cannot be detected or targeted by any divination magic, or perceived through magical scrying sensors unless you wish to be. If you choose to be affected, you can present false information to the divination magic or scrying sensor for the duration of the transformation.
*   You can choose a creature type other than your actual creature type. Spells and other magical effects treat you as if you were a creature of the chosen type. After the transformation expires, ongoing and future spells and magical effects treat you as your original creature type.
*   You have advantage on saving throws against effects which would cause you to be charmed, frightened, or possessed. If you succeed against such an effect, you have advantage on Charisma checks made against that source for the duration of the transformation.

## 18th Level - Dark One's Trespass

As an Action, you can force a target you can see within 60ft of you to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC or be charmed or frightened (your choice) and you are able to detect the creatures surface thoughts and emotional state for up to 8 hours (those foremost in its mind, reflecting its current emotions and what it is actively thinking about). You can spend sorcery points to extend the duration by an additional hour per sorcery point spent. Alternatively, if you are holding a thought strand created by your Thought Scribe feature, you can target that creature as long as they are on the same plane of existence.

While charmed or frightened in this way, it undertakes to perform any services or activities you ask of it to the best of its ability. You can set the creature new tasks when a previous task is completed, or if you decide to end its current task. If the service or activity might cause harm to the creature, or if it conflicts with the creature's desires, the creature can make another Wisdom saving throw to try to end the effect. This save is made with advantage if you or your companions are fighting the creature. If the activity would result in certain death for the creature, the effect ends. During that time you can use a Bonus Action to see and hear through that creatures senses until the end of your next turn. You are blinded and deafened while this persists.

Once you use this Action, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest, unless you spend 7 sorcery points to use it again.

Menny'S Ephemeral Script

# Menny's Ephemeral Script

*1st-level illusion. (Bard, Warlock, Wizard)*

**Meta**

**Casting Time:** 10 minutes (ritual)

**Range:** Touch

**Components:** S, M (enchanted ink worth at least 10 gp, which the spell consumes)

**Duration:** Special

**Description**

You imbue a piece of parchment, paper, or some other suitable writing material with illusory writing that lasts for the duration. This writing is not suitable for scribing a spell scroll or filling a spellbook.

When you cast this spell, the page is instantly filled with text of your choosing. Choose a duration between 1 minute and 1 year. After this amount of time has elapsed, the magic fades and the writing vanishes.

Any time before the spell ends, you, and any creatures you designate when you cast the spell, can cast it again to extend the duration, up to a maximum of 1 year. Extending the duration in this way does not require the material component.

Merfolk

# Merfolk

## Description

Graceful denizens of the sea, merfolk are at home beneath the waves, rarely surfacing except to defend their homes or trade with land-dwellers.

## Creature Type

Humanoid

## Ability Score Increase

+2 Dexterity, +1 Charisma

## Age

Mature at the same rate as humans and live about 120 years.

## Size

Medium (7–9 feet from head to tail)

## Speed

Walk 10 ft., Swim 40 ft.

## Amphibious

You can breathe air and water.

## Languages

Common, Aquan

## Subraces

### Bioluminescent Merfolk

You know the light cantrip. Once per long rest, you can cast color spray (Charisma).

### Deep Sea Merfolk

You have darkvision (60 ft.) and resistance to cold damage.

### Siren Merfolk

You know the friends cantrip. Once per long rest, you can cast charm person (Charisma).

Millwood Giantbow

# Millwood Giantbow

*Weapon: Martial, Ranged*

3000gp, 20 lbs. --- 3d10 piercing - ammunition, special, range (120/480), two-handed

Requires a Strength score of 21 or higher and have a carrying capacity of a Huge or larger creature to be able to wield this weapon

**Description**

When making an attack with this weapon, you use your Strength modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

The arrows used by this weapon costs 1000 gp (Items that create ammunition still require 1000 gp, gold is magically disappeared from your characters pouch, magic knows all).

Millwood Greatbow

# Millwood Greatbow

*Weapon: Martial, Ranged*

300gp, 6 lbs. --- 2d6 piercing - ammunition, special, range (150/600), two-handed

Requires a Strength score of 18 or higher and be Medium or larger to be able to wield this weapon

**Description**

When making an attack with this weapon, you use your Strength modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

Monk Initiate

# Monk Initiate

**Description**

Your training has taught you to strike faster and in key points rather than rely on brute force. You gain the following benefits:

*   Increase your Dexterity score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
*   You can use Dexterity instead of Strength for the attack and damage rolls of your unarmed strikes.
*   You can roll a d4 in place of the normal damage of your unarmed strikes.

Monk Subclasses

# Monk Subclasses

## [Way of the Feral Fang](/docs/way-fo-the-feral-fang "way fo the feral fang")

*Monks who follow the Way of the Feral Fang tap into their primal instincts, channeling the savage power of beasts. They unleash their inner ferocity, embodying the untamed fury of nature itself.*

### Feral Strikes

Starting at 3rd level, when you use your Flurry of Blows feature, you can replace one of the unarmed strikes with the primal savagery cantrip. The primal savagery cantrip uses wisdom as your spellcasting ability modifier for its attack rolls.

### Closer to Nature

Starting at 3rd level, you can choose to learn the druidcraft or shillelagh cantrip, you use wisdom as your spellcasting ability modifier for this cantrip

### Bestial Agility

At 6th level, your connection to primal instincts enhances your speed and agility. You gain the following benefits:

*   You have advantage on saving throws against effects that would cause you to be restrained or paralyzed
*   If you use your Primal savagery cantrip granted by Feral Strikes in a turn you can use your reaction when an enemy makes an attack of opportunity against you to roll a Martial Art Die and add the result to your AC until the end of your turn.

### Fury of the Beast

At 11th level, your strikes become even more savage and relentless. When you hit a creature with your primal savagery cantrip granted by your Feral Strikes feature, you can add your wisdom modifier to the damage dealt, in addition, if you spend 1 ki point, the target must also make a Wisdom saving throw (DC 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Wisdom modifier) or be frightened of you until the end of your next turn.

### Primal Resilience

Starting at 17th level, your primal connection enhances your body’s resilience and ferocity. As a bonus action, you can spend 4 ki points to enter a primal state of feral resilience that last for 1 minute. You gain the following benefits when in this state:

*   You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed or frightened.
*   You add an additional Martial Art die to your unarmed strikes
*   You have advantage on all Strength and Dexterity saving throws, and you gain temporary hit points equal to your Monk level at the start of each of your turns. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a long rest.

## [Way of the Mirror Soul](/docs/way-of-the-mirror-soul "way of the mirror soul")

### Light Echo’s

3rd-level Mirror Soul feature

Starting when you choose this tradition at 3rd level, you can create reflections that disorient your foes and bolster your defense. When you use your flurry of blows, you can spend 2 ki points to cast the mirror image spell, replacing one of the unarmed strikes. When casted this way, you only make one illusory duplicate, unless you spend one ki point for an additional duplicate (3 duplicates max). For each duplicate you create, you gain 5 temporary hit points.

### Fractal Vision

6th-level Mirror Soul feature

As an action you may magically project your consciousness into a reflective surface that you can see. Projecting allows you to see and hear, as well as project your voice as though you were in that reflections space. During this time, you are blinded and deafened. You can sustain this effect for up to 10 minutes, and you can end it at any time (requires no action).

While being used in this way, you can use an action to project to another reflective surface that you can see. While you are projecting into a reflection, your reflection is visible, and can be interacted with by others, such as speaking with them. If any reflective surface along this path is destroyed or disrupted (in the case of liquids), this effect ends.

### Mirror Stride

11th-level Mirror Soul feature

You gain the ability to magically enter a mirror or reflective surface within 5 feet of you and move from inside it to inside another mirror or reflective surface being effected by your Fractal Vision feature.

You must spend 1 ki point and use 5 feet of movement to enter a reflection. As part of the movement used to enter the reflection, you can either pass into one of those reflections or step out of the reflection you're in. You appear in a spot of your choice within 5 feet of the destination, using another 5 feet of movement. If you have no movement left, you appear within 5 feet of the reflection you entered.

You can use this ability once per round, and take 1d10 force damage if you end your turn inside a reflection.

### Soul of Reflection

17th-level Mirror Soul feature

Starting at 17th level, you empower your Mirror Images for both defense and offense. While your Mirror Images persist, you gain the following benefits:

You and your illusory duplicates gain a +1 to Armor Class and Saving Throws for each duplicate still active (up to a maximum of +3)

Your unarmed strikes and monk weapon attacks deal additional psychic damage equal to your Wisdom modifier. (edited)

## [Way of the Ki Blade](/docs/way-of-the-ki-blade "way of the ki blade")

*Masters of a martial discipline intertwined with the ethereal energies of ki, the practitioners of the Way of the Ki Blade are formidable warriors who wield weapons forged from the very essence of their inner strength. These adept martial artists combine their physical prowess with the disciplined art of ki manipulation, creating a dance of blades that transcends the limits of the mundane.*

### Ki-Forged Weapon

At 3rd level, you can spend 1 ki point as a bonus action to manifest a ki-forged weapon, turning a melee monk weapon into a weapon of pure energy for 1 minute. This weapon deals force damage instead of its regular damage type and uses your Wisdom modifier for attack and damage rolls.

In addition, at level 5 the weapon gains a +1 bonus to its attack and damage rolls, unless it is a magic weapon that already has a bonus to those rolls, this bonus increases to +2 at level 11

### Disruptive Weapon

At 6th level, once per turn you can spend a ki-point to temporarily transform your ki-forged weapon into an ethereal weapon. The next melee attack you make with it before the end of your turn ignores the target’s armor, requiring no attack roll. Instead, the target makes a Dexterity saving throw against your Ki save DC. On a failed save, the creature takes damage as if hit by an attack from your ki-forged blade and it's inner balance and movement is disrupted by your ki, the target's speed is halved, and it can't use reactions. On its next turn, it can use either an action or a bonus action, not both. On a successful save, the  target takes half damage from the attack and isn't disrupted. The blade returns to normal immediately after the attack

### Ki-Bound Weapon

At 6th level, If your Ki-Forged blade has the thrown property you can make it return to your hand immediately after it is thrown by spending 1 ki point.

### Ki-Enhanced Focus

At 11th level, your focused ki protects you from mental intrusions and damage. Your thoughts can't be read by telepathy or other means unless you allow it, and you also have resistance to psychic damage.

### Ki-Forged Flurry

At 11th level, when you use your flurry of blows, you can attack with your ki-forged blade instead of unarmed strikes.

### Ki-Blade Ascendancy

At 17th level, your connection to the essence of ki reaches its pinnacle. You can now infuse your ki-forged weapon with extraordinary power. As an action, you can spend 4 ki points to enter the state of Ki-Blade Ascendancy, which lasts for 1 minute.

While in this state:

*   Your ki-forged weapon deals an additional 1d8 force damage on each hit, and you gain a number of temporary hit points equal to twice the force damage dealt by this feature.
*   Whenever you hit a stunned creature with your ki-forged weapon, you can spend an additional ki point to attempt to paralyze the target. The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your Ki save DC or be paralyzed until the end of its next turn.

## [Way of the Dragon Turtle](/docs/way-of-the-dragon-turtle "way of the dragon turtle")

*Curiously, the Way of the Dragon Turtle is a bit of a misnomer for the monks that follow this style. Their link to the legendary beasts is practically nonexistent, only dubbed this way by others for their fortitude and control over heat and steam. Monks that follow this martial style are rarely taught formally, though they may occasionally take on no more than a few students who wish to learn the style. Those who follow the Way of the Dragon Turtle are self-made and remarkable, often gaining an innate understanding of the ki flowing through and around them after overcoming intense hardship. Although this style may seem less refined than its contemporaries, the technical ability of these monks is fatal to underestimate. They pose as much of a threat to their own bodies by manipulating pressure and heat as they do to others, and they must always understand their limits above all else.*

### Ki-Scorched Body

Perseverance manifested in the form of raw ki has scorched and hardened your soul and body. When calculating your Monk unarmored defense, you may instead use 13 + your Constitution modifier.

Additionally, your body is altered by the unrefined, explosive ki you harness, often leaving a strange mark on your likeness. You determine its appearance, or generate it randomly by rolling on the Ki-Scorched Characteristic table.

| d6 | Ki-Scorched Characteristic |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | Your hands and feet are blackened, giving them the appearance of charcoal. |
| 2 | Steam often rises from your ears, mouth and body when you experience intense emotions. |
| 3 | The skin around your elbows and shoulders is cracked and ossified, peeling slightly upward like the scutes of a turtle. |
| 4 | You have a large scar or burn mark on your body, which becomes searing hot in a fight. |
| 5 | Still sources of waters nearby you tend to become restless, showing tiny ripples and tremors. |
| 6 | Your skin fluctuates between feeling extremely hot and cold to the touch. |

### Impact Combustion

Your rudimentary abilities in channeling ki allow you to compress and expel moisture and heat. As an action, you can cast *fog cloud* without providing material components. Once you cast *fog cloud* using this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest, unless you spend 2 ki points to cast it again.

Additionally, you gain the ability to convert the heat and force from blows you take into powerful outbursts. As a bonus action, if you have taken damage between the end of your last turn and the start of your current turn, you may spend 1 ki point to enter the Combustion state until the end of your turn. While in the Combustion state, you gain the following benefits:

*   You make Strength-based attack rolls with advantage.
*   Your unarmed strikes deal additional fire damage on a hit equal to one roll of your Martial Arts die plus your Wisdom modifier.
*   You can make one additional unarmed strike as part of your Attack action.

### Pneumatic Blitz

At 6th level, you gain the ability to catalyze the heat and momentum you build by moving into a volatile enhancement. If you move at least 30 feet in a straight line on your turn, you may immediately spend 2 ki points to increase your walking speed by 30 feet until the end of your turn. If you continue the current movement for at least 60 feet in a straight line, you may choose to emit a blast of excess pressure around you. When you end this movement, each creature within a 10-foot radius of you must succeed on a Strength saving throw or take a number of d10s equal to your proficiency bonus in force damage and be knocked prone. If a creature passes this saving throw, it takes half damage and is not knocked prone.

### Body of Vapor

At 11th level, your control of ki becomes more refined, allowing you to manipulate the elements of your physical form. As an action, you can spend 4 ki points to cast *gaseous form* on yourself, without providing material components. While in this form, your movement speed is not restricted to 10 feet.

Additionally, you may exit this form at any time on your turn. When you exit this form, you may heal 5d8 hit points. Once you regain hit points using this ability, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

### Pressurized Retribution

At 17th level, your mastery over the convergence and direction of ki reaches explosive heights. As a reaction to taking damage from a source other than yourself, you may spend up to six ki points. For each ki point you spend as a part of this reaction, you mitigate 5 damage. This damage mitigation is applied before any resistances you have. You may also redirect the force from this mitigated damage, blasting out a 40-foot cone of crushing steam. Creatures in this area must pass a Constitution saving throw against your ki save DC or take 10 fire damage for each ki point you spent as part of this reaction, taking half damage if they pass. Being underwater does not grant resistance to this attack.

Additionally, you are no longer required to have taken damage to enter the Combustion state as a bonus action.

Monk: Perfect Self

# Monk: Perfect Self

Optional (Replaces Standard Perfect Self feature)

Starting at 20th level, when you roll for initiative and have  0-3 ki, your ki is reset to 4.

In addition, your Dexterity and Wisdom scores increase by 4.

Your max for these abilities is also increased by 4, up to a max of 30.

Monocle Of Clarity

# Monocle of Clarity

*Wondrous Item, rare*

**Description**

[![Monocle](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/0/00/Monocle.png/revision/latest?cb=20241008110146)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/0/00/Monocle.png/revision/latest?cb=20241008110146)

This single lens has three filters that you can peer through. You can change the monocle's filter by rotating the lens' outer ring. A small arrow along the edge of the frame indicates the monocle's selected filter. The monocle has 4 charges and regains 1d3+1 each day at dawn. As an action, you can expend a charge to look through and activate one of the filters to gain one of several benefits:

Filter of Comprehend Languages. When you peer through this filter, you can read and comprehend any written language as per the written language portion of the comprehend languages spell.

Filter of Darkvision. While looking through this filter, you have darkvision out to a range of 60 feet.

Filter of See Invisibility. When gazing through this filter, you see invisible creatures and objects as if they were visible.

You gain the benefits of the activated filter while you wear the monocle for 1 hour or until you change the filter.

Monster Hunter Armor

# Monster Hunter Armor

*Armor (any armor), very rare*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

Armor of this variety is crafted from the very creatures it was designed to hunt, Dragons, Monstrosities, Undead etc.

You have a +1 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.

While wearing this armor, you have advantage on attacks against all creatures this armor was made to hunt, skill checks to track this creature. Can be made of any creature type except humanoid.

Moonlight'S Shadow

# Moonlight's Shadow

*5th-level evocation. (Cleric, Paladin, Sorcerer, Ranger, Warlock, Wizard)*

**Meta**

**Casting Time:** 1 bonus action

**Range**: Self

**Components**: V, S, M (a melee weapon worth at least 1 sp)

**Duration**: Concentration, up to 10 minutes

**Description**

You imbue a melee weapon you touch with moonlight and enchant your own shadow to hinder your foes. Until the spell ends, the weapon emits bright light in a 15-foot radius and dim light for an additional 15. If the weapon isn't already a magic weapon, it becomes one for the duration.

When you make a melee weapon attack on your turn, that attack can originate from any space within the lights radius as if you were in that space. You make this choice for each attack, as the moonlight solidifies to attack them.

Once during this spell's duration, you may use an action to fill a 30-foot radius circle, centered on the ground underneath you, with a magical shadow that remains there for the spell's duration.
