If a creature ends it's turn within this area, you may force them to make a strength saving throw. On a failure they are knocked prone and must use all of their speed to stand back up, rather than half.

Myconid

# Myconid

## Ability Score Increase.

Your Wisdom score increases by 2 and your Constitution score increases by 1.

## Age.

Myconids reach maturity at 6 years old and can live to be over 1000 with the oldest ones becoming sovereigns.

## Alignment.

Most myconids are True Neutral with good myconids are fairly common and evil myconids are very rare.

## Size.

Myconids range from 4 to 8 feet and weigh 70 to 180 lbs. Your size is Medium.

## Speed

Your base walking speed is 30 feet.

## Superior Darkvision

Accustomed to the darkness of the underdark, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 120 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern colours in darkness, only shades of grey.

## Sunlight Sensitivity

You have disadvantage on attack rolls and Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight whenever you, the target of your attack, or whatever you are trying to perceive is in direct sunlight.

## A Fistful of Spores

Your fists are home to toxic spores that can harm your enemies upon contact, and count as natural weapons that you can use to make unarmed attacks. If you hit with them, you deal 1d4 + your Strength modifier poison damage.

## Pacifying Spores

As an action, you can eject spores at one creature you can see within 15 foot of you. At the start of the target’s turn, it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw equal to 8 + your proficiency modifier + your Constitution modifier or be stunned for 1 minute or until it takes damage. The target can repeat the saving throw at the start of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. The effect also ends if the target takes damage. You regain use of this trait once you have completed a short or long rest.

## Rapport Spores

You may, as an action, extend rapport spores in a 20-foot radius. These spores can go around corners and affect only creatures with an Intelligence of 2 or higher that aren't undead, constructs, or elementals. Affected creatures can communicate telepathically with one another while within 60 feet. The effect lasts for 1 hour.

Necklace Of The Second Heart

# Necklace of the Second Heart

*Wondrous Item, rare*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**:

A fine silver chain holds a small crimson gem cut to resemble a heart. The stone feels faintly warm, pulsing with a soft rhythm that never quite matches your own.

**Properties**

*   **Heart’s Reserve**. When you are reduced to 0 hit points but not killed outright, the necklace flares with a pulse of red light. You instead drop to 1 hit point, and the gem dims until the next dawn.
*   **Empathic Sense.** Once per short or long rest, you can focus on the necklace to read the emotions of a creature within 30 feet that you can see. You immediately sense its strongest emotion (such as fear, anger, or calm)

Night Reaver

# Night Reaver

## Description

*You learn the art of siphoning life from dying enemies and combining it with negetive energy to imbue necromancy into your fighting style. Reavers often kill those who are closest to death, whose spiritual binds are thin enough to cut free and use.* Features:

*   [3rd Level - Criticality](#3rd_Level_-_Criticality)
*   [9th Level - Rend](#9th_Level_-_Rend)
*   [13th Level - Ward of Mortality](#13th_Level_-_Ward_of_Mortality)
*   [17th Level - Echo of Reaping](#17th_Level_-_Echo_of_Reaping)

## 3rd Level - Criticality

Starting at 3rd level, creatures with less than half of their total hit points gain a Death Mark, granting you the following benefits:

*   You gain an additional way to use your sneak attack; you don’t need advantage on the attack roll to use your Sneak Attack against a marked creature, so long as you don’t have disadvantage on the attack roll.
*   Attack rolls against marked creatures crit on 19-20.
*   You have a bonus on attack rolls against marked creatures. This bonus equals half your proficiency bonus.

## 9th Level - Rend

Starting at 9th level, you gain the ability to **rend** those close to death.

When you hit a marked creature with your Sneak Attack, you can deal additional damage equal to your rogue level. Once you use this ability, you may not do so against until you complete a short or long rest. If you reduce a creature to zero hit points with this ability, you regain a use.

## 13th Level - Ward of Mortality

Starting at 13th level, you learn to collect and conserve the life force you steal from dying opponents, which materializes as a ward.

When you hit a marked creature with **rend**, roll your total sneak attack die. You gain temporary hit points equal to the total, unless you already have more.

## 17th Level - Echo of Reaping

Starting at 17th level, you learn to travel to and harness remnants of death.

When you hit a creature with **rend**, you may teleport to a corpse within 90 ft of you as a bonus action, and the next attack you make before the end of your next treats the target as being blood marked. In addition, through a one-minute ritual, you may travel to an area of mass death or a sight where you slew a creature within 2 miles, bringing a number of willing creatures equal to the number of creatures you have killed today (maximum of 10). Once you use this ability, you may not do so again until you complete a long rest.

Oath Of Deceit

# Oath of Deceit

*While many paladins may follow a just code, one holding their morals and beliefs above all else, there exist those who instead swear an oath only to themselves. Paladins who undertake an oath of deceit take it upon themselves to do whatever it may take to come out on top of whatever situation they may find themselves in. They may employ underhanded tactics, subterfuge, and other such tactics to ensure their success. Despite this, these paladins still follow a code, and are not always out for evil.*

***Tenets of Deceit:***

***Concealment** - Never tell too much about your intentions, and never let your foes know what you are going to do next.*

***Willingness** - Never settle for second best when victory is attainable, even if you need to employ less than savory means to do so.*

***Self**\-**Worth** - You are the most important person to completing your goals, and never let anyone let you doubt your worth to them.*

## Oath Spells

You gain oath spells at the paladin levels listed in the Oath of Deceit Spells table. See the Sacred Oath class feature for how oath spells work.

| Paladin Level | Spells |
| --- | --- |
| 3rd | Disguise Self, Silvery Barbs |
| 5th | Phantasmal Force, Invisibility |
| 9th | Enemies Abound, Hypnotic Pattern |
| 13th | Confusion, Phantasmal Killer |
| 17th | Dominate Person, Mislead |

## Channel Divinity

At 3rd level, you gain the following Channel Divinity options. See the Sacred Oath class feature for how Channel Divinity works.

### Upper Hand

You can use your channel divinity to feint an attack against an enemy. As a bonus action, you can distract a target that you can see within 30 ft of you. Doing so will grant you advantage on attack rolls against the target until the end of your next turn, and impose disadvantage on attack rolls for the target to hit you. Additionally, your next weapon attack against the target deals an additional 2d6 psychic damage. The extra damage increases to 3d6 at 7th level, 4d6 at 15th level, and 5d6 at 20th level.

### Grand Deception

As an action, you can use your channel divinity to invoke a supernatural sense of confidence within yourself. For the next minute, you make all charisma checks at advantage. If you already have advantage from another source, you may reroll one of the dice. Additionally, no matter what you say, magic that would determine if you are telling the truth indicates that you are being truthful if you so choose.

## Aura of Betrayal

At 7th level, You emit an aura of magical charm while you aren't incapacitated. Creatures hostile towards you within 10 ft must succeed a Wisdom Saving throw against your spell save DC or be charmed until you or an ally deal damage to it. When a charmed creature is hit by an attack from you or an ally, the attack is automatically critical. Any creature that succeeds this saving throw is immune to your Aura of Betrayal for 24 hours.

At 18th level, the range of this aura increases to 30 ft.

## Underhanded Tactics

Starting at 15th level, you gain the ability to undermine a foe's initiative. When you roll initiative, you may choose to gain advantage on the roll. In doing so, you must impose disadvantage on another creature you can see within 60 ft of you. Additionally, that creature's speed is halved until the end of its first turn.

## Cloak of Lies

At 20th level, none can hold an advantage over you no matter how they try. As an action, you gain the following benefits for one minute:

*   The area within 5 ft of you becomes heavily obscured to creatures other than you.
*   You add your charisma modifier to damage rolls of weapon attacks.
*   creatures have disadvantage against saving throws against your enchantment and illusion spells, as well as your aura of betrayal.
*   creatures have disadvantage on ability checks made to discern the true nature of illusions created by you.

Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest, unless you expend a 5th level spell slot to use it again.

Oath Of Providence

# Oath of Providence

*Paladins aligned to the Oath of Providence are protected by the mysterious forces that guide our fates. Proud and stoic, they seek to prove their devotion to a chosen cause or deity, with the utmost faith that they are a part of a grand design, protected by destiny and fulfilling a higher purpose.*

## Channel Divinity

3rd-level Oath of Providence feature

You gain the following Channel Divinity options. See the Sacred Oath class feature for how Channel Divinity works.

### Revelation.

You can use your Channel Divinity to invest your presence with the guiding powers of fate. As an action, you can choose a number of creatures you can see within 30 feet of you, up to a number equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one creature). For 1 minute, you and the chosen creatures have advantage on Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma ability checks.

### Suspended Judgement.

As a bonus action, you can suspend judgement between yourself and another creature you can see within 10 feet of you, using your Channel Divinity. The damage dealt by you and the chosen creature against each other is delayed until the end of your next turn. At the end of that turn both you and that creature take the damage that was delayed  and must make a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 + your Charisma modifier + your proficiency bonus). On a failed save, the damaged received from the delayed attacks is doubled, receiving the normal amount instead on a success.

## Aura of Penitence

7th-level Oath of Providence feature

Starting at 7th level, you emanate an aura of regretful penitence as long as you’re not incapacitated. Whenever a creature misses an attack against the paladin, or any friendly creatures within 10 feet of the paladin, the attacking creatures takes radiant damage equal to half your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1).

At 18th level, the range of this aura increases to 30 feet.

## Twist of Fate

15th-level Oath of Providence feature

You’ve learned to help your allies avoid a dire fate. Whenever a friendly creature you can see within 30 feet of you fails a Strength, a Dexterity, or a Constitution saving throw, you can use your reaction to have that creature regain hit points equal to 2d8 + your Charisma modifier if it has no more than half its hit points.

## Divine Champion

20th-level Oath of Providence feature

Starting at 20th level, once per turn when you reduce a creature to 0 hit points with your Divine Smite, you can regain a number of hit points equal to your paladin level, and regain one charge of your Channel Divinity. This is usable a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, resetting on a Long Rest

Oath Of The Dusk

# Oath of the Dusk

## Description

### Paladin Sacred Oath

Paladins who swear the Oath of the Dusk embody twilight’s mysteries. They walk the line between light and darkness, embracing dusk as both an ending and a beginning. Where the sun fades, they stand vigilant, comforting those who fear the night and striking at terrors that thrive in it. To them, dusk is balance — a veil between hope and despair, life and death.

### Tenets of the Dusk

The tenets of the Oath of the Dusk vary slightly between orders, but all share a devotion to twilight’s balance:

*    Balance. Neither light nor darkness holds dominion — each has its place.
*   Comfort in Darkness. Offer peace when hope wanes, and be a lantern when others are lost.
*   Vigilance. Stand watch when others sleep; protect them from the dangers that stalk the night.
*   Endings & Beginnings. Accept what must fade, but kindle what must endure.

**Features**

*   [3rd Level - Dusk Oath Spells](#3rd_Level_-_Dusk_Oath_Spells)
*   [3rd Level - Channel Divinity](#3rd_Level_-_Channel_Divinity)
*   [7th Level - Aura of Dusk – Protective Twilight](#7th_Level_-_Aura_of_Dusk_–_Protective_Twilight)
*   [15th Level - Midnight Envelopment](#15th_Level_-_Midnight_Envelopment)
*   [20th Level - Avatar of the Dusk](#20th_Level_-_Avatar_of_the_Dusk)

## 3rd Level - Dusk Oath Spells

You gain oath spells at the paladin levels listed.

**Paladin Level    Spells**

*   3rd    Faerie Fire, Sleep
*   5th    Moonbeam, Misty Step
*   9th    Aura of Vitality, Counterspell
*   13th    Greater Invisibility, Aura of Purity
*   17th    Wall of Light, Dream

## 3rd Level - Channel Divinity

When you take this oath at 3rd level, you gain the following Channel Divinity options:

*   Twilight Sanctuary. As an action, you emanate a 20-foot-radius aura of soothing dusk. Allies inside the aura immediately gain temporary hit points equal to your paladin level + your Charisma modifier.
*   Cloak of Shadows. As a bonus action, you can veil yourself in dusk for 1 minute. Until the effect ends, you have advantage on Stealth checks. This effect ends early if you make an attack, cast a spell, or force a creature to make a saving throw.

## 7th Level - Aura of Dusk – Protective Twilight

Starting at 7th level, you and friendly creatures within 10 feet of you are shrouded in twilight’s protective veil. While within this aura, allies gain resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage.

At 18th level, the range of this aura increases to 30 feet.

## 15th Level - Midnight Envelopment

At 15th level, your twilight presence smothers and binds those who oppose you. When a hostile creature starts its turn within 10 feet of you, it must succeed on a Strength saving throw against your paladin spell save DC or be restrained until the start of its next turn.

A creature that succeeds on this saving throw is immune to your Midnight Envelopment until the next dusk.

At 18th level, the range of this effect increases to 30 feet.

## 20th Level - Avatar of the Dusk

At 20th level, you can invoke the mantle of twilight, becoming dusk incarnate for 1 minute.

*   You emit a 30-foot aura of twilight (bright light in 15 feet, dim light in the next 15).
*    Hostile creatures that start their turn in the aura must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be frightened until the start of their next turn.
*   Allies in the aura regain 10 hit points at the start of their turns.
*   You gain a flying speed of 60 feet, surrounded by swirling dusk and shadow.

Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a long rest.

Oath Of The Grave

# Oath of the Grave

## Description

*The tenets of the Oath of the Grave have been passed down for generations.  This oath emphasizes the passing between the warmth of life to the everlasting cold of death, and punishes those that defile this passing with the unholy raising of undead.*

***Annihilate the Unholy.** The undead and those who foster them are unholy and wicked.  Destroy them before they defile more than they have already.*

***Preserve the Righteous.** Death should not come early to those who have led a blessed life.  Preserve the righteous, so they may live on without fear.*

***Let Souls Pass On.** Every soul deserves its place in the afterlife.  When you see a creature forced into undeath, let its soul go free.*

***Accept Death.** Death comes to us all eventually.  Accept it, and you will find a place in the afterlife.*

**Features**

*   [3rd Level - Channel Divinity](#3rd_Level_-_Channel_Divinity)
*   [3rd Level - Oath Spells](#3rd_Level_-_Oath_Spells)
*   [7th Level - Aura of Preservation](#7th_Level_-_Aura_of_Preservation)
*   [15th Level - Soul of Demise](#15th_Level_-_Soul_of_Demise)
*   [20th Level - Reaper of Winter](#3rd_Level_-_Channel_Divinity)

## 3rd Level - Channel Divinity

When you take this oath at 3rd level, you gain the following two Channel Divinity options.

### Icy Repose.

As an action, you present you holy symbol and speak a prayer against undeath, using your Channel Divinity. Each undead that can see or hear you within 30 feet of you must make a Wisdom saving throw.  If the creature fails its saving throw, it is frozen for 1 minute or until it takes damage. A frozen creature is affected by the petrified condition until the effect ends.

### **Vow of Death.**

As a bonus action, you can utter a vow of death against a creature you can see within 10 feet of you, using your Channel Divinity.  You deal an extra 1d6 necrotic damage to the creature whenever you hit it with an attack for 1 minute or until it drops to 0 hit points or falls unconscious.  If a creature under this effect dies, it cannot become undead.

## 3rd Level - Oath Spells

You gain oath spells at the paladin levels listed.

##### Oath of Devotion Spells

###### Paladin Level | Spells

*   3rd | inflict wounds, ice knife
*   5th | gentle repose, Snilloc's snowball swarm
*   9th | sleet storm, speak with dead
*   13th | blight , ice storm
*   17th | cone of cold, destructive wave

## 7th Level - Aura of Preservation

Starting at 7th level, you and friendly creatures within 10 feet of you can’t be charmed while you are conscious. In addition, you can choose one of two Grave features, Necrotic Protection or Necrotic Annihilation:

### Necrotic Annihilation

Damage dealt by your Divine Smite and Improved Divine Smite features can be changed to necrotic instead of radiant .You can change the damage type between necrotic and radiant when you finish a long rest.

### Necrotic Protection

You and any allies within your Aura gain resistance to Necrotic damage Once you choose a Grave feature it can only be changed when you reach a level in this class that grants the Ability Score Improvement feature

At 18th level, the range of this aura increases to 30

## 15th Level - Soul of Demise

Beginning at 15th level, when a creature under the effect of you vow of death dies, that creature cannot be raised from the dead for the next 30 days, outside of using a wish spell. Additionally, you can use a bonus action on a subsequent turn of yours to transfer the mark to a new creature.

## 20th Level - Reaper of Winter

At 20th level, as an action, you can emanate a deathly chill. For 1 minute, swirls of chilling wind surround you in a 30-foot radius. Whenever an enemy creature starts its turn within the area, the creature takes 10 cold damage and their movement speed is reduced by 10 feet. In addition, once a turn when a creature dies within the area, you regain 2d10+CON modifier hit points. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a long rest.

Oath Of The Howling Moon

# Oath of the Howling Moon

### *Tenets of the Howling Moon*

***Tame, Control, Become.** The "curse" of lycanthropy can be and must be controlled to not be defined by it, but rather to define it as a means of protecting others.*

***Great Power, Even Greater Responsibility.** Lycanthropy is not something that should be spread without caution, those who don't have the will or the drive to be able to master the curse, should not have it, and those who use it without understanding it must be taken care of. (Healed or Killed depending on you/your deities morality)*

***Shield the Innocent** Use your new strengths to shield the innocent from those who would harm them.*

***Never Take Vengeance Against The Ignorant.** Never take vengeance against those that do not understand the curse you bear. Instead show mercy and teach them that all is not always as it seems.*

## Channel Divinity

When you take this oath at 3rd level, you gain the following two Channel Divinity options.

### ***Predatory Strikes.***

As an action, you can reveal the beast inside and imbue your body with divine power, for 1 minute you can apply your Divine Smite feature to your unarmed strikes. You can use Dexterity instead of Strength for the attack and damage rolls of your unarmed strikes, which deal 1d6 bludgeoning, slashing, or piercing damage (your choice). This damage increases to 1d8 at 11th level. Additionally, when you use the Attack action to make an unarmed strike, you can make one additional unarmed strike as a bonus action.

### ***Lycan Scream.***

As an action, you channel the powerful roars/howls/screeches of your lycanthrope namesake. You force creatures of your choice equal to your proficiency bonus within 15 feet of you to make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, a creature becomes frightened of you for 5 rounds(half a minute). The frightened creature can repeat this saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

## Oath Spells

You gain oath spells at the paladin levels listed.

##### Oath of the Howling Moon Spells

| PALADIN LEVEL | SPELLS |
| --- | --- |
| 3rd | *hunter's mark, shield of faith* |
| 5th | *darkvision, pass without trace* |
| 9th | *haste, remove curse* |
| 13th | *dominate beast, freedom of movement* |
| 17th | *hold monster, mass cure wounds* |

## Aura of Feral Speed

Starting at 7th level, you fill nearby creatures with the feral speed of the beast inside. While you’re not incapacitated, you and creatures of your choice within 10 feet have their walking, swimming, and climbing speeds increased by 10 feet.

At 18th level, the range of this aura increases to 30 feet.

## Primal Roar

At 15th level you learn to channel your divine power and primal fury to others. As a bonus action, you unleash a primal roar infused with divine energy. Creatures of your choice up to half your paladin level (including yourself) within 40 feet of you that can hear you gain advantage on attack rolls and saving throws until the start of your next turn.

You can use this ability a number of times equal to your charisma modifier per long rest.

## True Alpha

At 20th level you have learned how to fully master the beast inside. You can use your action to perform one of the following options for 1 hour:

### ***Divine Transformation.***

You have resistance to nonmagical slashing, piercing, and bludgeoning damage from non-silvered weapons. You gain a +1 bonus to ac while not wearing heavy armor. You gain 5d8 + your Paladin Level temporary hit points.

### **Gift of the Lycans.**

You grant one willing creature resistance to nonmagical slashing, piercing, and bludgeoning damage from non-silvered weapons, a +1 bonus to ac while not wearing heavy armor, and 5d8 + your Paladin Level temporary hit points.

This effect ends early if you are incapacitated or die. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a long rest.

Oath Of The Sentinel

# Oath of the Sentinel

*You have given yourself to a cause of protection, to watch over a person, place, or thing until your dying breath. Their loyalty to their cause knows no bounds, and their patience is unending as they wait for a threat to arise. The few who take the Oath of the Sentinel are the most devout people that one would ever meet, almost machine-like in their duties. Paladins of this nature are most often found serving a monarch or other important political figure, guarding a vault or sacred object, or watching over a plot of holy land. In addition to their loyalty, the amount of power they have to muster to perform their duties is legendary even among other paladins. A paladin who has sworn this oath cannot ever falter in their watch. A single rest could spell doom for a countless number of innocents, regardless of the length.*

> ***TENETS OF THE SENTINEL***
>
> *Though the exact words and strictures of the Oath of the Sentinel vary, paladins of this oath share these tenets.*
>
> ***Lead from the Front.** Go gallantly and willingly to the front lines, placing yourself in danger to stir the hearts and minds of your companions. You need no standard-bearer; be the standard that draws the eyes of enemies and allies alike to the thickest part of the battle.*
>
> ***Be Ever Prepared.** Your enemy never rests, but you must never be unable to answer the call. Abstain from drink, and gluttony. Keep your body strong, and your will unbreakable.*
>
> ***Comrades Before Oneself.** A soldier alone is merely a soldier; many soldiers together are a force to be reckoned with. You are nothing without your comrades in arms; you must put the well-being and safety of your battle companions before your own, and when an ally falls, it is your duty to pick them up and carry them to safety if need be.*
>
> ***Defense for All.** Those who are living be able to live and be free from the fear of the uncertain as they deserve to until the end.*

## Channel Divinity

When you take this oath at 3rd level, you gain the following two Channel Divinity options.

### Forced Swing.

When you or a friendly creature within 60 feet of you takes damage, you can absorb the energy using a reaction and a use of your Channel Divinity. You reduce the damage taken by an amount equal to five times your paladin level. The first time you hit with a melee attack, you deal extra force damage equal to half of the damage mitigated.

### Absolution from Injury.

You can use your action or bonus action and a use of your Channel Divinity to don on armor you are proficient in that you can see within 30 feet of you. You must have worn the armor before.

## Oath Spells

You gain oath spells at the paladin levels listed.

##### Oath of the Sentinel Spells

| Paladin Level | Spells |
| --- | --- |
| 3rd | *alarm, sanctuary* |
| 5th | *ray of enfeeblement, spiritual weapon* |
| 9th | *protection from energy, slow* |
| 13th | *guardian of faith, otiluke's resilient sphere* |
| 17th | *antilife shell, wall of light* |

## Aura of Obstruction

Starting at 7th level, you constantly emanate a compelling force around you. Hostile creatures treat the area within 10 feet of you as difficult terrain. If those creatures attempt to leave the aura, they are forced to make a Charisma saving throw. On a fail, they take radiant damage equal to your paladin level and the force prevents them from leaving and can only move within the aura. Those creatures that are subjected to teleportation or planar travel out of the aura must also make the save, being unable to teleport or planar travel on a fail.

At 18th level, the range of this aura increases to 30 feet.

## Custodian's Directive

At 15th level, the first time you deal radiant damage on a turn, you can deal additional radiant damage equal to your proficiency bonus. If the target is an undead or fiend, they take double the amount of damage from this feature. The damage dealt by this feature bypasses resistances.

## Unfelled Bastion

At 20th level, you take a stand as a sentry that fights against all you deem an enemy. As an action, you gain the following benefits for 1 minute:

*   Your speed becomes 0. In exchange, you can hover and can use your bonus action to teleport to a location that you can see within 30 feet of you.
*   The reach of all your melee attacks extends up to 30 feet of you as a beam of energy reaches your targets instead. You can use this extended reach for purposes like opportunity attacks or other abilities. When you hit with attacks that are beyond your weapon's original reach, the damage dealt is radiant damage instead of the weapon's damage.
*   You can't be grappled, petrified knocked prone, restrained, or stunned.

Once you use this action, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest, unless you expend a 5th-level spell slot to use it again.

Oath Of The Steelsoul

# Oath of the Steelsoul

## Channel Divinity

When you take this oath at 3rd level, you gain the following two Channel Divinity options.

### Limitless Potential

As a bonus action, your proficiency bonus increases by 1 for one minute.

### Free the Chains

When you suffer an effect that requires you to make a saving throw at the end of your turn to end the effect, you can use your reaction to enter a focused state for 1 minute. While in this focused state you make end of turn saving throws at the beginning of your turn, instead of at the end. This saving throw is made with advantage.

## Oath Spells

You gain oath spells at the paladin levels listed.


Steelsoul Oath Spells
Paladin LevelSpells
3rdProtection from Evil and Good, Shield
5thSpiritual Weapon, Warding Bond
9thDispel Magic, Water Walk
13thFreedom of Movement, Otiluke's Resilient Sphere
17thGreater Restoration, Skill Empowerment


## Aura of the Steelsoul

At 7th level, when you or any creature of your choice within 10 feet of you take damage, that damage is reduced by an amount equal to your Charisma modifier(minimum of 1, maximum of 5).

At 18th level, this aura increases in range up to 30 feet.

## Immovable Stance

Starting at 15th level, you are immune to effects that would knock you prone.

If an effect moves you against your will along the ground(like being pushed or pulled), the distance you are moved is reduced by up to 10 feet. Alternatively, you can use a reaction to reduce the distance you are moved by up to 30 feet.

## Steelsoul Empyrean

Starting at 20th level, as an action, you gain the following benefits for 1 minute:

*   You gain 100 temporary hit points.
*   Any attack roll that isn't against you has disadvantage on any creature of your choice within 30 feet of you.
*   When a creature hits you with a melee attack, they must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or become stunned until the end of its next turn.

Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

Oath Of The Stormborn

# Oath of the Stormborn

## Description

*Paladins who swear the Oath of the Stormborn wield the fury of the heavens, channeling thunder and lightning as both shield and hammer. They are relentless warriors of the storm, striking down foes with divine force and protecting those caught in the tempest of war.*

**Tenets of the Stormborn**

*Thunder’s Wrath:* Let your enemies tremble before the storm’s fury. Strike down oppressors with righteous force.

*Storm’s Shelter:* Like the eye of the hurricane, you are a protector. Stand firm as a shield for the innocent.

*Unbreakable Tempest*: The storm does not yield; neither shall you. Remain steadfast in your convictions.

*Sky’s Judgment:* Let the wicked know there is no escape from the storm’s retribution.

**Features:**

*   [3rd Level - Stormborn Oath Spells](#3rd_Level_-_Stormborn_Oath_Spells)
*   [3rd Level - Channel Divinity](#3rd_Level_-_Channel_Divinity)
*   [7th Level - Thunder's Fury](#7th_Level_-_Thunder's_Fury)
*   [7th Level - Aura of the Tempest](#7th_Level_-_Aura_of_the_Tempest)
*   [15th Level - Stormforged Arsenal](#15th_Level_-_Stormforged_Arsenal)
*   [20th Level - Ascension of the Stormlord](#20th_Level_-_Ascension_of_the_Stormlord)

## 3rd Level - Stormborn Oath Spells

Paladin Level | Spells

*   3rd | Thunderwave, Fog Cloud
*   5th | Shatter, Gust of Wind
*   9th | Call Lightning, Fly
*   13th | Storm Sphere, Freedom of Movement
*   17th | Destructive Wave (thunder/lightning), Control Winds

## 3rd Level - Channel Divinity

### Thunderbound Weapon (Passive)

You summon a weapon bound to your soul, a conduit of the storm’s might.

*   You can summon or dismiss your Thunderbound Weapon as a bonus action.
*   Your Thunderbound Weapon must be a warhammer, battleaxe, greataxe, or maul.
*   You can spend 1 hour binding a magical weapon to make it your Thunderbound Weapon.
*   When using Divine Smite, you can choose to deal radiant, thunder, or lightning damage, the damage type is chosen whenever you use the Thunderbound weapon Channel divinity. This also applies to Improved Divine Smite.
*   The weapon deals 1d8 Additional thunder or lighting damage.

### Stormcaller’s Bolt (Active)

You can cast Witch Bolt without expending a spell slot. This version:

*   Deals thunder damage instead of lightning.
*   Automatically casts at the highest level available for a Paladin spell slot you have (up to 5th).

## 7th Level - Thunder's Fury

Your Thunderbound Weapon becomes magical for the purpose overcoming resistance.

## 7th Level - Aura of the Tempest

The storm’s energy crackles around you, offering both protection and retribution.

You and allies within 10 feet gain resistance to lightning and thunder damage.

Additionally once per turn when an enemy within 10 feet of you attacks an ally, you can, deal 1d8 thunder damage to the attacker and push the attacker 10 feet away from you.

At 18th level, the aura increases to 30 feet.

## 15th Level - Stormforged Arsenal

Your Thunderbound Weapon becomes an extension of the storm’s fury gaining the following benefits:

*   It gains the Thrown property and magically returns to your hand after an attack.
*   You can Divine Smite on ranged (thrown) attacks made with your thunderbound weapon
*   Versatile weapons (warhammer, battleaxe) have a thrown range of 30/60 feet.
*   Heavy weapons (greataxe, maul) have a thrown range of 15/30 feet.

## 20th Level - Ascension of the Stormlord

For 1 minute, you become a living storm, gaining the following benefits:

*   You gain a flying speed of 60 feet and hover.
*   **Storm Strikes:** Once per turn, when you hit a creature with your thunderbound weapon, you can cause a concussive thunder to explode on the target. You force them to make constitution saving thrown. On a failed save they attacked is incapacitated until the start of your next turn. If they fail the save by more than 5 they are instead stunned. Additional  a bolt of lightning arcs to another creature of your choice within 30 feet, forcing the creature to make dexterity saving thrown, dealing 4d10 lightning damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a save.
*   You also become immune to lighting and thunder damage for the duration

Once you use this feature, you can’t do so again until you finish a long rest or expend a 5th-level spell slot.

Oath Of The Valiant Guardian

# Oath of the Valiant Guardian

*In the darkest of times, when evil looms on the horizon and hope seems but a fleeting whisper, the Valiant Guardians rise. These Paladins are the beacons of courage, the bulwarks against despair, and the heralds of victory. They swear an oath not just to protect, but to inspire, lead, and become the legends that songs are sung about. Their deeds are the stories that will be told for generations, for they are the embodiment of heroism itself.*

Features:

*   [3rd level - Channel Divinity: Valiant Guardian](#3rd_level_-_Channel_Divinity:_Valiant_Guardian)
*   [3rd Level - Oath Spells: Valiant Guardian](#3rd_Level_-_Oath_Spells:_Valiant_Guardian)
*   [7th Level - Aura of Mighty Deeds](#7th_Level_-_Aura_of_Mighty_Deeds)
*   [15th Level - Never surrender!](#15th_Level_-_Never_surrender!)
*   [20th Level - A True Hero](#20th_Level_-_A_True_Hero)

## 3rd level - Channel Divinity: Valiant Guardian

### *To Me!*:

As a bonus action you can use your Channel Divinity to instantly teleport willing creatures within 60 feet of you to within 10 feet of yourself. The number of creatures you can teleport is equal to your Charisma Modifier (minimum of 1).

### *Inspiring Leader*:

You can use your Channel Divinity to augment your influence on those around you with divine favor. As a bonus action, you gain advantage on all Charisma checks for the next 10 minutes.

## 3rd Level - Oath Spells: Valiant Guardian

You gain oath spells at the paladin levels listed.

**Oath of the Valiant Guardian Spells**

**Paladin Level - Spells**

3rd - Expeditious Retreat, Heroism

5th - Enhance Ability, Enthrall

9th - Haste, Protection from Energy

13th - Freedom of Movement, Compulsion

17th - Commune, Steel Wind Strike

## 7th Level - Aura of Mighty Deeds

At 7th level, you build divine favor through your heroic deeds, for every hostile creature within 10 feet of you, you gain a +1 bonus to your AC, the bonus cannot exceed your Charisma Modifier. In addition non-hostile creatures already inside your Aura of Mighty Deeds when you use the To Me! Channel Divinity can make a weapon attack as a reaction against a foe within their range before or after teleporting to you.

At 18th level, this aura increases in range up to 30 feet.

## 15th Level - Never surrender!

At 15th level, your actions on the battlefield can supernaturally bolster your allies and demoralize your enemies. Whenever you score a critical hit or reduce a creature to 0 hit points, you can choose one or more creatures that you can see within 30 feet of you, up to a number equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one creature). All the chosen creatures are affected by one of the following effects of your choice.

*   The creature gains temporary hit points equal to 1d6 + your Charisma modifier.
*   The creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC or be frightened of you until the start of your next turn.

Once you use this feature you can't use it again until the start of your next turn.

## 20th Level - A True Hero

Starting at 20th level, you take on the visage of an ageless hero, breathing life into your companions, and possibly turning the tides of battle. As a bonus action, you gain the following benefits for 10 minutes.

*   Once on each of your turns when you use your Divine Smite feature, instead of dealing smite damage to the enemy you hit, you can heal a creature within 30 feet of you that you can see for the amount of damage you would have dealt with the smite.
*   Your presence in battle is overwhelming causing your opponents to focus their attention on you. When an attack is made against a friendly creature that you can see, if they or the attacker is within 10 feet of you, the attacker has disadvantage on attack rolls made against them.

Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest

Occult Seal

# Occult Seal

*Wondrous Item, uncommon(+1), rare(+2), very rare(+3)*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement by a spiritualist

**Description**

This small, square talisman is inscribed with occult runes. While this seal is adhered to your spiritual focus, you gain a bonus to the saving throw DCs of your spirit arts. The bonus is determined by the seal's rarity.

When a creature targets you with an attack or forces you to make a saving throw, you can activate this seal as a reaction, allowing you to instantly tether (one tether only) to the creature. Once you use this feature, you cannot use it again until the next dawn.

Oracle

# Oracle

Oracles are seers, soothsayers, and augurs, glimpsing into the future and oftentimes attempting to shape it. Whether your goal lies in preventing horrid events or keeping the future pure and unadulterated, an oracle bears the responsibility of influencing fate itself.

### Fate Seer

At 3rd level, you are able to predict the future to an extent. You can cast augury at will, using your spiritual focus in place of material components.

### Future Proofing

At 3rd level, you can subtly tug on the strings of fate to lessen the burden on your allies. Whenever you activate your spirit tether, you can choose whether to deal damage to a target or give them an equal amount of temporary hit points. These temporary hit points disappear if the tether breaks.

### Oracle Arts

Also at 3rd level, you gain access to new spirit arts that reflect your ability to shape the future.

#### Predestined Strike

When your linked target hits with an attack, you can use your reaction to spend 1 spirit point and choose between these options:

**Fortune.** Roll a d20. On a roll of 10 or above, the attack becomes a critical hit.

**Disaster.** Roll a d20. On a roll of 10 or below, the attack becomes a critical miss.

#### Eyes of Prescience

You can use an action and spend 1 spirit point to forsee any harm that might befall your target. Choose between these options:

**Fortune.** Until the beginning of your next turn, attack rolls against your target have disadvantage and the target makes Dexterity saving throws with advantage.

**Disaster.** Until the beginning of your next turn, the first attack roll against your target each turn has advantage.

#### Rewound Wounds

When you activate your spirit tether, you can spend 1 spirit point to use the following effects:

**Fortune.** If your target has taken damage within the last round, you can restore their hit points equal to 2d6 + your wisdom modifier, up to the total damage taken.

**Disaster.** If your target has had hit points restored or has gained temporary hit points within the last round, you can deal necrotic damage equal to 2d6 + your wisdom modifier up to the amount healed or the temporary hit points gained.

### Soothe-saying

At 6th level, your experience with fortune telling allows you to gain a deeper insight into the emotional states of others. While tethered to a creature, you gain an understand of their emotions, giving you insight into fine details. You know how they feel about their current situation, how they react to events that transpire around them, and are able to recognize how they feel about other people, yourself included.

### Spool of Fate

At 13th level, fate bows to your will. If you or an ally within 30 feet misses with an attack roll or fails an ability check or saving throw, you can use your reaction to allow that ally to reroll, potentially succeeding. The ally must use the new roll. You can only use this feature once per short rest, unless you spend 3 spirit points to use it again.

### Master Oracle

At 18th level, you master your influnece over controlling fate. You learn the following spirit art:

#### Divergence

If your tethered target becomes critical (10% or fewer hit points) or dies, you can use your reaction to spend 5 spirit points and use one of the following effects:

**Fortune.** You can shift fate so that such an event never occured. If dead, the target is brought back to life, all conditions on the tethered target are ended, and it regains 50 hit points.

**Disaster.** You can use the event to spell disaster for enemies. Tethered targets of your choice take 50 force damage and must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be stunned. If this damage reduces the target to 0 hit points, the target is disintegrated along with every nonmagical object it is wearing or carrying.

Each successful use of this spirit art each day increases its chance to fail by 33%. After three sucessful uses of this spirit art, further uses will fail automatically.

Orb Of Remembrance

# Orb of Remembrance

*Wondrous Item, uncommon*

**Description**

This glassy orb is 3 inches in diameter, weighs 1/2 pound, and is wrapped in a golden wire cage. Strange, wispy smoke drifts around inside the orb, creating cloud-like shapes that are almost recognizable as real-life objects.

While holding the orb, you can use an action to speak its command word, priming the orb and causing the smoke inside to restlessly billow and swirl. While holding the primed orb, you can gently squeeze it (no action required) to cause it to record that moment in time around you. When you do, the glassy sphere becomes perfectly clear over the course of 1 minute and reveals an exact duplicate of the area around you in up to a 30-foot radius as a miniature, illusory diorama inside it. While holding the orb, the scene inside soundlessly replays the next 6 seconds following the diorama's capture.

An orb remains primed for 1 minute or until used to capture a diorama. Touching a primed orb of remembrance to one that already has a diorama creates a duplicate of the diorama in the primed orb. Speaking the command word again while shaking the orb causes the diorama inside to disappear into smoke, allowing it to be primed once more.

Order Of The Symbiote

# Order of the Symbiote

## Description

*Those who enter the order of the symbiote are either a crazed or brave few. Taking in a new being into their own during an induction to hemocraft, they are able to tap into a special power granted by being now two halves of a greater whole. Whether the symbiote be embedded within their flesh, or coiling around their skin, as this new creature's host the Blood Hunters of this order boast an unmatched ability to tear down the biologies of those they may face.*

**Features**

*   [3rd Level - Rite of the Blighted](#3rd_Level_-_Rite_of_the_Blighted)
*   [3rd Level - Symbiotic Awareness](#3rd_Level_-_Symbiotic_Awareness)
*   [7th Level - Infected Exposure](#7th_Level_-_Infected_Exposure)
*   [7th Level - Brand of Contagion](#7th_Level_-_Brand_of_Contagion)
*   [11th Level - Mutualistic Host](#11th_Level_-_Mutualistic_Host)
*   [15th Level - Walking Dead](#15th_Level_-_Walking_Dead)
*   [18th Level - Blood Curse of Lethality](#18th_Level_-_Blood_Curse_of_Lethality)

## 3rd Level - Rite of the Blighted

When you join this order at 3rd level, you learn the rite of the blighted as part of your crimson rite features. When you activate the rite of the blighted, the extra damage dealt is poison damage. Additionally, hitting a creature with a weapon infused with this rite that is neither undead or a construct deals extra poison damage equal to your hemocraft modifier.

## 3rd Level - Symbiotic Awareness

A limited consciousness within your symbiote grants you an increased perception of your surroundings. You gain proficiency in the perception skill. Additionally, you may add your hemocraft modifier to your initiative rolls.

## 7th Level - Infected Exposure

At 7th level you know the Blood Curse of Exposure, which does not count towards your total number of blood curses known, and its potential rises to great power in combination with your toxins. If you apply your Blood Curse of Exposure to a creature after dealing poison damage from your Rite of the Blighted, the curse will instead last for 1 minute. If you have amplified the curse, you take damage equal to a roll of your hemocraft die at the start of each of your turns in exchange for your symbiote sustaining the infection.

If you already know the Blood Curse of Exposure, you may learn a different blood curse of your choice that you qualify for.

## 7th Level - Brand of Contagion

At 7th level, when you apply your Brand of Castigation to a creature while you have your Rite of the Blighted active, you may choose to inflict the target with a virulent infection. The target must succeed a constitution saving throw or experience one of the following effects, as determined by rolling your hemocraft die.


Contagion Table
Hemocraft Die RollEffect
1The target becomes Poisoned for 1 minute, and can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, expelling the poison on a successful save.
2A wave of weakness falls over the target. All attack rolls have advantage against the target until the start of its next turn.
3Your opponent's blood mixes with your symbiote, allowing a temporary breach in their immune system. The next blood curse you apply to that target within 1 minute that requires a saving throw will take effect regardless of whether or not their saving throw is successful or not.
4A mild paralytic compound infuses itself within the target, reducing all of its speeds by 10 ft for 1 minute. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.
5The toxins of your weapon attack invade the target's mind, causing them to make concentration saves at disadvantage until the start of your next turn.
6Your target has its physical attributes ravaged to some degree. The target suffers a penalty to all Strength, Dexterity, and Constitution Checks and Saving throws equal to your hemocraft die until the end of its next turn.
7Your target's vision fogs temporarily, causing them to become Blinded until the end of its next turn.
8Toxins invade your target's body entirely, causing their muscles to lock. The target is Paralyzed until the end of its next turn.
9An increased spike in potency causes your rite to deliver vastly increased lethality. For one minute, your Rite of the Blighted deals extra damage equal to three rolls of your hemocraft die instead of one.
10Toxins manage to tear into your target regardless of their biologies. The target loses all resistance and immunity to poison damage for 1 minute, gaining vulnerability instead. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a successful save.


Effects listed under a higher number than you can roll with your hemocraft die remain inaccessible until a level in which you can.

## 11th Level - Mutualistic Host

Starting at 11th level, your symbiote is able to grant you a further boon to your health. Whenever an effect would restore your hit points, you regain an additional amount of hit points equal to a roll of your hemocraft die.

Additionally, you learn the druidcraft cantrip. Your hemocraft ability is your spellcasting ability for this spell.

## 15th Level - Walking Dead

At 15th Level, If you are reduced to 0 hit points, your symbiote may assume control over your basic motor functions for a limited time in order to protect you. During this time you experience the following:

*   Your speed is halved
*   You cannot use your extra attack feature
*   You cannot take reactions
*   You Still make Death Saving Throws at the start of each of your turns
*   You suffer the normal effects of taking damage while at 0 hit points

The symbiote can maintain its control for a number of rounds equal to your hemocraft modifier (minimum of one), until you die, or until you regain at least one hit point. Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

## 18th Level - Blood Curse of Lethality

At 18th level, your symbiotic connection grants you the ability to entirely ravage a creature's health. You gain the Blood Curse of Lethality for your Blood Maledict feature. This doesn’t count against your number of blood curses known.

**Blood Curse of Lethality** - When you hit a creature with a weapon infused with your Rite of the Blighted, you may choose to apply this Blood Curse. The target must make a constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes 8d8 poison damage and suffers a random effect of your Brand of Contagion. The target takes half as much damage on a successful save and suffers no other effects.

Amplify - Amplifying this curse will instead reduce the target's hit points to 1 should they fail the saving throw, and take the full 8d8 poison damage on a success.

Paladin: Divine Smite

# Paladin: Divine Smite

Optional (Replaces Standard Divine Smite)

Starting at 2nd level, when you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can expend one spell slot to deal radiant damage to the target, in addition to the attack's damage. The extra damage is 2d8 for a 1st-level spell slot, plus 1d8 for each spell level higher than 1st, to a maximum of 5d8. The damage increases by 1d8 if the target is an undead or a fiend, to a maximum of 6d8.

Paladin: Improved Divine Smite

# Paladin: Improved Divine Smite

Optional (Replaces Improved Divine Smite)

By 11th level, you are so suffused with righteous might that all your melee weapon strikes carry divine power with them. Whenever you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, the creature takes an extra 1d8 radiant damage.

Path Of The Brawler

# Path of the Brawler

## Path of the Brawler:

*A Subclass of Barbarian that focus on unarmed fighting*

**Features:**

*   [3rd Level - Unarmed Combatant](#3rd_Level_-_Unarmed_Combatant)
*   [3rd Level - Brawler's Prowess](#3rd_Level_-_Brawler's_Prowess)
*   [6th Level - Reactive Fighting](#6th_Level_-_Reactive_Fighting)
*   [6th Level - Packin' Heat](#6th_Level_-_Packin'_Heat)
*   [10th Level - Fearsome Critical](#10th_Level_-_Fearsome_Critical)
*   [14th Level - Brawler's Focus](#14th_Level_-_Brawler's_Focus)

## 3rd Level - Unarmed Combatant

At level 3, your strength and general martial prowess increase the damage that you do with your bare fists. When you make an unarmed strike, your damage becomes 1d4 + your STR modifier. The damage dice increase with each level in this subclass, to become 1d6 at level 6, 1d8 at level 10 and finally 1d10 at level 14.

## 3rd Level - Brawler's Prowess

At level 3, While Raging, you gain new options to use as bonus actions, as long as you are not wielding any weapons or a shield.

*   Block: While raging, you can use your bonus action to raise your arms in a defensive stance. This gives you an AC boost of +2 until the start of your next turn. At level 10, this AC boost becomes +3
*   Jab: While raging, you can use your bonus action to make an unarmed strike against a target.

## 6th Level - Reactive Fighting

At level 6, while you are raging, when a creature attacks you and they are within 5 feet of you, you can make an unarmed strike against that creature as a reaction.

## 6th Level - Packin' Heat

At level 6, your unarmed strikes can even inflict damage on the toughest of foes, your unarmed strikes become magical for the purpose of overcoming non-magical resistance and immunity.

## 10th Level - Fearsome Critical

At level 10, when you score a Critical hit on a Target with your unarmed strike, you can apply brutal critical to your unarmed strike. Additionally when you score a critical hit on a target, they must save a Constitution saving throw against your save DC (8 + proficiency + strength bonus) or be frightened by you until the end of their next turn.

## 14th Level - Brawler's Focus

At level 14, while you are raging and you have only one creature within 20 feet of you, that creature has your undivided attention. You are in the "zone". You can make attacks against them with adv, and any wisdom saves they force you to make, are made with adv. While in the zone, if they try to leave your melee range, your Attack of Opportunity has the ability to stop them in their tracks, reducing their move speed to zero if they fail a strength save against your save DC (8+prof+strength bonus)

Path Of The Pact Sealer

# Path of the Pact Sealer

## Path of the Pact Sealer

*Many barbarians never concern themselves with the intricacies of magic, leaving others around them to cast their fancy spells and incantations. A Pact Sealer is no different, however enlisting the aid of a patron to grant them magical capabilities similar to a warlock. These barbarians know as much or as little about these spells as they wish, with their main concern being the power this magic grants them to crush their opponents on the battlefield.*

**Features:**

*   [3rd Level - Pact Magic](#3rd_Level_-_Pact_Magic)
*   [3rd Level - Raging Focus](#3rd_Level_-_Raging_Focus)
*   [3rd Level - Pact Resilience](#3rd_Level_-_Pact_Resilience)
*   [6th Level - Rage of Hadar](#6th_Level_-_Rage_of_Hadar)
*   [10th Level - Eldritch Invocations](#10th_Level_-_Eldritch_Invocations)
*   [14th Level - Eldritch Frenzy](#14th_Level_-_Eldritch_Frenzy)

## 3rd Level - Pact Magic

At 3rd level, you augment your combat techniques with the ability to cast spells. See chapter 10 of the Player’s Handbook for the general rules of spellcasting and chapter 11 of the Player’s Handbook for the warlock spell list.

### **Cantrips**

You learn two cantrips of your choice from the warlock spell list. You learn an additional warlock cantrip of your choice at 10th level.

### **Spell Slots**

The Pact Sealer Spellcasting table shows how many spell slots you have. The table also shows what the level of those slots is; all of your spell slots are the same level. To cast one of your warlock spells of 1st level or higher, you must expend a spell slot. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a short or long rest.

For example, when you are 8th level, you have two 2nd-level spell slots. To cast the 1st-level spell witch bolt, you must spend one of those slots, and you cast it as a 2nd-level spell.

Spells Known of 1st Level and Higher:

At 3rd level, you know two 1st-level spells of your choice from the warlock spell list.

The Spells Known column of the Pact Sealer Spellcasting table shows when you learn more warlock spells of your choice of 1st level and higher. A spell you choose must be of a level no higher than what’s shown in the table’s Slot Level column for your level. When you reach 13th level, for example, you learn a new warlock spell, which can be 1st, 2nd, or 3rd level.

Additionally, when you gain a level in this class, you can choose one of the warlock spells you know and replace it with another spell from the warlock spell list, which also must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

### **Spellcasting Ability**

Your spellcasting ability is charisma for your warlock spells, so you use your charisma whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability. In addition, you use your charisma modifier when setting the saving throw DC for a warlock spell you cast and when making an attack roll with one.

### Spellcasting Table

Barbarian Level - Cantrips Known - Spells Known - Spell Slots - Slot Level

3rd - 2 - 2 - 1 - 1st

4th - 2 - 2 - 1 - 1st

5th - 2 - 3 - 1 - 1st

6th - 2 - 3 - 2 - 1st

7th - 2 - 4 - 2 - 2nd

8th - 2 - 4 - 2 - 2nd

9th - 2 - 5 - 2 - 2nd

10th - 3 - 5 - 2 - 2nd

11th - 3 - 6 - 2 - 2nd

12th - 3 - 6 - 2 - 2nd

13th - 3 - 7 - 2 - 3rd

14th - 3 - 7 - 2 - 3rd

15th - 3 - 8 - 2 - 3rd

16th - 3 - 8 - 2 - 3rd

17th - 3 - 9 - 2 - 3rd

18th - 3 - 9 - 2 - 3rd

19th - 3 - 10 - 2 - 4th

20th - 3 - 11 - 2 - 4th

## 3rd Level - Raging Focus

At 3rd level, while raging, you are able to use any simple or martial weapon you are holding as a focus for your Pact Sealer spells

In addition, you are able to cast any spell granted by Pact Magic while raging. Doing so requires a successful constitution saving throw against your spell save DC. Starting at 11th level, you can also concentrate on spells granted by Pact Magic.

## 3rd Level - Pact Resilience

Determined by the patron you struck a deal with, you are able to gain an additional damage resistance while raging starting at 3rd level.

Patron - Damage Type

The Archfey - Psychic

The Celestial - Radiant

The Fathomless - Cold

The Fiend - Fire

The Genie (Efreeti) - Fire

The Genie (Marid) - Cold

The Genie (Djinni) - Thunder

The Genie (Dao) - Force

The Great Old One - Psychic

The Hexblade - Force

The Undead - Necrotic

The Undying - Poison

## 6th Level - Rage of Hadar

Starting at 6th level when you rage, once before your rage ends you can use a bonus action to cast the Arms of Hadar spell at the highest level available to you on the Pactsealer table without expending a spell slot.

## 10th Level - Eldritch Invocations

Starting at 10th level, you may choose 2 of the following invocations. You can choose a third invocation at 14th level. Whenever you gain a level in this class, you may change one of the invocations you selected for another.

### Enraged Casting

When you roll damage for a Pact Magic spell you cast while raging, you can add your rage damage modifier to the damage roll.

### Pact Bound Weapons

While you are raging, you can replace the bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage type of your weapon attacks with that related to your patron.

### Otherworldly Grip

You cannot be disarmed against your will, and you have advantage on checks made to grapple another creature. Creatures have disadvantage on checks made to escape your grapples.

### Boon of the Raven

While raging, you gain a flying speed of 30 feet.

### Evolving Resilience

When you take damage of the type corresponding to your patron, you can use your reaction to instead become immune to the damage until the start of your next turn. You can do this a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus before taking a long rest.

### Devil's Sight

You gain Darkvision out to a range of 120ft. Additionally, magical darkness does not impede your darkvision.

### Horrid Revitalization

As an action, you can bolster your natural regeneration. Doing so will cause you to regain hit points equal to 5 + your constitution modifier at the start of each of your turns for 1 minute. You cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

### Supernatural Strength

You gain proficiency in the athletics and acrobatics skills.

### Blinding Speed

You can cast haste once using a pact slot. You can’t do so again until you finish a long rest.

### Unholy Smite

You can cast Blinding Smite once without expending a spell slot. You regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest.

### Dimensional Instability

You can cast Blink once without expending a spell slot. You regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest.

## 14th Level - Eldritch Frenzy

When you score a critical hit against an enemy, you can recover one of your pact slots. You cannot restore another slot in this way until you finish a short rest

Path Of The Ravager

# Path of The Ravager

## Description

*A warrior who presents time in the way it was meant to be, a torrent of destruction. Barbarians who follow the Path of The Ravager use the breakage of time as their weapon, devastating enemies with powerful attacks with lasting impact.*

**Features**:

*   [3rd Level - Filling The Hourglass](#3rd_Level_-_Filling_The_Hourglass)
*   [3rd Level - Ravaging Strike](#3rd_Level_-_Ravaging_Strike)
*   [6th Level - Chronometry](#6th_Level_-_Chronometry)
*   [10th Level - Cadence](#10th_Level_-_Cadence)
*   [14th level - Time Templar](#14th_level_-_Time_Templar)
*   [14th level - Clockbreaker](#14th_level_-_Clockbreaker)

## **3rd Level - Filling The Hourglass**

Starting at 3rd level, you learn to extract a portion of a creature's lifespan from within the moments spent interacting with them. You extract a Grain of Time from a creature you land a critical hit against. Additionally you can touch the body of a creature you damaged in the last minute as a bonus action draining a portion of the time you stole gaining a Grain of Time. You can store a number of Grains of Time equal to twice your proficiency bonus. When rolling initiative you lose all Grains of Time you currently hold.

Furthermore you can consume a Grain of Time as bonus action to heal your injuries, when doing so you regain a number of hit points equal to half your Barbarian level.

## **3rd Level - Ravaging Strike**

Starting at 3rd level, you learn a powerful attack that allows you to drain time itself.

As an action while raging you may make a melee weapon with your weapon. On a hit, you can expend Grains of Time to deal an additional number of d8s equal to the number of Grains of Time spent necrotic damage. The creature is then afflicted with the Time Ravaged condition for 1 minute upon a failed Charisma Saving Throw, save dc equal to 8+your proficiency bonus+your Constitution modifier.

A creature afflicted with the Time Ravaged condition takes 1d4 (6th level (2d4), 10th level (3d4), and 14th level (4d4) necrotic damage at the start of each of its turns as you siphon grains of its future. As an action, the creature or an ally within its reach can disrupt the siphon, ending the Time Ravaged condition.

## **6th Level - Chronometry**

Starting at 6th level when taking the attack action you may use a Ravaging Strike in place of one of those attacks. Additionally, your Ravaging Strikes crit on a 19-20.

## **10th Level - Cadence**

Starting at 10th level The first time an enemy fails the saving throw for becoming time ravaged, they are also stunned until the end of your next turn.

Additionally you are resistant to necrotic damage, and immune to being magically aged, and you may use a Grain of Time as an action to gain another month on your natural lifespan.

## **14th level - Time Templar**

Beginning at 14th level due to your mastery over time after rolling initiative you only lose half the Grains of Time you have stored.

## **14th level - Clockbreaker**

Starting at 14th level when your Ravaging Strikes or the Time Ravaged condition reduces a creature to 0hp, you disintegrate them into sand, gaining an additional Grain of Time. Furthermore, you can make an additional Ravaging Strike as a Bonus Action.

Performer

# Performer

**Description**

You master performance so that you can command any stage. You gain the following benefits:

*   Increase your Charisma score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
*   You gain proficiency in the Performance skill. If you are already proficient in the skill, you add double your proficiency bonus to checks you make with it.
*   While performing, you can try to distract one humanoid you can see who can see and hear you. Make a Charisma(Performance) check contested by the humanoid’s Wisdom (Insight) check. If your check succeeds, you grab the humanoid’s attention enough that it makes Wisdom (Perception) and Intelligence (Investigation) checks with disadvantage until you stop performing.

Phalanx

# Phalanx

## Description

*A Phalanx draws their strength from their allies and coordination. Phalanx make excellent commanders as their leadership and guidance helps to assure not only their own success, but their entire unit’s as well.*

**Features:**

*   [3rd Level - Tactical Commander](#3rd_Level_-_Tactical_Commander)
*   [3rd Level - Unbreakable Formation](#3rd_Level_-_Unbreakable_Formation)
*   [7th Level - Tactical Authority](#7th_Level_-_Tactical_Authority)
*   [13th Level - Perfect Block](#13th_Level_-_Perfect_Block)
*   [18th Level - Battle Tide](#18th_Level_-_Battle_Tide)

## 3rd Level - Tactical Commander

When you select this specialization at 3rd level, you gain access to your cohesion die, which is a d4. Other features will refer to your cohesion die, and will allow you to roll it. Your cohesion die is not expended when rolled.

Your cohesion die increases to a d6 at 7th level, to a d8 at 13th, and to a d10 at 18th.

## 3rd Level - Unbreakable Formation

Additionally at 3rd level, you learn to weave your own defenses into those around you, resulting in a stronger whole for both you and your allies. Each creature of your choice within 10 feet of you gains a bonus to saving throws equal to one roll of your cohesion die. Additionally, creatures of your choice that start their turn within that range gain a +1 bonus to their armor class for each creature within 10 feet of you, with a maximum bonus equal to half your proficiency bonus.

## 7th Level - Tactical Authority

At 7th level, you gain the ability to have greater control over how a fight will play out. When you enter your defensive stance, choose either the dodge, disengage, or dash action. Creatures of your choice within 5 feet of you while your stance is active will be unable to take the chosen action. You may select a new action from the specified list at the start of each of your turns until your stance ends (no action required).

Additionally, creatures of your choice within the same range (other than you) gain a bonus to damage rolls equal to a roll of your cohesion die. They can use this bonus no more than once per turn.

## 13th Level - Perfect Block

Your ability to use shields extends to protecting your allies as well as you beginning at 13th level. When an ally within 5 feet of you is hit by an attack, you can use your reaction to give them a bonus to their armor class equal to one roll of your cohesion die. If this bonus causes the attack to miss, you may use this reaction again, even if you have already used your reaction this round.

## 18th Level - Battle Tide

When you reach 18th level, your ability to shift the tide of battle with your presence alone has been realized. As an action, you can grant each creature of your choice within 20 feet of you temporary hit points equal to twice your Bulwark level, and remove all conditions of your choice from them. Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

Phantom Coach

# Phantom Coach

*4th-level illusion. (Wizard (Legacy), Wizard, The Undead (VRGtR))*

## **Meta**

**Casting Time:** 1 minute (ritual)

**Range:** 30 feet

**Components:** V, S

**Duration:** 1 hour

## Description

A Huge, quasi-real, horse-drawn coach appears on the ground in an unoccupied space of your choice within range. You decide the creature's and coach's appearance, and it is equipped with the necessary gear to pull the carriage. Any of the equipment created by the spell vanishes in a puff of smoke if it is carried more than 10 feet away from the steed or coach.

For the duration, you and up to 4 other creatures you choose can ride in the coach. The steeds uses the Riding Horse stat block, except it has a Speed of 100 feet and can travel 11 miles in an hour. When the spell ends, the steeds and coach gradually fades, giving the riders 1 minute to dismount. The spell ends early if the steed takes any damage.

Phial Of Petulant Peculiarities

# Phial of Petulant Peculiarities

*Potion, Uncommon*

**Description**

For a period of 2d4 hours after you drink this potion, any mundane inanimate object you name will materialize in your hand for 1-minute as a sentient, uncooperative version of itself. This materialized sentient object will be capable of speaking any languages you are proficient in. This potion takes the form of an indigo-colored, effervescent liquid that pours like water.

Philosophy Of Diligence

# Philosophy of Diligence

*A sage who focuses on refining their own mind to fully control the magic they wield. Such arcanists hold themselves to particularly high degrees of perfection, allowing little room for failure even among the most basic of tasks. Their work in achieving this perfection however is reflected in full by their arcane mastery.*

## Quality Spellcasting

Meticulous work has gone into perfecting your spellcasting. When you cast a spell of 1st level or higher that deals damage, you may treat a roll of 1 on any damage die as a 2.

## Empowered Will

Through your constant work, your mental fortitude is nigh unbreakable. At 6th level, you gain proficiency in Charisma saving throws.

## Shaper of Willpower

At 10th level, you can further shape your spells to reflect your diligence. When you cast a focused spell, it ignores a target’s resistance to damage.

You also gain the following Mind Shape, which can be placed on your focused spells as normal:

### Willful

When you roll damage with the spell, you can treat a dice roll of one below its maximum amount as its maximum amount.

## Invariable Effect

At 14th level, control over your spells has given you a large degree of control over their success. When you cast a spell that requires you to make a check using your spellcasting ability, you can treat any d20 roll of 9 or lower as a 10.

Additionally, your Quality Spellcasting feature now treats a roll of 1 or 2 on any damage die as a 3.

## Absolution

At 18th level, your mastery over your very mind has granted you the power to exert your will on others to a greater, and invariable effect. When a creature succeeds on a saving throw against one of your focused spells, you can choose to make them fail instead. Alternatively, if you miss on an attack roll with a focused spell, you can cause the attack to hit instead. Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

Philosophy Of Evolution

# Philosophy of Evolution

Sages who follow this philosophy, often called “Blue Mages” due to the color of the robes many traditionally wear, embody the ever-flowing state of the world and others, taking that change into themselves and granting them the uncanny ability to adapt their very beings into something that can at times be completely unrecognizable.

**Class features:**

*   [2nd Level - Bonus Proficiencies](#2nd_Level_-_Bonus_Proficiencies)
*   [2nd Level - Azure Lores](#2nd_Level_-_Azure_Lore)
*   [6th Level - Flesh Writer](#6th_Level_-_Flesh_Writer)
*   [10th Level - Shaper of Evolution](#10th_Level_-_Shaper_of_Evolution)
*   [14th Level - Hardened Study](#14th_Level_-_Hardened_Study)
*   [18th Level - Ever-Evolving](#18th_Level_-_Ever-Evolving)

## **2nd Level - Bonus Proficiencies**

When you select this study at 2nd level, you gain proficiency with longswords and scimitars, as well as the survival and nature skills. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any check you make that uses either of those skills.

## **2nd Level - Azure Lore**

Additionally at 2nd level, you begin to uncover the ability to fundamentally change yourself in order to accommodate your continually evolving magic, starting with the ability to take the abilities of the monsters you face, using them as your own.

When you are targeted by a creature’s ability that does not originate from a weapon attack, spell, legendary action, or lair action, you can attempt to tap into a deeper understanding of it, and combine it with yourself. If you do so, any attack roll that is part of the ability automatically hits you, and any saving throw is automatically failed. Immediately after the effects are resolved, you may make a wisdom saving throw (DC 8 + the creature’s proficiency bonus + the creature’s constitution modifier). On a success, you may add the ability to your collection of Azure Lore, unless the creature’s challenge rating is higher than your sage level. You may only learn one Azure Lore ability per individual creature.

Whenever you use your Meditation feature to replace a spell that you know, you may replace it with one of your Azure Lore abilities. You can cast the ability as a spell, expending a spell slot of a level determined by the challenge rating of the creature. You automatically have any cantrip-level ability you gain through this feature available when you learn it. [Follow Link to view Table](#2nd_Level_-_Azure_Lore)


Challenge RatingSpell Slot Level
< 1Cantrip
1 - 31st
4 - 52nd
6 - 73rd
8 - 94th
10 - 115th
12 - 136th
14 - 157th
16 - 178th
18 - 209th


Casting the spell requires Somatic components, but not Verbal or Material, and has a casting time equal to the action it took the original creature. If the ability has a recharge condition, you must meet that condition before you are able to cast that Azure Lore ability again.

You may place mind shapes on your Azure Lore abilities as normal when you gain that feature at 3rd Level.

## **6th Level - Flesh Writer**

When you reach 6th level, you unlock the ability to tap into the abilities of the fallen and dead creatures you encounter. You may spend ten minutes while in contact with the corpse of a creature to attempt to absorb one of the abilities that it had in life. After the ritual has been completed, make a wisdom saving throw (DC 12 + the creature’s proficiency bonus + the creature’s constitution modifier). On a success, you may choose a single special trait from the creature to add to yourself. On a success or failure, you cannot use that corpse again to gain abilities through this feature.

The added trait cannot be Legendary Resistance or Limited Magic Immunity.

Once you add a trait to yourself, it cannot be removed by any means short of giving up the knowledge of the trait, which you can do as an action. You can maintain a number of these traits equal to your wisdom modifier. If you try to assimilate more traits than your maximum would allow, you may choose to give up one of your current traits for the new one.

## **10th Level - Shaper of Evolution**

At 10th level, your spells shift to better reflect the evolution that you yourself have undergone. When you cast any of your Azure Lore skills as Focused Spells, you may expend one spell slot level lower than you normally would need to (minimum of 1st level).

You also gain the following Mind Shape, which can be placed on your focused spells as normal:

**Evolving:** You can cast this spell as a reaction to a creature you can see within 60 feet of you ending their turn.

## **14th Level - Hardened Study**

When you reach 14th level, you no longer need to completely lower your defenses in order to intertwine your understanding of the abilities of others. When you attempt to learn an Azure Lore ability, attack rolls no longer automatically hit you, and you no longer automatically fail saving throws.

## **18th Level - Ever-Evolving**

Normal physical limits no longer apply to the abilities you learn from your Azure Lore feature when you reach 18th level. You are now able to copy natural weapons, and Legendary Actions that target you.

If you cast a creature’s Legendary Action as a spell, it takes an action to do so.

Philosophy Of Fallacy

# Philosophy of Fallacy

*There are ways of thinking that are often called incorrect by many, exhibiting illogical methods, or even simply being incorrect. However, certain Sages can utilize these ways of thinking in shaping their magic into something truly bizarre.*

## Bandwagon

The spells you cast carry the capacity to trick the very weave itself into allowing extra magic to be cast. When you cast a focused spell by expending a spell slot of first level or higher, the next time you or a creature within 30 feet of you casts that same spell at the same level before the start of your next turn, they do so without expending a spell slot, so long as the spell is of 5th level or lower.

## Straw Man

Your illogical reasoning extends far enough to prevent harm to you, though opening you to potentially greater damage.

Starting at 6th level, as a reaction when you take damage, you can teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within 30 feet of you, creating an illusory copy to take the damage instead. If you take damage from any source before the start of your next turn, you are vulnerable to that damage.

## Shaper of Delusion

At 10th level, you can further shape your spells to your own fallacy. When a creature casts one of your focused spells that benefits from your \[Bandwagon\] feature, that creature gets advantage on the next attack roll, ability check, or saving throw they make within the next minute.

You also gain the following Mind Shape, which can be placed on your focused spells as normal:

### Delusional

When you cast the spell, you can choose to have it not take effect until you cast your next spell.

## Red Herring

You learn to use your thoughts to distract those around you at 14th level. When a creature within 60 feet of you makes an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, you can use your reaction to impose disadvantage on the roll. If the creature fails their ability check, saving throw, or attack roll, it takes psychic damage equal to half your sage level. If it succeeds, you take the damage instead.

You can do so a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier, regaining all uses after you finish a long rest.

## Anti-Logic
