*   [15th level - Mental Dissonance](#15th_level_-_Mental_Dissonance)

## 3rd Level - Psionic Power

Starting at 3rd level, you harbor a wellspring of psionic energy within yourself. This energy is represented by your Psionic Energy dice, which are each a d6. You have a number of these dice equal to twice your proficiency bonus, and they fuel various psionic powers you have, which are detailed below.

Some of your powers expend the Psionic Energy die they use, as specified in a power's description, and you can't use a power if it requires you to use a die when your dice are all expended. You regain all your expended Psionic Energy dice when you finish a long rest. In addition, as a bonus action, you can regain one expended Psionic Energy die, but you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

When you reach certain levels in this class, the size of your Psionic Energy dice increases: at 5th level (d8), 11th level (d10), and 17th level (d12).

The powers below use your Psionic Energy dice.

**Psychic Mark**: Whenever you cast Hunters Mark, you can expend a Psionic Energy dice and modify it. When cast in this way, it requires no components and once per turn you can deal additional damage equal to one roll of your Psionic Energy Die when you hit with an attack.

**Psionic Fortitude**: When your nonpsionic training fails you, your psionic power can help: if you fail a saving throw with which you have proficiency, you can roll one Psionic Energy die, adding half the number rolled to the save, potentially turning failure into success. You expend the die only if the roll succeeds.

## 3rd Level - Psychic Spells

When you cast a spell from the Conjuration or Transmutation school, you can do so without Verbal or Somatic components.

## 7th Level - Empathic Awareness

Starting at 7th level, your psionics have deepened your awareness of the world around you. You can use your action to roll and expend one Psionic Energy die to open your mind to the emotional and mental resonance of your surroundings. For a number of hours equal to the number rolled, you can sense whether any of the following are present within 3 miles of you (or within 1 mile if you are underground), or have been present within a number of days equal to your Wisdom modifier:

*   **Intense thoughts or emotions** (fear, anger, grief, ecstasy, anxiety)
*   **Presence of Intelligent minds** (creatures with Intelligence 6 or higher)
*   **Psionic or magical phenomena** of significant power (DM’s discretion)

You learn the **direction** of each detected source within range, but not its exact distance or number.

Additionally, once per use of this feature, you can make a Wisdom (Survival) or Wisdom (Nature) check, with a DC equaling your spell save DC. On a success you learn the distance to one significant location or creature from one of the options above, regardless of distance. If you succeed the check by 5 or more, you learn the distance to a **specific** location or creature (DM’s discretion), provided you have seen or heard of them before.

Once you use this feature, you can’t do so again until you finish a short or long rest, unless you expend an additional Psionic Energy die to use it again.

## 11th Level - Cognitive Bypass

When you move, you can temporarily blur the distinction between your body and your psychic echo. When you use the Tireless feature, you can roll and expend up to two Psionic Energy dice and add the number rolled to the Temporary Hit Points gained.

Additionally, you become **partially incorporeal** granting the following benefits for up to 1 minute, or until you are incapacitated:

*   You can move through creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain.
*   The first time you enter a creature's space on a turn, that creature takes **force** damage equal to one roll of your Psionic Energy dice. Additionally, if you end your turn inside a creature or object, you and that target both take the **same amount** again in force damage.
*   When you hit a creature you have passed through with a melee weapon attack, you do not provoke opportunity attacks for leaving their reach.

## **15th level - Mental Dissonance**

Your mastery of psionics lets you unleash waves of psychic intention that collapses thought and will.

**Psychic Feedback**: Hostile creatures that start their turns within 30ft of you take take Psychic damage equal to your Wisdom modifier if you have attacked or dealt damage to them within the last turn.

**Intellect Spike**: Additionally, as an action you can target a number of creatures within 30 feet of you equal to half your Wisdom modifier. Creatures targeted in this way must make an Intelligence saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Wisdom modifier), taking psychic damage equal to your Wisdom modifier plus two rolls of your Psionic Energy dice and is incapacitated for up to 1 minute. On a successful save they take half damage and their speed is instead halved until the end of their next turn.

At the start of each of their turns while incapacitated, the damage they take from Psychic Feedback is doubled. They can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of their turns, ending on a successful save.

Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a short or long rest, unless you expend two Psionic Energy dice to use it again.

Pyromancy

# Pyromancy

## Heart of Fire

At 1st level, whenever you start casting a spell that deals fire damage, fiery magic erupts from you. This eruption causes creatures of your choice that you can see within 10 feet of you to take fire damage equal to half your sorcerer level (minimum of 1).

## Fire in the Veins

At 6th level, you gain resistance to fire damage. In addition, spells you cast and effects you create ignore resistance to fire damage.

## Pyromancer's Fury

Starting at 14th level, when you are hit by a melee attack, you can use your reaction to deal fire damage to the attacker. The damage equals your sorcerer level, and ignores resistance to fire damage.

## Fiery Soul

At 18th level, you gain immunity to fire damage. In addition, you ignore resistance to fire damage and treat immunity to fire damage as resistance to fire damage. When treating immunity as resistance, features or feats that allow you to ignore resistance do not do so.

Quicksilver Clay

# Quicksilver Clay

*Wondrous Item, common*

**Description**

This small orb of silver, watery liquid is strangely dry to the touch. While holding the 1-inch-diameter metallic sphere in the palm of your hand, you can mentally control the form that it takes using an action. The form stretches and turns into the imagined shape and remains centered in your hand. Regardless of the form, it can be no larger than the palm of your hand and is always a liquid. The form returns to its natural orb state when you end your focus on the quicksilver clay (no action required) or it leaves your hand.

Eating or drinking the liquid is harmless, but takes 1d4 days to exit the body.

Rakshasa'S Claw

# Rakshasa's Claw

*3rd-level evocation. (Sorcerer, Warlock, Wizard)*

**Meta**

**Casting Time**: 1 action

**Range**: 30 feet

**Components**: V, S

**Duration**: Instantaneous

**Description**

Channeling the vile and scheming nature of the Rakshasa, you evoke by splaying three fingers apart and making a downward vertical slash in the air. It can also be done with a horizontal swipe to deal less damage to more targets at once. A target you choose within range must make a Dexterity save or suffer 6d8 magical slashing damage. You can alternatively choose to target any creature in a 20-foot cube originating from yourself, dealing 3d8 magical slashing damage to each target. In either case, on a successful save, each target takes half as much magical slashing damage.

**At Higher Levels**

When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the damage increases by 1d8 for both single-target attacks and multi-target attacks for each slot level above 3rd.

Ranger (Version X)

# Ranger (Version X)

## **The Ranger Class**

Rough and wild looking, a human stalks alone through the shadows of trees, hunting the orcs he knows are planning a raid on a nearby farm. Clutching a shortsword in each hand, he becomes a whirlwind of steel, cutting down one enemy after another.

After tumbling away from a cone of freezing air, an elf finds her feet and draws back her bow to loose an arrow at the white dragon. Shrugging off the wave of fear that emanates from the dragon like the cold of its breath, she sends one arrow after another to find the gaps between the dragon’s thick scales.

Holding his hand high, a half-elf whistles to the hawk that circles high above him, calling the bird back to his side. Whispering instructions in Elvish, he points to the owlbear he’s been tracking and sends the hawk to distract the creature while he readies his bow.

Far from the bustle of cities and towns, past the hedges that shelter the most distant farms from the terrors of the wild, amid the dense-packed trees of trackless forests and across wide and empty plains, rangers keep their unending watch.

### Deadly Hunters

Warriors of the wilderness, rangers specialize in hunting the monsters that threaten the edges of civilization—humanoid raiders, rampaging beasts and monstrosities, terrible giants, and deadly dragons. They learn to track their quarry as a predator does, moving stealthily through the wilds and hiding themselves in brush and rubble. Rangers focus their combat training on techniques that are particularly useful against their specific favored foes.

Thanks to their familiarity with the wilds, rangers acquire the ability to cast spells that harness nature’s power, much as a druid does. Their spells, like their combat abilities, emphasize speed, stealth, and the hunt. A ranger’s talents and abilities are honed with deadly focus on the grim task of protecting the borderlands.

### Independent Adventurers

Though a ranger might make a living as a hunter, a guide, or a tracker, a ranger’s true calling is to defend the outskirts of civilization from the ravages of monsters that press in from the wild. In some places, rangers gather in secretive orders or join forces with druidic circles. Many rangers, though, are independent almost to a fault, knowing that, when a dragon or a band of orcs attacks, a ranger might be the first—and possibly the last—line of defense.

This fierce independence makes rangers well suited to adventuring, since they are accustomed to life far from the comforts of a dry bed and a hot bath. Faced with city-bred adventurers who grouse and whine about the hardships of the wild, rangers respond with some mixture of amusement, frustration, and compassion. But they quickly learn that other adventurers who can carry their own weight in a fight against monstrous foes are worth any extra burden. Coddled city folk might not know how to feed themselves or find fresh water in the wild, but they make up for it in other ways.

### Creating a Ranger

As you create your ranger character, consider the nature of the training that gave you your particular capabilities. Did you train with a single mentor, wandering the wilds together until you mastered the ranger’s ways? Did you leave your apprenticeship, or was your mentor slain—perhaps by the same kind of monster that became your favored enemy? Or perhaps you learned your skills as part of a band of rangers affiliated with a druidic circle, trained in mystic paths as well as wilderness lore. You might be self-taught, a recluse who learned combat skills, tracking, and even a magical connection to nature through the necessity of surviving in the wilds.

What’s the source of your particular hatred of a certain kind of enemy? Did a monster kill someone you loved or destroy your home village? Or did you see too much of the destruction these monsters cause and commit yourself to reining in their depredations? Is your adventuring career a continuation of your work in protecting the borderlands, or a significant change? What made you join up with a band of adventurers? Do you find it challenging to teach new allies the ways of the wild, or do you welcome the relief from solitude that they offer?

## Ranger Table

| Level | Proficiency
Bonus

 | Features | Spells

Known

 | —Spell Slots per Spell Level— |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1st | 2nd | 3rd | 4th | 5th |
| 1st | +2 | Favored Foe, Natural Explorer, Warden Specialization | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| 2nd | +2 | Fighting Style, Spellcasting | 2 | 2 | — | — | — | — |
| 3rd | +2 | Ranger Archetype, Primeval Awareness | 3 | 3 | — | — | — | — |
| 4th | +2 | Ability Score Improvement | 3 | 3 | — | — | — | — |
| 5th | +3 | Extra Attack | 4 | 4 | 2 | — | — | — |
| 6th | +3 | Vigilant Hunter, Natural Explorer Improvement | 4 | 4 | 2 | — | — | — |
| 7th | +3 | Ranger Archetype Feature | 5 | 4 | 3 | — | — | — |
| 8th | +3 | Ability Score Improvement, Land’s Stride | 5 | 4 | 3 | — | — | — |
| 9th | +4 | Warden’s Covenant | 6 | 4 | 3 | 2 | — | — |
| 10th | +4 | Swift Hunter, Natural Explorer Improvement | 6 | 4 | 3 | 2 | — | — |
| 11th | +4 | Ranger Archetype Feature | 7 | 4 | 3 | 3 | — | — |
| 12th | +4 | Ability Score Improvement | 7 | 4 | 3 | 3 | — | — |
| 13th | +5 | Nature’s Veil | 8 | 4 | 3 | 3 | 1 | — |
| 14th | +5 | Warden’s Pinnacle | 8 | 4 | 3 | 3 | 1 | — |
| 15th | +5 | Ranger Archetype Feature | 9 | 4 | 3 | 3 | 2 | — |
| 16th | +5 | Ability Score Improvement | 9 | 4 | 3 | 3 | 2 | — |
| 17th | +6 | — | 10 | 4 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 1 |
| 18th | +6 | Peerless Hunter | 10 | 4 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 1 |
| 19th | +6 | Ability Score Improvement | 11 | 4 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 2 |
| 20th | +6 | Foe Slayer | 11 | 4 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 2 |

## **Class Features**

As a ranger, you gain the following class features

**Hit Points**

**Hit Dice:** 1d10 per ranger level

**Hit Points at 1st Level:** 10 + your Constitution modifier

**Hit Points at Higher Levels:** 1d10 (or 6) + your constitution modifier per ranger level after 1st

**Proficiencies**

**Armor:** Light, Medium, Shields

**Weapons:** Simple and Martial Weapons

**Tools:** Cartographer’s Tools, Herbalism Kit

**Saving Throws:** Strength, Dexterity

**Skills:** Choose three from Animal Handling, Athletics, Insight, Investigation, Nature, Perception, Stealth, and Survival

### **Favored Foe**

You have become adept at singling out enemies during combat, and may select one during each fight to increase your potency against.

When you roll initiative, you may choose a creature that you can see to be designated as your Favored Foe, and you gain the following benefits:

*   You gain a bonus to attack rolls against your Favored Foe equal to +2.
*   Your Favored Foe always provokes opportunity attacks from you, even if they use an action or other special feature to prevent it.
*   If your Favored Foe flees from combat, you have advantage on any check you make to locate it again

If you already have a Favored Foe when you roll initiative, you can choose to keep it, without choosing a new one. Otherwise, you lose the benefits provided to you against your old Favored Foe.

### **Natural Explorer**

You are particularly familiar with one type of environment and are adept at traveling and surviving in such regions. Choose one type of favored terrain: arctic, coast, desert, forest, grassland, mountain, swamp, or the Underdark. When you make an Intelligence or Wisdom check related to your favored terrain, your proficiency bonus is doubled if you are using a skill that you're proficient in.

While traveling for an hour or more in your favored terrain, you gain the following benefits:

*   Difficult terrain doesn't slow your group's travel.
*   Your group can't become lost, except by magical means.
*   Even when you are engaged in another activity while traveling (such as foraging, navigating, or tracking), you remain alert to danger.
*   If you are travelling alone, you can move stealthily at a normal pace.
*   When you forage, you find twice as much food as you normally would.
*   While tracking other creatures, you also learn their exact number, their sizes, and how long ago they passed through the area.

You choose additional favored terrain types at 6th and 10th level.

### **Warden Specialization**

When you become a Ranger at 1st level, choose one of the following specializations to guide your playstyle.

**Iron Warden:** You gain proficiency with Heavy Armor

**Earthen Warden:** You can take the Hide action even if you are only lightly obscured.

**Nature’s Warden:** You learn two cantrips of your choice from the Druid spell list.

### **Fighting Style**

At 2nd Level, you adopt a particular style of fighting as your specialty. CHoose one of the following options.

You can’t take a Fighting Style option more than once, even if you later get to choose again.

**Archery:** You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls you make with ranged weapons.

**Blind Fighting:** You have blindsight with a range of 10 feet. Within that range, you can effectively see anything that isn’t behind total cover, even if you’re blinded or in darkness. Moreover, you can see an invisible creature within that range, unless the creature successfully hides from you.

**Defense:** While you are wearing armor, you gain a +1 bonus to AC.

**Dueling:** When you are wielding a melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons, you gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls with that weapon.

**Thrown Weapon Fighting:** You can draw a weapon that has the thrown property as part of the attack you make with the weapon.

In addition, when you hit with a ranged attack using a thrown weapon, you gain a +2 bonus to the damage roll.

**Two-Weapon Fighting:** When you engage in two-weapon fighting, you can add your ability modifier to the damage of the second attack.

### **Spellcasting**

By the time you reach 2nd level, you have learned to use the magical essence of nature to cast spells. See Spell Rules for general rules of spellcasting and the bottom of this document for the ranger spell list.

**Spell Slots:** The Ranger table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your ranger spells of 1st level and higher. To cast one of these spells, you must expend a slot of the spell’s level or higher. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a long rest.

For example, if you know the 1st-level spell animal friendship and have a 1st-level and a 2nd-level spell slot available, you can cast animal friendship using either slot.

**Spells Known of 1st Level and Higher:** You know two 1st-level spells of your choice from the ranger spell list.

The Spells Known column of the Ranger table shows when you learn more ranger spells of your choice. Each of these spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. For instance, when you reach 5th level in this class, you can learn one new spell of 1st or 2nd level.

Additionally, when you gain a level in this class, you can choose one of the ranger spells you know and replace it with another spell from the ranger spell list, which also must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

**Spellcasting Ability:** Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for your ranger spells, since your magic draws on your attunement to nature. You use your Wisdom whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability. In addition, you use your Wisdom modifier when setting the saving throw DC for a ranger spell you cast and when making an attack roll with one.

**Spell Save DC** = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Wisdom modifier

**Spell attack modifier** = your proficiency bonus + your Wisdom Modifier

### **Ranger Archetype**

At 3rd level, you choose an archetype that you strive to emulate. Your choice grants you features at 3rd level, and again at 7th, 11th, and 15th level.

### **Primeval Awareness**

Additionally at 3rd level, you can open your mind to the presence of certain types of creatures. As an action, you may choose from aberrations, celestials, dragons, elementals, fey, fiends, and undead. For one minute, you know if any creatures of that type are within 5 miles of you, and the distance to the closest one. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your wisdom modifier before finishing a long rest.

### **Ability Score Improvement**

When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can’t increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

Using the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

### **Extra Attack**

Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, when you take the attack action on your turn.

### **Vigilant Hunter**

When you reach 6th level, your ability to hunt your quarry reaches new heights. Your ranged attacks ignore half cover, or three-quarters cover if the target is your Favored Foe. Additionally, your melee attacks against your Favored Foe cannot have disadvantage.

### **Land’s Stride**

Starting at 8th level, you are no longer slowed by difficult terrain. Additionally, non-magical plants cannot slow your speed or harm you as you travel through them.

You have advantage on any saving throw against plants that are magically created or manipulated to slow or damage you.

### **Warden’s Covenant**

Your connection to your Warden specialization deepens when you reach 9th level, granting you further benefits based on your decision at 2nd level.

**Iron Warden:** You gain a bonus to Strength (Athletics) and Dexterity (Acrobatics) checks equal to your wisdom modifier.

**Earthen Warden:** You gain a bonus to Dexterity (Stealth) and Wisdom (Perception) checks equal to your wisdom modifier.

**Nature’s Warden:** You gain a bonus to Intelligence (Arcana) and Intelligence (Nature) checks equal to your wisdom modifier.

### **Swift Hunter**

Starting at 10th level, you gain a supernatural quickness while hunting your Favored Foe. While your Favored Foe lives, and you are in combat with it, your walking speed increases by 10 feet.

Additionally, the bonus to attack rolls granted by your Favored Foe increases to be equal to your wisdom modifier (minimum bonus of +2)

### **Nature’s Veil**

When you reach 13th level, you gain the ability to magically conceal yourself. As a bonus action, you can cause yourself, and all equipment you are wearing or carrying, to turn invisible until the end of your next turn.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

### **Warden’s Pinnacle**

At 14th level, the specialization you selected at 1st level grants you further benefits.

**Iron Warden:** When you take the attack action on your turn, you can make three attacks, instead of two.

**Earthen Warden:** You gain blindsight with a range of 60 feet, and tremorsense with a range of 30 feet.

**Nature’s Warden:** You learn one spell from the druid spell list of 6th level of your choice. This spell counts as a ranger spell for you. You can cast this spell once without expending a spell slot, regaining the ability to do so when you finish a long rest.

### **Peerless Hunter**

At 18th level, you have advantage on all attack rolls made against your Favored Foe, and it has disadvantage on all attack rolls against you.

### **Foe Slayer**

When you reach 20th level, being your Favored Foe is nothing short of a death sentence. Add your wisdom modifier to all damage rolls you make against your Favored Foe, weapon and spell alike.

Ranger: Natural Explorer

# Ranger: Natural Explorer

Optional (Replaces Natural Explorer)

You are particularly familiar with one type of natural environment and are adept at traveling and surviving in such regions. Choose one type of favored terrain: arctic, coast, desert, forest, grassland, mountain, swamp, ,Underdark, Hills, Underwater, or Urban. When you make an Intelligence or Wisdom check related to your favored terrain, your proficiency bonus is doubled if you are using a skill that you're proficient in.

While traveling for an hour or more in your favored terrain, you gain the following benefits:

*   Difficult terrain doesn't slow your group's travel.
*   Your group can't become lost except by magical means.
*   Even when you are engaged in another activity while traveling (such as foraging, navigating, or tracking), you remain alert to danger.
*   If you are traveling alone, you can move stealthily at a normal pace.
*   When you forage, you find twice as much food as you normally would.
*   While tracking other creatures, you also learn their exact number, their sizes, and how long ago they passed through the area.

You choose additional favored terrain types at 6th and 10th level.

Raven'S Claw Crossbow

# Raven's Claw Crossbow

*Weapon (crossbow, heavy), rare*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

With a bonus action you can deploy or retract the stand system.

When the stand is deployed you gain advantage on your attack rolls made by this weapon. But as a result you can't make any movement, or you lose the bonus.

Raven Knight

# Raven Knight

*A Raven Knight is fearsome in battle, fusing martial prowess with strange abilities gifted by the Raven Queen. Many Raven Knights work alone, though some may find a small band of companions to aid them in battle, and some may even command and lead entire armies into battle. Masters of death, Raven Knights seek to protect those who cannot protect themselves from undeath and the afterlife. Powerful Raven Knights may even become champions of the Raven Queen, and thus champions of the battlefield.*

## Knowledge of Death

When you choose this Archetype at 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the Investigation and Medicine skills. Additionally, you can make Intelligence (Investigation) and Wisdom (Medicine) checks with advantage when attempting to determine the cause of death of a creature.

## Soul Severing

At 3rd level, when you reduce a creature to 0 hit points you sever the connection of the creatures soul to its body. That creature cannot be turned into an undead. Additionally, as a reaction, when a creature is reduced to 0 hit points within 15ft of you you can empower your next attack with that creatures soul energy. Your next weapon attack deals additional necrotic damage equal to half your fighter level.

## Feather Step

At 7th level, you move with the grace of the raven. Your walking speed increases by 10ft. Additionally, as a bonus action you gain a flight speed equal to your walking speed until the end of your turn, if you end your turn in midair you drop to the ground.

## Queens Blessing

At 10th level, the Raven Queen grants you a blessing. You gain advantage on death saving throws, immunity to the frightened condition, and resistance to necrotic damage.

## Deaths Embrace

At 15th level, when you or an ally critically hit a creature, you can use your reaction to add 3d10 necrotic damage to the attacks damage roll.

## Duty in Death

At 18th level, when you are reduced to 0 hit points or killed outright you can use your reaction to make a weapon attack against the creature that killed you. On a hit you critically hit that creature and regain hit points equal to the damage dealt. You can do this once per long rest.

Reaper’S Kusarigama

# Reaper’s Kusarigama

*Weapon (Kusarigama), very rare*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

Based on the Scythe of Reaping, this Kusarigama is adorned by grotesque engravings of tragedy, and has an obsidian lining running the length of the kusari blade, along with depictions of mass graves on the kama. Within crafting, death of any living creature must take place, each being slain with one section of the weapon before the magic may be imbued within it.

Once per turn when you hit a creature with this Kusarigama, it gains a stack of reap, which lasts until its next short or long rest. Once per turn, this weapon deals an additional 1d6 necrotic damage for every stack of reap the target has. A target may have a maximum of 5 stacks of reap. A target looses all stacks of reap if it is targeted by greater restoration, heal, or a similar restorative magic.

When a target with at least 1 stack of reap is reduced to zero hit points with this weapon, you gain 5 temporary hit points for each stack of reap the target had.

Reassemble

# Reassemble

**Prerequisite**: Skeleton

*   Your Constitution Score increases by 1.
*   The necromancy that stitched you together is shockingly good at keeping you intact. When you become stabilized as a result of succeeding three death saving throws, you regain hit points equal to your level.

Red Powder

# Red Powder

*Flask - Adventuring Gear - 250 gp, 2 lbs.*

Also referred to as thermite in other settings, red powder is a volatile mixture of iron and aluminum powders. It burns rapidly and at extremely high temperatures, though it is not an explosive.

A single flask can be emptied and contains enough powder to cover one square foot. It can then be ignited, where it burns for 1 round, shedding bright light in a 30 foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet. The powder can burn or melt away a single tiny non-magical item that it remains in contact with while burning.

As an action, you can recollect or brush away red powder that is yet to be ignited. Once ignited, the powder sticks to the surface and can no longer be removed until it burns away.

A creature that touches red powder, or starts its turn touching red powder, while it is burning takes 4d8 fire damage, that ignores a target's resistance to fire damage.

Reflection

# Reflection

*6th-level abjuration. (Bard, Cleric, Sorcerer, Wizard)*

**Meta**

**Casting Time:** 1 bonus action

**Range**: Self

**Components**: V, S, M (a small sprinkling of powdered silver)

**Duration**: 10 minutes

**Description**

You create a reflective magical barrier that turns harmful attacks back upon their attackers. For the duration, when a creature hits you with an attack, it takes 2d8 radiant damage.

Additionally, if you fail a saving throw against a spell cast by a creature you can see, they take 1d8 radiant damage + an extra 1d8 for each spell slot level of the spell cast.

Revenant

# Revenant

**Prerequisite**

Elf

**Description**

You are descended from a master of the double-bladed scimitar, and some of that mastery has passed on to you. You gain the following benefits:

*   Increase your Dexterity or Strength score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
*   While you are holding a double-bladed scimitar with two hands, you gain a +1 bonus to Armor Class.
*   A double-bladed scimitar has the finesse property when you wield it.

Revised Arcane Archer

# Revised Arcane Archer

## Arcane Archer Lore

At 3rd level, you learn magical theory or some of the secrets of nature -- typical for practitioners of this elven martial tradition. You choose to gain proficiency in either the Arcana or the Nature skill, and you choose to learn either the prestidigitation or the druidcraft cantrip.

## Arcane Shot

At 3rd level, you learn to unleash special magical effects with some of your shots. When you gain this feature, you learn two Arcane Shot options of your choice (see Arcane Shot Options below).

Once per turn when you fire an arrow or bolt from any bow as part of the Attack action, you can apply one of your Arcane Shot options to that piece of ammunition. You decide to use the option when the ammunition hits a creature, unless the option doesn't involve an attack roll. You have a number of uses equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses of it when you finish a short or long rest.

You gain an additional Arcane Shot option of your choice when you reach certain levels in this class: 7th, 10th, 15th, and 18th level. Each option also improves when you become a 15th-level fighter.

### Arcane Shot Options (Revised)

The Arcane Shot feature lets you choose options for it at certain levels. The options are presented here in alphabetical order. They are all magical effects, and each one is associated with one of the schools of magic.

If an option requires a saving throw, your Arcane Shot save DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Intelligence modifier.

#### Banishing Arrow

You use abjuration magic to try to temporarily banish your target to a harmless location in the Feywild. The creature hit by the arrow must also succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be banished for 1 minute, or until you release the magic as a free action. While banished in this way, its speed is 0, and it is incapacitated. The affected creature can make a Charisma saving throw at the end of their turn, ending the effect on a success. At the end of the duration, the target reappears in the space it vacated or in the nearest unoccupied space if that space is occupied.

After you reach 15th level in this class, a target also takes 4d6 force damage when the arrow hits it.

#### Beguiling Arrow

Your enchantment magic causes this arrow to temporarily beguile its target. The creature hit by the arrow takes an extra 2d6 psychic damage, and choose one of your allies within 30 feet of the target. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw, or it is charmed by the chosen ally for 1 minute or until you release the magic as a free action. The target or any creature that can reach it can use its action to remove the arrow with a successful Strength (Athletics) check against your Arcane Shot save DC, ending the effect on a success. Additionally, this effect ends early if the chosen ally attacks the charmed target, deals damage to it, or forces it to make a saving throw.

The psychic damage increases to 4d6 when you reach 15th level in this class.

#### Bursting Arrow

You imbue your arrow with force energy drawn from the school of evocation. The arrow detonates after your attack. Immediately after the arrow hits the creature, the target and all other creatures within 10 feet of it take 2d6 force damage each and succeed on a Strength saving throw against your Arcane Shot save DC, or be knocked prone.

The force damage increases to 4d6 when you reach 15th level in this class. (edited)

#### Enfeebling Arrow

You weave necromantic magic into your arrow. The creature hit by the arrow takes an extra 2d6 necrotic damage on hit, as well as at the start of each of their turns for 1 minute or until you release the magic as a free action. The target must also succeed on a Constitution saving throw, or the damage dealt by its weapon attacks is halved until the start of your next turn. The target or any creature that can reach it can use its action to remove the arrow with a successful Strength (Athletics) check against your Arcane Shot save DC, ending the effect on a success.

The necrotic damage increases to 4d6 when you reach 15th level in this class.

#### Grasping Arrow

When this arrow strikes its target, conjuration magic creates grasping, poisonous brambles, which wrap around the target. The creature hit by the arrow takes an extra 2d6 poison damage, its speed is reduced by 10 feet, and it takes 2d6 slashing damage the first time on each turn it moves 1 foot or more without teleporting. The target or any creature that can reach it can use its action to remove the brambles with a successful Strength (Athletics) check against your Arcane Shot save DC. The brambles last for 1 minute, until you use another option, or until you release the magic as a free action.

The poison damage and slashing damage both increase to 4d6 when you reach 15th level in this class.

#### Piercing Arrow

You use transmutation magic to give your arrow an ethereal quality. When you use this option, you don’t make an attack roll for the attack. Instead, the arrow fires forward in a line, which is 5 foot wide and 30 feet long, before disappearing. The arrow passes harmlessly through objects, ignoring cover. Each creature in that line must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes damage as if it were hit by the arrow, plus an extra 2d6 piercing damage. On a successful save, a target takes half as much damage. This option lasts for 1 minute, until you use another option, or until you release the magic as a free action.

The piercing damage increases to 4d6 when you reach 15th level in this class.

#### Seeking Arrow

Using divination magic, you grant your arrow the ability to seek out your target, allowing the arrow to curve and twist its path in search of its prey. When you use this option, you don’t make an attack roll for the attack. Instead, choose one creature you have seen in the past minute. The arrow flies toward that creature, moving around corners if necessary and ignoring three-quarters cover and half cover. If the target is within the weapon’s range and there is a path large enough for the arrow to travel to the target, the target must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, it takes damage as if it were hit by the arrow, plus an extra 2d6 force damage, and you learn the target’s current location and distance for 1 hour or until you release the magic as a free action. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage, and you only learn the targets current location and distance for one round. The target or any creature that can reach it can use its action to remove the arrow with a successful Strength (Athletics) check against your Arcane Shot save DC, ending the effect on a success.

The force damage increases to 4d6 when you reach 15th level in this class. (edited)

#### Shadow Arrow

You weave illusion magic into your arrow, causing it to occlude your foe’s vision with shadows. The creature hit by the arrow takes an extra 2d6 psychic damage, and it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be unable to see anything farther than 5 feet away for 1 minute or until you release the magic as a free action. The target or any creature that can reach it can use its action to remove the arrow with a successful Strength (Athletics) check against your Arcane Shot save DC, ending the effect on a success.

The psychic damage increases to 4d6 when you reach 15th level in this class.

## Magic Ammunition

At 7th level, you gain the ability to infuse ammunition with magic. Whenever you fire a nonmagical arrow from a bow, you can make it magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage. The magic fades from the ammunition immediately after it hits or misses its target.

## Curving Shot

At 7th level, you learn how to direct an errant ammunition toward a new target. When you make an attack roll with a magic ammunition and miss, you can use a bonus action to reroll the attack roll against a different target within 60 feet of the original target.

## Quick-draw Shot

Starting at 15th level, your magical archery makes your movements swift. Whenever you hit a target with an Arcane Shot you can use your bonus action to make another ranged weapon attack against the same target using the same weapon.

## Eldritch Mastery

At 18th level, your mastery of blending magic with archery teaches you to infuse your arrows with even greater magic, as well as combining them for greater effect.

Once per attack action, you can infuse a single arrow with two different arcane shot options, excluding piercing shot, having the target be affected by and taking damage from both shots. However when doing this you expend two uses of your Arcane shot.

You have two uses of this ability, and you regain all expended uses of it when you finish a long rest.

Revised Path Of The Storm Herald

# Revised Path of the Storm Herald

## **Revised Path of the Storm Herald**:

*All barbarians harbor a fury within. Their rage grants them superior strength, durability, and speed. Barbarians who follow the Path of the Storm Herald learn to transform that rage into a mantle of primal magic, which swirls around them. When in a fury, a barbarian of this path taps into the forces of nature to create powerful magical effects.*

*Storm heralds are typically elite champions who train alongside druids, rangers, and others sworn to protect nature. Other storm heralds hone their craft in lodges in regions wracked by storms, in the frozen reaches at the world’s end, or deep in the hottest deserts.*

**Features:**

*   [3rd Level - Storm Aura](#3rd_Level_-_Storm_Aura)
*   [6th Level - Storm Soul](#6th_Level_-_Storm_Soul)
*   [10th Level - Shielding Storm](#10th_Level_-_Shielding_Storm)
*   [14th Level - Raging Storm](#14th_Level_-_Raging_Storm)

## 3rd Level - Storm Aura

Starting at 3rd level, you emanate a stormy, magical aura while you rage. The area within the aura is lightly obscured. The aura extends 10 feet from you in every direction, but not through total cover.

Your aura has an effect that activates when you enter your rage, and you can activate the effect again on each of your turns as a bonus action. Choose desert, sea, or tundra. Your aura’s effect depends on that chosen environment, as detailed below. You can change your environment choice whenever you rage.

If your aura’s effects require a saving throw, the DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution modifier.

**Desert.** When this effect is activated, creatures of your choice in your aura take 1d6 fire damage. The damage increases when you reach certain levels in this class, increasing to 2d6 at 5th level, 3d6 at 11th level, 4d6 at 17th level.

**Sea.** When this effect is activated, you can choose one other creature you can see in your aura to strike with lightning. The target must make a Strength saving throw. On a failure, the target takes 1d6 lightning damage on a failed save and is knocked prone. On a success, it takes half as much damage and isn’t knocked prone. The damage increases when you reach certain levels in this class, increasing to 2d6 at 5th level, 3d6 at 11th level, and 4d6 at 17th level.

**Tundra.** When this effect is activated, creatures of your choice in your aura must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, the creature’s speed is reduced to 0 until the end of your next turn, and the creature can’t take reaction for the duration.

## 6th Level - Storm Soul

At 6th level, the storm grants you benefits while your aura is active. The benefits are based on the environment you chose for your Storm Aura.

**Desert.** You gain resistance to fire damage. You can use an action to scatter sand in the area within your aura. Creatures within the area must make a Dexterity saving throw or be blinded until the end of your next turn.

**Sea.** You gain resistance to lightning damage, and you can breathe underwater. You can use an action to create a whirlpool around a creature you can see within your aura. It must make a Strength saving throw or be restrained until the end of your next turn.

**Tundra.** You gain resistance to cold damage. You can use an action to to grant temporary hit points to creatures of your choice in your aura equal to your barbarian level, as icy armor encases them.

## 10th Level - Shielding Storm

At 10th level, you learn to use your mastery of the storm to protect others. Each creature of your choice has the damage resistance you gained from the Storm Soul feature while the creature is in your Storm Aura.

## 14th Level - Raging Storm

At 14th level, the power of the storm you channel grows mightier, lashing out at your foes. The effect is based on the environment you chose for your Storm Aura.

**Desert.** Immediately after a creature in your aura hits you with an attack, you can use your reaction to force that creature to make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the creature catches fire; until someone takes an action to douse the fire, the creature takes fire damage equal to half your barbarian level at the end of each of its turns.

**Sea.** When you create a lighting strike with your Storm Aura or a whirlpool with your Storm Soul feature, you can target two creatures instead of one.

**Tundra.** The area in your aura is heavily obscured, except for yourself and creatures of your choice when you activate the aura.

Revised Purple Dragon Knight

# Revised Purple Dragon Knight

## Rallying Cry

When you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you learn how to inspire your allies to fight on past their injuries.

When you use your Second Wind feature, each creature of your choice within 15 feet of you regains hit points equal to your fighter level, provided that the creature can see or hear you.

## Royal Envoy

A Purple Dragon knight serves as an envoy of the Cormyrean crown. Knights of high standing are expected to conduct themselves with grace.

At 7th level, you gain proficiency in the Persuasion skill. If you are already proficient in it, you gain proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: Animal Handling, Insight, Intimidation, or Performance.

Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses Persuasion. You receive this benefit regardless of the skill proficiency you gain from this feature.

## Inspiring Surge

Starting at 10th level, when you use your Action Surge feature, you can choose an amount of creatures equal to half your fighter level (rounded down) within 15 feet of you. Those creatures can make one melee or ranged weapon attack with their reaction, provided that they can see or hear you.

Starting at 18th level, the range of your inspiration increases to 30 feet.

## Bulwark

Beginning at 15th level, you can extend the benefit of your Indomitable feature to your allies. When you decide to use Indomitable to reroll an Intelligence, a Wisdom, or a Charisma saving throw and you aren't incapacitated, you can choose an amount of creatures equal to half your proficiency bonus (rounded down) within 30 feet of you that also failed its saving throw against the same effect. If those creatures can see or hear you, they can reroll their saving throw and must use the new roll.

## Loyalty to the Crown

Starting at 18th level, when you target an ally with any of your bannerette features, that ally gains advantage on the next attack roll, ability check, or saving throw it makes until the end of its next turn.

Ring Of Cantrip Storing

# Ring of Cantrip Storing

*Ring, uncommon*

**Description**

This seemingly mundane ring can store a single cantrip. Only one of these rings can be worn at a time, if another Ring of Cantrip Storing is equipped both cease to function. The ring takes one turn to equip, and one turn to unequip. The cantrip, if used for a spell that requires a save, has a DC of 10, and if used for a attack roll receive no modifiers and is treated as a straight roll. If the spell is affected by the caster's level, such as eldritch blast, it is casted at its lowest level.

A cantrip of any class can be stored within the ring. To store a cantrip, one must be able to cast the cantrip themselves, or find someone else who knows the cantrip that can store it in the ring on their behalf through a 10 minute ritual.

Ring Of Emotional Suppression

# Ring of Emotional Suppression

*Ring, uncommon*

**Description**

This ring a golden band, with an amethyst gem and scribings in either Celestial, Draconic, or Sylvan. Activated by a spoken or mental command, the ring allows the wearer to either suppress a specific emotion, or emotion in general on themselves. The chosen effect lasts until cancelled. This does not confer the below effect. This effects takes a free action. Additionally, the ring can also be used to fight off fear and/or infatuation. When you make a saving throw to avoid or end the charmed and/or frightened conditions on yourself, you can give yourself advantage on the roll if you are wearing the ring. You must decide to do so before rolling the d20. If the save succeeds, you can't use the ring in this way until you finish a long rest. Only the wearer can receive the effects of this ring, and effects cannot stack with duplicate Rings of Emotional Suppression or Cuddly Strixhaven Mascots.

Ring Of Roses

# Ring of Roses

*Ring, common*

**Description**

As an action, you can cause a single rose to grow and appear in your hand. The stem is a foot long and has small, dull thorns. The rose blossoms in a color of your choice as it finishes growing, but withers and disintegrates after 24 hours.

You can cause a single rose to grow 5 times per day, which refreshes at dawn.

Ring Of Silverware

# Ring of Silverware

*Ring, Common*

**Description**

While wearing this ring, you can use a bonus action to mentally command the ring to transform into a fork, spoon, or other harmless element of a set of cook's utensils (such as a ladle or spatula). The transformed ring returns to its normal form on your finger if you mentally end the effect (no action required) or are no longer holding it.

Ring Of Triggered Spells

# Ring of Triggered Spells

*Wondrous Item, rare*

**Description**

This ring stores spells cast into it, holding them until the wearer uses them. The ring can store a single level 1 spell (Casted with a first level slot), but the casting time for this spell is automatically a reaction rather than an action or bonus action. Only one of these rings can be worn at a time, if another Ring of Triggered Spells is equipped both cease to function, and you cannot cast spells from it. This trigger must be specified when the spell is stored into the ring. For example: A Cure Wounds spell is triggered as a reaction if the wearer becomes bloodied. While wearing this ring, you can cast any spell stored in it. The spell uses the slot level, spell save DC, spell attack bonus, and spellcasting ability of the original caster, but is otherwise treated as if you cast the spell. The spell cast from the ring is no longer stored in it, freeing up space.

Roaring Thunder

# Roaring Thunder

*4th-level evocation. (Bard, Druid, Sage, Sorcerer, Warlock, Wizard)*

**Meta**

**Casting Time**: 1 action

**Range**: 30 feet

**Components**: V, M (a small paper cone)

**Duration**: Instantaneous

**Description**

You let out a deafening roar as a shockwave of energy collides with all around you. Each creature within range must succeed on a constitution saving throw or take 5d8 thunder damage and be pushed up to 20 feet away from you. On a successful save, a creature takes half damage and is not moved. Regardless of success or failure, a creature within range is deafened for 1 minute. Objects within range also take this damage.

**At Higher Levels**

When cast with a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the damage dealt increases by 1d8 for each spell level above 4th.

Rocket Spear

# Rocket Spear

*Martial Melee Weapon - 1d10 piercing - two-handed, thrown (range 20/20), heavy, special, topple - 500 gp, 12 lbs*

**Rocket spear.** It's a spear with a rocket. Pressurized combustion reimagined to strap a simple oil-powered rocket to the head of a spear to deliver a rather excessive amount of melee force.

**Special** - As an action, you can fuel the rocket spear with one flask of oil. While fueled, the rocket becomes active, and the weapon's thrown range increases to 60/60. While the rocket is active and you throw the spear, you can choose to hold onto it during its flight, landing in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the target. Once fueled, the rocket burns out of fuel at the end of your next turn or when you make an attack with it.

Rogue: Thief: Fast Hands

# Rogue: Thief: Fast Hands

Optional (replaces Fast Hands)

Starting at 3rd level, you can use the bonus action granted by your Cunning Action to make a Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check, use your thieves' tools to disarm a trap or open a lock, or take the Use an Object action. The DC of adventuring gear used with your Cunning Action increases to 8 + your Dexterity modifier + your proficiency bonus.

Rook

# Rook

## Description

Decisive tactics and thoughtful planning are both highly important factors in the game of chess, and as certain Bulwarks are aware, the same goes for the art of war. Rooks are master tacticians, able to turn the tide of battle in their favor from nearly any position.

**Features:**

*   [3rd Level - One Step Ahead](#3rd_Level_-_One_Step_Ahead)
*   [7th Level - Recapture](#7th_Level_-_Recapture)
*   [13th Level - Gambits](#13th_Level_-_Gambits)
*   [18th Level - Checkmate](#18th_Level_-_Checkmate)

## 3rd Level - One Step Ahead

When you select this specialization at 3rd level, you learn to apply yourself on the battlefield in a similar way to the pieces of a chess board.

You gain a pool of material equal to thrice your bulwark level to spend on plays. If a play allows you to move as part of it, you do so without expending your own movement, but you cannot have a speed of 0.

You recover all expended material when you finish a long rest.

Below is the list of plays:

**Advance:** When you take the attack action, you may expend three material to move up to your speed. You may take this movement before or after you make your attacks.

**Back Rank:** When you enter your defensive stance you can expend 3 material to impose disadvantage on all ranged attacks against you for the duration.

**En Passant:** When you attack a creature within 5 feet of you, you may expend 2 material to move up to 10 feet to another point within 5 feet of that creature. This movement does not provoke opportunity attacks, and your next attack roll against it has advantage.

**Pawn’s Fury:** During the first round of combat, you may expend 3 material to take an additional action. That action can be used to take only the Attack (one attack only), Dash, Disengage, Hide, or Interact with an Object action.

**Knight’s Leap:** When you jump, you may expend 1 material to triple your jump distance. It does not cost you any additional movement to do so.

**Bishop’s Grace:** As an action, you may expend 2 material to cast both guidance and resistance on yourself. Neither spell requires your concentration.

## 7th Level - Recapture

Starting at 7th level, you learn to reclaim the potential of the foes you fell. When you kill a creature with at least 30 hit points, you recover 5 material.

## 13th Level - Gambits

When you reach 13th level, you gain the ability to perform greater plays with great risk, though the potential payout is just as grand.

You gain access to the following plays:

**Castling:** When you enter your defensive stance, you may spend 7 material to choose one willing creature within 30 feet of you to trade places with. This movement does not provoke opportunity attacks. If when initiative ends, that creature has not taken damage since the switch, you regain 7 material.

**Discovered Attack:** When a creature within 5 feet of you targets you with an attack, you may use your reaction to expend 10 material. In doing so, you may make a melee attack against that target. If the attack reduces the creature to 0 hit points, you regain 10 material.

**Promote:** When you roll initiative, you may spend 20 material. For the duration of that combat you make plays, but not gambits, without expending material.

**Queen’s Majesty:** As a bonus action, you may expend 25 material in order to move up to twice your speed. For one minute after taking this bonus action, the amount of material you gain from your recapture feature is doubled.

**King’s Command:** As an action, you may spend 20 material to cast the Command spell (DC 8 + your strength modifier + your proficiency bonus) on any number of creatures within range. You must use the same command on each target. If no target succeeds their saving throw, you regain 10 material.

**Rook’s Defiance:** When you use your Fortitude feature, you may spend up to 20 material and further reduce the damage by that amount. If you reduce the damage to 0, you regain half the material spent.

## 18th Level - Checkmate

At 18th level, you have mastered the art of bringing fights to a decisive finish. When you take the attack action while your stance is active, you may choose to end your stance early. If you do, the first creature you hit takes extra damage equal to your bulwark level and is knocked prone.

Rosso Di Oakheart

# Rosso di Oakheart

*Wondrous Item (consumable), uncommon*

**Description**

This 12-ounce bottle of red grape wine is created by infusing the magicked flesh of sentient fungi into the yeast during the fermentation process. When ingested in its entirety, this potent alcohol inflicts the poisoned condition. A creature poisoned in this manner is considered drunk for a number of hours equal to 1d6 - their constitution modifier (minimum of 1 hour). A creature who is normally immune to poison still suffers this effect but can choose to resist the poison as an action, ending the condition.

Sage

# Sage

## The Sage Class

Alone in a cave sits a near withered old human. Having spent decades honing his very mind, he finally stands. And the very land quakes as he does.

A traveling monk spends years visiting temples across the world in an attempt to understand the nature of the very gods themselves. In doing so, a divine presence has rubbed off on them.

A ridiculed gnome stomps out of a mages college, kicked out on the grounds of being unteachable. Unknown to both the gnome and the college, the abstract and unpredictable are a conduit of power themselves.

A sage carries a deep understanding of arcane secrets, as well as knowledge of how magic truly works in regards to their spells and lives. Different ways of thinking are the true distinctions between sages, as no two have the exact same experiences or conclusions to their magical capabilities, even those who might have studied together. A sage draws on their understanding and connection to magic to call forth spells of great power.

### How to Think

Sages do not tend to study upon actual arcane knowledge, much like a wizard might. Instead using what they already know, and using that to expand upon their capabilities. The only true limiting factor of a sage is their own minds.

As such, the ways a sage reflects upon their magic can greatly influence how it is formed and projected. Very rarely does a sage know from the beginning how their magic will take shape as they allow it to grow.

### Creating a Sage

When creating a sage, knowing how your character perceives the world around them is crucial to both their development and their philosophies that shape their magic. Is your sage a hard working arcanist? Or perhaps more laid back? Your decisions should reflect your character's abilities and spells in how they are projected and used.

## Sage Table

## Class Features

As a Sage, you gain the following class features.

### Base Class Features

#### Hit Points

**Hit Dice:** 1d6 per sage level.

**Hit Points at 1st Level**: 6 + your constitution modifier

**Hit Points at Higher Levels:** 1d6 (or 4) + your constitution modifier per Sage level after first.

#### Proficiencies

**Armor:** none

**Weapons:** Daggers, darts, slings, quarterstaffs, light crossbows

**Tools:** none

**Saving Throws:** Intelligence, Wisdom

**Skills:** Choose two from Arcana, History, Insight, Medicine, Nature, Perception, Religion, Survival

#### Equipment

You start with the following equipment, in addition to the equipment granted by your background:

*   (a) a quarterstaff or (b) two daggers
*   A component pouch
*   (a) a scholar’s pack or (b) an explorer’s back
*   (a) a light crossbow and 20 bolts or (b) a sling and 20 bullets

### Spellcasting

A deep connection to the weave and your surroundings allow you to tap into and utilize magic.

#### Cantrips

At first level, you know two cantrips of your choice from the Sage spell list. You learn additional Sage cantrips of your choice at higher levels, as shown in the Cantrips Known column of the Sage table.

#### Spell Slots

The Sage table shows how many slots you have to cast your sage spells of 1st level or higher. To cast one of these sage spells, you must expend a slot of the spell’s level or higher. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a long rest.

#### Spells Known of 1st Level and Higher

You know two 1st-level spells of your choice from the sage spell list.

The Spells Known column of the Sage table shows you when you learn more sage spells of your choice. Each of these spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. For instance, when you reach 3rd level in this class, you can learn one new spell of 1st or 2nd level.

#### Spellcasting Ability

Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for your sage spells, since your magic relies on a deep understanding and connection to the weave. You use your Wisdom whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability. In addition, you use your Wisdom modifier when setting the saving throw DC for a sage spell you cast and when making an attack roll with one.

Spell save DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Wisdom modifier

Spell Attack Modifier = your proficiency bonus + your Wisdom modifier

#### Spellcasting Focus

A Sage cannot use a spellcasting focus for their sage spells, as they require a direct path to the source of their magic without interruption. A Sage must provide any material components their spells call for. Magical spellcasting foci do not provide benefits to sage spells.

### Meditation

You can spend a time in meditation to reflect and relearn what magic you have learned. When you finish a short rest, you can replace one sage spell you know for another. The new spell you learn must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

Additionally, once per long rest, when you replace a spell with this feature, you regain one expended spell slot of a level equal to the new spell or lower, but not higher than 5th level.

### Philosophical Study

When you reach 2nd level, you choose a philosophical study that guides the way you project your magic.

*   [Philosophy of Diligence](/docs/philosophy-of-diligence "philosophy of diligence")
*   [Philosophy of Fallacy](/docs/philosophy-of-fallacy "philosophy of fallacy")
*   [Philosophy of Finality](/docs/philosophy-of-finality "philosophy of finality")
*   [Philosophy of Mysticism](/docs/philosophy-of-mysticism "philosophy of mysticism")
*   [Philosophy of Patience](/docs/philosophy-of-patience "philosophy of patience")

Your choice grants you features at 2nd level and again at 6th, 10th, 14th, and 18th level.

### Mind Shaper

At 3rd level, you gain an increased ability to weave together both your own thoughts and the magic you cast. When you use your \[Meditation\] feature and learn a new spell, you can choose to make the new spell into your focused spell.

When you finish a long rest, you can replace any of your focused spells with another spell of 1st level or higher that you know. You can have a number of focused spells as designated by the Focused Spells column of the Sage Table.

When you focus on a spell, choose two of the following shapes to place on the spell. You can only have any given shape on one focused spell at a time. A mind shape’s effects apply only to the focused spell they are attached to, unless otherwise stated.

#### **Clarity**

*   The spell ignores material components, except for those consumed by the spell

#### **Cruel**

*   When a creature takes damage from the spell, it has disadvantage on ability checks until the end of its next turn.

#### **Ethereal**

*   The spell’s effects can travel through solid objects, and can target creatures and objects behind total cover. If a spell specifies you must see your target, you still must meet this requirement.

#### **Flowing**

*   You can change the casting time from one action to one bonus action.

#### **Focused**

*   You can treat your wisdom modifier as being 50% higher for the purposes of setting your spell save DC, your spell attack modifier, and for making any checks relating to the spell for the spell’s duration.

#### **Growing**

*   While you maintain concentration on the spell, you gain a +1 bonus to damage rolls for each round you do so. This bonus can be no greater than half your sage level.

#### **Guiding**

*   A creature targeted by the spell has advantage on ability checks until the end of its next turn.

#### **Prepared**

*   When you cast the spell, your proficiency bonus increases by 1 until the start of your next turn.  This mind shape does not exclusively affect this focused spell.

#### **Shared**

*   If the spell has a range of self, you can cast it targeting a willing creature within 30 feet of you.

#### **Siphoning**

*   When you target a creature with the spell, you gain temporary hit points equal to the spell’s level.

### Ability Score Improvement

When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can’t increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

Using the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

### Greater Meditation

At 10th level, you can perfectly clear your mind during meditation. When you replace a spell with your \[Meditation\] Feature, you can replace a number of spells equal to your wisdom modifier. You still gain only a single spell slot from meditation.

### Replenish

As high level magic is used for your spells, an amount flows back to you to fill the void it left behind. When you reach 17th level, casting a spell of 6th level or higher will replenish an expended spell slot of a level equal to half the level of the spell you cast (rounded up). You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining all uses when you complete a long rest.

### Enlightenment

At 20th level, your mind transcends your own body, sustaining you by mere thought alone. You no longer need to eat, drink, or sleep, and you are immune to the charmed and frightened conditions.

Additionally, you gain one additional reaction per round, that can only be used to cast a spell that has a casting time of one reaction.

# Subclasses

## Philosophy of Diligence

*A sage who focuses on refining their own mind to fully control the magic they wield. Such arcanists hold themselves to particularly high degrees of perfection, allowing little room for failure even among the most basic of tasks. Their work in achieving this perfection however is reflected in full by their arcane mastery.*

## Quality Spellcasting

Meticulous work has gone into perfecting your spellcasting. When you cast a spell of 1st level or higher that deals damage, you may treat a roll of 1 on any damage die as a 2.

## Empowered Will

Through your constant work, your mental fortitude is nigh unbreakable. At 6th level, you gain proficiency in Charisma saving throws.

## Shaper of Willpower

At 10th level, you can further shape your spells to reflect your diligence. When you cast a focused spell, it ignores a target’s resistance to damage.

You also gain the following Mind Shape, which can be placed on your focused spells as normal:

### Willful

When you roll damage with the spell, you can treat a dice roll of one below its maximum amount as its maximum amount.

## Invariable Effect

At 14th level, control over your spells has given you a large degree of control over their success. When you cast a spell that requires you to make a check using your spellcasting ability, you can treat any d20 roll of 9 or lower as a 10.

Additionally, your Quality Spellcasting feature now treats a roll of 1 or 2 on any damage die as a 3.

## Absolution

At 18th level, your mastery over your very mind has granted you the power to exert your will on others to a greater, and invariable effect. When a creature succeeds on a saving throw against one of your focused spells, you can choose to make them fail instead. Alternatively, if you miss on an attack roll with a focused spell, you can cause the attack to hit instead. Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

## Philosophy of Fallacy

*There are ways of thinking that are often called incorrect by many, exhibiting illogical methods, or even simply being incorrect. However, certain Sages can utilize these ways of thinking in shaping their magic into something truly bizarre.*

## Bandwagon

The spells you cast carry the capacity to trick the very weave itself into allowing extra magic to be cast. When you cast a focused spell by expending a spell slot of first level or higher, the next time you or a creature within 30 feet of you casts that same spell at the same level before the start of your next turn, they do so without expending a spell slot, so long as the spell is of 5th level or lower.

## Straw Man

Your illogical reasoning extends far enough to prevent harm to you, though opening you to potentially greater damage.

Starting at 6th level, as a reaction when you take damage, you can teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within 30 feet of you, creating an illusory copy to take the damage instead. If you take damage from any source before the start of your next turn, you are vulnerable to that damage.

## Shaper of Delusion

At 10th level, you can further shape your spells to your own fallacy. When a creature casts one of your focused spells that benefits from your \[Bandwagon\] feature, that creature gets advantage on the next attack roll, ability check, or saving throw they make within the next minute.

You also gain the following Mind Shape, which can be placed on your focused spells as normal:

### Delusional

When you cast the spell, you can choose to have it not take effect until you cast your next spell.

## Red Herring

You learn to use your thoughts to distract those around you at 14th level. When a creature within 60 feet of you makes an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, you can use your reaction to impose disadvantage on the roll. If the creature fails their ability check, saving throw, or attack roll, it takes psychic damage equal to half your sage level. If it succeeds, you take the damage instead.

You can do so a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier, regaining all uses after you finish a long rest.

## Anti-Logic

At 18th level, you gain the ability to turn other’s perceptions to better serve your spellcasting, and at a heavy detriment to them. As an action, you can force a creature within 60 feet of you to make a charisma saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the creature will willingly fail any saving throw against your sage spells, and is no longer considered willing for beneficial effects from allies. This effect lasts for 1 minute.

Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

## Philosophy of Finality

*Those who undertake the Philosophy of Finality hold the understanding that all things must come to their eventual end. Inclusive of their foes, and themselves. A Sage who follows this study may work to bring, delay, or even stop the meddling with of such events. Regardless of how these Sage’s may work, they have a full understanding that it cannot, and will not last forever.*

## Last Cast

As your pool of magic runs out, you manage to extort additional power drawing from its end. When you cast a spell of 1st level or higher, and expend the last spell slot of a given level, you gain a bonus to one damage roll equal to 2d8.

## Resolve to End

At 6th level, lasting harmful effects are far less effective against you, as your understanding forces them to end. When you are targeted by a harmful effect with a duration of one minute or longer, you can choose to end the effect at the end of your next turn (no action required). Once you do so, you cannot do so again until you complete a short or long rest.

## Shaper of Denial

At 10th level, your spells reflect the absolute finality that shapes your spells. When a focused spell ends on a creature, you can choose to end all beneficial or detrimental effects currently affecting them.

You also gain the following Mind Shape, which can be placed on your focused spells as normal:

### Denying

If the spell causes a creature to be reduced to 0 hit points, it does not consume a spell slot. Once your denying spell prevents a spell slot from being expended, it cannot do so again until you create a new denying spell.

## Forced Stop

Your understanding of the end of all around you can allow you to bring it to the present instantly, empowering you in the process.

At 14th level, you learn the Dispel Magic spell if you don’t already know it. You cannot replace this spell through meditation, and it does not count towards the total number of spells you know.

When you successfully end a spell or effect with Dispel Magic, you can use your Last Cast feature on the next spell you cast of 1st level or higher, even if you have spell slots remaining of the level you cast.

## Denial and Acceptance

At 18th level, the knowledge of your end fuels you to persist should you fall before your time. If you die to any source other than old age, you are returned to life 24 hours later with all of your hit points at the location you died, so long as your soul is able to. If your body was destroyed, a new one is created. Once you are restored to life by this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

## Philosophy of Mysticism

*There are those who take their connection to magic to a completely higher level, sometimes utilizing understanding through godly entities or beings of similar power. Sages who undertake the Philosophy of Mysticism may work closely with clerics, paladins, or even warlocks to further strengthen their connection to the divine.*

## Divine Initiate

When you take this philosophy at 2nd level, you gain a small spark connecting you to a divine entity, granting you enhanced magic. Choose two spells of 1st level from the Cleric or Warlock spell lists. Both spells need not be from the same list. These spells count as sage spells for you, and do not count towards your number of spells learned. If you choose to replace either of these spells through Meditation[\[1\]](#cite_note-:0-1), you can learn a Cleric or Warlock spell of any level of which you have spell slots to cast in its place.

## Medicinal Spells

Your spells carry with them the capacity to heal both yourself and your allies. At 6th level, when you cast a sage spell of 1st level or higher that targets either you or an ally, you can cause that creature to restore a number of hit points equal to your sage level. You can do so a number of times equal to your wisdom modifier, and regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

## Shaper of Fanaticism

At 10th level, you can further shape your spells to more closely fit the divinity you follow. When you cast a focused spell from the cleric or warlock spell lists, you can choose to have the spell count as one level higher than the slot used to cast it. In doing so, you can cast no spells other than focused spells for the next minute.

You also gain the following Mind Shape[\[1\]](#cite_note-:0-1), which can be placed on your focused spells as normal:

### Fanatical

When a celestial, fiend, or undead fails a saving throw against the spell, or are hit with a spell attack roll, they take extra damage equal to half your sage level.

## Enhanced Divinity

Your mental connections to divine magic have had a profound effect on both your mind and body at 14th level. When you make a charisma check, you can add your wisdom modifier to the total.

Additionally, angel or bat wings sprout from your back (your choice) giving you a flying speed of 30 ft. You also gain resistance to radiant damage (if you chose angel wings), or necrotic damage (if you chose bat wings).

## Envoy of the Heavens

At 18th level, your spells carry with them the very essence of the divine entities you’ve spent your life connected with. You gain the following benefits.

*   You can replace the damage type of your focused spells with radiant or necrotic.
*   Celestials, Fiends and Undead have disadvantage on saving throws against your sage spells.
*   When you restore hit points of another creature with a sage spell, you regain hit points equal to half the amount you healed.

1.  ↑ [1.0](#cite_ref-:0_1-0) [1.1](#cite_ref-:0_1-1) [Sage](/docs/sage "sage")

## Philosophy of Patience

*So it is said that with patience comes wisdom. That wisdom can come in the form of great magical power for those who take upon patience into their very being. A sage who has undertaken the philosophy of patience is never the first to start conflict, but is rarely the first to fall as a result of it.*

## Bonus Proficiencies

A sage of patience is always prepared should theirs outweigh that of others. As such, you gain proficiency in light armor, medium armor, and shields.

## Delayed Potency

Starting at 2nd level, you gain the ability to enhance your longer lasting spells at the cost of a slower start. When you cast a spell that makes multiple damage rolls, or lasts for multiple rounds, the first damage roll, or damage rolls during the first round, have all damage dice treated as their lowest value. In return, the last damage roll, or damage rolls during the final round, have all damage dice treated as their highest amount.

## Evasive Casting

Your spells open a window of clarity to you, allowing you to avoid harm for a short time immediately after casting them. Starting at 6th level, you can take the dodge action as a bonus action if you use your action to cast a sage spell of 1st level or higher.

## Shaper of Composure

At 10th level, your spells further evolve to reflect your patience. When you cast a focused spell, you gain temporary hit-points equal to your sage level.

You also gain the following Mind Shape, which can be placed on your focused spells as normal:

### Composed

Creatures cannot react to you casting this spell.

## Consummate Concentration

While concentrating on spells, both your mind and body become far more rigid. Starting at 14th level, while you maintain concentration on a spell, you gain a +2 bonus to your armor class and saving throws. Your concentration also cannot be broken as a result of taking damage.

## Paragon of Patience

At 18th level your patience opens opportunities to you that others could not even hope to achieve. When you roll initiative, you may choose to instead place yourself at initiative 0. If you do so, you may take a second turn immediately after your first turn in the first round of combat.

Sash Of The Eldr Rune

# Sash of the Eldr Rune

*Wondrous Item, very rare*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement by a monk

**Description**

This sash of elk hair from the frigid north is as white as the endless snowy dales. The eldr (focus) rune is sewn into it with bright red threads. The sash has the following properties while you wear it:

*   Heightened Focus. As an action, you can channel the sash's magic to increase the potency of your ki. For the next minute or until you lose concentration as if concentrating on a spell, you can increase the DC of your ki abilities by 2. This property can’t be used again until the next dawn.
*   Lightened Focus. By spending 3 ki points, you can double your movement speed for 1 minute.

**Imbuement of Focus**. You can transfer the sash's magic to a nonmagical item -- either one of your monk weapons or a pair of gloves -- by tracing the stein rune there with your finger. The transfer takes 8 hours of work that requires the two items to be within 5 feet of each other. At the end of the 8 hours, the sash is destroyed, and the rune appears in silver on the chosen item.
