You can only be attuned to one of the items at a time, which gains a benefit based on its form in addition to its normal effects:

*   **Weapon**. The weapon becomes a very rare magic weapon that requires attunement by a monk. While you wield it, you can use your Wisdom modifier instead of Strength or Dexterity for attack and damage rolls using the weapon and has a +2 bonus to the attack and damage rolls of those attacks. Whenever you use a ki point as part the attack made with the weapon, your attack deals additional damage equal to your Wisdom modifier.
*   **Gloves**. The pair of gloves is now a rare magic item that requires attunement by a monk. While you wear the gloves you can use your Wisdom modifier instead of Strength when making Strength (Athletics) checks and your unarmed strikes gains a +2 to damage rolls.

School Of Beating Your Ass

# School of Beating your Ass

## Description

**Features**:

*   [2nd Level - Arcane Might](#2nd_Level_-_Arcane_Might)
*   [6th Level - Special Delivery](#6th_Level_-_Special_Delivery)
*   [10th Level - Extra Attack](#10th_Level_-_Extra_Attack)
*   [14th Level - The ol' One-Two](#14th_Level_-_The_ol'_One-Two)

## 2nd Level - Arcane Might

When you take this tradition at 2nd level, you learn to better partake in unarmed combat. You can replace the damage of your unarmed strikes with a d4 instead of their normal damage, and is considered magical for the purposes of overcoming resistance and immunity to non-magical attacks. This damage increases to 1d6 at 6th level, to 1d8 at 10th level, and again to 1d10 at 14th level.

Additionally, your hit point maximum increases by 2, and again by one each time you gain a level in this class.

## 6th Level - Special Delivery

When you reach 6th level, your technique has been honed to cast your spells through your martial combat. Once per turn, when you make an unarmed strike, you can deliver a spell's effects to the target as if you had cast it on them, in addition to the effects of the unarmed strike. The spell must have a casting time of one action, and must either be able to target another creature or area. If the spell would normally effect an area, it instead only effects that target. If the spell requires a saving throw, the target must make the saving throw as usual. If the spell requires an attack roll, the spell hits if the unarmed strike hits.

You expend a spell slot to cast the spell as normal, and the spell must be of a level no greater than your proficiency bonus.

## 10th Level - Extra Attack

Beginning at 10th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

## 14th Level - The ol' One-Two

Your culmination of martial and arcane knowledge has combined to great effect, allowing you to use one to make targets more vulnerable to another. If you hit a target twice on the same turn with unarmed strikes, the target has disadvantage on saving throws from the next spell you cast before the end of your next turn.

Additionally, when you damage a creature with a spell, the next unarmed strike you make against them has advantage, and deals extra damage equal to your wizard level. If the spell requires a saving throw, the target must make the saving throw as usual. If the spell requires an attack roll, the spell hits if the unarmed strike hits.

You expend a spell slot to cast the spell as normal, and the spell must be of a level no greater than your proficiency bonus.

Scouting Knife

# Scouting Knife

*Weapon (dagger), uncommon*

**Description**

This dagger is adorned with a magical leaf at it's base, and is otherwise seemingly normal. This dagger may be thrown at an object or creature, at which point it will seamlessly embed itself. While embedded, the throwing creature may use it as 5 feet of stable ground no matter the height it is embedded. As a bonus action on a subsequent turn, the throwing creature may recall it through the use of a string tied to it's hilt.

Scrapper

# Scrapper

## Description

*Sometimes seen as the oddball of the various Artificer specialists, the Scrapper is a master of what other Artificers discard as junk. Make no mistake though, these hoarder misfits exemplify everything an artificer is at their core - Innovative, curious, with a spark of insanity mixed in with genius. They take what others see as trash, and turn it into works of art.*

**Features:**

*   [3rd Level - Tool of the Trade](#3rd_Level_-_Tool_of_the_Trade)
*   [3rd Level - Scrapper Spells](#3rd_Level_-_Scrapper_Spells)
*   [3rd Level - Another's Treasure](#3rd_Level_-_Another's_Treasure)
*   [5th Level - Extra Attack](#5th_Level_-_Extra_Attack)
*   [9th Level - Junkrat's Armor](#9th_Level_-_Junkrat's_Armor)
*   [15th Level - Sage of Junk](#15th_Level_-_Sage_of_Junk)

## 3rd Level - Tool of the Trade

You have proficiency with improvised weapons. You also gain proficiency with smith's tools. If you already have this proficiency, you gain proficiency with one other type of artisan's tools of your choice.

## 3rd Level - Scrapper Spells

Starting at 3rd level, you always have certain spells prepared after you reach particular levels in this class, as shown in the Scrapper Spells table. These spells count as artificer spells for you, but they don’t count against the number of artificer spells you prepare.

3rd level - magic missile, shield

5th level - cloud of daggers, locate object

9th level - leomund's tiny hut, melf's minute meteors

13th level - dimension door, fire shield

17th level - passwall, steel wind strike

## 3rd Level - Another's Treasure

Beginning at 3rd level, you can take anything lying around - A table leg, a shard of glass, a cooking pot - and turn it into a deadly weapon of war. When you hold/make an improvised weapon, the DM will determine if it deals slashing, piercing, or bludgeoning damage, and if it is one-handed (base 1d4 damage) or two-handed (base 1d6 damage) based on its resemblance to real weapons. You can use a bonus action to use your smithing tools to roll on the Upgrade Table to apply a random upgrade to an improvised weapon you are holding. An upgraded improvised weapon count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.

d6 - Effect

1 **Heavy Smashers.** You affix heavy weights to the weapon, adding a bonus 1d6 bludgeoning damage. When you damage a target with this weapon, roll 1d4. On a 4, you can knock the target away from you up to 10 feet.

2 **Spiky Stabbers.** You attach spiky things such as rusted nails, sharpened sticks, etc. to your weapon, adding a bonus 1d6 piercing damage. When you damage a target with this weapon, roll 1d4. On a 4, you reduce that target's AC by 2 until the end of its next turn.

3 **Sharp Slicers.** You add sharp metal scraps, broken glass, or other slicing edges to your weapon, adding a bonus 1d6 slashing damage. When you damage a target with this weapon, roll 1d4. On a 4, the target's speed is halved until the end of its next turn.

4 **Burning Flames.** You coat the weapon in flammable materials, and set it alight, adding a bonus 1d6 fire damage. When you damage a target with this weapon, roll 1d4. On a 4, you can ignite the target. A target on fire takes 1d10 fire damage at the start of its turn. The target can use an action to put the fire out.

5 **Shocking Coils.** You wrap your weapon with suspect electrical components and wires, adding a bonus 1d6 lightning damage. When you damage a target with this weapon, roll 1d4. On a 4, the target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be stunned until the end of its next turn.

6 **Venomous Goo.** You slather the weapon in dangerous chemicals and diseased meat, adding a bonus 1d6 poison damage. When you damage a target with this weapon, roll 1d4. On a 4, the target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or become poisoned for 1 minute. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

An upgraded improvised weapon can be used in a number of attacks equal to your Intelligence modifier plus your proficiency bonus(a minimum of twice) before they break and become unusable. You can apply only one upgrade on an improvised weapon and it is removed when you drop or stow the weapon. If you try to upgrade another improvised weapon, the old upgrade is immediately removed, and then the new upgrade applies. You can also consume a spell slot to apply an upgrade to an improvised weapon as a bonus action. If you do, you may choose the upgrade applied from the Upgrade Table.

You are able to apply your infusions to your upgraded improvised weapons.

## 5th Level - Extra Attack

Starting 5th level, you can attack twice, rather than once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

## 9th Level - Junkrat's Armor

At 9th level, you have found new uses for all the scrap you carry around for makeshift weapons. Now, you know how to enhance your armor with ad hoc ablative shielding.

After finishing a long rest and if you are wearing medium or heavy armor, your armor gains a bonus to Armor Class equal to your Intelligence modifier (a minimum bonus of +1). Whenever you take damage, roll 1d4. On a 4, pieces of this scrap-built extra armor break off, and your bonus decreases by 1 point until it returns to its normal base value.

## 15th Level - Sage of Junk

By 15th level, you have become a lethal force with any random object that fits your fancy:

*   Whenever you hold an improvised weapon, you can automatically apply a random upgrade from the Upgrade Table.
*   When you cast a spell of 1st level or higher, the next attack you make with an upgraded improvised weapon deals extra force damage equal to 1d10 times the level of the spell cast, to a maximum of 5d10.
*   You now gain the Junkrat's Armor bonus after a short or long rest, instead of only a long rest.

Scythe Of Reaping

# Scythe of Reaping

*Weapon (Scythe), very rare*

**Description**

This scythe is adorned by grotesque engravings of tragedy, and has an obsidian lining running the length of the blade. Each time you hit a creature with this scythe, it gains a stack of reap, which lasts until it’s next short or long rest. This weapon deals an additional 1 slashing damage for every stack of reap the target has. A target may have a maximum of 7 stacks of reap. A target looses all stacks of reap if it is targeted by greater restoration, heal, or a similar restorative magic. When a target with at least 1 stack of reap is reduced to zero hit points with this weapon, you gain 5 temporary hit points for each stack of reap the target had.

Scythe

# Scythe

*Weapon: Simple, Melee*

5gp, 2 lbs. --- 1d6 slashing - finesse, versatile (1d8)

**Description**

Proficiency with a scythe allows you to add your proficiency bonus to the attack roll for any attack you make with it.

**Finesse**: When making an attack with a finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

**Versatile**: This weapon can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property--the damage when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.

**Mastery Property - Cleave:** If you hit a creature with a melee attack roll using this weapon, you can make a melee attack roll with the weapon against a second creature within 5 feet of the first that is also within your reach. On a hit, the second creature takes the weapon’s damage, but don’t add your ability modifier to that damage unless that modifier is negative. You can make this extra attack only once per turn.

Seat Belt

# Seat Belt

*Wondrous Item, common*

**Description**

While standing on a solid surface, you begin to sit down in an open space as if there were a chair beneath you. When you would normally crouch or fall to the ground when doing this, you can mentally command the belt to summon an invisible, incorporeal chair as a bonus action instead. The chair can be of any style you choose, such as a stool, recliner, throne, or rocking chair, and can support up to 500 pounds. While it is comfortable, it is nevertheless invisible and cannot be interacted with beyond simply sitting on. The chair disappears if it is no longer on a solid surface, you stand up, or if you're pushed or moved while sitting on it.

Selkie

# Selkie

## **Description**

Seal-folk who slip between human and animal forms, selkies are gentle and mysterious beings tied to coastal waters.

## Creature Type

Monstrosity

## Ability Score Increase

+2 Dexterity, +1 Wisdom

## Age

Up to 150 years.

## Size

Medium

## Speed

Walk 30 ft., Swim 40 ft.

## Seal Shape

You can assume the form of a seal by donning or removing your sealskin (use a seal stat block).

## Fey Ancestry

You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed, and magic can’t put you to sleep.

## Languages

Common, Aquan

Sentinel Blade

# Sentinel Blade

The Sentinel Blade embodies the essence of unwavering defense, blending masterful swordplay with unbreakable resolve. Trained to stand as the ultimate shield for their allies, these warriors adopt a specialized stance that turns every strike against them into an opportunity. Their blades move with precision, deflecting blows with ease and delivering calculated ripostes.

## *Aegis Flow*

Starting at 2nd level, you learn to develop flow during the midst of combat, building on each success as you begin to flow like a dance. While using a melee weapon without a shield, whenever you land a successful hit your first time in a turn, an attack misses you, or you succeed on a saving throw from a hostile creature, you gain flow equal to 1d6 + half your total mastery. Whenever you take damage from an attack, you lose flow instead. If this damage reduces your flow to 0, you take any remaining damage. If you reduce an attack's damage to zero, you suffer no additional effects from the weapon. You can have a max amount of flow equal to twice your practitioner level, which depletes at the end of combat. You can only build flow whilst in combat.

## Riposte

Starting at 6th level, when an attack misses you, or you block one with flow completely, you may make a melee attack as a reaction against the target. Additionally, you may instead spend 1 mastery point to use a technique.

## Vigilant Guardian

Starting at 14th level, when an ally within melee range is hit by an attack, you can use your flow to block the attack as if it were targeting you.

## Blade Dance

Starting at 18th level, you learn to harness your flow into focus. When you have at least 75% of your maximum flow and focus on a technique or use a technique through riposte, you may sacrifice 7 flow to spend 1 less mastery point.

The following goes with the subclass as part of the practitioner class

## Unyielding Sentinel | Mastery

### Form 1: Flowing Strike

**Type**: Physical

**Prerequisites**: Novice, Sentinel Blade

Make a weapon attack against a creature or object. The attack deals a number of d6s equal to your proficiency bonus in additional damage.

**Focused (1 point)**

Hit or miss, roll a number or d4s equal to half your total mastery. You gain flow equal to the total.

### Form 2: Windfall Step

**Type**: Physical

**Prerequisites**: Intermediate, Sentinel Blade

You move in a mirage, teleporting to an area within 60ft of you.

**Focused (1 point, 0 if you have your maximum flow).**

You can use another technique that includes a weapon attack. This may be before or after you teleport.

### Form 3: Ebon Tide

**Type**: Mental

**Prerequisites**: Advanced, Sentinel Blade

You may temporarily steel yourself against other manners of attacks. Until the end of your next turn, you may block damage from any source using Aegis Flow.

**Focused (2 points, 1 if you your maximum flow).**

The duration of this technique becomes 1 minute. Until the end of your next turn, if you would succeed on a saving throw and normally take half damage, you would instead take no damage.

### Form 4: Dance of Death

**Type**: Physical

**Prerequisites**: Master, Sentinel Blade

**Duration**: 1 minute

For the next minute, your maximum flow and the flow you gain is doubled. During this time, you have vulnerability to attacks while your flow is 0.

You must use an action to end this effect

**Focused (1 points).**

You may use another technique from this form

Shardmind

# Shardmind

## Ability Score Increase.

When determining your character’s ability scores, increase one score by 2 and increase a different score by 1, or increase three different scores by 1. You can't raise any of your scores above 20.

## Creature Type

You are a construct.

## Size.

Your size is small or medium (your choice).

## Speed

Your base walking speed is 30 feet.

## Languages

You speak Common, and Deep Speech.

## Astral Mind

When you take a long rest, you must spend at least six hours in an inactive, motionless state, rather than sleeping. In this state, you appear inert, but it doesn’t render you unconscious, and you can see and hear as normal.

## Crystaline Soul

If the Mending spell is cast on you, you can spend a Hit Die, roll it, and regain a number of hit points equal to the roll plus your Constitution modifier (minimum of 1 hit point). In addition, your nature has allowed you to benefit from several spells that preserve life but that normally don't affect Constructs: Cure Wounds, Healing Word, Mass Cure Wounds, Mass Healing Word, and Spare the Dying.

## Telepathic

You can speak telepathically to any creature you can see, provided the creature is within 30ft of you. You don't need to share a language with the creature for it to understand your telepathic utterances, but the creature must be able to understand at least one language. Either creature can break this connection at any time.

## Crystalline Resilience

Crystalline nature is represented by the following benefits:

*   You have advantage on saving throws against being poisoned, and you have resistance to poison damage.
*   You don’t need to eat, drink, or breathe.
*   You are immune to disease.
*   You don't need to sleep.

## Outer Shell

Your crystalline shell provides you a base AC of 17 (your Dexterity modifier doesn’t affect this number). You can’t wear light, medium, or heavy armor, but if you are using a shield, you can apply the shield’s bonus as normal.

## Astral Knowledge

You gain proficiency in Arcana or Religion checks.

Shifter Adaptation

# Shifter Adaptation

**Prerequisite**

Shifter

**Description**

Your heritage has opened you to exceptional abilities as you tap into the unchecked powers that dwell in your blood.

*   Increase your Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, or Wisdom by 1, to a maximum of 20.
*   You can use your Shifting trait as a reaction to taking damage (you apply the temporary hit points before taking the damage).
*   Each of your transformations also gain one more effect:
    *   **Beasthide**. You gain resistance to nonmagical bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage (Your choice when you shift).
    *   **Longstooth**. You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift. If you use your Shifting trait as a reaction, you can use your fangs to make an unarmed strike against a target within range as part of the reaction.
    *   **Swiftstride**. You gain climbing speed equal to your movement speed.
    *   **Wildhunt**. You don't suffer from disadvantage when making an attack roll against a target within 30 feet of you.

Shinobi

# Shinobi

## Kozukura and Faerûn

The practice of the Shinobi originates from the distant nation of **Kozukura** , a land historically isolated by treacherous mountains and seas. Unlike the mages of Faerûn who rely on external incantations and manipulation of the Weave , the people of Kozukura developed an intense internal discipline.

### Chakra and the Weave

In Kozukura, the native population rarely developed an affinity for conventional spellcasting. Instead, they focused on channeling their **Chakra**—the energy formed by the perfect molding of their physical stamina and spiritual willpower. This internal manipulation allowed them to perform **Jutsu** (deadly techniques), achieving effects that mimic arcane magic without needing arcane components or external control. Shinobi techniques are, in essence, a hyper-focused method of drawing upon one's own life force to manipulate the ambient magical energy, providing results through sheer internal command.

### The Bloodlines and Dispersal

Generations ago in Kozukura, genetic traits granting unique chakra abilities began to manifest in certain **bloodlines**. As the Kozukura culture did not prioritize traditional Weave magic, those with similar **Bloodline abilities** naturally flocked together and founded the great **Clans** (such as the Uchiha and Hyuga equivalents) to protect and enhance their technique.

Over the centuries, economic necessity, trade, and occasional military conflict led to the gradual migration of Kozukura descendants across Faerûn. As this diaspora spread across the continent:

*   **Genetic Manifestation:** The Bloodline abilities—such as the **Copy Eye** or **Vitality**—began manifesting randomly in individuals throughout the Sword Coast and beyond, regardless of their immediate family name.

**Hidden Centers:** The most dedicated descendants established **hidden villages** or secluded **Dojos** across Faerûn. These centers remain vital for teaching the disciplines of chakra control and Jutsu mastery, ensuring that the Shinobi remains a rare and specialized operative utilizing unique tools and armor.

## The Shinobi Table


LevelProf BonusChakra PointsJutsu RankFeatures
1st+22-Shinobi Training, Nature Affinity, Chakra Molding, Jutsu Casting
2nd+24DShinobi Bloodline
3rd+26DShinobi Nindo (Subclass)
4th+28DAbility Score Improvement
5th+310CExtra Attack
6th+312CBloodline Awakening, Tactical Recovery
7th+314CNindo Feature
8th+316CAbility Score Improvement
9th+418B-
10th+420BImproved Nature, Surface Walking
11th+422BAdvanced Scouting
12th+424BAbility Score Improvement
13th+526A-
14th+528ABloodline Mastery
15th+530ANindo Feature
16th+532AAbility Score Improvement
17th+634SPersistent Nature
18th+636SJonin Commander
19th+638SAbility Score Improvement
20th+640SNindo Capstone


## Class Features

As a Shinobi, you gain the following class features.

### Hit Points

*   **Hit Dice:** 1d8 per Shinobi level
*   **Hit Points at 1st Level:** 8 + your Constitution modifier
*   **Hit Points at Higher Levels:** 1d8 (or 5) + your Constitution modifier per Shinobi level after 1st

### Proficiencies

*   **Armor:** Light armor, medium armor
*   **Weapons:** Simple weapons, shortswords, scimitars, hand crossbows, kusarigama
*   **Tools:** Calligrapher's Supplies, Herbalism kit
*   **Saving Throws:** Dexterity, Intelligence
*   **Skills:** Choose **three** from Acrobatics, Athletics, Deception, History, Insight, Investigation, Perception, Sleight of Hand, and Stealth

### Equipment

You start with the following equipment, in addition to the equipment granted by your background:

*   (a) a shortsword or (b) two simple weapons
*   (a) a dungeoneer's pack or (b) an explorer's pack
*   (a) leather armor or (b) chain Shirt
*   (a) Calligrapher's Supplies and 10x tags (empty scrolls) (b) herbalism Kit

## Level 1 - Shinobi Training

You have undergone rigorous conditioning in the arts of the silent kill and tactical movement. You gain the following benefits:

*   **Shinobi Weapons:** You are a master of small, concealable arms. When you wield a kunai, kama, or shuriken, its damage die changes to a d6. These weapons, along with tanto, wakizashi and Kusarigama count as "Shinobi Weapons" for you, Shinobi Weapons are used when performing Bukijutsu.
    *   Kunai = Dagger
    *   Kama = Sickle
    *   Shuriken = Dart
    *   Tanto = Shortsword
    *   Wakizashi = Scimitar
*   **Ballistics:** If a Shinobi Weapon has the Thrown property, its normal range increases to 30 feet and its long range increases to 90 feet.
*   **Armored Agility**: Your conditioning allows you to move unrestricted. You can use your Dexterity score instead of Strength for calculating **jumping distances** and **climbing checks**, even while wearing Light or Medium armor.

## Level 1 - Nature Affinity

Every Shinobi has an innate elemental affinity, determined by the alignment of their chakra at birth. Choose one of the following natures, alternatively roll a d8 to randomly select a nature.

| **Random (D8)** | **Nature** | **Associated Element** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Fire (Katon) | Flame and Heat |
| 2 | Water (Suiton) | Liquid and Ice |
| 3 | Earth (Doton) | Soil and Stone |
| 4 | Wind (Futon) | Air and Sound |
| 5 | Lightning (Raiton) | Electricity |
| 6 | Yin (Genjutsu) | Mind and Shadow |
| 7 | Yang | Body |
| 8 | Taijutsu & Bukijutsu | Life and Force |

*   **Primary Nature Efficiency:** You are most efficient when molding chakra for your natural affinity. When you cast a Jutsu that matches the **Primary Nature** you selected at 1st Level, you reduce the Chakra cost by **1** (to a minimum of **1** Chakra).
*   **Primary Nature Technique**: You learn a D Rank Jutsu of your Chosen Nature. This Jutsu doesn’t count against the amount of Jutsu known

## Level 1 - Chakra Molding

You have learned to mold your physical stamina and spiritual energy into **Chakra**, the fuel for your techniques.

*   **Chakra Points**: You have a pool of Chakra points represented by the following formula: Chakra Maximum = Shinobi Level × 2
*   **Recovery:** You regain all expended Chakra points when you finish a **Long Rest**.

## Level 1 - Jutsu Casting

You have trained your memory and dexterity to weave Hand Signs, manifesting reality-warping effects known as **Jutsu**.

*   **Jutsu Known:** You know a number of Jutsu equal to half your Shinobi level (rounded up) (minimum of 1).
    *   *Learning New Jutsu:* Whenever you gain a level in this class, you can replace one Jutsu you know with another Jutsu of a Rank you can cast.
*   **Jutsu Ranks:** Jutsu are organized by Rank, which corresponds to their power level and Chakra cost. At 1st Level, you can learn and cast **D-Rank** Jutsu. You unlock higher ranks as you level up.
*   **Spellcasting Ability:** **Intelligence** is your spellcasting ability for your Jutsu.
    *   **Jutsu Save DC** = 8 + your Proficiency Bonus + your Intelligence modifier
    *   **Jutsu Attack Modifier** = your Proficiency Bonus + your Intelligence modifier
*   **Jutsu Components:**
    *   **Hand Signs (Somatic Components):** Casting a Jutsu requires you to weave complex hand signs. You must have **two free hands** to cast a Jutsu, unless a feature allows otherwise. You cannot cast Jutsu while holding a shield or a two-handed weapon, unless you drop it or put it away first.
    *   **Material:** These are varying components required when casting a jutsu
        *   Motion: Cannot perform with a speed of 0
        *   Items: An Item that is required
        *   Gaze: Requires direct line of sight to the target
    *   **Weapon**: You must wield or have a weapon available to use a jutsu with a weapon component

#### Jutsu Rank & Cost Table


Jutsu RankEquivalent Spell LevelChakra CostLevel Unlocked
D-Rank1st Level21st
C-Rank2nd Level35th
B-Rank3rd Level59th
A-Rank4th-5th Level613th
S-Rank6th Level717th


## Level 2 - Shinobi Bloodline

Your capabilities are defined by your genetics or clan origin. Choose one **Bloodline** from the list below. This choice is permanent and cannot be changed. You automatically learn the [Bloodline Jutsu](/docs/bloodline-jutsu "bloodline jutsu") associated with your chosen bloodline at the applicable Rank level. These do not count against your Jutsu Known.

Optional Rule: For Characters that do not know their genetic origin or who got the bloodline through far of generational mean, roll a d10 and divide it by 2 (rounded up) to determine your Bloodline


Random Roll (1d10/2)Bloodline
1The Copy Eye
2The White Eye
3The Whirlpool
4The Shadow
5The Prodigy


### The Copy Eye

*Descendants of a clan known for intense emotions and the Sharingan, an eye that sees chakra and mimics movement, when awakened the eye turns red with a single rain drop pattern. At each Bloodline level, you gain another raindrop.*

*   **Kinetic Vision:** You can briefly read the muscle tension of your enemies. When a creature you can see makes an attack roll against you, you can use your **Reaction** to impose **Disadvantage** on that attack roll. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your **Proficiency Bonus**, regaining all expended uses on a Long Rest.

### The White Eye

*Descendants of a noble clan possessing the Byakugan, capable of seeing long distances and through objects. Their eyes, pupils and irises are permanently white.*

*   **All-Seeing:** You cannot be **Surprised** while you are conscious.

### The Whirlpool

*Descendants of a fallen clan renowned for their sealing jutsu and immense longevity and larger than normal chakra pools.*

*   **Massive Reserves:** Starting at 2nd Level, you add your **Proficiency Bonus** to your Maximum Chakra Points.

### The Shadow

*Descendants of a clan of high-IQ tacticians who manipulate shadows to bind enemies.*

*   **Shadow Arts:** You learn the *Minor Illusion* cantrip. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for it. You can cast it using only Somatic components (your shadow).

### The Prodigy (Natural Genius)

*You possess no special surname, but you were born with a freakish talent for chakra manipulation*

*   **Precision:** When you roll damage for a Jutsu or a Shinobi Weapon attack, you can reroll any **1s** on the damage dice. You must use the new roll.

## Level 3 - Shinobi Nindo

At 3rd level, you choose the personal philosophy that guides your actions and techniques. Your choice grants you features at **3rd, 7th, 15th, and 20th Level**.

*   Nindo of the Iron Fist
*   Nindo of the Battlemind
*   Nindo of the Elementalist
*   Nindo of the Illusionist
*   Nindo of the Healer
*   Nindo of the Assassin

## Level 4 - Ability Score Improvement

When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can’t increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

Using the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

## Level 5 - Extra Attack

Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

## Level 6 - Bloodline Awakening

*The latent power in your genetics begins to manifest more potent abilities. You gain the feature corresponding to the **Bloodline** you chose at 1st Level.*

**The Copy Eye**

*   **Visual Insight:** You can focus your chakra to see the unseen. You can cast See Invisibility or Detect Magic by spending **2 Chakra**. You do not need material components for these castings.
*   **Reflexive Analysis:** You gain proficiency in the **Insight** skill. If you are already proficient, you gain **Expertise** (doubling your proficiency bonus).

**The White Eye**

*   **Piercing Sight:** As a **Bonus Action**, you can activate your X-Ray vision for 1 minute. For the duration, you can see through solid objects to a range of **30 feet**. Within that range, you have **Blindsight**. You can use this feature once per long rest, unless you spend 3 Chakra for each subsequent use.
*   **Reflexive Analysis:** You gain proficiency in the **Perception** skill. If you are already proficient, you gain **Expertise** (doubling your proficiency bonus).

**The Whirlpool**

*   **Vigorous Casting:** Your chakra is so dense that it physically reinforces your body. Whenever you spend Chakra to cast a Jutsu of **C-Rank or higher**, you gain **Temporary Hit Points** equal to Your Constitution Modifier + The Jutsu's Chakra Cost.

**The Shadow**

*   **Split Shadow:** When you cast the Shadow Bind Jutsu, you can target **two** creatures instead of one, provided the targets are within 10 feet of each other. The chakra cost remains the same.

**The Prodigy**

*   **Polymath:** You gain **Expertise** in two skills of your choice in which you are proficient. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses either of the chosen skills.

## Level 6 - Tactical Recovery

*You have learned to center your breathing and stamina to recover quickly during downtime.*

*   **Chakra Recovery:** Anytime you spend a hit die to recover hit points you gain 1 Chakra per hit die spent

Level 10 - Improved Nature Affinty

**Primary Nature:** You gain more control over your Primary Nature and your  jutsu becomes more powerful. You gain a +1 to any Jutsu attack rolls and +1 to the Save DC of any Jutsu you cast from your Primary Nature.

**Secondary Nature:** You can choose any other Nature, or roll on the nature table (rerolling if you roll the same as your Primary) to be your Secondary, or secondary nature. You learn a D- Rank Jutsu from this nature, that does not count as one of your known jutsu

## Level 10 - Surface Walking

At 10th level, you master the art of focusing chakra into the soles of your feet to defy gravity and surface tension. You can move up, down, and across vertical surfaces and upside down along ceilings, as well as across liquids, without falling during the move. When you end your turn on a vertical surface, or upside down, you must spend 1 chakra to maintain the position or fall to the ground

## Level 11 - Advanced Scouting

At 10th level, your senses have become so attuned to the flow of chakra and the natural world that you can detect hidden threats and move unseen. You gain the following benefits:

*   **Chakra Sense:** As an **Action**, you can open your mind to detect the presence of chakra for 1 minute. For the duration, you know the location of any creature within **60 feet** of you that is not a Construct or Undead, even if the creature is hidden or invisible. You also detect the presence of magical objects or active spells/jutsu within the same range. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your **Intelligence modifier** (minimum of once), regaining all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.
*   **Traceless Step:** You leave no physical tracks, and you cannot be tracked by non-magical means unless you choose to leave a trail.
*   **Silent Signaling:** You can communicate simple ideas (such as "Stop," "Enemy Ahead," "Go," or "Help") to your allies using only hand signs and body language. Allies who can see you understand these signals automatically; other creatures must make an **Insight** check (DC equal to your Jutsu Save DC) to decipher them.

## Level 14 - Bloodline Mastery

Your genetic abilities have reached their mature state. You gain the feature corresponding to the **Bloodline** you chose at 1st Level.

**The Copy Eye**

**Susanoo Ribcage:**  You can manifest a spectral avatar of chakra to protect you from harm.When you take damage, you can use your **Reaction** and spend **5 Chakra** to reduce the damage by 5d10 + your Intelligence modifier. If the damage is reduced to 0, you suffer no secondary effects from the attack (such as being pushed or poisoned).

**The White Eye**

**Tenketsu Needle (Chakra Blocking):** You can inject chakra into an opponent's pressure points to shut down their body. When you hit a creature with an Unarmed Strike or a Shinobi Weapon, you can spend **3 Chakra** to force the target to make a **Constitution saving throw**. On a failed save, the target is **Stunned** until the end of your next turn. Additionally, whether they save or fail, they cannot take Reactions until the start of your next turn.

**The Whirlpool**

**Hereditary Fortitude:** You gain a bonus to your maximum hitpoint equal to the size of your Chakra pool

**The Shadow**

**Shadow Strangle:** You can crush the life out of those caught in your trap. As a Bonus Action, you can constrict the shadows around any creature currently Restrained by any of your bloodline jutsu. The target takes Necrotic damage equal to 3d8 + your Intelligence modifier. This damage does not break the hold or grant a new saving throw.

**The Prodigy**

**Perfect Recall:** Your understanding of chakra theory is absolute. You can cast a Jutsu from the Shinobi class list that you do not know. To do so, you must spend the standard Chakra cost of the Jutsu **plus 2 additional Chakra**. (e.g., Casting an unknown C-Rank Jutsu costs 3 + 2 = 5 Chakra).

**Mental Fortitude:** You gain proficiency in **Wisdom** saving throws.

## Level 17 - Persistent Nature

**Primary Nature:** The Bonus to your Jutsu Attack rolls and saving DC increases to 2 for All Jutsu tied to your Primary nature.

**Secondary Nature:** You add a +1 to your jutsu attack rolls and Save DC for your Secondary Nature

## Level 18 - Jonin Commander

At 18th level, your experience and efficiency in combat are second to none. You have mastered the art of weaving signs with minimal movement and blistering speed.

*   **One-Handed Seals**: You can perform the Somatic components (hand signs) of a Jutsu with only one free hand. This allows you to cast Jutsu while grappling a creature, holding an object, or wielding a weapon in your other hand.
*   **Swift Tactics**: You can alter the flow of your chakra to accelerate or delay the speed of your techniques to fit the situation.
    *   When you cast a Jutsu that has a casting time of 1 Action, you can change the casting time to 1 Bonus Action for this casting.
    *   When you cast a Jutsu that has a casting time of 1 Bonus Action, you can change the casting time to 1 Action for this casting.
    *   Cost: To utilize this speed change, you must spend 2 additional Chakra point on top of the Jutsu's standard cost.

Shock Cube

# Shock Cube

*Wondrous Item, uncommon*

**Description**

This cube deals 1 point of lightning damage to any creature that touches it, or starts its turn touching it. once it has dealt 20 lightning damage this way it loses this property for 1 hour.

Shock Hypercube

# Shock Hypercube

*Wondrous Item, legendary*

**Description**

This 3-inch cube crackles with electric potential energy, and resonates slightly when touched. Any creature that ends its turn touching the cube takes lightning damage equal to half their remaining hitpoints. Additionally, the creature cannot take reactions until the end of their next turn.

Once the cube has discharged, it cannot do so again until 1 hour has passed.

Silver Bow

# Silver Bow

*Weapon (Any Bow), Very Rare*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

Made of a mysterious yet flexible space-metal alloy, this bow gleams a radiant silver-white, like moonlight. The Silver Bow grants a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with it, and deals radiant damage instead of piercing damage.

While attuned to the Silver Bow, you gain the following properties:

**Sense Hidden**: While holding the Silver Bow, you have advantage on skill checks made to sense the presence of hidden creatures, shapechangers or creatures not in their true form.

**Unveiling**: As a bonus action, you can create a 60-foot-radius sphere around you that lasts for 1 minute, or until you lose your concentration (as if you were concentrating on a spell). Whenever a hidden creature, shapechanger or creature not in their true form enters the area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there, that creature must make a DC 17 Dexterity saving throw or glow with a faint light on a failure, gaining no benefit from being invisible until the end of its next turn. If the target is a creature in a form other than it's true form, you can also force it to make a DC 17 Charisma saving throw or revert to it's true form on a failure. You can use this feature a number of times equal to half your proficiency bonus, resetting on a long rest.

Siren’S Crescent

# Siren’s Crescent

*Weapon (scimitar), very rare (requires attunement)*

**Attunement:** Requires Attunement

**Description**

This finely crafted scimitar features a crescent-shaped blade adorned with intricate engravings. The blade is perforated with small holes and wind chambers, allowing it to emit a haunting, flute-like melody when swung or spun.

**Harmonic Transformation**: As a bonus action, you can transform the Siren’s Crescent into a musical instrument of your choice, provided you are proficient with that instrument. While using the transformed instrument, you have advantage on ability checks made to play it. You can revert the instrument back into the scimitar as a bonus action.

**Arcane Harmony:** If you can use a weapon as a spellcasting focus, the Siren’s Crescent grants a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls and to the saving throw DC of spells you cast through it.

**Weapon Bonuses:** You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls for melee weapon attacks made with this magic weapon.

[![Siren's Crescent](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/7/7c/Sirens_Crescent.png/revision/latest/scale-to-width-down/180?cb=20250129142847)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/7/7c/Sirens_Crescent.png/revision/latest?cb=20250129142847)

[](/docs/file:sirens-crescent.png)

Siren's Crescent

Skeleton (Race)

# Skeleton (Race)

## Description

*The reanimated corpse of a once living creature given new purpose in undeath. Often created by necromancers, sapient skeletons or other undead can arise when rituals go awry. Such skeletons walk in undeath for as long as they remain intact, often seeing the world undergo grand changes should they go undisturbed.*

## Language

You know common and one other language of your choice.

## Creature Type

One of the most famous examples of it, your creature type is undead.

## Size

Skeletons come in many shapes and sizes. Your size is medium or small.

## Speed

You have a walking speed of 30 feet.

## Frailty

Bludgeoning damage dealt to you is doubled.

## Undead Nature

Being undead sets you drastically apart from living creatures, granting you the following effects:

*   You are immune to poison damage, and the poisoned and exhaustion conditions.
*   You do not bleed. You succeed on death saving throws on a d20 roll of 5 or higher, rather than 10 or higher.
*   Nothing can cause you to add more than one failed death saving throw at once.
*   You do not need to eat, drink, sleep, or breathe.
*   If you die, you are destroyed, and cannot be returned to undeath via resurrection spells such as revivify or raise dead.

## Raised For War

You are proficient in light armor, medium armor, shields, and two weapons of your choice.

## Faded Knowledge

You gain one skill and one tool proficiency of your choice.

## Necromantic Healing

When you would take necrotic damage, you can choose to instead gain temporary hit points equal to the damage you would have taken. You can do so a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

Skeleton Surplus!

# Skeleton Surplus!

*9th Level Necromancy (Cleric, Warlock, Wizard)*

**Meta**

*   Casting Time: 1 Action
*   Range: 30 feet
*   Components: V, S
*   Duration: 1 minute

**Description**

One creature you can see within range becomes the target of an onslaught of ethereal skeletons. At the end of each of the target's turns, four skeletons immediately appear in the closest unoccupied spaces to the target and are hostile towards it.

The skeletons' attacks are considered magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunities, and last until either they or the target die, where they disappear leaving no trace. They cannot be commanded and will exclusively take actions harmful to the target, or to aid in harming the target.

If any skeletons remain when more are to be summoned, that many additional skeletons are summoned.

Skeleton Surprise

# Skeleton Surprise

*8th Level Necromancy (Cleric, Wizard, Warlock)*

**Casting Time:** 1 Action

**Range:** 60 feet

**Components**: N/A

**Duration**: Instantaneous

**Description**

When you cast this spell, target one creature you can see within range. 8d4 skeletons immediately appear in the closest unoccupied spaces to the target, and are hostile towards it. Alternatively, you can delay the appearance of the skeletons by up to an hour.

The skeletons' attacks are considered magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunities, and last until either they or the target die, where they disappear leaving no trace. They cannot be commanded and will exclusively take actions harmful to the target, or to aid in harming the target.

If a creature already under the effects of skeleton surprise is targeted, you instead change the time at which the skeletons appear, rather than causing more to appear. You may also change the time that the skeletons appear as an action, so long as the time you choose falls within one hour of the initial casting.

The casting of this spell does not require verbal or somatic components, hence the surprise.

Slug Shotgun

# Slug Shotgun

*Weapon( Blunderbuss, Scrapshot), rare*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

Using top-of-the-line polymorphic magic, a slug shotgun converts standard shells and ammunition into live slugs!

The weapon loses both the \[Point-Blank\] and \[Greater Point-Blank\] properties if it has them, and deals 1d4 extra acid damage, or 2d4 extra acid damage if the weapon had the \[Greater Point-Blank\] property.

Snowball Mittens

# Snowball Mittens

*Wondrous item, common*

**Description**

These cozy, knitted mittens have been enchanted to always bring a touch of winter with them. As an action while wearing the mittens, you can clamp your hands together, as if you were forming a snowball. When you open your hands again, a perfectly formed snowball appears in them. Any snow created in this way vanishes after 1 minute.

Solar Six Shooter

# Solar Six-Shooter

*Weapon (Pistol), legendary*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. Additionally, this weapon deals radiant damage instead of piercing damage.

This weapon has 6 charges. Once per turn, you can expend one charge when you attack with this weapon. If this attack hits, it deals 6d10 extra radiant damage, and the target must succeed on a DC18 constitution saving throw or be blinded until the end of it's next turn.

The weapon regains a single charge when it spends a full 8 hours in direct sunlight.

Sorcerer: Arcane Overflow

# Sorcerer: Arcane Overflow

Optional Class Feature (Replaces Sorcerous Restoration)

At 20th level, Arcane energy suffuses your body and abilities. Your maximum amount of sorcery points increases by 5, and you restore 3 sorcery points whenever you roll initiative if you have none remaining.

Additionally, you may now place two metamagic options on any spell you cast, granted that you still pay the sorcery point cost for each of them, even if the option normally would not allow you to.

Spellbreaker

# Spellbreaker

## Description

Bulwarks who spend their training bettering themselves against the ever-present threat of magic. Spellbreakers are the last thing a wizard wants to see on the field of battle, usually resulting in a fairly one sided encounter.

**Features:**

*   [3rd Level - Out of the Inferno](#3rd_Level_-_Out_of_the_Inferno)
*   [7th Level - Spell-Shatter](#7th_Level_-_Spell-Shatter)
*   [13th Level - Anticipation](#13th_Level_-_Anticipation)
*   [18th Level - Willbreaker](#18th_Level_-_Willbreaker)

## 3rd Level - Out of the Inferno

When you choose this specialization at 3rd level, you learn to incorporate methods to help you avoid magical effects into your stance. While your defensive stance is active, you also gain resistance to fire, cold, lightning, and acid damage.

## 7th Level - Spell-Shatter

Starting at 7th level, magical warding effects become inefficient at halting your progress. When you hit a creature or object with a weapon attack, all spells from the abjuration school immediately end if they are affecting the target.

## 13th Level - Anticipation

When you reach 13th level, you gain an understanding and fortitude against the ways that mages tend to fight. When you enter your defensive stance, you automatically succeed on the next intelligence, wisdom, or charisma saving throw you make before it ends.

## 18th Level - Willbreaker

At 18th level, your magical defenses allow you to nearly completely ignore the effects of lesser magic, making your foes need to drain their resources to even hope to defeat you. You are immune to the effects of cantrips and 1st and 2nd level spells, unless you wish to be affected by them.

Spiked Cestus

# Spiked Cestus

*Weapon: Simple, Melee*

1gp, 1.5 lbs. --- 1d4 piercing - light, two-handed

**Description**

Proficiency with a cestus allows you to add your proficiency bonus to the attack roll for any attack you make with it.

**Special**: As a bonus action, you can make one attack with this weapon.

Spirit Ejection

# Spirit Ejection

*5th-level necromancy. (Warlock, Wizard, Sorcerer, Death Domain, Grave Domain, Oathbreaker, College of Spirits)*

**Meta**

**Casting Time**: 1 action

**Range**: 30 feet

**Components**: V, S, M (a pinch of bone dust, and a tarnished silver chain)

**Duration**: Concentration, up to 1 minute

**Description**

You attempt to forcefully tear the spirit of a creature from its body, leaving it momentarily defenseless. Choose one humanoid creature within range (creatures under the effects of a spell, feature or magic item that wards them against Undead or possession cannot have their spirit ejected by this spell). The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, their spirit is ejected from their body and manifests as a spectral form within 10 feet of them. The target’s body becomes incapacitated, and has disadvantage on saving throws against effects that would possess them. The spirit resembles their mortal form in almost every way, replicating game statistics but not their possessions. Any damage or other effects that apply to your spirit or physical body affects the other. In addition, the spirit is incorporeal, resistant to all damage except psychic or radiant, and it must choose whether it gets a move, an action, or a bonus action on a turn; it gets only one of the three. The spirit is tethered to the body and cannot move more than 60 feet away from it.

At the start of each of the target’s turns, they can attempt a Wisdom saving throw to end the effect early. If they succeed, their spirit returns to their body, and they regain control at the start of their next turn.

If the body is reduced to 0 hit points while the spirit is still ejected, the creature dies instantly. If killed in this way, its spirit detaches from its corpse as a Specter under your control, the statistics of which are in the Monster Manual.

You can mentally command the Specter or any creature you made with this spell if the creature is within 60 feet of you (if you control multiple creatures, you can command any or all of them at the same time, issuing the same command to each one), and in combat the Specter shares your initiative count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours.

The Specter remains in your service for up to 10 days, at which point it vanishes to the afterlife. While the Specter is in your service, its soul is trapped and cannot be revived. A Remove Curse, Dispel Evil and Good, or Wish spell ends this spells effect.

At Higher Levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th or 7th level or higher, the target has disadvantage on its initial saving throw. At 8th or 9th level, the target instead rises as a Poltergeist if killed.

Spirit Ward

# Spirit Ward

*5th-level necromancy. (Warlock, Wizard, Sorcerer, Death Domain, Grave Domain, Oathbreaker, College of Spirits)*

**Meta**

**Casting Time**: 1 action

**Range**: Touch

**Components**: V, S, M (a silver chain mesh worth at least 1,500+ GP)

**Duration**: 10 days

**Description**

Weaving a web of necromantic energies around you or another, you are protected against influences upon your soul or animating spirit. You touch a living humanoid or a corpse. For the duration, the target has advantage on death saving throws, and is protected from spells and effects that would allow their soul, spirit or body to be queried, trapped, possessed or manipulated, such as Soul Cage, Speak with Dead, Magic Jar, Spirit Ejection or other related effects.

If the humanoid or corpse is targeted by such an effect, it automatically fails and the enemy takes 4d10 psychic damage, as the Ward lashes out to protect them.

At Higher Levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, the duration is increased by 5 days and the psychic damage is increased by 1d10 for each level beyond 5th.

Spiritualist

# Spiritualist

## Spiritualist

*Spreading a deck of cards out on a table, a fortune teller smiles at her guest, flipping three of the cards over one by one. With a silky voice and a flourished hand, she speaks of events that have transpired, events that weigh heavy on her guest’s mind, and of events yet to come.*

*Facing down a malevolent spirit, a man in clergical robes watches as it takes control of his companion’s movements. The man thrusts his spirit out towards his ally, wrenching the ghost’s grip away from her mind and allowing her the opportunity to cut the evil being down with a radiant blade.*

*Standing amidst candles and blood, a medium speaks words into the realm beyond life, listening for a response. The soul of an elder, long deceased, pools into existence next to him, giving him advice and information on how to defeat his greatest foe.*

*Regardless of the profession they take up, spiritualists utilize the power of their spirit to influence others, both in this world and the next. Whether they specialize in speaking to those long past, hunting down evil wherever it may rear its head, or taking subtle glances into the future to attempt to shape fate, these masters of souls seek to strengthen their ties to the world, both material and ethereal.*

### Earthly Ambitions

*Spiritualists are masters of their own spirits. Similar to monks that seek spiritual peace, spiritualists spend their lives dedicated to cultivating their souls through exercises and manipulation. Unlike monks, who care little for wealth or power, many spiritualists are driven by earthy goals and ambitions.*

*Some spiritualists cultivate their abilities to seek vengeance for being wronged, using their spiritual power to further their own agendas. Some have seen a grim future for themselves, and stop at nothing to prevent seeing that future come to pass. Others still are in the profession for material gain, using the opportunities from reading fortunes and hunting evil to fill their coffers with gold. Adventuring is just another means to an end for many of these individuals, often proving to be a quick (yet perilous) path to power.*

### *Mastery Over Spirit*

Constant training and practice allows a spiritualist to use their souls to manipulate the material plane, offering powerful results. The majority of them draw power from the ethereal plane, manipulating the borders between this world and that to gain strange and unconventional abilities. By linking their souls to entities in the material plane, they can influence such entities in a variety of ways, both beneficially and detrimentally. As they learn to control their spirit more effectively, a spiritualist can exploit and influence the world around them in peculiar ways.

### Creating a Spiritualist

*As you make a spiritualist, think of what drives you to cultivate your soul and gain power. Are you trying to get revenge on an evil spirit that hurt your friends or family? Do you want to vanquish anything you percieve as evil on your own terms, without the need to be tied down to oath or order? Do you want to make a fortune as a famed seer, that thousands flock towards to find out what lies ahead for their futures? Your motivations may have a big part in deciding the profession you eventually select for yourself.*

*Besides the things that drive you, it’s also important to decide what extent you’ll go to achieve your ambitions, as well as the methods you might take. Are you willing to make necessary sacrifices to come out ahead? Will you find allies to surround yourself with, and rely on them to assist you with your goals? Maybe you put more of your faith with the dead, and look to them for answers to your problems.*

#### Quick Build

You can make a spiritualist quickly by following these suggestions. First, Wisdom should be your highest ability score, followed by Dexterity or Constitution. Second, choose the Haunted One background.

## Class Features

As a spiritualist, you gain the following class features.

### Hit Points

**Hit Dice:**

1d6 per spiritualist level

**Hit Points at 1st Level:**

6 + your Constitution modifier

**Hit Points at Higher Levels:**

1d6 (or 4) + your Constitution modifier per spiritualist level after 1st

### Proficiencies

**Armor:**

Light armor

**Weapons:**

Daggers, darts, slings, quarterstaffs, light crossbows

**Tools:**

none

**Saving Throws:**

Wisdom, Charisma

**Skills:**

Choose two from Arcana, Deception, Insight, Investigation, Perception, Persuasion, Religion, and Sleight of Hand.

#### Equipment

You start with the following equipment, in addition to the equipment granted by your background:

*   *(a)* a dagger or *(b)* a quarterstaff
*   *(a)* a light crossbow and 20 bolts or *(b)* a sling with 20 bullets
*   *(a)* a scholar’s pack or *(b)* an explorer’s pack
*   leather armor and a spiritual focus

##### The Spiritualist Class Table

|  | Proficiency |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Level | Bonus | Spirit Points | Features |
| 1st | +2 | \- | Spirit Tether, Soul Sense |
| 2nd | +2 | 4 | Spirit Arts, Soul Exertion |
| 3rd | +2 | 6 | Spiritual Profession |
| 4th | +2 | 8 | Ability Score Improvement |
| 5th | +3 | 10 | Otherworldly Tenacity |
| 6th | +3 | 12 | Profession Feature |
| 7th | +3 | 14 | Twin Spirit |
| 8th | +3 | 16 | Ability Score Improvement |
| 9th | +4 | 18 | Advanced Spirit Arts |
| 10th | +4 | 20 | Improved Spirit Jolt |
| 11th | +4 | 22 | Heightened Spirit Arts |
| 12th | +4 | 24 | Ability Score Improvement |
| 13th | +5 | 26 | Profession Feature |
| 14th | +5 | 28 | Master Spirit Arts |
| 15th | +5 | 30 | Ether Walker |
| 16th | +5 | 32 | Ability Score Improvement |
| 17th | +6 | 34 | Soul Trinity |
| 18th | +6 | 36 | Profession Feature |
| 19th | +6 | 38 | Ability Score Improvement |
| 20th | +6 | 40 | Soulcast |

### Spirit Tether

At 1st level, you learn to project your own spirit outwards, allowing it to manipulate the material plane. This tether represents your soul’s influence and will be the baseline for your spiritualist abilities.

#### Spiritual Focus

You employ unorthodox tools through which to channel your spirit. You must have a spiritual focus to use your spirit tether, which can take the form of the following objects: a candle, crystal ball, skull, spirit board, or tarokka deck. Alternatively, you may choose a different object you and your DM agree on.

#### Activating the Spirit Tether

As a bonus action, you can use your spiritual focus to choose a creature or object other than yourself you can see within 120 feet to latch an invisible spirit rope onto. A creature that can see invisible objects sees a thin, spectral thread directly connecting you to the target. The tether exists in the ethereal plane, and is blocked by any effect that prevents ethereal travel.

You must concentrate to maintain the tether as if concentrating on a spell. The spirit rope breaks if you lose concentration, if you are ever more than 120 feet from the target, if it’s blocked in the ethereal plane, if you create another spirit tether, or if you end the connection as a bonus action.

When you create your tether or as a bonus action on your turn, you can deal 1d4 force damage to the target. The damage increases when you reach certain levels in this class, increasing to 2d4 at 5th level, 3d4 at 11th level, and 4d4 at 17th level.

While you’re tethered to a creature, you can communicate with it telepathically. As long as the creature can understand language, it understands what you communicate even if you don’t share a language and vice versa.

### Soul Sense

Also at 1st level, you have a heightened sensitivity to spirits and souls in your area. As an action, you can sense the general location of any souls within 30 feet of you, including creatures or objects that contain souls. You learn the direction as well as the quantity of the souls you sense, but cannot determine any other properties. This special vision can see through most barriers, but it is blocked by 1 foot of stone, 1 inch of common metal, a thin sheet of lead, or 3 feet of wood or dirt. This effect lasts for one minute and can only be used again after a short or long rest.

### Spirit Arts

At 2nd level, you can manipulate your spirit tether to accomplish feats unrestrained by your mortal body. The extent to which you can manipulate your spirit without exerting yourself is represented by a number of spirit points.

You can spend these spirit points on various spirit arts, which are detailed below. When you spend a spirit point, it is unavailable until you finish a long rest, upon which your spirit is revitalized enough to be utilized further. You can never have more spirit points than shown on the table for your level. Spirit arts can only ever target creatures and objects your spirit tether is connected to. If your spirit tether breaks, the effect of any spirit arts are immediately ended.

Some spirit arts require your target to make a saving throw to resist the art’s effects. The saving throw DC is calculated as follows:

**Art save DC** = 8 + your Proficiency Bonus + your Wisdom modifier

#### Corpus Enforcement

When your tethered target deals damage to you, you can spend 1 spirit point to halve the damage you take.

#### Spirit Jolt

As an action, you can spend up to 3 spirit points (min. 1) and force the target to make a Charisma saving throw. The target takes 3d6 force damage for each point you spend on a failed save, and half as much on a successful one.

#### Spirit Resonance

As an action, you can spend 1 spirit point to strengthen the tether between yourself and the tethered target. For one minute, both you and the target can add 1d4 to each saving throw or ability check you make.

#### Corrupt Spirit

As an action, you can spend 1 spirit point to corrupt the tethered target. It must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, the target must roll a d4 and subtract the amount from each ability check, attack roll, or saving throw it makes until the end of your next turn.

#### Spirit Influence

If the tethered target is a creature, you can use an action to spend 1 spirit point and make the target more malleable to influence. For one minute, your checks made against the target have advantage.

### Soul Exertion

At 2nd level, you can exert your spirit past its normal limits with some effort. When you finish a short rest, you can spend any number of hit dice. Rather than regaining hit points, you regain an amount of spirit points equal to the amount you spent.

### Spiritual Profession

At 3rd level, you select a profession to undertake that decides the nature of how you use your spiritual abilities. Your profession grants you features at 3rd level and then again at 6th, 13th, and 18th level.

### Ability Score Improvement

When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can’t increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

### Otherworldly Tenacity

At 5th level, if your concentration is broken and you’re not incapacitated, you can spend 1 spirit point to maintain concentration.

### Twin Spirit

At 7th level, when you create a spirit tether, you can target an additional target within range. Whenever you use a spirit art, you can decide if it targets one or both of the targets. If you target multiple creatures or objects with a spirit art, you only have to spend spirit points once. If the tether breaks for a target, only the spirit arts applied to that tether are ended.

### Advanced Spirit Arts

At 9th level, you further hone your spirit arts, allowing you to access more refined techniques. You have access to the following spirit arts:

#### Soul Blight

If your tethered target is a creature, you can use an action and spend 2 spirit points to attempt to taint them. The target must make a constitution saving throw or take 5d6 poison damage at the start of each of their turns. The creature can use an action to purge the blight from their body, ending this spirit art.

#### Tether Swap

As an action, you can spend 2 spirit points to force the target to make a Charisma save (the target may choose to fail). On a failure, you switch places with the target. If the target is too large to fit in your space or vice versa, the art fails and your spirit points remain unspent.

#### Panic

As an action, you can spend 2 spirit points to force the target to make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, the target is frightened. While frightened, the target must use all of its movement at the start of its turn to move away from you unless there is nowhere else to move. The target can make a Wisdom saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending this spirit art on a success.

### Improved Spirit Jolt

At 10th level, your spirit jolt strengthens in power. Its damage increases to 4d6 force damage for each point you spend.

### Heightened Spirit Arts

Once per turn starting at 11th level, when you use a spirit art that takes an action, you can immediately use a different spirit art that also requires an action. You expend spirit points from both arts.

### Master Spirit Arts

At 14th level, you master control over your spirit, culminating in powerful spirit arts.

#### Spirit Beyond Form

If the tethered target would reduce you to 0 hit points, you can spend 3 spirit points as a reaction to drop to 1 hit point instead.

#### Soul Surge

If your tethered target is a creature, you can use your action to spend 3 spirit points and strengthen their soul. During their next turn, they may take an extra action. That action can be used only to take the Attack (one weapon attack only), Dash, Disengage, Hide, or Use an Object action.

#### Oversoul Burst

If your tethered target is reduced to zero hit points, you can spend 3 spirit points to detonate your soul from its location. Each creature within 10 feet of the target must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, the target takes 8d6 force damage, taking half that damage on a success.

### Ether Walker

At 15th level, your spirit can slip into the ethereal without trouble. You can cast the etherealness spell on yourself without expending a spell slot. Once you do so, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest or spend 5 spirit points to cast it again.

### Soul Trinity

At 17th level, you can split your soul tether even further. You can now target up to three creatures or objects within range when you use your soul tether. Whenever you use a spirit art, you can choose which targets are affected by it.

### Soulcast

At 20th level, you fully master control over your spirit. When you use your soul tether, you can cast your soul out to instantaneously tether to any willing creature on the same plane as yourself. When using your tether in this way, it doesn’t break as a result of being out of range. A creature tethered in this manner can use an action to break the tether at will.

Spy Lord’S Scepter

# Spy Lord’s Scepter

*Weapon (rod), legendary*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

A sleek scepter of darkened mithril and carved obsidian gemstone, fitted with an elongated hilt and stylized silver in the shape of winding serpents and veiled masks. The shaft tapers into a ritual blade sheathed flush into the bottom of the rod. To all appearances, it is a ceremonial staff unless drawn and wielded with purpose.

While attuned to this item, you can use an action to cast the Message or Detect Thoughts spells (save DC 20). You may also use it as a spellcasting focus gaining the following benefits:

**Spellcasting Charges:**

The rod has 10 charges. It regains 1d6+4 expended charges daily at dusk.

You may expend charges to cast the following spells (save DC 20), not requiring material components:

Spell : Charges

*   Dissonant Whispers (5th level): 2     
*   Fast Friends: 2
*   Mislead: 3
*   Modify Memory: 4

**Hidden Blade.**

When unsheathed, the scepter functions as a +2 magical shortsword. While concealed, a creature can make a DC 20 Intelligence (Investigation) or Wisdom (Perception) check to identify its true nature.

While attuned to this item, when you damage a creature with an attack using this magic weapon, the target can't regain hit points and has disadvantage on spells cast from this scepter until the start of your next turn. If the attack is a critical hit, the target's Hit Point maximum also decreases by an amount equal to that damage. If this reduces their Hit Point maximum to 0, the target dies and its soul is trapped in the scepters gem, as if placed under the Soul Cage spell. Only one soul can be trapped at a time.

Staff Of Elemental Harmony

# Staff of Elemental Harmony

*Weapon (quarterstaff), rare*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement by a monk

**Description**

This finely crafted quarterstaff is made of a smooth, polished wood and bears intricate carvings depicting swirling elemental patterns along its length. This quarterstaff has 10 elemental charges that recharges at dawn, and it grants its wielder the following abilities while they are attuned to the staff:

*   **Elemental Strikes**: When you hit a creature with this staff, you can choose to imbue the attack with elemental energy. The staff becomes charged with one of the following elements: fire, lightning, or cold. The target must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC 13) or take an additional 1d8 damage of the chosen element. This feature consumes an elemental charge.
*   **Elemental Focus**: You gain a +1 bonus to your attack and damage rolls made with it.
*   **Elemental Absorption**: As a reaction when you take damage from a spell that deals fire, lightning, or cold damage, you can use your staff to absorb some of the energy. You gain resistance to the triggering damage type until the start of your next turn. This feature consumes an elemental charge.

Star Blade

# Star Blade

## Description

You have spent many a night training under the stars and became touched by cosmic energy to shape light as you see fit. Creating armaments of pure light and radiance you become a beacon for those lost in the dark.

**Features:**

*   [3rd Level - Hardlight](#3rd_Level_-_Hardlight)
*   [One With the Stars](#7th_Level_-_One_With_the_Stars)
*   [Astral Multitool](#7th_Level_-_Astral_Multitool)
*   [Shooting Stars](#10th_Level_-_Shooting_Stars)
*   [Galactic Aegis](#15th_Level_-_Galactic_Aegis)
*   [Stellar Expanse](#18th_Level_-_Stellar_Expanse)

## 3rd Level - Hardlight

3rd-Level Feature

At 3rd level, you gain an amount of Hardlight charges equal to your double proficiency bonus, resetting on a long rest. You can use one charge to conjure a mundane melee weapon you are proficient with out of radiant light. This weapon does radiant damage, and counts as a magical weapon. Alternatively, you can spend one charge to conjure up an instrument or tool you have proficiency with. Equipment conjured up using this feature lasts an hour.

At 15th level this changes from 1 hours to 8 hours.

## 7th Level - One With the Stars

7th-Level Feature

At 7th level, you gain a bonus to damage and attack rolls and ability checks with Hardlight equipment equal to half your Wisdom modifier.

## 7th Level - Astral Multitool

7th-Level Feature

At 7th level, you can spend a bonus action to change your Hardlight equipment into another piece of equipment. You can use this feature once per short rest.

## 10th Level - Shooting Stars

10th-Level Feature

Starting at 10th level, your mastery over hardlight weapons has granted you the ability to create ranged weapons. Creating a ranged weapons costs 2 hardlight and a bonus action to create. The ammunition it uses looks like a shooting star as you fire the weapons, and does not consume a hardlight charge.

## 15th Level - Galactic Aegis

15th-Level Feature

At 15th level, you can spend one Hardlight charge to create Hardlight armor. Hardlight armor counts as magical heavy armor, and has a base AC of 10 + your Dexterity modifier+ your Wisdom modifier. If a creature other than yourself wears this armor, it loses its properties.

Additionally, you can spend one Hardlight charge to create a Hardlight shield. This shield counts as a magical shield that provides 3/4 cover for yourself, this shield does not provide the base AC of a shield. If a creature other than yourself equips this shield, it loses its properties.

## 18th Level - Stellar Expanse

18th-Level Feature

At 18th level, you can spend 2 Hardlight charges to empower yourself for one minute. Whenever you hit a creature with a Hardlight weapon, the weapon does an additional 3d6 radiant damage. While you have a Hardlight shield equipped, if an enemy misses you with a weapon attack, you can use your reaction to gain advantage on all attack rolls against them until the end of your next turn.

Additionally, you can maintain one piece of Hardlight equipment indefinitely. While this equipment exists, your total amount of Hardlight charges is reduced by one until it is dismissed.

Star Gels

# Star Gels

*Wondrous Item, uncommon*

**Description**

With the adhesive property of your typical ooze and the florescent spores of certain mushrooms, these special gels can be attached to the walls or ceilings of rooms. Under dim light or darkness, they glow a light-tinted green providing dim light at a 15 ft radius. The gels, mimicking the stars, can also inspire a viewer and calm their nerves via the release of sweet-scented spores. If a creature spends a short rest in a room full of gels (gazing upon them), they gain 1d8 temp hp that wears off by their next LR. Once this property is used, it cannot be used again by any creature till the next evening, when the real stars first emerge.

Star Spawn

# Star Spawn

## Description

*Star Spawn are created emissaries for various outer beings, they present themselves as somewhat humanoid with some “oddities” associated with the eldritch being that sent them.*

## **Ability Score Increase**

When determining your character’s ability scores, increase one score by 2 and increase a different score by 1, or increase three different scores by 1. You can't raise any of your scores above 20.

## **Creature Type**

You are an aberration.

## **Size**

Your size is small or medium (your choice)

## **Speed**

Your walking speed is 30ft.

## **Languages**

You speak Common, and Deep Speech.

## **Unsettling Physiology**

Your body possesses an unnatural resilience and autonomy. You do not need to breathe, eat, drink, or sleep. Magic cannot force you to sleep. To gain the benefits of a long rest, you must spend 8 hours in a motionless state.

## **Eldritch Preservation**

When you are forced to make an Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma saving throw, you can call upon the unnatural resilience granted by your eldritch connection, granting yourself advantage on that saving throw (no action required). You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus. You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

## **See Beyond**

You commune with the entity that guides you. You can cast the spell Augury once per day, requiring no material components. The spell otherwise functions as normal.

## **Far Traveled**

Due to your nature you are more resistant to things that would terrify others, you have advantage on saving throws against being frightened.

## **Gifts From Beyond**

You gain proficiency in a skill or tool of your choice.

Storm Chaser

# Storm Chaser

*Storm chasers are a select few drifters along the astral plane, seeking out natural and magical phenomena to bolster their own power. While primarily existing on the astral plane, a storm chaser can be just as effective on the material plane, or anywhere a storm can be found, as they can make good use of bows and crossbows, in addition to the firearms traditionally used while on the astral plane.*

## Storm Chaser Magic

Starting at 3rd level, you learn an additional spell when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown in the Storm Chaser Spells table. The spell counts as a ranger spell for you, but it doesn’t count against the number of ranger spells you know.


Storm Chaser Spells
Ranger LevelSpells
3rdThunderwave
5thVortex Warp
9thCall Lightning
13thIce Storm
17thControl Winds


## Firearm Proficiency

At 3rd Level, you have learned to harness the potency of firearms to complement the powers of the storms you chase. You gain proficiency with firearms.

## Thundercrack

Starting at 3rd level, when you take this archetype at 3rd level, you are able to unleash a deafening boom when you use your ranged weapons. Once on each of your turns, when you attack with a ranged weapon, you can force each creature within 10 feet of you to make a constitution saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the creature takes 1d8 thunder damage. The damage increases to 2d8 at 11th level.

## Charged Shell

At 7th level, when you take the attack action, you can forgo your extra attack feature to make a single ranged weapon attack. This attack deals triple the damage dice of the weapon you used, and deals lightning damage instead of bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing. This attack is also critical on a d20 roll of 19 or 20 if used during a storm.

## Storm Caller

At 11th level, you have learned to bring storms to you. As an action, you call forth a vortex at a point you can see within 60 feet. The vortex is a cylinder with a radius of 30 feet, and a height of 90 feet. The vortex counts as a storm for the purposes of Charged Shell.

As a bonus action on each of your turns while the vortex lasts, you can target a creature inside, striking them with a bolt of lightning. The target must make a dexterity saving throw, taking 3d10 lightning on a failure, or half as much on a successful save.

The vortex lasts for 10 minutes, and once used, cannot be summoned again until you finish a long rest, or spend a spell slot of at least 3rd level or higher to summon it again. No more than 1 vortex can exist at once.

## Eye of the Storm

At 15th level, you gain resistance to lightning and thunder damage.

Additionally, you can target yourself with the bolt of lightning from your storm caller ability. If you do so, instead of taking damage, your next charged shell attack deals extra damage equal to twice the amount of damage the lightning would have dealt. This also causes the attack to be critical on a d20 roll of 18, 19, or 20.

Sub Ninth Rifle

# Sub-Ninth Rifle

*weapon (Precision Rifle), legendary, increased range (1200ft)*

**Attunement**

Requires attunement by a creature with a Strength and Dexterity score 19 or higher

**Description**
