This large sniper rifle is made with thicker material to propel projectiles at very long distances without losing accuracy, delivering destructive capabilities that can even pierce metal gates. Each shot made with this rifle sends a loud ripple on the battlefield that would bring dread whether or not they will be the next target. The obvious drawback to this rifle is its size and weight, making it difficult to move while in combat, as well as its noise, making it not really ideal for assassinations unless you are far enough, thus only a few choice marksmen challenge themselves in wielding this weapon. The creator explained that the reason for its name is because he " . . . wanted to make a weapon that can match the power of 9th-level spells" but it has yet to be seen in the results.

On a hit, this weapon deals 14d12 instead of 2d12. While equipped or simply on your body, your movement speed is reduced to a maximum of 10 feet. Each attack made with this weapon produces an explosive boom that is audible within 600 feet (the creator didn't factor in a silencer when making this weapon, thus trying to apply one would be ill-advised as it would just blow up at the first shot). This rifle deals maximum damage when you hit structures or objects that are not worn.

Special - An enhanced spyglass is mounted to the weapon. While using it, creatures and objects are magnified to four times their size.

This precision rifle can't be reloaded usign a bonus action

Sundisk

# Sundisk

*Wondrous Item, very rare*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

The Sundisk is a magical throwning weapon used to destroy hordes of enemies. The Sundisk has 5 charges. As an action, you can expend a charge to throw it in a line 30 feet long and 5 feet wide. All creatures in that area must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw with a DC of 15 + your dexterity modifier, taking 3d6 radiant damage on a failed save.

Once the disk reaches its maximum range of 30 feet, it remains in that space. You can use a bonus action to make the Sundisk return to you along the shortest path possible. All creatures in the area of the return path must repeat the same saving throw, taking 6d6 radiant damage on a failed save.

The Sundisk returns to you on your next turn even if you had moved since throwing the disk. All expended charges are regained at dawn when the sun's rays touch the disk.

Once the Sundisk have expended all charges, it can act as a magic boomerang.

Sunlight Globe

# Sunlight Globe

*Wondrous Item, uncommon*

**Description**

This small sphere of thick glass weighs 1 pound. If you are within 60 feet of it, you can speak one of two command words and cause it to emanate 60ft radius of daylight.

When activated, it will start dim and grow to match the time of day over the first hour.

Once used, the daylight effect can't be used again until the next dawn.The light follows a set pattern, granting 12 hours of daylight before dimming to dim light or no light (determined by the command word spoken) for 12 hours, then repeating.

You can speak another command word as an action to make the illuminated globe rise into the air and float no more than 5 feet off the ground. The globe hovers in this way until you or another creature grasps it.

Survivalist'S Wayfinder

# Survivalist's Wayfinder

[![The-griffons-saddlebag-survivalists-wayfinder-wondrous-item-v0-toa3ir24qmff1](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/9/93/The-griffons-saddlebag-survivalists-wayfinder-wondrous-item-v0-toa3ir24qmff1.png/revision/latest/scale-to-width-down/180?cb=20250818144708)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/9/93/The-griffons-saddlebag-survivalists-wayfinder-wondrous-item-v0-toa3ir24qmff1.png/revision/latest?cb=20250818144708)

[](/docs/file:the-griffons-saddlebag-survivalists-wayfinder-wondrous-item-v0-toa3ir24qmff1.png)

*Wondrous item, uncommon*

**Description**

This handheld device has four crystaline buttons that are each associated with a different element. The device has 4 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn. While holding the device, you can use an action to expend 1 of its charges and press a button. Each button causes the device to swivel and point like a compass to the nearest relevant location, as determined by the button, for 1 hour or until another button is pressed. If there's nothing for the device to point to, it spins in place for the duration or until it's within range of something to point to.

Air. If you're inside or underground, the device points to the nearest outdoor exit within 1 mile of you. If you're outside, it points in the direction of the nearest storm within 50 miles of you.

Earth. The device points to the nearest cave entrance that's Medium or larger and within 1 mile of you. If you're underground, it points in the direction of the nearest unpolished gemstone within 100 feet of you.

Fire. The device points to the nearest active campfire, firepit, forge, or hearth that's within 1 mile of you.

Water. The device points to the nearest source of fresh, drinkable water that's within 1 mile of you.

Swift Action

# Swift Action

**Prerequisite**

Dexterity 15 or higher

**Description**

Your reflexes show moments of great ability as a result of your training. You have the following benefits:

*   When you attack for the first time on your turn, you can have advantage on the attack roll. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
*   When your speed reaches to zero due to using it all or from an effect, you can use your reaction to gain an extra 10 feet to your speed. You can't use this reaction if the effect inflicted you with the grappled or restrained condition.

Swindler

# Swindler

## Description

*Cheaters go by many names; swindlers, con-artists, charlatans. Whatever they may call you, whoever you might be perceived as, you’ll always be the first to let the world know that the cards in your hand are there entirely legitimately— you couldn’t have stacked the deck, the dealer shuffled beforehand! You weren’t responsible for the incomprehensibly lucky die rolls, either— you were borrowing the dealer’s dice, after all. Oh, and the drinking game you outlasted the dwarf barbarian in? You couldn’t have watered your drink down, the dealer poured it out for you!*

*…What? You’re being accused of paying off the dealer? Poppycock.*

Features:

*   [3rd Level - Swindler’s Luck](#3rd_Level_-_Swindler’s_Luck)
*   [9th Level - Loaded Dice](#9th_Level_-_Loaded_Dice)
*   [13th Level - Sure Shot](#13th_Level_-_Sure_Shot)
*   [17th Level - Don of Con](#17th_Level_-_Don_of_Con)

## 3rd Level - Swindler’s Luck

At 3rd level, luck seems to appear whenever it benefits you, almost as if you’re cheating. Whenever you make a roll with advantage, you may meddle with the roll, forgoing that advantage, and roll normally. The next time you roll without advantage, you roll with advantage.

## 9th Level - Loaded Dice

At 9th level, you ~cheat~  act with more finesse, giving you more control over your actions. Whenever you make a roll with advantage, you may forgo that advantage to create a loaded die. You may store a number of loaded dice equal to half your proficiency bonus, rounded down. These loaded dice disappear on a short or long rest.

Whenever you roll an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, you may spend one of your loaded dice to give yourself advantage on that roll. You may only spend one loaded die per turn.

## 13th Level - Sure Shot

By 13th level, your “luck” is far too consistent to be inconspicuous. When you roll with advantage, you may forgo that advantage to add +3 to the roll’s total. If the roll succeeds, you gain one loaded die.

## 17th Level - Don of Con

At 17th level, it’s quite clear you’ve been cheating all this time, some way, somehow. On the bright side, you don’t have to hide it anymore— whenever you miss with an attack roll, you can spend 3 loaded dice to turn that miss into a hit. When you fail a saving throw, you can spend 2 loaded dice to succeed automatically. When you fail an ability check, you can spend 1 loaded die to make the result 20.

Sword Mastery

# Sword Mastery

**Prerequisite**

Proficiency with at least one type of sword

**Description**

You have become adept at using swords and can maneuver them effectively as a result.

*   When you wield a sword, it gains the finesse property if it does not already have it and lacks the heavy property.
*   Additionally, when a creature misses you with a melee attack while you are wielding a sword, you can use your reaction to make one attack with the sword against that creature.

Swords: Short Sword, Scimitar, Rapier, Longsword, Greatsword

Sword Saint

# Sword Saint

## Sword Saint

*Though colloquially referred to as “Sword Saints”, swords are only the most popular weapon they are seen mastering, not the only weapon. These practitioners devote themselves to training with a specific weapon of their choosing, rarely ever making use of other armaments.*

### Weapon Pledge

At 2nd level, choose a weapon you are proficient in to pledge your devotion to (ex. longsword, shortbow, glaive, etc). When wielding the a weapon, the following features are made available to you.

#### Flying Weapon

You learn how to wield the weapon without needing to even touch it. You can use a bonus action to move the weapon up to 30 feet in any direction or teleport it back to your hand instantly. You can attack and use techniques with the weapon as if you were standing in the weapon’s space.

While the weapon is being controlled in this way, it is treated as if you were still holding it (you require a free hand to attack with it, or two hands if it’s two-handed, etc). You cannot move it more than 120 feet away from yourself, and if brought outside of this range, you cannot control it further until it’s within the range.

#### Draining Strike

If you kill a hostile creature with an attack made with your flying weapon, you can use a reaction to drain the creature, regaining mastery points equal to half your maximum (rounded up). After you use this feature, you must take a short or long rest before you can use it again.

### Weapon Devotee

At 6th level, you become increasingly adept at using your weapon for techniques. In the same bonus action that you use to move the weapon, you can spend 1 mastery point to use the primary effect of a technique you know. Beginning at 14th level, you can focus the technique you use.

### Weapon Mount

At 14th level, your control over the weapon you devoted yourself to extends even to being able to ride it. Your weapon can be ridden in the air, during which it cannot be used for techniques. It has a flying speed equal to your movement speed plus an additional 30 feet and can carry up to 500 pounds before it can no longer fly.

### Armamentarium

At 18th level, you can control two flying weapons at once, with the second not being treated as if you were holding it. If you use a bonus action to use a technique with one, the other can make a single weapon attack.

Teleportation Codex

# Teleportation Codex

*Wondrous Item, Very Rare*

**Attunement**

Requires attunement

**Description**

In this tome is inscribed 2 teleportation circles that come with it(DM discretion on which ones). Throughout your travels as you study and find new Teleportation Circles you can take 1 minute to write them in this tome in order to be able to reference them later. This book has 3 charges regaining all expended charges each day at dawn. You can use these charges to either use Teleport(1charge) or Teleportation Circle(2 charges) using any circles you have inscribed inside the codex. Flip the book to the page with the Teleportation Circle you wish to go and speak the command word.

Temperature Controlled Lining

# Temperature Controlled Lining

*Wondrous Item, rare*

**Description**

This jar of mysterious purplish viscous goo was discovered to have exceptional insulative properties and can resist temperature changes for an extremely long time, it is often imbued with a little bit of magic to allow the user to alter the temperature of it like a cooling/heating unit

Over the course of 10 minutes, a creature can apply this goo to any armor or clothing, the goo doesn't impact movement or agility at all, and after application, any creature wearing the armor or clothing ignores the effect of extreme heat and extreme cold

Tenser'S Unbound Transformation

# Tenser's Unbound Transformation

*7th-level transmutation. (Wizard, Sorcerer, Warlock, College of Swords, College of Valor)*

**Meta**

**Casting Time:** 1 action

**Range**: Self

**Components**: V, S, M (a few hairs from a bull)

**Duration**: Concentration, up to 10 minutes

**Description**

You endow yourself with endurance and martial prowess fueled by magic. Until the spell ends, you can't cast spells of 6th level or higher, and you gain two of the following benefits:

*   You gain 50 temporary hit points. If any of these remain when the spell ends, they are lost.
*   You have advantage on attack rolls that you make with simple and martial weapons.
*   When you hit a target with a weapon attack, that target takes an extra 2d12 force damage.
*   You have proficiency with shields, simple weapons, and martial weapons.
*   You have proficiency in Strength and Constitution saving throws.
*   You can attack twice, instead of once, when you take the Attack action on your - turn. You ignore this benefit if you already have a feature, like Extra Attack, that gives you extra attacks.

Immediately after the spell ends, you must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or suffer one level of exhaustion.

**At Higher Levels**

When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 8th level or higher, you can choose an additional effect for every level above 7th.

The Extraplanar

# The Extraplanar

*Powerful entities of the outer, or even inner planes often seek out mortals to do their bidding on the material plane. Sometimes, however, instead of power similar to their own, they might grant their warlocks an ability to pull creatures from other planes to an extraordinary degree.*

## Extended Spell List

The Extraplanar lets you choose from an expanded list of spells when you learn a warlock spell. The following spells are added to the warlock spell list for you.

Spell Level - Spells:


Extraplanar Expanded spell list
Sorcerer LevelSpells
1stBane, Sanctuary
2ndArcanist's Magic Aura, Vortex Warp
3rdBestow Curse, Glyph of Warding
4thGiant Insect, Summon Elemental
5thCreation, Summon Celestial


## Planar Tether

When you form this pact at 1st level, you are granted the ability to tether your summoned creatures to your plane, granting them additional strengths.

When you cast a warlock spell that summons a single creature, you may undergo a special ritual during the casting of the spell. Doing so changes the spell's casting time to 10 minutes, and the summon lasts until it is dispelled. If the spell normally requires concentration, you may ignore the requirement.

When you summon a creature in this way, it is referred to as your tether, and it gains the following benefits:

*   You can replace its hit points with 5 x your warlock level.
*   It adds your proficiency bonus to its damage rolls.
*   If the creatures to-hit bonus is less than that of your spell attack bonus, it may instead use your spell attack bonus when attacking.

You learn the Find Familiar spell, it counts as a warlock spell for you, and it does not count towards your total spells known.

If you summon another creature using this ritual, any previous creatures summoned by it are immediately dispelled. After you complete this ritual, you must finish a long rest before you can do so again.

## Ethereal Stores

Starting at 6th level, you can briefly store items away for later use. You can cast the \[Wristpocket\] spell a number of times equal to your charisma modifier without expending a spell slot. When you do, the stored object is sent to the ethereal plane, and the spell does not require concentration. You regain all expended uses of this ability when you finish a long rest.

Additionally, you can see into the ethereal plane up to 10 ft.

## Undying Binds

At 10th level, when tether drops to 0 hit points, you can use your reaction to expend a pact slot. Doing so will restore 50 hit points to the summon.

## Sacrificial Summoning

Starting on 14th level, while your Planar Tether summon is active, you can use an action to sacrifice part of yourself to empower it and your spells, doing so allows you to channel this ability until your next long rest, or until you end it as an action. While channeling this ability, you have the following effects:

*   Your hit point maximum is reduced by 30.
*   You have disadvantage on Constitution checks and saving throws, except for those made to maintain concentration.
*   Your tether has advantage on all saving throws
*   You can use a bonus action to give your tether temporary hit points equal to your warlock level
*   Creatures within 10 ft of your tether have disadvantage on saving throws against your spells.

If you end this ability early, you cannot use it again until you finish a long rest.

Thermal Hypercube

# Thermal Hypercube

*Wondrous Item, Legendary*

**Description**

This 3-inch cube of condensed brimstone resonates slightly when touched, and generates enough dry heat to keep the temperature within 30 feet of it at 120 degrees Fahrenheit (~49 degrees Celsius). This area is subject to extreme heat.

Thornmail

# Thornmail

*Armor (spiked armor), legendary*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

You have a +2 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.

When an attacker hits you with a melee attack, they take 1d8 magical piercing damage and can't regain hit points until the end of its next turn.

Additionally, when you are grappled or restrained by a creature by making direct contact with you, they can't regain hit points until they end the condition and any damage dealt from feats or class features that does damage via grappling or being grappled counts as magical when wearing this armor.

Thought Forms

# Thought Forms

*7th-level transmutation. (Cleric, Druid, Sorcerer, Wizard)*

**Meta**

**Casting Time**: 1 action

**Range**: Self (30 ft. sphere)

**Components**: V, S, M ((a gemstone worth at least 5,000gp, which the spell consumes))

**Duration**: Concentration, up to 1 minute

**Description**

Through carefully crafted thought projections and the transmutation of mental energies, you create a field of potentiality around you, exceeding your normal limitations. When you cast this spell you create 6 Thought Forms. You can consume these Thought Forms in a number of ways, granting additional benefits depending on your choice:

*   When you or a friendly creature within 30 feet of you misses with an attack, you can consume any number of Thought Form. They gain a +2 bonus to the attack roll equal to the number of Thought Forms consumed, potentially turning the miss into a hit.
*   Once per turn, when you or a friendly creature within 30 feet of you take the Attack action, you can consume 2 Thought Forms to allow them to make an additional attack as part of that same action.
*   When you or a friendly creature within 30 feet of you take damage from an attack, you can consume any number of Thought Forms as a reaction, granting them 10 temporary hit points for each Thought Form consumed. These temporary hit points last until the start of your next turn.
*   When you or a friendly creature within 30 feet of you fails a saving throw against a spell or magical effect, you can use your reaction to consume any number of your Thought Forms. They gain a +2 bonus to your saving throw for each Thought Form consumed, potentially turning the failure into a success.

As a bonus action on your turn, you can expend an unused spell slot to create a number of Thought Forms equal to the level of the spell slot expended, up to a maximum of 6.

Thought Gem

# Thought Gem

*Wondrous Item, Very Rare*

**Description**

A Thought Gem is a smooth, hemispherical precious stone that swirls with the memories of past bearers While holding the gem, you can use the gem to store created thought strands (as with the Encode Thoughts spell) indefinitely, as well as recall them at a later time. It also functions as a spellbook for you, where you can retrieve and store information and spells within the gem at the same rate as reading and writing, its spells and other writing psychically encoded within it. While holding it, you become aware of the spells and thought strands stored within it, and can use it as a spellcasting focus for your spells.

You can cast encode thoughts as an action, with the duration lasting until dispelled, and the thought strand disappearing into the gem. Casting this spell while focusing on a particular thought strand allows you to instantly receive whatever memory, idea, or message the thought strand contains. (Casting detect thoughts on the Thought Gem has the same effect, but the thought strand is determined at random unless using the deeper probe option of the spell.)

Thumper

# Thumper

*Weapon (maul), legendary*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

[![THumper](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/5/53/THumper.png/revision/latest/scale-to-width-down/333?cb=20241008115213)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/5/53/THumper.png/revision/latest?cb=20241008115213)

**Description**

You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. When you hit with a melee attack using this weapon, it deals an extra 1d6 damage. In addition, the weapon ignores resistance to bludgeoning damage.

Attack rolls made with this weapon can't be hindered with spells and magical effects that increase the target's Armor Class except for the Haste spell(i.e. A target won't benefit from the bonus to AC from a Shield spell or Mage Armor spell against this magic weapon's attack rolls) as well as ignoring the magic bonuses of magical items.

When your attack roll is a critical hit, the target must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or be stunned until the end of its next turn.

Thunderlord'S Maul

# Thunderlord's Maul

*weapon (maul), very rare*

**Attunement:**

Creature with minimum 15 Strength

**Description**

This weapon was once wielded by the Thunderlord's an ancient paladin order that delivered swift and thunderous justice to those who opposed order.  Only the strongest of the Thunderlord's could wield this large hammer. It glows a deep electric blue, has runes that say "Thunderous Wrath". The head of the maul is shaped like a large thundercloud and appears to have a deep blue flashes across the head as if it trapped a thunderbolt waiting to be released.

**Thunderlord's Might**

This weapon has a +1 to attack and damage rolls. And once per turn when you hit a creature this weapon deals an additional 2d8 Thunder damage the creature suffers the weapons normal effect, and it then becomes sheathed in booming energy until the start of your next turn. The first time it willingly moves 5 feet or more before then the target takes 3d8 thunder damage is not sheathed in energy anymore.

**Thunderlord's Decree**

This weapon has 8 charges. You can use 4 charges to cast lightning bolt as an action. You can use 2 charges to cast Thunderwave as an action. You regain 1d6+2 charges at dawn.

Thunderstrike Musket

# Thunderstrike Musket

*Weapon (musket), rare*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

[![Thunderstrike Musket](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/d/d6/Thunderstrike.png/revision/latest/scale-to-width-down/180?cb=20241008115421)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/d/d6/Thunderstrike.png/revision/latest?cb=20241008115421)

[](/docs/file:thunderstrike.png)

Thunderstrike Musket

**Description**

This magical musket is imbued with the essence of thunder and has the following properties when attuned:

*   **Thunderous Blast**: On a critical hit, the weapon releases a powerful blast, dealing additional 2d6 thunder damage to the target and all creatures within a 10-foot radius of the target. The affected creatures must succeed on a DC 15 Strength saving throw or be pushed back 10 feet.
*   **Deafening Shot**: Once per day, the weapon can be loaded with a Deafening Shot. The next time you make an attack with this weapon, on a hit, it unleashes a concussive blast that creates a 20-foot radius sphere of silence centered on the target, that functions similar to a silence spell for 1 minute and requires the shooter to hold concentration. The sphere moves with the target.

Tidepiercer

# Tidepiercer

*Weapon (rapier), questable; uncraftable, legendary (requires attunement by an Eloquence Bard)*

**Description**

You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls with this magic weapon. Tidepiercer is a spellcasting focus for you.

**Sapphire Ferocity**

You can add your Charisma modifier (minimum of +1) to the damage rolls of attacks you make with this weapon. This bonus is in addition to the normal ability modifier you add to the damage roll. It applies to each attack you make with Tidepiercer (including opportunity attacks), but not to damage dealt by spells.

**Ribbon of the Pearl**

While attuned, you always know the direction and distance to the nearest coastline. Once per dawn, spend 10 minutes using Tidepiercer to cast augury without material components; the omen appears as foam-light along the blade.

**Sapphire Refrain**

You can weave brief, tide-kissed refrains into your inspiration. A number of times per short or long rest equal to your proficiency bonus, when a creature you can see rolls your Bardic Inspiration die or when you expend a die with Unsettling Words, you can invoke one of the following effects (no action):

*   **Seafoam Step.** The inspired creature may move up to 10 feet. This movement can provoke opportunity attacks as normal.
*   **Brineguard.** The inspired creature regains hit points equal to double your proficiency bonus.
*   **Clear the Wake.** The inspired creature may end the charmed or frightened condition on itself.
*   **Undercurrent (Unsettling Words only).** If the trigger was your Unsettling Words, you may instead choose the target of Unsettling Words. That creature can’t make opportunity attacks until the start of your next turn.

Tortle Protector

# Tortle Protector

**Prerequisite**

Tortle

**Description**

You have a higher grasp in making full use of your shell to better protect yourself and others. You gain the following benefits:

*   Increase your Strength or Wisdom by 1, to a maximum of 20.
*   Your *Natural Armor* trait now provides a base AC of 18 (your Dexterity modifier doesn't affect this number).
*   As a reaction when you are forced to make a Dexterity saving throw, you can choose which of the two will take effect:
    *   You gain a +4 bonus to Dexterity saving throws until the end of your next turn.
    *   All creatures within 5 feet of you are considered to have half cover against any harmful attacks or effects while within range until the end of your next turn.

Once you use this ability, you can't use it again until you've finished a short or long rest.

Totem Of Inception

# Totem of Inception

*Wondrous Item, common*

**Description**

What appears to be a typical paperweight has an innate magical quality to it, which is invoked when the paperweight is first touched by someone. When spun by the linked owner of the paperweight, it always loses momentum after exactly 6 seconds. If anyone other than the linked owner spins it the totem continues spinning until an outside force inhibits it.

Tower Shield

# Tower Shield

*Armor(Shield)*

*50 gp, 20lbs - AC +0*

Also known as a Scutum, the tower shield is a large piece of curved metal or wood, or a composite of both. It is the size of it's user, too big and it is unwieldy if not impossible to use, and too small it becomes ineffective.

The shield confers no AC bonus to it's user by default. Instead it grants a damage threshold to you and creatures that you are providing cover for, so long as they are the same size or smaller than the shield. The damage threshold equals your strength modifier, plus your proficiency bonus when wielded with one hand, or twice your strength modifier plus your proficiency bonus when wielded with two. The damage threshold increases for each allied tower shield wielder within 5 feet, adding up to two of their proficiency bonuses to the damage threshold.

A creature with a strength score less than 13 has their speed lowered by 10 feet while wielding a tower shield.

This shield cannot be used as a base for an animated shield.

Toxic Spike

# Toxic Spike

*2nd-level evocation. (Artificer, Druid, Ranger, Sorcerer, Wizard, Arcane Domain)*

**Meta**

**Casting Time:** 1 action

**Range**: 120 feet

**Components**: V, S, M (a tiny metal spike)

**Duration**: 1 minute

**Description**

You shoot a thin toxic spike at a creature within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. If it hits, the target takes 2d8 poison damage, and the spike is lodged in the creature’s body. At the start of each of the creature’s turns while the spike is embedded in the creature, the target takes 1d6 poison damage. The target may use their action to pull the spike out, after which the spell ends.

**At Higher Levels**

When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the initial damage increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 2nd and the subsequent damage increases by 1d6 for every two slot levels above 2nd.

Tracking Bookmark

# Tracking Bookmark

*Wondrous Item, Common*

**Description**

This small and lovingly hand-made bookmark comes in all shapes and sizes, but is perfect for any bookworms that need to keep track of their spot while perusing through thick tomes. Upon speaking a command word and flipping open the book, the tome the bookmark carries will magically adjust and flip directly to the marked page. Each tome can only contain one bookmark at a time.

Transdimensional Expansion

# Transdimensional Expansion

*8th-level conjuration. (College of Creation, Sorcerer, Wizard)*

**Meta**

**Casting Time:** 1 hour

**Range**: 100 feet

**Components**: V, S, M (1,000 gp worth of Residuum or any Arcane Dust/Powder)

**Duration**: 24 hours

**Description**

You magically expand the dimensions of an existing container, room, or otherwise fully enclosed space beyond its physical limits. Select an existing physical space (no larger than 125 sq ft.) with at most one single point of entry such as a lid, door, window or flap, (no larger than 10 sq ft), and with a solid border (no transparent walls, windows, etc.). As a part of this spell, the caster must inscribe runes around the opening and the borders of the space to maintain its external structure. The interior dimensions of that space expand by up to 5 feet in any direction that you choose as the spell takes effect, warping to accommodate any items or creatures inside.

When the spell ends, the space returns to its normal size and any items or occupants unable to fit in the original space are softly ejected through the opening. If there is no opening or the objects cannot fit through it, the items or creatures are scattered randomly in the Astral Plane as determined by the GM.

Casting this spell in the same space every day for 30 days makes the effect permanent; further castings can be used to expand an existing Transdimensional Space beyond its size provided that it still has a single exterior border with no more than one point of entry.

Treblecuff

# Treblecuff

*Wondrous item, uncommon*

**Description**

While wearing this ear cuff, you can add half your proficiency bonus to any ability check you make to play an instrument if you aren't proficient with it. In addition, you can magically discern (and consequently recreate) the note of any sound you hear.

Trick Card

# Trick Card

*Wondrous Item, uncommon*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

Ostensibly an ordinary playing card, though imbued with a subtle illusion magic. One attuned with it can, with intent, tap the back of the card to change its number or suit. A single-fingered tap iterates the number by one, a tap with two fingers alternates between the four suits, and a tap with three fingers changes the design of the card to match any other card it touches.

Trinket Of Freshness

# Trinket of Freshness

*Wondrous Item, uncommon*

**Description**

This item generates an aura of magical freshness by keeping the air in a 10ft by 10ft cube centered on the trinket clean, you can use an action to change what the aura smells like.

Food and other perishables do not decay while in the aura generated by the trinket.

True Awaken

# True Awaken

**Casting Time**: 8 hours

**Range**: Touch

**Components**: V, S, M (clay and runes worth 2,000 gp, consumed)

**Duration**: Instantaneous

You awaken the mind of a creature, granting it sentience and self-awareness. Choose one non-hostile creature you touch.

You can have a number of creatures awakened by this spell equal to your spellcasting ability modifier (minimum of one). If casting this spell would exceed your limit, the creature awakened longest ago immediately loses all benefits of True Awaken.

**Restrictions:**

This spell automatically fails if the target is summoned or maintained by a concentration spell, is planar bound, has Legendary Resistance, or has an Intelligence score of 10 or higher.

**Effects:**

If the spell succeeds, the target’s Intelligence becomes 10. The target gains the ability to understand one language you know and becomes capable of basic reasoning, learning, and self-awareness.

**Limitations:**

This spell grants no combat benefits, proficiencies, spellcasting, or CR increases. It does not remove or alter magical control already affecting the creature, and it does not restore lost memories or identity.

True Path

# True Path

## True Path

The True Path requires the constant repetition of martial forms until absolute perfection is achieved. With perfection comes “truth”, an aspect of life that such practitioners would consider their peak.

### Truest Strike

At 2nd level, you learn the True Strike cantrip and can cast it as a bonus action. When you cast it, you gain its benefits on the current turn rather than the next turn, and it doesn’t require concentration for you. The spell ends at the end of your turn.

Once per short or long rest, if you kill a hostile creature affected by True Strike, you can use your reaction to regain mastery points equal to half your maximum (rounded up).

### Truth Beyond Doubt

At 6th level, when you strike true, you can bypass the defenses of your enemies. A technique’s attack that has advantage due to True Strike’s effect ignores an enemy’s resistances. At 18th level, such an enemy’s damage immunities are treated as resistances (which cannot be ignored).

### Verify

At 14th level, when you cast true strike on a target, you can learn their resistances, immunities, and current hit points. Additionally, you gain advantage on insight checks against the target.

### Perfect Strike

At 18th level, you can analyze the intrinsic truth about a creature. When you cast True Strike, you can spend 1 mastery point to give your next technique’s attack a +10 bonus to the attack roll and have the creature make their next save against the technique with disadvantage.

True Reincarnate

# True Reincarnate

*9th-level transmutation. (Druid)*

**Meta**

**Casting Time**: 1 hour

**Range**: Touch

**Components**: V, S, M (rare oils and unguents worth at least 10,000 gp, which the spell consumes, and vial of willingly given blood from the race to be reincarnated into)

**Duration**: Instantaneous

**Description**

You touch a willing living humanoid. The spell transforms their body into an adult form of another race. If the target isn’t willing, the spell fails. The caster can choose a medium or small humanoid race for the humanoid to transform into, but must have a vial of willing given blood from a member of that race.

The reincarnated creature recalls its former life and experiences. It retains the capabilities it had in its original form, except it exchanges its original race for the new one and changes its racial traits accordingly.

Tubes Of Trickery

# Tubes of Trickery

*Wondrous item, uncommon*

**Description**

This drab pair of empty ceramic cylinders are magically linked. Each vessel is 3 inches in diameter and one foot tall. Opening one of the vessel's lids and placing an item inside will magically teleport it to the second tube, making a quiet whoosh sound when it does. A very quiet thud can be heard when receiving an item. A tube cannot hold more than 2 pounds of materials at any time, and both lids must be closed for the items to be teleported. Items can only be sent if the two tubes are on the same plane of existence. A creature cannot be sent through the tubes. Only one tube must be empty, or else nothing occurs. The tubes must be within a 30 mile radius of one another.

You can only use this property once per day, which refreshes at dawn.

Tuneful Earcuffs

# Tuneful Earcuffs

*Wondrous Item, rare*

Requires proficiency with at least one instrument to use

**Description**

Taking inspiration from an Earring of Message and a pair of Sending Stones, these metallic earcuffs are enchanted with evocation magic to recreate sounds or melodies from memory. This item has charges equal to your proficiency bonus that reset on a long rest: with an action, you can expend one charge to cause song familiar to you to play at a volume that is only audible to you. The song plays until completion in which the cuffs deactivate, requiring another charge to repeat the song or play a new one. You have disadvantage on perception checks related to hearing while this item is activated.

Umbral Lord Patron

# Umbral Lord Patron

## Description

*You have made your pact with a being of the Shadowfell—not a tyrant, not a monster, but a sovereign of sorrow. The Umbral Lord is a force of stillness and forgetting, a presence woven into the death of joy and the fading of names. It offers you no glory, only power—drawn from what others try not to remember.*

**Features:**

*   [3rd Level - Umbral Lord Spells](#3rd_Level_-_Umbral_Lord_Spells)
*   [3rd Level - Echoes of the Forgotten](#3rd_Level_-_Echoes_of_the_Forgotten)
*   [6th Level - Gravity of Sorrow](#6th_Level_-_Gravity_of_Sorrow)
*   [10th Level - Despair-Wrought Form](#10th_Level_-_Despair-Wrought_Form)
*   [14th Level - Embrace of Oblivion](#14th_Level_-_Embrace_of_Oblivion)

## 3rd Level - Umbral Lord Spells

The magic of your patron ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Warlock level specified in the Umbral Lord Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

#### Umbral Lord Spells

*   3rd: Dissonant Whispers, Phantasmal Force, Calm Emotions, Cause Fear
*   5th: Summon Shadowspawn, Fear
*   7th: Shadow of Moil, Phantasmal Killer
*   9th: Dream, Modify Memory

These spells reflect the patron’s nature—magic that distorts perception, suppresses emotion, and turns sorrow into power.

## 3rd Level - Echoes of the Forgotten

*3rd-Level Umbral Lord Feature*

Your presence carries the emotional residue of the Shadowfell—haunting, weighty, and impossible to shake. Even the fiercest mind stumbles under the echo of forgotten regrets.

You gain proficiency in **Insight** or **Intimidation** (your choice).

Additionally, when you damage a creature with a Warlock spell or cantrip, you can mark it with **Lingering Regret** until the end of your next turn. The next time the creature makes an attack roll or ability check, it subtracts your **Charisma modifier** (minimum 1) from the roll, as if gripped by an emotion it cannot name.

You can affect a number of creatures this way equal to your **Warlock level divided by 3 (rounded up)**, regaining all uses when you finish a **long rest**.

## 6th Level - Gravity of Sorrow

*6th-Level Umbral Lord Feature*

Despair halts even the strongest charge. You call forth a field of emotional gravity—a stillness that seeps into flesh and will alike.

As a bonus action, you designate a point within 60 feet. That point becomes the center of a **15-foot radius zone** of heavy emotional drag until the start of your next turn.

Each creature of your choice that starts its turn in the area or enters it for the first time on a turn must succeed on a **Strength saving throw** against your spell save DC or have its **speed reduced to 0** until the end of that turn. On a failed save, the creature also **cannot Dash** until the end of its next turn, as if the very act of movement had become too much to bear.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your **proficiency bonus**, regaining all uses when you finish a **long rest**.

## 10th Level - Despair-Wrought Form

*10th-Level Umbral Lord Feature*

Your patron reshapes you into an embodiment of psychic weight. You don’t lash out with fire or blade—you unravel resolve, layer by layer.

You gain **resistance to necrotic and psychic damage**.

As an action, you can manifest a mantle of oppressive sorrow for 1 minute. While active:

*   You emit a **20-foot radius aura** of emotional gravity.
*   Each enemy that starts its turn in the aura must succeed on a **Wisdom saving throw** or become **frightened** until the start of its next turn.
*   If a creature fails this saving throw a second time in a row, it becomes **incapacitated** until the start of its next turn, lost in overwhelming emotion.
*   If a creature fails the save on three consecutive turns, it becomes **Paralyzed** until it succeeds on the Wisdom saving throw or is outside the aura of oppressive sorrow. -The oppresive sorrow overrides any fear you are feeling, while the arua is active you are immune to frightened condition. You can use this feature **once per long rest**, or by expending a **pact magic spell slot**.

## 14th Level - Embrace of Oblivion

*14th-Level Umbral Lord Feature*

You carry the end in your wake. When a soul slips away nearby, you seize its unraveling and cast it like a shadow across the battlefield.

When a creature you can see within 60 feet drops to 0 hit points, you can use your **reaction** to claim its fading essence. Choose a number of creatures equal to your **Charisma modifier** (minimum 1) within 30 feet of the dying creature. Each must make a **Wisdom saving throw** against your spell save DC.

*   On a **failed save**, a creature takes **5d10 psychic damage** and is **frightened** until the end of its next turn.
*   On a **successful save**, the creature takes half damage and is not frightened.

While frightened in this way, a creature **loses all temporary hit points**, **cannot gain temporary hit points**, and **cannot benefit from healing**.

If a creature is already frightened by any means, it must use its action on its next turn to **Dash away from you**. If it cannot move, it instead takes an additional **5d10 psychic damage**, writhing in place under the pressure of oblivion.

You can use this reaction up to a number of times equal to your **Charisma modifier** per **long rest**.

> *You are not a torch in the darkness. You are the hush after the flame goes out.*

Undead Warlock 2024

# Undead Warlock 2024

## Description

You've made a pact with a deathless being, a creature that defies the cycle and life and death, forsaking its mortal shell so it might eternally pursue its unfathomable ambitions. For such beings, time and morality are fleeting things, the concerns of those for whom grains of sand still rush through life's hourglass. Having once been mortal themselves, these ancient undead know firsthand the paths of ambition and the routes past the doors of death. They eagerly share this profane knowledge, along with other secrets, with those who work their will among the living. Beings of this type include the demilich Acererak, the vampire tyrant Kas the Bloody-Handed, the githyanki lich-queen Vlaakith, the dracolich Dragotha, the undead pharaoh Ankhtepot, and the elusive Darklord, Azalin Rex.

## Expanded Spell List

*3rd-level Undead feature*

When you reach a Warlock level specified in the Undead Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells available and it doesn't count towards your amount of warlock spells known.

## Form of Dread

*3rd-level Undead feature* You manifest an aspect of your patron's dreadful power. As a bonus action, you transform for 1 minute. You gain the following benefits while transformed:

*   You gain temporary hit points equal to 1d10 + your warlock level.,
*   Once during each of your turns, when you hit a creature with an attack roll, you can force it to make a Wisdom saving throw, and if the saving throw fails, the target is frightened of you until the end of your next turn.,
*   You are immune to the frightened condition.,You can transform a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest. The appearance of your Form of Dread reflects some aspect of your patron. For example, your form could be a shroud of shadows forming the crown and robes of your lich patron, or your body might glow with glyphs from ancient funerary rites and be surrounded by desert winds, suggesting your mummy patron.

## Grave Touched

*6th-level Undead feature* Your patron's powers have a profound effect on your body and magic. You don't need to eat, drink, or breathe. In addition, once during each of your turns, when you hit a creature with an attack roll and roll damage against the creature, you can replace the damage type with necrotic damage. While you are using your Form of Dread, you can roll one additional damage die when determining the necrotic damage the target takes.

## Necrotic Husk

*10th-level Undead feature*

Your connection to undeath and necrotic energy now saturates your body. You have resistance to necrotic damage. If you are transformed using your Form of Dread, you instead become immune to necrotic damage. In addition, when you would be reduced to 0 hit points, you can use your reaction to retore your hit points to twice your warlock level instead and cause your body to erupt with deathly energy. Each creature of your choice that is within 30 feet of you takes necrotic damage equal to 2d10 + your warlock level. You then gain 1 level of exhaustion. Once you use this reaction, you can't do so again until you finish 1d4 long rests.

## Spirit Projection

Your spirit can become untethered from your physical form. As an action, you can project your spirit from your body. The body you leave behind is unconscious and in a state of suspended animation. Your spirit resembles your mortal form in almost every way, replicating your game statistics but not your possessions. Any damage or other effects that apply to your spirit or physical body affects the other. Your spirit can remain outside your body for up to 1 hour. When your projection ends, your spirit returns to your body or your body magically teleports to your spirit's space (your choice). While projecting your spirit, you gain the following benefits:

*   Your spirit and body gain resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage.,
*   When you cast a spell of the conjuration or necromancy school, the spell doesn't require verbal or somatic components or material components that lack a gold cost.,
*   You have a flying speed equal to your walking speed and can hover. You can move through creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain, but you take 1d10 force damage if you end your turn inside a creature or an object.,
*   While you are using your Form of Dread, once during each of your turns when you deal necrotic damage to a creature, you regain hit points equal to half the amount of necrotic damage dealt.,

Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest or by expending a Pact slot.

Unfettered

# Unfettered

## Description:

Wearing heavy armors tends to take immense physical strain, and as such usually restricts the movements of the wearer. Bulwarks who call themselves Unfettered work tirelessly to be just as, if not more efficient with their movements while donning these heavy suits.

**Features:**

[3rd Level - Heightened Agility](#3rd_Level_-_Heightened_Agility)

[3rd Level - Armored Movement](#3rd_Level_-_Armored_Movement)

[7th Level - Iron Fist](#7th_Level_-_Iron_Fist)

[13th Level - Quaking Vault](#13th_Level_-_Quaking_Vault)

[18th Level - Weightless Steel](#18th_Level_-_Weightless_Steel)

## 3rd Level - Heightened Agility

At 3rd level when you select this specialization, you learn to move with better flow while wearing heavy armor. It weighs half as much to you, and it does not apply disadvantage on your dexterity (stealth) checks.

## 3rd Level - Armored Movement

Additionally at 3rd level, you learn to move yourself with considerable speed for being weighed down. Your walking speed increases by 10 feet, and increases by another 10 feet at 18th level.

## 7th Level - Iron Fist

At 7th level, your movements are swift and fluid enough to carry the full force of your armor with them. When you make an unarmed attack while wearing heavy armor, you can choose to treat it as being a weapon with the heavy property, and you can roll a d8 in place of the normal damage of your unarmed attacks.

## 13th Level - Quaking Vault

Starting at 13th level, your poise and agility work in conjunction to make landing less deadly to you, and more so to those around you. You become resistant to falling damage while wearing heavy armor, and landing no longer knocks you prone. When you land while wearing heavy armor, creatures within 15 feet of you take 2d10 bludgeoning damage, and must succeed on a strength saving throw (DC 8 + your strength modifier + your proficiency bonus) or be knocked prone. A creature can only be affected by this ability once per turn.

## 18th Level - Weightless Steel

At 18th level, heavy armor ceases to be a burden for you in any meaningful way. Heavy armor weighs nothing to you while you are wearing it, and you may treat it as if it were medium armor for the purposes of adding your dexterity modifier to your armor class.

Updated Flame Tongue

# Updated Flame Tongue

*Weapon (any melee weapon), rare*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

You can use a bonus action to speak this magic weapon's command word, causing flames to erupt from it. These flames shed bright light in a 40-foot radius and dim light for an additional 40 feet. While the weapon is ablaze, it deals an extra 2d6 fire damage to any target it hits with a melee weapon attack. The flames last until you use a bonus action to speak the command word again or until you drop or sheathe the weapon.

Verdant Breastplate

# Verdant Breastplate

[![Verdant Breastplate](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/b/ba/Verdant_Breastplate.png/revision/latest/scale-to-width-down/180?cb=20241009130352)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/b/ba/Verdant_Breastplate.png/revision/latest?cb=20241009130352)

[](/docs/file:verdant-breastplate.png)

Verdant Breastplate

*Armor (breastplate), Rare*

**Attunement**

Requires attunement.

**Description**

When found, this breastplate resembles a leather cuirass covered in large, dried leaves. Once you wear and attune to it, however, it springs to life and flourishes; its leaves remain pliable and flexible, but harden against incoming harm to protect you. Sleeping in this armor has no adverse effect on you.

While wearing this armor, moving through nonmagical plants that impede movement (such as by creating difficult terrain) costs you no extra movement, and you don’t take damage from nonmagical plant hazards such as thorns or spines. In addition, the “entangle” spell has no effect on you, and you have advantage on any Dexterity (Stealth) check you make to hide in areas of dense foliage.

𝘾𝙝𝙖𝙧𝙜𝙚𝙨. This armor has 7 charges and regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. If you’re a druid or ranger and have cast the “plant growth” spell from the armor within the last 24 hours, the armor regains all expended charges instead.

𝙎𝙥𝙚𝙡𝙡𝙨. While wearing this armor, you can use an action to expend some of its charges to cast either the “entangle” (1 charges) or “plant growth” (3 charges) spell from it, using a spell save DC of 15. If you’re a druid or ranger, you can choose to cast the spell from the armor using your spell save DC instead.

When you cast “plant growth” using this armor, the plants affected by the spell are enriched for 30 days. The plants yield twice the normal amount of food when harvested.

Versipellis Chain

# Versipellis Chain

*Wonderous Item, common*

**Attunement**

Optional

**Description**

While attuned to a this ornate chain, you may alter it's shape as an action. The chain may be coaxed into a bracelet, necklace, earring, or similar jewelry so long as its total length is no more than 18". As part of the same action used to change the chains shape, jewels ,charms or other trinkets may be fixed to the chain, which reshapes to form custom settings as desired. The chain may be reshaped indefinitely, so long as it does not reach a total length of more than 18" - 1" /per attached trinket.

Vexatious Venom

# Vexatious Venom

*evocation cantrip. (Artificer, Druid, Sorcerer, Warlock, Wizard, Arcane Domain)*

Meta

**Casting Time:** 1 action

**Range:** 10 feet

**Components:** V, S

**Duration:** Instantaneous

**Description**

With an upward flourish of your hand, a splash of poisonous venom is released from your hand into the face of a creature in range. The targeted creature must make a Constitution saving throw or take 1d6 poison damage and be blinded until the start of your next turn on a failed save.

The spell’s damage increases by 1d6 when you reach 5th level (2d6), 11th level (3d6), and 17th level (4d6).

Vial Ring

# Vial Ring

*Ring, common*

[![Vial Ring](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/5/58/Vial.png/revision/latest/scale-to-width-down/180?cb=20241008120333)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/5/58/Vial.png/revision/latest?cb=20241008120333)

[](/docs/file:vial.png)

Vial Ring

**Description**

This ring is made of magic glass (That cannot be broken in ring-form) and has a cork stopper at its top like a set gemstone. You can unstopper the ring (no action required) to magically transform it into a small glass vial. The vial holds up to 1 ounce of liquid. The mouth of the vial and ring are both a half inch wide, allowing the cork to remain the same size even when the ring is transformed. When the vial is stoppered, it magically returns to its ring form. Anything contained by the vial magically fits within the ring while it's transformed.

While the ring itself is magical, the cork is not. Any cork of the right size can be used to stopper the ring or vial, allowing you to mark the corks as needed or replace them, should they become lost or destroyed by acid or other similar hazardous (albeit bottleable) materials.

Virulent Infection

# Virulent Infection

*3rd-level evocation. (Artificer, Druid, Sorcerer, Warlock, Wizard, Arcane Domain)*

**Meta**

**Casting Time:** 1 action

**Range**: 30 feet

**Components**: V, S, M (a splash of weak poison)

**Duration**: Concentration, up to 1 minute

**Description**

You attempt infect a creature nearby with a nasty strain of poison. The creature must make a Constitution saving throw or take 3d8 poison damage and be infected. An infected creature is poisoned and takes an additional 2d8 poison damage at the start of each of their turns. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much poison damage and is not infected. The spell has no effect on creatures immune to the poisoned condition.

When an infected creature dies, they explode in a poisonous burst. Each creature within 5 feet of the infected creature when it dies must roll a constitution saving throw or be infected as well, suffering the full effects of the spell.

At the end of each of each infected creature’s turns until the spell ends, they can make another Constitution saving throw. On a success, the spell ends for that creature.

**At Higher Levels**

When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the initial damage increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 3rd.

Vocal Drops

# Vocal Drops

*Wondrous Item, uncommon*

**Description**

This hand-held leather pouch of multi-colored gumdrops can be made in batches of 1d10+5 candies at a time. When ingesting one of these gumdrops, your vocal qualities are enhanced. You gain advantage on performance checks involving vocals for 10 minutes. However, if you ingest more than one gumdrop at a time, you lose your voice for 10 minutes instead.

Voidknights Maul

# Voidknights Maul

Voidknight’s Maul

Weapon (Maul), very rare

Attunement

Requires attunement by a Paladin

Description

The crystalline head of this magic hammer has been bathed in the latent magic of the Astral Plane. While you remain attuned to it, you age at one-tenth your normal rate.

𝘼𝙨𝙩𝙧𝙖𝙡 𝙎𝙢𝙞𝙩𝙚. Whenever you use your Divine Smite feature against a creature, you can choose for it to deal psychic damage instead of radiant damage.

Furthermore, when you expend a spell slot of 2nd level or higher to use your Divine Smite, you can force the target of the attack to make a Constitution saving throw against your paladin spell save DC. On a failed save, the creature is affected by the “levitate” spell.

This version of the spell can affect multiple creatures at once, though you must still concentrate on the spell as normal.

If your concentration on the spell ends, any affected creature gently floats back to the ground. Otherwise, you can use your action to move one or multiple targets affected by the spell vertically, up to 20 feet, dividing the movement between them.

Each affected creature must remain within the spell’s range

Vorpal Weapon

# Vorpal Weapon

*Weapon (any melee weapon that deals slashing damage), legendary*

**Attunement**

Requires Attunement

**Description**

You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. In addition, the weapon ignores resistance to slashing damage.

When you attack a creature that has at least one head with this weapon and roll a 20 on the attack roll, you cut off one of the creature's heads. The creature dies if it can't survive without the lost head. A creature is immune to this effect if it is immune to slashing damage, doesn't have or need a head, has legendary actions, or the GM decides that the creature is too big for its head to be cut off with this weapon. Such a creature instead takes an extra 6d8 slashing damage from the hit.

Warlock 2024: Pact Boon, Pact Of The Garb

# Warlock 2024: Pact Boon, Pact of the Garb

Your patron bestows upon you special clothing in the form of ceremonial garbs or eldritch garments that can protect you and bolster your abilities. While not holding a shield, the garb is light armor with an AC of 10 + your charisma modifier + your dexterity modifier

You can don and doff the garb as an action, and it cannot be removed unless you wish it to be. If the garb is lost or destroyed, you can perform a 1 hour ceremony to receive another from your patron. This ceremony can be performed on a short or long rest, whereupon the previous garbs disappear. The garbs fade away when you die. You cannot benefit from the garbs or it's invocations while wearing other armor.

## Invocations:

### Weaver’s Birthright

**Prerequisites**: Pact of the Garb

While wearing your garb, you can use an action to transform your garb’s appearance to any form of mundane clothing you wish. This does not change the garb’s statistics or properties.

### Eldritch Defense

**Prerequisites**: 5th level, Pact of the Garb

By spending an hour in ritual with any shield, you bind it to your eldritch garb, you can only bind a single shield to your garbs. You gain proficiency with shields and retain you garb AC, while wielding a shield.

### Eldritch Armor

**Prerequisites**: 5th level, Pact of the Garb

Choose two damage types. You are resistant to those damage types while you are wearing your garb. At the end of a long rest, you may change the damage types you chose.

### Insurmountable Linens

**Prerequisites**: 12th level, Pact of the Garb

When you are subjected to an effect that allows you to make a saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw.

Warlock: Eldritch Invocations: Eldritch Flux

# Warlock: Eldritch Invocations: Eldritch Flux

Invocation Option

*Requires 5th level, eldritch blast cantrip*

Once per short rest, when you cast Eldritch Blast targeting more than one creature, each targeted creature hit must make a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC. A creature failing this save is marked.

Once within the next minute, you can use a bonus action to trigger Eldritch Flux. When activated, you and all marked creatures within the Eldritch Blast range experience a dimensional shift. You may swap positions of yourself and the marked creatures within this range.

If the target would not have the space after being shifted, the target is unaffected.

Warlock: Eldritch Invocations: Improved Pact Weapon

# Warlock: Eldritch Invocations: Improved Pact Weapon

Optional (Replaces Improved Pact Weapon Invocation)

**Prerequisite**: Pact of the Blade feature

You can use any weapon you summon with your Pact of the Blade feature as a spellcasting focus for your warlock spells.

In addition, the weapon gains a +1 bonus to its attack and damage rolls, unless it is a magic weapon that already has a bonus to those rolls.

Finally, bows and firearms can be conjured or transformed into pact weapons.

Warlock: Eldritch Invocations: Investment Of The Chain Master

# Warlock: Eldritch Invocations: Investment of the Chain Master

Optional (Replaces Investment of the Chain Master)

**Prerequisite**: Pact of the Chain feature When you cast find familiar, you infuse the summoned familiar with a measure of your eldritch power, granting the creature the following benefits:

*   The familiar gains either a flying speed or a swimming speed (your choice) of 40 feet.
*   As a bonus action, you can command the familiar to take the Attack action.
*   The familiar's weapon attacks are considered magical for the purpose of overcoming immunity and resistance to nonmagical attacks.
*   If the familiar forces a creature to make a saving throw, it uses your spell save DC.
*   When the familiar takes damage, you can use your reaction to grant it resistance against that damage
*   Your familiar obtains a bonus to hit equal to your charisma modifier.

Warlock: Pact Boon: Pact Of The Core

# Warlock: Pact Boon: Pact of the Core

## Pact of the Core

Your patron gives you a piece of itself that you can use to great benefit. At the end of a short or long rest, your patron gives you a core trinket that takes the form of a tiny object of your choosing. You can use a bonus action to consume this trinket, either by crushing it, eating it, or a similar action, restoring hit points and bestowing temporary hit points equal to your warlock level.

You may also use an action to create a core trinket by consuming one of your pact magic slots. You may consume the trinket as part of that action.

## Invocations:

### Sacrifice to Higher Power

**Prerequisites**: Pact of the Core

When you reduce a creature to 0 hit points, you can create a core trinket as a reaction. You may only use this invocation once per short or long rest.

### Lifegiving Core

Prerequisites: 5th level, Pact of the Core

When you consume the core trinket, you may choose a creature within 60ft to benefit from its effects rather than yourself.

### Core Herald

Prerequisites: 12th level, Pact of the Core

Consuming the core trinket grants advantage on your next attack roll, saving throw, or ability check.

### Greater Trinket

Prerequisites: 15th level, Pact of the Core

When you use a pact magic slot to create and consume a core trinket, roll a number of d8s equal to the level of the slot used. That total is added to the temporary hp gained from consuming the core.

Warlock: Pact Boon: Pact Of The Garb

# Warlock: Pact Boon: Pact of the Garb

## Pact of the Garb

Your patron bestows upon you special clothing in the form of ceremonial garbs or eldritch garments that can protect you and bolster your abilities. The garb is light armor with an AC of 10 + your charisma modifier + your dexterity modifier.

You can don and doff the garb as an action, and it cannot be removed unless you wish it to be. If the garb is lost or destroyed, you can perform a 1 hour ceremony to receive another from your patron. This ceremony can be performed on a short or long rest, whereupon the previous garbs disappear. The garbs fade away when you die.

## Invocations:

### Weaver’s Birthright

**Prerequisites**: *Pact of the Garb*

While wearing your garb, you can use an action to transform your garb’s appearance to any form of mundane clothing you wish. This does not change the garb’s statistics or properties.

### Thickened Garbs

**Prerequisites**: *5th level, Pact of the Garb*

While wearing your garb, your AC increases by 1.

### Eldritch Armor

Prerequisites: *5th level, Pact of the Garb*

Choose two damage types. You are resistant to those damage types while you are wearing your garb. At the end of a long rest, you may change the damage types you chose.

### Insurmountable Linens

**Prerequisites**: *12th level, Pact of the Garb*

When you are subjected to an effect that allows you to make a saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw.

Warmog'S Armor

# Warmog's Armor

*Armor (plate), legendary*

**Attunement**

[![Warmog's Armor](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/2/2d/War_mog.png/revision/latest/scale-to-width-down/180?cb=20241008120555)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/2/2d/War_mog.png/revision/latest?cb=20241008120555)

[](/docs/file:war-mog.png)

Warmog's Armor

Requires Attunement

**Description**

You have a +2 bonus to AC while wearing this armor. While you don this armor, your hit point maximum increases by 30.

You regain 10 hit points at the start of your turn. If you take damage, this feature doesn't function at the start of your next turn.

Warp Ward

# Warp Ward

*2nd-level conjuration. (Artificer, Bard, Sorcerer, Warlock, Wizard)*

**Meta**

**Casting Time**: 1 reaction (when a creature within 5 ft of you hits you with an attack)

**Range**: Self (5 ft. radius)

**Components**: V, S

**Duration**: Instantaneous

**Description**

The attacker must make a constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is teleported to an unoccupied space you can see within 30 ft of you. If the result of this teleport places the target in a location where the attack would no longer be in range, it misses.

Warrior Of The Unbound Mind

# Warrior of the Unbound Mind

## Description

*Monks who follow the Warrior of the Unbound Mind manifest fragments of their consciousness as psionic projections. These spectral clones serve as both weapons and tools, allowing the monk to fight across multiple positions at once, confuse enemies, and strike with thought alone.*

Features:

*   [3rd Level - Psionic Projection](#3rd_Level_-_Psionic_Projection)
*   [6th Level - Mental Anchor](#6th_Level_-_Mental_Anchor)
*   [11th Level - Unbound Multiplicity](#11th_Level_-_Unbound_Multiplicity)
*   [17th Level - Ascendant Mind](#17th_Level_-_Ascendant_Mind)

## 3rd Level - Psionic Projection

At 3rd Level you learn to manifest one psychic shadow that shares your mobility.

*   **Summon Projection**: As a Bonus Action summon one Projection (lasts 1 hour) in an unoccupied space within 30 ft, the projection is a Construct of Psionic energy.
    *   **Stats:** AC equals your Focus Save DC. HP equals two Martial Arts dice + Wisdom Mod. It is vulnerable to force damage, but immune to all conditions.
    *   **Mobile Strike:** Any unarmed strike you make can originate from your space or the Projection's space. Attacks originating from the Projection deal Psychic damage.
    *   **Projection Movement**: The Projection shares your movement speed. You can move the Projection up to your speed, using your movement allowance. It must remain within 60 ft. of you.
*   **Psionic Burst:** When the Projection is destroyed, use a Reaction and 1 Focus Point. Creatures of your choice within 10 ft. make an Intelligence Save, taking 2 Martial Arts dice psychic damage on a failure, or half damage on a success.

## 6th Level - Mental Anchor

Your Projection can now bind foes with psychic energy.

*   **Psionic Grapple:** Once per turn, when a Projection hits a creature with an unarmed strike, you can forgo the damage to attempt a psionic grapple. The target must succeed on an Intelligence saving throw or be grappled by psionic force. The DC for the saving throw and any escape attempts equals your Focus Point DC.
*   The Projection immediately dissipates when the grapple succeeds, and cannot trigger Psionic Burst.

## 11th Level - Unbound Multiplicity

Your mind’s grasp over its projections expands, granting superior tactical options.

*   **Dual Existence:** You can maintain up to two Psionic Projections at the same time. You may use all features from either Projection.
*   **Projection Tactics:** When a creature is hit by your stunning strike and within 5 feet of both your projections, they have disadvantage on the saving throw to resist the stun

## 17th Level - Ascendant Mind

You gain the power to lock down multiple foes and achieve psychic dominance.

*   **Unbound Grip:** Your Psionic Grapple no longer requires you to forgo damage on the attack, and it can target a creature of any size.
*   **Domination Burst:** When a creature fails its save against your Psionic Burst, the psychic damage increases to 3 rolls of your martial arts die, and they have disadvantage on all wisdom, intelligence and charisma saving throws and checks until the end of their next turn.
*   **Relentless Command:** While a creature is grappled by your Psionic Grapple, you can spend 1 Focus Point as a bonus action to move that creature up to 30 ft. This movement uses up to 30 feet of your own movement allowance.

Way Fo The Feral Fang

# Way fo the Feral Fang

*Monks who follow the Way of the Feral Fang tap into their primal instincts, channeling the savage power of beasts. They unleash their inner ferocity, embodying the untamed fury of nature itself.*

## Feral Strikes

Starting at 3rd level, when you use your Flurry of Blows feature, you can replace one of the unarmed strikes with the primal savagery cantrip. The primal savagery cantrip uses wisdom as your spellcasting ability modifier for its attack rolls.

## Closer to Nature

Starting at 3rd level, you can choose to learn the druidcraft or shillelagh cantrip, you use wisdom as your spellcasting ability modifier for this cantrip

## Bestial Agility

At 6th level, your connection to primal instincts enhances your speed and agility. You gain the following benefits:

*   You have advantage on saving throws against effects that would cause you to be restrained or paralyzed
*   If you use your Primal savagery cantrip granted by Feral Strikes in a turn you can use your reaction when an enemy makes an attack of opportunity against you to roll a Martial Art Die and add the result to your AC until the end of your turn.

## Fury of the Beast

At 11th level, your strikes become even more savage and relentless. When you hit a creature with your primal savagery cantrip granted by your Feral Strikes feature, you can add your wisdom modifier to the damage dealt, in addition, if you spend 1 ki point, the target must also make a Wisdom saving throw (DC 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Wisdom modifier) or be frightened of you until the end of your next turn.

## Primal Resilience

Starting at 17th level, your primal connection enhances your body’s resilience and ferocity. As a bonus action, you can spend 4 ki points to enter a primal state of feral resilience that last for 1 minute. You gain the following benefits when in this state:

*   You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed or frightened.
*   You add an additional Martial Art die to your unarmed strikes
*   You have advantage on all Strength and Dexterity saving throws, and you gain temporary hit points equal to your Monk level at the start of each of your turns. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a long rest.

Way Of The Crimson Veil

# Way of the Crimson Veil

## Description

*The Way of the Crimson Veil is an ancient discipline, its roots tangled in the dark history of Toril. Legends trace the origin of these blood monks to the city of Yûlash, a fallen city located near the ruined kingdom of Cormanthor. In the twilight of the city’s prosperity, a small sect of monks sought knowledge in places best left undisturbed. They unearthed forbidden tomes from the deep catacombs beneath Yûlash, written in blood and sealed by ancient rites.*

Features:

[3rd Level - Crimson Transfusion](#3rd_Level_-_Crimson_Transfusion)

[3rd Level - Sanguine Arts](#3rd_Level_-_Sanguine_Arts)

[6th Level - Bloodbound Pact](#6th_Level_-_Bloodbound_Pact)

[11th Level - Improved Sanguine Arts](#11th_Level_-_Improved_Sanguine_Arts)

[17th Level - Sanguine Edict](#17th_Level_-_Sanguine_Edict)

## 3rd Level - Crimson Transfusion

Starting at 3rd level, you learn to harness the power of your blood to fuel your abilities. You may use Crimson Transfusion to substitute your life force in place of ki. When you would normally expend a ki point, you can instead expend a use of Crimson Transfusion. When you do so, you take necrotic damage equal to three rolls of your Martial Arts die for each ki point replaced. Necrotic damage dealt by this ability treats immunity as resistance. You may use this ability a number of times equal to your wisdom modifier, regaining all uses upon completing a short or long rest.

## 3rd Level - Sanguine Arts

Starting at 3rd level, you unlock blood magic techniques that tap into your life force. You take necrotic damage equal to two rolls of your martial arts die. Necrotic damage dealt by this ability treats immunity as resistance. You gain access to the following Sanguine Arts abilities, which allow you to manipulate the power of blood for various effects:

*   **Fleshweaver’s Mantle:** As a bonus action, you may spend up to 3 ki points to weave a ward through blood magic. For each ki point spent, you may roll three of your martial arts die and gain temporary hit points equal to the total.
*   **Leeching Blows:** When you use your flurry of blows, you may choose to expend 2 ki points. During this flurry, your attacks deal additional necrotic damage equal to two rolls of your martial arts die. You regain hit points equal to the total necrotic damage dealt by these attacks.

## 6th Level - Bloodbound Pact

Starting at 6th level, you bind yourself and another willing creature through spilled blood. You may spend 2 ki to initiate a one-minute ritual, after which both you and the creature take necrotic damage equal to three rolls of your Martial Arts die. Once the ritual is complete, until your next long rest, both of you are aware of each other’s total hit points, general emotional state, and any conditions the other is afflicted by, provided they are also aware of the condition. In addition, both you and the creature gain resistance to necrotic damage for the duration.

Only one such pact may be active at a time.

## 11th Level - Improved Sanguine Arts

Starting at 11th level, you learn how to harness more sanguine arts.

Hemal Torrent: When you spend a ki point or use crimson transfusion, you may imbue your unarmed strikes with blood magic. Until the end of your next turn, your unarmed strikes deal slashing or piercing damage instead of bludgeoning, and you can target enemies within 30 feet.

Crimson Mist: As a bonus action, when expending a ki point, or when using crimson transfusion, you can expend 1 ki point to prepare yourself for an incoming attack. Before the end of your next turn, when you are hit by an attack, you can use your reaction to transform into a crimson mist, avoiding the attack and reappearing in an unoccupied space within 15 feet of your original location. This movement does not provoke opportunity attacks.

## 17th Level - Sanguine Edict

Starting at 17th level, when you take necrotic damage, as a reaction, you can make a ranged attack against a creature within 30 feet of you. On a hit, the target takes necrotic damage equal to the necrotic damage you received, before any damage reductions. This attack uses your Wisdom modifier for both the attack and damage rolls, and counts as an unarmed strike for the purpose of bonuses.

Way Of The Dragon Turtle

# Way of the Dragon Turtle

*Curiously, the Way of the Dragon Turtle is a bit of a misnomer for the monks that follow this style. Their link to the legendary beasts is practically nonexistent, only dubbed this way by others for their fortitude and control over heat and steam. Monks that follow this martial style are rarely taught formally, though they may occasionally take on no more than a few students who wish to learn the style. Those who follow the Way of the Dragon Turtle are self-made and remarkable, often gaining an innate understanding of the ki flowing through and around them after overcoming intense hardship. Although this style may seem less refined than its contemporaries, the technical ability of these monks is fatal to underestimate. They pose as much of a threat to their own bodies by manipulating pressure and heat as they do to others, and they must always understand their limits above all else.*

## Ki-Scorched Body

Perseverance manifested in the form of raw ki has scorched and hardened your soul and body. When calculating your Monk unarmored defense, you may instead use 13 + your Constitution modifier.

Additionally, your body is altered by the unrefined, explosive ki you harness, often leaving a strange mark on your likeness. You determine its appearance, or generate it randomly by rolling on the Ki-Scorched Characteristic table.

| d6 | Ki-Scorched Characteristic |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | Your hands and feet are blackened, giving them the appearance of charcoal. |
| 2 | Steam often rises from your ears, mouth and body when you experience intense emotions. |
| 3 | The skin around your elbows and shoulders is cracked and ossified, peeling slightly upward like the scutes of a turtle. |
| 4 | You have a large scar or burn mark on your body, which becomes searing hot in a fight. |
| 5 | Still sources of waters nearby you tend to become restless, showing tiny ripples and tremors. |
| 6 | Your skin fluctuates between feeling extremely hot and cold to the touch. |

## Impact Combustion

Your rudimentary abilities in channeling ki allow you to compress and expel moisture and heat. As an action, you can cast *fog cloud* without providing material components. Once you cast *fog cloud* using this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest, unless you spend 2 ki points to cast it again.
