Additionally, you gain the ability to convert the heat and force from blows you take into powerful outbursts. As a bonus action, if you have taken damage between the end of your last turn and the start of your current turn, you may spend 1 ki point to enter the Combustion state until the end of your turn. While in the Combustion state, you gain the following benefits:

*   You make Strength-based attack rolls with advantage.
*   Your unarmed strikes deal additional fire damage on a hit equal to one roll of your Martial Arts die plus your Wisdom modifier.
*   You can make one additional unarmed strike as part of your Attack action.

## Pneumatic Blitz

At 6th level, you gain the ability to catalyze the heat and momentum you build by moving into a volatile enhancement. If you move at least 30 feet in a straight line on your turn, you may immediately spend 2 ki points to increase your walking speed by 30 feet until the end of your turn. If you continue the current movement for at least 60 feet in a straight line, you may choose to emit a blast of excess pressure around you. When you end this movement, each creature within a 10-foot radius of you must succeed on a Strength saving throw or take a number of d10s equal to your proficiency bonus in force damage and be knocked prone. If a creature passes this saving throw, it takes half damage and is not knocked prone.

## Body of Vapor

At 11th level, your control of ki becomes more refined, allowing you to manipulate the elements of your physical form. As an action, you can spend 4 ki points to cast *gaseous form* on yourself, without providing material components. While in this form, your movement speed is not restricted to 10 feet.

Additionally, you may exit this form at any time on your turn. When you exit this form, you may heal 5d8 hit points. Once you regain hit points using this ability, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

## Pressurized Retribution

At 17th level, your mastery over the convergence and direction of ki reaches explosive heights. As a reaction to taking damage from a source other than yourself, you may spend up to six ki points. For each ki point you spend as a part of this reaction, you mitigate 5 damage. This damage mitigation is applied before any resistances you have. You may also redirect the force from this mitigated damage, blasting out a 40-foot cone of crushing steam. Creatures in this area must pass a Constitution saving throw against your ki save DC or take 10 fire damage for each ki point you spent as part of this reaction, taking half damage if they pass. Being underwater does not grant resistance to this attack.

Additionally, you are no longer required to have taken damage to enter the Combustion state as a bonus action.

Way Of The Ki Blade

# Way of the Ki Blade

*Masters of a martial discipline intertwined with the ethereal energies of ki, the practitioners of the Way of the Ki Blade are formidable warriors who wield weapons forged from the very essence of their inner strength. These adept martial artists combine their physical prowess with the disciplined art of ki manipulation, creating a dance of blades that transcends the limits of the mundane.*

## Ki-Forged Weapon

At 3rd level, you can spend 1 ki point as a bonus action to manifest a ki-forged weapon, turning a melee monk weapon into a weapon of pure energy for 1 minute. This weapon deals force damage instead of its regular damage type and uses your Wisdom modifier for attack and damage rolls.

In addition, at level 5 the weapon gains a +1 bonus to its attack and damage rolls, unless it is a magic weapon that already has a bonus to those rolls, this bonus increases to +2 at level 11

## Disruptive Weapon

At 6th level, once per turn you can spend a ki-point to temporarily transform your ki-forged weapon into an ethereal weapon. The next melee attack you make with it before the end of your turn ignores the target’s armor, requiring no attack roll. Instead, the target makes a Dexterity saving throw against your Ki save DC. On a failed save, the creature takes damage as if hit by an attack from your ki-forged blade and it's inner balance and movement is disrupted by your ki, the target's speed is halved, and it can't use reactions. On its next turn, it can use either an action or a bonus action, not both. On a successful save, the  target takes half damage from the attack and isn't disrupted. The blade returns to normal immediately after the attack

## Ki-Bound Weapon

At 6th level, If your Ki-Forged blade has the thrown property you can make it return to your hand immediately after it is thrown by spending 1 ki point.

## Ki-Enhanced Focus

At 11th level, your focused ki protects you from mental intrusions and damage. Your thoughts can't be read by telepathy or other means unless you allow it, and you also have resistance to psychic damage.

## Ki-Forged Flurry

At 11th level, when you use your flurry of blows, you can attack with your ki-forged blade instead of unarmed strikes.

## Ki-Blade Ascendancy

At 17th level, your connection to the essence of ki reaches its pinnacle. You can now infuse your ki-forged weapon with extraordinary power. As an action, you can spend 4 ki points to enter the state of Ki-Blade Ascendancy, which lasts for 1 minute.

While in this state:

*   Your ki-forged weapon deals an additional 1d8 force damage on each hit, and you gain a number of temporary hit points equal to twice the force damage dealt by this feature.
*   Whenever you hit a stunned creature with your ki-forged weapon, you can spend an additional ki point to attempt to paralyze the target. The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your Ki save DC or be paralyzed until the end of its next turn.

Way Of The Mirror Soul

# Way of the Mirror Soul

## Light Echo’s

3rd-level Mirror Soul feature

Starting when you choose this tradition at 3rd level, you can create reflections that disorient your foes and bolster your defense. When you use your flurry of blows, you can spend 2 ki points to cast the mirror image spell, replacing one of the unarmed strikes. When casted this way, you only make one illusory duplicate, unless you spend one ki point for an additional duplicate (3 duplicates max). For each duplicate you create, you gain 5 temporary hit points.

## Fractal Vision

6th-level Mirror Soul feature

As an action you may magically project your consciousness into a reflective surface that you can see. Projecting allows you to see and hear, as well as project your voice as though you were in that reflections space. During this time, you are blinded and deafened. You can sustain this effect for up to 10 minutes, and you can end it at any time (requires no action).

While being used in this way, you can use an action to project to another reflective surface that you can see. While you are projecting into a reflection, your reflection is visible, and can be interacted with by others, such as speaking with them. If any reflective surface along this path is destroyed or disrupted (in the case of liquids), this effect ends.

## Mirror Stride

11th-level Mirror Soul feature

You gain the ability to magically enter a mirror or reflective surface within 5 feet of you and move from inside it to inside another mirror or reflective surface being effected by your Fractal Vision feature.

You must spend 1 ki point and use 5 feet of movement to enter a reflection. As part of the movement used to enter the reflection, you can either pass into one of those reflections or step out of the reflection you're in. You appear in a spot of your choice within 5 feet of the destination, using another 5 feet of movement. If you have no movement left, you appear within 5 feet of the reflection you entered.

You can use this ability once per round, and take 1d10 force damage if you end your turn inside a reflection.

## Soul of Reflection

17th-level Mirror Soul feature

Starting at 17th level, you empower your Mirror Images for both defense and offense. While your Mirror Images persist, you gain the following benefits:

You and your illusory duplicates gain a +1 to Armor Class and Saving Throws for each duplicate still active (up to a maximum of +3)

Your unarmed strikes and monk weapon attacks deal additional psychic damage equal to your Wisdom modifier. (edited)

Weapon, +4

# Weapon, +4

*Weapon (Any), Legendary*

You have a +4 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

Weapon Master

# Weapon Master

## Deft Grip

Starting at 3rd level, after you make a weapon attack using the attack action you can use a free action to swap to another weapon.

When you attack with a weapon, you receive a bonus to the attack based on the list below, however you may only benefit from a single bonus once per turn, even if your attack misses; You can however receive an additional bonus if you attack with a different weapon that causes a different type of damage. Ranged weapons do not benefit from Deft Grip, nor do generic “Elemental Weapons” (which collectively count as a single weapon), consumable items that grant additional damage, or spells.

Deft Grip DC: 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Dexterity modifier


Deft Grip List:
Damage TypeEffectBonus Damage
BludgeoningThe heavy blow causes both stone and steel to crumple. Additional 1d6 damage if AC ≥ 151d6 or 2d6 bludgeoning damage
PiercingA swift thrust gives you extra reach. +5ft attack range1d6 piercing damage
SlashingA swift slice carves through lightly armored opponents with ease. Additional 1d6 damage if AC ≤ 151d6 or 2d6 slashing damage
AcidThe acid begins to eat away at flesh and damage armor. Dexterity saving throw. Upon a failure, the creature’s AC is reduced by 1 until the end of your next turn. At level 10 the AC reduction is increased to 2, and at level 20 it is increased to 3.1d6 acid damage
ColdFrost begins to coalesce around the creature’s body, restricting movement. Constitution saving throw. Upon a failure the creature’s speed is reduced by half until the end of your next turn.1d6 cold damage
FireFlames engulf the target. Dexterity saving throw. Upon a failure the creature is set ablaze and takes 1d6 fire damage at the end of their turn. The fame can be put out by a suitable liquid or by using an action to extinguish it.1d6 fire damage
ForceThe weapon impacts with incredible force. Strength saving throw. Upon a failure the creature is knocked prone.1d6 force damage
LightningLightning arcs to the flesh of another creature on impact. A random hostile creature within 10ft is chosen. That creature makes a dexterity saving throw. Upon a failure, they take the full damage of the attack.1d6 lightning damage
NecroticThe necrotic energy tears apart facsimiles of life. Doubled damage against temporary hp and creatures not in their original form (wildshape, poly, true-poly, shapechange, etc). Any damage that goes over the temp HP or the HP of the transformed creature is not doubled when applied to their HP/base form.1d6 necrotic damage
PoisonPoison seeps into the wound. Constitution saving throw. Upon a failure the creature suffers from the poisoned condition. The creature can repeat the save at the end of each of their turns.1d6 poison damage
PsychicPsychic damage racks the mind of the target. The creature has disadvantage on any concentration saving throws for the damage caused by this attack.1d6 psychic damage
RadiantRadiant light bursts from the weapon. Constitution saving throw. Upon a failure the creature is blinded. The creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns.1d6 radiant damage
ThunderA thundercrack roars forth from the weapon. Constitution saving throw. Upon a failure the creature is deafened. The creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns.1d6 thunder damage


## Flowing Stance

At 3rd level, when you successfully hit another creature with a weapon that benefits from the Deft Grip bonus you receive a +1 to hit and to damage for your next attack if your next attack will also benefit from the Deft Grip bonus. These bonuses stack then reset to zero at the end of your turn.

## Rapid Strike

Starting at 7th level, you learn to trade accuracy for swift strikes. If you take the Attack action on your turn and have advantage on an attack roll against against one of the targets, you can forgo the advantage for that roll to make an additional weapon attack against that target, as part of the same action. You can do so no more than once per turn.

## Shatter the Horde

Starting at 10th level you can expend a use of your Action Surge to cause your melee attacks to strike any number of creatures within 5 feet of you, with a separate attack roll for each target. This benefit lasts for a single turn. This does not prevent a second Action Surge from being used on the same turn.

## Focused Strike

Starting at level 15 you can expend a use of your Action Surge to focus all of your ability into a singular strike. Instead of making multiple attacks, you make a single attack against a target, and if it hits that attack is essentially multiplied by however many attacks you have remaining, including bonuses for each attack from Deft Grip and Flowing Stance; Additionally, after you use this ability, you can make no more attacks on the same turn. This does not prevent a second Action Surge from being used on the same turn. This ability must be declared prior to knowing if the Focused Strike hits.

## Omnipath

At level 18, attunement slots for weapons that require attunement can shared in a single slot however, you can not dual wield weapons that require attunement without using a second slot, you receive no benefit from the weapon when it is not in use (such as auras or other passive buffs), and you can use one of the weapons at a time.

Weapon Of Sniping

# Weapon of Sniping

*Weapon (any weapon with the range or thrown property), uncommon*

**Description**

When you make an attack with this weapon, the short and long range is doubled.

Weighted Weapon

# Weighted Weapon

*5th-level transmutation. (Artificer, Cleric, Paladin)*

**Meta**

**Casting Time**: 1 action

**Range**: Touch

**Components**: V, S

**Duration**: Concentration, up to 1 hour

**Description**

A weapon you touch becomes heavier on impact towards enemies. For the duration, the weapon becomes magical, if it isn't already, and deals one extra die of the weapon's damage die.

**At Higher Levels**

When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 7th level or higher, the extra damage increases to two dice.

Whip Of Constriction

# Whip of Constriction

*Weapon (whip), uncommon*

**Description**

If you roll a 20 on an attack roll against a creature you can choose to automatically grapple the creature. A creature grappled in this way is restrained. While grappling a creature with this whip the wielder can’t move further than the reach of the whip from the creature, and can't attack with the whip, without releasing the grapple(takes no action).

A creature grappled in this way can use an action to make a strength (Athletics) or dexterity (acrobatics) check with a DC 13 to break the grapple.

Wind Up Phoenix

# Wind-Up Phoenix

*Wondrous Item, common*

**Description**

This carefully constructed automaton was constructed in the image of a Phoenix. When you speak the command word, small illusory flames spring to life within its empty eye sockets and the gears begin to turn, causing the small mechanical bird to fly around the speaker in circles and occasionally perch on their shoulder. This effect end if the distance between the toy and its owner is 25 feet or greater.

Inscribed on one wing of this bird is the ornate depiction of a tiny smoldering Phoenix egg, a signature left there by the toy's designer.

Windswept Kite

# Windswept Kite

*Wondrous item, uncommon*

**Description**

[![Windswept KITE](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/4/42/Windswept_KITE.png/revision/latest/scale-to-width-down/180?cb=20241009130623)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/4/42/Windswept_KITE.png/revision/latest?cb=20241009130623)

[](/docs/file:windswept-kite.png)

This silk kite (colors, shape, etc. are customizable) is 5 feet across and is attached to a 60-foot string. It weighs 1 pound, can carry up to one pound in weight, and can be folded into a 6-inch square. While holding the kite, you can use a bonus action to toss it aloft and speak its command word. When you do, you also choose how much string to let out, between 10 to 60 feet. The kite then expands to its full size (if it was folded) and creates a cylinder of moderate wind of 10 miles per hour, which allows it to continue flying. The cylinder's height and diameter is the same as the amount of let-out string, up to 60 feet. The kite and top of the cylinder remain centered above your location. You can use another bonus action to speak the flying kite's command word to end the effect and cause it to fall softly back into your hand. You can change the wind's direction at the start of each of your turns for the duration.

You can use the kite to create this wind for up to 1 hour, all at once or in several shorter intervals, each one using a minimum of 1 minute from the duration. The effect ends early if you are no longer holding the string, at which point it flies up to 1d10 times 10 feet in a random direction before landing on the ground. Dealing 5 slashing damage to the string (AC 15) severs the string, causing the kite to fly away as if you let go of the string. The severed string can later be reattached by holding its ends together for 1 minute.

Withering Smite

# Withering Smite

*1st-level necromancy. (Paladin)*

**Meta**

**Casting Time:** 1 bonus action

**Range**: Self

**Components:** V

**Duration:** Concentration, up to 1 minute

**Description**

Your weapon radiates terrible energy that empowers the next strike you make with it. The next time you hit with a melee weapon attack during this spell’s duration, your attack deals an extra 1d6 necrotic damage. If the target of that attack was a creature, it must make a successful Constitution saving throw or take 1d6 necrotic damage at the start of each of its turns for 1 minute, its flesh rotting around the wound. An affected creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. The rotting effect also ends if the affected creature receives magical healing.

**At Higher Levels**

When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the extra damage dealt by the weapon’s hit increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 1st. Subsequent necrotic damage doesn’t increase.

Elysium: Floating City In The Sky

# Elysium: Floating City in the Sky

## The Beginning

And so it came to pass that the evils of long-past would not rest idly by. Arising from the crater of Dockers Bay, the entity which had wrought its destruction upon the town arose once again, and headed toward the survivors' encampment. Hastily were plans made, and the evacuation of all that remained was underway.

[![Comet Entity Emerges](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/4/49/Comet_Entity_Emerges.png/revision/latest/scale-to-width-down/350?cb=20241009014931)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/4/49/Comet_Entity_Emerges.png/revision/latest?cb=20241009014931)

With groups of adventurers splitting to achieve differing objectives, ultimately the entity was stalled in a climactic battle, and in its haste, rent its destructive energy prematurely.

Having successfully evacuated North of the Cloud Peaks into the Wood of Sharp Teeth, the survivors once again set camp. In the wake of the comet entity's second destructive event, several changes had been observed:

First, the light of the comet in the sky dimmed to a level in which it is only just barely visible in the night sky.

Second, unlike in the wake of the destruction of Dockers Bay, there seemed to have been very little residual energy in the second crater - Almost no Nightwalkers or shadow entities of any kind.

Lastly, as the comet's light dimmed, something appeared to break off of it, hurtling across the sky like a meteor before impacting the ground some distance to the East of the new encampment. The expelled mass from the comet turned out to be a device created in the ancient Nethereil Empire by the Magos of Xiandlier Enclave. The floating enclaves of Netheril, powered by Mythallars, ultimately fell when the Mythallars, not wholly unexpectedly, were destroyed in what became known as Karsus's Folly.

Within the unique device was the creator himself, Magos Esra. Finding himself a man out of time, he was met by several adventurers from the encampment who had come to investigate the meteor which had fallen from the sky. The Magos explained that his enclave had been destroyed by the elder-evil, the Star of Ice and Hate, Delban. His description of the entity which came to do its master's bidding was identical to that of the Comet Entity which had destroyed Dockers Bay, and had sought to destroy the encampment at the Cloud Peaks. Seeking the power of the Mythallars of Xiandlier, it also took interest in the Magos' experiment, and ripped it straight from the enclave's core as well, taking the Magos along with it.

Now, having learned of the fate of the Netheril Empire, and much of the history that had passed in the thousand years or so since his own time, Esra seeks to rebuild his device, improve upon it, and re-create an enclave like those of ancient Netheril. At the invitation of the adventurers who had come across him after crashing into the forest, he appeared above the encampment with the beginnings of a new enclave: a mass of land and earth approximately 800 x 800 ft. He suggested that if he were able to get more power to the crystal core which sustained its floating and traveling capability, that it might be expanded upon in the future.

To that end, he invited the people of the encampment knowing they, too, had suffered at the destructive hand of Delban, to come aboard the nascent enclave, and stay there. In exchange, he asked that they render their aid should the floating island come under attack, or for the occasional task that might be required to maintain its integrity. He added that all were welcome to stay on the island, so long as they also did not interfere with his work, nor the operations of the crystal entombed at the core of the island mass.

## The Realm Wanders

The island, which Esra named Elysium (for the hubris of the Netherese would naturally lead one to name their island for a Celestial Plane), was designed specifically to travel. It would not do to have it remain stationery over the Cloud Peaks once the refugees of Docker's Bay had come to settle the floating landmass.

### Waterdeep

Having developed on the island a bit, and established a permanent residence and facilities, the new residents of Elysium decided that the island should set sail for Waterdeep. After several days of travel, Elysium arrived just over the horizon, staying fifty miles outside the border of Waterdeep.

The city's denizens, quite suspicious and wary of the sudden appearance of a giant floating landmass, resorted to keeping scouts on patrol regularly. Never was Elysium left without the watching eyes of Waterdeep scouts. While the adventurers of Elysium were regarded with mistrust and suspicion in Waterdeep, they had not as yet been barred from entering or conducting business there. Tensions however were high, and the slightest provocation, perceived or not, may tilt relations into open hostility.

#### The Annexation of Elysium

For a time, Elysium and Waterdeep coexisted relatively peacefully, though never without tension. Helping Waterdeep with some of its contracts helped the Elysians gain rapport, but it wasn't long before those in power found another avenue of taxation in the rich adventurers of the floating island, and Ramiel's Crusade in the east becoming an increasing threat provided yet more (albeit convenient) reason for Waterdeep to annex Elysium.

The annexation began cordially, if not gladly. While the adventurers weren't happy to have to pay taxes -- some even going so far as to find every way possible to avoid doing so -- many did see the value in such a close alignment with the Lords' Alliance. But it wasn't long before even that thin connection began to break down.

Word soon spread among Waterdeep's nobility of the new ward and the rich adventurers inhabiting it. Not to be outdone by these overpaid mercenaries who merely played at being nobles, but who clearly didn't see value of the land under their very feet, rich Waterdhavians began purchasing it and moving in. With the influx of people came an influx of problems, culminating in the attempted murder of a noble in the middle of the Cloud Top Inn.

#### Elysium Regains Independence

As the island further developed and began to move again, relations with Waterdeep were strained and Gray Wardens were placed as a proxy for the city staff to alleviate tensions and allow the island to roam without inciting diplomatic incidents. However, this was but a band aid, and relations with Waterdeep were stretched to the breaking point, resulting in Waterdeep releasing Elysium and allow it to become its own independent island.

Negotiations were conducted among the land owners within the Cloud Top Inn about what should be done for the new government, and it was decided that an unusual form of government known as a democracy would be set up on the island. So Thorlas, Lord Commander of the Gray Wardens and Noble Lord of Waterdeep, acting as the city-state's representative, officialized the documents set up during the discussion and independence was declared, resulting in the withdrawal of the Warden peacekeepers and the passing of the torch to the new government.

A woman by the name of Phoenix was elected to Mayor, with a supporting council comprised of Kaynes, Jerry, Alina, Alkafyn, Jack, and Cabbage. However they were not the only ones involved in the city's independence. Bryan, Clark Aiwon, Ajani, Loops Silva, Pon, Anastasia, and Vapor, among a number of other land owners, were present as well and contributed to the discussion. Together, they began the long and arduous process of nurturing the fledgling government.

## Map of Elysium

[![Elysium 2.0 Gridless](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/a/a4/Elysium_2.0_Gridless.jpg/revision/latest/scale-to-width-down/180?cb=20241112174437)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/a/a4/Elysium_2.0_Gridless.jpg/revision/latest?cb=20241112174437)

[](/docs/file:elysium-2.0-gridless.jpg)

[![Elysium 2.0](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/7/76/Elysium_2.0.jpg/revision/latest/scale-to-width-down/180?cb=20241112174524)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/7/76/Elysium_2.0.jpg/revision/latest?cb=20241112174524)

[](/docs/file:elysium-2.0.jpg)

Lore

# Lore

Managed by @Loremasters

The Wandering Realm's world is based on the world of Faerûn, but as a living world, it has evolved over time.

Sigil

# Sigil

Sigil Clarifications

Sigil, the City of Doors, is a fairly special place in the multiverse. It is the ultimate neutral ground where people of all planes and background can meet and coexist. It also comes with some special rules, courtesy of the Lady of Pain, the godlike ruler of the mind bending city.

Access to the city is granted via portals, which can be any threshold from a doorway to window or a simple clearing in a forest, and is accessed via a portal key which can be anything from a literal key to a simple thought or whistled tune as you pass the threshold. These portals are not static and can be open for centuries, seconds, or for a five uses. In other words, access is based DM/Lady of Pain discretion.

Teleportation within the city does not function. Need to get around using good old fashioned leg power and it would be prudent to hire a guide if you're not a native or very familiar with the occasional loops of Mc Escher esque architecture.

Creatures or people spotted in Sigil are not considered to be within their natural environment for the purposes of Shapechange, Poly, etc unless the creature is native/unique to the city or Outlands such as a Cranium Rat Squeaker or Sunfly.

Binding and summoning magics also do not function within the city. Can't exactly allow the celestials to go around binding the fiends walking about, and vice versa. This is a city of neutrality, where all are permitted so long as they behave.

Items or knowledge brought back from alternate universes, timelines, settings, don't transfer to our main setting.

Space is... weird... in the city. Buildings move seemingly on their own, space is simultaneously compact and infinite, travel between any two places seeming to take a indeterminate amount of time. One can walk for years to get down a city block or seconds to cross to the other side of the city. It is as difficult to traverse as the DM desires and the space for new construction is seemingly infinite.

Laws of the Lady

Worship of the Lady is prohibited.

No harm may come to any dabus.

There are to be no challenges to the Lady's ultimate rule or authority within Sigil.

Any action which harms the city of Sigil either directly or indirectly will be considered as a direct attack against the Lady herself, and punished appropriately.

No divine entity may enter Sigil.

There may be at no time more than 15 factions operating within Sigil. (Though this rule was put into place during the Great Upheaval, it is unknown if it is still amongst her laws, and the dabus have refused comment. Most haven't risked testing it.)

However, their are more laws, but are determined and enforced by internal factions of the city and are not ultimate rules laid down by the Lady of Pain, who has instantly flayed and killed even the most powerful of adventurers for so much as looking at her. Thankfully she isn't seen around much.

The Destruction Of Docker'S Bay

# The Destruction of Docker's Bay

## The End

It is often the case that fairy tales have a definitive beginning and end. The real world, however, seldom confines itself to such limitations. Our story, set as it is within the Forgotten Realms, thus begins at the end of another: The port-town of Dockers Bay, located along the Sword Coast just West of The Cloud Peaks, saw a curious collection of characters: from the typical humans making their way in the world, to the demigods that lived among them as adventurers.

Long had the city been protected by powerful magic, beyond even that of the adventurers who resided there. So, when cataclysm befell the land, it confounded beliefs and shattered the sense of safety that all had resided comfortably under. It had started quite simply: A comet in the skies above. Over time, the comet revealed an unnatural nature - mages felt pain in their heads, people and animals were prone to emotional outbursts, and magical energies became unpredictable. As time passed, these effects grew in gravity, and new, worse effects appeared. Shadow creatures crept around the area, stalking the night - and certain people and creatures started being targeted.

[![Crater](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/9/93/Crater.jpg/revision/latest/scale-to-width-down/400?cb=20241009014416)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/9/93/Crater.jpg/revision/latest?cb=20241009014416)

None of these events, however, hinted at what was to come. A group of some of the most powerful adventurers - not just in Dockers Bay, but indeed the world - set out to investigate the comet, determine its origin, and put a stop to its increasingly dangerous effects. What they found was nothing short of an incomprehensibly overwhelming presence, nearly killing them all simply by way of hearing its thoughts through one of them as a medium. As the intrepid heroes retreated back to safety, events only grew worse. The sun turned red, and a massive, imposing figure appeared, levitating in the skies above the bay's massive harbor.

And then it awoke. None who were present to witness it directly survived, but what happened in its wake was apparent for all to see: The massive magical crystal towers which had long protected the town exploded, their devastating eldritch energy wiping out most ordinary people in the area.

The town was engulfed in magical flames of purple and green, and the dark silhouette of the massive imposing figure stood silent and still in the sky above as devastation spread across the land. A few of the adventurers, at least, were quick to act: escaping by various methods of teleportation and fast-moving travel on foot. Most others were not so fortunate, and were consumed in the cataclysm.

Fleeing East, the surviving adventurers of Dockers Bay set up a makeshift camp settlement at the foot of The Cloud Peaks. With only their clothes and equipment on their backs, and very little in the way of food and supplies, they regrouped, with more survivors finding their way to the makeshift settlement. It was there that these fine adventurers rested - discussing plans regarding the founding of a new settlement; perhaps somewhere far away, or perhaps somewhere else along the coast.

Alchemy System

# Alchemy System

### **Alchemy System**

##### **Gathering**

1) Use Downtime to Gather Herbs, max usage **3x a day** (uses 8 hours in a session). **Note:** You can no longer sacrifice adventuring time to harvest extra herbs.

2) Choose one environment \[Cave, Forest, Desert, Grasslands, Swamp\] + Rarity \[Common, Uncommon, Rare, Very Rare\]

3) Roll Nature/Medicine/Arcana + Herbalism/Poisoner/Brewer/Alchemist Tool Prof Bonus (If proficient) --> DC scales depending on rarity of ingredient. Once check is completed, post in transcript using template found in pins of transcript . Craftmaster will roll for the herb gathered randomly depending on location chosen and DC rarity met.

###### **DCs for Enchantment Ingredients:**

| Rarity | DC |
| --- | --- |
| Common | DC 10 |
| Uncommon | DC 13 |
| Rare | DC 17 |
| Very Rare | DC 20 |

##### Targeted Gathering

1) You can target herbs based on which potion you wish to make! If you are targeting a herb for a specific potion, use the Targeted gathering Template pinned in transcript

2) If the herb you are searching for isn't in the environment you chose, you get nothing. If it is, a Craftmaster will roll the herb normally, and you only get the herb if the dice comply.

###### The DCs for targeted gathering are as followed:

| Rarity | DC |
| --- | --- |
| Common | DC 13 |
| Uncommon | DC 17 |
| Rare | DC 20 |
| Very Rare | DC 23 |

###### Bonuses:

1.  You can only target one herb per day until you have gathered 3 of that herb, at which point you may target for two herbs per day due to your familiarity.
2.  You cannot use help actions in herb gathering. Self-Buffs are allowed for now.
3.  **Nat20 Rule:** If you roll nat20 on the check (RAW Nat20 without modifiers and bonuses) you get 1 Extra Herb.
4.  **Over10 Rule:** If your total roll is 10 over the applicable DC you get 1 Extra Herb. Example, if your total roll for a rare is 31, you get 2 Herbs instead of 1.
5.  Nat20 and Over10 rules can both apply, to a max of 3 Herbs per roll.

### Potion Brewing

Potions are brewed using herbs, each herb is used for a specific potion, the potion is hinted at in the description.

Time, base Cost, Herb Requirement to brew:

| Rarity | Time (Days) | Cost | Herb Requirement |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Common | 2.5 | 25 gp | 1 Herb |
| Uncommon | 5 | 100 gp | 1 Herb |
| Rare | 12.5 | 1,000 gp | 2 Herbs |
| Very Rare | 25 | 10,000 gp | 3 Herbs |

#### Monster Part Substitution

You can substitute 1 Herb for a monster part, however uncommon and higher potions need at least 1 Herb.

| Potion Rarity | Monster CR | Loot DC |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Common | CR 1-3 | DC 13 |
| Uncommon | CR 4-8 | DC 17 |
| Rare | CR 9-13 | DC 21 |
| Very Rare | CR 14-18 | DC 24 |
| Legendary | CR 19+ | DC 26 |

#### Bulk Brewing

*   Bulk Brewing has a discount on overall cost now. For every 10 potions you make at a time, you get a 5% discount on cost, cap for the discount is 20%.
*   Bulk Brewing also requires less herbs. For every 10 potions you brew, you only need 7x the amount of herbs for one potion of the same rarity. Example: 10 common potions of healing - 7 enchantment herbs needed.

#### Time Reduction

*   You get a 50% time reduction if you use double the amount of enchantment herbs for the potion.
*   Sacrificing Potions:

1.    You can sacrifice one potion per brew.
2.    Lowers base time by half the time it takes to brew a potion of that rarity.

Allowed 2024 Rules Content

# Allowed 2024 Rules Content

## General Rules

General:

*   2024 character creation content, subclasses, classes, feats, items can be submitted via the Homebrew channel

Classes:

*   Any approved 2024 Class can be played with a 2014 subclass, unless otherwise stated in the wiki.
*   You can choose a 2024 Subclass on a 2014 Class, unless otherwise stated in the wiki.
*   19th Level Epic Boon feats are replaced with ASI or a Feat
*   Once you chose the 2024 version of a Class you can't multiclass into it's 2014 version or choose the 2014 version in Paragon leveling, and vice versa
*   2024 Classes can start with their starting gold instead of equipment

Feats:

*   If you take a 2024 feat, you can't take its 2014 version as a feat, and vice versa

## Classes and Subclasses

### Approved Classes, with Approved Subclasses

*   Bard
    *   College of Dance
    *   College of Valor
    *   College of Lore
    *   College of Glamour
*   Barbarian
    *   Path of the Berserker
    *   Path of the Wild Heart
    *   Path of the World Tree
*   Cleric
    *   Life Domain
    *   Light Domain
    *   War Domain
*   Druid
    *   Circle of the Moon
*   Fighter
    *   Champion
        *   Heroic Warrior Errata "When you roll a die in combat, you can choose to reroll it. You must use the new result. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until the start of your next turn."
    *   Eldritch Knight

*   Monk

\- Martial Arts die scaling reverted to 2014 version (1d4 > 1d6 > 1d8 > 1d10)

\- Deflect Attack damage reduction reduced from level + dexterity mod + 1d10 to proficiency + dexterity mod + martial arts die.

\- Body and Mind now only grants +4 dexterity **or** wisdom, not both. (also applies to 2014 monks using the same feature)

*   *   Warrior of Shadow
    *   Warrior of Mercy
    *   Warrior of the Elements
    *   Warrior of the Open Hand 11th Level feature replace with: 2024 Warrior of the Open Hand

        > Beginning at 11th level, As an action you can enter a special meditation that surrounds you with an aura of peace. You gain the effect of a sanctuary spell for it's normal duration. You can enter this state a number of times equal to your wisdom modifier per long rest. The saving throw DC for the spell equals 8 + your Wisdom modifier + your proficiency bonus


*   Paladin (2024 Paladin can only be used with 2024 paladin subclasses, and vice versa)
    *   Oath of Glory
    *   Oath of Vengeance
    *   Oath of Devotion
    *   Oath of the Ancients

*   Sorcerer
    *   Draconic Sorcery
        *   **Dragon Companion becomes Draconic Might** you can cast the Draconic transformation spell without expending a spell slot and doesn't require concentration. you may do this once per long rest otherwise it requires 11 sorc points to do again.
*   Ranger
    *   Beast Master
*   Rogue
    *   Assassin
    *   Thief
    *   Scion of the Three

*   Warlock
    *   Archfey Patron
    *   Great Old One Patron
        *   Create Thrall: When you cast Summon Aberration with a **Pact Slot**, you can modify it so that it doesn't require Concentration. If you do so, the spell's duration becomes 1 minute for that casting, and when summoned, the Aberration has a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to your Warlock level plus your Charisma modifier. In addition, the first time each turn the Aberration hits a creature under the effect of your Hex, the Aberration deals extra Psychic damage to the target equal to the bonus damage of that spell.
    *   Fiend Patron
    *   Celestial
*   Wizard (not Approved)
    *   Abjurer

## Feats

    *   Cunning Strike (Level 5)

    *   Devious Strike (level 14)

## Special Rules

*   Allowing 2024 monks and College of Dance Bard to use Dexterity for the Athletics check required for Grappling and Shoving, using the current (2014) grapple rules
*   2024 Pact of the Chain Familiars

Animal Taming Rules

# Animal Taming Rules

## Animal Taming Rules

### Overview

*   A PC may attempt to tame animals over an extended period of time.

#### Taming Check

**Check:** Wisdom (Animal Handling)

**Tame DC:** 15 + (2 \* CR) (minimum 11)

**Time:** 1 full day of calm interaction (one day of downtime)

#### Initial Tame

Before training begins, the creature must first be made non-hostile through patience, calmness, or courage.

| Roll | Result |
| --- | --- |
| Natural 20 | Start at 25% Bond Progress |
| Success | Creature becomes non-hostile and open to training |
| Fail by 5 or less | You fail to train the creature, you may retry after 1 day |
| Fail by 5 or more | You fail to train the creature, you may retry after 3 days |
| Natural 1 | The creature flees or attacks, you may retry after 1 week |

#### Long-Term Bond Training

Each day requires 8 hours of focused interaction. You can train only one creature at a time. Each success adds your roll to a cumulative total.

| Roll | Result |
| --- | --- |
| Natural 20 | Add roll plus 10% of total progress threshold |
| Success | You Progress your bond with the creature |
| Fail by 5 or less | Your bond with the creature does not progress |
| Fail by 5 or more | The creature refuses training, you may retry after 3 days |
| Natural 1 | The creature flees or attacks, you may retry after 1 week |


Progress cannot fall below your current bond stage unless broken by neglect or narrative reason.

#### Bond Progression

| Bond Stage | Progress Threshold |
| --- | --- |
| Non-Hostile | Success on Initial Tame |
| Sociable | 10 + (10 \* CR) |
| Basic Commands
Stay. Come. Stop. | 15 + (15 \* CR) |
| Complex Commands
Dodge. Attack X creature. Hide. | 20 + (20 \* CR) |
| Mounted / Complex Tasks
Scout X area. Return to X location. Find X person. | 25 + (25 \* CR) |


**Example:** A CR 3 beast needs 80 total progress to reach Complex Commands.

Once a stage is reached, it remains permanent unless broken through RP.

### Food and Shelter

Proper care sustains trust.

**Food**

| Creature Size | Daily Cost |
| --- | --- |
| Tiny | 10gp |
| Small | 30gp |
| Medium | 50gp |
| Large | 100gp |
| Huge | 150gp |
| Gargantuan | 200gp |


**Unique Diets:** Costs may be altered at DM Discretion

**Shelter:** Will be determined by DM

**Luxury Care:** Double Daily Cost

#### Care Factors

*   **One day without care:** Lose 10% Progress
*   **Two days without care:** Creature may flee and reset to 0%
*   **Luxury Care:** After 3 days of Luxury Care you gain advantage on the next training roll

### Class and Subclasses Modifiers

| Class/Sub-class | Benefit |
| --- | --- |
| Ranger (Any) | Advantage on Initial Tame for beasts with CR level beasts with CR ≤ half proficiency bonus |
| Ranger (Beast Master) | Advantage on Initial Tame for equal to or less than half your ranger |
| Ranger (Drakewarden) | Advantage on Initial Tame for draconic creatures or wyrmlings with CR equal to or less than half your level |
| Druid (Any) | +2 to one Animal Handling check per long rest; reduce daily food cost by 25% |
| Druid (Circle of the Shepherd) | Add 1d4 once per day to a training check. |
| Barbarian (Totem Warrior) | Advantage when taming beasts matching your Totem Spirit |

###### \* Only one class bonus applies at a time.

### Tamable Creatures

*   Beasts & Monstrosities comprised of Beasts (ex: Griffon, Hippogriff)
*   Subject to DM discretion. A number of monsters that may technically fit the criteria may not be tamable for lore reasons, or may otherwise be inaccessible due to where they may be located.
*   Young Griffin (CR 0, ¼, 1)
*   Griffon (CR 2)
*   Owlbear (CR 3)
*   Amphisbaena (CR ½)
*   Peryton (CR 2)
*   Kamadan (CR 4)
*   Death Dog (CR 1)
*   Shadow Mastiff (CR 2)
*   Giant Strider (CR 1)
*   Skyjek Roc (CR 2)
*   Two-Headed Owlbear (CR 3)
*   Hippogriff (CR 1)
*   Giant Ice Toad (CR 3)
*   Two-Headed Cerberus (CR 2)
*   Snowy Owlbear (CR 3)

### Limitations

*   It must be reasonable for the creature to be tamed, per DM discretion.
*   It must make sense within the context of TWR lore, per DM discretion.
*   The creatures you can potentially tame are **limited by the CR.** In order to have the skill necessary to safely handle a creature, you **must be capable of hitting the initial tame DC through just a straight animal handling roll** without additional modifiers from items, spells, abilities, etc.

**Maximum Tamable CR = (5 + Your Base Animal Handling Modifier) / 2**

*   Creatures with legendary or mythic abilities can not be tamed.
*   Creatures with an INT of 1 can not be tamed.
*   Creatures with an INT of 4+ can not be tamed.
*   The initial tame, and subsequent checks, must be RPed for and submitted in transcript.
*   For CR 1+ The initial tame must occur in a mini-quest or RPed targeted hunt run by DM. A DM can not run their own quest if they are attempting to tame a creature.
*   For any creatures intended as a mount, the initial tame must occur in a mini-quest or RPed targeted hunt run by DM. A DM can not run their own quest if they are attempting to tame a creature.
*   Purchasable domesticated mounts are an exception, and can be purchased as normal.
*   No more than 1 creature can be tamed per day.
*   Exceptions may exist for things such as domesticated animals, per discretion of the DM.
*   Only creatures at least one size larger than a PC are mountable.

##### Owl-Bear Taming Example Steps

*   ~Go on a mini quest hosted by a DM.~
*   ~RP to find and convince the Owl-Bear that you are both not food and non hostile.~
*   ~CR3 creature, so DC 30 animal handling check for an adult Owl-Bear.~
*   ~Upon success, it will be non hostile but still aggressive.~
*   ~Cumulative DC of 300. The daily RPed animal handling checks would be added together.~
*   ~With an average roll of 10, with the +10 modifier required to initially tame the creature, it would take 15 days in real life of daily checks and RP to tame the creature.~
*   ~At 75 points, it would become sociable~
*   ~At 150 points, it would accept simple commands~
*   ~At 225 points, it would accept complex commands and barding~
*   ~At 300 points, it would accept complex tasks and be mountable~

*To be updated*

### Use of Tamed Creatures In Combat

The creature acts on its own initiative. You control it as you would a summon RAW from spells such as Conjure Animals.

### Mounted Combat for Tamed Creatures

*   Because of the inherent danger of things such as meteor swarms, when you are mounted the creature shares your HP.
*   If you fall to zero HP, your mount falls to 1 HP, you are dismounted if on land, and if it is a flying creature it begins to crashland at 60ft/round.
*   For the purposes of movement, the mount replaces your move speed(s).
*   HP when dismounting: When you dismount, if your HP is lower than the mount's HP, the mount will adopt the same current HP as you.
*   If you choose to have the mount attack, take any action, or otherwise treat it as a separate creature, ignore points 1~4 and it will be treated as a normal mount RAW.

###### Mounted Combatant Feat interaction

Under this system you can use either RAW and risk damage to the mount or the alternative rules in the Animal Taming doc. If you're using the alternative rules only point 1 of the feat pertaining to advantaged against creatures is applicable, the other two are nullified.

### Sale and Transfer of Animals

*   The recipient must be capable of hitting the initial tame DC to be capable of controlling the creature in any way.
*   By default, the bond level resets to zero. If you are proficient in Animal Handling, it resets to 25%. If you have expertise in Animal Handling, it resets to 50%

### Tame Death

If your tamed creature dies and is revived it will regress one cumulative level. If your tame is regressed past 0 the taming process with restart requiring a new initial tame roll.

Approved And Disapproved Modules

# Approved and Disapproved Modules

TWR is primarily a Dungeons & Dragons 5e (2014) server. Some 2024 content has been approved for use as well; the list can be found here. The full list of approved UA/Homebrew content found at: [Homebrew System & Index](/docs/homebrew-system-%26-index "homebrew system & index")

##### Permitted Source Books for Character Creation

*   Basic Rules for Dungeons & Dragons
*   Player’s Handbook
*   Dungeon Master’s Guide
*   Monster Manual
*   Acquisitions Inc
*   Ebberon: Rising from the Last War
*   Mordenkainen’s Tome of Foes
*   Mythic Odysseys of Theros
*   Sword Coast Adventurer’s Guide
*   Tasha’s Cauldron of Everything
*   Volo’s Guide to Monsters
*   Guildmasters’ Guide to Ravnica *(inquire before submitting a character)*
*   Explorer’s Guide to Wildemount *(inquire before submitting a character)*
*   One Grung Above
*   Xanathar’s Guide to Everything
*   Van Richten’s Guide to Ravenloft
*   Strixhaven: Curriculum of Chaos (Excluding backgrounds)
*   Fizban's Treasure of Dragons
*   Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
*   Spelljammer: Adventures in Space (See Disapproved Content for Exclusion List)
*   Legendary Magic Items

##### Permitted Adventure Content

*   Baldur’s gate Descent into Avernus
*   Curse of Strahd
*   Divine Contention
*   Dragon of Icespire Peak
*   Wildemount: Frozen Sick
*   Ghosts of Saltmarsh
*   Hoard of the Dragon Queen
*   Hunt for the Thessalhydra
*   Icewind Dale: Rime of the Frostmaiden
*   Locath rising
*   Lost laboratory of Kwalish
*   Lost mine of Phandelver
*   Out of the Abyss
*   Princes of the Apocalypse
*   Rise of Tiamat
*   Rrakkma
*   Essentials Kit: Sleeping Dragon’s Wake
*   Storm King’s Thunder
*   Storm Lord’s Wrath
*   Tales from the Yawning Portal
*   The Tortle Package
*   Waterdeep: Dragon heist
*   Waterdeep: Dungeon of the Mad Mage
*   The Wild Beyond the Witchlight
*   Call of the Netherdeep (Excluding Ruidium Items)
*   Infernal Machine Rebuild

##### Permitted Supplements

*   Mordenkainen’s Fiendish Folio Volume 1
*   Sage Advice Compendium
*   Blood Hunter
*   Revised Ranger

##### Disapproved Content

*   Any background which provides a Feat is disallowed.
*   Dragonmarks are not allowed as part of the Eberron content.
*   Ravnica Backgrounds are not allowed as part of the MTG content.
*   Strixhaven Backgrounds are not allowed as part of the MTG content.
*   Spelljammer Disapproved content:

1.  Spelljammers (See [Astral Expansion](/docs/astral-expansion "astral expansion") for more details on Spelljammer content)
2.  Backgrounds
    1.  Wildspacer
    2.  Astral Drifter

Barding

# Barding

### Barding

Barding is equipment made for creatures not Medium size. Barding can be either mundane or magical.

The base cost of creating the item will be increased by the following modifier for each size above and below Medium:

| Size | Cost Modifier |
| --- | --- |
| Diminutive | x 64 |
| Tiny | x 16 |
| Small | x 4 |
| Large | x 4 |
| Huge | x 16 |
| Gargantuan | x 64 |

Character Creation

# Character Creation

The rules for Character Creation are below. For how to manage characters as they level up, see the [Character Progression](/docs/character-progression "character progression") page.

### 2014 vs 2024

2014 characters follow the rules in this document as written. 2024 characters, or mixing the two, follow an additional set of rules: [Allowed 2024 Rules Content](/docs/allowed-2024-rules-content "allowed 2024 rules content")

## Character Creation

1.  Unless otherwise stated, rules regarding characters are RAW (rule as written).
    *   Approved/Disapproved Modules can be found here: [Approved and Disapproved Modules](/docs/approved-and-disapproved-modules "approved and disapproved modules")
2.  Characters can start at level 1-3 (player choice)
3.  Items, Experience, Abilities, etc do not carry over from other games/servers/campaigns.
4.  Backgrounds which grant feats are disallowed.
5.  You may choose Fixed or Rolled HP for your character, but must continue with that method for the lifetime of your PC. If rolling, ping a @Dungeonmaster to record your rolls.
6.  Character level advancement is via XP, not Milestone.
7.  All character stats are Point Buy or Standard Array.
8.  Character sheets must be kept up to date. If there is ever a question as to what level you are supposed to be, what items you have, how much gold you have, then we need to be able to refer to your sheet and check it against what we have on record in [#records](https://discord.com/channels/817263494818693170/817297426335006751), [#story-log](https://discord.com/channels/817263494818693170/882397674907783168) or [#transaction-log](https://discord.com/channels/817263494818693170/817297478034522112).
9.  You may have no more than 8 player characters to begin with. Once a character reaches T3, you may submit a request for additional PC slots.

### Starting Equipment

1.  New PCs begin with standard starting equipment per the PHB.
2.  Starting gold is not allowed.
3.  PCs may select a single Uncommon magic item to start with, **if they submit a written backstory** that includes receiving it.
    1.  This item must be available from the Favor Tables. This can be access in discord with ,  or any +1 weapon or shield.
    2.  The base value of the item may not exceed a gold value of 400gp (i.e. no Mithral Plate, firearms, except a pistol or arquebus, etc.)
4.  PCs who visit Elysium and complete an Entry Survey in character will receive 400gp in additional gold from the player-provided Adventurer's Fund. (LINK NEEDED)

##### Character Sheet Generators

Dndbeyond is recommended for easy character creation, but you may use another system if you wish. (Avrae supports Dndbeyond, Dicecloud, and Gsheet)

For DNDBeyond users, you may join one of the [donated campaigns](/docs/shared-campaigns "shared campaigns") for access to sourcebooks.

#### Disclosures

1.  DMs have the right to refuse characters a spot in their quests, including those using UA/Homebrew supplements otherwise permitted by the server

## Steps to Submitting a New Character

1.  Submit a link to your character sheet using the Character Submission template pinned in [#transcript](https://discord.com/channels/817263494818693170/817301000770551828) for Staff review and approval. Once approved, it will be posted by a Staff Member to [#active-pc-sheet-links](https://discord.com/channels/817263494818693170/817309328348413952) at which time your character can officially be played on the server.
2.  While not required, it is highly recommended you set up a "tupperbot" for your character. This allows you to speak "in character" easier when participating in role-play. Refer to [Discord Commands](/docs/discord-commands "discord commands") for more information on the tools we use.
3.  While not required, if you desire you may introduce your character in [#pc-introductions](https://discord.com/channels/817263494818693170/817277968070082570) and include an image and short description of them.

## Changing your Starting Choices

Sometimes your original concept for a build doesn't exactly work out. We get it. While you are in T1, you can make any changes you wish to your character to bring it in line with what will be most fun for you to play. Once you have made changes, post a summary to [#transcript](https://discord.com/channels/817263494818693170/817301000770551828) for review and records keeping. Once your PC is in T2, your choices are locked in without following [Retraining Rules](/docs/retraining-rules "retraining rules") where they apply.

Regardless of any other changes **your PC's name must not change.**

Character Progression

# Character Progression

### Character Progression

#### Gaining Experience

Once your character has been approved, any successful gameplay logged in [#story-log](https://discord.com/channels/817263494818693170/882397674907783168) or [#records](https://discord.com/channels/817263494818693170/817297426335006751) will provide you with XP to add to your sheet. **Characters level up following the normal XP Tables defined in the Dungeonmaster’s Guide.**

#### PC Tiers and Levels

Gameplay is generally restricted to “Tiers” as defined in the Dungeonmaster’s Guide.

| Tier | Level Range |
| --- | --- |
| Tier 1 | 1-4 |
| Tier 2 | 5-10 |
| Tier 3 | 11-16 |
| Tier 4 | 17-20 |
| [Post-20](/docs/post-20-rewards "post-20 rewards") | “21-26” |
| [Paragon](/docs/prestige-systems "prestige systems") | Special |

#### Item Rarity and PC Level Restrictions

The item restrictions below determine whether your PC is able to use/attune to a magic item:

| Tier | Level Range | Rarity |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Tier 1 | 1-4 | Uncommon |
| Tier 2 | 5-10 | Rare |
| Tier 3 | 11-16 | Very Rare |
| Tier 4 | 17-20 | Legendary |

#### Soft Level Cap

You have the option to apply a level cap to any of your PCs. **If you choose to do this, any experience you would have gained while capped cannot be applied at a later time/date.** You must state your intention to cap a character in [#transcript](https://discord.com/channels/817263494818693170/817301000770551828). If you wish to uncap the character, you must announce your intention to do so the same way. Either instance will be posted to [#records](https://discord.com/channels/817263494818693170/817297426335006751) for record keeping.

#### Character Retirement

Sometimes the arc of a character results in their retirement from the life of an adventurer. Other times you may simply wish to no longer play that character. It is possible to turn that character into an NPC via ‘retirement’. See [Character Retirement](/docs/character-retirement "character retirement") for a full understanding of how to retire a PC.

Character Retirement

# Character Retirement

#### Character Retirement

Sometimes the arc of a character results in their retirement from the life of an adventurer. Other times you may simply wish to no longer play that character. It is possible to turn that character into an NPC via ‘retirement’. Simply make a post in the [#transcript](https://discord.com/channels/817263494818693170/817301000770551828) channel stating your intention to retire the character as an NPC. To unretire a PC NPC, they start at level 1-3 and need to start as if they are new PC.

#### Defining What A Retired PC Can Do in Regards for Their Downtime Activities

**Retired PCs Can:**

*   Craft
*   Perform Services (Including spellcasting.)
*   Run Businesses, Organizations, and other passive income/influence.

**Retired PCs Cannot**

*   Train
*   Obtain Vehicle or Tool Profs
*   Obtain Languages
*   Other Item Production (Such as using class features for item production)

#### Converting an NPC to a PC

To unretire a PC NPC, they start at level 1-3 and need to start as if they are a new PC.

#### Unretirement

*   Unretiring requires a PC slot available.
*   Unretiring characters are limited to level 20 (post20’s revert back to level 20).
*   Retired PC sheet link must be in ⁠npc-listings to keep any form of wealth or level. If the sheet isn’t there, then the PC must be created as any new PC would be

#### Unretiring an ex PC

Unretire at previous level with old items and gold, provided the following:

*   2d4 \* PC level days of retraining (via downtime)
*   PC can still be used to roleplay/adventure while retraining
*   Cooldown of 1 month to unretire another PC
*   Make a report and post in ⁠transcript (staff approving will roll the 2d4)
*   Report should consist of player name, character name, and character level

Otherwise, the PC will

#### Methods to Speed Up Unretirement

RP related to returning to adventuring, retraining, or something of the sort. Submit a rp xp request, but state you want it to speed up retraining instead. Staff approving the request decides how many days to remove, to a max of 10.

###### Money Pit

| Tier | Daily Cost | Maximum # of Days |
| --- | --- | --- |
| T1 | \- | \- |
| T2 | 1,000 gp | 3 Days |
| T3 | 10,000 gp | 5 Days |
| T4 | 20,000 gp | 5 Days |

Code Of Conduct

# Code of Conduct

# Code of Conduct

**TWR is a server based on mutual respect.** Rude, disruptive, and generally toxic behavior will be subject to moderator review. Below are some explanations of behavior which is not acceptable, but this is by no means an exhaustive list:

1.  **Abuse/harassment/bullying/threats of mental or physical harm** against one or more people.
2.  **Insults directed against any player or staff on the server.**
3.  **Repeated insensitive or inappropriate jokes.** Be respectful of subjects such as race, religion, politics, etc.
4.  **Use of any type of slur.**
5.  **Threats of self harm** Self harm is a serious matter and should not be joked about or taken lightly.
6.  **Follow Discord TOS found here https://discord.com/terms**
7.  **No spamming, raiding, or anything that disturbs the peace.**
    1.  While mods and admins are here to help you, **abuse of moderator or staff pings may result in a warning.**
    2.  **Voice chats:** Don’t play any alarmingly loud sounds or scream into the mic.
8.  **Please keep channel content appropriate to the channel being used**
    1.  Keep memes and shitposting to #the-c-o-r-n-e-r where possible. #general can be a fun place, but please don’t flood the channel with memes.
    2.  Swearing is banned in #the-peoples-court
    3.  Roleplay in RP channels, Out of Character talk in OOC etc
9.  **Art Advertising or solicitation of any kind is not allowed.**

If you are ever made to feel uncomfortable by content on the server, or by someone’s behavior, please report it to anyone with the @Moderator or @Admin role. **We want everyone to feel comfortable and safe here.**

Companion Pcs

# Companion PCs

## What this Document Contains:

*   [Companion Leveling System](#Companion_Leveling_System)
*   [Converting your Companions to Player Characters](#Converting_your_Companions_to_Player_Characters)
*   [Boon of Ascension](#Boon_of_Ascension)

## Companion Leveling System

Presenting the Companion Leveling System, allowing creatures special to your characters to gain a spotlight of their own, matching the power-scaling of the character themselves. Below details the system and how your companions can gain levels, and which are eligible for use in this system.

### Eligible Companions

All subclass companions are eligible for this system. This includes, but is not limited to:

*   Beast Master’s Animal Companion
*   The Extraplanar’s Planar Tether
*   Purple Dragon Knight’s Amethyst Dragon
*   Battle Smith’s Steel Defender

Other creatures can be eligible, but must be either permanently available to you, or otherwise not tied to a duration. Examples of this include:

*   Creatures Tamed by your character
*   Artificer’s Homunculus Servant
*   Spirits Summoned by Find Familiar, Find Steed, or similar magic

Hirelings are not eligible for this system.

### Starting the Leveling Process

To start the leveling process for a character’s companion, the character must first take the \[Boon of Ascension\] detailed later in this document. This boon may be taken multiple times, but only once for each 20 class levels a character has.

When the character takes the boon, they may select a single companion that they control and is eligible for this system, and grant that creature a single level in a class of their choosing. The creature then may gain experience through RP as if they were a paragon PC, with the ability to level up all the way to P26. A companion under this system has the following restrictions:

*   The companion must fill an empty PC slot. You cannot add a creature to this system if you do not have an available slot.
*   The companion does not gain a Boon, Blessing, or Feat at P26, but must still meet the requisite xp to enter a new paragon cycle in order to do so.
*   The companion counts as a controlled entity for the purposes of Hunts, Trials, Raids, etc. A DM can rule otherwise for the purpose of quests.
*   The companion may not reach a level higher than the original character.

In return, the companion gains the following benefits:

*   If the companions actions are normally determined by the original character’s actions, bonus actions, or other similar requirements, they no longer need to do so in order to act.
*   The companion rolls its own initiative in combat.
*   The companion retains any benefits that it had as part of the original character’s class features

## Converting Your Companions to Playable Characters

This section contains guidelines for converting your companions into player characters.

### Hit Points

When your companion becomes a player character through this system, it retains its previous hit point maximum. It then gains hit points equal to the \[Hit Points at 1st Level\] hit points of the class it gains, and \[Hit Points at Higher Levels\] as it gains levels.

### Proficiencies

Your companion also retains any skill or saving throw proficiencies it had, as well as any bonuses to them that your class features provided.

In addition, your companion gains the skill, saving throw, tool, weapon, and armor proficiencies that would normally be given with their selected class.

Armor and Other Equipment: More bestial creatures can still make use of barding, of which they are always considered proficient. In order to use weapons, a creature must have a reasonable capacity to use it. If a creature is a size other than medium, any weapons and armor it uses must be properly sized for its use, following the same price conversions as barding.

### Submission

You may create a character sheet for your companion on any of the available platforms that allow you to do so. Some may be better suited than others for this. When your sheet is completed, you may submit it as normal to #transcript for approval. Please provide the original character’s name, and specify that the sheet is for a companion when you do so.

`Example: docs.google.com/spreadsheets/therestoftheurl Companion Sheet of Syn. Original Character: Bim`

## Boon of Ascension

Below are the details of the Boon of Ascension

### Boon of Ascension - Boon - Repeatable

Prerequisites: Having at least one companion eligible for ascension

One eligible companion of your choice gains a level in a single class of your choosing. The companion then may level as normal as through the Paragon System.

Daily Life On Twr

# Daily Life on TWR

### The Passage of Time

Time passes at the same rate within the world of TWR that it passes in our world. For easy reference, there is a channel in the server that lists the time on Elysium. This is an optional time you can use for coordination and envinronmental change, but you may act out your character’s life within each day as it meets your schedule.

#### Daily Reset

For the purposes of downtime and daily activities, your PC’s day begins when you wake up in the morning and ends when you go to bed. For fringe cases, daily resets should occur roughly every 24 hours.

### Daily Resources

Each day, characters have the following resources:

###### Hunting Tags - 5

###### Trial Tags - 2

###### Downtime Hours - 8

###### Long Rest - 1 \*

###### Short Rest - 8 \*

#### Long and Short Rests

Aside from rests which occur in Trials or Quests, players are limited to 1 Long Rest and 8 Short Rests per day. These can be taken at the player’s discretion by using the !sr or !lr command in discord.

### Quest Locks & Restrictions

During the course of a quest, characters may be restricted by the DM from pursuing hunts, trials, downtime options and rests as usual. While your PC is attending a quest, work with your DM to determine what parts of their daily routine can be continued.

### Repetitive Actions

Some spells or projects require daily activity to fully realize. In cases like this, players should post in #transcript describing the action, how long they will repeat it, and outlining all the materials required (which must be procured ahead of time). Staff will then review the post, notify you of any issues or extra requirements, and when the time has passed your actions will become official.

### Downtime

Each PC may utilize **8 hours per day of downtime** to further their character’s goals and story. Downtime options can be pursued as **outlined by RAW in books such as the PHB, DMG, and XGtE.** Some examples include crafting magical items, performing research, gambling, or working a mundane job. Additional downtime options may be defined in other TWR supplements.

**This server uses the “Optional Rule” for sleep deprivation** located in Xanathar’s Guide to Everything if you wish to work through the night.

Death And Retrieval

# Death and Retrieval

## Death and Retrieval

If you are being revived by a member of your own party **during the course of any content**, revival is successful with no penalty aside from the typical cost of casting and any damage to your body or belongings.

If you died **but your party succeeded,** you may skip the below retrieval steps provided that the party members state your corpse was recovered.

### Body Retrieval: Hunts

Characters wishing to return to the scene of a failed hunt to recover their fallen allies or missing friends may do so by engaging the creature that killed them, by targeting them in a hunt. Once the enemy has been defeated, make a body retrieval (Investigation) check.

### Body Retrieval: Trials

To retrieve lost bodies in Trials of Tempus, a new party must first spawn into the trial per all the usual rules at the same level of the bodies. The players must then fight through the temple to secure the room containing the corpses before recovery is possible. Once the room is secured, make a body retrieval (Investigation) check.

### Body Retrieval: Mapped Hunts

Characters wishing to return to the scene of a failed hunt to recover their fallen allies or missing friends may do so by engaging in a random deadly hunt using the applicable alias of the same tier as the dead party. Once the enemy has been defeated, make a body retrieval (Investigation) check.

### Body Retrieval: Delves

1.  The Party is banished to a special retrieval zone that acts like a hunting ground
2.  Any Tier party, excluding Paragon, can attempt to retrieve them, but there needs to be In character motivation or reason to look for the party. #leaveanote
3.  The Party will do a random hunt at deadly difficulty for the Party, upon conclusion of the hunt. A single Investigation check per dead body can be made to retrieve the dead party members.
    1.  If the party fails the check, they must do another random hunt to repeat the attempt

### Body Retrieval: Other

If you die as a part of a quest or roleplay session run by a *@Dungeonmaster*, who will provide you details about the location and condition of your body, as well as any necessary information regarding retrieval.

##### Body Retrieval Checks

Only PCs within the same tier as the dead character may participate in body retrieval. Once the enemy has been defeated, make a body retrieval (Investigation) check:

| Tier | Investigation DC |
| --- | --- |
| T1 | 10 |
| T2 | 15 |
| T3 | 20 |
| T4 | 25 |
| Post-20 | 30 |

### Leaving a Note

If your PC is going on a dangerous mission, read below!

Leave a Note Before You Go:

• Tag: @YourName | PC Name

• What part of the map are you’re exploring (coords or pin)

• How long do you expect to be gone • When others should start searching

• How you want to be revived if you die

• If you don’t list revival plans, retrieval parties decide your fate

• Unknown/isolated PCs may not be retrieved at all

•Leaving a note does not guarantee that the party will perform the task as wished as this extends outside of the player's control

**Pre Retrievals MUST be roleplayed and require staff approval. It is not necessary to roleplay dungeons/hunts/raids**

Further Options: Fate’s Offer – Return from the Dead Quest Hosted by: @ todo (Entity: Fate) If your PC dies and can’t be retrieved, this quest gives a final chance to return. High risk. If you fail, your PC is likely lost forever. Open and always available.

### Elysium Corpse Collection Agency

The first time a PC dies within a trial or hunt in tier 1, their body is automatically collected and brought to a nearby clinic, where they are revived via raise dead or resurrection (in cases of disintegration). Note that this only occurs once per pc, and thus will not be available after prestige, or retirement.

For tracking reasons, when this is done, add Collection Agency in a separate line within the hunt or trial transcript, with the collected character's name printed again after it in the case of multiple characters.

Disciplinary Info

# Disciplinary Info

## Disciplinary Info

1.  Violation of any of the rules will generally result in a verbal warning.
2.  A second violation of the same rule will result in a **strike.** A strike typically lasts for 2 months from the date of issue.
3.  Players with **three active strikes** will be **banned from TWR for a 3 month period**
4.  **Any other disciplinary action up to and including a permanent ban may be dispensed by the mods following a majority vote.**
    1.  Some examples of what might bypass the process include, but are not limited to:
        1.  Art Advertising (specifically public posting or DMing other server members)
        2.  Trolling and Raids (coming to the server with the sole intent of causing trouble)
        3.  Extreme cases of rule #1 violations (slurs, excessively vulgar/targeted language, harassment, etc.)
        4.  Illegal Activities
        5.  Repeated disregard of staff instructions regarding rules or server norms. (Especially if such disregard comes in the form of blatantly disrespectful or hostile behavior in violation of of our [Code of Conduct](/docs/code-of-conduct "code of conduct"))

If you are ever made to feel uncomfortable by content on the server, or by someone’s behavior, please report it to anyone with the @Moderator or @Admin role. **We want everyone to feel comfortable and safe here.**

Bans issued for any reason are, of course, subject to an optional appeal by the offending player, if they should choose to do so. Contact a @Moderator or @Admin if you wish to appeal a ban issued to you or to another member of the server.

Downtime Buffs

# Downtime Buffs

### Downtime Buffs

When performing any downtime activities, any bonuses to skill checks made must last for the full eight hours of downtime to be applicable to the final skill check.

(Cases that specifically state otherwise overrules this, i.e. herb gathering)

Followers And Attunement

# Followers and Attunement

Summoned and PC controlled entities, can attune to magic items, but they share the attunement slot of the PC. Examples of Entities: Simulacrum, Familiars, Beast companions, steel defender, undead, planar bound summons, etc.

When a controlled entity uses a PC's attunement slot to attune to an item. Only one item of the same name can be attuned by either PC or Controlled entity. ex: A Wizard and their Simulacrum can't both be attuned to Illusionist Bracers.

Full & Part Time Jobs

# Full & Part Time Jobs

### Part Time & Full Time Jobs

You can now find gainful employment within the city of Waterdeep (and abroad). This can be done in two ways, a weekly job or daily job.

#### Daily Jobs

Daily Jobs uses an alias in discord and can only be done once a day per PC, this also takes your downtime. To do the job, use the alias: !job skill Then link the result in #transaction-log with a short deception of what you did in character and the amount earned

#### Weekly Jobs

Part time jobs, such as casual work at a smithy or a bard playing for a tavern, will take up all of your downtime (meaning no crafting or learning skills) but still let you quest, hunt, and trial.

Full time jobs pay more but in addition to no downtime you will no longer have time to trail, quest, or freely adventure.

Q: How do you find employment/work?

A: Provide a brief description of the job you’re looking to do, such as a part time job at an apothecary, then roll 2d20 and add your proficiency bonus if applicable (In this case it would be alchemy or herbalism). (2d20 + applicable mod) \* 14 \* Tier = gp/wk using all downtime. x2 for full time, but no hunts/trials as a result Part Time tier averages T1 = 294 T2 = 588 T3 = 882 T4 = 1,176

##### Weekly Job Report

@ Player PC & Tier: Lanscan Tallfast, T2 Job: Bard at a the The Silken Slyph Tavern & Inn Roll: 22 Link to the roll: https://discord.com/channels/817263494818693170/817269893514395658/928341274916962386 Wages: Start Date: MM/DD/YYYY End Date: MM/DD/YYYY

Gameplay Rules

# Gameplay Rules

## Gameplay Rules

1.  **Rules and mechanics are RAW by default.** Any exceptions to this rule will be documented in the Homebrew section or Errata section.
2.  **DM rulings are final.** You don’t have to agree with it, but you must abide by it.
    1.  If you have an issue with a DM’s ruling you may bring it up to the DM directly after the game/quest, not mid-session.
    2.  If you speak with the DM and still disagree with the ruling, you may bring it up in [#the-peoples-court](https://discord.com/channels/817263494818693170/817283469494190131) for further discussion.
3.  Within player-led content (quests, trials, whatever), **the DM of the session is king.**
4.  **All staff and players are expected to respect each others’ time** by remaining present and attentive during gameplay.
5.  Do not leave a quest, trial, hunt, or other event without permission from the DM or person running it, or you will be **[cursed](/docs/the-cursed-role "the cursed role").**
6.  If your DM or the person running your content leaves unexpectedly, contact a @Moderator or @Dungeonmaster for guidance
7.  **Do NOT cheat.** Modifying your loot or experience past their actual values is strictly prohibited.
    1.  If you are controlling an enemy in a Trial or Hunt, **playing the enemy poorly on purpose is cheating.**
    2.  If you are uncertain about anything, ping the DM/DM trainee that posted the record or post your question/concern to #questions.
8.  You **may not play as or with a character on the server** until it has been posted by a DM, DM Trainee, or Admin in [#active-pc-sheet-links](https://discord.com/channels/817263494818693170/817309328348413952).
9.  **RP and Gameplay should not include erotic or overly sexual themes.** A fade to black is generally acceptable.
10.  Monsters in hunts and trials should use their pre-2024 statblocks. MotM statblocks take priority over legacy statblocks. Quest statblocks are on DM-discretion.

##### Tracking PC Activity in Discord

All changes to your PC’s character sheet, canonical events, rewards, downtime results, etc. must be recorded in one of the following four channels:

[#transcript](https://discord.com/channels/817263494818693170/817301000770551828): Players can submit any player-run RP, gametype or event, downtime activity, or other official changes to #transcript for review. Once the post has been moved to #records by staff, it will become official TWR Canon.
