[#transaction-log](https://discord.com/channels/817263494818693170/817297478034522112): Players submit any financial transactions with other players or NPCs (including mundane item purchases) in this channel for review.

[#records](https://discord.com/channels/817263494818693170/817297426335006751): Staff will post final reports from #transcript or related to their own content in Records, where it becomes official and rewards are applied to the player.

[#story-log](https://discord.com/channels/817263494818693170/882397674907783168): Contains the results of quests, large scale plotlines, and other

### Character Interaction Consent

All character interactions are based on the consent of the players controlling them. That means that if someone refuses to interact with someone’s character for any reason, they may do so. This rule is to protect players from unwanted interaction, not to shield players from consequences.

Hirelings

# Hirelings

[![Hireling points breakdown](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/0/0b/Hireling_points_breakdown.webp/revision/latest/scale-to-width-down/180?cb=20241112160125)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/0/0b/Hireling_points_breakdown.webp/revision/latest?cb=20241112160125)

[](/docs/file:hireling-points-breakdown.webp)

### Hirelings

For those of you running organizations and have had questions regarding growth rates and the like, we now have a official system in place to manage wages, growth rates, CRs of hired staff, etc. It will soon be integrated into a larger document for readability and the like, but this is the back end system for folks to use and take a look at. If you have any questions, please ping a <@&879237772047712326> in <#819298824602255360>. If you are running a organization that has NPCs, please note this does not include crafting NPCs or PC-NPCs, please ping a <@&879237772047712326> in <#823408859444019240> so we can add your information to the sheet and get it in <#817297426335006751> to make it official.

https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1viG\_QtNhQLvkYm479pvcepFO37hgQruSIZ9WDb7pqEk/edit?usp=sharing

Also, just as a general addendum, we’re trying to shift to better tracking methods so we have a better grasp on how everything is working together regarding player orgs, the economy, crafting, etc, so we can bring a better experience to you fine folks. The more we know, the better we can make informed decisions regarding the macro systems.

Also also, the system is subject to minor edits as it gets dialed in.

### Hireling Update

Hireling Addendum, minor update Team: @Loremaster

Creature type is restricted to humanoid (already a thing but clarified) Statblock does not nessicary determine appearance, alignment, etc (already a thing but clarified) All of them by default speak common (new) If you have excess high CR creatures you dont want to use, you can use that “slot” for a creature of a lower CR. So for example, if you don’t want to use CR 8 creatures due to costs (or whatever), you can use a CR 3 creature in its place instead. (new) Creatures that use unique named items, such as Steel, can not be used (new)

### Hireling Update 2

Team involved: @Loremaster

Hello lurkers!

This update is an addendum to the previously released Hireling spreadsheet for NPC pricing. Utilizing this, you will have guidelines with which you may hire NPCs over time, up to a predetermined maximum amount, for whatever individual, group, or organizational needs you have. If there is confusion on how this works, please use the attached spreadsheet, or ask a @Loremaster.

Contracting NPC Requirements:

Maximum CR that can be hired as a single unit is capped at CR 20

Total CR across all hired units is capped at CR = 30

Hiring occurs weekly, using a point deduction system (Hire Points)

Points accrue at a rate of 1/week from the time interest is expressed, and equate to the Total CR that can be used to hire units

Point deductions are based against the CR being hired.

Example: Player currently has total of 5 Hire Points. The player can Hire any combination of humanoid creatures less than or equal to the total CR of 5.

Spreadsheet for CR to Hire Points breakdown can be seen here: https://docs.google.com/file/d/1Qv2-Cna8BUpkpW0geMdIO68BIL-coSrd/edit?usp=docslist\_api&filetype=msexcel

Please submit these hire requests to transcript and ping a @Loremaster for approval.

**Attunement and Magic Items for Hirelings** Hirelings can equip and attune to magic items provided to them by their employer based upon their CR, as shown below:

*   CR 1/8 - CR 1/2: Common (no attunement)

*   CR 1 - CR 4: Uncommon (attunement up to 1 item)

*   CR 5 - 10: Rare (attunement up to 2 items)

*   CR 11 - 16: Very Rare (attunement up to 3 items)

*   CR 17+: Legendary (attunement up to 3 items)

*If the hirelings statblock has an armor or weapon listed, they are treated as proficient with all weapons or armor of that same type (e.g. leather - light armor, Shortsword - martial weapons).* If you have questions or concerns, please me know, and I will be working with existing groups/organizations to get you switched over to this new framework

#### From #Patch-Notes

Much discussion and collaboration has gone into the merger of Hireling system with the previously used Organizations system. They will now be brought under one framework, with some added rules for how groups and organizations will work under this system, how hirelings can be equipped with items from their employers, and some promising prospects for future updates.

Individual & Group Hirelings

The ability for individuals and established groups to hire NPCs to augment their personal or collaborative efforts will be broken into one of two categories, with slightly different considerations for each. Hirelings can be reskinned to represent any humanoid race or alignment, and can speak common by default.

Contracting Requirements (Individual)

Hiring updates occur once per week, using a point deduction system (Hire Points)

Points accrue at a rate of 1/week, requiring an approved post from a Loremaster These points are logged via ⁠transcript and the total equates to the Total CR that can be used to hire units for that update.

Point deductions are based on the CR being hired. CR 0 units count as CR 1/8 for this, there is a graph that breaks down these numbers in more detail.

Individuals contracting NPCs will use the 'Deployed Monthly' rate, as opposed the normal Daily/Monthly rate. These expenses are logged via ⁠transaction-log after the update is approved by a Loremaster. These expenses occur at the 1st of every month.

Maximum CR that can be hired as a single unit is capped at CR 20

Total CR that can be hired is capped at CR 20

The screenshot below shows a week-by-week breakdown of how hire points can be spent on the corresponding CR.


Contracting Requirements (Group/Organizational)

Hiring updates occur once per week, using a point deduction system (Hire Points)

Points accrue based upon the modifiers calculated for your group, from 1-10, requiring an approved post from a Loremaster. These points are logged via ⁠transcript and the total equates to the Total CR that can be used to hire units for that update.

Publicity (popularity, reputation, goodwill)

Accessibility (access, reach)

Logistics (training, supplies, budget)

Opportunity (job security, importance, need)

Temp buffs (quests/significant events)

Groups/Organizations consisting of multiple PCs behave as a single ‘entity’ for the sake of point accumulation and spending. Updates will be logged with a single PC representing the group.

Point deductions are based on the CR being hired. CR 0 units count as CR 1/8 for this, there is a graph that breaks down these numbers in more detail.

Groups/Organizations contracting NPCs will use the normal 'Monthly' rate. These expenses are logged via ⁠transaction-log after the update is approved by a Loremaster. These expenses occur at the 1st of every month.

Maximum CR that can be hired as a single unit is capped at CR 20

[![NPC pricing](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/c/c1/NPC_pricing.jpg/revision/latest/scale-to-width-down/180?cb=20241112155947)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/c/c1/NPC_pricing.jpg/revision/latest?cb=20241112155947)

[](/docs/file:npc-pricing.jpg)

Leaving The Server

# Leaving the Server

## Leaving TWR

Sometimes, you feel the need to take a break from the server or leave. That's okay! However, if you DO plan on leaving, you should retire your PCs so that the sheets are not lost.

When a player leaves, their sheets will be moved to the [Inactive PC Links](https://discord.com/channels/817263494818693170/1268620942200799339) thread.

If you wish to bring back an Inactive PC, just post in [#transcript](https://discord.com/channels/817263494818693170/817301000770551828) with a link to their inactive sheet and a staff member will move it for you.

Misc. Rulings

# Misc. Rulings

### Warding & Vehicles

Gargantuan sea and land vehicles in addition to moving landmasses (such as Elysium) can have wards, glyphs, etc placed upon them as though they were a stationary structure or location.

### Raising the Mast

Takes 5 min to raise or lower the mast on a ship if fully staffed.

### Astral Projection

Expending resources in the Astral Projection form carries over to the Main body. This is not an errata just a clarification as RAW it only says any damage or effect on the projection doesnt carry over.

Paragon Systems

# Paragon Systems

## Prestige of the Gods

### Prestige of the Gods

A character who has reached level 20 and achieved the 6 post-20 milestones may choose to undergo a divine transformation in which the gods revert their mind and body to the state of a neophyte adventurer. Starting again at level 1-3, a ‘prestiged’ adventurer may begin again - choosing any new class, feats, or even remodeling their own personal attributes. They retain all knowledge, wealth, and any items they had prior to this.

Upon choosing to undergo the transformation, the adventurer may choose a **cosmetic effect** to be bestowed upon them. This effect must be proposed by the player, and approved by a DM in the request to prestige the character (in #transcript). Examples of such an effect could include: Flittering sparkles that follow the character, an ethereal halo above their head, or a glowing/changing tattoo.

## Paragon Systems: Gestalt and Ascendant

Gestalts are now considered a Paragon system, with the addition of Ascendants, which operate in a similar way - granting class levels past 20 - without having to reset.

A character’s Paragon stage represents the number of times they have completed a full class level system (finished p20), starting at a value of 1. This number is used to determine experience gain, and for communicating your character’s progress, as seen in “How are Levels Counted”. Such systems and changes can and will be subject to change and active monitoring.

#### How Levels are Counted

Levels are counted through the following format A#L#, where A represents your paragon stage, and L represents your remaining levels. For example, a P26 wizard with 10 additional levels from this system would be an A1L10. This can be similarly done with tiers.

This is similarly done with gestalt, in which the format is G#L#, where G represents your Paragon stage, and L represents your current level, up to 26. This can be similarly done with tiers.

## Ascendant Paragon

Characters can now progress past level 20, continuing to gain character levels, being known as Ascendants rather than Gestalts. The character’s XP counter would reset to zero, though they would keep their character levels, proficiency bonus, and P20 rewards. Such Ascendants would proceed to level another class, starting from T1, and gaining an appropriate amount of XP, with the ability to do so again upon completing P20.

### Build Rules

*   To qualify a PC must be fully leveled, meaning level 20 with all post 20 levels completed.
*   Ascendant PCs cannot gain experience from hunts, trials, DM-XP, Transcript-XP, etc. They can only gain experience from RP-XP or Quests.
*   In quests that involve combat, while you will receive XP for the RP, you will receive no XP for the creatures killed.
*   Ascendant hit dice are restricted to the two highest hit dice per level (as opposed to getting all hit dice per level).
*   Rather than gaining the save proficiencies of all classes, Ascendants choose one strong save and one weak save from the options given to them by their classes:
    *   Strong Saves: Dex, Con, Wis
    *   Weak Saves: Str, Int, Cha

*   Any changes to the locked classes or subclasses are allowed and follow the normal retraining rules.
*   Due to the documentation required for this system, character sheets must be handled in G-Sheets.
*   ASIs stack, but do not stack past the normal cap of 20 unless otherwise specified. Stats may not exceed 30 for any reason.
*   You can not take the same feat multiple times unless otherwise specified. Feats that scale based on level are calculated based on your class levels.
*   Ascendant PC's, May only hunt, trial or delve with PC's who are also Ascendant
*   Spell save DCs and spellcasting follow multi classing rules. Spell Save DCs and Spell Attack Bonuses calculate based on the class providing the spell.
*   Class features that references class levels are self contained for the applicable class and paragon cycle

### Stacking

Class Features, Spell Slots, Proficiencies and Stat Changes generally stack unless otherwise specified.

#### Exceptions

The following features do NOT stack when creating a Ascendant PC:

*   Proficiency Bonus
*   Extra Attack
*   Unarmored Defense
*   Duplicated Paladin Auras
*   Movement speed increases from duplicate sources.
*   Bladesong, and other features with limited uses such as rage, do not get doubled buffs but do get a increased number of uses.

### Prestige Classes

The Ascendant Paragon system also includes Prestige Classes, classes that have specific requirements for their use and play, but are generally stronger than the normal classes. Further information can be found at the [Prestige Classes](/docs/prestige-classes "prestige classes") page.

## Gestalt Paragon

This is a variant to the typical prestige system, and while it may grant great boons it also has great restrictions as well. When you Gestalt Paragon, you must post in #transcript including both the new and original sheets for reference.

### Gestalt Overview

*   Upon gestalt prestige you can select a class to level alongside your current one.
*   The starting proficiencies, skills, and other benefits are granted to the character.
*   When you level up, both classes level simultaneously, and you gain the benefits from both.
*   Put simply, you have build A which was gestalted, getting added to build B. This is similar to if you had two separate characters who were merged into one.
*   ASIs granted through level ups can be reassigned.
*   This process can be repeated, allowing for a singular character to slowly grow in power over the years as they grow more involved in the world at large.

Cumulative Level

*   Cumulative Level is the total number of levels from all your classes combined.
*   Cumulative Level is used to calculate what trials, hunts, and quests you can be involved in.

#### Current Level

*   Current Level is the current base level of the character and calculated as it would be normally, as though you were leveling a normal character.
*   The Current Level is what is used to calculate proficiency bonus, character progression, and RP experience gains. Things that scale based on level use your Current Level.

### Gestalt Reversion

*   At any time a character can revert a gestalt prestige. This will reset the character to a level 1 class of their choice akin to a normal prestige, provide zero cosmetic and stat benefits, and allow the character to once again gain experience from trials, hunts, etc.
*   The character will then be locked out of the gestalt prestige system for 6 months.
*   This can be done at any time and at any level.

### Build Rules

*   All Gestalt PCs begin at level 1
*   Class levels do not mix between Gestalt Prestiges. Each prestige is treated as a unique PC build.
*   To qualify a PC must be fully leveled, meaning level 20 with all post 20 levels completed.
*   Gestalt PCs cannot gain experience from hunts, trials, DM-XP, Transcript-XP, etc. They can only gain experience from RP-XP or Quests.
*   In quests that involve combat, while you will receive XP for the RP, you will receive no XP for the creatures killed.
*   Gestalt PCs may not change their race, background, Racial ASIs, class or subclass choice, or anything else regarding the basis of the character which was set during their initial creation.
*   On level up, the initial class chosen will follow the same build path (for example Fighter Champion) and multi class level split (if applicable) that the character had PRIOR to that prestige.
*   Gestalt hit dice are restricted to the two highest hit dice per level (as opposed to gestalts getting all hit dice per level).
*   Rather than gaining the save proficiencies of all classes, Gestalts choose one strong save and one weak save from the options given to them by their classes:
    *   Strong Saves: Dex, Con, Wis
    *   Weak Saves: Str, Int, Cha

*   Gestalt PC's may choose to redistribute their ASI's and redo their first class in any way that is within the rules.
*   Due to the documentation required for this system, character sheets must be handled in G-Sheets.
*   ASIs stack, but do not stack past the normal cap of 20 unless otherwise specified. Stats may not exceed 30 for any reason.
*   You can not take the same feat multiple times unless otherwise specified. Feats that scale based on level are calculated based on your Current Level not your Total Level.
*   Gestalt PCs may attune to any item they own, however, Gestalt PCs may not use items which exceed their “Current Level” during hunts or trials with non-Gestalt PCs.
*   Gestalt PCs may choose to join hunts above their Current Level, but not if non-Gestalt PCs are present. This does not affect Body Retrieval Hunts. If a gestalt hunting party is a mix of tiers, they can not recover a body.
*   Spell save DCs and spellcasting follow multi classing rules. Spell Save DCs and Spell Attack Bonuses calculate based on the class providing the spell.

### Stacking

Class Features, Spell Slots, Proficiencies and Stat Changes generally stack unless otherwise specified.

#### Exceptions

The following features do NOT stack when creating a Gestalt PC:

*   Proficiency Bonus
*   Extra Attack
*   Unarmored Defense
*   Duplicated Paladin Auras
*   Movement speed increases from duplicate sources.
*   Bladesong, and other features with limited uses such as rage, do not get doubled buffs but do get a increased number of uses.

## Paragon Wish Patch:

tl;dr - Introducing a very arduous method to regain wish through a whole gestalt prestige and questline using a Post20 Blessing/Boon Slot.

### Requirements

1.  Paragon into a spellcasting class that also learns wish
2.  Complete self RP and DM-led quests (at least 4) throughout your prestige that meet the following guidelines
    1.  Integrate with an existing group of worshipers for a particular deity
    2.  Receive character references from leadership within the church/group
    3.  Reach a level that would otherwise grant you wish from your prestige class
    4.  Travel to the chosen deity’s home plane and petition their aid directly with offerings and references prepared
    5.  Donate at least 2 items to the god directly, sacrificing them to prove your devotion. One item must be of legendary power and the second must be of great personal importance to your PC.
    6.  Perform a quest for that god directly, run by a Dungeon master and approved by at least 2 more DMs or LMs

### Regaining Wish

Once all of the requirements have been met and your PC is eligible for a Post20 Boon/Blessing Slot, the God will decide whether or not to grant you the Blessing of Forgiveness:

**Blessing of Forgiveness** - This blessing removes one existing instance of permanent loss of wish. Once a creature has received this blessing, they can no longer receive it from the same deity.

Note: It may not be possible to convince any other gods to grant this blessing or you may attract significant attention from your previous god if so. It would require unfathomable time and effort to regain wish a second time.

## Gestalt and Ascension Interaction:

Gestalt or Ascension can be chosen when a character finishes p20 or completes a paragon cycle (finishes p20 on another class system). In doing so, a character may choose to Gestalt as a way to prestige and change through their class choices, though would still level according to their paragon cycle number.

For example, a P3L0 (has finished this cycle but has not started a new one) may choose to Gestalt, and become a G4L1, where they may again choose to move back into ascension after leveling to p20.

Player Run Npcs

# Player-Run NPCs

## Player-ran NPCs

A player may run an NPC with the following caveats. Breaking any requires the permission of a @Dungeonmaster or @Loremaster.

1: The NPC must act according to its character, or alignment.

2: The NPC cannot be a high-standing or of great importance individual.

3: The NPC must be given adequate reason to physically exist in this plane.

4: The NPC cannot grant PC's mechanical benefits of any sort. Items, Crafting, Gifts, all of the like. Only acceptable as shopkeep or storefronts selling items.

\----

Rule 1 dictates that monsters must act relevant to lore. To break such action, or to play an outlier, requires permission.

Rule 2 dictates that NPC roleplayed must be of low bearing. Shopkeep, Bartenders, Apprentices and the generic like. A player cannot run any characters which could influence a greater plot that they are not actively running.

Rule 3 dictates that no creature may exist without bearing. A mind flayer would not exist in a town as a vampire lord would not be sleeping in a forest.

Rule 4 dictates that NPC may not grant mechanical benefits. Pure and simple, save shopkeeps selling mundane items at reasonable (per market discretion) prices.

Post 20 Rewards

# Post-20 Rewards

## Post 20 Rewards

A Player Character is considered to be Post20 if they have reached 385,000xp, and receive rewards at the following thresholds:

| XP Threshold | Reward |
| --- | --- |
| 385,000 | ASI +1 |
| 415,000 | AS1 +1 |
| 445,000 | Boon/Feat/Blessing |
| 475,000 | AS1 +1 |
| 505,000 | AS1 +1 |
| 535,000 | Boon/Feat/Blessing |

### Post-20 Rules

*   Boon/Blessing/Feat choices are permanent.
*   Your PC's first Boon/Blessing may be chosen from the list of Boons or Blessings once the slot has been unlocked (use the !boon or !blessing command in discord)
    *   For PCs who earned Boons via a quest before this change, you may choose the next Boon slot that you unlock through the Post-20 or Paragon systems
*   Feats can be selected once unlocked.
*   Feats can be retrained into quested Boons/Blessings if desired
*   Boon and Blessing choices are permanent
*   Blessings/Charms can be removed by the granting Deity at DM discretion.
*   Blessings are not limited to a fixed list, and can be shaped and created to better fit a circumstance, at the DM’s discretion. This would require a majority vote to proceed.
*   Post 20 Boons and Blessings can be obtained using the Wish spell, but the Wish must be worded appropriately. This only applies to the second Boon slot of a PC's first 20 levels. PCs cannot use Wish to gain boons or blessings for any paragon cycle boon slots.
*   *A comprehensive list of blessing and boons can be viewed on discord using !boon or !blessing*
*   Please post your Post 20 rewards in #transcript as you receive them
*   A PC that gets Post20 rewards is still considered a level 20 character for all intents and purposes

Prestige Systems

# Prestige Systems

## Prestige of the Gods

### Prestige of the Gods

A character who has reached level 20 and achieved the 6 post-20 milestones may choose to undergo a divine transformation in which the gods revert their mind and body to the state of a neophyte adventurer. Starting again at level 1-3, a ‘prestiged’ adventurer may begin again - choosing any new class, feats, or even remodeling their own personal attributes. They retain all knowledge, wealth, and any items they had prior to this.

Upon choosing to undergo the transformation, the adventurer may choose a cosmetic effect to be bestowed upon them. This effect must be proposed by the player, and approved by a DM in the request to prestige the character (in #transcript). Examples of such an effect could include: Flittering sparkles that follow the character, an ethereal halo above their head, or a glowing/changing tattoo.

## Paragon Systems: Gestalt and Ascendant

Gestalts are now considered a Paragon system, with the addition of Ascendants, which operate in a similar way - granting class levels past 20 - without having to reset.

A character’s Paragon stage represents the number of times they have completed a full class level system (finished p20), starting at a value of 1. This number is used to determine experience gain, and for communicating your character’s progress, as seen in “How are Levels Counted”. Such systems and changes can and will be subject to change and active monitoring.

### Ascendant

Characters can now progress past level 20, continuing to gain character levels, being known as Ascendants rather than Gestalts. The character’s XP counter would reset to zero, though they would keep their character levels, proficiency bonus, and P20 rewards. Such Ascendants would proceed to level another class, starting from T1, and gaining an appropriate amount of XP, with the ability to do so again upon completing P20.

#### How can you Swap

For Gestalts currently in place, they may choose to revert to P20 and take 20 levels in each class system they have already finished leveling. They would take levels equal to their level before the shift in the class levels they had taken. For example, an A2 Level 13 whose base classes are 20 Fighter and 20 Wizard, and is currently leveling Artificer, would revert to 20 Fighter, 20 Wizard, and 13 Artificer, along with P20 rewards.

#### How Levels are Counted

Levels are counted through the following format A#L#, where A represents your paragon stage, and L represents your remaining levels. For example, a P26 wizard with 10 additional levels from this system would be an A1L10. This can be similarly done with tiers.

This is similarly done with gestalt, in which the format is G#L#, where G represents your Paragon stage, and L represents your current level, up to 26. This can be similarly done with tiers.

#### After Ascension:

When a pre-existing character begins their first ascension cycle (starting to level a new class system) or converts from gestalt, they can choose to adjust their base ability scores through point buy and may choose to swap current feats, asi, and p20 asi at no penalty. This must be submitted through transcript

#### Gestalt and Ascension Interaction:

Gestalt or Ascension can be chosen when a character finishes p20 or completes a paragon cycle (finishes p20 on another class system). In doing so, a character may choose to Gestalt as a way to prestige and change through their class choices, though would still level according to their paragon cycle number.

For example, a P3L0 (has finished this cycle but has not started a new one) may choose to Gestalt, and become a G4L1, where they may again choose to move back into ascension after leveling to p20.

#### Changes to Gestalt System:

Gestalt would remain with the ability to adjust stats upon reset, as stated previously. In addition, they would no longer be required to level their base class as they would normally, and may choose to level a different class instead.

#### Retroactive Adjustments:

For those who choose to stay in gestalt, characters may receive retroactive rewards. Firstly, a character may adjust base stats and character levels, submitting a new sheet to transcript along with the old (make a copy and do changes from there) to be used as a reference. Additionally, players may reclaim downtime for changing class levels for characters or training characters, since the gestalt system was first introduced. Players must submit the time they would receive to transcript, with relevant links, where they would then be able to use that time in the future. Players may complete this same process for the gold cost.

###### Note:

All character changes must be done through the transcript. It is recommended that you make a copy of your old gsheet when adjusting things.

PART 6 | GESTALT PRESTIGE

Gestalt Prestige System

This is a variant to the typical prestige system, and while it may grant great boons it also has great restrictions as well. When you Gestalt Prestige, you must post in #transcript including both the new and original sheets for reference. Once approved, the new sheet will be posted in #3-gestalt-pc-links

Please note this system is still in playtesting and is subject to change at any time

Gestalt Overview

All Gestalt PCs begin at level 1

Upon gestalt prestige you can select a class to level alongside your current one.

The starting proficiencies, skills, and other benefits are granted to the character. See Here for a detailed explanation of how class features stack.

When you level up, both classes level simultaneously, and you gain the benefits from both.

Put simply, you have build A which was prestiged and locked in, getting added to build B. This is similar to if you had two separate characters who were merged into one.

ASIs granted through level ups can be reassigned.

Original class and subclass levels are locked in.

This process can be repeated, allowing for a singular character to slowly grow in power over the years as they grow more involved in the world at large.

Cumulative Level

Cumulative Level is the total number of levels from all your classes combined.

Cumulative Level is used to calculate what trials, hunts, and quests you can be involved in.

Current Level

Current Level is the current base level of the character and calculated as it would be normally, as though you were leveling a normal character.

The Current Level is what is used to calculate proficiency bonus, character progression, and RP experience gains. Things that scale based on level use your Current Level.

Gestalt Reversion

At any time a character can revert a gestalt prestige. This will reset the character to a level 1 class of their choice akin to a normal prestige, provide zero cosmetic and stat benefits, and allow the character to once again gain experience from trials, hunts, etc.

The character will then be locked out of the gestalt prestige system for 6 months.

This can be done at any time and at any level.

Restrictions

Class levels do not mix between Gestalt Prestiges. Each prestige is treated as a unique PC build.

To qualify a PC must be fully leveled, meaning level 20 with all post 20 levels completed.

Gestalt PCs cannot gain experience from hunts, trials, DM-XP, Transcript-XP, etc. They can only gain experience from RP-XP or Quests.

In quests that involve combat, while you will receive XP for the RP, you will receive no XP for the creatures killed.

Gestalt PCs may not change their race, background, Racial ASIs, class or subclass choice, or anything else regarding the basis of the character which was set during their initial creation.

On level up, the initial class chosen will follow the same build path (for example Fighter Champion) and multi class level split (if applicable) that the character had PRIOR to that prestige.

Gestalt hit dice are restricted to the two highest hit dice per level (as opposed to gestalts getting all hit dice per level).

Rather than gaining the save proficiencies of all classes, Gestalts choose one strong save and one weak save from the options given to them by their classes:

Strong Saves Weak Saves

DEX STR

CON INT

WIS CHA

Any changes to the locked classes or subclasses are allowed and follow the normal retraining rules based on the level of the class being retrained.

Due to the documentation required for this system, character sheets must be handled in G-Sheets.

ASIs stack, but do not stack past the normal cap of 20 unless otherwise specified. Stats may not exceed 30 for any reason.

You can not take the same feat multiple times unless otherwise specified. Feats that scale based on level are calculated based on your Current Level not your Total Level.

Gestalt PCs may attune to any item they own, however, Gestalt PCs may not use items which exceed their “Current Level” during hunts or trials with non-Gestalt PCs.

Gestalt PCs may choose to join hunts above their Current Level, but not if non-Gestalt PCs are present. This does not affect Body Retrieval Hunts. If a gestalt hunting party is a mix of tiers, they can not recover a body.

Spell save DCs and spellcasting follow multi classing rules. Spell Save DCs and Spell Attack Bonuses calculate based on the class providing the spell.

Combining Gestalt Classes

Class Features, Spell Slots, Proficiencies and Stat Changes generally stack unless otherwise specified.

Exceptions

The following features do NOT stack when creating a Gestalt PC:

Proficiency Bonus

Multi Attack

Unarmored Defense

Duplicated Paladin Auras

Movement speed increases from duplicate sources.

Bladesong, and other features with limited uses such as rage, do not get doubled buffs but do get a increased number of uses.

Gestalt FighterWatermark

Gestalt/Wish Patch:

tl;dr - Introducing a very arduous method to regain wish through a whole gestalt prestige and questline using a Post20 Blessing/Boon Slot.

Requirements

Gestalt prestige into a spellcasting class that also learns wish

Complete self RP and DM-led quests (at least 4) throughout your prestige that meet the following guidelines

Integrate with an existing group of worshipers for a particular deity

Receive character references from leadership within the church/group

Reach a level that would otherwise grant you wish from your prestige class

Travel to the chosen deity’s home plane and petition their aid directly with offerings and references prepared

Donate at least 2 items to the god directly, sacrificing them to prove your devotion. One item must be of legendary power and the second must be of great personal importance to your PC.

Perform a quest for that god directly, run by a Dungeonmaster and approved by at least 2 more DMs or LMs

Regaining Wish

Once all of the requirements have been met and your PC is eligible for a Post20 Boon/Blessing Slot, the God will decide whether or not to grant you the Blessing of Forgiveness:

Blessing of Forgiveness - This blessing removes one existing instance of permanent loss of wish. Once a creature has received this blessing, they can no longer receive it from the same deity.

Note: It may not be possible to convince any other gods to grant this blessing or you may attract significant attention from your previous god if so. It would require unfathomable time and effort to regain wish a second time.

### Regarding Simulacrums

1.  Sims Simulacrums are considered NPCs. Paragons will not be able to earn rpxp and quest xp by way of proxy

### Gestalt Sheet Link

https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1Xxia-UzLvdfPTbyFgmM\_WYM3HlRVFKcwsGwwVq-ceoI/edit?usp=sharing

Research Downtime

# Research Downtime

**Research and Lore Writing**

A player would access a forum for research and lore writing. Then, ping dms with the goal they have in mind. In character, the player character must access a place where information relevant to their goal can be found. Or go and acquire objects or permission to do so.

For research, using the Forgotten Realms wiki as a basis for lore, pitch ideas to dms and other players. The option to run your own adventure will be made available with dm oversight.

For lore, pitch your timelines, details, and events to dms to approve and append to the server storyline

Research

@ \[Player\]  \[PC Name\]

Brief Description of your research/What you are trying to find. And location of conducting research.

Duration:

Costs: 50 gp + \[Intervals of 50, for a + \[X\] bonus to your check

     \-Library fees

     \-Stationery supplies

     \-Anything else deemed necessary for his research

Cost Doubles for every +1

Intelligence Check Result

\[Link\]

Start and End Dates

RP is encouraged and beneficial.  Lack of RP will often lead to a lack of results.

Link to beginning of RP:

Link to end of RP:

Retraining Rules

# Retraining Rules

## Retraining Rules

#### Class Retraining

By spending 10 workweeks of downtime, a character can choose to replace one of their levels in one class with a level in another class. This time is reduced by a number of workweeks equal to the character’s ability modifier in the key ability of the new class. If their new class has more than one key ability, the characters lowest ability modifier should be used for this calculation. The character must have a minimum of 13 in the key abilities of both their current class and their new class They must have an instructor of at least 1 level higher (or be level 20) than your new level in the new class and must spend 50gp per week for the instructor and relevant supplies.

#### Fighting Style Retraining

(For Fighters, Rangers, Paladins, Blood Hunters, and College of Swords Bards only) By spending a week of downtime, you can choose one of the (insert class name here) fighting style you know and replace it with another fighting style from the (insert class name here) class.

#### Feat / ASI Retraining

By spending a week of downtime, you can choose to replace one Feat or ASI you qualify for with another Feat or ASI you qualify for. Post20 ASIs can be retrained only to ASIs, and Post20 Feats can be retrained only to Boons or Feats.

#### Spell Retraining

(For sorcerers, warlocks, bards and rangers only) By spending a week of downtime,you can choose one of the (insert class name here) spells you know and replace it with another spell from the (insert class name here) spell list, which also must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

### Mystic Arcanum Retraining

Warlocks can spend 1 week of downtime to retrain one of their mystic arcanum spells for another spell that is applicable for that version of the mystic arcanum classfeat.

## RP Reduction

When you undergo retraining, you can submit an RP-XP request for roleplay related to the retraining and receive a reduction in the overall time spent retraining. Each RP-XP request is worth a 5% reduction, up to a maximum of 25%.

Retroactive Downtime

# Retroactive Downtime

We get it, you can’t always plan your character’s lives in real time. That’s why we’ve designed Retroactive Downtime, which lets you apply unused downtime at a reduced wait to cover gaps in gameplay and incetivize coming back to TWR!

Starting from the last date your PC used their downtime, you can now file a report to use **1/3 of the total days (rounded down**) on a **single goal or downtime activity** with staff approval.

## How does it work?

Players wishing to file for retroactive downtime must complete the report on the right and submit it to #transcript. Staff will determine the appropriate daily value for your character and resources and return the report with any important notes.

#### Custom Retroactive Downtime

We are also introducing a few new forms of “retroactive” downtime you can add to the report:

##### General Adventuring: XP Bonus

Characters who spend their time hunting, traveling, or otherwise generally adventuring receive bonus XP:

| Tier | Bonus XP Cap (Per Day) |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | 5 xp |
| 2 | 20 xp |
| 3 | 100 xp |
| 4 | 200 xp |
| Post-20 | 300 xp |

##### Service to a Deity: Favor Bonus

Characters who spend time in service of a deity will be rewarded for their faith with favor:

| Tier | Bonus Favor Cap (Per Day) |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | 1 Favor |
| 2 | 3 Favor |
| 3 | 5 Favor |
| 4 | 8 Favor |
| Post-20 | 10 Favor |

##### The Career Path: Gold Bonus

Through smart investments, performance bonuses, side hustles and promotions, business ownership, etc., you receive bonus Gold:

| Tier | Bonus Gold Cap (Per Day) |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | 5 gp |
| 2 | 25 gp |
| 3 | 50 gp |
| 4 | 200 gp |
| Post-20 | 250 gp |

#### Roleplay Bonuses

Players who perform roleplay with themselves or others to justify their downtime may include a summary and link to the roleplay for additional rewards.

##### Transcript Report: Retroactive Downtime

:clock1: Retroactive Downtime Request :clock1:

@(Player)

Last Downtime Date: DD/MM/YYYY

Retroactive Downtime: (Days since last downtime) / 3 = X

Summary of Downtime: Type of a brief summary of your character’s actions during their time away. You may only select a single downtime option or project.

Specifics: If you are selecting a specific downtime option as outlined in the source books, this document, or other TWR systems, include the report here using the “Retroactive Downtime” value above as the number of days spent. Then perform any rolls, checks, transactions, etc. as necessary here. You may also choose to perform more complicated projects such as starting a business, building a home, making friends in the form of NPCs, etc. through expanded roleplay.

Staff Review: Craftmasters and Dungeonmasters will review the report and consider any complications, necessary materials, or other concerns and work with you to apply your downtime in a reasonable way.

Approved By: @(DM or CM)

Requirements to Complete: Any costs, roleplay requirements, or contingencies for the downtime to become official

##### Plain Report

:clock1: Retroactive Downtime Request :clock1:

@(Player)

Last Downtime Date: DD/MM/YYYY

Retroactive Downtime: (Days since last downtime) / 3 = X

Summary of Downtime:

Specifics:

Staff Review:

Approved By: @(DM or CM)

Requirements to Complete:

Shared Campaigns

# Shared Campaigns

## DnD Beyond Shared Campaigns

Join the campaigns provided below to gain access to DnD sourcebooks for free. A maximum of 12 Players can join per campaign. Please ensure ALL of your characters are only in ONE campaign link. Spreading your Characters across different campaigns can lead to disciplinary action.

If a campaign reaches its max player count, try another link. If all become full, please notify a DM.
