Soulbound Item

# Soulbound Item

## Soulbound Items

*In the ancient epochs, when the world was young and the weave of magic was still being shaped, the gods and primordial forces sought to leave their mark upon the mortal realm. They imbued certain objects with Runes of their essence, creating artifacts that resonated with the very souls of those who wielded them. These are the Soulbound Items.*

*A Soulbound Item is more than just a tool; it is a companion, a reflection of the bond between the bearer and the object. To attune to such an item is to forge a pact, intertwining one's fate with the artifact's power. As the wielder grows in strength and experience, so too does the Soulbound Item, awakening latent abilities and evolving in response to the challenges faced together.*

*However, this bond is not without its trials. The path to unlocking a Soulbound Item's full potential is fraught with challenges, requiring the bearer to prove their worth and commitment. Only through perseverance, valor, and a deep connection can the true power of the Soulbound Item be realized.*

## Soulbound Item Mechanics

1.  A Player Character can only ever be attuned to one Soulbound Item
2.  The Item is a *relic*

### Progressing the Soulbound Item

1.  Each Soulbound Item has its progression on the relevant Item Progression table
2.  At certain levels the weapon gains one of 4 types of features, Base, Ability, Unique and Runes. Each feature will have options to choose from.
3.  For Ability and Unique features, The player can decide whether to take a Novice Rank option or to promote an previously chosen option to the next rank
4.  Runes provide minor benefits, where some Runes you need more than 1 before unlocking its benefits
5.  When you choose an option as part of a feature you cannot choose that option again, other to promote it, unless it's stated otherwise.
6.  Ability and Unique feature ranks are constrained limited by the strength of its owner. To unlock an Expert feature the owner must be level 11 or higher, to unlock a Master feature they must be level 17 or higher
7.  The Weapon can at no point be at a higher level than it's owner

#### Item Leveling

1.  While the item is attuned to a character, any experience that the player gains while attuned to the item is split 50/50 between the player and their soulbound item
    1.  The XP will be scaled to the item's level via an alias.
    2.  This includes XP from random hunts, dungeons, raids and quests.
    3.  It is the players choice if up to 50% of RPXP goes to the weapon
    4.  Example:
        1.  If a level 8 Player gets 5000XP for Killing a Monster in a hunt and the item is also level 8, the Player and the Item each gets 2500XP
        2.  If the weapon was at level 5, Player would get 2500XP which is 18% of XP Required to level to level 9. Thus the Item will get 18% of the exp required to go from level 5 to level 6, which is 1350 XP.
    5.  When a player caps their level, the Item will still get 50% of the Exp that the player would have received
    6.  This seems like complicated math, but there will be an alias to take care of all that
2.  Paragons:
    1.  50% of the experience gained from non-hunt combat participation can still go towards the weapon, this will be scaled for the Weapons level
    2.  Paragon's Soulbound Items receive 50% of the Experience they would have gotten from combat. This includes Non-targetted hunts, Delves, Trials and Raids

### Obtaining a Soulbound Item

1.  The secret of creating a soulbound item has been unlocked, but as the magic is tied to the wielder and not the crafting they can only be crafted at its common stage.
2.  In lieu of choosing an uncommon item tied to the backstory of a submitted character, a player can choose a Soulbound\* item, with the item starting at the same level of the Character, however this should be covered in the backstory
3.  Soulbound Items can be traded, but they will start at level 1 for a new PC and progress will be reset. For all intents and purposes when being traded, they use common item selling values.

### Retraining Item Levels

1.  By spending 1 workweek of downtime a Level feature can be retrained to a different option for that level.
2.  Only a single level can be retrained at a a time
    *   If you are Master of item Feature A, and want to retrain the weapon into a Journeyman of Path B and Path A, you will spend 1 workweek to go to Expert Path A and Novice Path B and then another workweek to reach Journeyman Path A and Journeyman Path B

### Reset

1.  A soulbound weapon can be reset at anytime. This does mean that that it restarts at level 1, and any progress or experience it gained will be lost.

### Homebrewing

1.  Players are Encouraged to Homebrew their own Unique Features and Runes via [Homebrew System & Index](/docs/homebrew-system-%26-index "homebrew system & index"), this will expand the options for other players as well

### Future Updates

1.  As this is a new system, it may receive some balancing updates, either with nerf, buffs or sideways adjustments. Participation in this system acknowledges that your Item may change.

## Progression

The following is the General Progression of each Soulbound Item.

| **Soulbound Level Progression Table** |
| --- |
| Weapon level | Item Feature |
| 1 | Base |
| 2 | Unique Feature 1 |
| 3 | Ability 1 |
| 4 | Rune 1 |
| 5 | Ability 2 |
| 6 | Rune 2 |
| 7 | Ability 3 |
| 8 | Unique Feature 2 |
| 9 | Ability 4 |
| 10 | Rune 3 |
| 11 | Ability 5 |
| 12 | Rune 4 |
| 13 | Unique Feature 3 |
| 14 | Rune 5 |
| 15 | Ability 6 |
| 16 | Rune 6 |
| 17 | Ability 7 |
| 18 | Unique Feature 4 |
| 19 | Rune 7 |
| 20 | Ability 8 |


The page will first look at the Base and Ability Features, then it will list the Runes, First general and then the different type Runes. Lastly we will look at the available Unique features for each type of item.

## Base and Ability Features

### Soulbound Weapons

*"To draw steel that bleeds with your soul is to accept its hunger. A soulbound weapon is not a weapon forged in a smith’s fire but in the crucible of oath and intent. The weapon listens to its wielder’s will, whispering back in sparks of battle-born instinct. As its bearer grows, the weapon awakens, reshaping itself to mirror the paths chosen. In time, the warrior and the weapon are no longer two, but one sharpened edge against fate itself."*

#### **Base:**

Each weapon starts with the following Base Stats, when you unlock a Ability feature, you can choose one of the below options to promote to a higher rank.

**Base Attack:** Attacks made with this weapon is considered to be magical

**Path Die:** D4, this applies anytime a Path option references Path Damage Die

**Weapon DC**: *8 + Wielder Proficiency Bonus*, whenever a Path feature requires a saving throw or allows the casting of a spell, it will use the Weapon DC has it's DC

**Charges**: 2, Some Path option may Require the use of Charges. Charges reset to full at dawn.

#### **Ability Features**

| Rank | Base Attack | Path Die | Weapon DC | Charges |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Novice | +1 Attack Bonus | d6 | Base + 2 | Base + 2 |
| Journeyman | +1 Attack and Damage Bonus | d8 | Base + 3 | Base + 3 |
| Expert | +2 Attack and Damage Bonus | d10 | Base + 4 | Base + 4 |
| Master | +3 Attack and Damage Bonus | d12 | Base + Wielders Choice of Ability Modifier, set when choosing | Wielders Choice of Ability Modifier, set when choosing |

### Soulbound Armor and Soulbound Shields

*To carry a soulbound ward is to carry more than protection — it is a covenant between bearer and bulwark. These wards are not merely hammered plates or shaped wood; they are vessels of resolve, tempered by sacrifice and sealed with spirit. At first they guard as any armor or shield might, but as the bond deepens, the ward awakens — answering the bearer’s instincts, hardening in defiance, softening in stealth, or shining in defense of others. In time, the warrior is no longer clad in armor, but in a living testament of their soul’s endurance.*

#### **Base:**

Each armor or shield starts with the following Base Stats, when you unlock a Ability feature, you can choose one of the below options to promote to a higher rank

*   **Base AC Bonus:** determined by the armor type (light, medium, heavy) or shield.
*   **Ward Die: d4,**
*   **Armor DC:** Used for any feature that requires a saving throw or reactive effect. Starts at ***8 + Wielder proficiency bonus***.

*   **Charges:** 2 (for active Ward abilities).Charges reset to full at dawn.

#### **Physical and Mental Features**

| Rank | AC Bonus | Ward Die | Armor DC | Charges |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Novice | Armor: Your Armor provides you with a damage threshold of 2(light armor), 3 (medium armor) or 5(heavy armor) any damage your receive that is less than 5, would not effect your hp, but you take all damage if it is above the threshold . This applies after any reductions
Shield: +1 AC

 | d6 | Base + 2 | Base + 2 |
| Journeyman | Armor: Temp HP, +1 AC

Shield: +2 AC

 | d8 | Base+ 3 | Base+ 3 |
| Expert | Armor: Temp HP, +2 AC

Shield: +3 AC

 | d10 | Base + 4 | Base + 4 |
| Master | Armor: Temp HP, +3 AC

Shield: +3 AC, When you're hit by an attack while you're wielding the shield, any Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage dealt to you by that attack is reduced by 3 Damage

 | d12 | Base + Wielders Choice of Ability Modifier, set when choosing | Wielders Choice of Ability Modifier, set when choosing |

### Soulbound Foci

*To wield a conduit is to place your soul in the current of the unseen. A staff, gem, or relic can be bound not by chains, but by resonance—its lattice aligned with the bearer’s essence. At first, it channels spells as any focus would, but soon the conduit hums with hidden voices, amplifying thought, bending the weave, answering only to the one who claimed it. By journey’s end, mage and conduit are indistinguishable: the caster’s will flows as lightning, and the relic itself becomes the echo of their soul.*

A Soulbound focus can be any focus as determined by the Spellcasting feature of class. However the Owner can only attune to it if the item is an acceptable focus. This does include instruments and tools as well.

If the player character can use a weapon as a spellcasting focus, they can choose whether to use it as a soulbound focus or weapon. However once the choice is made the item will be locked into either beign a soulbound or soulbound weapon. For example if a player decided to make their weapon a soulbound focus, the progression is locked into conduit and focus abilities.

#### **Base:**

Each Focus starts with the following Base Stats, when you unlock a Ability feature, you can choose one of the below options to promote to a higher rank.

Base Focus: 0. Spell Save DC granted by class items do not stack with this focus. The Spell Save DC and spell attack bonus only applies if using the focus to cast.

**Conduit Die:** *d4*, this applies anytime a Conduit option references Conduit Die

**Focus DC**: *8 + Wielder Proficiency Bonus*, whenever a Conduit feature requires a saving throw or allows the casting of a spell, it will use the Focus DC as it's DC

**Charges**: 2, Some Path option may require the use of Charges. Charges reset to full at dawn.

#### **Physical and Mental Features**

| Rank | Base Focus | Conduit Die | focus DC | Charges |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Novice | +1 Spell Attack | d6 | Base + 2 | Base + 2 |
| Journeyman | +2 Spell and +1 Spell Save DC | d8 | Basic + 3 | Basic + 3 |
| Expert | +3 Spell and +2 Spell Save DC | d10 | Base + 4 | Base + 4 |
| Master | +3 Spell Save DC | d12 | Spell Save DC | Wielders Choice of Ability Modifier, set when choosing |

## Runes

As the Soulbound Item grows in power, etchings, runes or engraving may appear on it, this will either directly confer benefits, or in combination with other runes provide additional benefits.

### Universal Runes:

These Runes can be used on weapon, armor, shields and foci:

*   **Reclamation Rune** - You can return the item to your hand (or your body in the case of armor) as an action.
*   **Quick Reclamation Rune -** You can return the item to your hand (or your body in the case of armor) as a bonus action, requires Reclamation Rune
*   **Glamour Rune** - You can freely change the appearance of the item as an action.
*   **Compass Rune** - While holding or wearing the item, you can determine the direction of true north, or the direction to a creature of which you speak its name.
*   **Echo Rune** - You can cast the message cantrip while the item is on your person.
*   **Skill Runes** - Once the item has accumulated two skill Runes, you gain proficiency in one skill of your choice while attuned to the item. An item can take skill Runes more than two times, and grant a new skill for every two of them it has accumulated.
*   **Shifting Rune** - Once the item has two shifting Runes, over the course of a long rest, you can move the properties of the item to another of the same category, in your possession, making the new item your soulbound item. For example, you can move the properties of a soulbound dagger onto a mundane spear, receiving a soulbound spear.
*   **Shining Rune** - The item sheds bright light out 10 feet, and dim light another 10 feet. This Rune can be taken a second time, doubling the radius of the light.
*   **Specialization Rune** - Once an item has accumulated four specialization Runes, the item gains one level of any path, ward, or conduit.
*   **Intricate Rune** - Once an item has accumulated four intricate Runes, the item gains one point to spend on any base feature.
*   **Mnemonic Rune (2x)** – The item can “store” one memory you designate. At 2 Mnemonic Runes, you can replay it vividly.
*   **Expertise Runes** - Once the item has accumulated two skill Runes, and two expertise Runes, you can add double your proficiency to the skill Rune skill, instead of normal proficiency.
*   **Awakening Rune** - When you have two Awaking Runes, your weapon gains sentience. See link for detail information [Awakening Rune](/docs/awakening-rune "awakening rune")

### Weapon Runes:

These Runes can only be taken on weapons:

*   **Twin Rune** - As a bonus action, you may split your weapon into two identical light versions for two-weapon fighting. They recombine as a bonus action, only works with weapons with the light property. Each of these weapons function as your Soulbound weapon, but still only requires one attunement slot.
*   **Master Reclamation Rune -** Immediately after hitting or missing, the weapon flies back to your hand, this requires a Quick Reclamation Rune, Thrown Property and a Level 12+ Soulbound weapon
*   **Identical Rune** - This rune bypasses the light requirement of the twin rune, allowing you split one handed and versatile weapons with the twin rune, that don't have the light property
*   **Hunter’s Rune** - While attuned, you can spend 1 minute studying a creature’s tracks or remnants to learn the direction it traveled within the past 24 hours.
*   **Sanguine Rune** - Once per short rest, when you reduce a creature to 0 HP, you get THP equal to your Path Die.
*   **Dueling Spirit Runes** - With 2 Deuling Spirit Runes, while fighting with the weapon in one hand and no other weapon or shield in the other, you gain +1 AC. With 4 invested, increase it to +2 AC.

### Armor/Shield Runes:

These Runes can only be taken on Armor or Shields:

*   **Defiant Rune** – When you have 2 Defiant Runes, Once per long rest, when reduced to 0 HP while wearing the ward, you drop to 1 HP instead.
*   **Aegis Rune** – When you have 2 Aegis Runes and you succeed on a saving throw against a spell, you can use your reaction to grant advantage to one ally within 10 feet against the same effect.
*   **Mirrorplate Rune** – As a reaction once per short rest, impose disadvantage on an attack against you.
*   **Beacon Runes** – When you have 2 Beacon Runes, allies within 5 feet gain +1 on saving throws against being frightened. At 4 Runes, the aura extends to 10 feet and grants advantage instead.
*   **Masterwork Rune** - If you have 3 Masterwork Runes, you can upgrade your armor to highest tier armor of its type, any light armor, becomes studded leather, medium armor becomes half-plate and heavy armor becomes plate.

### Focus Runes:

These Runes can only be taken on a focus:

*   **Sigil Rune** – While holding the conduit, you can inscribe temporary glowing runes that last 1 hour. You may leave one “mark” at a time that sheds dim light in 5 feet.
*   **Spellkeeper Rune** – You may store a cantrip in the conduit each long rest. You can cast it once as if you knew it, you can take this Rune again. If you have more than 1 you can store a spell you know during a long rest. The maximum level of spell you can store is equal to the number of Spellkeeper Runes minus 1. Regardless of how many Runes you have, you can only store 1 spell, and you can cast the spell without expending a spell slot.
*   **Scryer’s Rune** – Once per long rest, you can use the conduit as a divining rod, giving you advantage on a single Wisdom (Perception) or (Survival) check.
*   **Resonance Runes** – (Requires 2+) When you cast a spell, faint motes of energy linger. Allies within 5 feet gain +1 to their next attack roll. At 4 resonance Runes, the bonus is +1d4 instead.

## Unique Features

Unique can bee seen on the following Pages:

*   Weapons: [Soulbound Paths](/docs/soulbound-paths "soulbound paths")
*   Armor and Shields: [Soulbound Wards](/docs/soulbound-wards "soulbound wards")
*   Foci: [Soulbound Conduits](/docs/soulbound-conduits "soulbound conduits")

Spell Copying Rules

# Spell Copying Rules

## Spell Copying Rules

### How to copy a spell on TWR

*   Locate a source in-character to copy the desired spell from
*   Post the above template to [#transcript](https://discord.com/channels/817263494818693170/817301000770551828), including all of the relevant information
*   Once the post is moved to [#records](https://discord.com/channels/817263494818693170/817297426335006751), you may add the spell to your spellbook and deduct the money from your sheet

#### Other Useful Tips

*   Check the "Your Spellbook" section of the PHB for information on creating a backup or replacing a spellbook
*   Check the [Retroactive Downtime](/docs/retroactive-downtime "retroactive downtime") rules if you forget or get busy!
*   Don't forget, copying takes time! You haven't finished coyping until the post moves to [#records](https://discord.com/channels/817263494818693170/817297426335006751)

\*\* 📚 Spell Copying 📚 \*\*

\*PC Name:\* \[Name\] (@discordtag)

\*Spell Source:\* Name the player or NPC providing the spell

\*Spells Copied:\* List any spells learned here

\*Reductions:\* List any Subclass reductions or other buffs here

\*Gold Spent:\* List the final cost for all copied spells here

\*Time Taken:\* List the amount of downtime spent copying (round to the next day) here

Staff Disciplinary Process

# Staff Disciplinary Process

# Staff Disciplinary Process

This is the general process through which our staff process any complaints or necessary review of a player's actions.

## Moderators - Warnings and Strikes

1.  In the event that a Warning or strike is needed, the person first calling for a vote will create the poll in the Moderator Votes thread with a Moderator role ping, a brief description and a link to the comment.  A 24 hour period will be given for moderators to discuss or vote.  At the end of the 24 hours, or a majority is reached whichever comes first, the Moderator creating the poll will issue the action to the player and post in #The-Strike-Zone.

## Bans

1.  In the event a player incurs a 3rd strike, the moderator issuing the strike will instead issue the ban message from the template, ban the player, and make the record in the strike zone.
2.  If it is for a major event, or other concern, any moderator may open a discussion and call for a vote.  Once the vote is open with the ping, the issue is decided as soon as there is a majority vote or super majority vote either direction.
    1.  The three strike process may be bypassed if especially toxic or repeated inappropriate behavior is present, to the discretion of a supermajority of the Mods.
    2.  A supermajority is 70% of available persons voting in favor of a particular position.
3.  A majority vote is required for Moderators to escalate such a case to Admins for final vote approval to ban or not.
4.  IF a Supermajority for Ban is reached by Moderators then the moderator starting the vote will ban the player, record the reason in Strike Zone, and ping admins with a summary so they’re aware.

The Cursed Role

# The Cursed Role

### The @Cursed Role

1.  If a player leaves during a quest, trial, or other event without the permission of the DM or person running it, they will be cursed, and put into the @Cursed role for two weeks.
2.  During this time, all characters belonging to the player receive only half XP and gold rewards, and cannot be resurrected if killed (except via Revivify spell).
3.  The @cursed role may also be given for other offenses at the discretion of the DM, Moderator, and Admin teams.

Training Downtime

# Training Downtime

#### Training Downtime

Characters can perform activities to train and practice in their free time, those who do not wish to hunt can spend time training themselves as well, this could be tinkering and experimenting for Artificers, praying and studying for Clerics, scribing and practicing spells for Wizards, sparring for fighters, and stealthing around for rogues, all forms of training are applicable and make no mechanical difference

Characters can choose to spend 5 Hunting Tags or expend 8 hours of downtime in training which awards experience points to reflect the development of skills. Players who choose to roleplay the lessons will be awarded with additional RPXP as per usual

Tier | XP Per Day (not RPXP) 1 | 100 xp 2 | 250 xp 3 | 500 xp 4 | 1,000 xp Post-20 | 2,000 xp

Twr Dm'S Guide

# TWR DM's Guide

## TWR DM's Guide

### What is this Guide?

This page is a guide to getting started running Quests on TWR, a discord-based Westmarches campaign. Quests can be run by any player, with certain exceptions where the player may need to seek approval from staff for lore/balance reasons. As noted in our [Code of Conduct](/docs/code-of-conduct "code of conduct"), while running a quest or other content, the player acts as the DM and is considered such during play.

### Preparing to DM

Being a DM on TWR is easy and fun. Most of the adventures can be prepared in a short period of time, and you don’t have to create an entire setting and form a campaign or spend a ton of time plotting out a large overarching plot. Most Quests on TWR are run as “one-shots,” though more advanced DMs may choose to run quests in a series to tell a more intricate story.

### What you Need to Run a Game

In order to run a game as a DM on TWR, you’ll need the following:

#### Players

The #quest-board channel is the perfect place to seek out players, if you do not have a full group in mind already. For traditional adventures a group of 4-6 players is recommended, but there is no specific number of players required to participate in a quest. Just be sure to balance any potential combat or roleplay encounters around the number of participants to ensure everyone gets to play.

#### A Plot

Balance the three pillars of gameplay (Roleplay, Adventuring, Combat) to your liking and create a plot for your quest. It is best to keep plots simple at first until you’ve got some practice running Quests. Consider what type of story you want to tell, what type of rewards might fit with the quest, Prepare any puzzles, difficult choices, or monster statblocks for combat ahead of time so that you can spend gameplay time interacting with the players and building the story. Remember, CR values translate to what an average difficulty is for a group of 4 well balanced adventurers. Though combat should be challenging, it should still be possible to win (unless your story dictates that the party loses!).

#### A Format

Some DMs prefer to run quests in a dedicated session for a more traditional experience, planning a start and end time. Others prefer a play-by-post (PBP) format, which can span multiple days or weeks and provide a more relaxed pace. Each option has benefits and detriments, so it's important to specify which one in your #quest-board post to attract the right players.

#### Other Tools

##### Avrae

For combat, spellcasting, and other mechanical functions it is highly recommended that you use Avrae which will automate the majority of tracking and run combat. You can also upload maps to use for tactical placement during combat.

##### Tupperbot

You will want to set up at least a "Narrator Tupper" to describe scenes, effects and other miscellaneous actions during play. You may also consider creating specific Tuppers for your NPCs to improve immersion and create a more aesthetically pleasing experience.

### Group Composition

There’s no general rule that requires members of a group be of the same level. In quests that revolve around combat, we recommend restricting them by Tier as defined in the Player’s Handbook and Rules Compendium. For quests that are more roleplay-centric, this is less necessary, but bear in mind that some high level PCs may have spells and abilities that far outshine the rest of the group.

## Facilitator of Fun

You have the most important role. You guide the narrative and provide the players with an immersive and enjoyable quest. While guiding the players, it is also your responsibility to make everyone feel welcomed, creating a fun and fair environment. Here are some things to keep in mind:

### You are Empowered

Make decisions about how the group interacts with the quest; adjust or improvise your plan as needed. You have the ability to create NPCs, locations, challenges and scenarios with broad authority within your quest. Interacting with official D&D NPCs, Factions, Deities and other key lore entities may require approval from Staff; we encourage DMs who are concerned that their content may not accurately reflect the setting reach out to an official Dungeonmaster or Loremaster for guidance. See the "When to Seek Staff Approval" section below for a better understanding of when to reach out. **Be aware that, in the instance of particularly challenging deviations from the setting Staff may have to retcon the contents of your quests to maintain fairness and server continuity.**

#### Challenge your Players

Gauge the experience level of your players, as well as what they enjoy in a game and attempt to deliver what they’re after; everyone should be able to shine. You can adjust an encounter’s difficulty by adding or removing thematically appropriate monsters but can’t create new monsters or modify them in such a way that modifies their challenge rating (aside from changing their hit points within the range afforded by their hit dice). Be careful of going too far, however. The intent is to have a collaborative and challenging story-telling experience; it’s not to do everything you can to kill your players’ characters. Similarly, destroying equipment generally shouldn’t happen unless an encounter specifically directs you to. In fact, unless they have conditions detailing their destruction (such as an ioun stone), permanent magic items can’t be destroyed.

#### Dealing with Disruptive Players

If a player is acting in a disruptive manner during a play session, attempt to address the issue as soon as it becomes clear that someone (including you) might be uncomfortable. Take the player aside and explain why the behavior might be causing others to feel uncomfortable. If the problem persists, contact a Moderator or Admin for help with resolution.

#### Disruptive Magic Items, Spells or Class Features

There are several magic items, spells, class features and homebrew submissions within the rules of TWR that might become disruptive to the overall enjoyment of others at the table. If use of any personal equipment, features etc. through play leads to an uncomfortable play experience, take the player aside and explain the issue. You may wish to discuss temporarily removing the item or ability from the character and providing them an alternative replacement instead. You may choose to review a character’s sheet prior to play to help identify any potential issues, You should not mandate removal of magic items or gear solely on your preferences, but rather on their impact to the story and enjoyment of the players/DM as a group.

#### Safety Tools

Safety tools help ensure players aren’t pushed beyond their comfort levels. These tools let your players know you want them to have a positive experience. Some common safety tools are provided below, each with a summary of how they work. An article about one such safety kit is provided to help with further information. Ensure that you discuss these tools with your players before the game:

*   **[Code of Conduct](/docs/code-of-conduct "code of conduct"):** TWR has a code of conduct that outlines rules for play across the server. It defines desired and prohibited behaviors and possible consequences. Familiarize yourself with the Code of Conduct before running a quest to help understand what is acceptable and what is not.
*   **Pregame, Postgame, and Out of Character Discussions:** Use time before the game to explain content warnings and set boundaries. This helps you understand the players' various goals and comfort levels. Be sure to discuss the possibility of lethal combat, if applicable, as many players prefer to avoid risking the safety of their long-running PCs. During play, most DMs prefer to have an Out of Character (OOC) thread for combat and ongoing discussion of the story. After the quest, decompress and discuss what worked and what didn't with the players.
*   **Be Welcoming:** Welcome and encourage respectful and open conversations about issues as they arise. Show that you’re listening by avoiding defensive responses.
*   **Direct Communication:** Allow direct messaging between the players and yourself as the DM. Generally, do not share information sent to you in private with the rest of the group without permission.

#### Keep the Game Moving

If the game gets bogged down, provide hints and clues to your players facing puzzles or engaging in combat and roleplay interactions that get frustrating. This gives players “little victories” for making good choices based on clues. Don't be afraid to skip someone's turn or allow them the ability to act retroactively in the future if one player is unavailable and disrupting the flow of play.

#### Running the Game

As the DM, you are charged with not only guiding the story, but also fairly adjudicating the game’s rules and rewarding your players’ characters for their accomplishments.

#### The Rules of the Game

TWR generally uses Dungeons & Dragons 5th Edition (2014) with some added Homebrew Rules. You can issue rulings to your table when the rules of the game are ambiguous or vague, but you must otherwise adhere to the rules as they are provided in the core rulebooks and can’t change them or make up your own. You must always use the most current incarnation of a rule. Oftentimes, if a rule is in dispute, you can go to the #questions channel or ping a Dungeonmaster for assistance; generally, we follow Sage Advice for clarification from the game developers and, in some cases, Homebrew or #town-hall posts will include rules clarifications. Don't be afraid to ask for help, or issue a temporary ruling while you wait to maintain the flow of play. Otherwise, the rules as written in the rulebooks always take precedence.

#### Quest Locks

Depending on the content of your quest, you have the ability to "Quest Lock" PCs who are participating. Locked PCs cannot gain experience from or participate in Hunts, Trials, or any form of activity outside of your quest. They are also unable to benefit from downtime while engaged in your quest. Whether or not to lock a PC is up to you, as is whether or not their possible progression applies within your quest. Keep in mind travel, in-character duration, and mechanical balance when deciding whether or not to lock the PCs, and **be sure to communicate a quest lock during your original post or pregrame discussion.**

#### Death, Disease and Curses

Adventuring is a risky job, and bad things often happen to adventurers. Diseases, poisons, and other similar effects persist until they’re removed, and death within a quest applies to a PC outside of the quest as well. However, thanks to the proliferation of healing and revival services from players in our setting, they are often simple to overcome. **If you wish to enact permanent effects, be sure to communicate that possibility to the players during your original post or pregame discussion.**

## Character Rewards

You award the players rewards for their accomplishments in the form of experience, gold, and magic items for their characters, which must be detailed in your #story-log post recording the quest.

##### Experience

Players should receive experience for participating in combat and for engaging in good roleplay. DMs should follow the usual guidelines for [granting Experience](/docs/experience "experience"), and are encouraged to request approval for more than the general amount in quests which are long-running or highly impactful.

###### Magic Items

Player generally expect to receive a Magic Item as a reward for their participation in your quest. It is often most exciting to include these items as possible rewards within the quest which they can then use or keep, but sometimes it is most appropriate to provide the items as a form of payment at the end. Regardless of the source, the below table outlines what rarity of items can be rewarded per PC without Staff Approval, provided that the items are available on the loot list:


Maximum Item Rewards
TierRarity
Tier 1Uncommon
Tier 2Rare
Tier 3Very Rare
T4 and AboveLegendary


##### Misc. Treasure

You may wish to award the players with gold or valuable treasures which they may keep or sell as an additional reward from the questgiver or discovery during play. The below table outlines what the maximum amount of additional treasure which can be rewarded per PC without Staff Approval. If no Magic Items are awarded, you may increase this amount by the item's trade value:


Maximum Treasure Rewards
TierAmountItem Trade Value
Tier 1200 gp400 gp
Tier 22,000 gp4,000 gp
Tier 320,000gp40,000gp
Tier 4+100,000 gp200,000 gp


### Cosmetic Rewards

DMs may provide cosmetic rewards such as eye color changes, floating halos, or other thematically appropriate character options as they see fit, provided that they **do not allow for any mechanical benefits.**

## When to seek Staff Approval

As mentioned throughout the above document, there are a number of times when it is appropriate to seek approval from Staff to assist with running your quest or to ensure that your quest is in-line with the general lore and balance expectations of our server. The following list outlines the most common reasons you may need to request Staff oversight:

*   Running named NPCs, Deities or important factions from the Forgotten Realms setting
*   Providing players with information relevant to ongoing Questlines or content provided by members of staff
*   Running content inside of an area of the Forgotten Realms which a staff member is currently focused on
*   Attempting to exceed the XP, Item or Gold reward limits for long-running quests or exemplary gameplay
*   Rewarding magic items which do not appear on the existing loot lists
*   Rewarding high-level awards such as Super Legendary items, Artifacts or Boons
*   Rewarding items which provide a permanent stat boost
*   Providing other custom rewards such as automatons, legendary crafting materials, custom items, or anything that may impact future gameplay on TWR
*   Including homebrewed monsters or equipment within your quest (if included, such additions cannot leave your quest without majority Dungeonmaster approval)

## DM Rewards

Running a quest takes time and effort that we feel should be rewarded. Rewards for running a quest come in two main ways:

### Omni-XP

[Omni-XP](/docs/omni-xp "omni-xp") is awarded to the DM based on the average amount of XP delivered to their players. Omni-XP is restricted by tier and only applies to non-Paragon PCs.

### Paragon Tokens

[Paragon Tokens](/docs/paragon-tokens "paragon tokens") can be taken in lieu of Omni-XP and can be applied to future roleplay to increase the amount of RP-XP rewarded to one of your Paragon PCs.

**Please note that you MAY choose instead to have one of your PCs participate in the request and receive the same rewards as the players, instead of choosing one of the above rewards. If you do so, Staff must approve the rewards whether they fall within the otherwise stated limits or not.**

**Dhampir**

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

The dhampirs empowered bite attack only grants a bonus to the ability check granted by the bite, without the addition of other modifiers.

Additional Weapon Properties

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

### Silent:

*   Silent Weapon Attacking while hidden with a silent weapon does not reveal your position

### Added Finesse:

*   Sickle
*   Spear
*   Yklwa

Amulet Of The Devout

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

The bonus to spell attack rolls and saving throw DCs from an Amulet of the Devout now only apply to Cleric and Paladin spells.

Artificer

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

### Artillerist Artificer Subclass Addition

\- They are now granted proficiency with firearms if they do not already have it.

*\- Eldritch Cannons*, when tiny, can now be integrated into a firearm. They now move with you if integrated, but otherwise function RAW.

\- The *Arcane Firearm* feature can now be used on firearms as well, instead of just a wand, staff, or rod.

### Infusion Master (18th level Artificer) - Optional Class Feature

Starting at 18th level, you can apply infusions and magical tinkering on magic items, however bonuses to attack rolls, damage, and armor class cannot exceed +3

### Spell Storing Addition

 At 11th level, you can now store a spell in an object. Whenever you finish a long rest, you can touch one simple or martial weapon or one item that you can use as a spellcasting focus, and you store a spell in it, choosing a 1st- or 2nd-level spell from the artificer spell list that requires 1 action to cast (you needn't have it prepared). You may upcast a 1st level spell to use a 2nd level slot, if previous conditions are applicable. (edited)

### Artificer: Alchemist Balance Update

**Experimental Elixir**

Drinking an Elixir: As a Bonus Action, a creature can drink the elixir or administer it to another creature within 5 feet of itself.

You can create additional experimental elixirs by expending a spell slot of 1st level or higher (to a maximum of 5th level) for each one. When you do so, you use your action to create the elixir in an empty flask you touch, and you choose the elixir's effect from the Experimental Elixir table.

**Experimental Elixir**

d6    Effect

1    Healing. The drinker regains a number of hit points equal to 2d4 + your Intelligence Modifier (+2d4 hit points restored for every spell slot level above 1st)

2    Swiftness. The drinker's walking speed increases by 10 feet for 1 hour. (+10 feet walking speed for every two spell slot levels above 1st)

3    Resilience. The drinker gains a +1 bonus to AC for 10 minutes. (+1 bonus to AC for every two spell slot levels above 1st)

4    Boldness. The drinker can roll a d4 and add the number rolled to every attack roll and saving throw they make for the next minute.

5    Flight. The drinker gains a flying speed of 10 feet for 10 minutes. (+10 feet flying speed for every two spell slot levels above 1st)

6    Transformation. The drinker's body is transformed as if by the Alter Self spell. The drinker determines the transformation caused by the spell, the effects of which last for 10 minutes.

**Restorative Reagents**

Whenever a creature drinks an experimental elixir you created, the creature gains temporary hit points equal to your Intelligence modifier plus your Artificer level

### Armorer Artificer Option. Dreadnought

Dreadnought: you design your armor to become a towering juggernaut in battle it has the following features:
Armor Flail: an iron ball on a chain appears on one of your armor's gauntlets and has the following traits:
    Weapon damage: 1d10+ability modifier used for the attack roll (bludgeoning)
    Weapon Category: Simple melee
    Properties: Reach

Giant Stature: As a bonus action you transform and enlarge your armor for one minute. For the duration your reach increases by 5 ft, and if you are smaller then large you become large, along with anything your wearing. If there isnt enough room for you to increase your size, your size doesnt change. You can use this Bonus Action a number of times equal to your intelligence modifier (minimum of once). You regain all uses when you finish a Long Rest.

Wrecking ball: If you hit a creature that is atleast one size smaller then you with the flail, you can push that creature up to 10 ft straight away from yourself or pull the creature up to 10 ft toward yourself.

Level 15 Perfected Armor: Your arcane armor gains additional benefits based on its model as detailed below:

Dreadnought: The damage for your flail increases to be 2d6 bludgeoning. In addition when you use your Giant Stature, you can increase your size to large or huge, your choice. you also gain a fly speed equal to your speed.

Barbarians & Armor

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

All Barbarian features, include subclass features, do not function when using Heavy Armor.

Bard

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

### Magical Secrets Errata

The chosen spells count as bard spells for you and are not included as part of the Spells Known column of the Bard table.

### Everlasting Inspiration - Optional Class Feature - Replaces Superior Inspiration

At 20th level your inspiration becomes more than a just a short lived burst. When a creature uses your bardic inspiration, the effect is not expended, allowing for the creature to use it multiple times. Your bardic inspiration now only ends once used a number of times equal to your charisma modifier, or once ten minutes pass.

Blood Hunter

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

### Blood Hunter: Martial Versatility

Whenever you reach a level in this class that grants the Ability Score Improvement feature, you can replace a fighting style you know with another fighting style available to paladins. This replacement represents a shift of focus in your martial practice.

### Blood Hunter-Order of the Profane Soul-Patron: Greatwyrm

At 3rd lvl the blood hunter gains the ability to read and write draconic as well as gain a proficiency in either intimidation, persuasion, or deception.

At 7th level, once per long rest, the blood hunter can cast the 2nd level spell dragons breath without using a spell slot

At 15th level, once per long rest, the blood hunter can cast the third level spell Ashardalon's Stride without using a spell slot

Bloodshed Blade

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

Extra damage from the Bloodshed blade Rune feature:

> If this attack hits, you can also spend and roll any number of your unspent Hit Dice and add the total rolled to the weapon's damage.

doesn't benefit from a critical hit.

Boon Of Fortitude

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

Your hit point maximum increases by 40. If you paragon, your hit point maximum increases by 80.

Boon Of Resilience

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

The Boon of Resilience now gives resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from weapon attacks

Clone

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

Clone Limitations

All PCs can have one active clone without fear of reprisal.

To unlock the second clone slot, there must be a significant entreaty to Kelemvor, or alternative god of death, or a significant effort to control the manipulation of the soul.

To unlock the third clone slot temporarily, the same process as above is applied but with greater scrutiny. A higher bar to achieve.

**Mechanics Note:**

Those that have more than one clone right now can pick one from what they have. The rest go inert and are permanently inactive.

**Monetary Note:**

Materials costing gold that were used in the casting/storage of clones are refunded. Log the refund in ⁠transaction-log and provide proof of the expense. If you casted the Clone spell via Wish, you only get a refund of the storage item used.

Corpse Slaying Weapon

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

The Corpse Slaying weapons are no longer attunement the same as the Dragon Slaying and Giant Slaying weapons.

Create Magen

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

1.  The magen created by the Create Magen spell disappear when the creator/caster dies
2.  The max hit point reduction from this spell can not reduced or prevented through any spell other than wish.

Create Spelljamming Helm

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

This spell:

*   cannot be taken by wizards on level up
*   cannot be prepared by artificers by default
*   cannot be copied from one spellbook to another
*   cannot be transcribed onto a scroll
*   cannot be replicated with wish

in order to acquire this spell, you must obtain a scroll from space raids. once you have a scroll, a wizard may copy it as normal, an artificer may consume the scroll in order to be able to prepare it normally, or you may use the scroll to cast the spell a single time.

Dc Boosting Clarification

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

A PC can only benefit from the effect of one of the following items at a time. They can attune to more than one, but a spell or feature can only benefit from one at a time:

*   All Purpose Tools
*   Rhythm Makers Drum
*   Arcane Grimoire
*   Bloodwell Vial
*   Rod of the Pactkeeper
*   Occult Seal
*   Channelers Clay
*   Fighter's Band
*   Moon Sickle
*   Dragonhide Belt
*   Amulet of the Devout
*   Bloodhunters Brand

Dragonscale Mail

This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

Includes Gem Dragons.

*   crystal = radiant
*   emerald = psychic
*   sapphire = thunder
*   topaz = necrotic
*   solar = radiant
*   lunar = cold
*   time = force
*   moonstone = radiant

Druid

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

### Druid Wildshape Expansion

Expand Druid Wildshape to include the beast-like monstrosities listed in taming rules:

[https://homebrewery.naturalcrit.com/share/\_1SlxfhScWWD](https://homebrewery.naturalcrit.com/share/_1SlxfhScWWD)

The server rule of having to have seen the said creature in RP still applies and non of these monstrosities can be assumed to be easily found outside of Dm approved RP.

### Wildfire Druid Errata

Expanded Spell Gems

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

Spell gems are commonly use for their ability to store magical energy. However, some are crafted by isolating the components of different schools of magic, allowing them to wildly vary. Able to have more of a specialty with the magic at work. When a creature uses this spell gem to cast a spell, it uses the creatures Spell Save DC and spell attack roll instead of the standard values for normal spell gems. These spell gems are restricted to a specific school of magic, though their rarity remain the same.

Fighter

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

### Eldritch Knight: War Magic - Optional Class Feature

### Eldritch Knight: Improved War Magic - Optional Class Feature

When you take the Attack action on your turn, you can replace two of the attacks with a casting of one of your Wizard spells of 1st to 4th level that has a casting time of one action.

Find Greater Steed

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

Dragonnel are valid options for find greater steed

Flame Blade

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

*2nd-level evocation (Druid, Sorcerer, Wizard)*

**Casting Time:** 1 bonus action

**Range:** Self

**Components**: V, S, M (leaf of sumac)

**Duration**: Concentration, up to 10 minutes

**Description**

You evoke a fiery blade in your free hand. The blade is similar in size and shape to a scimitar, lasts for the duration, **and counts as a simple melee weapon with which you are proficient. It deals 3d6 fire damage on a hit and has the finesse and light properties.** If you let go of the blade, it disappears, but you can evoke the blade again as a bonus action.

The flaming blade sheds bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet.

**At Higher Levels**

**When you cast this spell using a 3rd- or 4th-level spell slot, the damage increases to 4d6. When you cast it using a 5th- or 6th-level spell slot, the damage increases to 5d6. When you cast it using a spell slot of 7th level or higher, the damage increases to 6d6.**

*(Changes: It now conjures a real weapon like Shadow Blade does and scales at higher levels the same as Shadow Blade. This makes it usable for Eldritch Knights and Channelers.)*

Flanking

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

When flanking with an allied creature, you gain advantage on melee attack rolls against the creature being flanked. If you have advantage from a seperate source, you instead gain a +3 bonus to melee attack rolls against the creature being flanked.

Gloves Of Soul Catching

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

The Gloves' additional damage is reduced from 2d10 to 1d10 force damage

Glyph Of Warding

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

1.  The range from which a glyph can be triggered is limited to within 10ft of the location the glyph is centered on.
2.  Two or more glyphs cannot exist within 10 ft of each other regardless of size, if two glyphs are brought together or one is cast next to one, both fizzle out harmlessly

Grappling

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

When you are being grappled by another creature you are considered it be within melee of the creature when making an attack roll. This does not apply when being grappled by a weapon or inanimate object/spell

Hallow

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

"Daylight. Bright light fills the area. Magical darkness created by spells of a lower level than the slot you used to cast this spell can't extinguish the light."

**to**

"Daylight. Bright daylight fills the area. Magical darkness created by spells of a lower level than the slot you used to cast this spell can't extinguish the light." tldr; This makes the daylight effect of the Hallow spell send out actual daylight along with it's normal affects.

Heavy Property And Small Creatures

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

Small and Tiny creatures can wield weapons with the heavy property if they have 13 str or more without suffering disadvantage on attack rolls

Kobold (Motm)

It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

### Size (Update):

You are tiny or small

### Innate Craftiness (new)

You have proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: Arcana, Investigation, Medicine, Sleight of Hand, or Survival.

### Kobold Legacy: Craftiness (update)

You have proficiency in one more of the skills available from Innate Craftiness.

Monk

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

*   Draconic Expansion (Ascendant Dragon)

### Perfect Self - Optional Feature (Replaces Original Perfect self)

Starting at 20th level, when you roll for initiative and have 0-3 ki, your ki is reset to 4. In addition, your Dexterity and Wisdom scores increase by 4. Your max for these abilities is also increased by 4, up to a max of 30.

*   Kensei Monk Update
    *   Kensei Weapons. Choose two types of weapons to be your kensei weapons: one melee weapon and one ranged weapon. Each of these weapons can be any simple or martial weapon that lacks the heavy and special properties. The longbow is also a valid choice. You can use the master properties of these weapons, you can only have access to mastery properties of one melee and one ranged weapon. You gain proficiency with these weapons if you don't already have it. Weapons of the chosen types are monk weapons for you. Many of this tradition's features work only with your kensei weapons. When you reach 6th, 11th, and 17th level in this class, you can choose another type of weapon either melee or ranged to be a kensei weapon for you, following the criteria above.

It is preserved here for completeness.

Periapt Of Proof Against Poison

It is preserved here for completeness.

This item is considered Very Rare and requires attunement.

Planar Binding Limitations

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

1.  Players are not allowed to bring any controlled entities, affected by the Planar Binding spell, into a Hunt.
2.  Players are allowed to bring up to two controlled entities, affected by the Planar Binding spell, into a Trial. These entities count towards the limit of 2 controlled entities for Trials.

Plant Growth Errata

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

Casting this on a normal seedling or any plant past this state of growth will have a chance to magically grow it, cutting off 50% of its current growth time, applicable until its fully aged. Having a 25% chance to fail, yielding nothing on a failed roll. Ex. Tree has 25 years to grow, rolling a 26 or higher on a d100 causes the tree to only need to grow for 12.5 more years.

This spell channels vitality into plants within a specific area. There are two possible uses for the spell, granting either immediate or long-term benefits.

If you cast this spell using 1 action, choose a point within range. All normal plants in a 100-foot radius centered on that point become thick and overgrown with a year's worth of growth. A creature moving through the area must spend 4 feet of movement for every 1 foot it moves.

Any harvestable plant in this area produces a poor quality harvest for the next \[month\]. Additional casts in this manner will regrow damaged areas to maintain the rough terrain, but grants no additional agricultural benefits beyond what was granted in the first cast until the cooldown period has elapsed.

You can exclude one or more areas of any size within the spell's area from being affected.

If you cast this spell over 8 hours, you enrich the land. All plants in a half-mile radius centered on a point within range become enriched for 1 year. The plants yield twice the normal amount of food when harvested then return to their normal production levels.

When the land is enriched this way, casting Plant Growth in a attempt to enrich the soil again within one year and six months after the initial casting will grant no additional agricultural benefits.

Simulacrum

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

1.  Simulacrum has the same rules and limitations as found in Adventurer’s League.
    1.  If a caster creates a second simulacrum, the first one disappears. .
    2.  A simulacrum cannot cast Simulacrum.
    3.  A simulacrum which casts Wish confers the risks and penalties of that wish onto its creator.
    4.  There can only be ONE simulacrum of a PC at any given time. It doesn’t matter if multiple casters casted the spell on the same person.

Sorcerer

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

*   Arcane Overflow - Optional Class Feature
*   Draconic Expansion (Draconic Bloodline)

Specific Triggers

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

Triggers and activation conditions must have a known/detectable effect, for instance a readied action for when a creature comes through the door, contingency cant be set to activate with a thought, glyph of warding doesn't work if its "when a neutral good creature steps on it" because alignment can't be perceived immediately

Some exceptions: You can ready an action to attack if the target thinks something, if you have detect thoughts or a way to hear their thinks

Note: this applies to anything that has an effect based on a condition or requirement

Spell Scroll Usage

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

We revert to 2014 RAW for spell scroll usage

Previous: While all people can use spell scrolls on this server, those who do not have access to the spell slots required to cast the spell on the scroll must make an ability check using your spellcasting ability (Int, Cha, Wis). For PC that don’t have a spellcasting ability it can be the highest Ability of WIS, CHA, INT with a DC of 10 + Spell Level.

Spellwrought Tattoos

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

Spellwrought Tattoos whose spells requires a material component with a cost require those materials to be provided at the time of crafting and are consumed if the spell consumes them, eg. Raise dead's 300 gp diamond

Equipping one tattoo takes a minute. Using it remains the casting time of the spell.

Every character has 5 'points' worth of tattoo space to work with on their body:

*   Rare - 3 points
*   Uncommon - 2 points
*   Common - 1 point

This can also be interpreted as the size of each tattoo, but the point system is a clear cut way to define it and prevent abuse such as playing as a race that has an additional set of limbs. Additionally, familiars and other magical summons that are smaller than Medium are no longer eligible for Spellwrought Tattoos.

Storm Boomerang

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

This boomerang is a ranged weapon carved from griffon bone and etched with the symbol of elemental air. When thrown, it has a range of 60/120 feet, and any creature that is proficient with the javelin is also proficient with this weapon. On a hit, the boomerang deals 1d4 bludgeoning damage and 3d4 thunder damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 10 Constitution saving throw or be stunned until the end of its next turn. On a miss, the boomerang returns to the thrower's hand.

Once the boomerang deals thunder damage to a target, the weapon loses its ability to deal thunder damage and its ability to stun a target. These properties return after a long rest.

True Polymorph And Shapechange

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

For the purpose of spells and features such as Polymorph, True Polymorph, Wild Shape, and Shapechange, you must have seen them in approved canon content, and will lose access to those forms if you have not.

Monsters in Trials and Hunts do not count towards having seen a monster.

Warforged

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

Warforged are considered a humanoid race in recent editions of D&D.

However it is arguable they could be considered constructs due to their mechanical nature that separates them from any other humanoid race A playable construct race already exists in Autognome being playable and thus having the option to make Warforged be classified as construct be nice

### **Construct healing**

(only applies if Warforged is a Construct)

If the mending spell is cast on you, you can spend a Hit Die, roll it, and regain a number of hit points equal to the roll plus your Constitution modifier (minimum of 1 hit point). In addition, your creator designed you to benefit from several spells that preserve life but that normally don't affect Constructs: cure wounds, healing word, mass cure wounds, mass healing word, and spare the dying.

This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

*   Warlock: Eldritch Invocations: Investment of the Chain Master
*   Eldritch Flux - Optional Class Feature
*   Improved Pact Weapon Errata
