*   Pact of the Core - Optional Class Feature
*   Warlock Optional Feature - Eldritch Versatility

Weapon Mastery

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

*Add the weapon mastery properties and class features from the 2024 PHB*

### Cleave

If you hit a creature with a melee attack roll using this weapon, you can make a melee attack roll with the weapon against a second creature within 5 feet of the first that is also within your reach. On a hit, the second creature takes the weapon’s damage, but don’t add your ability modifier to that damage unless that modifier is negative. You can make this extra attack only once per turn.

### Graze

If your attack roll with this weapon misses a creature, you can deal damage to that creature equal to the ability modifier you used to make the attack roll. This damage is the same type dealt by the weapon, and the damage can be increased only by increasing the ability modifier.

### Nick

When you make the extra attack from two-weapon fighting, you can make it as part of the Attack action instead of as a Bonus Action. You can make this extra attack only once per turn.

### Push

If you hit a creature with this weapon, you can push the creature up to 10 feet straight away from yourself if it is Large or smaller.

### Sap

If you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.

### Slow

If you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage to it, you can reduce its Speed by 10 feet until the start of your next turn. If the creature is hit more than once by weapons that have this property, the Speed reduction doesn’t exceed 10 feet.

### Topple

If you hit a creature with this weapon, you can force the creature to make a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 plus the ability modifier used to make the attack roll and your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, the creature has the Prone condition.

### Vex

If you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage to the creature, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.

### Added to Fighter:

#### Weapon Mastery - 1st Level

Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of three kinds of Simple or Martial weapons of your choice. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can practice weapon drills and change one of those weapon choices.

When you reach certain Fighter levels, you gain the ability to use the mastery properties of more kinds of weapons, gaining one additional selection at 4th, 10th, and 16th level.

#### Tactical Master - 9th Level

When you attack with a weapon whose mastery property you can use, you can replace that property with the Push, Sap, or Slow property for that attack.

### Added to Rogue:

#### Weapon Mastery

Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of weapons of your choice with which you have proficiency, such as Daggers and Shortbows.

Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change the kinds of weapons you chose. For example, you could switch to using the mastery properties of Scimitars and Shortswords.

### Added to Barbarian:

#### Weapon Mastery

Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of Simple or Martial Melee weapons of your choice. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can practice weapon drills and change one of those weapon choices.

When you reach certain Barbarian levels, you gain the ability to use the mastery properties of more kinds of weapons, gaining one additional selection at 4th and 10th level.

### Added to Paladin:

#### Weapon Mastery

Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of weapons of your choice with which you have proficiency, such as Longswords and Javelins.

Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change the kinds of weapons you chose. For example, you could switch to using the mastery properties of Halberds and Flails.

### Added to Ranger:

#### Weapon Mastery

Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of weapons of your choice with which you have proficiency, such as Longbows and Shortswords.

Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change the kinds of weapons you chose. For example, you could switch to using the mastery properties of Scimitars and Longswords.

### Added to Artificer:

#### Weapon Mastery

Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of weapons of your choice with which you have proficiency, such as Muskets and Daggers.

Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change the kinds of weapons you chose. For example, you could switch to using the mastery properties of Pistols and Quarterstaffs.

### Added to Blood Hunter:

#### Weapon Mastery

Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of weapons of your choice with which you have proficiency, such as Longbows and Shortswords.

Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change the kinds of weapons you chose. For example, you could switch to using the mastery properties of Shortbows and Rapiers.

### Added to Channeler:

#### Weapon Mastery

Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of weapons of your choice with which you have proficiency, such as Daggers and Quarterstaffs.

Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change the kinds of weapons you chose. For example, you could switch to using the mastery properties of Sickles and Slings.

### Mastery Properties of Weapons:

*   Club - Slow
*   Dagger - Nick
*   Greatclub - Push
*   Handaxe - Vex
*   Javelin - Slow
*   Light Hammer - Nick
*   Mace - Sap
*   Quarterstaff - Topple
*   Sickle - Nick
*   Spear - Sap
*   Dart - Vex
*   Light Crossbow - Slow
*   Shortbow - Vex
*   Sling - Slow
*   Battleaxe - Topple
*   Flail - Sap
*   Glaive - Graze
*   Greataxe - Cleave
*   Greatsword - Graze
*   Halberd - Cleave
*   Lance - Topple
*   Longsword - Sap
*   Maul - Topple
*   Morningstar - Sap
*   Pike - Push
*   Rapier - Vex
*   Scimitar - Nick
*   Shortsword - Vex
*   Trident - Topple
*   Warhammer - Push
*   War Pick - Sap
*   Whip - Slow
*   Blowgun - Vex
*   Hand Crossbow - Vex
*   Heavy Crossbow - Push
*   Longbow - Slow
*   Musket - Slow
*   Pistol - Vex

### Mastery Properties of HB Weapons:

*   Cestus - Push
*   Scythe - Cleave
*   Chakram - Vex
*   Kusarigama - Vex
*   Blunderbuss - Graze
*   Scrapshot - Graze
*   Precision Rifle - Vex
*   Carbine - Slow
*   Millwood Greatbow - Push
*   Millwood Giantbow - Topple

Wish

:::warning [LEGACY GATE DISCLAIMER]
This errata applies to a standard 2014 spell/feature that does not have a corresponding page in the TWR Wiki.
It is preserved here for completeness.
:::

1.  Wish cannot be used to give more than one damage resistance to more than a single creature.

1.  Any effects other than what is clearly stated in the text of the Wish spell needs DM approval prior to casting wish
2.  When using Wish for anything other than casting a 8th level or lower spell, the DM or DM trainee role needs to be pinged, and their presence acknowledged before rolling the d100.
3.  Wish is a 9th level spell, regardless of what you use it for. If you use it to replicate a spell of 8th level or lower, it still counts as having cast a 9th level spell. (for the purposes of counterspell/dispel magic, spell storing items, etc.)
4.  Wish has a range of self, and its effect cannot be twinned as a result of that

Agriculture System

# Agriculture System

### Agriculture System

#### How to Start Your Farm

1.  Choose what crops and land you will be using: Select a crop from the list available in the document below. Each crop has a maximum amount that can be planted per acre, a cost per pound, and a base yield stat which determines the multiplier of how much crops you gain at the end of a growing period. The minimum amount of land you must have to use the agriculture system is one acre, and each crop takes 90 days to plant and/or harvest. To use the agriculture system, you must select an amount of acres of land that you own to become farmland.
2.  Determine the Statistics of your Land: Ping Craftmaster in ⁠#crafting-ooc stating where geographically you are planting. You will be told the statistics of the land, including Disease Resistance, Pest Resistance, Ground quality, and weather. Multiply these together with the yield- this is your overall amount of crops gained. These statistics can be increased for the next growing period using roleplay and money investments submitted to the Craftmaster team.
3.  Find Workers: A hireling of any CR is required to work your land. Each hireling can work on 3 acres for the entire 90 days of a growing period. You must pay the deployed rate for the hireling for each day of the growing period. The amount of time and gold it takes to originally till an acre of land to be ready for crops is negligible. Any supplies and resources used in the growing process are included in the hireling’s pay.
4.  Submit a filled in Template to ⁠#crafting-submissions.

#### Agriculture Report Template

@you (PC Name) would like to grow  in 

Total Yield: (Amount planted) x (Base Yield) x (Disease Resistance) x (Pest Resistance) x (Ground Quality) x (Weather)

Total Acres:

Hirelings Used:

Start Date:
End Date:

[https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/10NkTBM6IeO6RNk\_rxIioqGoj6HV2f7u7Q0t-3evMGnM/edit?usp=sharing](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/10NkTBM6IeO6RNk_rxIioqGoj6HV2f7u7Q0t-3evMGnM/edit?usp=sharing)

Alchemy System

# Alchemy System

### **Alchemy System**

##### **Gathering**

1) Use Downtime to Gather Herbs, max usage **3x a day** (uses 8 hours in a session). **Note:** You can no longer sacrifice adventuring time to harvest extra herbs.

2) Choose one environment \[Cave, Forest, Desert, Grasslands, Swamp\] + Rarity \[Common, Uncommon, Rare, Very Rare\]

3) Roll Nature/Medicine/Arcana + Herbalism/Poisoner/Brewer/Alchemist Tool Prof Bonus (If proficient) --> DC scales depending on rarity of ingredient. Once check is completed, post in transcript using template found in pins of transcript . Craftmaster will roll for the herb gathered randomly depending on location chosen and DC rarity met.

###### **DCs for Enchantment Ingredients:**

| Rarity | DC |
| --- | --- |
| Common | DC 10 |
| Uncommon | DC 13 |
| Rare | DC 17 |
| Very Rare | DC 20 |

##### Targeted Gathering

1) You can target herbs based on which potion you wish to make! If you are targeting a herb for a specific potion, use the Targeted gathering Template pinned in transcript

2) If the herb you are searching for isn't in the environment you chose, you get nothing. If it is, a Craftmaster will roll the herb normally, and you only get the herb if the dice comply.

###### The DCs for targeted gathering are as followed:

| Rarity | DC |
| --- | --- |
| Common | DC 13 |
| Uncommon | DC 17 |
| Rare | DC 20 |
| Very Rare | DC 23 |

###### Bonuses:

1.  You can only target one herb per day until you have gathered 3 of that herb, at which point you may target for two herbs per day due to your familiarity.
2.  You cannot use help actions in herb gathering. Self-Buffs are allowed for now.
3.  **Nat20 Rule:** If you roll nat20 on the check (RAW Nat20 without modifiers and bonuses) you get 1 Extra Herb.
4.  **Over10 Rule:** If your total roll is 10 over the applicable DC you get 1 Extra Herb. Example, if your total roll for a rare is 31, you get 2 Herbs instead of 1.
5.  Nat20 and Over10 rules can both apply, to a max of 3 Herbs per roll.

### Potion Brewing

Potions are brewed using herbs, each herb is used for a specific potion, the potion is hinted at in the description.

Time, base Cost, Herb Requirement to brew:

| Rarity | Time (Days) | Cost | Herb Requirement |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Common | 2.5 | 25 gp | 1 Herb |
| Uncommon | 5 | 100 gp | 1 Herb |
| Rare | 12.5 | 1,000 gp | 2 Herbs |
| Very Rare | 25 | 10,000 gp | 3 Herbs |

#### Monster Part Substitution

You can substitute 1 Herb for a monster part, however uncommon and higher potions need at least 1 Herb.

| Potion Rarity | Monster CR | Loot DC |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Common | CR 1-3 | DC 13 |
| Uncommon | CR 4-8 | DC 17 |
| Rare | CR 9-13 | DC 21 |
| Very Rare | CR 14-18 | DC 24 |
| Legendary | CR 19+ | DC 26 |

#### Bulk Brewing

*   Bulk Brewing has a discount on overall cost now. For every 10 potions you make at a time, you get a 5% discount on cost, cap for the discount is 20%.
*   Bulk Brewing also requires less herbs. For every 10 potions you brew, you only need 7x the amount of herbs for one potion of the same rarity. Example: 10 common potions of healing - 7 enchantment herbs needed.

#### Time Reduction

*   You get a 50% time reduction if you use double the amount of enchantment herbs for the potion.
*   Sacrificing Potions:

1.    You can sacrifice one potion per brew.
2.    Lowers base time by half the time it takes to brew a potion of that rarity.

Barding

# Barding

### Barding

Barding is equipment made for creatures not Medium size. Barding can be either mundane or magical.

The base cost of creating the item will be increased by the following modifier for each size above and below Medium:

| Size | Cost Modifier |
| --- | --- |
| Diminutive | x 64 |
| Tiny | x 16 |
| Small | x 4 |
| Large | x 4 |
| Huge | x 16 |
| Gargantuan | x 64 |

Construction Rules

# Construction Rules

### Building Rules

Walls and floors are all considered to be 1 feet thick, a 1 foot by 1 foot by 1 foot cube of material is considered to be 1 unit.

#### How to Build a Structure

Determine the floor plan of your building. For example, you wish to build a 20 by 20 shack made of stone brick, the walls are say 8 feet high and for ease of calculation the roof is flat, there are no extra internal walls and is just open.

If the floor is 2 feet thick, it will be 20 long x 20 wide x 2 thick = 800 units The ceiling is normal (1 feet thick) and is 400 units Walls are 8 feet high and 20 long x 8 high x 1 wide, and you have 4 of them, therefore they equate to 640 units The building is a total of 1840 units, and can now be priced and the time can be found accordingly.

#### Base Construction Time

**Base Time:** Units/100/number of workers = # workweeks it takes to construct.

#### Workers

Decide the wages you want to advertise to determine how many NPCs will work. A Craftmaster will roll only 1 time and that number is final.

| Cost (Per NPC/Day) | Roll for Workers |
| --- | --- |
| 5 gp | 1d20 NPC Workers |
| 10 gp | 1d15 + 5 NPC Workers |
| 15 GP | 1d10 + 10 NPC Workers |
| 20 GP | 1d5 + 15 NPC Workers |

**Note:** You can do Craftmaster-approved roleplay to increase worker count, but what you roll is limited to what you can achieve without roleplay.

#### Notes:

*   **On Elysium,** the limit is 20 workers
*   Limit is based off population, depending on where you want to build. Contact craftmasters for a different limit.
*   You must decide on the wage **before** rolling for results
*   If you have some kind of other material you plan on using, ping a *@craftmaster*

#### Construction Material Costs

| Material Selected | Cost (per Unit) |
| --- | --- |
| Stone Brick | 5 gp |
| Low-End Wood | 4 gp |
| Mid-Range Wood | 8 gp |
| High-End Wood | 12 gp |
| Glass | 10 gp |

**Note:** Materials not listed above can be taken on a case-by-case basis. Please ping Craftmaster for approval and costs of those materials.

#### Elysium Map Relevant Information and Lot Purchasing Form

Parcels are sold in 2,500 sq. ft. increments only.

Please submit construction proposals to ⁠transcript in the following format:

\*\*\*\_\_Charter Request\_\_\*\*\*
@Loremaster
@Craftmaster

\*\*Buyer:\*\* @yourself (PC\_Name)
\*\*Plot Size:\*\* \[dimensions of plot, in feet\]
\*\*Construction Plans:\*\* \[description of what is being built, where on the island, physical appearance, etc.\]

Crafting An Item

# Crafting an Item

## Crafting Items

### **Crafter Information**

*   **Unnamed NPCs** can only work on mundane items, and building projects. These types of crafters require a Named NPC or PC to lead the project. Unnamed NPCs working on mundane equipment are required to be paid at least 25 gp a day.
*   **Named NPCs** originate one of two ways; either through roleplay ran by a Dungeonmaster and approved by a Craftmaster, or when a PC retires. These types of crafters can lead their own projects, and can increase their magical crafting capabilities through means separate of both PCs and Unnamed NPCs.
*   **PCs** are characters that are actively being controlled by a player. These types of crafters can lead their own projects, make their own decisions (unless given up by the player to a guild), and are limited in what they can craft based on rarity which is determined based on the level of the character.

**Magic Item Proficiency Requirements:** A character needs to be proficient with the tools needed to craft an item and have access to the appropriate equipment. Everyone who collaborates needs to have the appropriate tool proficiency. You need to make any judgment calls regarding whether a character has the correct equipment. The following provides some examples.

*   Herbalism Kit: Antitoxin, Potion of Healing
*   Leatherworker's Tool: leather Armor, Boots
*   Smith's Tools: Armor, Weapons
*   Weaver's Tools: Cloaks, Robes
*   Tinker's Tools; Wondrous Items

**Note:** The above are just examples. If you can justify the use of a tool in a craft/project, it would be acceptable for that craft/project.

#### Magical Crafting Subsection

**Any player characters (active or retired)** involved with the crafting needs to meet a level requirement to craft items of certain rarities:

*   Level 1-4: Uncommon and Below
*   Level 5-10: Rare
*   Level 11-16: Very Rare
*   level 17 and up: Legendary

**Named NPC:** To allow for Named NPC crafters to upgrade the rarities they can craft. A specific amount of crafts of a certain rarity must be made (listed below) as well as a Wisdom check (made by PC) following the final craft (DCs also listed below).

*   Common -> Uncommon: 20 Common crafts with DC 15 Wisdom Check
*   Uncommon -> Rare : 40 Uncommon crafts with DC 18 Wisdom Check
*   Rare -> Very Rare: 60 Rare crafts with DC 20 Wisdom Check
*   Very Rare -> Legendary: 80 Very Rare crafts with DC 22 Wisdom Check

### **Crafting Time Calculation**

When calculating time reduction, the order in which a player should calculate is as follows:

1.  Magic Item Sacrifice
2.  Magic Item Adept (MIA)
3.  Master Scrivener (MS)
4.  Workers
5.  Craft Roleplay Reduction

#### Magic Item Sacrificing

*   You can sacrifice one item per craft.
*   Lowers base time by half the time it takes to craft an item of the rarity of the item sacrificed.
*   Sacrificing an item that would make the craft instant instead results in the craft taking 1 day.

#### Magic Item Adept

*   An artificer only class feature that reduces both time and cost of crafting common and uncommon items.

#### Master Scrivener

*   A wizard only class feature that reduces both time and cost of scribing scrolls.

#### Workers

*   To a max of five; the more people working on a project, the quicker it gets done. To calculate this reduction method, you divide the base time by the amount of workers you have on the project.
*   Players who have not received the @player role cannot assist with a craft as a worker

#### Craft Roleplay Reduction

*   Add the initial roleplay in the crafting submission.
*   Additional roleplay regarding the craft, brew, or construction can be submitted in the crafting submissions channel.
*   No XP or favor can be given out in the same request as time reduction.
*   The Craftmaster approving the log, and any subsequent RP submissions that may follow, will determine how long it reduces by, to a max of three days per submission. No reduction can take the completion time below 1 day.
*   Reductions are capped at a 25% total reduction to the original crafting time.
*   Each roleplay request can reduce the log by more than three days, but this requires two or more Craftmaster approval.

### Crafting Report Template

@you (PC Name) would like to craft x

The process includes (The crafting process. Be creative!)

(Summary of all PCs and Third Parties involved)

Total time taken: Base Time - (Item Sacrifice) / (Magic Item Adept) / (Workers) - (Roleplay Request)

Total cost: Base Cost + Wages for Workers

Materials:
- (List of all materials used in crafting)

### Mundane Crafting Summary

*   Crafting Cost = Listed Value / 4
*   Crafting Time = Listed Value / 50 Days

### Magical Crafting Summary

**Rarity | Time | Crafting Cost | Materials**

*   Common | 5 days | 50gp | 1x CR 1-3
*   Uncommon | 10 Days | 200gp | 1x CR 4-8
*   Rare | 25 Days | 2k gp | 2x CR 9-13
*   Very Rare | 50 days | 20k gp | 3x CR 14 -18
*   Legendary | 100 days | 100k gp| 3x CR19+, of which two of the three materials for legendary crafting needs to be resulting from a quest.

Consumables take half the time and cost needed, and require 1 less monster material to craft, down to a minimum of 1 material.

#### Crafting material Harvesting

A player can harvest crafting materials from hunts.

A player can use survival, nature or investigation to harvest material. The DC of the check is based on CR as seen below:

*   CR 1-3: DC 13
*   CR 4-8: DC 17
*   CR 9-13: DC 21
*   CR 14-18: DC 24
*   CR 19+: DC 26

### Homebrew Upgrading Rules

You can add a + enchantment onto an existing magic item. This upgrade will boost the crafted item's rarity a tier up, starting at the higher rarity item. The item's rarity can't exceed legendary, and the + enchantment can't exceed +3.

*   Example 1: Adding +1 to a Dragon Slayer Sword will make it Very Rare and +2
*   Example 2: Adding +1 to a Serpent Scale Armor will make it a Very Rare and +1

**This upgrading process is limited to weapons and armors only.**

### Re-forging

You can re-forge the type of item you have according to the class and enchantment of the item.

*   The class of item refers to the grouping allowed by the enchantment, found in parenthesis after the item type in its description. Whether that be simple/martial, melee/ranged, any, any kind of sword, etc.
*   The enchantment of the item refers to the magical ability of the item.

You can only re-forge an item to another item of the same class and enchantment. *For example, a Dragon Slayer Longsword couldn't be re-forged into a Flame Tongue Greatsword, but it could be re-forged into a Dragon Slayer Rapier.*

**Re-forge Cost:** Half of the cost it takes to craft an item of that rarity.

**Re-forge Time:** Half of the time it takes to craft an item of that rarity.

*   Re-forging is only applicable with weapons and armor.
*   Magic Item Adept feature works with re-forging.

### Crafting Updates

Base costs: Magic items will cost more if the base item is worth 100 gp or more will cost more accordingly

Example: Double bladed scimitar (100 gp): +1 DBS is 500 gp Plate armor (1500 gp): +1 Plate armor is 5500 gp

(these are the minimum prices)


**\*\*Leg Quested Mat Use and Acquisition Expansion\*\***

Acheron artifacts will be considered quested leg mats and leg mats to contribute to the formation of gravity drives.

Transforming an Acheron artifact to a quested leg mat will alert enemies and players within Acheron.

Gravity drives require one quested leg mat

can you shove this in the crafting wiki

Downtime Buffs

# Downtime Buffs

### Downtime Buffs

When performing any downtime activities, any bonuses to skill checks made must last for the full eight hours of downtime to be applicable to the final skill check.

(Cases that specifically state otherwise overrules this, i.e. herb gathering)

Economics

# Economics

## Economics

### Minimum Trade Value

To ensure a fair and predictable economy on TWR, trades and purchases are limited by a minimum value, which can only be exceeded through Roleplay with *@dungeonmaster* or *@craftmaster* approval.

| Rarity | Minimum Trade Value |
| --- | --- |
| Common | 100 gp |
| Uncommon | 400 gp |
| Rare | 4,000 gp |
| Very Rare | 40,000 gp |
| Legendary | 200,000 gp |

\* Consumable items can be traded for (rarity)/2 gp

### Selling Magic Items

Players can now sell magic items to NPCs for gold, or offer it to a deity of their choice for favor.

Selling to NPCs gives the character gold equal to half the minimum price of that item. Offering it up to a deity of their choice gives the character favor equal to a quarter of what it costs to roll a random item of that category.

### Selling Mundane Items

There are three item types: Scrap, Old, and New.

*   Scrap sells at 10% of sale value. ***Unusable***
*   Old sells at 50%. ***Used & Starter Gear***
*   New sells at 100%. ***Recently Forged***

Repairing takes 1/8 of the item cost in material, and value/100 days. For example, 15 days for plate, around 200 gp to repair.

If Mending is used, scrap items are repaired into functional old items. Halving time on repair, unless additional replacement material is required.

### Monster Parts

Crafting materials are only interchangeable for money. **Monster Parts may not be traded for magic items**

### Gifting Rules

**Characters may not gift each other money or items at will.** Characters within the same party may loan each other items during the course of a quest, trial, or other content, but must retrieve their belongings at the end. **If your character would like to gift an item, you must first roleplay a justification for the gift,** then submit the below report to #transcript for review.

##### Gift Request Transcript Template

Gifting Request

Characters Involved: (Please @ any players involved)

Item being gifted:

Start: (Please link the start of your rp here)

End: (Please link the end of your rp here)

Summary: (Summarize your rp here as well as brief reasoning/justification for gifting)

#### Siege weapon prices

*   Ballista, 8,000gp
*   Cannon (arcane), 20,000gp
*   Cannon (powder), 18,000gp
*   Suspended-Cauldron 4,000gp
*   Mangonel 10,000gp
*   Ram 4,000gp
*   Siege-Tower 5,000gp
*   Trebuchet 15,000gp
*   Boilerdrak 25,000gp
*   Gnomeflinger 22,000gp

### Taxes

For the sale of objects

*   Any object valued under 200gp will not be taxed.
*   Any object valued between 200gp and under 20,000gp will be taxed at 5%.
*   Any object valued at 20,000 GP and above will be taxed at 20% tax.
*   This applies per item, meaning bulk purchases are taxed based on the value of each individual object.
*   Taxes only apply when transactions occur in civilized areas

#### Tax Evasion Guidelines

To evade taxes of any amount, you need to roleplay the transaction at a location that would prevent the enforcement of tax.

Requirements below:

*   Administratively, the roleplay must be **linked into the ⁠transaction-log.**
*   **Title the transaction as “Tax Evasion”** for ease of search.

Failure to do so may summon an angry pink god(dess) and void the transaction or enforce the tax and other demerits.

### Crafting Update

Base costs: Magic items will cost more if the base item is worth 100 gp or more will cost more accordingly

Example: Double bladed scimitar (100 gp): +1 DBS is 500 gp Plate armor (1500 gp): +1 Plate armor is 5500 gp

(these are the minimum prices)

### Monster Material Base Prices (in gp)

CR 1-3 (Common): 20

Cr 4-8 (Uncommon): 80

Cr 9-13 (Rare): 400

Cr 14-18 (Very Rare): 2,000

Cr 19 (Legendary): 5,000

Quested Legendary Mats: can be sold for whatever price

\--------------------------------

### Herb Base Prices (in gp)

Common: 60

Uncommon: 150

Rare: 300

Very Rare: 600

\--------------------------------

No item can be sold at more than 250% of their base cost, unless there is approval from a Craftmaster independent of the transactional party.

Finally, when any item is sold, it can only be bought back at the highest price of which it was sold. This requirement ends a month after the item is sold (to ease tracking).

Full & Part Time Jobs

# Full & Part Time Jobs

### Part Time & Full Time Jobs

You can now find gainful employment within the city of Waterdeep (and abroad). This can be done in two ways, a weekly job or daily job.

#### Daily Jobs

Daily Jobs uses an alias in discord and can only be done once a day per PC, this also takes your downtime. To do the job, use the alias: !job skill Then link the result in #transaction-log with a short deception of what you did in character and the amount earned

#### Weekly Jobs

Part time jobs, such as casual work at a smithy or a bard playing for a tavern, will take up all of your downtime (meaning no crafting or learning skills) but still let you quest, hunt, and trial.

Full time jobs pay more but in addition to no downtime you will no longer have time to trail, quest, or freely adventure.

Q: How do you find employment/work?

A: Provide a brief description of the job you’re looking to do, such as a part time job at an apothecary, then roll 2d20 and add your proficiency bonus if applicable (In this case it would be alchemy or herbalism). (2d20 + applicable mod) \* 14 \* Tier = gp/wk using all downtime. x2 for full time, but no hunts/trials as a result Part Time tier averages T1 = 294 T2 = 588 T3 = 882 T4 = 1,176

##### Weekly Job Report

@ Player PC & Tier: Lanscan Tallfast, T2 Job: Bard at a the The Silken Slyph Tavern & Inn Roll: 22 Link to the roll: https://discord.com/channels/817263494818693170/817269893514395658/928341274916962386 Wages: Start Date: MM/DD/YYYY End Date: MM/DD/YYYY

Hirelings

# Hirelings

[![Hireling points breakdown](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/0/0b/Hireling_points_breakdown.webp/revision/latest/scale-to-width-down/180?cb=20241112160125)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/0/0b/Hireling_points_breakdown.webp/revision/latest?cb=20241112160125)

[](/docs/file:hireling-points-breakdown.webp)

### Hirelings

For those of you running organizations and have had questions regarding growth rates and the like, we now have a official system in place to manage wages, growth rates, CRs of hired staff, etc. It will soon be integrated into a larger document for readability and the like, but this is the back end system for folks to use and take a look at. If you have any questions, please ping a <@&879237772047712326> in <#819298824602255360>. If you are running a organization that has NPCs, please note this does not include crafting NPCs or PC-NPCs, please ping a <@&879237772047712326> in <#823408859444019240> so we can add your information to the sheet and get it in <#817297426335006751> to make it official.

https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1viG\_QtNhQLvkYm479pvcepFO37hgQruSIZ9WDb7pqEk/edit?usp=sharing

Also, just as a general addendum, we’re trying to shift to better tracking methods so we have a better grasp on how everything is working together regarding player orgs, the economy, crafting, etc, so we can bring a better experience to you fine folks. The more we know, the better we can make informed decisions regarding the macro systems.

Also also, the system is subject to minor edits as it gets dialed in.

### Hireling Update

Hireling Addendum, minor update Team: @Loremaster

Creature type is restricted to humanoid (already a thing but clarified) Statblock does not nessicary determine appearance, alignment, etc (already a thing but clarified) All of them by default speak common (new) If you have excess high CR creatures you dont want to use, you can use that “slot” for a creature of a lower CR. So for example, if you don’t want to use CR 8 creatures due to costs (or whatever), you can use a CR 3 creature in its place instead. (new) Creatures that use unique named items, such as Steel, can not be used (new)

### Hireling Update 2

Team involved: @Loremaster

Hello lurkers!

This update is an addendum to the previously released Hireling spreadsheet for NPC pricing. Utilizing this, you will have guidelines with which you may hire NPCs over time, up to a predetermined maximum amount, for whatever individual, group, or organizational needs you have. If there is confusion on how this works, please use the attached spreadsheet, or ask a @Loremaster.

Contracting NPC Requirements:

Maximum CR that can be hired as a single unit is capped at CR 20

Total CR across all hired units is capped at CR = 30

Hiring occurs weekly, using a point deduction system (Hire Points)

Points accrue at a rate of 1/week from the time interest is expressed, and equate to the Total CR that can be used to hire units

Point deductions are based against the CR being hired.

Example: Player currently has total of 5 Hire Points. The player can Hire any combination of humanoid creatures less than or equal to the total CR of 5.

Spreadsheet for CR to Hire Points breakdown can be seen here: https://docs.google.com/file/d/1Qv2-Cna8BUpkpW0geMdIO68BIL-coSrd/edit?usp=docslist\_api&filetype=msexcel

Please submit these hire requests to transcript and ping a @Loremaster for approval.

**Attunement and Magic Items for Hirelings** Hirelings can equip and attune to magic items provided to them by their employer based upon their CR, as shown below:

*   CR 1/8 - CR 1/2: Common (no attunement)

*   CR 1 - CR 4: Uncommon (attunement up to 1 item)

*   CR 5 - 10: Rare (attunement up to 2 items)

*   CR 11 - 16: Very Rare (attunement up to 3 items)

*   CR 17+: Legendary (attunement up to 3 items)

*If the hirelings statblock has an armor or weapon listed, they are treated as proficient with all weapons or armor of that same type (e.g. leather - light armor, Shortsword - martial weapons).* If you have questions or concerns, please me know, and I will be working with existing groups/organizations to get you switched over to this new framework

#### From #Patch-Notes

Much discussion and collaboration has gone into the merger of Hireling system with the previously used Organizations system. They will now be brought under one framework, with some added rules for how groups and organizations will work under this system, how hirelings can be equipped with items from their employers, and some promising prospects for future updates.

Individual & Group Hirelings

The ability for individuals and established groups to hire NPCs to augment their personal or collaborative efforts will be broken into one of two categories, with slightly different considerations for each. Hirelings can be reskinned to represent any humanoid race or alignment, and can speak common by default.

Contracting Requirements (Individual)

Hiring updates occur once per week, using a point deduction system (Hire Points)

Points accrue at a rate of 1/week, requiring an approved post from a Loremaster These points are logged via ⁠transcript and the total equates to the Total CR that can be used to hire units for that update.

Point deductions are based on the CR being hired. CR 0 units count as CR 1/8 for this, there is a graph that breaks down these numbers in more detail.

Individuals contracting NPCs will use the 'Deployed Monthly' rate, as opposed the normal Daily/Monthly rate. These expenses are logged via ⁠transaction-log after the update is approved by a Loremaster. These expenses occur at the 1st of every month.

Maximum CR that can be hired as a single unit is capped at CR 20

Total CR that can be hired is capped at CR 20

The screenshot below shows a week-by-week breakdown of how hire points can be spent on the corresponding CR.


Contracting Requirements (Group/Organizational)

Hiring updates occur once per week, using a point deduction system (Hire Points)

Points accrue based upon the modifiers calculated for your group, from 1-10, requiring an approved post from a Loremaster. These points are logged via ⁠transcript and the total equates to the Total CR that can be used to hire units for that update.

Publicity (popularity, reputation, goodwill)

Accessibility (access, reach)

Logistics (training, supplies, budget)

Opportunity (job security, importance, need)

Temp buffs (quests/significant events)

Groups/Organizations consisting of multiple PCs behave as a single ‘entity’ for the sake of point accumulation and spending. Updates will be logged with a single PC representing the group.

Point deductions are based on the CR being hired. CR 0 units count as CR 1/8 for this, there is a graph that breaks down these numbers in more detail.

Groups/Organizations contracting NPCs will use the normal 'Monthly' rate. These expenses are logged via ⁠transaction-log after the update is approved by a Loremaster. These expenses occur at the 1st of every month.

Maximum CR that can be hired as a single unit is capped at CR 20

[![NPC pricing](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/c/c1/NPC_pricing.jpg/revision/latest/scale-to-width-down/180?cb=20241112155947)](https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/the-wandering-realm/images/c/c1/NPC_pricing.jpg/revision/latest?cb=20241112155947)

[](/docs/file:npc-pricing.jpg)

Magic Item Bazaar

# Magic Item Bazaar

⁠magic-item-bazaar is now open. One item per player till 3 days pass. Stocks reset Sunday

Magic Item Recycling

# Magic Item Recycling

### Magical Item Recycling

| Rarity | Recycle Length | Raw Materials Yielded | Magical Materials Yielded |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Common | 1 Day | 10 gp of Raw Materials | 0.25 CR 1 Material |
| Uncommon | 3 Days | 50 gp of Raw Materials | 0.25 CR 4 Material |
| Rare | 7 Days | 500 gp of Raw Materials | 0.5 CR 9 Material |
| Very Rare | 15 Days | 5,000 gp of Raw Materials | 1 CR 14 Material |
| Legendary | 25 Days | 25,000 gp of Raw Materials | 1 CR 19 Material |

**Note:** The CR material recovered from a recycled legendary is considered a Non-Quested material.

*   **Consumable Items:** Consumable items cannot be recycled.
*   **Magic Item Adept:** MIA doubles gold and materials yield from recycling Common/Uncommon magic items.
*   **Workers:** All workers recycling a magic item must possess appropriate proficiencies and tier level required to craft the item being recycled.

Npcs And Workers

# NPCs and Workers

## NPCs & Workers

### NPC Crafting Update

A) Unnamed NPCs can still be hired to make mundane crafts, with minimum/no RP. Unlimited NPCs per mundane craft, although a PC still needs to lead it. You have our blessing, changes will be implemented if this is abused.

B) Named NPC Crafters require RP to be created. What ‘skill level’ this NPC starts with varies from case-to-case depending on the RP being done. Follow the chart below for upgrading rules.

### NPC Upgrading

To allow for Named NPC crafters to upgrade the rarities they can craft. A specific amount of crafts of a certain rarity must be made (listed below) as well as a Wisdom check (made by PC) following the final craft (DCs also listed below).

##### NPC Crafter Upgrade Requirements

| New Tier | Minimum # of Crafts | Wisdom DC |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Uncommon | 20 Common | DC 15 |
| Rare | 40 Uncommon | DC18 |
| Very Rare | 60 Rare | DC20 |
| Legendary | 80 Very Rare | DC22 |

Notes:

*   Mundane Crafters will never be able to upgrade to common or higher.
*   Link the check in the final craft (whether the check passes or not).
*   Failing the check sends you back to 50% progress.
*   Worker Limit is still 5

### Retired PC Crafter Limit

Players are limited to **three retired PC crafters** at any one point. All retired PCs may do other allowed forms of downtime, but only three can craft at any one time.

**Reason:** To prevent people from overpopulating their personal supply of crafters by making and retiring PCs over and over again.

### Crafter Rule Change

If a player leaves the server, their characters can not be used to craft. If a character is retired and their sheet is not posted in ⁠retired-listings, they can not be used to craft. This change also removes the 3 month mandatory check-in between Guild Owner and Employee, because the assumption is the employee can leave the guild whenever they see fit.

**Note:** If a craft has already been initiated, and the player or players involved in the craft leave the server, the craft will be completed on schedule.

### Worker Rights

When you are using a PC to craft (everyone aside from the 'starter'), you MUST pay them each a minimum of 25 GP per day of crafting. This includes your own PCs, if applicable.

Player Organizations

# Player Organizations

## Player Organizations

### Regarding Guilds?

Guild/organization stock and coin cannot be taken out of the guild or used in non-guild pursuits by the leaders and officers unless rules in its constitution grants them the power to do so.

The rules being amendable are dependent on the guild/organization. Server default is yes, rules are amendable.

#### Guild Legislation

For sole leaders, if you have Player Characters working under you. It is required that changes to the guild constitution first be presented to all the players in employment for a vote, passing on at a majority.

This will give them the chance to respond to changes, or at least fair notice. We know that a sole owner has significant authority of their enterprise, but this rule is in place in interest of providing a fun environment for all players.

### Guild Donations

Donations to Guilds or Player Organizations require Craftmaster Approval.

Ping Craftmaster in all Guild or Player Organization donations. This does not include selling materials or items to guilds.

Poisons

# Poisons

### Poison Crafting Errata

Take the DC of the harvester for monster poisons or the crafter of the ones not obtained via monsters at the time of creation/harvest.

Research Downtime

# Research Downtime

**Research and Lore Writing**

A player would access a forum for research and lore writing. Then, ping dms with the goal they have in mind. In character, the player character must access a place where information relevant to their goal can be found. Or go and acquire objects or permission to do so.

For research, using the Forgotten Realms wiki as a basis for lore, pitch ideas to dms and other players. The option to run your own adventure will be made available with dm oversight.

For lore, pitch your timelines, details, and events to dms to approve and append to the server storyline

Research

@ \[Player\]  \[PC Name\]

Brief Description of your research/What you are trying to find. And location of conducting research.

Duration:

Costs: 50 gp + \[Intervals of 50, for a + \[X\] bonus to your check

     \-Library fees

     \-Stationery supplies

     \-Anything else deemed necessary for his research

Cost Doubles for every +1

Intelligence Check Result

\[Link\]

Start and End Dates

RP is encouraged and beneficial.  Lack of RP will often lead to a lack of results.

Link to beginning of RP:

Link to end of RP:

Retraining Rules

# Retraining Rules

## Retraining Rules

#### Class Retraining

By spending 10 workweeks of downtime, a character can choose to replace one of their levels in one class with a level in another class. This time is reduced by a number of workweeks equal to the character’s ability modifier in the key ability of the new class. If their new class has more than one key ability, the characters lowest ability modifier should be used for this calculation. The character must have a minimum of 13 in the key abilities of both their current class and their new class They must have an instructor of at least 1 level higher (or be level 20) than your new level in the new class and must spend 50gp per week for the instructor and relevant supplies.

#### Fighting Style Retraining

(For Fighters, Rangers, Paladins, Blood Hunters, and College of Swords Bards only) By spending a week of downtime, you can choose one of the (insert class name here) fighting style you know and replace it with another fighting style from the (insert class name here) class.

#### Feat / ASI Retraining

By spending a week of downtime, you can choose to replace one Feat or ASI you qualify for with another Feat or ASI you qualify for. Post20 ASIs can be retrained only to ASIs, and Post20 Feats can be retrained only to Boons or Feats.

#### Spell Retraining

(For sorcerers, warlocks, bards and rangers only) By spending a week of downtime,you can choose one of the (insert class name here) spells you know and replace it with another spell from the (insert class name here) spell list, which also must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

### Mystic Arcanum Retraining

Warlocks can spend 1 week of downtime to retrain one of their mystic arcanum spells for another spell that is applicable for that version of the mystic arcanum classfeat.

## RP Reduction

When you undergo retraining, you can submit an RP-XP request for roleplay related to the retraining and receive a reduction in the overall time spent retraining. Each RP-XP request is worth a 5% reduction, up to a maximum of 25%.

Retroactive Downtime

# Retroactive Downtime

We get it, you can’t always plan your character’s lives in real time. That’s why we’ve designed Retroactive Downtime, which lets you apply unused downtime at a reduced wait to cover gaps in gameplay and incetivize coming back to TWR!

Starting from the last date your PC used their downtime, you can now file a report to use **1/3 of the total days (rounded down**) on a **single goal or downtime activity** with staff approval.

## How does it work?

Players wishing to file for retroactive downtime must complete the report on the right and submit it to #transcript. Staff will determine the appropriate daily value for your character and resources and return the report with any important notes.

#### Custom Retroactive Downtime

We are also introducing a few new forms of “retroactive” downtime you can add to the report:

##### General Adventuring: XP Bonus

Characters who spend their time hunting, traveling, or otherwise generally adventuring receive bonus XP:

| Tier | Bonus XP Cap (Per Day) |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | 5 xp |
| 2 | 20 xp |
| 3 | 100 xp |
| 4 | 200 xp |
| Post-20 | 300 xp |

##### Service to a Deity: Favor Bonus

Characters who spend time in service of a deity will be rewarded for their faith with favor:

| Tier | Bonus Favor Cap (Per Day) |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | 1 Favor |
| 2 | 3 Favor |
| 3 | 5 Favor |
| 4 | 8 Favor |
| Post-20 | 10 Favor |

##### The Career Path: Gold Bonus

Through smart investments, performance bonuses, side hustles and promotions, business ownership, etc., you receive bonus Gold:

| Tier | Bonus Gold Cap (Per Day) |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | 5 gp |
| 2 | 25 gp |
| 3 | 50 gp |
| 4 | 200 gp |
| Post-20 | 250 gp |

#### Roleplay Bonuses

Players who perform roleplay with themselves or others to justify their downtime may include a summary and link to the roleplay for additional rewards.

##### Transcript Report: Retroactive Downtime

:clock1: Retroactive Downtime Request :clock1:

@(Player)

Last Downtime Date: DD/MM/YYYY

Retroactive Downtime: (Days since last downtime) / 3 = X

Summary of Downtime: Type of a brief summary of your character’s actions during their time away. You may only select a single downtime option or project.

Specifics: If you are selecting a specific downtime option as outlined in the source books, this document, or other TWR systems, include the report here using the “Retroactive Downtime” value above as the number of days spent. Then perform any rolls, checks, transactions, etc. as necessary here. You may also choose to perform more complicated projects such as starting a business, building a home, making friends in the form of NPCs, etc. through expanded roleplay.

Staff Review: Craftmasters and Dungeonmasters will review the report and consider any complications, necessary materials, or other concerns and work with you to apply your downtime in a reasonable way.

Approved By: @(DM or CM)

Requirements to Complete: Any costs, roleplay requirements, or contingencies for the downtime to become official

##### Plain Report

:clock1: Retroactive Downtime Request :clock1:

@(Player)

Last Downtime Date: DD/MM/YYYY

Retroactive Downtime: (Days since last downtime) / 3 = X

Summary of Downtime:

Specifics:

Staff Review:

Approved By: @(DM or CM)

Requirements to Complete:

Scribing Scrolls

# Scribing Scrolls

### **Scribing Scrolls**

Scribing a spell scroll takes an amount of time and money related to the level of the spell the character wants to scribe, as shown in the Spell Scroll Costs table. In addition, the character must have proficiency in the Arcana skill and must provide any material components required for the casting of the spell. Moreover, the character must have the spell prepared, or it must be among the character’s known spells, in order to scribe a scroll of that spell.

##### **Spell Scroll Costs Table**

| Spell Level | Time | Cost |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Cantrip | 1 Day | 15 gp |
| 1st | 1 Day | 25 gp |
| 2nd | 3 Days | 250 gp |
| 3rd | 5 Days | 500 gp |
| 4th | 10 Days | 2,500 gp |
| 5th | 12 Days | 5,000 gp |
| 6th | 24 Days | 15,000 gp |
| 7th | 38 Days | 25,000 gp |
| 8th | 96 Days | 50,000 gp |
| 9th | 144 Days | 200,000 gp |
| **Note:** Wish Requires a quested legendary material
to be used as part of the scribing process.

 |

Spell Copying Rules

# Spell Copying Rules

## Spell Copying Rules

### How to copy a spell on TWR

*   Locate a source in-character to copy the desired spell from
*   Post the above template to [#transcript](https://discord.com/channels/817263494818693170/817301000770551828), including all of the relevant information
*   Once the post is moved to [#records](https://discord.com/channels/817263494818693170/817297426335006751), you may add the spell to your spellbook and deduct the money from your sheet

#### Other Useful Tips

*   Check the "Your Spellbook" section of the PHB for information on creating a backup or replacing a spellbook
*   Check the [Retroactive Downtime](/docs/retroactive-downtime "retroactive downtime") rules if you forget or get busy!
*   Don't forget, copying takes time! You haven't finished coyping until the post moves to [#records](https://discord.com/channels/817263494818693170/817297426335006751)

\*\* 📚 Spell Copying 📚 \*\*

\*PC Name:\* \[Name\] (@discordtag)

\*Spell Source:\* Name the player or NPC providing the spell

\*Spells Copied:\* List any spells learned here

\*Reductions:\* List any Subclass reductions or other buffs here

\*Gold Spent:\* List the final cost for all copied spells here

\*Time Taken:\* List the amount of downtime spent copying (round to the next day) here

Training Downtime

# Training Downtime

#### Training Downtime

Characters can perform activities to train and practice in their free time, those who do not wish to hunt can spend time training themselves as well, this could be tinkering and experimenting for Artificers, praying and studying for Clerics, scribing and practicing spells for Wizards, sparring for fighters, and stealthing around for rogues, all forms of training are applicable and make no mechanical difference

Characters can choose to spend 5 Hunting Tags or expend 8 hours of downtime in training which awards experience points to reflect the development of skills. Players who choose to roleplay the lessons will be awarded with additional RPXP as per usual

Tier | XP Per Day (not RPXP) 1 | 100 xp 2 | 250 xp 3 | 500 xp 4 | 1,000 xp Post-20 | 2,000 xp

Ventures

# Ventures

Business empires and peddler wagons. Player characters can now obtain money, experience, and more by roleplaying their business ventures. The owner of a venture, need not be personally involved. Narration of employees and the setting or providing venues for an event qualifies for gain.

**Investment:**

The value of things committed to make coin. Capital is the stuff that are not consumed in the venture. General examples: land, property, and facilities.

**Gain:**

Scaling from 0 to 200% of the investment. Graded by pinks and bookworms.

Because we do not capture depreciation here, more weight will be given to expended coin than capital, which can be reused. Do not be surprised if your million gold cargo ship does not get you 1.5 million in returns.

**Distribution:**

Player notes the percentage of gain given to people involved.

If you wish for particular items, materials, or other gains that are not coin, do it here and mark to whom it should go to.

Pinks/bookworms will revise this section which tells the player(s) what was earned. Including RPXP.
